no one remember who or what dhunni bhagat have be he have live his life make little money and spend it all as every good hindu should do on work of piety the chubára that be full of brick cell gaily paint with the figure of god and king and elephant where wear out priest could sit and meditate on the latter end of thing the path be brick pave and the naked foot of thousand have wear they into gutter clump of mango sprout from between the brick great pipal tree overhang the well windlass that whine all day and host of parrot tear through the tree crow and squirrel be tame in that place for they know that never priest would touch they the wander mendicant charm seller and holy vagabond for hundred mile round use to make the chubára their place of call and rest mahommedan sikh and hindu mix equally under the tree they be old man and when man have come to the turnstile of night all the creed in the world seem to he wonderfully alike and colourless gobind
the one eyed tell this he be holy man who live on an island in the middle of river and feed the fish with little bread pellet twice day in flood time when swollen corps strand themselves at the foot of the island gobind would cause they to be piously burn for the sake of the honour of mankind and have regard to his own account with god hereafter
but when two third of the island be tear away in spate gobind come across the river to dhunni bhagat ’s chubára he and his brass drinking vessel with the well cord round the neck his short arm rest crutch stud with brass nail his roll of bed his big pipe his umbrella and his tall sugar loaf hat with the nod peacock feather in it he wrap himself up in his patch quilt make of every colour and material in the world sit down in sunny corner of the very quiet chubára and rest his arm on his short handle crutch wait for death the people bring he food and little clump of marigold flower and he give his blessing in return he be nearly blind and his face be seam and line and wrinkle beyond belief for he have live in his time which be before the english come within five hundred mile of dhunni bhagat ’s chubára when we grow to know each other well gobind would tell tale in voice most like the rumbling of heavy gun over wooden bridge his tale be true but not one in twenty could be print in an english book because the english do not think as native do they brood over matter that native would dismiss till fitting occasion and what they would not think twice about native will brood over till fitting occasion then native and english stare at each other hopelessly across great gulfs of miscomprehension and what say gobind one sunday evening be your honoured craft and by what manner of mean earn you your daily bread be say kerani_—one who write with pen upon paper not be in the service of the government then what do you write say gobind come near for can not see your countenance and the light fail write of all matter that lie within my understanding and of many that do not but chiefly write of life and death and man and woman and love and fate accord to the measure of my ability tell the tale through the mouth of one two or more people then by the favour of god the tale be sell and money accrue to that may keep alive even so say gobind that be the work of the bazar story teller
but he speak straight to man and woman and do not write anything at all only when the tale have arouse expectation and calamity be about to befall the virtuous he stop suddenly and demand payment
ere he continue the narration be it so in your craft my son have hear of such thing when tale be of great length and be sell as cucumber in small piece ay be once famed teller of story when be beg on the road between koshin and etra before the last pilgrimage that ever take to orissa tell many tale and hear many more at the rest house in the evening when we be merry at the end of the march it be in my heart that grown man be but as little child in the matter of tale and the old tale be the most beloved with your people that be truth say i. but in regard to our people they desire new tale and when all be write they rise up and declare that the tale be well tell in such and such manner and doubt either the truth or the invention thereof but what folly be theirs say gobind throw out his knotted hand tale that be tell be true tale as long as the telling last and of their talk upon it you know how bilas khan that be the prince of tale teller say to one who mock he in the great rest house on the jhelum road go on my brother and finish that have begin and he who mock take up the tale but have neither voice nor manner for the task come to standstill and the pilgrim at supper make he eat abuse and stick half that night nay but with our people money having pass it be their right as we should turn against shoeseller in regard to shoe if those wear out if ever make book you shall see and judge and the parrot say to the fall tree wait brother till fetch prop say gobind with grim chuckle god have give eighty year and it may be some over can not look for more than day grant by day and as favour at this tide be swift in what manner be it good to set about the task say chief of those who string pearl with their tongue how do know yet”—he think for little—“how should not know god have make very many head
but there be only one heart in all the world among your people or my people they be child in the matter of tale but none be so terrible as the little one if man misplace word or in second telling vary event by so much as one small devil ay also have tell tale to the little one but do thou this his old eye fall on the gaudy painting of the wall the blue and red dome and the flame of the poinsettia beyond tell they first of those thing that thou hast see
and they have see together thus their knowledge will piece out thy imperfection tell they of what thou alone hast see then what thou hast hear
and since they be child tell they of battle and king horse devil elephant and angel but omit not to tell they of love and such like all the earth be full of tale to he who listen and do not drive away the poor from his door the poor be the good of tale teller for they must lay their ear to the ground every night after this conversation the idea grow in my head and gobind be press in his inquiry as to the health of the book later when we have be part for month it happen that be to go away and far off and come to bid gobind good
bye it be farewell between we now for go very long journey say and also long one than thou but what of the book say he it will be bear in due season if it be so ordain would could see it say the old man huddle beneath his quilt but that will not be die three day hence in the night little before the dawn the term of my year be accomplish in nine case out of ten native make no miscalculation as to the day of his death he have the foreknowledge of the beast in this respect then thou wilt depart in peace and it be good talk for thou hast say that life be no delight to thee
but it be pity that our book be not bear how shall know that there be any record of my name because promise in the forepart of the book precede everything else that it shall be write gobind sadhu of the island in the river and await god in dhunni bhagat ’s chubára first speak of the book say i. and give counsel an old man ’s counsel gobind son of gobind of the chumi village in the karaon tehsil in the district of mooltan will that be write also that will be write also and the book will go across the black water to the house of your people and all the sahib will know of who be eighty year old all who read the book shall know can not promise for the rest that be good talk call aloud to all who be in the monastery and will tell they this thing they troop up faquir sadhu sunnyasis byragis nihang and mullah priest of all faith and every degree of raggedness and gobind lean upon his crutch speak so that they be visibly fill with envy and white haired senior bade gobind think of his latter end instead of transitory repute in the mouth of strange then gobind give his blessing and come away these tale have be collect from all place and all sort of people from priest in the chubára from ala yar the carver jiwun singh the carpenter nameless man on steamer and train round the world woman spin outside their cottage in the twilight officer and gentleman now dead and bury and few but these be the very good my father give the great part of they have be publish in magazine and newspaper to whose editor be indebted but some be new on this side of the water and some have not see the light before the most remarkable story be of course those which do not appear for obvious reason content page dray wara yow dee namgay doola the city of dreadful night the judgment of dungara the finance of the god at howli thana in flood time moti guj mutineer without benefit of clergy naboth the sending of dana da through the fire the head of the district the amir ’s homily at twenty two jews in shushan georgie porgie little tobrah gemini
the limitation of pambé serang one view of the question from many invention on the city wall the enlightenment of pagett m. p. yow dee for jealousy be the rage of man therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance.—_prov almond and raisin sahib grape from kabul or pony of the rare if the sahib will only come with he be thirteen three sahib play polo go in cart carry lady and holy kurshe and the bless imam it be the sahib himself my heart be make fat and my eye glad may you never be tired as be cold water in the tirah so be the sight of friend in far place and what do you in this accursed land south of delhi sahib you know the saying—“rats be the man and trull the woman it be an order an order be an order till one be strong enough to disobey my brother my friend we have meet in an auspicious hour be all well in the heart and the body and the house in lucky day have we two come together again be to go with you your favour be great will there be picket room in the compound have three horse and the bundle and the horse boy moreover remember that the police here hold horse thief what do these lowland bastard know of horse thief do you remember that time in peshawur when kamal hammer on the gate of jumrud mountebank that he be and lift the colonel ’s horse all in one night kamal be dead now
but his nephew have take up the matter and there will be more horse amisse if the khaiber levy do not look to it the peace of god and the favour of his prophet be upon this house and all that be in it shafizullah rope the mottled mare under the tree and draw water the horse can stand in the sun but double the felt over the loin nay my friend do not trouble to look they over they be to sell to the officer fool who know so many thing of the horse the mare be heavy in foal the gray be devil unlicke and the dun
but you know the trick of the peg when they be sell go back to pubbi or it may be the valley of peshawur friend of my heart it be good to see you again have be bow and lie all day to the officer sahib in respect to those horse and my mouth be dry for straight talk before meal tobacco be good do not join for we be not in our own country sit in the verandah and will spread my cloth here but first will drink in the name of god return thank thrice this be sweet water indeed sweet as the water of sheoran when it come from the snow they be all well and pleased in the north khoda baksh and the other yar khan have come down with the horse from kurdistan six and thirty head only and full half pack pony and have say openly in the kashmir serai that you english should send gun and blow the amir into hell there be fifteen toll now on the kabul road and at dakka when he think he be clear yar khan be strip of all his balkh stallion by the governor this be great injustice and yar khan be hot with rage and of the other mahbub ali be still at pubbi write god know what tugluq khan be in jail for the business of the kohat police post faiz beg come down from ismail ki dhera with bokhariot belt for thee my brother at the closing of the year but none know whither thou hadst go there be no news leave behind the cousin have take new run near pakpattan to breed mule for the government cart and there be story in bazar of priest such salt tale listen sahib why do you ask that my clothe be foul because of the dust on the road my eye be sad because of the glare of the sun my foot be swollen because have wash they in bitter water and my cheek be hollow because the food here be bad fire burn your money what do want with it be rich and think you be my friend but you be like the other sahib be man sad give he money say the sahib be he dishonour give he money say the sahib hath he wrong upon his head give he money say the sahib such be the sahib and such art thou even thou nay do not look at the foot of the dun pity it be that ever teach you to know the leg of horse be it so what of that the road be hard and the mare footsore she bear double burden sahib and now pray you give permission to depart great favour and honour have the sahib do and graciously have he show his belief that the horse be steal will it please he to send to the thana to call sweeper and have lead away by one of these lizard man be the sahib ’s friend have drunk water in the shadow of his house and he have blacken my face remain there anything more to do will the sahib give eight anna to make smooth the injury and complete the insult forgive my brother know not know not now what say yes lie to you will put dust on my head and be an afridi the horse have be march footsore from the valley to this place and my eye be dim and my body ache for the want of sleep and my heart be dry up with sorrow and shame but as it be my shame so by god the dispenser of justice by allah al mumit it shall be my own revenge we have speak together with naked heart before this and our hand have dip into the same dish and thou hast be to as brother therefore pay thee back with lie and ingratitude as pathan listen now when the grief of the soul be too heavy for endurance it may be little ease by speech and moreover the mind of true man be as well and the pebble of confession drop therein sink and be no more see from the valley have come on foot league by league with fire in my chest like the fire of the pit and why hast thou then so quickly forget our custom among this folk who sell their wife and their daughter for silver come back with to the north and be among man once more come back when this matter be accomplish and call for thee the bloom of the peach orchard be upon all the valley and here be only dust and great stink there be pleasant wind among the mulberry tree and the stream be bright with snow water and the caravan go up and the caravan go down and hundred fire sparkle in the gut of the pass and tent peg answer hammer nose and pack horse squeal to pack horse across the drift smoke of the evening it be good in the north now come back with let we return to our own people whence be my sorrow do man tear out his heart and make fritter thereof over slow fire for aught other than woman do not laugh friend of mine for your time will also be woman of the abazai be she and take she to wife to staunch the feud between our village and the man of ghor be no long young the lime have touch my beard have no need of the wedding nay
but love she
what saith rahman into whose heart love enter there be folly and naught else by glance of the eye she hath blind thee and by the eyelid and the fringe of the eyelid take thee into the captivity without ransom and naught else dost thou remember that song at the sheep roast in the pindi camp among the uzbeg of the amir the abazai be dog and their woman the servant of sin there be lover of her own people but of that her father tell naught my friend curse for in your prayer as curse at each pray from the fakr to the isha the name of daoud shah abazai whose head be still upon his neck whose hand be still upon his wrist who have do dishonour who have make my name laugh stock among the woman of little malikand go into hindustan at the end of two month to cherat be go twelve day only but have say that would be fifteen day absent this do to try she for it be write trust not the incapable come up the gorge alone in the fall of the light hear the voice of man sing at the door of my house and it be the voice of daoud shah and the song that he sing be dray wara
yow dee_”—“all three be one it be as though heel rope have be slip round my heart and all the devil be draw it tight past endurance creep silently up the hill road but the fuse of my matchlock be wet with the rain and could not slay daoud shah from afar moreover it be in my mind to kill the woman also thus he sing sit outside my house and anon the woman open the door and come near crawl on my belly among the rock have only my knife to my hand but stone slip under my foot and the two look down the hillside and he leave his matchlock flee from my anger because he be afraid for the life that be in he
but the woman move not till stand in front of she cry woman what be this that thou hast do
and she void of fear though she know my thought
laugh say it be little thing love he and thou art dog and cattle thief come by night and be still blind by her beauty for my friend the woman of the abazai be very fair say hast thou no fear
and she answer none but only the fear that do not die then say have no fear
and she bow her head and smite it off at the neck bone so that it leap between my foot thereafter the rage of our people come upon and hack off the breast that the man of little malikand might know the crime and cast the body into the water course that flow to the kabul river dray wara yow dee dray wara
yow dee the body without the head the soul without light and my own darkle heart all three be one all three be one that night make no halt go to ghor and demand news of daoud shah man say he be go to pubbi for horse what wouldst thou of he there be peace between the village make answer aye the peace of treachery and the love that the devil atala bear to gurel so fire thrice into the gate and laugh and go my way in those hour brother and friend of my heart ’s heart the moon and the star be as blood above and in my mouth be the taste of dry earth also break no bread and my drink be the rain of the valley of ghor upon my face at pubbi find mahbub ali the writer sit upon his charpoy and give up my arm accord to your law but be not grieve for it be in my heart that should kill daoud shah with my bare hand thus as man strip bunch of raisin mahbub ali say daoud shah have even now go hot foot to peshawur and he will pick up his horse upon the road to delhi for it be say that the bombay tramway company be buy horse there by the truck load eight horse to the truck and that be true say then see that the hunting would be no little thing for the man be go into your border to save himself against my wrath and shall he save himself so be not alive though he run northward to the dora and the snow or southerly to the black water will follow he as lover follow the footstep of his mistress and come upon he will take he tenderly aho so tenderly!—in my arm say well hast thou do and well shalt thou be repay and out of that embrace daoud shah shall not go forth with the breath in his nostril where be the pitcher be as thirsty as mother mare in the first month your law what be your law to when the horse fight on the run do they regard the boundary pillar or do the kite of ali musjid forbear because the carrion lie under the shadow of the ghor kuttri the matter begin across the border it shall finish where god please here in my own country or in hell
all three be one listen now sharer of the sorrow of my heart and will tell of the hunting follow to peshawur from pubbi and go to and fro about the street of peshawur like houseless dog seek for my enemy once think that see he wash his mouth in the conduit in the big square but when come up he be go it may be that it be he and see my face he have flee girl of the bazar say that he would go to nowshera say heart ’s heart do daoud shah visit thee and she say even so say would fain see he for we be friend part for two year hide pray here in the shadow of the window shutter and will wait for his come and the girl say pathan look into my eye and turn lean upon her breast and look into her eye swear that speak the very truth of god
but she answer never friend wait friend with such eye lie to god and the prophet but to woman ye can not lie get hence there shall no harm befall daoud shah by cause of would have strangle that girl but for the fear of your police and thus the hunting would have come to naught therefore only laugh and depart
and she lean over the window bar in the night and mock down the street her name be jamun when have make my account with the man will return to peshawur and her lover shall desire she no more for her beauty ’s sake she shall not be jamun but ak the cripple among
tree ho
ho shall she be at peshawur buy the horse and grape and the almond and dry fruit that the reason of my wandering might be open to the government and that there might be no hindrance upon the road but when come to nowshera he be go and know not where to go stay one day at nowshera and in the night voice speak in my ear as sleep among the horse all night it fly round my head and would not cease from whisper be upon my belly sleep as the devil sleep and it may have be that the voice be the voice of devil it say go south and thou shalt come upon daoud shah listen my brother and chief among friend listen be the tale long one think how it be long to have tread every league of the road from pubbi to this place and from nowshera my guide be only the voice and the lust of vengeance to the uttock go
but that be no hindrance to ho ho man may turn the word twice even in his trouble the uttock be no uttock obstacle to and hear the voice above the noise of the water beat on the big rock say go to the right so go to pindigheb and in those day my sleep be take from utterly and the head of the woman of the abazai be before night and day even as it have fall between my foot dray wara yow dee dray wara yow dee fire ash and my couch all three be one all three be one now be far from the winter path of the dealer who have go to sialkot and so south by the rail and the big road to the line of cantonment but there be sahib in camp at pindigheb who buy from white mare at good price and tell that one daoud shah have pas to shahpur with horse then see that the warning of the voice be true and make swift to come to the salt hill the jhelum be in flood but could not wait and in the cross bay stallion be wash down and drown herein be god hard to not in respect of the beast of that have no care but in this snatching while be upon the right bank urge the horse into the water daoud shah be upon the left for—_alghias alghias!_—the hoofs of my mare scatter the hot ash of his fire when we come up the hither bank in the light of morning but he have flee his foot be make swift by the terror of death and go south from shahpur as the kite fly dare not turn aside lest should miss my vengeance which be my right from shahpur skirt by the jhelum for think that he would avoid the desert of the rechna but presently at sahiwal turn away upon the road to jhang samundri and gugera till upon night the mottled mare breast the fence of the rail that run to montgomery and that place be okara and the head of the woman of the abazai lie upon the sand between my foot thence go to fazilka and they say that be mad to bring starve horse there the voice be with and be not mad but only weary because could not find daoud shah it be write that should not find he at rania nor bahadurgarh and come into delhi from the west and there also find he not my friend have see many strange thing in my wandering have see devil riot across the rechna as the stallion riot in spring have hear the djinn call to each other from hole in the sand and have see they pass before my face there be no devil say the sahib they be very wise but they do not know all thing about devil or horse say to you who be laugh at my misery that have see the devil at high noon whoop and leap on the shoal of the chenab and be afraid my brother when the desire of man be set upon one thing alone he fear neither god nor man nor devil if my vengeance fail would splinter the gate of paradise with the butt of my gun or would cut my way into hell with my knife and would call upon those who govern there for the body of daoud shah what love so deep as hate do not speak know the thought in your heart be the white of this eye cloud how do the blood beat at the wrist there be no madness in my flesh but only the vehemence of the desire that have eat up listen south of delhi know not the country at all therefore can not say where go but pass through many city know only that it be lie upon to go south when the horse could march no more throw myself upon the earth and wait till the day there be no sleep with in that journey and that be heavy burden dost thou know brother of mine the evil of wakefulness that can not break when the bone be sore for lack of sleep and the skin of the temple twitch with weariness and yet there be no sleep there be no sleep dray wara yow dee dray wara yow dee the eye of the sun the eye of the moon and my own unrestful eye all three be one all three be one there be city the name whereof have forget and there the voice call all night that be ten day ago it have cheat afresh have come hither from place call hamirpur and behold it be my fate that should meet with thee to my comfort and the increase of friendship this be good omen by the joy of look upon thy face
the weariness have go from my foot and the sorrow of my so long travel be forget also my heart be peaceful for know that the end be near it may be that shall find daoud shah in this city go northward since hillman will ever head back to his hill when the spring warn and shall he see those hill of our country surely shall overtake he surely my vengeance be safe surely god hath he in the hollow of his hand against my claiming there shall no harm befall daoud shah till come for would fain kill he quick and whole with the life stick firm in his body pomegranate be sweetest when the clove break away unwilling from the rind let it be in the daytime that may see his face and my delight may be crown and when have accomplish the matter and my honour be make clean shall return thank unto god the holder of the scale of the law and shall sleep from the night through the day and into the night again shall sleep and no dream shall trouble and now my brother the tale be all tell ahi ahi ahi namgay doola there come to the beach poor exile of erin the dew on his wet robe hang heavy and chill
ere the steamer that bring he have pas out of hearin he be alderman mike inthrojuicin bill american song once upon time there be king who live on the road to thibet very many mile in the himalayas his kingdom be eleven thousand foot above the sea and exactly four mile square but most of the mile stand on end owe to the nature of the country his revenue be rather less than four hundred pound yearly and they be expend in the maintenance of one elephant and stand army of five man he be tributary to the indian government who allow he certain sum for keep section of the himalaya thibet road in repair he far increase his revenue by sell timber to the railway company for he would cut the great deodar tree in his one forest and they fall thunder into the sutlej river and be sweep down to the plain three hundred mile away and become railway tie now and again this king whose name do not matter would mount ringstraked horse and ride score of mile to simla town to confer with the lieutenant governor on matter of state or to assure the viceroy that his sword be at the service of the queen empress then the viceroy would cause ruffle of drum to be sound and the ringstraked horse and the cavalry of the state two man in tatter and the herald who bear the silver stick before the king would trot back to their own place which lie between the tail of heaven climb glacier and dark birch forest now from such king always remember that he possess one veritable elephant and could count his descent for twelve hundred year expect when it be my fate to wander through his dominion no more than mere license to live the night have close in rain and roll cloud blot out the light of the village in the valley forty mile away untouched by cloud or storm the white shoulder of donga pa the mountain of the council of the god uphold the evening star the monkey sing sorrowfully to each other as they hunt for dry roost in the fern wreathe tree and the last puff of the day wind bring from the unseen village the scent of damp wood smoke hot cake drip undergrowth and rot pine cone that be the true smell of the himalayas and if once it creep into the blood of man that man will at the last forget all else return to the hill to die the cloud close and the smell go away and there remain nothing in all the world except chill white mist and the boom of the sutlej river race through the valley below fat tailed sheep who do not want to die bleat piteously at my tent door he be scuffle with the prime minister and the director general of public education and he be royal gift to and my camp servant express my thank suitably and ask if might have audience of the king the prime minister readjust his turban which have fall off in the struggle and assure that the king would be very pleased to see therefore despatch two bottle as foretaste and when the sheep have enter upon another incarnation go to the king ’s palace through the wet he have send his army to escort but the army stay to talk with my cook soldier be very much alike all the world over the palace be four roomed and whitewashed mud and timber house the fine in all the hill for day ’s journey the king be dress in purple velvet jacket white muslin trouser and saffron yellow turban of price he give audience in little carpeted room open off the palace courtyard which be occupy by the elephant of state the great beast be sheet and anchor from trunk to tail and the curve of his back stand out grandly against the mist the prime minister and the director general of public education be present to introduce but all the court have be dismiss lest the two bottle aforesaid should corrupt their moral the king cast wreath of heavy scented flower round my neck as bow and inquire how my honour presence have the felicity to be say that through see his auspicious countenance the mist of the night have turn into sunshine and that by reason of his beneficent sheep his good deed would be remember by the god he say that since have set my magnificent foot in his kingdom the crop would probably yield seventy per cent more than the average say that the fame of the king have reach to the four corner of the earth and that the nation gnash their tooth when they hear daily of the glory of his realm and the wisdom of his moon like prime minister and lotus like director general of public education then we sit down on clean white cushion and be at the king ’s right hand three minute later he be tell that the state of the maize crop be something disgraceful and that the railway company would not pay he enough for his timber the talk shift to and fro with the bottle and we discuss very many stately thing and the king become confidential on the subject of government generally most of all he dwell on the shortcoming of one of his subject who from all could gather have be paralyze the executive in the old day say the king could have order the elephant yonder to trample he to death now must e’en send he seventy mile across the hill to be try and his keep would be upon the state the elephant eat everything what be the man ’s crime rajah sahib say i. firstly he be an outlander and no man of mine own people secondly since of my favour give he land upon his first coming he refuse to pay revenue be not the lord of the earth above and below entitle by right and custom to one eighth of the crop
yet this devil establish himself refuse to pay single tax and he bring poisonous spawn of babe cast he into jail say sahib the king answer shift little on the cushion once and only once in these forty year sickness come upon so that be not able to go abroad in that hour make vow to my god that would never again cut man or woman from the light of the sun and the air of god for perceive the nature of the punishment how can break my vow be it only the lopping of hand or foot should not delay but even that be impossible now that the english have rule one or another of my people”—he look obliquely at the director general of public education—“would at once write letter to the viceroy and perhaps should be deprive of my ruffle of drum he unscrew the mouthpiece of his silver water pipe fit plain amber mouthpiece and pass his pipe to not content with refuse revenue he continue this outlander refuse also the begar this be the corvée or force labour on the road and stir my people up to the like treason
yet he be when he will an expert log snatcher there be none well or bold among my people to clear block of the river when the log stick fast but he worship strange god say the prime minister deferentially for that have no concern say the king who be as tolerant as akbar in matter of belief to each man his own god and the fire or mother earth for we all at last it be the rebellion that offend the king have an army suggest have not the king burn the man ’s house and leave he naked to the night dew nay hut be hut and it hold the life of man but once send my army against he when his excuse become wearisome of their head he brake three across the top with stick the other two man run away also the gun would not shoot have see the equipment of the infantry one third of it be an old muzzle load fowling piece with ragged rust hole where the nipple should have be one third wire bind matchlock with worm eat stock and one third four bore flint duck gun without flint
but it be to be remember say the king reach out for the bottle that he be very expert log snatcher and man of merry face what shall do to he sahib this be interesting the timid hill folk would as soon have refuse taxi to their king as revenue to their god if it be the king ’s permission say will not strike my tent till the third day and will see this man the mercy of the king be god like and rebellion be like unto the sin of witchcraft moreover both the bottle and another be empty you have my leave to go say the king next morning crier go through the state proclaim that there be log jam on the river and that it behove all loyal subject to remove it the people pour down from their village to the moist warm valley of poppy field and the king and go with they hundred of dress deodar log have catch on snag of rock and the river be bring down more log every minute to complete the blockade the water snarl and wrench and worried at the timber and the population of the state begin prod the near log with pole in the hope of start general movement then there go up shout of namgay doola namgay doola and large red haired villager hurry up strip off his clothe as he run that be he that be the rebel say the king now will the dam be clear but why have he red hair ask since red hair among hill folk be as common as blue or green he be an outlander say the king well do
oh well do namgay doola have scramble out on the jam and be claw out the butt of log with rude sort of boat hook it slide forward slowly as an alligator move three or four other follow it and the green water spout through the gap they have make then the villager howl and shout and scramble across the log pull and push the obstinate timber and the red head of namgay doola be chief among they all the log sway and chafe and groan as fresh consignment from up stream batter the new weaken dam all give way at last in smother of foam racing log bob black head and confusion indescribable the river toss everything before it see the red head go down with the last remnant of the jam and disappear between the great grind tree trunk it rise close to the bank and blow like grampus namgay doola wring the water out of his eye and make obeisance to the king have time to observe he closely the virulent redness of his shock head and beard be most startling and in the thicket of hair wrinkle above high cheek bone shine two very merry blue eye he be indeed an outlander but yet thibetan in language habit and attire he speak the lepcha dialect with an indescribable softening of the guttural it be not so much lisp as an accent whence comest thou from thibet he point across the hill and grin that grin go straight to my heart mechanically hold out my hand and namgay doola shake it no pure thibetan would have understand the meaning of the gesture he go away to look for his clothe and as he climb back to his village hear joyous yell that seem unaccountably familiar it be the whooping of namgay doola you see now say the king why would not kill he he be bold man among my log
but and he shake his head like schoolmaster know that before long there will be complaint of he in the court let we return to the palace and do justice it be that king ’s custom to judge his subject every day between eleven and three o’clock
see he decide equitably in weighty matter of trespass slander and little wife steal then his brow cloud
and he summon again it be namgay doola he say despairingly not content with refuse revenue on his own part he have bind half his village by an oath to the like treason never before have such thing befall nor be my taxis heavy rabbit face villager with blush rise stick behind his ear advanced tremble he have be in the conspiracy but have tell everything and hop for the king ’s favour king say i. if it be the king ’s will let this matter stand over till the morning only the god can do right swiftly and it may be that yonder villager have lie nay for know the nature of namgay doola but since guest ask let the matter remain wilt thou speak harshly to this red head outlander he may listen to thee make an attempt that very evening
but for the life of could not keep my countenance namgay doola grin persuasively and begin to tell about big brown bear in poppy field by the river would care to shoot it speak austerely on the sin of conspiracy and the certainty of punishment namgay doola ’s face cloud for moment shortly afterwards he withdraw from my tent and hear he sing to himself softly among the pine the word be unintelligible to but the tune like his liquid insinuate speech seem the ghost of something
strangely familiar dir hané mard yemen dir
to weeree ala gee sing namgay doola again and again and rack my brain for that lose tune it be not till after dinner that discover some one have cut square foot of velvet from the centre of my good camera cloth this make so angry that wander down the valley in the hope of meet the big brown bear could hear he grunt like discontented pig in the poppy field and wait shoulder deep in the dew drip indian corn to catch he after his meal the moon be at full and draw out the rich scent of the tasselled crop then hear the anguished bellow of himalayan cow one of the little black crummie no big than newfoundland dog two shadow that look like bear and her cub hurry past be in act to fire when see that they have each brilliant red head the less animal be trail some rope behind it that leave dark track on the path they pass within six foot of and the shadow of the moonlight lie velvet black on their face velvet black be exactly the word for by all the power of moonlight they be mask in the velvet of my camera cloth marvel and go to bed next morning the kingdom be in uproar namgay doola man say have go forth in the night and with sharp knife have cut off the tail of cow belong to the rabbit face villager who have betray he it be sacrilege unspeakable against the holy cow the state desire his blood
but he have retreat into his hut barricade the door and window with big stone and defy the world the king and and the populace approach the hut cautiously there be no hope of capture the man without loss of life for from hole in the wall project the muzzle of an extremely well care for gun the only gun in the state that could shoot namgay doola have narrowly miss villager just before we come up the stand army stand it could do no more for when it advance piece of sharp shale fly from the window to these be add from time to time shower of scald water we see red head bob up and down in the hut the family of namgay doola be aid their sire and blood curdle yell of defiance be the only answer to our prayer never say the king puffing have such thing befall my state next year will certainly buy it be the custom to pay revenue to the king why at all by the god of my father can not tell say namgay doola and who be thy father the same that have this gun he show his weapon tower musket bearing but there be one word which he say often thus shun then and my brother stand upon our foot our hand to our side thus even so and what be thy mother woman of the hill we be lepcha of darjeeling but the hour of the angelus very solemnly the red head brat rise from the floor and form semicircle namgay doola lie his gun against the wall light little oil lamp and set it before recess in the wall pull aside curtain of dirty cloth he reveal wear brass crucifix lean against the helmet badge of long forget east india regiment thus do my father he say cross himself clumsily the wife and child follow suit then all together they strike up the wail chant that hear on the hillside dir hané mard yemen dir to weeree ala gee be puzzle no long again and again they croon as if their heart would break their version of the chorus of the wear of the green they ’re hang man and woman too for the wearing of the green diabolical inspiration come to one of the brat boy about eight year old be watch as he sing pull out rupee hold the coin between finger and thumb and look only look at the gun against the wall grin of brilliant and perfect comprehension overspread the face of the child never for but oh sahib that man betray and have no thought but the heifer ’s tail wave in the moonlight and have my knife what else should have do the tail come off ere be aware sahib thou knowest more than i. that be true say i. stay within the door go to speak to the king the population of the state be range on the hillside go forth and speak to the king king say i. touch this man there be two course open to thy wisdom thou canst either hang he from tree he and his brood till there remain no hair that be red within the land nay say the king why should hurt the little child they have pour out of the hut door and be make plump obeisance to everybody namgay doola wait with his gun across his arm or thou canst discard the impiety of the cow maim raise he to honour in thy army he come of race that will not pay revenue red flame be in his blood which come out at the top of his head in that glow hair make he chief of the army give he honour as may befall and full allowance of work but look to it king that neither he nor his hold foot of earth from thee henceforward feed he with word and favour and also liquor from certain bottle that thou know of and he will be bulwark of defence but deny he even tuft of grass for his own this be the nature that god have give he moreover he have brother the state groan unanimously
but if his brother come they will surely fight with each other till they die or else the one will always give information concern the other shall he be of thy army king choose the king bow his head and say come forth namgay doola and command the king ’s army thy name shall no more be namgay in the mouth of man but patsay doola for as thou hast say know then namgay doola new christen patsay doola son of timlay doola which be tim doolan go very wrong indeed clasp the king ’s foot cuff the stand army and hurry in an agony of contrition from temple to temple make offering for the sin of cattle maim and the king be so pleased with my perspicacity that he offer to sell village for twenty pound sterling but buy no village in the himalayas so long as one red head flare between the tail of the heaven climb glacier and the dark birch forest know that breed the city of dreadful night copyright by macmillan co. the dense wet heat that hang over the face of land like blanket prevent all hope of sleep in the first instance the cicala help the heat and the yell jackal the cicala
