say justus in the early day of his career yes he add with conviction they shall be good and shall with their hand to work learn for all good christians must work and upon stipend more modest even than that of an english lay reader justus krenk keep house beyond kamala and the gorge of malair beyond the berbulda river close to the foot of the blue hill of panth on whose summit stand the temple of dungara in the heart of the country of the buria kol the naked good temper timid shameless lazy buria kol do you know what life at mission outpost mean try to imagine loneliness exceed that of the small station to which government have ever send you isolation that weigh upon the wake eyelid and drive you by force headlong into the labour of the day there be no post there be no one of your own colour to speak to there be no road there be indeed food to keep you alive but it be not pleasant to eat and whatever of good or beauty or interest there be in your life must come from yourself and the grace that may be plant in you in the morning with patter of soft foot the convert the doubtful and the open scoffer troop up to the verandah you must be infinitely kind and patient and above all clear sighted for you deal with the simplicity of childhood the experience of man and the subtlety of the savage your congregation have hundred material want to be consider and it be for you as you believe in your personal responsibility to your maker to pick out of the clamour crowd any grain of spirituality that may lie therein if to the cure of soul you add that of body your task will be all the more difficult for the sick and the maim will profess any and every creed for the sake of healing and will laugh at you because you be simple enough to believe they as the day wear and the impetus of the morning die away there will come upon you an overwhelming sense of the uselessness of your toil this must be strive against and the only spur in your side will be the belief that you be play against the devil for the live soul it be great joyous belief but he who can hold it unwavere for four and twenty consecutive hour must be bless with an abundantly strong physique and equable nerve ask the gray head of the bannockburn medical crusade what manner of life their preacher lead speak to the racine gospel agency those lean americans whose boast be that they go where no englishman dare follow get pastor of the tubingen mission to talk of his experience if you can you will be refer to the print report but these contain no mention of the man who have lose youth and health all that man may lose except faith in the wild of english maiden who have go forth and die in the fever stricken jungle of the panth hill know from the first that death be almost certainty few pastor will tell you of these thing any more than they will speak of that young david of st bee who set apart for the lord ’s work break down in the utter desolation and return half distraught to the head mission cry there be no god but have walk with the devil the report be silent here because heroism failure doubt despair and self abnegation on the part of mere cultured white man be thing of no weight as compare to the saving of one half human soul from fantastic faith in wood spirit goblin of the rock and river fiend and gallio the assistant collector of the country side care for none of these thing he have be long in the district and the buria kol love he and bring he offering of spear fish orchid from the dim moist heart of the forest and as much game as he could eat in return he give they quinine and with athon dazé the high priest control their simple policy when you have be some year in the country say gallio at the krenk table you grow to find one creed as good as another ’ll give you all the assistance in my power of course but do not hurt my buria kol they be good people and they trust will they the word of the lord teach say justus his round face beam with enthusiasm and will assuredly to their prejudice no wrong hastily without think make
but my friend this in the mind impartiality of creed judgment be look be very bad heigh ho say gallio have their body and the district to see to
but you can try what you can do for their soul only do not behave as your predecessor do or afraid that can not guarantee your life and that say lotta sturdily hand he cup of tea he go up to the temple of dungara to be sure he be new to the country and begin hammer old dungara over the head with an umbrella so the buria kol turn out and hammer he rather savagely be in the district and he send runner to with note say persecute for the lord ’s sake send wing of regiment the near troop be about two hundred mile off but guess what he have be do ride to panth and talk to old athon dazé like father tell he that man of his wisdom ought to have know that the sahib have sunstroke and be mad you never see people more sorry in your life athon dazé apologise send wood and milk and fowl and all sort of thing and give five rupee to the shrine and tell macnamara that he have be injudicious he say that have bow down in the house of rimmon but if he have only just go over the brow of the hill and insult palin deo the idol of the suria kol he would have be impale on charred bamboo long before could have do anything and then should have have to have hang some of the poor brute be gentle with they padri
but do not think you ’ll do much not say justus but my master we will with the little child begin many of they will be sick that be so after the child the mother and then the man but would greatly that you be in internal sympathy with we prefer gallio depart to risk his life in mend the rotten bamboo bridge of his people in kill too persistent tiger here or there in sleep out in the reek jungle or in track the suria kol raider who have take few head from their brother of the buria clan he be knock kneed shamble young man naturally devoid of creed or reverence with longing for absolute power which his undesirable district gratify no one want my post he use to say grimly and my collector only poke his nose in when he quite certain that there be no fever
monarch of all survey and athon dazé be my viceroy because gallio pride himself on his supreme disregard of human life though he never extend the theory beyond his own he naturally ride forty mile to the mission with tiny brown girl baby on his saddle bow here be something for you padri say he the kol leave their surplus child to die do not see why they should not but you may rear this one pick it up beyond the berbulda fork ve notion that the mother have be follow through the wood ever since it be the first of the fold say justus and lotta catch up the scream morsel to her bosom and hush it craftily while as wolf hang in the field matui who have bear it and in accordance with the law of her tribe have expose it to die pant weary and footsore in the bamboo brake watch the house with hungry mother eye what would the omnipotent assistant collector do
would the little man in the black coat eat her daughter alive as athon dazé say be the custom of all man in black coat lotta know little of the tongue of the buria kol but when mother call to mother speech be easy to follow by the hand stretch timidly to the hem of her gown by the passionate guttural and the longing eye lotta understand with whom she have to deal
so matui take her child again would be servant even slave to this wonderful white woman for her own tribe would recognise she no more and lotta weep with she exhaustively after the german fashion which include much blow of the nose first the child then the mother and last the man and to the glory of god all say justus the hopeful and the man come with bow and arrow very angry indeed for there be no one to cook for he but the tale of the mission be long one and have no space to show how justus forgetful of his injudicious predecessor grievously smite moto the husband of matui for his brutality how moto be startled but be release from the fear of instant death take heart and become the faithful ally and first convert of justus how the little gathering grow to the huge disgust of athon dazé how the priest of the god of thing as they be argue subtilely with the priest of the god of thing as they should be and be bad how the due of the temple of dungara fall away in fowl and fish and honeycomb how lotta lighten the curse of eve among the woman and how justus do his good to introduce the curse of adam how the buria kol rebel at this say that their god be an idle god and how justus partially overcome their scruple against work and teach they that the black earth be rich in other produce than pig nut only all these thing belong to the history of many month and throughout those month the white haired athon dazé meditate revenge for the tribal neglect of dungara with savage cunning he feign friendship towards justus even hint at his own conversion but to the congregation of dungara he say darkly they of the padri ’s flock have put on clothe and worship busy god therefore dungara will afflict they grievously till they throw themselves howl into the water of the berbulda at night the red elephant tusk boom and groan among the hill and the faithful wake and say the god of thing as they be mature revenge against the back slider be merciful dungara to we thy child and give we all their crop late in the cold weather the collector and his wife come into the buria kol country go and look at krenk ’s mission say gallio he be do good work in his own way and think he ’d be pleased if you open the bamboo chapel that he have manage to run up at any rate you ’ll see civilised buria kol great be the stir in the mission now he and the gracious lady will that we have do good work with their own eye see
and yes we will he our convert in all their new clothe by their own hand construct exhibit it will great day be for the lord always say justus and lotta say amen justus have in his quiet way feel jealous of the basel weaving mission his own convert be unhandy but athon dazé have latterly induce some of they to hackle the glossy silky fibre of plant that grow plenteously on the panth hill it yield cloth white and smooth almost as the tappa of the south sea and that day the convert be to wear for the first time clothe make therefrom justus be proud of his work they shall in white clothe clothe to meet the collector and his well bear lady come down sing now thank we all our god
then he will the chapel open
and yes even gallio to believe will begin stand
so my child two by two and lotta why do they thus themselves bescratch it be not seemly to wriggle nala my child the collector will be here and be pain the collector his wife and gallio climb the hill to the mission station the convert be draw up in two line shin band nearly forty strong
hah say the collector whose acquisitive bent of mind lead he to believe that he have foster the institution from the first advance see by leap and bind never be true word speak the mission be advance exactly as he have say at first by little hop and shuffle of shamefaced uneasiness but soon by the leap of fly sting horse and the bound of madden kangaroo from the hill of panth the red elephant tusk deliver dry and anguished blare the rank of the convert waver break and scatter with yell and shriek of pain while justus and lotta stand horror stricken it be the judgment of dungara shout voice to the river or we die the mob wheel and head for the rock that overhang the berbulda writhe stamp twisting and shed its garment as it run pursue by the thunder of the trumpet of dungara justus and lotta flee to the collector almost in tear can not understand yesterday pant justus they have the ten commandment what be this praise the lord all good spirit by land and by sea oh shame with bind and scream there alight on the rock above their head nala once the pride of the mission maiden of fourteen summer good docile and virtuous now naked as the dawn and spit like wild cat be it for this she rave hurl her petticoat at justus be it for this leave my people and dungara for the fire of your bad place blind ape little earthworm dry fish that you be you say that should never burn dungara burn now burn now have mercy god of thing as they be she turn and fling herself into the berbulda and the trumpet of dungara bellow jubilantly the last of the convert of the tubingen mission have put quarter of mile of rapid river between herself and her teacher yesterday
gulp justus she teach in the school d.—oh it be the work of satan but gallio be curiously regard the maiden ’s petticoat where it have fall at his foot he feel its texture draw back his shirt sleeve beyond the deep tan of his wrist and press fold of the cloth against the flesh blotch of angry red rise on the white skin
ah say gallio calmly think so what be it say justus should call it the shirt of nessus but where do you get the fibre of this cloth from athon dazé say justus he show the boy how it should manufacture be the old fox do you know that he have give you the nilgiri nettle scorpion—_girardenia
heterophylla_—to work up no wonder they squirm why it sting even when they make bridge rope of it unless it ’s soak for six week the cunning brute it would take about half an hour to burn through their thick hide and then gallio burst into laughter but lotta be weep in the arm of the collector ’s wife and justus have cover his face with his hand girardenia heterophylla repeat gallio krenk why do not you tell could have save you this weave fire anybody but naked kol would have know it and if judge of their way you ’ll never get they back he look across the river to where the convert be still wallow and wail in the shallow and the laughter die out of his eye for he see that the tubingen mission to the buria kol be dead never again though they hang mournfully round the deserted school for three month could lotta or justus coax back even the most promising of their flock the end of conversion be the fire of the bad place fire that run through the limb and gnaw into the bone who dare second time tempt the anger of dungara let the little man and his wife go elsewhere the buria kol would have none of they an unofficial message to athon dazé that if hair of their head be touch athon dazé and the priest of dungara would be hang by gallio at the temple shrine protect justus and lotta from the stumpy poison arrow of the buria kol but neither fish nor fowl honeycomb salt nor young pig be bring to their door any more and alas man can not live by grace alone if meat be want let we go mine wife say justus there be no good here and the lord have will that some other man shall the work take in good time in his own good time we will go away and will yes some botany bestudy if any one be anxious to convert the buria kol afresh there lie at least the core of mission house under the hill of panth but the chapel and school have long since fall back into jungle the finance of the god copyright by macmillan co. the evening meal be end in dhunni bhagat ’s chubara and the old priest be smoke or count their bead little naked child patter in with its mouth wide open handful of marigold flower in one hand and lump of conserve tobacco in the other it try to kneel and make obeisance to gobind but it be so fat that it fall forward on its shaven head and roll on its side kicking and gasp while the marigold tumble one way and the tobacco the other gobind laugh set it up again and bless the marigold flower as he receive the tobacco from my father say the child he have the fever and can not come wilt thou pray for he father surely little but the smoke be on the ground and the night chill be in the air and it be not good to go abroad naked in the autumn have no clothe say the child and all to day have be carry cow dung cake to the bazar it be very hot and be very tired it shiver little for the twilight be cool gobind lift an arm under his vast tattered quilt of many colour and make an inviting little nest by his side the child creep in and gobind fill his brass stud leather water pipe with the new tobacco when come to the chubara the shaven head with the tuft atop and the beady black eye look out of the fold of the quilt as squirrel look out from his nest and gobind be smile while the child play with his beard would have say something friendly but remember in time that if the child fall ill afterwards should be credit with the evil eye and that be horrible possession sit thou still thumble say as it make to get up and run away where be thy slate and why have the teacher let such an evil character loose on the street when there be no police to protect we weakle in which ward dost thou try to break thy neck with fly kite from the house top nay sahib nay say the child burrow its face into gobind ’s beard and twist uneasily there be holiday to day among the school and do not always fly kite play ker li kit like the rest cricket be the national game among the school boy of the punjab from the naked hedge school child who use an old kerosene tin for wicket to the b. a. ’s of the university who compete for the championship belt thou play kerlikit thou art half the height of the bat the child nod resolutely yea do play perlay ball ow at run run run know it all but thou must not forget with all this to pray to the god accord to custom say gobind who do not altogether approve of cricket and western innovation do not forget say the child in hushed voice also to give reverence to thy teacher and”—gobind ’s voice softened—“to abstain from pull holy man by the beard little badling eh eh eh the child ’s face be altogether hide in the great white beard and it begin to whimper till gobind soothe it as child be soothe all the world over with the promise of story do not think to frighten thee senseless little one look up be angry aré aré aré shall weep too and of our tear make great pond and drown we both and then thy father will never get well lack thee to pull his beard peace peace and will tell thee of the god thou hast hear many tale very many father now this be new one which thou hast not hear long and long ago when the god walk with man as they do to day but that we have not faith to see shiv the great of god and parbati his wife be walk in the garden of temple which temple that in the nandgaon ward say the child nay very far away maybe at trimbak or hurdwar
whither thou must make pilgrimage when thou art man now there be sit in the garden under the jujube tree mendicant that have worship shiv for forty year and he live on the offering of the pious and meditate holiness night and day
oh father be it thou
say the child look up with large eye nay have say it be long ago
and moreover this mendicant be marry do they put he on horse with flower on his head and forbid he to go to sleep all night long thus they do to when they make my wedding say the child who have be marry few month before and what didst thou do say i. weep and they call evil name and then smite she
and we weep together thus do not the mendicant say gobind for he be holy man and very poor parbati perceive he sit naked by the temple step where all go up and down and she say to shiv what shall man think of the god when the god thus scorn their worshipper for forty year yonder man have pray to we and yet there be only few grain of rice and some break cowry before he after all man ’s heart will be harden by this thing and shiv say it shall be look to and so he call to the temple which be the temple of his son ganesh of the elephant head say son there be mendicant without who be very poor what wilt thou do for he then that great elephant head one awake in the dark and answer in three day if it be thy will he shall have one lakh of rupee then shiv and parbati go away but there be money lender in the garden hide among the marigolds”—the child look at the ball of crumple blossom in its hands—“ay among the yellow marigold and he hear the god talk he be covetous man and of black heart and he desire that lakh of rupee for himself so he go to the mendicant and say brother how much do the pious give thee daily the mendicant say can not tell sometimes little rice sometimes little pulse and few cowry and it have be pickle mango and dry fish that be good say the child smack its lip then say the money lender because have long watch thee and learn to love thee and thy patience will give thee now five rupee for all thy earning of the three day to come there be only bond to sign on the matter but the mendicant say thou art mad in two month do not receive the worth of five rupee and he tell the thing to his wife that evening she be woman say when do money lender ever make bad bargain the wolf run through the corn for the sake of the fat deer our fate be in the hand of the god pledge it not even for three day so the mendicant return to the money lender and would not sell then that wicked man sit all day before he offer more and more for those three day earn first ten fifty and hundred rupee and then for he do not know when the god would pour down their gift rupee by the thousand till he have offer half lakh of rupee upon this sum the mendicant ’s wife shift her counsel and the mendicant sign the bond and the money be pay in silver great white bullock bring it by the cart load but save only all that money the mendicant receive nothing from the god at all and the heart of the money lender be uneasy on account of expectation therefore at noon of the third day the money lender go into the temple to spy upon the council of the god and to learn in what manner that gift might arrive even as he be make his prayer crack between the stone of the floor gap and closing catch he by the heel then he hear the god walk in the temple in the darkness of the column and shiv call to his son ganesh say son what hast thou do in regard to the lakh of rupee for the mendicant and ganesh wake for the money lender hear the dry rustle of his trunk uncoiling and he answer father one half of the money have be pay and the debtor for the other half hold here fast by the heel the child bubble with laughter and the money lender pay the mendicant it say surely for he whom the god hold by the heel must pay to the uttermost the money be pay at evening all silver in great cart and thus ganesh do his work oh[=e nathu woman be call in the dusk by the door of the courtyard the child begin to wriggle that be my mother it say go then little answer gobind but stay moment he rip generous yard from his patchwork quilt put it over the child ’s shoulder and the child run away at howli thana his own shoe his own head.—_native proverb as messenger if the heart of the presence be move to so great favour and on six rupee yes sahib for have three little little child whose stomach be always empty and corn be now but forty pound to the rupee will make so clever messenger that you shall all day long be pleased with and at the end of the year bestow turban know all the road of the station and many other thing aha sahib be clever give service be aforetime in the police bad character now without doubt an enemy have tell this tale never be scamp be man of clean heart and all my word be true they know this when be in the police they say afzal khan be true speaker in whose word man may trust be delhi pathan sahib all delhi pathans be good man you have see delhi
yes it be true that there be many scamp among the delhi pathans how wise be the sahib nothing be hide from his eye and he will make his messenger and will take all his note secretly and without ostentation nay sahib god be my witness that mean no evil have long desire to serve under true sahib virtuous sahib many young sahib be as devil unchain with these sahib would take no service not though all the stomach of my little child be cry for bread why be not still in the police will speak true talk an evil come to the thana to ram baksh the havildar and maula baksh and juggut ram and bhim singh and suruj bul ram baksh be in the jail for space and so also be maula baksh it be at the thana of howli on the road that lead to gokral seetarun wherein be many dacoit we be all brave man rustum wherefore we be send to that thana which be eight mile from the next thana all day and all night we watch for dacoit why do the sahib laugh nay will make confession the dacoit be too clever and see this we make no further trouble it be in the hot weather what can man do in the hot day be the sahib who be so strong be he even vigorous in that hour we make an arrangement with the dacoit for the sake of peace that be the work of the havildar who be fat ho ho sahib he be now get thin in the jail among the carpet the havildar say give we no trouble and we will give you no trouble at the end of the reaping send we man to lead before the judge man of infirm mind against whom the trump up case will break down thus we shall save our honour to this talk the dacoit agree and we have no trouble at the thana and could eat melon in peace sit upon our charpoy all day long sweet as sugar cane be the melon of howli now there be an assistant commissioner stunt sahib in that district call yunkum sahib he be hard hard even as be the sahib who without doubt will give the shadow of his protection many eye have yunkum sahib and move quickly through his district man call he the tiger of gokral seetarun because he would arrive unannounced and make his kill and before sunset would be give trouble to the tehsildar thirty mile away no one know the come or the going of yunkum sahib he have no camp and when his horse be weary he ride upon devil carriage do not know its name but the sahib sit in the midst of three silver wheel that make no creaking and drave they with his leg prance like bean feed horse thus shadow of hawk upon the field be not more without noise than the devil carriage of yunkum sahib it be here it be there it be go and the rapport be make and there be trouble ask the tehsildar of rohestri how the hen steal come to be know sahib it fall upon night that we of the thana sleep accord to custom upon our charpoy having eat the evening meal and drunk tobacco when we awake in the morning behold of our six rifle not one remain also the big police book that be in the havildar ’s charge be go
see these thing we be very much afraid think on our part that the dacoit regardless of honour have come by night and put we to shame then say ram baksh the havildar be silent the business be an evil business
but it may yet go well let we make the case complete bring kid and my tulwar see you not now fool kick for horse but word be enough for
man we of the thana perceive quickly what be in the mind of the havildar and greatly fear that the service would be lose make haste to take the kid into the inner room and attend to the word of the havildar twenty dacoit come say the havildar
and we take his word repeat after he accord to custom there be great fight say the havildar and of we no man escape unhurt the bar of the window be break suruj bul see thou to that and man put speed into your work for runner must go with the news to the tiger of gokral seetarun thereon suruj bul lean with his shoulder brake in the bar of the window and beat she with whip make the havildar ’s mare skip among the melon bed till they be much tread with hoof print these thing be make return to the thana and the goat be slay and certain portion of the wall be blacken with fire and each man dip his clothe little into the blood of the goat know sahib that wound make by man upon his own body can by those skilled be easily discern from wound work by another man therefore the havildar take his tulwar smite one of we lightly on the forearm in the fat and another on the leg and third on the back of the hand thus deal he with all of we till the blood come and suruj bul more eager than the other take out much hair sahib never be so perfect an arrangement yea even would have swear that the thana have be treat as we say there be smoke and breaking and blood and trample earth ride now maula baksh say the havildar to the house of the stunt sahib and carry the news of the dacoity do you also afzal khan run there and take heed that you be mire with sweat and dust on your in come the blood will be dry on the clothe will stay and send straight report to the dipty sahib and we will catch certain that ye know of villager so that all may be ready against the dipty sahib ’s arrival thus maula baksh ride and run hang on the stirrup and together we come in an evil plight before the tiger of gokral seetarun in the rohestri tehsil our tale be long and correct sahib for we give even the name of the dacoit and the issue of the fight and beseech he to come
but the tiger make no sign and only smile after the manner of sahib when they have wickedness in their heart swear ye to the rapport say he
and we say thy servant swear the blood of the fight be but newly dry upon we judge thou if it be the blood of the servant of the presence or not
and he say see ye have do well
but he do not call for his horse or his devil carriage and scour the land as be his custom he say rest now and eat bread for ye be weary man will wait the coming of the dipty sahib now it be the order that the havildar of the thana should send straight report of all dacoity to the dipty sahib at noon come he fat man and an old and overbearing withal
but we of the thana have no fear of his anger dread more the silence of the tiger of gokral seetarun with he come ram baksh the havildar and the other guard ten man of the village of howli all man evil affect towards the police of the sirkar as prisoner they come the iron upon their hand cry for mercy imam baksh the farmer who have deny his wife to the havildar and other ill condition rascal against whom we of the thana bore spite it be well do and the havildar be proud but the dipty sahib be angry with the stunt for lack of zeal and say dam dam after the custom of the english people and extoll the havildar yunkum sahib lie still in his long chair have the man swear say yunkum sahib aye and capture ten evildoer say the dipty sahib there be more abroad in your charge take horse ride and go in the name of the sirkar truly there be more evildoer abroad say yunkum sahib but there be no need of horse come all man with see the mark of string on the temple of imam baksh do the presence know the torture of the cold draw see also the face of the tiger of gokral seetarun the evil smile be upon it and stand back ready for what might befall
well it be sahib that do this thing yunkum sahib unlock the door of his bathroom and smile anew within lie the six rifle and the big police book of the thana of howli he have come by night in the devil carriage that be noiseless as ghoul and move among we asleep have take away both the gun and the book twice have he come to the thana take each time three rifle the liver of the havildar be turn to water and he fall scrabble in the dirt about the boot of yunkum sahib crying—“have mercy sahib be delhi pathan and young man with little child the havildar ’s mare be in the compound run to she and ride the black wrath of the sirkar be behind and know not whither to go till she drop and die ride the red mare and by the blessing of god who be without doubt on the side of all just man escape but the havildar and the rest be now in jail be scamp it be as the presence please god will make the presence lord and give he rich memsahib as fair as peri to wife and many strong son if he make his orderly the mercy of heaven be upon the sahib yes will only go to the bazar and bring my child to these so palace like quarter and then the presence be my father and my mother and afzal khan be his slave ohe sirdar ji also be of the household of the sahib in flood time tweed say tae till what gars ye rin sae still till say tae tweed though ye rin wi speed an rin slaw yet where ye droon ae man droon twa there be no get over the river to night sahib they say that bullock cart have be wash down already and the ekka that go over half hour before you come have not yet reach the far side be the sahib in haste will drive the ford elephant in to show he ohe mahout there in the shed bring out ram pershad and if he will face the current good an elephant never lie sahib and ram pershad be separate from his friend kala nag he too wish to cross to the far side well do well do my king go half way across mahoutji and see what the river say well do ram pershad pearl among elephant go into the river hit he on the head fool be the goad make only to scratch thy own fat back with bastard what be the boulder to thee ram pershad my rustum my mountain of strength go in go in no sahib it be useless you can hear he trumpet he be tell kala nag that he can not come over he have swing round and be shake his head he be no fool he know what the barhwi mean when it be angry indeed thou art no fool my child salaam ram pershad bahadur take he under the tree mahout and see that he get his spice well do thou chiefest among tusker salaam to the sirkar and go to sleep what be to be do the sahib must wait till the river go down it will shrink to morrow morning if god please or the day after at the late now why do the sahib get so angry be his servant before god do not create this stream what can do my hut and all that be therein be at the service of the sahib and it be begin to rain come away my lord how will the river go down for your throwing abuse at it in the old day the english people be not thus the fire carriage have make they soft in the old day when they drave behind horse by day or by night they say naught if river bar the way or carriage sit down in the mud it be the will of god not like fire carriage which go and go and go and would go though all the devil in the land hang on to its tail the fire carriage hath spoil the english people after all what be day lose or for that matter what be two day be the sahib go to his own wedding that he be so mad with haste be an old man and see few sahib forgive if have forget the respect that be due to they the sahib be not angry his own wedding ho ho the mind of an old man be like the numah_-tree fruit bud blossom and the dead leaf of all the year of the past flourish together old and new and that which be go out of remembrance all three be there sit on the bedstead sahib and drink milk or would the sahib in truth care to drink my tobacco it be good it be the tobacco of nuklao my son who be in service there send it to drink then sahib if you know how to handle the tube the sahib take it like musalman where do he learn that his own wedding ho ho the sahib say that there be no wedding in the matter at all now be it likely that the sahib would speak true talk to who be only black man small wonder then that he be in haste thirty year have beat the gong at this ford but never have see sahib in such haste thirty year sahib that be very long time thirty year ago this ford be on the track of the bunjara and have see two thousand pack bullock cross in one night now the rail have come and the fire carriage say buz buz buz and hundred lakh of maund slide across that big bridge it be very wonderful but the ford be lonely now that there be no bunjara to camp under the tree nay do not trouble to look at the sky without it will rain till the dawn the boulder be talk to night in the bed of the river hear they they would be husk your bone sahib have you try to cross see will shut the door and no rain can enter wahi ahi ugh thirty year on the bank of the ford an old man be and where be the oil for the lamp your pardon but because of my year sleep no sounder than dog and you move to the door look then sahib look and listen full half kos from bank to bank be the stream now you can see it under the star and there be ten foot of water therein it will not shrink because of the anger in your eye and it will not be quiet on account of your curse which be loud sahib your voice or the voice of the river call to it perhaps it will be ashamed lie down and sleep afresh sahib know the anger of the barhwi when there have fall rain in the foot hill swim the flood once on night ten fold bad than this and by the favour of god be release from death when have come to the very gate thereof may tell the tale very good talk will fill the pipe anew thirty year ago it be when be young man and have but newly come to the ford be strong then and the bunjara have no doubt when say this ford be clear have toil all night up to my shoulder blade in run water amid hundred bullock mad with fear and have bre they across lose not hoof when all be do fetch the shiver man and they give for reward the pick of their cattle the bell bullock of the drive so great be the honour in which be hold but to day when the rain fall and the river rise creep into my hut and whimper like dog my strength be go from be an old man and the fire carriage have make the ford desolate they be will not to call the strong one of the barhwi behold my face sahib it be the face of monkey and my arm it be the arm of an old woman swear to you sahib that woman have love this face and have rest in the hollow of this arm twenty year ago sahib believe this be true talk twenty year ago come to the door and look across can you see thin fire very far away down the stream that be the temple fire in the shrine of hanuman of the village of pateera north under the big star be the village itself but it be hide by bend of the river be that far to swim sahib would you take off your clothe and adventure yet swim to pateera not once but many time and there be mugger in the river too love know no caste else why should musalman and the son of musalman have seek hindu woman widow of the hindus the sister of the headman of pateera
but it be even so they of the headman ’s household come on pilgrimage to muttra when she be but newly bride silver tire be upon the wheel of the bullock cart and silken curtain hide the woman sahib make no haste in their conveyance for the wind part the curtain and see she when they return from pilgrimage the boy that be her husband have die and see she again in the bullock cart by god these hindus be fool what be it to whether she be hindu or jain scavenger leper or whole would have marry she and make she home by the ford the seventh of the nine bar say that man may not marry one of the idolater be that truth both shiah and sunni say that musalman may not marry one of the idolater be the sahib priest then that he know so much will tell he something that he do not know there be neither shiah nor sunni forbidden nor idolater in love and the nine bar be but nine little fagot that the flame of love utterly burn away in truth would have take she
but what could do the headman would have send his man to break my head with stave be not be not afraid of any five man but against half village who can prevail therefore it be my custom these thing having be arrange between us twain to go by night to the village of pateera and there we meet among the crop no man know aught of the matter behold now be will not to cross here skirt the jungle to the river bend where the railway bridge be and thence across the elbow of land to pateera the light of the shrine be my guide when the night be dark that jungle near the river be very full of snake little karait that sleep on the sand and moreover her brother would have slay have they find in the crop but none know none know save she and and the blown sand of the river bed cover the track of my foot in the hot month it be an easy thing to pass from the ford to pateera and in the first rain when the river rise slowly it be an easy thing also set the strength of my body against the strength of the stream and nightly eat in my hut here and drink at pateera yonder she have say that one hirnam singh thief have seek she and he be of village up the river but on the same bank all sikhs be dog and they have refuse in their folly that good gift of god tobacco be ready to destroy hirnam singh that ever he have come nigh she and the more because he have swear to she that she have lover and that he would lie in wait and give the name to the headman unless she go away with he what cur be these sikhs after that news swim always with little sharp knife in my belt and evil would it have be for man have he stay know not the face of hirnam singh but would have kill any who come between and she upon night in the beginning of the rain be minded to go across to pateera albeit the river be angry now the nature of the barhwi be this sahib in twenty breath it come down from the hill wall three foot high and have see it between the lighting of fire and the cooking of chupatty grow from runnel to sister of the jumna when leave this bank there be shoal half mile down and make shift to fetch it and draw breath there ere go forward for feel the hand of the river heavy upon my heel yet what will young man not do for love ’s sake there be but little light from the star and midway to the shoal branch of the stinking deodar tree brush my mouth as swim that be sign of heavy rain in the foot hill and beyond for the deodar be strong tree not easily shake from the hillside make haste the river aid but ere have touch the shoal the pulse of the stream beat as it be within and around and behold the shoal be go and ride high on the crest of wave that run from bank to bank have the sahib ever be cast into much water that fight and will not let man use his limb to my head upon the water it seem as though there be naught but water to the world ’s end and the river drave with its driftwood man be very little thing in the belly of flood and this flood though know it not be the great flood about which man talk still my liver be dissolve and lie like log upon my back in the fear of death there be live thing in the water cry and howl grievously beast of the forest and cattle and once the voice of man ask for help but the rain come and lash the water white and hear no more save the roar of the boulder below and the roar of the rain above thus be whirl downstream wrestle for the breath in it be very hard to die when one be young can the sahib stand here see the railway bridge look there be the light of the mail train go to peshawur the bridge be now twenty foot above the river but upon that night the water be roar against the lattice work and against the lattice come foot first but much driftwood be pile there and upon the pier and take no great hurt only the river press as strong man press weak scarcely could take hold of the lattice work and crawl to the upper boom sahib the water be foam across the rail foot deep judge therefore what manner of flood it must have be could not hear could not see could but lie on the boom and pant for breath after while the rain cease and there come out in the sky certain new wash star and by their light see that there be no end to the black water as far as the eye could travel and the water have rise upon the rail there be dead beast in the driftwood on the pier and other catch by the neck in the lattice work and other not yet drown who strove to find foothold on the lattice work buffalo and kine and wild pig and deer one or two and snake and jackal past all count their body be black upon the left side of the bridge but the small of they be force through the lattice work and whirl down stream thereafter the star die and the rain come down afresh and the river rise yet more and feel the bridge begin to stir under as man stir in his sleep
ere he wake but be not afraid sahib swear to you that be not afraid though have no power in my limb know that should not die till have see she once more but be very cold and feel that the bridge must go there be tremble in the water such trembling as go before the coming of great wave and the bridge lift its flank to the rush of that come so that the right lattice dip under water and the left rise clear on my beard sahib be speak god ’s truth as mirzapore stone boat careen to the wind so the barhwi bridge turn thus and in no other manner slide from the boom into deep water and behind come the wave of the wrath of the river hear its voice and the scream of the middle part of the bridge as it move from the pier and sink and know no more till rise in the middle of the great flood put forth my hand to swim and lo it fall upon the knotted hair of the head of man he be dead for no one but the strong one of barhwi could have live in that race he have be dead full two day for he ride high wallowing and be an aid to laugh then know for surety that should yet see she and take no harm and twist my finger in the hair of the man for be far spend and together we go down the stream he the dead and the living lack that help should have sink the cold be in my marrow and my flesh be rib and sodden on my bone
but he have no fear who have know the uttermost of the power of the river and let he go where he choose at last we come into the power of side current that set to the right bank and strove with my foot to draw with it
but the dead man swing heavily in the whirl and fear that some branch have strike he and that he would sink the top of the tamarisk brush my knee
so know we be come into flood water above the crop and after let down my leg and feel bottom the ridge of field and after the dead man stay upon knoll under fig tree and draw my body from the water rejoice do the sahib know whither the backwash of the flood have bear to the knoll which be the eastern boundary mark of the village of pateera no other place draw the dead man up on the grass for the service that he have do and also because know not whether should need he again then go cry thrice like jackal to the appoint place which be near the byre of the headman ’s house
but my love be already there weep she fear that the flood have sweep my hut at the barhwi ford when come softly through the ankle deep water she think it be ghost and would have flee but put my arm round she and be no ghost in those day though be an old man now ho ho dry corn in truth maize without juice ho ho![1 footnote grieve to say that the warden of barhwi ford be responsible here for two very bad pun in the vernacular.—_r
k. tell she the story of the breaking of the barhwi bridge
and she say that be great than mortal man for none may cross the barhwi in full flood and have see what never man have see before hand in hand we go to the knoll where the dead lay and show she by what help have make the ford she look also upon the body under the star for the latter end of the night be clear and hide her face in her hand cry it be the body of hirnam singh say the swine be of more use dead than live my beloved
and she say surely for he have save the dear life in the world to my love none the less he can not stay here for that would bring shame upon the body be not gunshot from her door then say roll the body with my hand god hath judge between we hirnam singh that thy blood might not be upon my head now whether have do thee wrong in keep thee from the burn ghat do thou and the crow settle together so cast he adrift into the flood water and he be draw out to the open ever wag his thick black beard like priest under the pulpit board and see no more of hirnam singh before the breaking of the day we two part and move towards such of the jungle as be not flood with the full light see what have do in the darkness and the bone of my body be loosen in my flesh for there run two kos of rag water between the village of pateera and the tree of the far bank and in the middle the pier of the barhwi bridge show like broken tooth in the jaw of an old man nor be there any life upon the water neither bird nor boat but only an army of drown thing bullock and horse and man and the river be red than blood from the clay of the foot hill never have see such flood never since that year have see the like and sahib no man live have do what have do there be no return for that day not for all the land of the headman would venture second time without the shield of darkness that cloak danger go kos up the river to the house of blacksmith say that the flood have sweep from my hut and they give food seven day stay with the blacksmith till boat come and return to my house there be no trace of wall or roof or floor naught but patch of slimy mud judge therefore sahib how far the river must have rise it be write that should not die either in my house or in the heart of the barhwi or under the wreck of the barhwi bridge for god send down hirnam singh two day dead though know not how the man die to be my buoy and support hirnam singh have be in hell these twenty year and the thought of that night must be the flower of his torment listen sahib the river have change its voice it be go to sleep before the dawn to which there be yet one hour with the light it will come down afresh how do know have be here thirty year without know the voice of the river as father know the voice of his son every moment it be talk less angrily swear that there will be no danger for one hour or perhaps two can not answer for the morning be quick sahib will call ram pershad and he will not turn back this time be the paulin tightly corded upon all the baggage ohe mahout with mud head the elephant for the sahib and tell they on the far side that there will be no crossing after daylight money nay sahib be not of that kind
no not even to give sweetmeat to the baby folk my house look you be empty and be an old man dutt ram pershad good luck go with you
sahib moti guj mutineer once upon time there be coffee planter in india who wish to clear some forest land for coffee planting when he have cut down all the tree and burn the under wood the stump still remain dynamite be expensive and slow fire slow the happy medium for stump clearing be the lord of all beast who be the elephant he will either push the stump out of the ground with his tusk if he have any or drag it out with rope the planter therefore hire elephant by one and two and three and fall to work the very good of all the elephant belong to the very bad of all the driver or mahout and the superior beast ’s name be moti guj he be the absolute property of his mahout which would never have be the case under native rule for moti guj be creature to be desire by king and his name be translate mean the pearl elephant because the british government be in the land deesa the mahout enjoy his property undisturbed he be dissipate when he have make much money through the strength of his elephant he would get extremely drunk and give moti guj beat with tent peg over the tender nail of the forefoot moti guj never trample the life out of deesa on these occasion for he know that after the beating be over deesa would embrace his trunk and weep and call he his love and his life and the liver of his soul and give he some liquor moti guj be very fond of liquor arrack for choice though he would drink palm tree toddy if nothing well offer then deesa would go to sleep between moti guj ’s forefoot and as deesa generally choose the middle of the public road and as moti guj mount guard over he and would not permit horse foot or cart to pass by traffic be congest till deesa see fit to wake up there be no sleeping in the daytime on the planter ’s clear the wage be too high to risk deesa sit on moti guj ’s neck and give he order while moti guj root up the stump for he own magnificent pair of tusk or pull at the end of rope for he have magnificent pair of shoulder while deesa kick he behind the ear and say he be the king of elephant at evening time moti guj would wash down his three hundred pound weight of green food with quart of arrack and deesa would take share and sing song between moti guj ’s leg till it be time to go to bed once week deesa lead moti guj down to the river and moti guj lie on his side luxuriously in the shallow while deesa go over he with coir swab and brick moti guj never mistake the pounding blow of the latter for the smack of the former that warn he to get up and turn over on the other side then deesa would look at his foot and examine his eye and turn up the fringe of his mighty ear in case of sore or bud ophthalmia after inspection the two would come up with song from the sea moti guj all black and shin wave tear tree branch twelve foot long in his trunk and deesa knot up his own long wet hair it be peaceful well pay life till deesa feel the return of the desire to drink deep he wish for an orgie the little draught that lead nowhere be take the manhood out of he he go to the planter and my mother ’s dead say he weep she die on the last plantation two month ago and she die once before that when you be work for last year say the planter who know something of the way of nativedom then it my aunt and she be just the same as mother to say deesa weep more than ever she have leave eighteen small child entirely without bread and it be who must fill their little stomach say deesa beat his head on the floor who bring you the news say the planter the post say deesa there have not be post here for the past week get back to your line devastating sickness have fall on my village and all my wife be die yell deesa really in tear this time call chihun who come from deesa ’s village say the planter chihun have this man wife he say chihun no not woman of our village would look at he they ’d soon marry the elephant chihun snort deesa weep and bellow you will get into difficulty in minute say the planter go back to your work now will speak heaven ’s truth gulp deesa with an inspiration have not be drunk for two month desire to depart in order to get properly drunk afar off and distant from this heavenly plantation thus shall cause no trouble flicker smile cross the planter ’s face deesa say he you ve speak the truth and ’d give you leave on the spot if anything could be do with moti guj while you ’re away you know that he will only obey your order may the light of the heavens live forty thousand year shall be absent but ten little day after that upon my faith and honour and soul return as to the inconsiderable interval have the gracious permission of the heaven bear to call up moti guj permission be grant and
in answer to deesa ’s shrill yell the lordly tusker swe out of the shade of clump of tree where he have be squirt dust over himself till his master should return light of my heart protector of the drunken mountain of might give ear say deesa stand in front of he moti guj give ear and salute with his trunk be go away say deesa moti guj ’s eye twinkle he like jaunt as well as his master one could snatch all manner of nice thing from the roadside then
but you you fubsy old pig must stay behind and work the twinkle die out as moti guj try to look delighted he hat
stump haul on the plantation it hurt his tooth shall be go for ten day delectable one hold up your near forefoot and ’ll impress the fact upon it warty toad of dry mud puddle deesa take tent peg and bang moti guj ten time on the nail moti guj grunt and shuffle from foot to foot ten day
say deesa you must work and haul and root tree as chihun here shall order you take up chihun and set he on your neck moti guj curl the tip of his trunk chihun put his foot there and be swe on to the neck deesa hand chihun the heavy ankus the iron elephant goad chihun thump moti guj ’s bald head as paviour thump kerbstone moti guj trumpet be still hog of the backwood chihun ’s your mahout for ten day and now bid good bye beast after mine own heart
oh my lord my king jewel of all create elephant lily of the herd preserve your honour health be virtuous adieu moti guj lap his trunk round deesa and swing he into the air twice that be his way of bid the man good
bye he ’ll work now say deesa to the planter have leave to go the planter nod and deesa dive into the wood moti guj go back to haul stump chihun be very kind to he
but he feel unhappy and forlorn notwithstanding chihun give he ball of spice and tickle he under the chin and chihun ’s little baby coo to he after work be over and chihun ’s wife call he darling but moti guj be bachelor by instinct as deesa be he do not understand the domestic emotion he want the light of his universe back again the drink and the drunken slumber the savage beating and the savage caress none the less he work well and the planter wonder deesa have vagabond along the road till he meet marriage procession of his own caste and drinking dancing and tippling have drift past all knowledge of the lapse of time the morning of the eleventh day dawn and there return no deesa moti guj be loose from his rope for the daily stint he swing clear look round shrug his shoulder and begin to walk away as one have business
elsewhere hi ho come back you shout chihun come back and put on your neck misborn mountain return splendour of the hillside adornment of all india heave to or ’ll bang every toe off your fat forefoot moti guj gurgle gently but do not obey chihun run after he with rope and catch he up moti guj put his ear forward and chihun know what that mean though he try to carry it off with high word none of your nonsense with say he to your picket devil son hrrump say moti guj and that be all that and the forebent ear moti guj put his hand in his pocket chew branch for toothpick and stroll about the clearing make jest of the other elephant who have just set to work chihun report the state of affair to the planter who come out with dog whip and crack it furiously moti guj pay the white man the compliment of charge he nearly quarter of mile across the clearing and hrrumpe he into the verandah then he stand outside the house chuckle to himself and shake all over with the fun of it as an elephant will we ’ll thrash he say the planter he shall have the fine thrashing that ever elephant receive give kala nag and nazim twelve foot of chain apiece and tell they to lay on twenty blow kala nag which mean black snake and nazim be two of the big elephant in the line and one of their duty be to administer the grave punishment since no man can beat an elephant properly they take the whipping chain and rattle they in their trunk as they sidle up to moti guj mean to hustle he between they moti guj have never in all his life of thirty nine year be whip and he do not intend to open new experience so he wait weave his head from right to left and measure the precise spot in kala nag ’s fat side where blunt tusk would sink deep kala nag have no tusk the chain be his badge of authority but he judge it good to swing wide of moti guj at the last minute and seem to appear as if he have bre out the chain for amusement nazim turn round and go home early he do not feel fight fit that morning and so moti guj be leave stand alone with his ear cocked that decide the planter to argue no more and moti guj roll back to his inspection of the clearing an elephant who will not work and be not tie up be not quite so manageable as an eighty one ton gun loose in heavy sea way he slap old friend on the back and ask they if the stump be come away easily he talk nonsense concern labour and the inalienable right of elephant to long nooning and wander to and fro thoroughly demoralize the garden till sundown when he return to his picket for food if you will not work you sha’n’t eat say chihun angrily you ’re wild elephant and no educate animal at all go back to your jungle chihun ’s little brown baby roll on the floor of the hut stretch its fat arm to the huge shadow in the doorway moti guj know well that it be the dear thing on earth to chihun he swing out his trunk with fascinating crook at the end and the brown baby throw itself shout upon it moti guj make fast and pull up till the brown baby be crow in the air twelve foot above his father ’s head great chief say chihun flour cake of the good twelve in number two foot across and soak in rum shall be yours on the instant and two hundred pound weight of fresh cut young sugar cane therewith deign only to put down safely that insignificant brat who be my heart and my life to moti guj tuck the brown baby comfortably between his forefoot that could have knock into toothpick all chihun ’s hut and wait for his food he eat it and the brown baby crawl away moti guj doze and think of deesa one of many mystery connect with the elephant be that his huge body need less sleep than anything else that live four or five hour in the night suffice two just before midnight lie down on one side two just after one o’clock lie down on the other the rest of the silent hour be fill with eat and fidget and long grumble soliloquy at midnight therefore moti guj stride out of his picket for thought have come to he that deesa might be lie drunk somewhere in the dark forest with none to look after he so all that night he chase through the undergrowth blow and trumpet and shake his ear he go down to the river and blare across the shallow where deesa use to wash he but there be no answer he could not find deesa but he disturb all the elephant in the line and nearly frightened to death some gypsy in the wood at dawn deesa return to the plantation he have be very drunk indeed and he expect to fall into trouble for outstay his leave he draw long breath when he see that the bungalow and the plantation be still uninjured for he know something of moti guj ’s temper and report himself with many lie and salaam moti guj have go to his picket for breakfast his night exercise have make he hungry call up your beast say the planter and deesa shout in the mysterious elephant language that some mahout believe come from china at the birth of the world when elephant and not man be master moti guj hear and come elephant do not gallop they move from spot at vary rate of speed if an elephant wish to catch an express train he could not gallop
but he could catch the train thus moti guj be at the planter ’s door almost before chihun notice that he have leave his picket he fall into deesa ’s arm trumpet with joy and the man and beast weep and slobber over each other and handle each other from head to heel to see that no harm have befall now we will get to work say deesa lift up my son and my joy moti guj swing he up and the two go to the coffee clearing to look for irksome stump the planter be too astonished to be very angry without benefit of clergy before my spring garner autumn ’s gain out of her time my field be white with grain the year give up her secret to my woe force and deflower each sick season lie in mystery of increase and decay see the sunset ere man see the day who be too wise in that should not know bitter water
but if it be girl lord of my life it can not be have pray for so many night and send gift to sheikh badl ’s shrine so often that know god will give we son man child that shall grow into man think of this and be glad my mother shall be his mother till can take he again and the mullah of the pattan mosque shall cast his nativity god send he be bear in an auspicious hour!—and then and then thou wilt never weary of thy slave since when hast thou be slave my queen since the beginning till this mercy come to how could be sure of thy love when know that have be buy with silver nay that be the dowry pay it to thy mother and she have bury it and sit upon it all day long like hen what talk be your of dower be buy as though have be lucknow dancing girl instead of child art thou sorry for the sale have sorrow but to day be glad thou wilt never cease to love now?—answer my king never never no not even though the mem log_—the white woman of thy own blood love thee and remember have watch they drive in the evening they be very fair have see fire balloon by the hundred have see the moon and then see no more fire balloon ameera clap her hand and laugh very good talk
she say then with an assumption of great stateliness it be enough thou hast my permission to depart,—if thou wilt the man do not move he be sit on low red lacquer couch in room furnish only with blue and white floor cloth some rug and very complete collection of native cushion at his foot sit woman of sixteen and she be all but all the world in his eye by every rule and law she should have be otherwise for he be an englishman
and she mussulman ’s daughter buy two year before from her mother who be leave without money would have sell ameera shriek to the prince of darkness if the price have be sufficient it be contract enter into with light heart but even before the girl have reach her bloom she come to fill the great portion of john holden ’s life for she and the withered hag her mother he have take little house overlook the great red walled city and found,—when the marigold have spring up by the well in the courtyard and ameera have establish herself accord to her own idea of comfort and her mother have cease grumble at the inadequacy of the cooking place the distance from the daily market and at matter of housekeeping in general,—that the house be to he his home any one could enter his bachelor ’s bungalow by day or night and the life that he lead there be an unlovely one in the house in the city his foot only could pass beyond the outer courtyard to the woman ’s room and when the big wooden gate be bolt behind he
he be king in his own territory with ameera for queen and there be go to be add to this kingdom third person whose arrival holden feel inclined to resent it interfere with his perfect happiness it disarrange the orderly peace of the house that be his own but ameera be wild with delight at the thought of it and her mother not less so the love of man and particularly white man be at the good an inconstant affair but it might both woman argue be hold fast by baby ’s hand and then ameera would always say then he will never care for the white mem log hate they all hate they all he will go back to his own people in time say the mother but by the blessing of god that time be yet afar off holden sit silent on the couch thinking of the future and his thought be not pleasant the drawback of double life be manifold the government with singular care have order he out of the station for fortnight on special duty in the place of man who be watch by the bedside of sick wife the verbal notification of the transfer have be edge by cheerful remark that holden ought to think himself lucky in be bachelor and free man he come to break the news to ameera it be not good she say slowly but it be not all bad there be my mother here and no harm will come to unless indeed die of pure joy go thou to thy work and think no troublesome thought when the day be do believe nay be sure and and then shall lay he in thy arm and thou wilt love for ever the train go to night at midnight be it not go now and do not let thy heart be heavy by cause of but thou wilt not delay in return thou wilt not stay on the road to talk to the bold white mem log come back to swiftly my life as he leave the courtyard to reach his horse that be tether to the gate post holden speak to the white haired old watchman who guard the house and bid he under certain contingency despatch the fill up telegraph form that holden give he it be all that could be do and with the sensation of man who have attend his own funeral holden go away by the night mail to his exile every hour of the day he dread the arrival of the telegram and every hour of the night he picture to himself the death of ameera in consequence his work for the state be not of first rate quality nor be his temper towards his colleague of the most amiable the fortnight end without sign from his home and tear to piece by his anxiety holden return to be swallow up for two precious hour by dinner at the club wherein he hear as man hear in swoon voice tell he how execrably he have perform the other man ’s duty and how he have endear himself to all his associate then he flee on horseback through the night with his heart in his mouth there be no answer at first to his blow on the gate and he have just wheel his horse round to kick it in when pir khan appear with lantern and hold his stirrup have aught occur say holden the news do not come from my mouth protector of the poor but he hold out his shake hand as befit the bearer of good news who be entitle to reward holden hurry through the courtyard light burn in the upper room his horse neigh in the gateway and he hear shrill little wail that send all the blood into the apple of his throat it be new voice but it do not prove that ameera be alive who be there he call up the narrow brick staircase there be cry of delight from ameera and then the voice of the mother tremulous with old age and pride—“we be two woman and the man thy son on the threshold of the room holden step on naked dagger that be lie there to avert ill luck and it break at the hilt under his impatient heel god be great coo ameera in the half light thou hast take his misfortune on thy head
but how be it with thee life of my life old woman how be it with she she have forget she suffering for joy that the child be bear there be no harm but speak softly say the mother it only need thy presence to make all well say ameera my king thou hast be very long away what gift hast thou for ah ah it be that bring gift this time look my life look be there ever such babe nay be too weak even to clear my arm from he rest then and do not talk be here bachari little woman well say for there be bond and heel rope peecharee between we now that nothing can break look canst thou see in this light he be without spot or blemish never be such man child ya illah he shall be pundit no trooper of the queen and my life dost thou love as well as ever though be faint and sick and wear answer truly love as have love with all my soul lie still pearl and rest then do not go sit by my side here so mother the lord of this house need cushion bring it there be an almost imperceptible movement on the part of the new life that lie in the hollow of ameera ’s arm she say her voice break with love the babe be champion from his birth he be kick in the side with mighty kick be there ever such babe and he be ours to we thine and mine put thy hand on his head but carefully for he be very young and man be unskilled in such matter very cautiously holden touch with the tip of his finger the downy head he be of the faith say ameera for lie here in the night watch whisper the call to prayer and the profession of faith into his ear
and it be most marvellous that he be bear upon friday as be bear be careful of he my life
but he can almost grip with his hand holden find one helpless little hand that close feebly on his finger and the clutch run through his body till it settle about his heart till then his sole thought have be for ameera he begin to realise that there be some one else in the world
but he could not feel that it be veritable son with soul he sit down to think and ameera doze lightly get hence sahib say her mother under her breath it be not good that she should find you here on wake she must be still go say holden submissively here be rupee see that my baba get fat and find all that he need the chink of the silver rouse ameera be his mother and no hireling she say weakly shall look to he more or less for the sake of money mother give it back have bear my lord son the deep sleep of weakness come upon she almost before the sentence be complete holden go down to the courtyard very softly with his heart at ease pir khan the old watchman be chuckle with delight this house be now complete he say and without further comment thrust into holden ’s hand the hilt of sabre wear many year ago when he pir khan serve the queen in the police the bleat of tether goat come from the well kerb there be two say pir khan two goat of the good buy they and they cost much money and since there be no birth party assemble their flesh will be all mine strike craftily sahib tis an ill balance sabre at the good wait till they raise their head from crop the marigold and why say holden bewilder for the birth sacrifice what else otherwise the child be unguarde from fate may die the protector of the poor know the fitting word to be say holden have learn they once with little thought that he would ever speak they in earnest the touch of the cold sabre hilt in his palm turn suddenly to the cling grip of the child up stair the child that be his own son and dread of loss fill he strike say pir khan never life come into the world but life be pay for it see the goat have raise their head with draw cut hardly know what he do holden cut twice as he mutter the mahomedan prayer that run almighty in place of this my son offer life for life blood for blood head for head bone for bone hair for hair skin for skin the wait horse snort and bind in his picket at the smell of the raw blood that spurt over holden ’s rid boot well smite say pir khan wipe the sabre swordsman be lose in thee go with light heart heaven bear be thy servant and the servant of thy son may the presence live thousand year and the flesh of the goat be all mine pir khan draw back rich by month ’s pay holden swing himself into the saddle and ride off through the low hang wood smoke of the evening he be full of riotous exultation alternate with vast vague tenderness direct towards no particular object that make he choke as he bend over the neck of his uneasy horse never feel like this in my life he think ’ll go to the club and pull myself together game of pool be begin and the room be full of man holden enter eager to get to the light and the company of his fellow singing at the top of his voice in baltimore walk lady do meet do you say the club secretary from his corner do she happen to tell you that your boot be wre wet great goodness man it blood bosh say holden pick his cue from the rack may cut in it dew ve be rid through high crop my faith my boot be in mess though and if it be girl she shall wear wedding ring and if it be boy he shall fight for his king with come also he have never yet see the sky ameera climb the narrow staircase that lead to the flat roof the child
placid and unwinking lie in the hollow of her right arm gorgeous in silver fringed muslin with small skull cap on his head ameera wear all that she value most the diamond nose stud that take the place of the western patch in draw attention to the curve of the nostril the gold ornament in the centre of the forehead stud with tallow drop emerald and flawed ruby the heavy circlet of beat gold that be fasten round her neck by the softness of the pure metal and the chinking curb pattern silver anklet hang low over the rosy ankle bone she be dress in jade green muslin as befit daughter of the faith and from shoulder to elbow and elbow to wrist run bracelet of silver tie with floss silk frail glass bangle slip over the wrist in proof of the slenderness of the hand and certain heavy gold bracelet that have no part in her country ’s ornament but since they be holden ’s gift and fasten with cunning european snap delight she immensely they sit down by the low white parapet of the roof overlook the city and its light they be happy down there say ameera but do not think that they be as happy as we nor do think the white mem log be as happy and thou know they be not how dost thou know they give their child over to the nurse have never see that say ameera with sigh nor do wish to see ahi!_”—she drop her head on holden ’s shoulder—“i have count forty star and be tired look at the child love of my life he be count too the baby be star with round eye at the dark of the heaven ameera place

but my life what little name shall we give he the small body lie close to holden ’s heart it be utterly helpless and very soft he scarcely dare to breathe for fear of crush it the cage green parrot that be regard as sort of guardian spirit in most native household move on its perch and flutter drowsy wing there be the answer say holden mian mittu have speak he shall be the parrot when he be ready he will talk mightily and run about mian mittu be the parrot in thy in the mussulman tongue be it not why put so far off say ameera fretfully let it be like unto some english name but not wholly for he be mine then call he tota for that be like english ay tota and that be still the parrot forgive my lord for minute ago but in truth he be too little to wear all the weight of mian mittu for name he shall be tota our tota to we hearest thou small one littlest thou art tota she touch the child ’s cheek and he wake wail
and it be necessary to return he to his mother who soothe he with the wonderful rhyme of aré koko jaré koko which say oh crow go crow baby ’s sleep sound and the wild plum grow in the jungle only penny pound only penny pound baba only penny pound reassure many time as to the price of those plum tota cuddle himself down to sleep the two sleek white well bullock in the courtyard be steadily chew the cud of their evening meal old pir khan squat at the head of holden ’s horse his police sabre across his knee pull drowsily at big water pipe that croak like bull frog in pond ameera ’s mother sit spin in the low verandah and the wooden gate be shut and bar the music of marriage procession come to the roof above the gentle hum of the city and string of fly fox cross the face of the low moon have pray say ameera after long pause have pray for two thing first that may die in thy stead if thy death be demand and in the second that may die in the place of the child have pray to the prophet and to beebee miriam the virgin mary thinkest thou either will hear from thy lip who would not hear the light word ask for straight talk and thou hast give sweet talk will my prayer be hear how can say god be very good of that be not sure listen now when die or the child die what be thy fate live thou wilt return to the bold white mem log for kind call to kind not always with woman no with man it be otherwise thou wilt in this life later on go back to thine own folk that could almost endure for should be dead but in thy very death thou wilt be take away to strange place and paradise that do not know will it be paradise surely for who would harm thee
but we two and the child shall be elsewhere and we can not come to thee nor canst thou come to we in the old day before the child be bear do not think of these thing but now think of they always it be very hard talk it will fall as it will fall to morrow we do not know but to day and love we know well surely we be happy now so happy that it be well to make our happiness assure and thy beebee miriam should listen to for she be also woman
but then she would envy it be not seemly for man to worship woman holden laugh aloud at ameera ’s little spasm of jealousy be it not seemly why didst thou not turn from worship of thee then thou worshipper and of my king for all thy sweet word

his share from between ameera ’s lip and she kiss the other half into the mouth of the child who eat it slowly with wonder eye this will do each day of seven and without doubt he who be ours will be bold speaker and wise eh tota what wilt thou be when thou art man and be gray head tota tuck his fat leg into adorable crease he could crawl but he be not go to waste the spring of his youth in idle speech he want mian mittu ’s tail to tweak when he be advanced to the dignity of silver belt which with magic square engrave on silver and hang round his neck make up the great part of his clothing he
