oh you brute you utter brute the news of his trouble be already in his bungalow he read the knowledge in his butler ’s eye when ahmed khan bring in food and for the first and last time in his life lie hand upon his master ’s shoulder say eat sahib eat meat be good against sorrow also have know moreover the shadow come and go sahib the shadow come and go these be curry egg holden could neither eat nor sleep the heaven send down eight inch of rain in that night and wash the earth clean the water tear down wall break road and scour open the shallow graf on the mahomedan bury ground all next day it rain and holden sit still in his house consider his sorrow on the morning of the third day he receive telegram which say only ricketts myndonie holden relieve then he think that before he depart he would look at the house wherein he have be master and lord there be break in the weather and the rank earth steam with vapour he find that the rain have tear down the mud pillar of the gateway and the heavy wooden gate that have guard his life hang lazily from one hinge there be grass three inch high in the courtyard pir khan ’s lodge be empty and the sodden thatch sag between the beam gray squirrel be in possession of the verandah as if the house have be untenante for thirty year instead of three day ameera ’s mother have remove everything except some mildewed matting the tick tick of the little scorpion as they hurry across the floor be the only sound in the house ameera ’s room and the other one where tota have live be heavy with mildew and the narrow staircase lead to the roof be streak and stain with rain bear mud holden see all these thing and come out again to meet in the road durga dass his landlord,—portly affable clothe in white muslin and drive cee spring buggy he be overlook his property to see how the roof stand the stress of the first rain have hear say he you will not take this place any more sahib

road across as they desire from the burn ghaut to the city wall so that no man may say where this house stand naboth copyright by macmillan co. this be how it happen and the truth be also an allegory of empire meet he at the corner of my garden an empty basket on his head and an unclean cloth round his loin that be all the property to which naboth have the shadow of claim when first see he he open our acquaintance by beg he be very thin and show nearly as many rib as his basket and he tell long story about fever and lawsuit and an iron cauldron that have be seize by the court in execution of decree put my hand into my pocket to help naboth as king of the east have help alien adventurer to the loss of their kingdom rupee have hide in my waistcoat lining never know it be there and give the trove to naboth as direct gift from heaven he reply that be the only legitimate protector of the poor he have ever know next morning he reappear little fat in the round and curl himself into knot in the front verandah he say be his father and his mother and the direct descendant of all the god in his pantheon besides control the destiny of the universe he himself be but sweetmeat seller and much less important than the dirt under my foot have hear this sort of thing before so ask he what he want my rupee quoth naboth have raise he to the everlasting heaven and he wish to prefer request he wish to establish sweetmeat pitch near the house of his benefactor to gaze on my revered countenance as go to and fro illumine the world be graciously pleased to give permission and he go away with his head between his knee now at the far end of my garden the ground slop toward the public road and the slope be crown with thick shrubbery there be short carriage road from the house to the mall which pass close to the shrubbery next afternoon see that naboth have seat himself at the bottom of the slope down in the dust of the public road and in the full glare of the sun with starve basket of greasy sweet in front of he he have go into trade once more on the strength of my munificent donation and the ground be as paradise by my honour favour remember there be only naboth his basket the sunshine and the gray dust when the sap of my empire first begin next day he have move himself up the slope near to my shrubbery and wave palm leaf fan to keep the fly off
the sweet so judge that he must have do fair trade four day later notice that he have back himself and his basket under the shadow of the shrubbery and have tie an isabella colour rag between two branch in order to make more shade there be plenty of sweet in his basket think that trade must certainly be look up seven week later the government take up plot of ground for chief court close to the end of my compound and employ nearly four hundred cooly on the foundation naboth buy blue and white strip blanket brass lamp stand and small boy to cope with the rush of trade which be tremendous five day later he buy huge fat red back account book and glass inkstand thus see that the cooly have be get into his debt and that commerce be increase on legitimate line of credit also see that the one basket have grow into three and that naboth have back and hack into the shrubbery and make himself nice little clearing for the proper display of the basket the blanket the book and the boy one week and five day later he have build mud fireplace in the clearing and the fat account book be overflow he say that god create few englishman of my kind and that be the incarnation of all human virtue he offer some of his sweet as tribute and by accept these acknowledge he as my feudatory under the skirt of my protection three week later notice that the boy be in the habit of cook naboth ’s mid day meal for he and naboth be begin to grow stomach he have hack away more of my shrubbery and own another and fat account book eleven week later naboth have eat his way nearly through that shrubbery and there be reed hut with bedstead outside it stand in the little glade that he have erode two dog and baby sleep on the bedstead so fancy naboth have take wife he say that he have by my favour do this thing and that be several time fine than krishna six week and two day later mud wall have grow up at the back of the hut there be fowl in front and it smell little the municipal secretary say that cess pool be form in the public road from the drainage of my compound and that must take step to clear it away speak to naboth he say be lord paramount of his earthly concern and the garden be all my own property and send some more sweet in second hand duster two month later coolie bricklayer be kill in scuffle that take place opposite naboth ’s vineyard the inspector of police say it be serious case go into my servant quarter insult my butler ’s wife and want to arrest my butler the curious thing about the murder be that most of the cooly be drunk at the time naboth point out that my name be strong shield between he and his enemy and he expect that another baby would be bear to he shortly four month later the hut be all mud wall very solidly build and naboth have use most of my shrubbery for his five goat silver watch and an aluminium chain shine upon his very round stomach my servant be alarmingly drunk several time and use to waste the day with naboth when they get the chance speak to naboth he say by my favour and the glory of my countenance he would make all his woman folk lady and that if any one hint that he be run an illicit still under the shadow of the tamarisk why his suzerain be to prosecute week later he hire man to make several dozen square yard of trellis work to put round the back of his hut that his woman folk might be screen from the public gaze the man go away in the evening and leave his day ’s work to pave the short cut from the public road to my house be drive home in the dusk and turn the corner by naboth ’s vineyard quickly the next thing know be that the horse of the phaeton be stamp and plunge in the strong sort of bamboo net work both beast come down one rise with nothing more than chip knee the other be so badly kick that be force to shoot he naboth be go now and his hut be plough into its native mud with sweetmeat instead of salt for sign that the place be accurse have build summer house to overlook the end of the garden and it be as fort on my frontier whence guard my empire know exactly how ahab feel he have be shamefully misrepresent in the scripture the sending of dana da when the devil rid on your chest remember the chamar native proverb once upon time some people in india make new heaven and new earth out of break tea cup miss brooch or two and hair brush these be hide under bush or stuff into hole in the hillside and an entire civil service of subordinate god use to find or mend they again and every one say there be more thing in heaven and earth than be dream of in our philosophy several other thing happen also but the religion never seem to get much beyond its first manifestation though it add an air line postal service and orchestral effect in order to keep abreast of the time and choke off competition this religion be too elastic for ordinary use it stretch itself and embrace piece of everything that the medicine man of all age have manufacture it approve of and steal from freemasonry loot the latter day rosicrucians of half their pet word take any fragment of egyptian philosophy that it find in the encyclopædia britannica annex as many of the veda as have be translate into french or english and talk of all the rest build in the german version of what be leave of the zend avesta encourage white gray and black magic include spiritualism palmistry fortune telling by card hot chestnut double kernelled nut and tallow dropping would have adopt voodoo and oboe have it know anything about they and show itself in every way one of the most accommodating arrangement that have ever be invent since the birth of the sea when it be in thorough working order with all the machinery down to the subscription complete dana da come from nowhere with nothing in his hand and write chapter in its history which have hitherto be unpublished he say that his first name be dana and his second be da now set aside dana of the new york sun dana be bhil name and da fit no native of india unless you accept the bengali dé as the original spelling da be lap or finnish and dana da be neither finn chin bhil bengali lap nair gond romaney magh bokhariot kurd armenian levantine jew persian punjabi madrasi parsee nor anything else know to ethnologist he be simply dana da and decline to give further information for the sake of brevity and as roughly indicate his origin he be call the native he might have be the original old man of the mountain who be say to be the only authorize head of the tea cup creed some people say that he be but dana da use to smile and deny any connection with the cult explain that he be an independent experimenter as have say he come from nowhere with his hand behind his back and study the creed for three week sit at the foot of those good competent to explain its mystery then he laugh aloud and go away but the laugh might have be either of devotion or derision when he return he be without money but his pride be unabate he declare that he know more about the thing in heaven and earth than those who teach he and for this contumacy be abandon altogether his next appearance in public life be at big cantonment in upper india and he be then tell fortune with the help of three leaden dice very dirty old cloth and little tin box of opium pill he tell well fortune when he be allow half bottle of whiskey but the thing which he invent on the opium be quite worth the money he be in reduce circumstance among other people he tell the fortune of an englishman who have once be interested in the simla creed but who later on have marry and forget all his old knowledge in the study of baby and thing the englishman allow dana da to tell fortune for charity ’s sake and give he five rupee dinner and some old clothe when he have eat dana da profess gratitude and ask if there be anything he could do for his host in the esoteric line be there any one that you love say dana da the englishman love his wife but have no desire to drag her name into the conversation he therefore shake his head be there any one that you hate say dana da the englishman say that there be several man whom he hat deeply very good say dana da upon whom the whiskey and the opium be begin to tell only give their name and will despatch send to they and kill they now send be horrible arrangement first invent they say in iceland it be thing send by wizard and may take any form but most generally wander about the land in the shape of little purple cloud till it find the sendee and he it kill by change into the form of horse or cat or man without face it be not strictly native patent though chamar of the skin and hide caste can if irritated despatch send which sit on the breast of their enemy by night and nearly kill he very few native care to irritate chamar for this reason let despatch send say dana da be nearly dead now with want and drink and opium but should like to kill man before die can send send anywhere you choose and in any form except in the shape of man the englishman have no friend that he wish to kill but partly to soothe dana da whose eye be roll and partly to see what would be do he ask whether modify send could not be arrange for such sending as should make man ’s life burden to he and yet do he no harm if this be possible he notify his willingness to give dana da ten rupee for the job be not what be once say dana da and must take the money because be poor to what englishman shall send it send send to lone sahib say the englishman name man who have be most bitter in rebuke he for his apostasy from the tea cup creed dana da laugh and nod could have choose
no well man myself say he will see that he find the send about his path and about his bed he lie down on the hearth rug turn up the white of his eye shiver all over and begin to snort this be magic or opium or the send or all three when he open his eye he vow that the send have start upon the war path and be at that moment fly up to the town where lone sahib live give my ten rupee say dana da wearily and write letter to lone sahib tell he and all who believe with he that you and friend be use power great than theirs they will see that you be speak the truth he depart unsteadily with the promise of some more rupee if anything come of the send the englishman send letter to lone sahib couch in what he remember of the terminology of the creed he write also in the day of what you hold to be my backsliding have obtain enlightenment and with enlightenment have come power then he grow so deeply mysterious that the recipient of the letter could make neither head nor tail of it and be proportionately impressed for he fancy that his friend have become fifth round when man be fifth rounder he can do more than slade and houdin combine lone sahib read the letter in five different fashion and be begin sixth interpretation when his bearer dash in with the news that there be cat on the bed now if there be one thing that lone sahib hat more than another it be cat he scold the bearer for not turn it out of the house the bearer say that he be afraid all the door of the bedroom have be shut throughout the morning and no real cat could possibly have enter the room he would prefer not to meddle with the creature lone sahib enter the room gingerly and there on the pillow of his bed sprawl and whimper wee white kitten not jumpsome frisky little beast but slug like crawler with its eye barely open and its paw lack strength or direction,—a kitten that ought to have be in basket with its mamma lone sahib catch it by the scruff of its neck hand it over to the sweeper to be drown and fin the bearer four anna that evening as he be read in his room he fancy that he see something move about on the hearth rug outside the circle of light from his reading lamp when the thing begin to myowl he realise that it be kitten wee white kitten nearly blind and very miserable he be seriously angry and speak bitterly to his bearer who say that there be no kitten in the room when he bring in the lamp and real kitten of tender age generally have mother cat in attendance if the presence will go out into the verandah and listen say the bearer he will hear no cat how therefore can the kitten on the bed and the kitten on the hearth rug be real kitten lone sahib go out to listen and the bearer follow he but there be no sound of any one mew for her child he return to his room having hurl the kitten down the hillside and write out the incident of the day for the benefit of his co religionist those people be so absolutely free from superstition that they ascribe anything little out of the common to agency as it be their business to know all about the agency they be on term of almost indecent familiarity with manifestation of every kind their letter drop from the ceiling un stamp and spirit use to squatter up and down their staircase all night but they have never come into contact with kitten lone sahib write out the fact note the hour and the minute as every psychical observer be bind to do and append the englishman ’s letter because it be the most mysterious document and might have have bear upon anything in this world or the next an outsider would have translate all the tangle thus look out you laugh at once and now be go to make you sit up lone sahib ’s co religionist find that meaning in it but their translation be refine and full of four syllable word they hold sederunt and be fill with tremulous joy for in spite of their familiarity with all the other world and cycle they have very human awe of thing send from ghost land they meet in lone sahib ’s room in shrouded and sepulchral gloom and their conclave be break up by clink among the photo frame on the mantelpiece wee white kitten nearly blind be loop and writhe itself between the clock and the candlestick that stop all investigation or doubt here be the manifestation in the flesh it be so far as could be see devoid of purpose but it be manifestation of undoubted authenticity they draft round robin to the englishman the backslider of old day adjure he in the interest of the creed to explain whether there be any connection between the embodiment of some egyptian god or other have forget the name and his communication they call the kitten ra or toth or tum or something and when lone sahib confes that the first one have at his most misguided instance be drown by the sweeper they say consolingly that in his next life he would be bounder and not even round of the low grade these word may not be quite correct but they accurately express the sense of the house when the englishman receive the round robin it come by post he be startled and bewilder he send into the bazar for dana da who read the letter and laugh that be my send say he tell you would work well now give another ten rupee but what in the world be this gibberish about egyptian god ask the englishman cat say dana da with hiccough for he have discover the englishman ’s whiskey bottle cat and cat and cat never be such send hundred of cat now give ten more rupee and write as dictate dana da ’s letter be curiosity it bear the englishman ’s signature and hint at cat at send of cat the mere word on paper be creepy and uncanny to behold what have you do though say the englishman be as much in the dark as ever do you mean to say that you can actually send this absurd send you talk about judge for yourself say dana da what do that letter mean in little time they will all be at my foot and your and glory!—will be drug or drunk all day long dana da know his people when man who hat cat wake up in the morning and find little squirm kitten on his breast or put his hand into his ulster pocket and find little half dead kitten where his glove should be or open his trunk and find vile kitten among his dress shirt or go for long ride with his mackintosh strap on his saddle bow and shake little squawling kitten from its fold when he open it or go out to dinner and find little blind kitten under his chair or stay at home and find writhe kitten under the quilt or wriggle among his boot or hang head downwards in his tobacco jar or be mangle by his terrier in the verandah,—when such man find one kitten neither more nor less once day in place where no kitten rightly could or should be he be naturally upset when he dare not murder his daily trove because he believe it to be manifestation an emissary an embodiment and half dozen other thing all out of the regular course of nature he be more than upset he be actually distressed some of lone sahib ’s co religionist think that he be highly favour individual but many say that if he have treat the first kitten with proper respect as suit toth ra tum sennacherib embodiment all this trouble would have be avert they compare he to the ancient mariner but none the less they be proud of he and proud of the englishman who have send the manifestation they do not call it send because icelandic magic be not in their programme after sixteen kitten that be to say after one fortnight for there be three kitten on the first day to impress the fact of the send the whole camp be uplift by letter it come fly through window from the old man of the mountain the head of all the creed explain the manifestation in the most beautiful language and soak up all the credit for it himself the englishman say the letter be not there at all he be backslider without power or asceticism who could not even raise table by force of volition much less project an army of kitten through space the entire arrangement say the letter be strictly orthodox work and sanction by the high authority within the pale of the creed there be great joy at this for some of the weak brother see that an outsider who have be work on independent line could create kitten whereas their own ruler have never go beyond crockery and break at well be show desire to break line on their own trail in fact there be the promise of schism second round robin be draft to the englishman begin scoffer and end with selection of curse from the rite of mizraim and memphis and the commination of jugana who be fifth round upon whose name an upstart third round once trade papal excommunication be billet doux compare to the commination of jugana the englishman have be prove under the hand and seal of the old man of the mountain to have appropriate virtue and pretend to have power which in reality belong only to the supreme head naturally the round robin do not spare he he hand the letter to dana da to translate into decent english the effect on dana da be curious at first he be furiously angry and then he laugh for five minute have think he say that they would have come to in another week would have show that send the send and they would have dis crown the old man of the mountain who have send this send of mine do you do nothing the time have come for to act write as dictate and will put they to shame but give ten more rupee at dana da ’s dictation the englishman write nothing less than formal challenge to the old man of the mountain it wind up and if this manifestation be from your hand then let it go forward but if it be from my hand will that the sending shall cease in two day time on that day there shall be twelve kitten and thenceforward none at all the people shall judge between we this be sign by dana da who add pentacle and pentagram and crux ansata and half dozen swastika and triple tau to his name just to show that he be all he lie claim to be the challenge be read out to the gentleman and lady
and they remember then that dana da have laugh at they some year ago it be officially announce that the old man of the mountain would treat the matter with contempt dana da be an independent investigator without single round at the back of he
but this do not soothe his people they want to see fight they be very human for all their spirituality lone sahib who be really be wear out with kitten submit meekly to his fate he feel that he be be kitten to prove the power of dana da as the poet say when the state day dawn the shower of kitten begin some be white and some be tabby and all be about the same loathsome age three be on his hearth rug three in his bath room and the other six turn up at interval among the visitor who come to see the prophecy break down never be more satisfactory sending on the next day there be no kitten and the next day and all the other day be kittenless and quiet the people murmur and look to the old man of the mountain for an explanation letter write on palm leaf drop from the ceiling but every one except lone sahib feel that letter be not what the occasion demand there should have be cat there should have be cats,—full grow one the letter prove conclusively that there have be hitch in the psychic current which collide with dual identity have interfere with the percipient activity all along the main line the kitten be still go on but owe to some failure in the develop fluid they be not materialise the air be thick with letter for few day afterwards unseen hand play glück and beethoven on finger bowl and clock shade but all man feel that psychic life be mockery without materialise kitten even lone sahib shout with the majority on this head dana da ’s letter be very insulting and if he have then offer to lead new departure there be no know

so he hold his peace until they come to the charcoal burner clearing where the die flame say whit whit whit as they flutter and whisper over the white ash it must have be great fire when at full height man have see it at donga pa across the valley wink and blaze through the night and say that the charcoal burner of kodru be get drunk
but it be only suket singh sepoy of the 102d punjab native infantry and athira woman burn burn burn this be how thing befall and the policeman ’s diary will bear out athira be the wife of madu who be charcoal burner one eyed and of malignant disposition week after their marriage he beat athira with heavy stick month later suket singh sepoy come that way to the cool hill on leave from his regiment and electrify the villager of kodru with tale of service and glory under the government and the honour in which he suket singh be hold by the colonel sahib bahadur and desdemona listen to othello as desdemona have do all the world over and as she listen she love ve wife of my own say suket singh though that be no matter when you come to think of it be also due to return to my regiment after time and can not be deserter who intend to be havildar there be no himalayan version of could not love thee dear as much love not honour more but suket singh come near to make one never mind say athira stay with and if madu try to beat you beat he very good say suket singh
and he beat madu severely to the delight of all the charcoal burner of kodru that be enough say suket singh as he roll madu down the hillside now we shall have peace but madu crawl up the grass slope again and hover round his hut with angry eye he ’ll kill dead say athira to suket singh you must take away there ’ll be trouble in the line my wife will pull out my beard but never mind say suket singh will take you there be loud trouble in the line and suket singh ’s beard be pull and suket singh ’s wife go to live with her mother and take away the child that all right say athira and suket singh say yes that all right so there be only madu leave in the hut that look across the valley to donga pa and since the beginning of time no one have have any sympathy for husband so unfortunate as madu he go to juseen dazé the wizard man who keep the talk monkey ’s head get back my wife say madu can not say juseen dazé until you have make the sutlej in the valley run up the donga pa
no riddle say madu
and he shake his hatchet above juseen dazé ’s white head give all your money to the headman of the village say juseen dazé
and they will hold communal council and the council will send message that your wife must come back so madu give up all his worldly wealth amount to twenty seven rupee eight anna three pice and silver chain to the council of kodru and it fall as juseen dazé foretell they send athira ’s brother down into suket singh ’s regiment to call athira home suket singh kick he once round the line and then hand he over to the havildar who beat he with belt come back
yell athira ’s brother where to say athira to madu say he never say she then juseen dazé will send curse and you will wither away like bark tree in the springtime
say athira ’s brother athira sleep over these thing next morning she have rheumatism be begin to wither away like bark tree in the springtime she say that be the curse of juseen dazé
and she really begin to wither away because her heart be dry up with fear and those who believe in curse die from curse suket singh too be afraid because he love athira well than his very life two month pass and athira ’s brother stand outside the regimental line again and yelp aha you be wither away come back will come back say athira say rather that we will come back say suket singh ai
but when say athira ’s brother upon day very early in the morning say suket singh
and he tramp off to apply to the colonel sahib bahadur for one week ’s leave be wither away like bark tree in the spring moan athira you will be well soon say suket singh
and he tell she what be in his heart and the two laugh together softly for they love each other but athira grow well from that hour they go away together travel third class by train as the regulation provide and then in cart to the low hill and on foot to the high one athira sniff the scent of the pine of her own hill the wet himalayan hill it be good to be alive say athira hah say suket singh where be the kodru road and where be the forest ranger ’s house it cost forty rupee twelve year ago say the forest ranger hand the gun here be twenty say suket singh
and you must give the good bullet it be very good to be alive say athira wistfully sniff the scent of the pine mould
and they wait till the night have fall upon kodru and the donga pa
madu have stack the dry wood for the next day ’s charcoal burn on the spur above his house it be courteous in madu to save we this trouble say suket singh as he stumble on the pile which be twelve foot square and four high we must wait till the moon rise when the moon rise athira kneel upon the pile if it be only government snider say suket singh ruefully squint down the wire bind barrel of the forest ranger ’s gun be quick say athira and suket singh be quick but athira be quick no long then he light the pile at the four corner and climb on to it reload the gun the little flame begin to peer up between the big log atop of the brushwood the government should teach we to pull the trigger with our toe say suket singh grimly to the moon that be the last public observation of sepoy suket singh be still rise under the moon litter bear by six bearded man all unused to the work stop in the white sand that border the whiter plain it god ’s will they say we dare not cross to night even in boat let we light fire and cook food we be tired man they look at the litter inquiringly within the deputy commissioner of the kot kumharsen district lie die of fever they have bre he across country six fight man of frontier clan that he have win over to the path of moderate righteousness when he have break down at the foot of their inhospitable hill and tallantire his assistant ride with they heavy dry whisper wait till the river go down think we should reach camp before the dawn polly know she ’ll meet one of the litter man star across the river and catch faint twinkle of light on the far side he whisper to tallantire there be his camp fire and his wife they will cross in the morning for they have well boat can he live so long tallantire shake his head yardley orde be very near to death what need to vex his soul with hope of meeting that could not be the river gulp at the bank bring down cliff of sand and snarl the more hungrily the litter man seek for fuel in the waste dry camel thorn and refuse of the camp that have wait at the ford their sword belt clink as they move softly in the haze of the moonlight and tallantire ’s horse cough to explain that he would like blanket cold too say the voice from the litter fancy this be the end poor polly tallantire rearrange the blanket
khoda dad khan see this strip off his own heavy wad sheepskin coat and add it to the pile shall be warm by the fire presently say he tallantire take the waste body of his chief into his arm and hold it against his breast perhaps if they keep he very warm orde might live to see his wife once more if only blind providence would send three foot fall in the river that well say orde faintly sorry to be nuisance but be be there anything to drink they give he milk and whiskey and tallantire feel little warmth against his own breast orde begin to mutter it be not that mind die he say it leave polly and the district thank god we have no child dick you know dip awfully dip debt in my first five year service it be not much of pension but enough for she she have her mother at home get there be the difficulty
and and you see not be soldier ’s wife we ’ll arrange the passage home of course say tallantire quietly it not nice to think of send round the hat but good lord how many man lie here and remember that have to do it morten dead he be of my year shaughnessy be dead and he have child remember he use to read we their school letter what bore we think he evans be dead kot kumharsen kill he ricketts of myndonie be dead and go too man that be bear of woman be small potato and few in the hill that remind dick the four khusru kheyl village in our border want one third remittance this spring that fair their crop be
bad see that they get it and speak to ferris about the canal should like to have live till that be finish
it mean so much for the north indus village but ferris be an idle beggar wake he up you ’ll have charge of the district till my successor come wish they would appoint you permanently you know the folk suppose it will be bullow though good man but too weak for frontier work and he do not understand the priest the blind priest at jagai will bear watch you ’ll find it in my papers,—in the uniform case think call the khusru kheyl man up ’ll hold my last public audience
dad khan the leader of the man spring to the side of the litter his companion follow man die say orde quickly in the vernacular and soon there will be no more orde sahib to twist your tail and prevent you from raid cattle god forbid this thing break out the deep bass chorus the sahib be not go to die
yes he be
and then he will know whether mahomed speak truth or moses
but you must be good man when be not here such of you as live in our border must pay your taxi quietly as before have speak of the village to be gently treat this year such of you as live in the hill must refrain from cattle lift and burn no more thatch and turn deaf ear to the voice of the priest who not know the strength of the government would lead you into foolish war wherein you will surely die and your crop be eat by strange and you must not sack any caravan and must leave your arm at the police post when you come in as have be your custom and my order and tallantire sahib will be with you but do not know who take my place speak now true talk for be as it be already dead my children,—for though ye be strong man ye be child and thou art our father and our mother break in khoda dad khan with an oath what shall we do now there be no one to speak for we or to teach we to go wisely there remain tallantire sahib go to he he know your talk and your heart keep the young man quiet listen to the old man and obey khoda
dad khan take my ring the watch and chain go to thy brother keep those thing for my sake and will speak to whatever god may encounter and tell he that the khusru kheyl be good man ye have my leave to go khoda dad khan the ring upon his finger choke audibly as he catch the well know formula that close an interview his brother turn to look across the river the dawn be break and speck of white show on the dull silver of the stream she come say the man under his breath can he live for another two hour
and he pull the newly acquire watch out of his belt and look uncomprehendingly at the dial as he have see englishman do for two hour the belly sail tack and blunder up and down the river tallantire great of all the viceroy his administration be base upon principle and the principle must be enforce in season and out of season his pen and tongue have create the new india teem with possibility loud of successor to yardley orde there be gentleman and member of the bengal civil service who have win his place and university degree to boot in fair and open competition with the son of the english he be cultured of the world and if report speak truly have wisely and above all sympathetically rule crowded district in south eastern bengal he have be to england and charm many drawing room there his name if the viceroy recollect aright be mr grish chunder dé m. a. in short do anybody see any objection to the appointment always on principle of man of the people to rule the people the district in dad khan oil his long lock with great care put on his good bokhara belt new turban cap and fine green shoe and accompany by few friend come down from the hill to pay visit to the new deputy commissioner of kot kumharsen also he bear tribute four or five priceless gold mohur of akbar ’s time in white handkerchief these the deputy commissioner would touch and remit the little ceremony use to be sign that so far as khoda
dad khan ’s personal influence go the khusru kheyl would be good boys,—till the next time especially if khoda dad khan happen to like the new deputy commissioner in yardley orde ’s consulship his visit conclude with sumptuous dinner and perhaps forbid liquor certainly with some wonderful tale and great good fellowship then khoda dad khan would swagger back to his hold vow that orde sahib be one prince and tallantire sahib another and that whosoever go raid into british territory would be flay alive on this occasion he find the deputy commissioner ’s tent look much as usual regard himself as privileged he stride through the open door to confront suave portly bengali in english costume write at table unversed in the elevate influence of education and not in the least caring for university degree
khoda dad khan promptly set the man down for babu the native clerk of the deputy commissioner hat and despise animal

khoda dad khan in the face but if from your early infancy you have be accustom to look on battle murder and sudden death if spilt blood affect your nerve as much as red paint and above all if you have faithfully believe that the bengali be the servant of all hindustan and that all hindustan be vastly inferior to your own large lustful self you can endure even though uneducated very large amount of look over you can even stare down graduate of an oxford college if the latter have be bear in hothouse of stock breed in hothouse and fear physical pain as some man fear sin especially if your opponent ’s mother have frighten he to sleep in his youth with horrible story of devil inhabit afghanistan and dismal legend of the black north the eye behind the gold spectacle seek the floor khoda

khan explain as clearly as he could that though he himself intend to be good he really could not answer for the more reckless member of his tribe under the leadership of the blind mullah they might or they might not give trouble but they certainly have no intention whatever of obey the new deputy commissioner be tallantire perfectly sure that in the event of any systematic border raid the force in the district could put it down promptly tell the mullah if he talk any more fool ’s talk say tallantire curtly that he take his man on to certain death and his tribe to blockade trespass fine and blood money
but why do talk to one who no long carry weight in the counsel of the tribe khoda dad khan pocket that insult he have learn something that he much want to know and return to his hill to be sarcastically compliment by the mullah whose tongue rag round the camp fire be deadly flame than ever dung cake fed iv be pleased to consider here for moment the unknown district of kot kumharsen
it lie cut lengthways by the indus under the line of the khusru hill rampart of useless earth and tumble stone it be seventy mile long by fifty broad maintain population of something less than two hundred thousand and pay taxi to the extent of forty thousand pound year on an area that be by rather more than half sheer hopeless waste the cultivator be not gentle people the miner for salt be less gentle still and the cattle breeder least gentle of all police post in the top right hand corner and tiny mud fort in the top left hand corner prevent as much salt smuggling and cattle lift as the influence of the civilian could not put down and in the bottom right hand corner lie jumala the district headquarter pitiful knot of lime wash barn facetiously rent as house reek with frontier fever leak in the rain and oven in the summer it be to this place that grish chunder dé be travel there formally to take over charge of the district but the news of his come have go before bengalis be as scarce as poodle among the simple borderer who cut each other ’s head open with their long spade and worship impartially at hindu and mahomedan shrine they crowd to see he point at he and diversely compare he to gravid milch buffalo or break down horse as their limited range of metaphor prompt they laugh at his police guard and wish to know how long the burly sikhs be go to lead bengali ape they inquire whether he have bring his woman with he and advise he explicitly not to tamper with theirs it remain for wrinkled hag by the roadside to slap her lean breast as he pass cry have suckle six that could have eat six thousand of he the government shoot they and make this that king whereat blue turbaned huge bone plough mender shout have hope mother mine he may yet go the way of thy wastrel and the child the little brown puff ball regard curiously it be generally good thing for infancy to stray into orde sahib ’s tent where copper coin be to be win for the mere wishing and tale of the most authentic such as even their mother know but the first half of this fat black man could never tell they how pir prith haul the eye tooth out of ten devil how the big stone come to lie all in row of those who be more english than the english,”—of oxford and home with much curious book knowledge of bump supper cricket match hunting run and other unholy sport of the alien we must get these but if you go to hell that place can not be hot than this place and we be not tell that we shall there suffer from fever consequently be not afraid of die file out there they grin and go it will be long ere the khusru kheyl forget their night attack on the lowland village the mullah have promise an easy victory and unlimited plunder but behold armed trooper of the queen have rise out of the very earth cut slash and rid down under the star so that no man know where to turn and all fear that they have bre an army about their ear and run back to the hill in the panic of that flight more man be see to drop from wound inflict by an afghan knife jab upwards and

then khoda dad khan eloquent and unbreathed for he have take no part in the fight rise to improve the occasion he point out that the tribe owe every item of its present misfortune to the blind mullah who have lie in every possible particular and talk they into trap it be undoubtedly an insult that bengali the son of bengali should presume to administer the border but that fact do not as the mullah pretend herald general time of license and lifting and the inexplicable madness of the english have not in the least impair their power of guard their march on the contrary the baffled and out generalle tribe would now just when their food stock be low be blockade from any trade with hindustan until they have send hostage for good behaviour pay compensation for disturbance and blood money kheyl lack shrine whereat to pray we be weaken and henceforth how shall we dare to cross into the madar kheyl border as have be our custom to kneel to pir sajji ’s tomb the madar man will fall upon we dad khan stuff forage net in his hand and the trooper behind he may you never be tired say he cheerily well sahib that be good fight and naim shah ’s mother be in debt to you tallantire sahib clean cut they tell through jaw wad coat and deep into the collar bone well do but speak for the tribe there have be fault great fault thou know that and mine tallantire sahib keep the oath we sware to orde sahib on the bank of the indus as an afghan keep his knife sharp on one side blunt on the other say tallantire the well swing in the blow then but speak god ’s truth only the blind mullah carry the young man on the tip of his tongue and say that there be no more border law because bengali have be send and we need not fear the english at all so they come down to avenge that insult and get plunder ye know what befall and how far help now five score of we be dead or wind and we be all sham and sorry and desire no further war moreover that ye may well listen to we we have take off the head of the blind mullah whose evil counsel have lead we to folly bring it for
proof,”—and he heave on the floor the head he will give no more trouble for be chief now and so sit in high place at all audience
yet there be an offset to this head that be another fault one of the man find that black bengali beast through whom this trouble arise wander on horseback and weep reflect that he have cause loss of much good life alla dad khan whom if you choose will to morrow shoot whip off this head and bring it to you to cover your shame that ye may bury it see no man keep the spectacle though they be of gold slowly roll to tallantire ’s foot the crop haired head of spectacled bengali gentleman open eyed open mouth the head of terror incarnate bullow bend down yet another blood fine and heavy one khoda dad khan for this be the head of debendra nath the man ’s brother the babu be safe long since all but the fool of the khusru kheyl know that well care not for carrion quick meat for the thing be under our hill ask the road to jumala and alla dad khan show he the road to jehannum be as thou say but fool remain now what the government will do to we as to the blockade who art thou seller of dog ’s flesh thunder tallantire to speak of term and treaty get hence to the hill go and wait there starve till it shall please the government to call thy people out for punishment child and fool that ye be count your dead and be still rest assure that the government will send you man
ay return khoda
dad khan for we also be man as he look tallantire between the eye he add and by god sahib may thou be that man the amir ’s homily copyright by macmillan co.
his royal highness abdur rahman amir of afghanistan g. c. s. i. and trust ally of her imperial majesty the queen of england and empress of india be gentleman for whom all right think people should have profound regard like most other ruler he govern not as he would but as he can and the mantle of his authority cover the most turbulent race under the star to the afghan neither life property law nor kingship be sacred when his own lust prompt he to rebel he be thief by instinct murderer by heredity and training and frankly and bestially immoral by all three none the less he have his own crooked notion of honour and his character be fascinating to study on occasion he will fight without reason give till he be hack in piece on other occasion he will refuse to show fight till he be drive into corner herein he be as unaccountable as the gray wolf who be his blood brother and these man his highness rule by the only weapon that they understand the fear of death which among some oriental be the beginning of wisdom some say that the amir ’s authority reach no far than rifle bullet can range but as none be quite certain when their king may be in their midst and as he alone hold every one of the thread of government his respect be increase among man gholam hyder the commander in chief of the afghan army be fear reasonably for he can impale all kabul city fear the governor of kabul who have power of life and death through all the ward but the amir of afghanistan though outlying tribe pretend otherwise when his back be turn be dread beyond chief and governor together his word be red law by the gust of his passion fall the leaf of man ’s life and his favour be terrible he have suffer many thing and be hunt fugitive before he come to the throne and he understand all the class of his people by the custom of the east any man or woman have complaint to make or an enemy against whom to be avenge have the right of speak face to face with the king at the daily public audience this be personal government as it be in the day of harun al raschid of blessed memory whose time exist still and will exist long after the english have pas away the privilege of open speech be of course exercise at certain personal risk the king may be pleased and raise the speaker to honour for that very bluntness of speech which three minute later bring too imitative petitioner to the edge of the ever ready blade and the people love to have it so for it be their right it happen upon day in kabul that the amir choose to do his day ’s work in the baber gardens which lie short distance from the city of kabul light table stand before he and round the table in the open air be group general and finance minister accord to their degree the court and the long tail of feudal chief man of blood feed and cow by blood stand in an irregular semicircle round the table and the wind from the kabul orchard blow among they all day long sweat courier dash in with letter from the outlying district with rumour of rebellion intrigue famine failure of payment or announcement of treasure on the road and all day long the amir would read the docket and pass such of these as be less private to the official whom they directly concern or call up wait chief for word of explanation it be well to speak clearly to the ruler of afghanistan then the grim head under the black astrachan cap with the diamond star in front would nod gravely and that chief would return to his fellow once that afternoon woman clamour for divorce against her husband who be bald and the amir hear both side of the case bid she pour curd over the bare scalp and lick they off that the hair might grow again and she be content here the court laugh and the woman withdraw curse her king under her breath
but when twilight be fall and the order of the court be little relaxed there come before the king in custody tremble haggard wretch sore with much buffeting but of stout enough build who have steal three rupee of such small matter do his highness take cognisance why do you steal say he and when the king ask question they do themselves service who answer directly be poor and no one give hungry and there be no food why do you not work could find no work protector of the poor and be starve you lie you steal for drink for lust for idleness for anything but hunger since any man who will may find work and daily bread the prisoner drop his eye he have attend the court before and he know the ring of the death tone any man may get work who know this so well as do for too have be hunger not like you bastard scum but as any honest man may be by the turn of fate and the will of god grow warm the amir turn to his noble all arow and thrust the hilt of his sabre aside with his elbow you have hear this son of lie hear tell true tale also be once starve and tighten my belt on the sharp belly pinch nor be alone for with be another who do not fail in my evil day when be hunt before ever come to this throne and wander like houseless dog by kandahar my money melt melt melt till he fling out bare palm before the audience and day upon day faint and sick go back to that one who wait and god know how we live till on day take our good lihaf_—silk it be fine work of iran such as no needle now work warm and coverlet for two and all that we have bring it to money lender in by lane and ask for three rupee upon it he say to who be now the king you be thief this be worth three hundred be no thief answer but prince of good blood and be hungry prince of wander beggar say that money lender have no money with but go to my house with my clerk and he will give you two rupee eight anna for that be all will lend so go with the clerk to the house and we talk on the way and he give the money we live on it till it be spend and we fare hard and then that clerk say be young man of good heart surely the money lender will lend yet more on that lihaf and he offer two rupee these refuse say nay but get some work and he get work and even abdur rahman amir of afghanistan work day by day as coolie bear burden and labour of my hand receive four annas wage day for my sweat and backache
but he this bastard son of naught must steal for year and four month work
and none dare say that lie for have witness even that clerk who be now my friend then there rise in his place among the sirdar and the noble one clothe in silk who fold his hand and say this be the truth of god for who by the favour of god and the amir be such as you know be once clerk to that money lender there be pause and the amir cry hoarsely to the prisoner throw scorn upon he till he end with the dread dar arid which clinch justice
so they lead the thief away and the whole of he be see no more together and the court rustle out of its silence whispering before god and the prophet but this be man at twenty two narrow as the womb deep as the pit and dark as the heart of man.—_sonthal miner ’s proverb weaver go out to reap but stay to unravel the corn stalk be there any sense in weaver
janki meah glare at kundoo but as janki meah be blind kundoo be not impressed he have come to argue with janki meah
and if chance favour to make love to the old man ’s pretty young wife this be kundoo ’s grievance and he speak in the name of all the five man who with janki meah compose the gang in number seven gallery of twenty two janki meah have be blind for the thirty year during which he have serve the jimahari colliery with pick and crowbar all through those thirty year he have regularly every morning before go down draw from the overseer his allowance of lamp oil just as if he have be an eyed miner what kundoo ’s gang resent as hundred of gang have resent before be janki meah ’s selfishness he would not add the oil to the common stock of his gang but would save and sell it know these work before you be bear
janki meah use to reply do not want the light to get my coal out by and be not go to help you the oil be mine and intend to keep it strange man in many way be janki
meah the white haired hot temper sightless weaver who have turn pitman all day long except sunday and monday when he be usually drunk he work in the twenty two shaft of the jimahari colliery as cleverly as man with all the sen at evening he go up in the great steam haul cage to the pit bank and there call for his pony rusty coal dusty beast nearly as old as janki
meah the pony would come to his side and janki meah would clamber on to its back and be take at once to the plot of land which he like the other miner receive from the jimahari company the pony know that place and when after six year the company change all the allotment to prevent the miner from acquire proprietary right
janki meah represent with tear in his eye that be his hold shift he would never be able to find his way to the new one my horse only know that place plead
janki meah
and so he be allow to keep his land on the strength of this concession and his accumulate oil saving
janki meah take second wife girl of the jolaha main stock of the meah and singularly beautiful
janki meah could not see her beauty wherefore he take she on trust and forbid she to go down the pit he have not work for thirty year in the dark without know that the pit be no place for pretty woman he load she with ornament not brass or pewter but real silver one
and she reward he by flirt outrageously with kundoo of number seven gallery gang kundoo be really the gang head
but janki meah insist upon all the work be enter in his own name and choose the man that he work with custom strong even than the jimahari company dictate that janki by right of his year should manage these thing and should also work despite his blindness in indian mine where they cut into the solid coal with the pick and clear it out from floor to ceiling he could come to no great harm at home where they undercut the coal and bring it down in crash avalanche from the roof he would never have be allow to set foot in pit he be not popular man because of his oil saving but all the gang admit that janki know all the khad or work that have ever be sink or work since the jimahari company first start operation on the tarachunda field pretty little unda only know that her old husband be fool who could be manage she take no interest in the colliery except in so far as they swallow up kundoo five day out of the seven and cover he with coal dust kundoo be great workman and do his good not to get drunk because when he have save forty rupee unda be to steal everything that she could find in janki ’s house and run with kundoo to land where there be no mine and every one keep three fat bullock and milch buffalo while this scheme ripen it be his custom to drop in upon janki and worry he about the oil saving unda sit in corner and nod approval on the night when kundoo have quote that objectionable proverb about weaver janki grow angry
listen you pig say he blind be and old be but before ever you be bear be gray among the coal even in the day when the twenty two khad be unsunk and there be not two thousand man here be know to have all knowledge of the pit what khad be there that do not know from the bottom of the shaft to the end of the last drive be it the baromba khad the old or the twenty two where tibu ’s gallery run up to number five hear the old fool talk say kundoo nod to unda no gallery of twenty two will cut into five before the end of the rain we have month ’s solid coal before we the babuji say so what do these fat slug from calcutta know he draw and draw and draw and talk and talk and talk and his map be all wrong
janki know that this be so when man have be shut up in the dark for thirty year god give he knowledge the old gallery that tibu ’s gang make be not six foot from number five without doubt god give the blind knowledge say kundoo with look at unda let it be as you say for my part do not know where lie the gallery of tibu ’s gang but be not wither monkey who need oil to grease his joint with kundoo swing out of the hut laugh and unda giggle janki turn his sightless eye toward his wife and swear have land and have sell great deal of lamp oil muse
janki but be fool to marry this child week later the rain set in with vengeance and the gang paddle about in coal slush at the pit bank then the big mine pump be make ready and the manager of the colliery plough through the wet towards the tarachunda river swell between its soppy bank lord send that this beastly beck do not misbehave say the manager piously and he go to take counsel with his assistant about the pump
but the tarachunda misbehave very much indeed after fall of three inch of rain in an hour it be oblige to do something it top its bank and join the flood water that be hem between two low hill just where the embankment of the colliery main line cross when large part of rain feed river and few acre of flood water make dead set for nine foot culvert the culvert may spout its fine but the water can not all get out the manager prance upon one leg with excitement and his language be improper he have reason to swear because he know that one inch of water on land mean pressure of one hundred ton to the acre and here be about five foot of water form behind the railway embankment over the shallow working of twenty two you must understand that in coal mine the coal near the surface be work first from the central shaft that be to say the miner may clear out the stuff to within ten twenty or thirty foot of the surface and when all be work out leave only skin of earth uphold by some few pillar of coal in deep mine where they know that they have any amount of material at hand man prefer to get all their mineral out at one shaft rather than make number of little hole to tap the comparatively unimportant surface coal and the manager watch the flood the culvert spout nine foot gush but the water still form and word be send to clear the man out of twenty two the cage come up crammed and cram again with the man near the pit eye as they call the place where you can see daylight from the bottom of the main shaft all away and away up the long black gallery the flare lamp be wink and dance like so many firefly and the man and the woman wait for the clank rattle thunder cage to come down and fly up again but the out work be very far off and word could not be pas quickly though the head of the gang and the assistant shout and swear and tramp and stumble the manager keep one eye on the great troubled pool behind the embankment and pray that the culvert would give way and let the water through in time with the other eye he watch the cage come up and see the headman count the roll of the gang with all his heart and soul he swear at the winder who control the iron drum that wind up the wire rope on which hang the cage in little time there be down draw in the water behind the embankment suck whirlpool all yellow and yeasty the water have smash through the skin of the earth and be pour into the old shallow working of twenty two deep down below rush of black water catch the last gang wait for the cage and as they clamber in the whirl be about their waist the cage reach the pit bank and the manager call the roll the gang be all safe except gang janki gang mogul and gang rahim eighteen man with perhaps ten basket woman who load the coal into the little iron carriage that run on the tramway of the main gallery these gang be in the out work three quarter of mile away on the extreme fringe of the mine once more the cage go down but with only two englishman in it and drop into swirl roar current that have almost touch the roof of some of the low side gallery one of the wooden balk with which they have prop the old working shoot past on the current just miss the cage if we do not want our rib knock out we ’d well go say the manager we can not even save the company ’s prop the cage draw out of the water with splash and few minute later it be officially report that there be at least ten foot of water in the pit ’s eye now ten foot of water there mean that all other place in the mine be flood except such gallery as be more than ten foot above the level of the bottom of the shaft the deep working would be full the main gallery would be full but in the high work reach by incline from the main road there would be certain amount of air cut off so to speak by the water and squeeze up by it the little science primer explain how water behave when you pour it down test tube the flooding of twenty two be an illustration on large scale by the holy grove what the slope of my gallery and feel the water there have be no water in the cutting in our time clamour the woman why can not we go away be silent say janki long ago when my father be here water come to ten no eleven cut and there be great trouble let we get away to where the air be well the three gang and the basket woman leave number nine gallery and go far up number sixteen at one turn of the road they could see the pitchy black water lap on the coal it have touch the roof of gallery that they know well gallery where they use to smoke their huqas and manage their flirtation see this they call aloud upon their god and the meah who be thrice bastard muhammadan strove to recollect the name of the prophet they come to great open square whence nearly all the coal have be extract it be the end of the out working and the end of the mine far away down the gallery small pumping engine use for keep dry deep working and feed with steam from above be throb faithfully they hear it cease they have cut off the steam say kundoo hopefully they have give the order to use all the steam for the pit bank pump they will clear out the water if the water have reach the smoking gallery say janki all the company ’s pump can do nothing for three day it be very hot moan jasoda the meah basket woman there be very bad air here because of the lamp put they out say janki why do you want lamp the lamp be put out and the company sit still in the utter dark somebody rise quietly and begin walk over the coal it be janki who be touch the wall with his hand where be the ledge he murmur to himself sit sit say kundoo if we die we die the air be very bad but janki still stumble and creep and tap with his pick upon the wall the woman rise to their foot stay all where you be without the lamp you can not see and be always see say janki then he pause and call out
oh you who have be in the cutting more than ten year what be the name of this open place be an old man and have forget bullia ’s room answer the sonthal who have complain of the vileness of the air again say janki bullia ’s room then have find it say janki the name only have slip my memory tibu ’s gang ’s gallery be here lie say kundoo there have be no gallery in this place since my day three and when it be fairly under way the manager think that the hour have come for the pump there be no fresh inrush into the mine the tall red iron clamp pump beam rise and fall and the pump snore and your engine show what they can do now gur sahai grin and nod with his right hand upon the lever and an oil can in his left he could do no more than he be do but he could keep that up till the dawn be the company ’s pump to be beat by the vagary of that troublesome tarachunda river never never and the pump sob and pant never never the manager sit in the shelter of the pit bank roofing try to dry himself by the pump boiler fire and in the dreary dusk he see the crowd on the dam scatter and fly that the end he groan twill take we six week to persuade ’em that we have not try to drown their mate on purpose oh for decent rational geordie but the flight have no panic in it man have run over from five with astounding news and the foreman could not hold their gang together presently surround by
