to say so now but do not believe you old fellow jews in shushan copyright by macmillan co. my newly purchase house furniture be at the least insecure the leg part from the chair and the top from the table on the slight provocation but such as it be it be to be pay for and ephraim agent and collector for the local auctioneer wait in the verandah with the receipt he be announce by the mahomedan servant as ephraim yahudi”—ephraim the jew he who believe in the brotherhood of man should hear my elahi bukhsh grind the second word through his white tooth with all the scorn he dare show before his master ephraim be personally meek in manner so meek indeed that one could not understand how he have fall into the profession of bill collect he resemble an over feed sheep and his voice suit his figure there be fix unvarye mask of childish wonder upon his face if you pay he he be as one marvel at your wealth if you send he away he seem puzzled at your hard heartedness never be jew more unlike his dread breed ephraim wear list slipper and coat of duster cloth so preposterously pattern that the most brazen of british subaltern would have shy from they in fear very slow and deliberate be his speech and carefully guard to give offense to no one after many week ephraim be induce to speak to of his friend there be eight of we in shushan and we be wait till there be ten then we shall apply for synagogue and get leave from calcutta to day we have no synagogue and only be priest and butcher to our people be of the tribe of judah think but be not sure my father be of the tribe of judah and we wish much to get our synagogue shall be priest of that synagogue shushan be big city in the north of india count its dweller by the ten thousand and these eight of the choose people be shut up in its midst wait till time or chance send they their full congregation miriam the wife of ephraim two little child an orphan boy of their people ephraim ’s uncle jackrael israel white haired old man his wife hester jew from cutch one hyem benjamin and ephraim priest and butcher make up the list of the jews in shushan they live in one house on the outskirt of the great city amid heap of saltpetre rotten brick herd of kine and fix pillar of dust cause by the incessant passing of the beast to the river to drink in the evening the child of the city come to the waste place to fly their kite and ephraim ’s son hold aloof watch the sport from the roof but never descend to take part in it at the back of the house stand small brick enclosure in which ephraim prepare the daily meat for his people after the custom of the jews once the rude door of the square be suddenly smash open by struggle from inside and show the meek bill collector at his work nostril dilated lip draw back over his tooth and his hand upon half madden sheep he be attire in strange raiment have no relation whatever to duster coat or list slipper and knife be in his mouth as he struggle with the animal between the wall the breath come from he in thick sobs and the nature of the man seem change when the ordain slaughter be end he see that the door be open and shut it hastily his hand leave red mark on the timber while his child from the neighbour house top look down awe stricken and open eyed glimpse of ephraim busy in one of his religious capacity be no thing to be desire twice summer come upon shushan turn the trodden waste ground to iron and bring sickness to the city it will not touch we say ephraim confidently before the winter we shall have our synagogue my brother and his wife and child be come up from calcutta and then shall be the priest of the synagogue jackrael israel the old man would crawl out in the stifle evening to sit on the rubbish heap and watch the corps be bear down to the river it will not come near we say jackrael israel feebly for we be the people of god and my nephew will be priest of our synagogue let they die he creep back to his house again and bar the door to shut himself off from the world of the gentile but miriam the wife of ephraim look out of the window at the dead as the bier pass and say that she be afraid ephraim comfort she with hope of the synagogue to be and collect bill as be his custom in one night the two child die and be bury early in the morning by ephraim the death never appear in the city return the sorrow be my sorrow say ephraim and this to he seem sufficient reason for set at naught the sanitary regulation of large flourish and remarkably well govern empire the orphan boy dependent on the charity of ephraim and his wife could have feel no gratitude and must have be ruffian he beg for whatever money his protector would give he and with that flee down country for his life week after the death of her child miriam leave her bed at night and wander over the country to find they she hear they cry behind every bush or drown in every pool of water in the field
and she beg the cartman on the grand trunk road not to steal her little one from she in the morning the sun rise and beat upon her bare head and she turn into the cool wet crop to lie down and never come back though hyem benjamin and ephraim seek she for two night the look of patient wonder on ephraim ’s face deepen
but he presently find an explanation there be so few of we here and these people be so many say he that it may be our god have forget we in the house on the outskirt of the city old jackrael israel and hester grumble that there be no one to wait on they and that miriam have be untrue to her race ephraim go out and collect bill and in the evening smoke with hyem benjamin till one dawn hyem benjamin die having first pay all his debt to ephraim jackrael israel and hester sit alone in the empty house all day and when ephraim return weep the easy tear of age till they cry themselves asleep week later ephraim stagger under huge bundle of clothe and cooking pot lead the old man and woman to the railway station where the bustle and confusion make they whimper we be go back to calcutta say ephraim to whose sleeve hester be cling there be more of we there and here my house be empty he help hester into the carriage and turn back say to should have be priest of the synagogue if there have be ten of we surely we must have be forget by our god the remnant of the break colony pass out of the station on their journey south while subaltern turn over the book on the bookstall be whistle to himself the ten little nigger boy but the tune sound as solemn as the dead march it be the dirge of the jews in shushan georgie porgie copyright by macmillan co. georgie porgie pudding and pie kiss the girl and make they cry when the girl come out to play georgie porgie run away if you will admit that man have no right to enter his drawing room early in the morning when the housemaid be set thing right and clear away the dust you will concede that civilised people who eat out of china and own card case have no right to apply their standard of right and wrong to an unsettled land when the place be make fit for their reception by those man who be tell off to the work they can come up bring in their trunk their own society and the decalogue and all the other apparatus where the queen ’s law do not carry it be irrational to expect an observance of other and weak rule the man who run ahead of the car of decency and propriety and make the jungle way straight can not be judge in the same manner as the stay at home folk of the rank of the regular tchin not many month ago the queen ’s law stop few mile north of thayetmyo on the irrawaddy there be no very strong public opinion up to that limit
but it exist to keep man in order when the government say that the queen ’s law must carry up to bhamo and the chinese border the order be give and some man whose desire be to be ever little in advance of the rush of respectability flock forward with the troop these be the man who could never pass examination and would have be too pronounce in their idea for the administration of bureau work province the supreme government step in as soon as might be with code and regulation and all but reduce new burma to the dead indian level but there be short time during which strong man be necessary and plough field for themselves among the fore runner of civilisation be georgie porgie reckon by all who know he strong man he hold an appointment in low burma when the order come to break the frontier and his friend call he georgie porgie because of the singularly burmese like manner in which he sing song whose first line be something like the word georgie porgie most man who have be in burma will know the song it mean puff puff puff puff great steamboat georgie sing it to his banjo and his friend shout with delight so that you could hear they far away in the teak forest when he go to upper burma he have no special regard for god or man
but he know how to make himself respect and to carry out the mixed military civil duty that fall to most man ’s share in those month he do his office work and entertain now and again the detachment of fever shake soldier who blunder through his part of the world in search of fly party of dacoit sometimes he turn out and dress down dacoit on his own account for the country be still smoulder and would blaze when least expect he enjoy these charivaris but the dacoit be not so amused all the official who come in contact with he depart with the idea that georgie porgie be valuable person well able to take care of himself and on that belief he be leave to his own device at the end of few month he weary of his solitude and cast about for company and refinement the queen ’s law have hardly begin to be feel in the country and public opinion which be more powerful than the queen ’s law have yet to come also there be custom in the country which allow white man to take to himself wife of the daughter of heth upon due payment the marriage be not quite so binding as be the nikkah ceremony among mahomedan but the wife be very pleasant when all our troop be back from burma there will be proverb in their mouth as thrifty as burmese wife and pretty english lady will wonder what in the world it mean the headman of the village next to georgie porgie ’s post have fair daughter who have see georgie porgie and love he from afar when news go abroad that the englishman with the heavy hand who live in the stockade be look for housekeeper the headman come in and explain that for five hundred rupee down he would entrust his daughter to georgie porgie ’s keep to be maintain in all honour respect and comfort with pretty dress accord to the custom of the country this thing be do and georgie porgie never repent it he find his rough and tumble house put straight and make comfortable his hitherto unchecked expense cut down by one half and himself pet and make much of by his new acquisition who sit at the head of his table and sing song to he and order his madrassee servant about and be in every way as sweet and merry and honest and win little woman as the most exacting of bachelor could have desire no race man say who know produce such good wife and head of household as the burmese when the next detachment tramp by on the war path the subaltern in command find at georgie porgie ’s table hostess to be deferential to woman to be treat in every way as one occupy an assured position when he gather his man together next dawn and replunge into the jungle he think regretfully of the nice little dinner and the pretty face and envy georgie porgie from the bottom of his heart yet he be engage to girl at home and that be how some man be construct the burmese girl ’s name be not pretty one but as she be promptly christen georgina by georgie porgie the blemish do not matter georgie porgie think well of the petting and the general comfort and vow that he have never spend five hundred rupee to well end after three month of domestic life great idea strike he matrimony english matrimony could not be such bad thing after all if he be so thoroughly comfortable at the back of beyond with this burmese girl who smoke cheroot how much more comfortable would he be with sweet english maiden who would not smoke cheroot and would play upon piano instead of banjo also he have desire to return to his kind to hear band once more and to feel how it feel to wear dress suit again decidedly matrimony would be very good thing he think the matter out at length of evening while georgina sing to he or ask he why he be so silent and whether she have do anything to offend he as he think he smoke and as he smoke he look at georgina and in his fancy turn she into fair thrifty amusing merry little english girl with hair come low down on her forehead and perhaps cigarette between her lip certainly not big thick burma cheroot of the brand that georgina smoke he would we girl with georgina ’s eye and most of her way but not all she could be improve upon then he blow thick smoke wreath through his nostril and stretch himself he would taste marriage georgina have help he to save money and there be six month leave due to he see here little woman he say we must put by more money for these next three month want it that be direct slur on georgina ’s housekeeping for she pride herself on her thrift but since she god want money she would do
she good you want money she say with little laugh have money she run to her own room and fetch out small bag of rupee of all that you give keep back some one hundred and seven rupee can you want more money than that take it it be my pleasure if you use it she spread out the money on the table and push it towards he with her quick little pale yellow finger georgie porgie never refer to economy in the household again three month later after the despatch and receipt of several mysterious letter which georgina could not understand and hat for that reason georgie porgie say that he be go away and she must return to her father ’s house and stay there georgina weep she would go with her god from the world ’s end to the world ’s end why should she leave he she love he be only go to rangoon
say georgie porgie shall be back in month but it be safe to stay with your father will leave you two hundred rupee if you go for month what need of two hundred fifty be more than enough there be some evil here do not go or at least let go with you georgie porgie do not like to remember that scene even at this date in the end he get rid of georgina by compromise of seventy five rupee she would not take more then he go by steamer and rail to rangoon the mysterious letter have grant he six month leave the actual flight and an idea that he might have be treacherous hurt severely at the time but as soon as the big steamer be well out into the blue thing be easy and georgina ’s face and the queer little stockaded house and the memory of the rush of shout dacoit by night the cry and struggle of the first man that he have ever kill with his own hand and hundred other more intimate thing fade and fade out of georgie porgie ’s heart and the vision of approach england take its place the steamer be full of man on leave all rampantly jovial soul who have shake off the dust and sweat of upper burma and be as merry as schoolboy they help georgie porgie to forget then come england with its luxury and decency and comfort and georgie porgie walk in pleasant dream upon pavement of which he have nearly forget the ring wonder why man in their sen ever leave town he accept his keen delight in his furlough as the reward of his service providence far arrange for he another and great delight all the pleasure of quiet english woo quite different from the brazen business of the east when half the community stand back and bet on the result and the other half wonder what mrs
and so will say to it it be pleasant girl and perfect summer and big country house near petworth where there be acre and acre of purple heather and high grass water meadow to wander through georgie porgie feel that he have at last find something worth the living for and naturally assume that the next thing to do be to ask the girl to share his life in india she in her ignorance be willing to go on this occasion there be no bartering with village headman there be fine middle class wedding in the country with stout papa and weep mamma and good man in purple and fine linen and six snub nosed girl from the sunday school to throw rise on the path between the tombstone up to the church door the local paper describe the affair at great length even down to give the hymn in full but that be because the direction be starve for want of material then come honeymoon at arundel and the mamma weep copiously before she allow her one daughter to sail away to india under the care of georgie porgie the bridegroom beyond any question georgie porgie be immensely fond of his wife and she be devoted to he as the good and great man in the world when he report himself at bombay he feel justify in demand good station for his wife ’s sake and because he have make little mark in burma and be begin to be appreciate they allow he nearly all that he ask for and post he to station which we will call sutrain it stand upon several hill and be style officially sanitarium for the good reason that the drainage be utterly neglect here georgie porgie settle down and find marry life come very naturally to he he do not rave as do many bridegroom over the strangeness and delight of see his own true love sit down to breakfast with he every morning as though it be the most natural thing in the world he have be there before as the americans say and check the merit of his own present grace by those of georgina he be more and more inclined to think that he have do well
but there be no peace or comfort across the bay of bengal under the teak tree where georgina live with her father wait for georgie porgie to return the headman be old and remember the war of he have be to rangoon and know something of the way of the kullah sit in front of his door in the evening he teach georgina dry philosophy which do not console she in the least the trouble be that she love georgie porgie just as much as the french girl in the english history book love the priest whose head be break by the king ’s bully one day she disappear from the village with all the rupee that georgie porgie have give she and very small smattering of english also gain from georgie porgie the headman be angry at first but light fresh cheroot and say something uncomplimentary about the sex in general georgina have start on search for georgie porgie who might be in rangoon or across the black water or dead for aught that she know chance favour she an old sikh policeman tell she that georgie porgie have cros the black water she take steerage passage from rangoon and go to calcutta keep the secret of her search to herself in india every trace of she be lose for six week and no one know what trouble of heart she must have undergo she reappear four hundred mile north of calcutta steadily head northwards very worn and haggard but very fix in her determination to find georgie porgie she could not understand the language of the people but india be infinitely charitable and the woman folk along the grand trunk give her food something make she believe that georgie porgie be to be find at the end of that pitiless road she may have see sepoy who know he in burma but of this no one can be certain at last she find regiment on the line of march and meet there one of the many subaltern whom georgie porgie have invite to dinner in the far off old day of the dacoit hunting there be certain amount of amusement among the tent when georgina throw herself at the man ’s foot and begin to cry there be no amusement when her story be tell but collection be make and that be more to the point one of the subaltern know of georgie porgie ’s whereabout but not of his marriage so he tell georgina and she go her way joyfully to the north in railway carriage where there be rest for tired foot and shade for dusty little head the march from the train through the hill into sutrain be try
but georgina have money and family journey in bullock cart give her help it be an almost miraculous journey and georgina feel sure that the good spirit of burma be look after she the hill road to sutrain be chilly stretch and georgina catch bad cold still there be georgie porgie at the end of all the trouble to take she up in his arm and pet she as he use to do in the old day when the stockade be shut for the night and he have approve of the evening meal georgina go forward as fast as she could and her good spirit do her one last favour an englishman stop she in the twilight just at the turn of the road into sutrain say good heaven what be you do here he be gillis the man who have be georgie porgie ’s assistant in upper burma and who occupy the next post to georgie porgie in the jungle georgie porgie have apply to have he to work with at sutrain because he like he have come say georgina simply it be such long way and have be month in come where be his house gillis gasp he have see enough of georgina in the old time to know that explanation would be useless you can not explain thing to the oriental you must show ’ll take you there say gillis
and he lead georgina off the road up the cliff by little pathway to the back of house set on platform cut into the hillside the lamp be just light but the curtain be not draw now look say gillis stop in front of the drawing room window georgina look and see georgie porgie and the bride she put her hand up to her hair which have come out of its top knot and be straggle about her face she try to set her ragged dress in order but the dress be past pull straight
and she cough queer little cough for she really have take very bad cold gillis look too but while georgina only look at the bride once turn her eye always on georgie porgie gillis look at the bride all the time what be you go to do say gillis who hold georgina by the wrist in case of any unexpected rush into the lamplight will you go in and tell that english woman that you live with her husband
no say georgina faintly let go be go away swear that be go away she twist herself free and run off into the dark poor little beast say gillis drop on to the main road ’d ha give she something to get back to burma with what narrow shave though and that angel would never have forgive it this seem to prove that the devotion of gillis be not entirely due to his affection for georgie porgie the bride and the bridegroom come out into the verandah after dinner in order that the smoke of georgie porgie ’s cheroot might not hang in the new drawing room curtain what be that noise down there say the bride both listen
oh say georgie porgie suppose some brute of hillman have be beat his wife beat his wife how ghastly say the bride fancy your beat she slip an arm round her husband ’s waist and lean her head against his shoulder look out across the cloud fill valley in deep content and security but it be georgina cry all by herself down the hillside among the stone of the water course where the washerman wash the clothe little tobrah copyright by macmillan co. prisoner ’s head do not reach to the top of the dock as the english newspaper say this case however be not report because nobody care by so much as hempen rope for the life or death of little tobrah the assessor in the red courthouse sit upon he all through the long hot afternoon and whenever they ask he question he salaam and whine their verdict be that the evidence be inconclusive and the judge concur it be true that the dead body of little tobrah ’s sister have be find at the bottom of the well and little tobrah be the only human be within half mile radius at the time but the child might have fall in by accident therefore little tobrah be acquit and tell to go where he please this permission be not so generous as it sound for he have nowhere to go to nothing in particular to eat and nothing whatever to wear he trot into the court compound and sit upon the well curb wonder whether an unsuccessful dive into the black water below would end in force voyage across the other black water groom put down an empty nose bag on the brick and little tobrah be hungry set himself to scrape out what wet grain the horse have overlook thief and but newly set free from the terror of the law come along say the groom and little tobrah be lead by the ear to large and fat englishman who hear the tale of the theft
hah say the englishman three time only he say strong word put he into the net and take he home so little tobrah be throw into the net of the cart and nothing doubt that he should be stick like pig be drive to the englishman ’s house
hah say the englishman as before wet grain by jove feed the little beggar some of you
and we ’ll make rid boy of he wet grain good lord give an account of yourself say the head of the groom to little tobrah after the meal have be eat and the servant lie at ease in their quarter behind the house you be not of the groom caste unless it be for the stomach ’s sake how come you into the court and why answer little devil ’s spawn there be not enough to eat say little tobrah calmly this be good place talk straight talk say the head groom or will make you clean out the stable of that large red stallion who bite like camel we be telis oil presser say little tobrah scratch his toe in the dust we be telis_—my father my mother my brother the elder by four year myself and the sister she who be find dead in the well say one who have hear something of the trial even so say little tobrah gravely she who be find dead in the well it befall upon time which be not in my memory that the sickness come to the village where our oil press stand and first my sister be smite as to her eye and go without sight for it be mata_—the small pox thereafter my father and my mother die of that same sickness so we be alone my brother who have twelve year who have eight and the sister who could not see yet be there the bullock and the oil press remain and we make shift to press the oil as before but surjun dass the grain seller cheat we in his dealing
and it be always stubborn bullock to drive we put marigold flower for the god upon the neck of the bullock and upon the great grind beam that rise through the roof but we gain nothing thereby and surjun dass be hard man bapri bap mutter the groom wife to cheat child so
but we know what the bunnia_-folk be sister the press be an old press and we be not strong man my brother and nor could we fix the neck of the beam firmly in the shackle nay indeed say the gorgeously clothe wife of the head groom join the circle that be strong man ’s work when be maid in my father ’s house peace woman say the head groom go on boy it be nothing say little tobrah the big beam tear down the roof upon day which be not in my memory and with the roof fall much of the hinder wall and both together upon our bullock whose back be break thus we have neither home nor press nor bullock my brother myself and the sister who be blind we go cry away from that place hand in hand across the field and our money be seven anna and six pie there be famine in the land do not know the name of the land so on night when we be sleep my brother take the five anna that remain to we and run away do not know whither he go the curse of my father be upon he
but and the sister beg food in the village and there be none to give only all man said—‘go to the englishman and they will give do not know what the englishman be
but they say that they be white live in tent go forward but can not say whither go and there be no more food for myself or the sister and upon hot night she weep and call for food we come to well and bid she sit upon the curb and thrust she in for in truth she could not see and it be well to die than to starve ai ahi wail the groom wife in chorus he thrust she in for it be well to die than to starve would have throw myself in also but that she be not dead and call to from the bottom of the well and be afraid and run and one come out of the crop say that have kill she and defile the well
and they take before an englishman white and terrible live in tent and he send here
but there be no witness and it be well to die than to starve she furthermore could not see with her eye and be but little child be but little child echo the head groom ’s wife but who art thou weak as fowl and small as day old colt what art thou who be empty be now full say little tobrah stretch himself upon the dust and would sleep the groom ’s wife spread cloth over he while little tobrah sleep the sleep of the just gemini great be the justice of the white man great the power of lie.—_native proverb this be your english justice protector of the poor look at my back and loin which be beat with stick heavy stick be poor man and there be no justice in court there be two of we and we be bear of one birth but swear to you that be bear the first and ram dass be the young by three full breath the astrologer say so and it be write in my horoscope the horoscope of durga dass
but we be alike and my brother who be beast without honour so alike that none know together or apart which be durga dass be mahajun of pali in marwar and an honest man this be true talk when we be man we leave our father ’s house in pali and go to the punjab where all the people be mud head and son of ass we take shop together in isser jang and my brother near the big well where the governor ’s camp draw water but ram dass who be without truth make quarrel with and we be divide he take his book and his pot and his mark and become bunnia_—a money lender in the long street of isser jang near the gateway of the road that go to montgomery it be not my fault that we pull each other ’s turban be mahajun of pali and always speak true talk ram dass be the thief and the liar now no man not even the little child could at one glance see which be ram dass and which be durga dass but all the people of isser jang may they die without sons!—said that we be thief they use much bad talk but take money on their bedstead and their cooking pot and the stand crop and the calf unborn from the well in the big square to the gate of the montgomery road they be fool these people unfit to cut the toe nail of marwari from pali lend money to they all little very little only here pice and there pice god be my witness that be poor man the money be all with ram dass may his son turn christian and his daughter be burn fire and shame in the house from generation to generation may she die unwed and be the mother of multitude of bastard let the light go out in the house of ram dass my brother this pray daily twice with offering and charm thus the trouble begin we divide the town of isser jang between we and my brother there be landholder beyond the gate living but one short mile out on the road that lead to montgomery and his name be muhammad shah son of nawab he be great devil and drink wine so long as there be woman in his house and wine and money for the marriage feast he be merry and wipe his mouth ram dass lend he the money lakh or half lakh how do know?—and so long as the money be lend the landholder care not what he sign the people of isser jang be my portion and the landholder and the out town be the portion of ram dass for
so we have arrange be the poor man for the people of isser jang be without wealth do what could but ram dass have only to wait without the door of the landholder ’s garden court and to lend he the money take the bond from the hand of the steward in the autumn of the year after the lending ram dass say to the landholder pay my money but the landholder give he abuse but ram dass go into the court with the paper and the bond all correct and take out decree against the landholder and the name of the government be across the stamp of the decree ram dass take field by field and mango tree by mango tree and well by well put in his own man debtor of the out town of isser jang to cultivate the crop so he creep up across the land for he have the paper and the name of the government be across the stamp till his man hold the crop for he on all side of the big white house of the landholder it be well do but when the landholder see these thing he be very angry and curse ram dass after the manner of the muhammadan and thus the landholder be angry but ram dass laugh and claim more field as be write upon the bond this be in the month of phagun take my horse and go out to speak to the man who make lac bangle upon the road that lead to montgomery because he owe debt there be in front of upon his horse my brother ram dass and when he see he turn aside into the high crop because there be hatred between we and go forward till come to the orange bush by the landholder ’s house the bat be fly and the evening smoke be low down upon the land here meet four man swashbuckler and muhammadan with their face bind up lay hold of my horse ’s bridle and cry out this be ram dass beat they beat with their stave heavy stave bind about with wire at the end such weapon as those swine of punjabi use till have cry for mercy fall down senseless
but these shameless one still beat say ram dass this be your interest well weigh and count into your hand ram dass cry aloud that be not ram dass but durga dass his brother yet they only beat the more and when could make no more outcry they leave but see their face there be elahi baksh who run by the side of the landholder ’s white horse and nur ali the keeper of the door and wajib ali the very strong cook and abdul latif the messenger all of the household of the landholder these thing can swear on the cow ’s tail if need be but—_ahi ahi!_—it have be already swear and be poor man whose honour be lose when these four have go away laugh my brother ram dass come out of the crop and mourn over as one dead but open my eye and pray he to get water when have drunk he carry on his back and by byway bring into the town of isser jang my heart be turn to ram dass my brother in that hour because of his kindness and lose my enmity but snake be snake till it be dead and liar be liar till the judgment of the god take hold of his heel be wrong in that trust my brother the son of my mother when we have come to his house and be little restore tell he my tale
and he say without doubt it be whom they would have beat
but the law court be open and there be the justice of the sirkar above all and to the law court do thou go when this sickness be over past now when we two have leave pali in the old year there fall famine that run from jeysulmir to gurgaon and touch gogunda in the south at that time the sister of my father come away and live with we in isser jang for man must above all see that his folk do not die of want when the quarrel between us twain come about the sister of my father lean she dog without tooth say that ram dass have the right and go with he into her hand because she know medicine and many cure ram dass my brother put faint with the beating and much bruise even to the pouring of blood from the mouth when have two day sickness the fever come upon and set aside the fever to the account write in my mind against the landholder the punjabi of isser jang be all the son of belial and she ass
but they be very good witness bear testimony unshakingly whatever the pleader may say would purchase witness by the score and each man should give evidence not only against nur ali wajib ali abdul latif and elahi baksh but against the landholder say that he upon his white horse have call his man to beat and far that they have rob of two hundred rupee for the latter testimony would remit little of the debt of the man who sell the lac bangle
and he should say that he have put the money into my hand and have see the robbery from afar but be afraid have run away this plan tell to my brother ram dass and he say that the arrangement be good and bid take comfort and make swift work to be abroad again my heart be open to my brother in my sickness and tell he the name of those whom would call as witness all man in my debt but of that the magistrate sahib could have no knowledge nor the landholder the fever stay with and after the fever be take with colic and grip very terrible in that day think that my end be at hand but know now that she who give the medicine the sister of my father widow with widow ’s heart have bre about my second sickness ram dass
my brother say that my house be shut and lock and bring the big door key and my book together with all the money that be in my house even the money that be bury under the floor for be in great fear lest thief should break in and dig speak true talk there be but very little money in my house perhaps ten rupee perhaps twenty how can tell god be my witness that be poor man one night when have tell ram dass all that be in my heart of the lawsuit that would bring against the landholder and ram dass have say that he have make the arrangement with the witness give their name write be take with new great sickness and they put on the bed when be little recover can not tell how many day afterwards make enquiry for ram dass and the sister of my father say that he have go to montgomery upon lawsuit take medicine and sleep very heavily without wake when my eye be open there be great stillness in the house of ram dass and none answer when call not even the sister of my father this fill with fear for know not what have happen take stick in my hand go out slowly till come to the great square by the well and my heart be hot in against the landholder because of the pain of every step take call for jowar singh the carpenter whose name be first upon the list of those who should bear evidence against the landholder say be all thing ready and do you know what should be say jowar singh answer
what be this and whence do you come durga dass say from my bed where have so long lie sick because of the landholder where be ram dass my brother who be to have make the arrangement for the witness surely you and yours know these thing then jowar singh say what have this to do with we liar have bear witness and have be pay and the landholder have by the order of the court pay both the five hundred rupee that he rob from ram dass and yet other five hundred because of the great injury he do to your brother the well and the jujube tree above it and the square of isser jang become dark in my eye but lean on my stick and say nay this be child ’s talk and senseless it be who suffer at the hand of the landholder and be come to make ready the case where be my brother ram dass but jowar singh shake his head and woman cry what lie be here what quarrel have the landholder with you bunnia it be only shameless one and one without faith who profit by his brother ’s smart have these bunnia no bowel cry again say by the cow by the oath of the cow by the temple of the blue throate mahadeo and only be beat beat to the death let your talk be straight people of isser jang and will pay for the witness and totter where stand for the sickness and the pain of the beating be heavy upon then ram narain who have his carpet spread under the jujube tree by the well and write all letter for the man of the town come up and say to day be the one and fortieth day since the beating and since these six day the case have be judge in the court and the assistant commissioner sahib have give it for your brother ram dass allow the robbery to which too bear witness and all thing else as the witness say there be many witness and twice ram dass become senseless in the court because of his wound and the stunt sahib the baba stunt sahib give he chair before all the pleader why do you howl durga dass these thing fall as have say be it not so and jowar singh say that be truth be there and there be red cushion in the chair and ram narain say great shame have come upon the landholder because of this judgment and fear his anger ram dass and all his house have go back to pali ram dass tell we that you also have go first the enmity be heal between you to open shop in pali indeed it be well for you that you go even now for the landholder have swear that if he catch any one of your house he will hang he by the heel from the well beam and swing he to and fro will beat he with stave till the blood run from his ear what have say in respect to the case be true as these man here can testify even to the five hundred rupee say be it five hundred and kirpa ram the jat say five hundred for bear witness also and groan for it have be in my heart to have say two hundred only then new fear come upon and my bowel turn to water and run swiftly to the house of ram dass seek for my book and my money in the great wooden chest under my bedstead there remain nothing not even cowrie ’s value all have be take by the devil who say he be my brother go to my own house also and open the board of the shutter
but there also be nothing save the rat among the grain basket in that hour
my sens leave and tear my clothe run to the well place cry out for the justice of the english on my brother ram dass and in my madness tell all that the book be lose when man see that would have jump down
the well they believe the truth of my talk more especially because upon my back and bosom be still the mark of the stave of the landholder jowar singh the carpenter withstand and turn in his hand for he be very strong man show the scar upon my body and bow down with laughter upon the well curb he cry aloud so that all hear he from the well square to the caravanserai of the pilgrim oho the jackal have quarrel and the gray one have be catch in the trap in truth this man have be grievously beat and his brother have take the money which the court decree
oh bunnia
this shall be tell for year against you the jackal have quarrel
and moreover the book be burn people indebted to durga dass and know that ye be many the book be burn then all isser jang take up the cry that the book be burned—_ahi ahi that in my folly have let that escape my mouth and they laugh throughout the city they give the abuse of the punjabi which be terrible abuse and very hot pelt also with stick and cow dung till fall down and cry for mercy ram narain the letter writer bid the people cease for fear that the news should get into montgomery and the policeman might come down to enquire he say use many bad word this much mercy will do to you durga dass though there be no mercy in your dealing with my sister ’s son over the matter of the dun heifer have any man pony on which he set no store that this fellow may escape if the landholder hear that one of the twain and god know whether he beat one or both but this man be certainly beat be in the city there will be murder do and then will come the police make inquisition into each man ’s house and eat the sweet seller ’s stuff all day long kirpa ram the jat say have pony very sick but with beat he can be make to walk for two mile if he die the hide seller will have the body then chumbo the hide seller say will pay three annas for the body and will walk by this man ’s side till such time as the pony die if it be more than two mile will pay two anna only kirpa ram say be it so man bring out the pony and ask leave to draw little water from the well because be dry up with fear then ram narain say here be four anna god have bring you very low durga dass and would not send you away empty even though the matter of my sister ’s son ’s dun heifer be an open sore between we it be long way to your own country go and if it be so willed live but above all do not take the pony ’s bridle for that be mine and go out of isser jang amid the laughing of the huge thighe jat and the hide seller walk by my side wait for the pony to fall dead in one mile it die and be full of fear of the landholder run till could run no more and come to this place but swear by the cow swear by all thing whereon hindus and musalman and even the sahib swear that and not my brother be beat by the landholder
but the case be shut and the door of the law court be shut and god know where the baba stunt sahib the mother ’s milk be not yet dry upon his hairless lip be go ahi ahi have no witness and the scar will heal and be poor man but on my father ’s soul on the oath of mahajun from pali and not my brother be beat by the landholder what can do the justice of the english be as great river having go forward it do not return
howbeit do you sahib take pen and write clearly what have say that the dipty sahib may see and remove the stunt sahib who be colt yet unlicke by the mare so young be he and not my brother be beat and he be go to the west do not know where but above all thing write so that sahib may read and his disgrace be accomplish that ram dass my brother son of purun dass mahajun of pali be swine and night thief taker of life an eater of flesh jackal spawn without beauty or faith or cleanliness or honour the limitation of pambé serang copyright by macmillan co. if you consider the circumstance of the case it be the only thing that he could do but pambé serang have be hang by the neck till he be dead and nurkeed be dead also three year ago when the elsass lothringen steamer saarbruck be coal at aden and the weather be very hot
indeed nurkeed the big fat zanzibar stoker who feed the second right furnace thirty foot down in the hold get leave to go ashore he depart seedee boy as they call the stoker he return the full blooded sultan of zanzibar his highness sayyid burgash with bottle in each hand
then he sit on the fore hatch grating eat salt fish and onion and sing the song of far country the food belong to pambé the serang or head man of the lascar sailor he have just cook it for himself turn to borrow some salt and when he come back nurkeed dirty black finger be spade into the rice serang be person of importance far above stoker though the stoker draw well pay he set the chorus of hya hulla
hee ah heh when the captain ’s gig be pull up to the davit he heave the lead too and sometimes when all the ship be lazy he put on his white muslin and big red sash and play with the passenger child on the quarter deck
then the passenger give he money and he save it all up for an orgy at bombay or calcutta or pulu penang ho you fat black barrel you ’re eat my food say pambé in the other lingua franca that begin where the levant tongue stop and run from port say eastward till east be west and the sealing brig of the kurile island gossip with the stray hakodate junk son of eblis monkey face dry shark ’s liver pig man be the sultan sayyid burgash and the commander of all this ship take away your garbage and nurkeed thrust the empty pewter rice plate into pambé ’s hand pambé beat it into basin over nurkeed woolly head nurkeed draw his sheath knife and stab pambé in the leg pambé draw his sheath knife but nurkeed drop down into the darkness of the hold and spit through the grating at pambé who be stain the clean fore deck with his blood only the white moon see these thing for the officer be look after the coaling and the passenger be tos in their close cabin all right say pambé and go forward to tie up his leg—“we will settle the account later on he be malay bear in india marry once in burma where his wife have cigar shop on the shwe dagon road once in singapore to chinese girl and once in madras to mahomedan woman who sell fowl the english sailor can not owe to postal and telegraph facility marry as profusely as he use to do but native sailor can be uninfluence by the barbarous invention of the western savage pambé be good husband when he happen to remember the existence of wife but he be also very good malay and it be not wise to offend malay because he do not forget anything moreover in pambé ’s case blood have be draw and food spoil next morning nurkeed rise with blank mind he be no long sultan of zanzibar but very hot stoker so he go on deck and open his jacket to the morning breeze till sheath knife come like fly fish and stick into the wood work of the cook ’s galley half an inch from his right armpit he run down below before his time try to remember what he could have say to the owner of the weapon at noon when all the ship ’s lascar be feed nurkeed advance into their midst and be placid man with large regard for his own skin he open negotiation say man of the ship last night be drunk and this morning know that behave unseemly to some one or another of you who be that man that may meet he face to face and say that be drunk pambé measure the distance to nurkeed ’s naked breast if he spring at he he might be trip up and blind blow at the chest sometimes only mean gash on the breast bone rib be difficult to thrust between unless the subject be asleep so he say nothing nor do the other lascar their face immediately drop all expression as be the custom of the oriental when there be kill on the carpet or any chance of trouble nurkeed look long at the white eyeball he be only an african and could not read character big sigh almost groan break from he and he go back to the furnace the lascar take up the conversation where he have interrupt it they talk of the good method of cook rice nurkeed suffer considerably from lack of fresh air during the run to bombay he only come on deck to breathe when all the world be about and even then heavy block once drop from derrick within foot of his head and an apparently firm lash grate on which he set his foot begin to turn over with the intention of drop he on the cased cargo fifteen foot below and one insupportable night the sheath knife drop from the fo’c’s’le and this time it draw blood so nurkeed make complaint and when the saarbruck reach bombay flee and bury himself among eight hundred thousand people and do not sign article till the ship have be month go from the port pambé wait too but his bombay wife grow clamorous and he be force to sign in the spicheren to hongkong because he realise that all play and no work give jack ragged shirt in the foggy china sea he think great deal of nurkeed and when elsass lothringen steamer lie in port with the spicheren inquire after he and find he have go to england via the cape on the gravelotte pambé come to england on the worth the spicheren meet she by the nore light nurkeed be go out with the spicheren to the calicut coast want to find friend my trap mouthed coal scuttle say gentleman in the mercantile service nothing easy wait at the nyanza dock till he come every one come to the nyanza dock wait you poor heathen the gentleman speak truth there be three great door in the world where if you stand long enough you shall meet any one you wish the head of the suez canal be one but there death come also char cross station be the second for inland work and the nyanza dock be the third at each of these place be man and woman look eternally for those who will surely come so pambé wait at the dock time be no object to he and the wife could wait as he do from day to day week to week and month to month by the blue diamond funnel the red dot smoke stack the yellow streak and the nameless dingy gypsy of the sea that load and unload jostle whistle and roar in the everlasting fog when money fail kind gentleman tell pambé to become christian and pambé become one with great speed get his religious teaching between ship and ship ’s arrival and six or seven shilling week for distribute tract to mariner what the faith be pambé do not in the least care
but he know if he say native ki lis ti an sar to man with long black coat he might get few copper and the tract be vendible at little public house that sell shag by the dottel which be even small weight than the half screw which be less than the half ounce and most profitable retail trade but after eight month pambé fall sick with pneumonia contract from long stand still in slush and much against his will he be force to lie down in his two and sixpenny room rag against fate the kind gentleman sit by his bedside and grieve to find that pambé talk in strange tongue instead of listen to good book and almost seem to become benighted heathen again till one day he be rouse from semi stupor by voice in the street by the dock head my friend he whisper pambé call now call nurkeed god have send he he want one of his own race say the kind gentleman and go out he call nurkeed at the top of his voice an excessively coloured man in rasping white shirt and brand new slop shin hat and breast pin turn round many voyage have teach nurkeed how to spend his money and make he citizen of the world
yes say he when the situation be explain command he black nigger when be in the saarbruck ole pambé good ole pambé dam lascar show he up sar
and he follow into the room one glance tell the stoker what the kind gentleman have overlook pambé be desperately poor nurkeed drive his hand deep into his pocket then advance with clenched fist on the sick shout hya pambé hya
