dekho jee dam big fat lazy lascar pambé beckon with his left hand his right be under his pillow nurkeed remove his gorgeous hat and stoop over pambé till he could catch faint whisper how beautiful say the kind gentleman how these oriental love like child spit he out say nurkeed lean over pambé yet more closely touch the matter of that fish and onion say pambé and send the knife home under the edge of the rib bone upwards and forwards there be thick sick cough and the body of the african slide slowly from the bed his clutch hand let fall shower of silver piece that run across the room now can die say pambé
but he do not die he be nurse back to life with all the skill that money could buy for the law want he and in the end he grow sufficiently healthy to be hang in due and proper form pambé do not care particularly
but it be sad blow to the kind gentleman one view of the question copyright by d. appleton co. from shafiz ullah khan son of hyat ullah khan in the honoured service of his highness the rao sahib of jagesur which be in the northern border of hindustan and orderly to his highness this to kazi jamal ud din son of kazi ferisht ud din khan in the service of the rao sahib minister much honour from that place which they call the northbrook club in the town of london under the shadow of the empress it be write between brother and choose brother be no long protestation of love and sincerity heart speak naked to heart and the head answer for all glory and honour on thy house till the ending of the year and tent in the border of paradise my brother,—in regard to that for which be despatch follow the account have purchase for the rao sahib and pay sixty pound in every hundred the thing he most desire thus two of the great fawn colour tiger dog male and female their pedigree be write upon paper and silver collar adorn their neck for the rao sahib ’s great pleasure send they at once by the steamer in charge of man who will render account of they at bombay to the banker there they be the good of all dog in this place of gun have buy five two silver sprig in the stock with gold scroll work about the hammer both double barrelled hard strike case in velvet and red leather three of unequalled workmanship but lack adornment pump gun that fire fourteen time this when the rao sahib drive pig double barrelled shell gun for tiger and that be miracle of workmanship and fowling piece no light than feather with green and blue cartridge by the thousand also very small rifle for blackbuck that yet would slay man at four hundred pace the harness with the golden crest for the rao sahib ’s coach be not yet complete by reason of the difficulty of line the red velvet into leather but the two horse harness and the great saddle with the golden holster that be for state use have be put with camphor into tin box and have sign it with my ring of the grain leather case of woman ’s tool and tweezer for the hair and beard of the perfume and the silk and all that be want by the woman behind the curtain have no knowledge they be matter of long come and the hawk bell hood and jesse with the golden lettering be as much delay as they read this in the rao sahib ’s ear and speak of my diligence and zeal that favour may not be abate by absence and keep the eye of constraint upon that jesting dog without tooth bahadur shah for by thy aid and voice and what have do in regard to the gun look as thou know for the headship of the army of jagesur that conscienceless one desire it also and have hear that the rao sahib lean thatward have ye do then with the drinking of wine in your house my brother or have bahadur shah become forswearer of brandy would not that drink should end he but the well mix draught lead to madness and now in regard to this land of the sahib follow that thou hast demand god be my witness that have strive to understand all that see and little of what hear my word and intention be those of truth
yet it may be that write of nothing but lie since the first wonder and bewilderment of my beholding be go we note the jewel in the ceiling dome but later the filth on the floor see clearly that this town london which be as large as all jagesur be accursed be dark and unclean devoid of sun and full of low bear who be perpetually drunk and howl in the street like jackal man and woman together at nightfall it be the custom of countless thousand of woman to descend into the street and sweep they roar make jest and demand liquor at the hour of this attack it be the custom of the householder to take their wife and child to the playhouse and the place of entertainment evil and good thus return home together as do kine from the pool at sundown have never see any sight like this sight in all the world and doubt that double be to be find on the hither side of the gate of hell touch the mystery of their craft it be an ancient one but the householder assemble in herd be man and woman and cry aloud to their god that it be not there the say woman pound at the door without moreover upon the day when they go to prayer the drink place be only open when the mosque be shut as who should dam the jumna river for friday only therefore the man and woman be force to accomplish their desire in the short space become the more furiously drunk and roll in the gutter together they be there regard by those go to pray far and for visible sign that the place be forget of god there fall upon certain day without warn cold darkness whereby the sun ’s light be altogether cut off from all the city and the people male and female and the driver of the vehicle grope and howl in this pit at high noon none see the other the air be fill with the smoke of hell sulphur and pitch as it be write they die speedily with gasp and so be bury in the dark this be terror beyond the pen but by my hand write of what have see it be not true that the sahib worship one god as do we of the faith or that the difference in their creed be like those now run between shiah and sunni be but fight man and no darvesh care as thou knowest as much for shiah as sunni but have speak to many people of the nature of their god one there be who be the head of the mukht fauj,[2 and he be worship by man in blood red clothe who shout and become without sense another be an image before whom they burn candle and incense in just such place as have see when go to rangoon to buy burma pony for the rao yet third have naked altar face great assembly of dead to he they sing chiefly and for other there be woman who be the mother of the great prophet that be before mahomed the common folk have no god but worship those who may speak to they hang from the lamp in the street the most wise people worship themselves and such thing as they have make with their mouth and their hand and this be to be find notably among the barren woman of whom there be many thou wilt not believe this my brother nor do when be first tell
but now it be nothing to so greatly have the foot of travel let out the stirrup hole of belief footnote salvation army but thou wilt say what matter to we whether ahmed ’s beard or mahmud ’s be the long speak what thou canst of the accomplishment of desire would that thou wert here to talk face to face to walk abroad with and learn with this people it be matter of heaven and hell whether ahmed ’s beard and mahmud ’s tally or differ but by hair thou knowest the system of their statecraft it be this certain man appoint themselves go about and speak to the low bear the peasant the leather worker and the cloth dealer and the woman say give we leave by your favour to speak for you in the council secure that permission by large promise they return to the council place and sit unarmed some six hundred together speak at random each for himself and his own ball of low bear the vizier and dewan of the empress must ever beg money at their hand for unless more than half of the six hundred be of one heart towards the spending of the revenue neither horse can be shod rifle load or man clothe throughout the land remember this very continually the six hundred be above the empress above the viceroy of india above the head of the army and every other power that thou hast ever know because they hold the revenue they be divide into two horde the one perpetually hurl abuse at the other and bid the low bear hamper and rebel against all that the other may devise for government except that they sit unarmed and so call each other liar dog and bastard without fear even under the shadow of the empress ’s throne they be at bitter war which be without any end they pit lie against lie till the low bear and common folk grow drunk with lie and in their turn begin to lie and refuse to pay the revenue far they divide their woman into band and send they into this fight with yellow flower in their hand and since the belief of woman be but her lover ’s belief strip of judgment very many wild word be add well say the slave girl to mámún in the delectable page of the son of abdullah oppression and the sword slay fast thy breath kill slowly but at last if they desire thing they declare that it be true if they desire it not though that be death itself they cry aloud it have never be thus their talk be the talk of child and like child they snatch at what they covet not consider whether it be their own or another and in their council when the army of unreason have come to the defile of dispute and there be no more talk leave on either side they divide count head and the will of that side which have the large number of head make that law
but the outnumber side run speedily among the common people and bid they trample on that law and slay the officer thereof follow slaughter by night of man unarmed and the slaughter of cattle and insult to woman they do not cut off the nose of woman but they crop their hair and scrape the flesh with pin then those shameless one of the council stand up before the judge wipe their mouth and make oath they say before god we be free from blame do we say heave that stone out of that road and kill that one and no other
so they be not make short by the head because they say only here be stone and yonder be such fellow obey the law which be no law because we do not desire it read this in the rao sahib ’s ear and ask he if he remember that season when the manglôt headman refuse revenue not because they could not pay but because they judge the cess extreme and thou go out with the trooper all one day and the black lance raise the thatch so that there be hardly any need of firing and no man be slay but this land be at secret war and veil killing in five year of peace they have slay within their own border and of their own kin more man than would have fall have the ball of dissension be leave to the mallet of the army and yet there be no hope of peace for soon the side again divide
and then they will cause to be slay more man unarmed and in the field and so much for that matter which be to our advantage there be well thing to be tell and one tend to the accomplishment of desire read here with fresh mind after sleep write as understand above all this war without honour lie that which find hard to put into writing and thou knowest be unhandy of the pen will ride the steed of inability sideways at the wall of expression the earth underfoot be sick and sour with the much handling of man as graze ground sour under cattle and the air be sick too upon the ground they have lie in this town as it be the stinking board of stable and through these board between thousand thousand house the rank humour of the earth sweat through to the over burden air that return they to their breeding place for the smoke of their cooking fire keep all in as the cover the juice of the sheep and in like manner there be green sickness among the people and especially among the six hundred man who talk neither winter nor autumn abate that malady of the soul have see it among woman in our own country and in boy not yet blooded to the sword but have never see so much thereof before through the peculiar operation of this air the people abandon honour and steadfastness question all authority not as man question but as girl whimperingly with pinch in the back when the back be turn and mow if one cry in the street there have be an injustice they take he not to make complaint to those appoint but all who pass drink his word fly clamorously to the house of the accuse and write evil thing of he his wife and his daughter for they take no thought to the weighing of evidence but be as woman and with one hand they beat their constable who guard the street and with the other beat the constable for resent that beating and fine they when they have in all thing make light of the state they cry to the state for help and it be give so that the next time they will cry more such as be oppress riot through the street bear banner that hold four day labour and week ’s bread in cost and toil and when neither horse nor foot can pass by they be satisfied other receive wage refuse to work till they get more and the priest help they and also man of the six hundred for where rebellion be one of those man will come as kite to dead bullock and priest talker and man together declare that it be right because these will not work that no other may attempt in this manner they have so confuse the loading and the unloading of the ship that come to this town that in send the rao sahib ’s gun and harness see fit to send the case by the train to another ship that sail from another place there be now no certainty in any send but who injure the merchant shut the door of well be on the city and the army and ye know what sa’adi saith how may the merchant westward fare when he hear the tale of the tumult there no man can keep faith because he can not tell how his underling will go they have make the servant great than the master for that he be the servant not reckon that each be equal under god to the appoint task that be thing to be put aside in the cupboard of the mind far the misery and outcry of the common folk of whom the earth ’s bosom be weary have so work upon the mind of certain people who have never sleep under fear nor see the flat edge of the sword on the head of mob that they cry out let we abate everything that be and altogether labour with our bare hand their hand in that employ would fester at the second stroke and have see for all their unrest at the agony of other that they abandon no whit of soft living unknowe the common folk or indeed the mind of man they offer strong drink of word such as they themselves use to empty belly and that wine breed drunkenness of soul the distressful person stand all day long at the door of the drink place to the number of very many thousand the well wish people of small discernment give they word or pitifully attempt in school to turn they into craftsman weaver or builder of whom there be more than enough yet they have not the wisdom to look at the hand of the teach whereon man ’s craft and that of his father be write by god and necessity they believe that the son of drunkard shall drive straight chisel and the charioteer do plaster work they take no thought in the dispensation of generosity which be as the closed finger of water scoop palm therefore the rough timber of very great army drift unhewn through the slime of their street if the government which be to day and to morrow change spend on these hopeless one some money to clothe and equip should not write what write
but these people despise the trade of arm and rest content with the memory of old battle the woman and the talk man aid they thou wilt say why speak continually of woman and fool answer by god the fashioner of the heart the fool sit among the six hundred and the woman sway their council hast thou forget when the order come across the sea that rot out the army of the english with we so that soldier fall sick by the hundred where but ten have sicken before that be the work of not more than twenty of the man and some fifty of the barren woman have see three or four of they male and female and they triumph openly in the name of their god because three regiment of the white troop be not this be to our advantage because the sword with the rust spot break over the turban of the enemy but if they thus tear their own flesh and blood ere their madness be rise to its height what will they do when the moon be full see that power lie in the hand of the six hundred and not in the viceroy or elsewhere have throughout my stay seek the shadow of those among they who talk most and most extravagantly they lead the common folk and receive permission of their good will it be the desire of some of these man indeed of almost as many as cause the rotting of the english army that our land and people should accurately resemble those of the english upon this very day may god the contemner of folly forbid myself be account show among they and of we and our they know naught some call hindu and other rajput and use towards in ignorance slave talk and expression of great disrespect some of they be well bear but the great part be low bear coarse skin wave their arm high voice without dignity slack in the mouth shifty eyed and as have say sway by the wind of woman ’s cloak now this be tale but two day old there be company at meat and high voice woman speak to in the face of the man of the affair of our womankind it be her ignorance that make each word an edge insult remember this hold my peace till she have speak new law as to the control of our zenanas and of all who be behind the curtain then i—“hast thou ever feel the life stir under thy heart or lay little son between thy breast most unhappy thereto she hotly with haggard eye—“no for be free woman and no servant of babe then softly—“god deal lightly with thee my sister for thou art in heavy bondage than any slave and the full half of the earth be hide from thee the first ten year of the life of man be his mother ’s and from the dusk to the dawn surely the wife may command the husband be it great thing to stand back in the wake hour while the man go abroad unhampere by thy hand on the bridle rein then she wonder that heathen should speak thus yet she be woman honour among these man and openly profess that she hath no profession of faith in her mouth read this in the ear of the rao sahib and demand how it would fare with if bring such woman for his use it be bad than that yellow desert breed girl from cutch who set the girl to fight for her own pleasure and slippere the young prince across the mouth rememberest thou in truth the fountain head of power be putrid with long stand still these man and woman would make of all india dung cake and would fain leave the mark of the finger upon it and they have power and the control of the revenue and that be why be so particular in description they have power over all india of what they speak they understand nothing for the low bear ’s soul be bind by his field and he grasp not the connection of affair from pole to pole they boast openly that the viceroy and the other be their servant when the master be mad what shall the servant do some hold that all war be sin and death the great fear under god other declare with the prophet that it be evil to drink to which teach their street bear evident witness and other there be specially the low bear who aver that all dominion be wicked and sovereignty of the sword accurse these protest to make as it be an apology that their kin should hold hindustan and hop that some day they would withdraw know well the breed of white man in our border would have laugh but forbore remember that these speaker have power in the counting of head yet other cry aloud against the taxation of hindustan under the sahib rule to this assent remember the yearly mercy of the rao sahib when the turban of the trooper come through the blight corn and the woman ’s anklet go into the melting pot but be no good speaker that be the duty of the boy from bengal hill ass with an eastern bray mahratta from poona and the like these move among fool represent themselves as the son of some one be beggar teach offspring of grain dealer currier seller of bottle and money lender as thou know now we of jagesur owe naught save friendship to the english who take we by the sword and having take we let we go assure the rao sahib ’s succession for all time
but these base bear having win their learning through the mercy of the government attire in english clothe forswear the faith of their father for gain spread rumour and debate against the government and be therefore very dear to certain of the six hundred have hear these cattle speak as prince and ruler of man and have laugh but not altogether once it happen that son of some grain bag sit with at meat who be array and speak after the manner of the english at each mouthful he commit perjury against the salt that he have eat the man and woman applaud when craftily falsify he have magnify oppression and invent untold wrong together with the desecration of his tun belly god he demand in the name of his people the government of all our land and turn lay palm to my shoulder saying—“here be one who be with we albeit he profess another faith he will bear out my word this he deliver in english and as it be exhibit to that company preserve smile countenance answer in our own tongue—“take away that hand man without father or the folly of these folk shall not save thee nor my silence guard
thy reputation sit off herd and in their speech said—“he speak truth when the favour and wisdom of the english allow we yet little large share in the burden and the reward the musalman will deal with the hindu he alone see what be in my heart be merciful towards he because he be accomplish our desire but remember that his father be one durga charan laha in calcutta lie thy hand upon his shoulder if ever chance send it be not good that bottle dealer and auctioneer should paw the son of prince walk abroad sometimes with the man that all the world may know the hindu and musalman be one but when we come to the unfrequente street bid he walk behind and that be sufficient honour and why do eat dirt thus my brother it seem to my heart which have almost burst in the consideration of these matter the bengalis and the beggar teach boy know well that the sahib power to govern come neither from the viceroy nor the head of the army but from the hand of the six hundred in this town and peculiarly those who talk most they will therefore yearly address themselves more and more to that protection and work on the green sickness of the land as have ever be their custom will in time cause through the perpetually instigate interference of the six hundred the hand of the indian government to become inoperative so that no measure nor order may be carry through without clamour and argument on their part for that be the delight of the english at this hour have overset the bound of possibility even thou must have hear that one of the six hundred have neither knowledge fear nor reverence before his eye have make in sport new and write scheme for the government of bengal and openly show it abroad as king might read his crowning proclamation and this man meddle in affair of state speak in the council for an assemblage of leather dresser maker of boot and harness and openly glory in that he have no god have either minister of the empress empress viceroy or any other raise voice against this leather man be not his power therefore to be seek and that of his like thinker with it thou se the telegraph be the servant of the six hundred and all the sahib in india omit not one be the servant of the telegraph
yearly too thou know the beggar teach will hold that which they call their congress first at one place and then at another leaven hindustan with rumour echo the talk among the low bear people here and demand that they like the six hundred control the revenue and they will bring every point and letter over the head of the governor and the lieutenant governor and whoever hold authority and cast it clamorously at the foot of the six hundred here and certain of those word confounder and the barren woman will assent to their demand and other will weary of disagreement thus fresh confusion will be throw into the council of the empress even as an island near by be help and comfort into the smother war of which have write then yearly as they have begin and we have see the low bear man of the six hundred anxious for honour will embark for our land and stay little while will gather round they and fawn before the beggar teach and these depart from their side will assuredly inform the peasant and the fight man for whom there be no employ that there be change toward and come of help from over the sea that rumour will not grow small in the spread and most of all the congress when it be not under the eye of the six hundred who though they foment dissension and death pretend great reverence for the law which be no law will step aside deliver uneasy word to the peasant speak as it have do already of the remission of taxation and promise new rule that be to our advantage but the flower of danger be in the seed of it thou knowest what evil rumour may do though in the black year when thou and be young our standing to the english bring gain to jagesur and enlarge our border for the government give we land on both side of the congress itself
nothing be to be fear that ten trooper could not remove but if its word too soon perturb the mind of those wait or of prince in idleness flame may come before the time and since there be now many white hand to quench it all will return to the former condition if the flame be keep under we need have no fear because sweat and pant the one trample on the other the white people here be dig their own graf the hand of the viceroy will be tie the heart of the sahib will be downcast and all eye will turn to england disregard any order meantime keep tally on the sword hilt against the hour when the score must be make smooth by the blade it be well for we to assist and greatly befriend the bengali that he may get control of the revenue and the post we must even write to england that we be of one blood with the schoolman it be not long to wait by my head it be not long this people be like the great king ferisht who eat with the scab of long idleness pluck off his crown and dance naked among the dung hill but have not forget the profitable end of that tale the vizier set he upon horse and lead he into battle presently his health return
and he cause to be engraven on the crown though be cast away by the king yet through god return
and he add to my brilliance two great ruby balkh and iran if this people be purge and bleed out by battle their sickness may go and their eye be clear to the necessity of thing
but they be now far go in rottenness even the stallion too long heel rope forget how to fight and these man be mule do not lie when say that unless they be blee and teach with the whip they will hear and obey all that be say by the congress and the black man here hop to turn our land into their own orderless jehannum for the man of the six hundred be chiefly low bear and unused to authority desire much to exercise rule extend their arm to the sun and moon and shout very greatly in order to hear the echo of their voice each one say some new strange thing and part the good and honour of other among the rapacious that he may obtain the favour of the common folk and all this be to our advantage therefore write that they may read of gratitude and of love and the law myself when return will show how the dish should be dress to take the taste here for it be here that we must come cause to be establish in jagesur newspaper and fill it with translation of their paper beggar teach may be bre from calcutta for thirty rupee month and if he write in gurmukhi our people can not read create far council other than the panchayat of headman village by village and district by district instruct they beforehand what to say accord to the order of the rao print all these thing in book in english and send it to this place and to every man of the six hundred bid the beggar teach write in front of all that jagesur follow fast on the english plan if thou squeezest the hindu shrine at theegkot and it be ripe remit the head tax and perhaps the marriage tax with great publicity but above all thing keep the troop ready and in good pay even though we glean the stubble with the wheat and stint the rao sahib ’s woman all must go softly protest thou thy love for the voice of the common people in all thing and affect to despise the troop that shall be take for witness in this land the headship of the troop must be mine see that bahadur shah ’s wit go wander over the wine but do not send he to god be an old man but may yet live to lead if this people be not blee out and regain strength we watch how the tide run when we see that the shadow of their hand be all but lift from hindustan must bid the bengali demand the removal of the residue or set go an uneasiness to that end we must have care neither to hurt the life of the englishman nor the honour of their woman for in that case six time the six hundred here could not hold those who remain from make the land swim we must care that they be not mob by the bengalis but honourably escort while the land be hold down with the threat of the sword if hair of their head fall thus we shall gain good name and when rebellion be unaccompanie by bloodshed as have lately befall in far country the english disregard honour call it by new name even one who have be minister of the empress but be now at war against the law praise it openly before the common folk so greatly be they change since the day of nikhal seyn![3
and then if all go well and the sahib who through continual checking and browbeating will have grow sick at heart see themselves abandon by their kin for this people have allow their great to die on dry sand through delay and fear of expense we may go forward this people be sway by name new name therefore must be give to the rule of hindustan and that the bengalis may settle among themselves and there will be many writing and oath of love such as the little island over sea make when it would fight more bitterly and after that the residue be diminish the hour come
and we must strike so that the sword be never any more question footnote nicholson gentleman once of some notoriety in india by the favour of god and the conservation of the sahib these many year hindustan contain very much plunder which we can in no way eat hurriedly there will be to our hand the scaffolding of the house of state for the bengali shall continue to do our work and must account to we for the revenue and learn his seat in the order of thing whether the hindu king of the west will break in to share that spoil before we have sweep it altogether thou knowest well than but be certain that then strong hand will seek their own throne and it may be that the day of the king of delhi will return if we only curb our desire pay due obedience to the outward appearance and the name thou rememberest the old song hadst thou not call it love have say it be draw sword but since thou hast speak believe and die it be in my heart that there will remain in our land few sahib undesirous of return to england these we must cherish and protect that by their skill and cunning we may hold together and preserve unity in time of war the hindu king will never trust sahib in the core of their counsel say again that if we of the faith confide in they we shall trample upon our enemy be all this dream to thee gray fox of my mother ’s bearing have write of what have see and hear but from the same clay two man will never fashion platter alike nor from the same fact draw equal conclusion once more there be green sickness upon all the people of this country they eat dirt even now to stay their craving honour and stability have depart from their council and the knife of dissension have bre down upon their head the flap tent fly of confusion the empress be old they speak disrespectfully of she and hers in the street they despise the sword and believe that the tongue and the pen sway all the measure of their ignorance and their soft belief be great than the measure of the wisdom of solomon the son of david all these thing have see whom they regard as wild beast and spectacle by god the enlightener of intelligence if the sahib in india could breed son who live so that their house might be establish would almost fling my sword at the viceroy ’s foot say let we here fight for kingdom together thine and mine disregard the babble across the water write letter to england say that we love they but would depart from their camp and make all clean under new crown
but the sahib die out at the third generation in our land and it may be that dream dream yet not altogether until white calamity of steel and bloodshe the bearing of burden the trembling for life and the hot rage of insult—_for pestilence would unman they if eye not unused to man see clear_—befall this people our path be safe they be sick the fountain of power be gutter which all may defile and the voice of the man be overborne by the squealing of mule and the whinnying of barren mare if through adversity they become wise then my brother strike with and for they and later when thou and be dead and the disease grow up again the young man breed in the school of fear and trembling and word confound have yet to live out their appoint span those who have fight on the side of the english may ask and receive what they choose at present seek quietly to confuse and delay and evade and make of no effect in this business four score of the six hundred be our true helper now the pen and the ink and the hand weary together as thy eye will weary in this reading be it know to my house that return soon but do not speak of the hour letter without name have come to touch my honour the honour of my house be thine if they be as believe the work of dismiss groom futteh lal that run at the tail of my wine colour katthiawar stallion his village be beyond manglôt look to it that his tongue no long lengthen itself on the name of those who be mine if it be otherwise put guard upon my house till come and especially see that no seller of jewelry astrologer or midwife have entrance to the woman ’s room we rise by our slave and by our slave we fall as it be say to all who be of my remembrance bring gift accord to their worth have write twice of the gift that would cause to be give to bahadur shah the blessing of god and his prophet on thee and thine till the end which be appoint give felicity by inform of the state of thy health my head be at the rao sahib ’s foot my sword be at his left side little above my heart follow my seal on the city then she let they down by cord through the window for her house be upon the town wall
and she dwell upon the wall.—_joshua ii lalun be member of the most ancient profession in the world lilith be she very great grand mamma and that be before the day of eve as every one know in the west people say rude thing about lalun ’s profession and write lecture about it and distribute the lecture to young person in order that morality may be preserve in the east where the profession be hereditary descend from mother to daughter nobody write lecture or take any notice and that be distinct proof of the inability of the east to manage its own affair lalun ’s real husband for even lady of lalun ’s profession in the east must have husband be big jujube tree her mamma who have marry fig tree spend ten thousand rupee on lalun ’s wedding which be bles by forty seven clergyman of mamma ’s church and distribute five thousand rupee in charity to the poor and that be the custom of the land the advantage of have jujube tree for husband be obvious you can not hurt his feeling and he look impose lalun ’s husband stand on the plain outside the city wall and lalun ’s house be upon the east wall face the river if you fall from the broad window seat you drop thirty foot sheer into the city ditch but if you stay where you should and look forth you see all the cattle of the city be drive down to water the student of the government college playing cricket the high grass and tree that fringe the river bank the great sand bar that rib the river the red tomb of dead emperor beyond the river and very far away through the blue heat haze glint of the snow of the himalayas wali dad use to lie in the window seat for hour at time watch this view he be young muhammadan who be suffer acutely from education of the english variety and know it his father have send he to mission school to get wisdom and wali dad have absorb more than ever his father or the missionary intend he should when his father die wali dad be independent and spend two year experiment with the creed of the earth and read book that be of no use to anybody after he have make an unsuccessful attempt to enter the roman catholic church and the presbyterian fold at the same time the missionary find he out and call he name
but they do not understand his trouble he discover lalun on the city wall and become the most constant of her few admirer he possess head that english artist at home would rave over and paint amid impossible surround face that female novelist would use with delight through nine hundred page in reality he be only clean breed young muhammadan with pencil eyebrow small cut nostril little foot and hand and very tired look in his eye by virtue of his twenty two year he have grow neat black beard which he stroke with pride and keep delicately scent his life seem to be divide between borrow book from and make love to lalun in the window seat he compose song about she and some of the song be sing to this day in the city from the street of the mutton butcher to the copper smiths ward one song the pretty of all say that the beauty of lalun be so great that it trouble the heart of the british government and cause they to lose their peace of mind that be the way the song be sing in the street
but if you examine it carefully and know the key to the explanation you will find that there be three pun in it on beauty heart and peace of mind,”—so that it run by the subtlety of lalun the administration of the government be trouble and it lose such and such man when wali dad sing that song his eye glow like hot coal and lalun lean back among the cushion and throw bunch of jasmine bud at wali dad
but first it be necessary to explain something about the supreme government which be above all and below all and behind all gentleman come from england spend few week in india walk round this great sphinx of the plain and write book upon its way and its work denounce or praise it as their own ignorance prompt consequently all the world know how the supreme government conduct itself
but no one not even the supreme government know everything about the administration of the empire year by year england send out fresh draft for the first fighting line which be officially call the indian civil service these die or kill themselves by overwork or be worried to death or break in health and hope in order that the land may be protect from death and sickness famine and war and may eventually become capable of stand alone it will never stand alone but the idea be pretty one and man be willing to die for it and yearly the work of push and coax and scold and pet the country into good living go forward if an advance be make all credit be give to the native while the englishman stand back and wipe their forehead if failure occur the englishman step forward and take the blame overmuch tenderness of this kind have bree strong belief among many native that the native be capable of administer the country and many devout englishman believe this also because the theory be state in beautiful english with all the late political colour there be other man who though uneducated see vision and dream dream
and they too hope to administer the country in their own way that be to say with garnish of red sauce such man must exist among two hundred million people and if they be not attend to may cause trouble and even break the great idol call pax britannic which as the newspaper say live between peshawur and cape comorin be the day of doom to dawn to morrow you would find the supreme government take measure to allay popular excitement and put guard upon the graveyard that the dead might troop forth orderly the young civilian would arrest gabriel on his own responsibility if the archangel could not produce deputy commissioner ’s permission to make music or other noise as the license say whence it be easy to see that mere man of the flesh who would create tumult must fare badly at the hand of the supreme government
and they do there be no outward sign of excitement there be no confusion there be no knowledge when due and sufficient reason have be give weigh and approve the machinery move forward and the dreamer of dream and the seer of vision be go from his friend and follow he enjoy the hospitality of government there be no restriction upon his movement within certain limit but he must not confer any more with his brother dreamer once in every six month the supreme government assure itself that he be well and take formal acknowledgment of his existence no one protest against his detention because the few people who know about it be in deadly fear of seem to know he and never single newspaper take up his case or organise demonstration on his behalf because the newspaper of india have get behind that lie proverb which say the pen be mighty than the sword and can walk
delicately so now you know as much as you ought about wali dad the educational mixture and the supreme government lalun have not yet be describe she would need so wali dad say thousand pen of gold and ink scent with musk she have be variously compare to the moon the dil sagar lake spot quail gazelle the sun on the desert of kutch the dawn the star and the young bamboo these comparison imply that she be beautiful exceedingly accord to the native standard which be practically the same as those of the west her eye be black and her hair be black and her eyebrow be black as leech her mouth be tiny and say witty thing her hand be tiny and have save much money her foot be tiny and have tread on the naked heart of many man
but as wali dad sing lalun be lalun and when you have say that you have only come to the beginning of knowledge the little house on the city wall be just big enough to hold lalun and her maid and pussy cat with silver collar big pink and blue cut glass chandelier hang from the ceiling of the reception room petty nawab have give lalun the horror
and she keep it for politeness sake the floor of the room be of polished chunam white as curd lattice window of carve wood be set in one wall there be profusion of squabby pluffy cushion and fat carpet everywhere and lalun ’s silver huqa stud with turquoise have special little carpet all to its shin self wali dad be nearly as permanent fixture as the chandelier as have say he lie in the window seat and meditate on life and death and lalun specially lalun the foot of the young man of the city tend to her doorway and then retire for lalun be particular maiden slow of speech reserve of mind and not in the least inclined to orgy which be nearly certain to end in strife if be of no value be unworthy of this honour say lalun if be of value they be unworthy of and that be crooked sentence in the long hot night of latter april and may all the city seem to assemble in lalun ’s little white room to smoke and to talk shiah of the grim and most uncompromising persuasion sufis who have lose all belief in the prophet and retain but little in god wander hindu priest pass southward on their way to the central india fair and other affair pundit in black gown with spectacle on their nose and undigested wisdom in their inside bearded headman of the ward sikhs with all the detail of the late ecclesiastical scandal in the golden temple red eyed priest from beyond the border look like trap wolf and talk like raven m. a. ’s of the university very superior and very voluble all these people and more also you might find in the white room wali dad lie in the window seat and listen to the talk it be lalun ’s salon
say wali dad to
and it be electic be not that the word outside of freemason ’s lodge have never see such gathering din once with jew yahoudi he spit into the city ditch with apology for allow national feeling to overcome he though have lose every belief in the world say he and try to be proud of my losing can not help hat jew lalun admit no jews here
but what in the world do all these man do the curse of our country say wali dad they talk it be like the athenian always hear and tell some new thing ask the pearl and she will show you how much she know of the news of the city and the province lalun know everything lalun say at random she be talk to gentleman of the kurd persuasion who have come in from god know where—“when do the 175th regiment go to agra it do not go at all say lalun without turn her head they have order the 118th to go in its stead that regiment go to lucknow in three month unless they give fresh order that be so say wali dad without shade of doubt can you with your telegram and your newspaper do well always hear and tell some new thing he go on my friend have your god ever smite european nation for gossip in the bazar india have gossip for century always stand in the bazar until the soldier go by therefore you be here today instead of starve in your own country and be not muhammadan be product demnition product that also owe to you and your that can not make an end to my sentence without quote from your author he pull at the huqa and mourn half feelingly half in earnest for the shatter hope of his youth wali dad be always mourn over something or other the country of which he despair or the creed in which he have lose faith or the life of the english which he could by no mean understand lalun never mourn she play little song on the sitar and to hear she sing peacock cry again be always fresh pleasure she know all the song that have ever be sing from the war song of the south that make the old man angry with the young man and the young man angry with the state to the love song of the north where the sword whinny whicker like angry kite in the pause between the kiss and the pass fill with armed man and the lover be tear from his beloved and cry ai ai ai she know how to make up tobacco for the huqa so that it smell like the gate of paradise and waft you gently through they she could embroider strange thing in gold and silver and dance softly with the moonlight when it come in at the window also she know the heart of man and the heart of the city and whose wife be faithful and whose untrue and more of the secret of the government office than be good to be set down in this place nasiban her maid say that her jewelry be worth ten thousand pound and that some night thief would enter and murder she for its possession but lalun say that all the city would tear that thief limb from limb and that he whoever he be know it
so she take she sitar and sit in the window seat and sing song of old day that have be sing by girl of her profession in an armed camp on the eve of great battle the day before the fords of the jumna run red and sivaji flee fifty mile to delhi with toorkh stallion at his horse ’s tail and another lalun on his saddle bow it be what man call mahratta laonee
and it say their warrior force chimnajee before the peishwa lead the child of the sun and fire behind he turn and flee and the chorus say with they there fight who rid so free with sword and turban red the warrior youth who earn his fee at peril of his head at peril of his head say wali dad in english to thank to your government all our head be protect and with the educational facility at my command”—his eye twinkle wickedly—“i might be distinguish member of the local administration perhaps in time might even be member of legislative council do not speak english say lalun bend over her sitar afresh the chorus go out from the city wall to the blacken wall of fort amara which dominate the city no man know the precise extent of fort amara three king build it hundred of year ago
and they say that there be mile of underground room beneath its wall it be people with many ghost detachment of garrison artillery and company of infantry in its prime it hold ten thousand man and fill its ditch with corps at peril of his head sing lalun again and again
head move on one of the rampart the gray head of an old man and voice rough as shark skin on sword hilt send back the last line of the chorus and break into song that could not understand though lalun and wali dad listen intently what be it who be it consistent man say wali dad he fight you in when he be warrior youth refought you in and he try to fight you in but you have learn the trick of blow man from gun too well now he be old but he would still fight if he could be he wahabi then why should he answer to mahratta laonee if he be wahabi or sikh say i. do not know say wali dad he have lose perhaps his religion perhaps he wish to be king perhaps he be king do not know his name that be lie wali dad if you know his career you must know his name that be quite true belong to nation of liar would rather not tell you his name think for yourself lalun finish her song point to the fort and say simply khem singh hm say wali dad if the pearl choose to tell you the pearl be fool translate to lalun who laugh choose to tell what choose to tell they keep khem singh in burma say she they keep he there for many year until his mind be change in he so great be the kindness of the government find this they send he back to his own country that he might look upon it before he die he be an old man but when he look upon this his country his memory will come moreover there be many who remember he he be an interesting survival say wali dad pull at the huqa he return to country now full of educational and political reform but as the pearl say there be many who remember he he be once great man there will never be any more great man in india they will all when they be boy go whore after strange god
and they will become citizens—‘fellow citizens’—‘illustrious fellow citizen what be it that the native paper call they wali dad seem to be in very bad temper lalun look out of the window and smile into the dust haze go away think about khem singh who have once make history with thousand follower and would have be princele but for the power of the supreme government aforesaid the senior captain commanding fort amara be away on leave but the subaltern his deputy have drift down to the club where find he and enquire of he whether it be really true that political prisoner have be add to the attraction of the fort the subaltern explain at great length for this be the first time that he have hold command of the fort and his glory lie heavy upon he
yes say he man be send in to about week ago from down the line thorough gentleman whoever he be of course do all could for he he have his two servant and some silver cooking pot and he look for all the world like native officer call he subadar sahib just as well to be on the safe side y’know look here subadar sahib say you ’re hand over to my authority and suppose to guard you now do not want to make your life hard
but you must make thing easy for all the fort be at your disposal from the flag staff to the dry ditch and shall be happy to entertain you in any way can
but you must not take advantage of it give your word that you will not try to escape subadar sahib and ’ll give you my word that you shall have no heavy guard put over you think the good way of get at he be by go at he straight y’know
and it be by jove the old man give his word and move about the fort as contented as sick crow he rummy chap always ask to be tell where he be and what the building about he be have to sign slip of blue paper when he turn up acknowledge receipt of his body and all that and responsible y’know that he do not get away queer thing though look after johnnie old enough to be your grandfather be not it come to the fort one of these day and see he for reason which will appear never go to the fort while khem singh be then within its wall know he only as gray head see from lalun ’s window gray head and harsh voice but native tell that day by day as he look upon the fair land round amara his memory come back to he and with it the old hatred against the government that have be nearly efface in far off burma so he rag up and down the west face of the fort from morning till noon and from evening till the night devise vain thing in his heart and croak war song when lalun sing on the city wall as he grow more acquainted with the subaltern he unburden his old heart of some of the passion that have wither it sahib he use to say tap his stick against the parapet when be young man be
one of twenty thousand horseman who come out of the city and ride round the plain here sahib be the leader of hundred then of thousand then of five thousand and now!”—he point to his two servant but from the beginning to to day would cut the throat of all the sahib in the land if could hold fast sahib lest get away and return to those who would follow forget they when be in burma but now that be in my own country again remember everything do you remember that you have give your honour not to make your tendance hard matter say the subaltern yes to you only to you sahib say khem singh to you because you be of pleasant countenance if my turn come again sahib will not hang you nor cut your throat
thank you say the subaltern gravely as he look along the line of gun that could pound the city to powder in half an hour let we go into our own quarter khem singh come and talk with after dinner khem singh would sit on his own cushion at the subaltern ’s foot drink heavy scented anise seed brandy in great gulp and tell strange story of fort amara which have be palace in the old day of begum and ranee torture to death aye in the very vaulted chamber that now serve as mess room would tell story of sobraon that make the subaltern ’s cheek flush and tingle with pride of race and of the kuka rise from which so much be expect and the foreknowledge of which be share by hundred thousand soul
but he never tell tale of because as he say he be the subaltern ’s guest and be year that no man black or white care to speak of once only when the anise seed brandy have slightly affect his head he say sahib speak now of matter which lie between sobraon and the affair of the kukas it be ever wonder to we that you stay your hand at all and that having stay it you do not make the land one prison now hear from without that you do great honour to all man of our country and by your own hand be destroy the terror of your name which be your strong rock and defence this be foolish thing will oil and water mix now in be not bear then subadar sahib say the subaltern and khem singh reel to his quarter the subaltern would tell of these conversation at the club and my desire to see khem singh increase
but wali dad sit in the window seat of the house on the city wall say that it would be cruel thing to do and lalun pretend that prefer the society of grizzled old sikh to hers here be tobacco here be talk here be many friend and all the news of the city and above all here be myself will tell you story and sing you song and wali dad will talk his english nonsense in your ear be that bad than watch the cage animal yonder go to morrow then if you must but to day such and such an one will be here and he will speak of wonderful thing it happen that to morrow never come and the warm heat of the latter rain give place to the chill of early october almost before be aware of the flight of the year the captain command the fort return from leave and take over charge of khem singh accord to the law of seniority the captain be not nice man he call all native nigger which besides be extreme bad form show gross ignorance what the use of tell off two tommie to watch that old nigger say he fancy it soothe his vanity say the subaltern the man be order to keep well out of his way but he take they as tribute to his importance poor old wretch will not have line man take off regular guard in this way put on couple of native infantry sikhs say the subaltern lift his eyebrow sikhs pathans dogra they ’re all alike these black vermin and the captain talk to khem singh in manner which hurt that old gentleman ’s feeling fifteen year before when he have be catch for the second time every one look upon he as sort of tiger he like be regard in this light
but he forget that the world go forward in fifteen year and many subaltern be promote to captaincy the captain pig be in charge of the fort say khem singh to his native guard every morning and the native guard say yes subadar sahib in deference to his age and his air of distinction
but they do not know who he be in those day the gathering in lalun ’s little white room be always large and talk more than before the greeks say wali dad who have be borrow my book the inhabitant of the city of athens where they be always hear and tell some new thing rigorously seclude their woman who be fool hence the glorious institution of the heterodox woman be it not?—who be amusing and not fool all the greek philosopher delight in their company tell my friend how it go now in greece and the other place upon the continent of europe be your woman folk also fool wali dad say you never speak to we about your woman folk and we never speak about ours to you that be the bar between we yes say wali dad it be curious to think that our common meeting place should be here in the house of common how do you call she he point with the pipe mouth to lalun lalun be nothing but lalun say and that be perfectly true but if you take your place in the world wali dad and give up dream dream might wear an english coat and trouser might be lead muhammadan pleader might be receive even at the commissioner ’s tennis party where the english stand on one side and the native on the other in order to promote social intercourse throughout the empire heart ’s heart say he to lalun quickly the sahib say that ought to quit you the sahib be always talk stupid talk return lalun with laugh in this house be queen and thou art king the sahib”—she put her arm above her head and think for moment—“the sahib shall be our vizier thine and mine wali dad because he have say that thou shouldst leave wali dad laugh immoderately and laugh too be it so say he my friend be you willing to take this lucrative government appointment lalun what shall his pay be but lalun begin to sing and for the rest of the time there be no hope of get sensible answer from she or wali dad when the one stop the other begin to quote persian poetry with triple pun in every other line some of it be not strictly proper but it be all very funny and it only come to an end when fat person in black with gold pince nez send up his name to lalun and wali dad drag into the twinkling night to walk in big rose garden and talk heresy about religion and government and man ’s career in life the mohurrum the great mourning festival of the muhammadan be close at hand and the thing that wali dad say about religious fanaticism would have secure his expulsion from the loose think muslim sect there be the rose bush round we the star above we and from every quarter of the city come the boom of the big mohurrum drum you must know that the city be divide in fairly equal proportion between the hindus and the musalman and where both creed belong to the fighting race big religious festival give ample chance for trouble when they can that be to say when the authority be weak enough to allow it the hindus do their good to arrange some minor feast day of their own in time to clash with the period of general mourning for the martyr hasan and hussain the hero of the mohurrum gilt and paint paper presentation of their tomb be bear with shout and wail music torch and yell through the principal thoroughfare of the city which fakement be call tazia their passage be rigorously lie down beforehand by the police and detachment of police accompany each tazia lest the hindus should throw brick at it and the peace of the queen and the head of her loyal subject should thereby be break mohurrum time in fight town mean anxiety to all the official because if riot break out the official and not the rioter be hold responsible the former must foresee everything and while not make their precaution ridiculously elaborate must see that they be at least adequate listen to the drum say wali dad that be the heart of the people empty and make much noise how think you will the mohurrum go this year think that there will be trouble he turn down side street and leave alone with the star and sleepy police patrol then go to bed and dream that wali dad have sack the city and be make vizier with lalun ’s silver huqa for mark of office all day the mohurrum drum beat in the city and all day deputation of tearful hindu gentleman besiege the deputy commissioner with assurance that they would be murder ere next dawning by the muhammadan which say the deputy commissioner in confidence to the head of police be pretty fair indication that the hindus be go to make emselve unpleasant think we can arrange little surprise for they have give the head of both creed fair warning if they choose to disregard it so much the bad for they there be large gathering in lalun ’s house that night but of man that have never see before if except the fat gentleman in black with the gold pince nez wali dad lie in the window seat more bitterly scornful of his faith and its manifestation than have ever know he lalun ’s maid be very busy cut up and mix tobacco for the guest we could hear the thunder of the drum as the procession accompany each tazia march to the central gathering place in the plain outside the city preparatory to their triumphant re entry and circuit within the wall all the street seem ablaze with torch and only fort amara be black and silent when the noise of the drum cease no one in the white room speak for time the first tazia have move off say wali dad look to the plain that be very early say the man with the pince nez it be only half past eight the company rise and depart some of they be man from ladakh say lalun when the last have go they bring brick tea such as the russians sell and tea urn from peshawur show now how the english memsahib make tea the brick tea be abominable when it be finish wali dad suggest go into the street be nearly sure that there will be trouble to night he say all the city think so and vox populi be vox dei as the babu say now tell you that at the corner of the padshahi gate you will find my horse all this night if you want to go about and to see thing it be most disgraceful exhibition where be the pleasure of say ya hasan ya hussain twenty thousand time in night all the procession there be two and twenty of they be now well within the city wall the drum be beat afresh the crowd be howl
ya hasan ya hussain and beat their breast the brass band be play their loud and at every corner where space allow muhammadan preacher be tell the lamentable story of the death of the martyr it be impossible to move except with the crowd for the street be not more than twenty foot wide in the hindu quarter the shutter of all the shop be up and cross bar as the first tazia gorgeous erection ten foot high be bear aloft on the shoulder of score of stout man into the semi darkness of the gully of the horseman brickbat crash through its talc and tinsel side into thy hand lord murmur wali dad
profanely as yell go up from behind and native officer of police jam his horse through the crowd another brickbat follow and the tazia stagger and sway where it have stop go on in the name of the sirkar go forward shout the policeman but there be an ugly cracking and splintering of shutter and the crowd halt with oath and growl before the house whence the brickbat have be throw then without any warning break the storm not only in the gully of the horseman but in half dozen other place the tazias rock like ship at sea the long pole torch dip and rise round they while the man shout the hindus be dishonour the tazia strike strike into their temple for the faith the six or eight policeman with each tazia draw their baton and strike as long as they could in the hope of force the mob forward but they be overpower and as contingent of hindus pour into the street the fight become general half mile away where the tazia be yet untouched the drum and the shriek of ya hasan ya hussain continue but not for long the priest at the corner of the street knock the leg from the bedstead that support their pulpit and smite for the faith while stone fall from the silent house upon friend and foe and the pack street bellow din din din tazia catch fire and be drop for flame barrier between hindu and musalman at the corner of the gully
then the crowd surge forward and wali dad draw close to the stone pillar of well it be intend from the beginning he shout in my ear with more heat than blank unbelief should be guilty of the brick be carry up to the house beforehand these swine of hindus we shall be gutte kine in their temple to night tazia after tazia some burn other tear to piece hurry past we and the mob with they howl shriek and strike at the house door in their flight at last we see the reason of the rush hugonin the assistant district superintendent of police boy of twenty have get together thirty constable and be force the crowd through the street his old gray police horse show no sign of uneasiness as it be spur breast on into the crowd and the long dog whip with which he have arm himself be never still they know we have not enough police to hold ’em he cry as he pass mop cut on his face they know we have not be not any of the man from the club come down to help get on you son of burn father the dog whip crack across the writhe back and the constable smite afresh with baton and gun butt with these pass the light and the shouting and wali dad begin to swear under his breath from fort amara shoot up single rocket then two side by side it be the signal for troop petitt the deputy commissioner cover with dust and sweat but calm and gently smile canter up the clean sweep street in rear of the main body of the rioter no one kill yet he shout ’ll keep ’em on the run till dawn do not let ’em halt hugonin trot ’em about till the troop come the science of the defence lie solely in keep the mob on the move if they have breathing space they would halt and fire house and then the work of restore order would be more difficult to say the least of it flame have the same effect on crowd as blood have on wild beast word have reach the club and man in evening dress be begin to show themselves and lend hand in head off and break up the shout mass with stirrup leather whip or chance find stave they be not very often attack for the rioter have sense enough to know that the death of european would not mean one hang but many and possibly the appearance of the thrice dread artillery the clamour in the city redouble the hindus have descend into the street in real earnest and ere long the mob return it be strange sight there be no tazias_—only their riven platform and there be no police here and there city dignitary hindu or muhammadan be vainly implore his co religionist to keep quiet and behave themselves advice for which his white beard be pull then native officer of police unhorse but still use his spur with effect would be bear along warn all the crowd of the danger of insult the government everywhere man strike aimlessly with stick grasp each other by the throat howl and foam with rage or beat with their bare hand on the door of the house it be lucky thing that they be fight with natural weapon say to wali dad else we should have half the city kill turn as speak and look at his face his nostril be distend his eye be fix and he be smite himself softly on the breast the crowd pour by with renew riot gang of musalmans hard press by some hundred hindu fanatic wali dad leave my side with an oath and shout ya hasan ya hussain plunge into the thick of the fight where lose sight of he flee by side alley to the padshahi gate where find wali dad ’s horse and thence ride to the fort once outside the city wall the tumult sink to dull roar very impressive under the star and reflect great credit on the fifty thousand angry able bodied man who be make it the troop who at the deputy commissioner ’s instance have be order to rendezvous quietly near the fort show no sign of be impress two company of native infantry squadron of native cavalry and company of british infantry be kick their heel in the shadow of the east face wait for order to march in be sorry to say that they be all pleased unholily please at the chance of what they call little fun the senior officer to be sure grumble at having be keep out of bed and the english troop pretend to be sulky but there be joy in the heart of all the subaltern and whisper run up and down the line no ball cartridge what beastly shame d’you think the beggar will really stand up to we hope shall meet my money lender there owe he more than can afford
oh they will not let we even unsheathe sword up go the fourth rocket fall in there the garrison artillery who to the last cherish wild hope that they might be allow to bombard the city at hundred yard range line the parapet above the east gateway and cheer themselves hoarse as the british infantry double along the road to the main gate of the city the cavalry canter on to the padshahi gate and the native infantry march slowly to the gate of the butcher the surprise be intend to be of distinctly unpleasant nature and to come on top of the defeat of the police who have be just able to keep the muhammadan from fire the house of few lead hindus the bulk of the riot lie in the north and north west ward the east and south east be by this time dark and silent and ride hastily to lalun ’s house for wish to tell she to send some one in search of wali dad the house be unlighte but the door be open and climb upstairs in the darkness one small lamp in the white room show lalun and her maid lean half out of the window breathe heavily and evidently pull at something that refuse to come thou art late very late gasp lalun without turn her head

vizier after all the enlightenment of pagett m. p. because half dozen grasshopper under fern make the field ring with their importunate chink while thousand of great cattle repose beneath the shadow other than the little shrivelled meagre hopping though loud and troublesome insect of the hour burke reflection on the revolution in france they be sit in the verandah of the splendid palace of an indian pro consul surround by all the glory and mystery of the immemorial east in plain english it be one storied ten roomed whitewash mud roof bungalow set in dry garden of dusty tamarisk tree and divide from the road by low mud wall the green parrot scream overhead as they fly in battalion to the river for their morning drink beyond the wall cloud of fine dust show where the cattle and goat of the city be pas afield to graze the remorseless white light of the winter sunshine of northern india lie upon everything and improve nothing from the whine persian wheel by the lawn tennis court to the long perspective of level road and the blue domed tomb of mahommedan saint just visible above the tree happy new year say orde to his guest it the first you ve ever spend out of england be not it yes happy new year say pagett smile at the sunshine what divine climate you have here just think of the brown cold fog hang over london now and he rub his hand it be more than twenty year since he have last see orde his schoolmate and their path in the world have divide early the one have quit college to become cog wheel in the machinery of the great indian government the other more bless with good have be whirl into similar position in the english scheme three successive election have not affect pagett ’s position with loyal constituency and he have grow insensibly to regard himself in some sort as pillar of the empire whose real worth would be know later on after few year of conscientious attendance at many division after newspaper battle innumerable and the publication of interminable correspondence and more hasty oratory than in his calm moment he care to think upon it occur to he as it have occur to many of his fellow in parliament that tour to india would enable he to sweep large lyre and address himself to the problem of imperial administration with firm hand accept therefore general invitation extend to he by orde some year before pagett have take ship to karachi and only over night have be receive with joy by the deputy commissioner of amara they have sit late discuss the change and chance of twenty year recall the name of the dead and weigh the future of the living as be the custom of man meet after interval of action next morning they smoke the after breakfast pipe in the verandah still regard each other curiously pagett in light gray frock coat and garment much too thin for the time of the year and puggrie sun hat carefully and wonderfully make orde in shoot coat rid breech brown cowhide boot with spur and batter flax helmet he have ride some mile in the early morning to inspect doubtful river dam the man ’s face differ as much as their attire orde ’s wear and wrinkle about the eye and grizzled at the temple be the hard and more square of the two and it be with something like envy that the owner look at the comfortable outline of pagett ’s blandly receptive countenance the clear skin the untroubled eye and the mobile clean shave lip and this be india say pagett for the twentieth time star long and intently at the gray feathering of the tamarisk one portion of india only it very much like this for mile in every direction by the way now that you have rest little would not ask the old question before what d’you think of the country tis the most pervasive country that ever yet be see acquire several pound of your country
