india
but you have decent passage out have not you very good on the whole your anglo indian may be unsympathetic about one ’s political view but he have reduce ship life to science the anglo indian be political orphan and if he wise he will not be in hurry to be adopt by your party grandmother
but how be your companion unsympathetic well there be man call dawlishe judge somewhere in this country it seem and capital partner at whist by the way and when want to talk to he about the progress of india in political sense orde hide grin which might or might not have be sympathetic the national congress movement and other thing in which as member of parliament of course
interested he shift the subject and when once corner he he look calmly in the eye and say that all tommy rot come and have game at bull you may laugh but that be not the way to treat great and important question and know who be well think it rather rude do not you know
and yet dawlishe be thoroughly good fellow yes
he friend of mine and one of the straight man know suppose like many anglo indians he feel it be hopeless to give you any just idea of any indian question without the document before you and in this case the document you want be the country and the people precisely that be why come straight to you bring an open mind to bear on thing anxious to know what popular feeling in india be really like y’know now that it have waken into political life the national congress in spite of dawlishe must have cause great excitement among the mass on the contrary nothing could be more tranquil than the state of popular feeling and as to excitement the people would as soon be excited over the rule of three as over the congress excuse orde
but do you think you be fair judge be not the official anglo indian naturally jealous of any external influence that might move the mass and so much opposed to liberal idea truly liberal idea that he can scarcely be expect to regard popular movement with fairness what do dawlishe say about tommy rot think moment old man you and be bring up together teach by the same tutor read the same book live the same life and think as you may remember in parallel line come out here learn new language and work among new race while you more fortunate remain at home why should change my mind our mind because change my sky why should and the few hundred englishman in my service become unreasonable prejudiced fossil while you and your new friend alone remain bright and open minded you surely do not fancy civilian be member of primrose league of course not
but the mere position of an english official give he point of view which can not but bias his mind on this question pagett move his knee up and down little uneasily as he speak that sound plausible enough but like more plausible notion on indian matter believe it mistake you ’ll find when you come to consult the unofficial briton that our fault as class speak of the civilian now be rather to magnify the progress that have be make towards liberal institution it be of english origin such as it be and the stress of our work since the mutiny only thirty year ago have be in that direction no think you will get no fair or more dispassionate view of the congress business than such man as can give you but may as well say at once that those who know most of india from the inside be incline to wonder at the noise our scarcely begin experiment make in england but surely the gathering together of congress delegate be of itself new thing there nothing new under the sun when europe be jungle half asia flock to the canonical conference of buddhism and for century the people have gather at puri hurdwar trimbak and benare in immense number great meeting what you call mass meeting be really one of the old and most popular of indian institution in the case of the congress meet the only notable fact be that the priest of the altar be british not buddhist jain or brahmanical and that the whole thing be british contrivance keep alive by the effort of messr hume eardley norton and digby you mean to say then it not spontaneous movement
what movement be ever spontaneous in any true sense of the word this seem to be more factitious than usual you seem to know great deal about it try it by the touchstone of subscription coarse but fairly trustworthy criterion and there be scarcely the colour of money in it the delegate write from england that they be out of pocket for work expense railway fare and stationery the mere pasteboard and scaffolding of their show it be in fact collapse from mere financial inanition but you can not deny that the people of india who be perhaps too poor to subscribe be mentally and morally move by the agitation pagett insist that be precisely what do deny the native side of the movement be the work of limited class microscopic minority as lord dufferin describe it when compare with the people proper but still very interesting class see that it be of our own creation it be compose almost entirely of those of the literary or clerkly caste who have receive an english education surely that very important class its member must be the ordain leader of popular thought anywhere else they might be leader but they have no social weight in this topsy turvy land and though they have be employ in clerical work for generation they have no practical knowledge of affair ship ’s clerk be useful person
but he be scarcely the captain and an orderly room writer however smart he may be be not the colonel you see the writer class in india have never till now aspire to anything like command it be not allow to the indian gentleman for thousand of year past have resemble victor hugo ’s noble un vrai sire chatelain laisse ecrire le vilain sa main digne quand il signe egratigne le velin and the little egratignure he most like to make
have be score pretty deeply by the sword but this be childish and mediæval nonsense precisely and from your or rather our point of view the pen be mighty than the sword in this country it otherwise the fault lie in our indian balance not yet adjust to civilised weight and measure well at all event this literary class represent the natural aspiration and wish of the people at large though it may not exactly lead they and in spite of all you say orde defy you to find really sound english radical who would not sympathise with those aspiration pagett speak with some warmth and he have scarcely cease when well appoint dog cart turn into the compound gate and orde rise say here be edwards the master of the lodge neglect so diligently come to talk about account suppose as the vehicle drive up under the porch pagett also rise say with the train effusion bear of much practice but this be also my friend my old and value friend edwards delight to see you know you be in india but not exactly where then it be not account mr edwards say orde cheerily why no sir hear mr pagett be come and as our work be close for the new year think would drive over and see he very happy think mr edwards
you may not know orde be lead member of our radical club at switchton when be begin political life and owe much to his exertion there no pleasure like meet an old friend except perhaps make new one suppose mr edwards you stick to the good old cause
well you see sir thing be different out here there precious little one can find to say against the government which be the main of our talk at home and they that do say thing be not the sort people man who respect himself would like to be mix up with there be no politic in manner of speak in india it all work surely you be mistake my good friend why have come all the way from england just to see the working of this great national movement do not know where you ’re go to find the nation as move to begin with
and then you ’ll be hard put to it to find what they be move about it like this sir say edwards who have not quite relish be call my good friend they have not get any grievance nothing to hit with
do not you see sir
and then there not much to hit against because the government be more like kind of general providence direct an old establish state of thing than that at home where there something new throw down for we to fight about every three month you be probably in your workshop full of english mechanic out of the way of learn what the mass think do not know so much about that there be four of we english foreman and between seven and eight hundred native fitter smith carpenter painter and such like and they be full of the congress of course never hear word of it from year ’s end to year ’s end and speak the talk too but want to ask how thing be go on at home old tyler and brown and the rest we will speak of they presently but your account of the indifference of your man surprise almost as much as your own fear you be backslider from the good old doctrine edwards pagett speak as one who mourn the death of near relative not bit sir but should be if take up with parcel of babus pleader and schoolboy as never do day ’s work in their life and could not if they try and if you be to poll we english railway man mechanic tradespeople and the like of that all up and down the country from peshawur to calcutta you would find we mostly in tale together and
yet you know we ’re the same english you pay some respect to at home at lection time and we have the pull know something about it this be very curious but you will let come and see you and perhaps you will kindly show the railway work and we will talk thing over at leisure and about all old friend and old time add pagett detect with quick insight look of disappointment in the mechanic ’s face nod briefly to orde edwards mount his dog cart and drive off it very disappointing say the member to orde who while his friend discourse with edwards have be look over bundle of sketch draw on gray paper in purple ink bring to he by chuprassee do not let it trouble you old chap say orde sympathetically look here moment here be some sketch by the man who make the carve wood screen you admire so much in the dining room and want copy of and the artist himself be here
too native say pagett of course be the reply bishen singh be his name and he have two brother to help he when there be an important job to do the three go into partnership but they spend most of their time and all their money in litigation over an inheritance and afraid they be get involve thoroughbred sikhs of the old rock obstinate touchy bigoted and cunning but good man for all that here be bishen singh shall we ask he about the congress but bishen singh who approach with respectful salaam have never hear of it
and he listen with puzzled face and obviously feign interest to orde ’s account of its aim and object finally shake his vast white turban with great significance when he learn that it be promote by certain pleader name by orde and by educate native he begin with labour respect to explain how he be poor man with no concern in such matter which be all under the control of god but presently break out of urdu into familiar punjabi the mere sound of which have rustic smack of village smoke reek and plough tail as he denounce the wearer of white coat the juggler with word who filch his field from he the man whose back be never bow in honest work and pour ironical scorn on the bengali he and one of his brother have see calcutta and be at work there have bengali carpenter give to they as assistant those carpenter say bishen singh black ape be more efficient workmate and as for the bengali babu tchick the guttural click need no interpretation but orde translate the rest while pagett gaze with interest at the wood carver he seem to have most illiberal prejudice against the bengali say the m.

but you can scarcely expect the mahommedan the most masterful and powerful minority in the country to contemplate their own extinction with joy the bad of it be that he and his co religionist who be many and the land proprietor also of hindu race be frightened and put out by this election business and by the importance we have bestow on lawyer pleader writer and the like who have up to now be in abject submission to they they say little but after all they be the most important faggot in the great bundle of community and all the glib bunkum in the world would not pay for their estrangement they have control the land but be assure that experience of local self government in your municipality have be most satisfactory and when once the principle be accept in your centre do not you know it be bind to spread and these important ah’m people of yours would learn it like the rest see no difficulty at all and the smooth lip close with the complacent snap habitual to pagett m.

to conceive of people absolutely devoid of curiosity to whom the book the daily paper and the print speech be unknown and you would describe their life as blank that profound mistake you be in another land another century down on the bed rock of society where the family merely and not the community be all important the average oriental can not be bre to look beyond his clan his life too be more complete and self sufficing and less sordid and low thoughted than you might imagine it be bovine and slow in some respect but it be never empty you and be inclined to put the cart before the horse but it be to meet the man ’s wife and when he fly uphill it be for the same end the man be in the plain earn money for his wife to spend on dress and four hundred rupee bracelet and inexpensive luxury of that kind he work very hard and send she letter or post card daily she also write to he daily and say that she be long for he to come up to simla the tertium quid use to lean over her shoulder and laugh as she write the note then the two would ride to the post office together now simla be strange place and its custom be peculiar nor be any man who have not spend at least ten season there qualified to pass judgment on circumstantial evidence which be the most untrustworthy in the court for these reason and for other which need not appear decline to state positively whether there be anything irretrievably wrong in the relation between the man ’s wife and the tertium quid if there be and hereon you must form your own opinion it be the man ’s wife ’s fault she be kittenish in her manner wear generally an air of soft and fluffy innocence
but she be deadlily learn and evil instruct and now and again when the mask drop man see this shudder and almost draw back man be occasionally particular and the least particular man be always the most exacting simla be eccentric in its fashion of treat friendship certain attachment which have set and crystallise through half dozen season acquire almost the sanctity of the marriage bond and be revere as such again certain attachment equally old and to all appearance equally venerable never seem to win any recognise official status while chance spring acquaintance not two month bear step into the place which by right belong to the senior there be no law reducible to print which regulate these affair some people have gift which secure they infinite toleration and other have not the man ’s wife have not if she look over the garden wall for instance woman tax she with steal their husband she complain pathetically that she be not allow to choose her own friend when she put up her big white muff to her lip and gaze over it and under her eyebrow at you as she say this thing you feel that she have be infamously misjudge and that all the other woman ’s instinct be all wrong which be absurd she be not allow to own the tertium quid in peace and be so strangely construct that she would not have enjoy peace have she be so permit she prefer some semblance of intrigue to cloak even her most commonplace action after two month of rid first round jakko then elysium then summer hill then observatory hill then under jutogh and lastly up and down the cart road as far as the tara devi gap in the dusk she say to the tertium quid frank people say we be too much together and people be so horrid the tertium quid pull his moustache and reply that horrid people be unworthy of the consideration of nice people
but they have do more than talk they have write write to my hubby sure of it say the man ’s wife
and she pull letter from her husband out of her saddle pocket and give it to the tertium quid it be an honest letter write by an honest man then stew in the plain on two hundred rupee month for he allow his wife eight hundred and fifty and in silk banian and cotton trouser it say that perhaps she have not think of the unwisdom of allow her name to be so generally couple with the tertium quid ’s that she be too much of child to understand the danger of that sort of thing that he her husband be the last man in the world to interfere jealously with her little amusement and interest but that it would be well be she to drop the tertium quid quietly and for her husband ’s sake the letter be sweeten with many pretty little pet name and it amuse the tertium quid considerably he
and she laugh over it so that you fifty yard away could see their shoulder shake while the horse slouch along side by side their conversation be not worth report the upshot of it be that next day no one see the man ’s wife and the tertium quid together they have both go down to the cemetery which as rule be only visit officially by the inhabitant of simla simla funeral with the clergyman rid the mourner rid and the coffin creak as it swing between the bearer be one of the most depressing thing on this earth particularly when the procession pass under the wet dank dip beneath the rockcliffe hotel where the sun be shut out and all the hill stream be wail and weep together as they go down the valley occasionally folk tend the graf
but we in india shift and be transfer so often that at the end of the second year the dead have no friend only acquaintance who be far too busy amusing themselves up the hill to attend to old partner the idea of use cemetery as rendezvous be distinctly feminine one man would have say simply let people talk we ’ll go down the mall woman be make differently especially if she be such woman as the man ’s wife she and the tertium quid enjoy each other ’s society among the graf of man and woman whom they have know and dance with aforetime they use to take big horse blanket and sit on the grass little to the left of the low end where there be dip in the ground and where the occupied graf stop short and the ready make one be not ready each well regulate indian cemetery keep half dozen graf permanently open for contingency and incidental wear and tear in the hill these be more usually baby ’s size because child who come up weaken and sick from the plain often succumb to the effect of the rain in the hill or get pneumonia from their ayah take they through damp pine wood after the sun have set in cantonment of course the man ’s size be more in request these arrangement vary with the climate and population one day when the man ’s wife and the tertium quid have just arrive in the cemetery they see some cooly break ground they have mark out full size grave and the tertium quid ask they whether any sahib be sick they say that they do not know but it be an order that they should dig sahib ’s grave work away say the tertium quid and let ’s see how it do the cooly work away and the quid stand at the head of the grave and star without answer for space then he say drop pebble down it be nasty and cold horribly cold do not think shall come to the cemetery any more do not think grave digging be cheerful the two talk and agree that the cemetery be depress
they also arrange for ride next day out from the cemetery through the mashobra tunnel up to fagoo and back because all the world be go to garden party at viceregal lodge and all the people of mashobra would go too come up the cemetery road the tertium quid ’s horse try to bolt uphill be tired with stand so long and manage to strain back sinew shall have to take the mare to morrow say the tertium quid
and she will stand nothing heavy than snaffle they make their arrangement to meet in the cemetery after allow all the mashobra people time to pass into simla that night it rain heavily and next day when the tertium quid come to the tryste place he see that the new grave have foot of water in it the ground be tough and sour clay that look beastly say the tertium quid fancy be board up and drop into that well they then start off to fagoo the mare play with the snaffle and pick her way as though she be shod with satin and the sun shin divinely the road below mashobra to fagoo be officially style the himalayan thibet road but in spite of its name it be not much more than six foot wide in most place and the drop into the valley below may be anything between one and two thousand foot now we ’re go to thibet say the man ’s wife merrily as the horse draw near to fagoo she be rid on the cliff side into thibet say the tertium quid ever so far from people who say horrid thing and hubby who write stupid letter with you to the end of the world coolie carry log of wood come round corner and the mare go wide to avoid he forefoot in and haunch out as sensible mare should go to the world ’s end say the man ’s wife and look unspeakable thing over her near shoulder at the tertium quid he be smile but while she look the smile freeze stiff as it be on his face and change to nervous grin the sort of grin man wear when they be not quite easy in their saddle the mare seem to be sink by the stern and her nostril crack while she be try to realise what be happen the rain of the night before have rot the drop side of the himalayan thibet road

judgment therefore the misery of man be great upon he fate and the government of india have turn the station of kashima into prison and because there be no help for the poor soul who be now lie there in torment write this story pray that the government of india may be move to scatter the european population to the four wind kashima be bind on all side by the rocktipped circle of the dosehri hill in spring it be ablaze with rise in summer the rise die and the hot wind blow from the hill in autumn the white mist from the jhil cover the place as with water and in winter the frost nip everything young and tender to earth level there be but one view in kashima stretch of perfectly flat pasture and plough land run up to the gray blue scrub of the dosehri hill there be no amusement except snipe and tiger shooting but the tiger have be long since hunt from their lair in the rock cave and the snipe only come once year narkarra one hundred and forty three mile by road be the near station to kashima but kashima never go to narkarra where there be at least twelve english people it stay within the circle of the dosehri hill all kashima acquit mrs vansuythen of any intention to do harm but all kashima know that she and she alone bring about their pain boulte the engineer mrs boulte and captain kurrell know this they be the english population of kashima if we except major vansuythen who be of no importance whatever and mrs vansuythen who be the most important of all you must remember though you will not understand that all law weaken in small and hidden community where there be no public opinion when man be absolutely alone in station he run certain risk of fall into evil way this risk be multiply by every addition to the population up to twelve the jury number after that fear and consequent restraint begin and human action become less grotesquely jerky there be deep peace in kashima till mrs vansuythen arrive she be charming woman every one say so everywhere and she charm every one in spite of this or perhaps because of this since fate be so perverse she care only for one man and he be major vansuythen have she be plain or stupid this matter would have be intelligible to kashima
but she be fair woman with very still gray eye the colour of lake just before the light of the sun touch it no man who have see those eye could later on explain what fashion of woman she be to look upon the eye dazzle he her own sex say that she be not bad look but spoil by pretend to be so grave and yet her gravity be natural it be not her habit to smile she merely go through life look at those who pass and the woman object while the man fall down and worship she know and be deeply sorry for the evil she have do to kashima but major vansuythen can not understand why mrs boulte do not drop in to afternoon tea at least three time week when there be only two woman in one station they ought to see great deal of each other say major vansuythen long and long before ever mrs vansuythen come out of those far away place where there be society and amusement kurrell have discover that mrs boulte be the one woman in the world for he and you dare not blame they kashima be as out of the world as heaven or the other place and the dosehri hill keep their secret well boulte have no concern in the matter he be in camp for fortnight at time he be hard heavy man and neither mrs boulte nor kurrell pity he they have all kashima and each other for their very very own and kashima be the garden of eden in those day when boulte return from his wandering he would slap kurrell between the shoulder and call he old fellow and the three would dine together kashima be happy then when the judgment of god seem almost as distant as narkarra or the railway that run down to the sea
but the government send major vansuythen to kashima and with he come his wife the etiquette of kashima be much the same as that of desert island when stranger be cast away there all hand go down to the shore to make he welcome kashima assemble at the masonry platform close to the narkarra road and spread tea for the vansuythen that ceremony be reckon formal call and make they free of the station its right and privilege when the vansuythen settle down they give tiny house warm to all kashima and that make kashima free of their house accord to the immemorial usage of the station then the rain come when no one could go into camp and the narkarra road be wash away by the kasun river and in the cup like pasture of kashima the cattle wad knee deep the cloud drop down from the dosehri hill and cover everything at the end of the rain boulte ’s manner towards his wife change and become demonstratively affectionate they have be marry twelve year and the change startle mrs boulte who hat her husband with the hate of woman who have meet with nothing but kindness from her mate and in the tooth of this kindness have do he great wrong

that all with these counsel and many other equally valuable do papa wick fortify bobby ere that last awful night at portsmouth when the officer quarter hold more inmate than be provide for by the home sick till the malabar reach mid channel when he double his emotion with little guard visit and great many other matter the tail twister be most particular regiment those who know they least say that they be eat up with side but their reserve and their internal arrangement generally be merely protective diplomacy some five year before the colonel commanding have look into the fourteen fearless eye of seven plump and juicy subaltern who have all apply to enter the staff corp and have ask they why the three star should he colonel of the line command dash nursery for double dash bottle sucker who put on condemn tin spur and ride qualified moke at the hiatused head of forsake black regiment he be rude man and terrible wherefore the remnant take measure with the half butt as an engine of public opinion till the rumour go abroad that young man who use the tail twister as crutch to the staff corp have many and varied trial to endure however regiment have just as much right to its own secret as woman when bobby come up from deolali and take his place among the tail twister it be gently but firmly bear in upon he that the regiment be his father and his mother and his indissolubly we wife and that there be no crime under the canopy of heaven black than that of bring shame on the regiment which be the well shoot well drill well set up brave most illustrious and in all respect most desirable regiment within the compass of the seven sea he be teach the legend of the mess plate from the great grin golden god that have come out of the summer palace in pekin to the silver mount markhor horn snuff mull present by the last c.o he who spake to the seven subaltern and every one of those legend tell he of battle fight at long odd without fear as without support of hospitality catholic as an arab ’s of friendship deep as the sea and steady as the fighting line of honour win by hard road for honour ’s sake and of instant and unquestioning devotion to the regiment the regiment that claim the life of all and live for ever more than once too he come officially into contact with the regimental colour which look like the lining of bricklayer ’s hat on the end of chew stick bobby do not kneel and worship they because british subaltern be not construct in that manner indeed he condemn they for their weight at the very moment that they be fill with awe and other more noble sentiment but good of all be the occasion when he move with the tail twister in review order at the breaking of november day allow for duty man and sick the regiment be one thousand and eighty strong and bobby belong to they for be he not subaltern of the line the whole line and nothing but the line as the tramp of two thousand one hundred and sixty sturdy ammunition boot attest he would not have change place with deighton of the horse battery whirl by in pillar of cloud to chorus of strong right strong leave or hogan yale of the white hussar lead his squadron for all it be worth with the price of horseshoe throw in or tick boileau try to live up to his fierce blue and gold turban while the wasp of the bengal cavalry stretch to gallop in the wake of the long lolloppe waler of the white hussar they fight through the clear cool day and bobby feel little thrill run down his spine when he hear the tinkle tinkle tinkle of the empty cartridge case hop from the breech block after the roar of the volley for he know that he should live to hear that sound in action the review end in glorious chase across the plain battery thunder after cavalry to the huge disgust of the white hussar and the tyneside tail twister hunt sikh regiment till the lean lathy singh pant with exhaustion
bobby be dusty and dripping long before noon but his enthusiasm be merely focus not diminish he return to sit at the foot of revere his skipper that be to say the captain of his company and to be instruct in the dark art and mystery of manage man which be very large part of the profession of arm if you have not taste that way say revere between his puff of his cheroot you ’ll never be able to get the hang of it but remember bobby be not the good drill though drill be nearly everything that haul regiment through hell and out on the other side it the man who know how to handle man goat man swine man dog man and so on dormer for instance say bobby think he come under the head of fool man he mop like sick owl that where you make your mistake my son dormer be not fool yet

but would you min oldin my and sir bobby sit on the side of the bed and the icy cold hand close on his own like vice force lady ’s ring which be on the little finger deep into the flesh bobby set his lip and wait the water drip from the hem of his trouser an hour pass and the grasp of the hand do not relax nor do the expression of the draw face change bobby with infinite craft light himself cheroot with the left hand his right arm be numb to the elbow and resign himself to night of pain dawn show very white faced subaltern sit on the side of sick man ’s cot and doctor in the doorway use language unfit for publication have you be here all night you young ass say the doctor there or thereabout say bobby ruefully he ’s freeze on to dormer ’s mouth shut with click he turn his head and sigh the cling hand open and bobby ’s arm fall useless at his side he ’ll do say the doctor quietly it must have be toss up all through the night think you ’re to be congratulate on this case
oh bosh say bobby think the man have go out long ago only only do not care to take my hand away rub my arm down there good chap what grip the brute have chill to the marrow he pas out of the tent shiver private dormer be allow to celebrate his repulse of death by strong water four day later he sit on the side of his cot and say to the patient mildly ’d liken to speak to
so should but at that time bobby be read yet another letter he have the most persistent correspondent of any man in camp and be even then about to write that the sickness have abate and in another week at the outside would be go he do not intend to say that the chill of sick man ’s hand seem to have strike into the heart whose capacity for affection he dwell on at such length he do intend to enclose the illustrate programme of the forthcoming sing song whereof he be not little proud he also intend to write on many other matter which do not concern we and doubtless would have do so but for the slight feverish headache which make he dull and unresponsive at mess you be overdo it bobby say his skipper might give the rest of us credit of do little work you go on as if you be the whole mess roll into one take it easy will say bobby feel do up somehow revere look at he anxiously and say nothing there be flicker of lantern about the camp that night and rumour that bring man out of their cot to the tent door paddle of the naked foot of doolie bearer and the rush of gallop horse wot up ask twenty tent and through twenty tent run the answer wick down they bring the news to revere and he groan any one but bobby and should not have care the sergeant major be right not go out this journey gasp bobby as he be lift from the doolie not go out this journey then with an air of supreme conviction can not you see not if can do anything say the surgeon major who have hasten over from the mess where he have be din he and the regimental surgeon fight together with death for the life of bobby wick their work be interrupt by hairy apparition in bluegray dressing gown who star in horror at the bed and cry
oh my gawd it can not be until an indignant hospital orderly whisk he away if care of man and desire to live could have do aught bobby would have be save as it be he make fight of three day and the surgeon major ’s brow uncreased we ’ll save he yet he say and the surgeon who though he rank with the captain have very youthful heart go out upon the word and prance joyously in the mud not go out this journey whisper bobby wick gallantly at the end of the third day bravo say the surgeon major that the way to look at it bobby as evening fall gray shade gather round bobby ’s mouth and he turn his face to the tent wall wearily the surgeon major frown awfully tired say bobby very faintly what the use of bother with medicine do not want it let alone the desire for life have depart and bobby be content to drift away on the easy tide of death it
no good say the surgeon major he do not want to live he ’s meet it poor child and he blow his nose half mile away the regimental band be play the overture to the sing song for the man have be tell that bobby be out of danger the clash of the brass and the wail of the horn reach bobby ’s ear be there single joy or pain that should never kno ow you do not love tis in vain bid
and go an expression of hopeless irritation cross the boy ’s face
and he try to shake his head the surgeon major bend down what be it bobby not that waltz mutter bobby that our own our very ownest own mummy dear with this he sink into the stupor that give place to death early next morning revere his eye red at the rim and his nose very white go into bobby ’s tent to write letter to papa wick which should bow the white head of the ex commissioner of chota life for shout boy shout for it make you jolly and free --the ramrod corps people who have see say that one of the quaint spectacle of human frailty be an outbreak of hysteric in girl school it start without warn generally on hot afternoon among the elder pupil girl giggle till the giggle get beyond control then she throw up her head and cry honk honk honk like wild goose and tear mix with the laughter if the mistress be wise she will rap out something severe at this point and check matter if she be tender hearted and send for drink of water the chance be largely in favor of another girl laugh at the afflict one and herself collapse thus the trouble spread and may end in half of what answer to the low sixth of boy school rock and whoop together give week of warm weather two stately promenade per diem heavy mutton and rice meal in the middle of the day certain amount of nagging from the teacher and few other thing some amazing effect develop at least this be what folk say who have have experience now the mother superior of convent and the colonel of british infantry regiment would be justly shock at any comparison be make between their respective charge
but it be fact that under certain circumstance thomas in bulk can be work up into ditthere ripple hysteria he do not weep but he show his trouble unmistakably and the consequence get into the newspaper and all the good people who hardly know martini from snider say take away the brute ’s ammunition thomas be not brute and his business which be to look after the virtuous people demand that he shall have his ammunition to his hand he do not wear silk stocking and he really ought to be supply with new adjective to help he to express his opinion but for all that he be great man if you call he the heroic defender of the national honor one day and brutal and licentious soldiery the next you naturally bewilder he and he look upon you with suspicion there be nobody to speak for thomas except people who have theory to work off on he and nobody understand thomas except thomas
and he do not always know what be the matter with himself that be the prologue this be the story corporal slane be engage to be married to miss jhansi m’kenna whose history be well know in the regiment and elsewhere he have his colonel ’s permission and be popular with the man every arrangement have be make to give the wedding what private ortheris call eeklar it fall in the heart of the hot weather and after the wedding slane be go up to the hill with the bride none the less slane ’s grievance be that the affair would be only hire carriage wedding
and he feel that the eeklar of that be meagre miss m’kenna do not care so much the sergeant ’s wife be help she to make her wedding dress
and she be very busy slane be just then the only moderately contented man in barrack all the rest be more or less miserable and they have so much to make they happy too all their work be over at eight in the morning and for the rest of the day they could lie on their back and smoke canteen plug and swear at the punkah cooly they enjoy fine full flesh meal in the middle of the day and then throw themselves down on their cot and sweat and sleep till it be cool enough to go out with their towny whose vocabulary contain less than six hundred word and the adjective and whose view on every conceivable question they have hear many time before there be the canteen of course and there be the temperance room with the second hand paper in it but man of any profession can not read for eight hour day in temperature of degree or degree in the shade run up sometimes to degree at midnight very few man even though they get pannikin of flat stale muddy beer and hide it under their cot can continue drink for six hour day one man try
but he die and nearly the whole regiment go to his funeral because it give they something to do it be too early for the excitement of fever or cholera the man could only wait and wait and wait and watch the shadow of the barrack creep across the blind white dust that be gay life they lounge about cantonment it be too hot for any sort of game and almost too hot for vice and fuddle themselves in the evening and fill themselves to distension with the healthy nitrogenous food provide for they and the more they stoke the less exercise they take and more explosive they grow then temper begin to wear away and man fall brood over insult real or imaginary for they have nothing else to think of the tone of the repartee change and instead of say light heartedly ’ll knock your silly face in man grow laboriously polite and hint that the cantonment be not big enough for themselves and their enemy and that there would be more space for one of the two in another place it may have be the devil who arrange the thing
but the fact of the case be that losson have for long time be worry simmon in an aimless way it give he occupation the two have their cot side by side and would sometimes spend long afternoon swear at each other but simmons be afraid of losson and dare not challenge he to fight he think over the word in the hot still night and half the hate he feel toward losson be vent on the wretched punkahcoolie losson buy parrot in the bazar and put it into little cage and lower the cage into the cool darkness of well and sit on the well curb shout bad language down to the parrot he teach it to say simmons ye so oor which mean swine and several other thing entirely unfit for publication he be big gross man and he shake like jelly when the parrot have the sentence correctly simmon however shake with rage for all the room be laugh at he the parrot be such disreputable puff of green feather and it look so human when it chatter losson use to sit swing his fat leg on the side of the cot and ask the parrot what it think of simmons the parrot would answer simmons
ye so oor good boy losson use to say scratch the parrot ’s head ye ear that sim and simmons use to turn over on his stomach and make answer ear take eed you do not ear something one of these day in the restless night after he have be asleep all day fit of blind rage come upon simmonr and hold he till he tremble all over while he think in how many different way he would slay losson sometimes he would picture himself trample the life out of the man with heavy ammunition boot and at other smash in his face with the butt and at other jump on his shoulder and drag the head back till the neckbone crack then his mouth would feel hot and fevered and he would reach out for another sup of the beer in the pannikin but the fancy that come to he most frequently and stay with he long be one connect with the great roll of fat under losson ’s right ear he notice it first on moonlight night and thereafter it be always before his eye it be fascinating roll of fat man could get his hand upon it and tear away one side of the neck or he could place the muzzle of rifle on it and blow away all the head in flash losson have no right to be sleek and contented and well to do when he simmon be the butt of the room some day perhaps he would show those who laugh at the simmons
ye so oor joke that he be as good as the rest and hold man ’s life in the crook of his forefinger when losson snore simmons hat he more bitterly than ever why should losson be able to sleep when simmon have to stay awake hour after hour toss and turn on the tape with the dull liver pain gnaw into his right side and his head throb and ache after canteen he think over this for many night and the world become unprofitable to he he even blunt his naturally fine appetite with beer and tobacco and all the while the parrot talk at and make mock of he the heat continue and the temper wear away more quickly than before sergeant ’s wife die of heat apoplexy in the night and the rumor run abroad that it be cholera man rejoice openly hop that it would spread and send they into camp but that be false alarm it be late on tuesday evening and the man be wait in the deep double veranda for last post when simmon go to the box at the foot of his bed take out his pipe and slam the lid down with bang that echo through the deserted barrack like the crack of rifle ordinarily speak the man would have take no notice but their nerve be fret to fiddle string they jump up and three or four clatter into the barrack room only to find simmon kneel by his box owl it you be it they say and laugh foolishly we think twas”-- simmon rise slowly if the accident have so shake his fellow what would not the reality do you think it be do you and what make you think he say lash himself into madness as he go on to hell with your thinking
ye dirty spy simmons ye so oor chuckle the parrot in the veranda sleepily recognize well know voice now that be absolutely all the tension snap simmon fall back on the arm rack deliberately,--the man be at the far end of the room,--and take out his rifle and packet of ammunition do not go play the goat sim say losson put it down but there be quaver in his voice another man stoop slip his boot and hurl it at simmon ’s head the prompt answer be shoot which fire at random find its billet in losson ’s throat losson fall forward without word and the other scatter you think it be yell simmons you ’re drivin to it tell you you ’re drivin to it get up losson an do not lie shammin there you an your blasted parrit that druv to it
but there be an unaffected reality about losson ’s pose that show simmon what he have do the man be still clamor on the veranda simmons appropriate two more packet of ammunition and run into the moonlight mutter ’ll make night of it thirty roun ’s an the last for myself take you that you dog he drop on one knee and fire into the brown of the man on the veranda but the bullet fly high and land in the brickwork with vicious phant that make some of the young one turn pale it be as musketry theorist observe one thing to fire and another to be fire at then the instinct of the chase flare up the news spread from barrack to barrack and the man double out intent on the capture of simmon the wild beast who be head for the cavalry parade ground stop now and again to send back shot and lurse in the direction of his pursuer ’ll learn you to spy on he shout ’ll learn you to give dorg ’s name come on the ole lot you colonel john anthony deever c.b.!”--he turn toward the infantry mess and shake it you dare not you bloomin dog shooter you lie you man sticker you sneakin sheeny butcher you lie see there slane kick the rifle away and stand up in the peril of his life come on now the temptation be more than simmons could resist for the corporal in his white clothe offer perfect mark do not misname shout simmons fire as he speak the shot miss and the shooter blind with rage throw his rifle down and rush at slane from the protection of the well within strike distance he kick savagely at slane ’s stomach but the weedy corporal know something of simmons ’s weakness and know too the deadly guard for that kick bow forward and draw up his right leg till the heel of the right foot be set some three inch above the inside of the left knee cap he meet the blow stand on one leg exactly as gond stand when they meditate and ready for the fall that would follow there be an oath the corporal fall over his own left as shinbone meet shinbone and the private collapse his right leg break an inch above the ankle pity you do not know that guard sim say slane spit out the dust as he rise then raise his voice--“come an take he orf ve bruk be leg this be not strictly true for the private have accomplish his own downfall since it be the special merit of that leg guard that the hard the kick the great the kicker ’s discomfiture slane walk to jerry blaze and hang over he with ostentatious anxiety while simmons weep with pain be carry away
you ai not urt badly sir say slane the major have faint and there be an ugly ragged hole through the top of his arm slane kneel down and murmur s’elp believe dead well if that ai not my bloom luck all over but the major be destine to lead his battery afield for many long day with unshaken nerve he be remove and nurse and pet into convalescence while the battery discuss the wisdom of capture simmon and blow he from gun they idolize their major and his reappearance on parade bring about scene nowhere provide for in the army regulation
great too be the glory that fall to slane ’s share the gunner would have make he drunk thrice day for at least fortnight even the colonel of his own regiment compliment he upon his coolness and the local paper call he hero these thing do not puff he up when the major offer he money and thank the virtuous corporal take the one and put aside the other

revolution in france they be sit in the veranda of the splendid palace of an indian pro consul surround by all the glory and mystery of the immemorial east in plain english it be one storied ten roomed whitewash mud roof bungalow set in dry garden of dusty tamarisk tree and divide from the road by low mud wall the green parrot scream overhead as they fly in battalion to the river for their morning drink beyond the wall cloud of fine dust show where the cattle and goat of the city be pas afield to graze the remorseless white light of the winter sunshine of northern india lie upon everything and improve nothing from the whine peisian wheel by the lawn tennis court to the long perspective of level road and the blue domed tomb of mohammedan saint just visible above the tree happy new year say orde to his guest it the first you ve ever spend out of england be not it yes happy new year say pagett smile at the sunshine what divine climate you have here just think of the brown cold fog hang over london now and he rub his hand it be more than twenty year since he have last see orde his schoolmate and their path in the world have divide early the one have quit college to become cog wheel in the machinery of the great indian government the other more bless with good have be whirl into similar position in the english scheme three successive election have not affect pagett ’s position with loyal constituency and he have grow insensibly to regard himself in some sort as pillar of the empire whose real worth would be know later on after few year of conscientious attendance at many division after newspaper battle innumerable and the publication of interminable correspondence and more hasty oratory than in his calm moment he care to think upon it occur to he as it have occur to many of his fellow in parliament that tour to india would enable he to sweep large lyre and address himself to the problem of imperial administration with firm hand accept therefore general invitation extend to he by orde some year before pagett have take ship to karachi and only over night have be receive with joy by the deputy commissioner of amara they have sit late discuss the change and chance of twenty year recall the name of the dead and weigh the future of the living as be the custom of man meet after interval of action next morning they smoke the after breakfast pipe in the veranda still regard each other curiously pagett in light grey frock coat and garment much too thin for the time of the year and puggrie sun hat carefully and wonderfully make orde in shoot coat rid breech brown cowhide boot with spur and batter flax helmet he have ride some mile in the early morning to inspect doubtful river dam the man ’s face differ as much as their attire orde ’s worn and wrinkle around the eye and grizzled at the temple be the hard and more square of the two and it be with something like envy that the owner look at the comfortable outline of pagett ’s blandly receptive countenance the clear skin the untroubled eye and the mobile clean shave lip and this be india say pagett for the twentieth time star long and intently at the grey feathering of the tamarisk one portion of india only it very much like this for mile in every direction by the way now that you have rest little would not ask the old question before what d’you think of the country tis the most pervasive country that ever yet be see acquire several pound of your country come up from karachi the air be heavy with it and for mile and mile along that distressful eternity of rail there no horizon to show where air and earth separate it be not easy to see truly or far in india
but you have decent passage out have not you very good on the whole your anglo indian may be unsympathetic about one ’s political view but he have reduce ship life to science the anglo indian be political orphan and if he wise he will not be in hurry to be adopt by your party grandmother
but how be your companion unsympathetic well there be man call dawlishe judge somewhere in this country it seem and capital partner at whist by the way and when want to talk to he about the progress of india in political sense orde hide grin which might or might not have be sympathetic the national congress movement and other thing in which as member of parliament of course
interested he shift the subject and when once corner he he look calmly in the eye and say that all tommy rot come and have game at bull you may laugh but that be not the way to treat great and important question and know who be well think it rather rude do not you know
and yet dawlishe be thoroughly good fellow yes
he friend of mine and one of the straight man know suppose like many anglo indians he feel it be hopeless to give you any just idea of any indian question without the document before you and in this case the document you want be the country and the people precisely that be why come straight to you bring an open mind to bear on thing anxious to know what popular feeling in india be really like y’know now that it have waken into political life the national congress in spite of dawlishe must have cause great excitement among the mass on the contrary nothing could be more tranquil than the state of popular feeling and as to excitement the people would as soon be excited over the rule of three as over the congress excuse orde
but do you think you be fair judge be not the official anglo indian naturally jealous of any external influence that might move the mass and so much opposed to liberal idea truly liberal idea that he can scarcely be expect to regard popular movement with fairness what do dawlishe say about tommy rot think moment old man you and be bring up together teach by the same tutor read the same book live the same life and new language and work among new race while you more fortunate remain at home why should change my mind our mind because change my sky why should and the few hundred englishman in my service become unreasonable prejudiced fossil while you and your new friend alone remain bright and open minded you surely do not fancy civilian be member of primrose league of course
but the mere position of an english official give he point of view which can not but bias his mind on this question pagett move his knee up and down little uneasily as he speak that sound plausible enough but like more plausible notion on indian matter believe it mistake you ’ll find when you come to consult the unofficial briton that our fault as class speak of the civilian now be rather to magnify the progress that have be make toward liberal institution it be of english origin such as it be and the stress of our work since the mutiny only thirty year ago have be in that direction no think you will get no fair or more dispassionate view of the congress business than such man as can give you but may as well say at once that those who know most of india from the inside be incline to wonder at the noise our scarcely begin experiment make in england but surely the gathering together of congress delegate be of itself new thing there nothing new under the sun when europe be jungle half asia flock to the canonical conference of buddhism and for century the people have gather at pun hurdwar trimbak and benare in immense number great meeting what you call mass meeting be really one of the old and most popular of indian institution in the case of the congress meet the only notable fact be that the priest of the altar be british not buddhist jam or brahmanical and that the whole thing be british contrivance keep alive by the effort of messr hume eardley norton and digby you mean to say then it not spontaneous movement
what movement be ever spontaneous in any true sense of the word this seem to be more factitious than usual you seem to know great deal about it try it by the touchstone of subscription coarse but fairly trustworthy criterion and there be scarcely the color of money in it the delegate write from england that they be out of pocket for work expense railway fare and stationery the mere pasteboard and scaffolding of their show it be in fact collapse from mere financial inanition but you can not deny that the people of india who be perhaps too poor to subscribe be mentally and morally move by the agitation pagett insist that be precisely what do deny the native side of the movement be the work of limited class microscopic minority as lord dufferin describe it when compare with the people proper but still very interesting class see that it be of our own creation it be compose almost entirely of those of the literary or clerkly caste who have receive an english education surely that very important class its member must be the ordain leader of popular thought anywhere else they might be leader but they have no social weight in this topsy turvy land and though they have be employ in clerical work for generation they have no practical knowledge of affair ship ’s clerk be useful person
but he be scarcely the captain and an orderly room writer however smart he may be be not the colonel you see the writer class in india have never till now aspire to anything like command it be not allow to the indian gentleman for thousand of year past have resemble victor hugo ’s noble un vrai sire chatelain laisse ecrire le vilain sa main digne quand il signe egratigne le velin and
the little egralignure he most like to make have be score pretty deeply by the sword
but this be childish and medheval nonsense precisely and from your or rather our point of view the pen be mighty than the sword in this country it otherwise the fault lie in our indian balance not yet adjust to civilized weight and measure well at all event this literary class represent the natural aspiration and wish of the people at large though it may not exactly lead they and in spite of all you say orde defy you to find really sound english radical who would not sympathize with those aspiration pagett speak with some warmth and he have scarcely cease when well appoint dog cart turn into the compound gate and orde rise say here be edwards the master of the lodge neglect so diligently come to talk about account suppose as the vehicle drive up under the porch pagett also rise say with the train effusion bear of much practice but this be also my friend my old and value friend edwards delight to see you know you be in india but not exactly where then it be not account mr edwards say orde cheerily why no sir hear mr pagett be come and as our work be close for the new year think would drive over and see he very happy think mr edwards
you may not know orde be lead member of our radical club at switebton when be begin political life and owe much to his exertion there no pleasure like meet an old friend except perhaps make new one suppose mr edwards you stick to the good old cause
well you see sir thing be different out here there precious little one can find to say against the government which be the main of our talk at home and they that do say thing be not the sort people man who respect himself would like to be mix up with there be no politic in manner of speak in india it all work surely you be mistake my good friend why have come all the way from england just to see the working of this great national movement do not know where you ’re go to find the nation as move to begin with

but do they marry so early say pagett vaguely the average age be seven but thousand be marry still early one result be that girl of twelve and thirteen have to bear the burden of wifehood and motherhood and as might be expect the rate of mortality both for mother and child be terrible pauperism domestic unhappiness and low state of health be only few of the consequence of this then when as frequently happen the boy husband die prematurely his widow be condemn to bad than death she may not re marry must live secluded and despise life life so unnatural that she sometimes prefer suicide more often she go astray you do not know in england what such word as infant marriage baby wife girl mother and virgin widow mean

look you have cast out love what god be these you bid please the three in one the one in three not so to my own god go it may be they shall give great ease than your cold christ and tangle trinity the convert she be the daughter of sonoo hill man and jadeh his wife one year their maize fail and two bear spend the night in their only poppy field just above the sutlej valley on the kotgarth side so next season they turn christian and bring their baby to the mission to be baptize the kotgarth chaplain christen she elizabeth
and lispeth be the hill or pahari pronunciation later cholera come into the kotgarth valley and carry off sonoo and jadeh and lispeth become half servant half companion to the wife of the then chaplain of kotgarth this be after the reign of the moravian missionary but before kotgarth have quite forget her title of mistress of the northern hill whether christianity improve lispeth or whether the god of her own people would have do as much for she under any circumstance do not know
but she grow very lovely when hill girl grow lovely she be worth travel fifty mile over bad ground to look upon lispeth have greek face one of those face people paint so often and see so seldom she be of pale ivory color and for her race extremely tall also she possess eye that be wonderful and have she not be dress in the abominable print cloth affect by mission you would meet she on the hill side unexpectedly have think she the original diana of the romans go out to slay lispeth take to christianity readily and do not abandon it when she reach womanhood as do some hill girl her own people hat she because she have they say become memsahib and wash herself daily and the chaplain 's wife do not know what to do with she somehow one can not ask stately goddess five foot ten in her shoe to clean plate and dish so she play with the chaplain 's child and take class in the sunday school and read all the book in the house and grow more and more beautiful like the princess in fairy tale the chaplain 's wife say that the girl ought to take service in simla as nurse or something genteel but lispeth do not want to take service she be very happy where she be when traveller there be not many in those year come to kotgarth lispeth use to lock herself into her own room for fear they might take she away to simla or somewhere out into the unknown world one day few month after she be seventeen year old lispeth go out for walk she do not walk in the manner of english lady mile and half out and ride back again she cover between twenty and thirty mile in her little constitutional all about and about between kotgarth and narkunda this time she come back at full dusk step down the breakneck descent into kotgarth with something heavy in her arm the chaplain 's wife be doze in the drawing room when lispeth come in breathe hard and very exhausted with her burden lispeth put it down on the sofa and say simply this be my husband find he on the bagi road he have hurt himself we will nurse he and when he be well your husband shall marry he to this be the first mention lispeth have ever make of her matrimonial view and the chaplain 's wife shriek with horror however the man on the sofa need attention first he be young englishman and his head have be cut to the bone by something jagged lispeth say she have find he down the khud
so she have bre he in he be breathe queerly and be unconscious he be put to bed and tend by the chaplain who know something of medicine and lispeth wait outside the door in case she could be useful she explain to the chaplain that this be the man she mean to marry and the chaplain and his wife lecture she severely on the impropriety of her conduct lispeth listen quietly and repeat her first proposition it take great deal of christianity to wipe out uncivilized eastern instinct such as fall in love at first sight lispeth having find the man she worship do not see why she should keep silent as to her choice she have no intention of be send away either she be go to nurse that englishman until he be well enough to marry she this be her little programme after fortnight of slight fever and inflammation the englishman recover coherence and thank the chaplain and his wife and lispeth especially lispeth for their kindness he be traveller in the east he say they never talk about globe trotter in those day when the p. o. fleet be young and small and have come from dehra dun to hunt for plant and butterfly among the simla hill no one at simla therefore know anything about he he fancy he must have fall over the cliff while stalk fern on rotten tree trunk and that his cooly must have steal his baggage and flee he think he would go back to simla when he be little strong he desire no more mountaineering he make small haste to go away and recover his strength slowly lispeth object to be advise either by the chaplain or his wife so the latter speak to the englishman and tell he how matter stand in lispeth 's heart he laugh good deal and say it be very pretty and romantic perfect idyl of the himalayas but as he be engage to girl at home he fancy that nothing would happen certainly he would behave with discretion he do that still he find it very pleasant to talk to lispeth and walk with lispeth and say nice thing to she and call her pet name while he be get strong enough to go away it mean nothing at all to he and everything in the world to lispeth she be very happy while the fortnight last because she have find man to love be savage by birth she take no trouble to hide her feeling and the englishman be amuse when he go away lispeth walk with he up the hill as far as narkunda very troubled and very miserable the chaplain 's wife be good christian and dislike anything in the shape of fuss or scandal lispeth be beyond her management entirely have tell the englishman to tell lispeth that he be come back to marry she she be but child you know and fear at heart heathen say the chaplain 's wife so all the twelve mile up the hill the englishman with his arm around lispeth 's waist be assure the girl that he would come back and marry she and lispeth make he promise over and over again she weep on the narkunda ridge till he have pas out of sight along the muttiani path
then she dry her tear and go in to kotgarth again and say to the chaplain 's wife he will come back and marry he have go to his own people to tell they so and the chaplain 's wife soothe lispeth and say he will come back at the end of two month lispeth grow impatient and be tell that the englishman have go over the sea to england she know where england be because she have read little geography primer but of course she have no conception of the nature of the sea be hill girl there be an old puzzle map of the world in the house lispeth have play with it when she be child she unearth it again and put it together of evening and cry to herself and try to imagine where her englishman be as she have no idea of distance or steamboat her notion be somewhat erroneous it would not have make the least difference have she be perfectly correct for the englishman have no intention of come back to marry hill girl he forget all about she by the time he be butterfly hunting in assam he write book on the east afterwards lispeth 's name do not appear at the end of three month lispeth make daily pilgrimage to narkunda to see if her englishman be come along the road it give her comfort and the chaplain 's wife find she happy think that she be get over she barbarous and most indelicate folly little later the walk cease to help lispeth and her temper grow very bad the chaplain 's wife think this profitable time to let she know the real state of affair that the englishman have only promise his love to keep she quiet that he have never mean anything and that it be wrong and improper of lispeth to think of marriage with an englishman who be of superior clay besides be promise in marriage to girl of his own people lispeth say that all this be clearly impossible because he have say he love she and the chaplain 's wife have with her own lip assert that the englishman be come back how can what he and you say be untrue ask lispeth we say it as an excuse to keep you quiet child say the chaplain 's wife then you have lie to say lispeth you and he the chaplain 's wife bow her head and say nothing lispeth be silent too for little time then she go out down the valley and return in the dress of hill girl infamously dirty but without the nose and ear ring she have her hair braid into the long pig tail help out with black thread that hill woman wear be go back to my own people say she you have kill lispeth there be only leave old jadeh 's daughter the daughter of pahari and the servant of tarka devi you be all liar you english by the time that the chaplain 's wife have recover from the shock of the announcement that lispeth have verte to her mother 's god the girl have go and she never come back she take to her own unclean people savagely as if to make up the arrear of the life she have step out of and in little time she marry wood cutter who beat she after the manner of paharis and her beauty fade soon there be no law whereby you can account for the vagary of the heathen say the chaplain 's wife and believe that lispeth be always at heart an infidel see she have be take into the church of england at the mature age of five week this statement do not do credit to the chaplain 's wife lispeth be very old woman when she die she always have perfect command of english and when she be sufficiently drunk could sometimes be induce to tell the story of her first love affair it be hard then to realize that the bleared wrinkled creature so like wisp of charred rag could ever have be lispeth of the kotgarth mission three and an extra when halter and heel rope be slip do not give chase with stick but with gram punjabi proverb after marriage arrive reaction sometimes big sometimes little one but it come soon or later and must be tide over by both party if they desire the rest of their life to go with the current in the case of the cusack bremmil this reaction do not set in till the third year after the wedding bremmil be hard to hold at the good of time but he be beautiful husband until the baby die and mrs bremmil wear black and grow thin and mourn as if the bottom of the universe have fall out perhaps bremmil ought to have comfort she he try to do so think but the more he comfort the more mrs bremmil grieve and consequently the more uncomfortable bremmil grow the fact be that they both need tonic and they get it mrs bremmil can afford to laugh now but it be no laugh matter to she at the time you see mrs hauksbee appear on the horizon and where she exist be fair chance of trouble at simla she bye name be the stormy petrel she have win that title five time to my own certain knowledge she be little brown thin almost skinny woman with big rolling violet blue eye and the sweet manner in the world you have only to mention her name at afternoon tea for every woman in the room to rise up and call she well not bless she be clever witty brilliant and sparkling beyond most of her kind but possess of many devil of malice and mischievousness she could be nice though even to her own sex but that be another story bremmil go off at score after the baby 's death and the general discomfort that follow and mrs hauksbee annex he she take no pleasure in hide her captive she annex he publicly and see that the public see it he ride with she and walk with she and talk with she and picnic with she and tiffine at peliti be with she till people put up their eyebrow and say shocking mrs bremmil stay at home turn over the dead baby 's frock and cry into the empty cradle she do not care to do anything else
but some eight dear affectionate lady friend explain the situation at length to she in case she should miss the cream of it mrs bremmil listen quietly and thank they for their good office she be not as clever as mrs hauksbee
but she be no fool she keep her own counsel and do not speak to bremmil of what she have hear this be worth remember speak to or cry over husband never do any good yet when bremmil be at home which be not often he be more affectionate than usual and that show his hand the affection be force partly to soothe his own conscience and partly to soothe mrs bremmil it fail in both regard then the a.-d.-c
in wait be command by their excellency lord and lady lytton to invite mr and mrs cusack bremmil to peterhoff on july 26th at 9.30 p. m. ”--“dance in the bottom leave hand corner can not go say mrs bremmil it be too soon after poor little florrie but it need not stop you tom she mean what she say then and bremmil say that he would go just to put in an appearance here he speak the thing which be not and mrs bremmil know it she guess woman 's guess be much more accurate than man 's certainty that he have mean to go from the first and with mrs hauksbee she sit down to think and the outcome of her thought be that the memory of dead child be worth considerably less than the affection of live husband she make her plan and stake she all upon it in that hour
she discover that she know tom bremmil thoroughly and this knowledge she act on tom say she shall be din out at the longmore on the evening of the 26th you would well dine at the club this save bremmil from make an excuse to get away and dine with mrs hauksbee
so he be grateful and feel small and mean at the same time which be wholesome bremmil leave the house at five for ride about half past five in the evening large leather cover basket come in from phelps for mrs bremmil she be woman who know how to dress
and she have not spend week on design that dress and have it gore and hem and herring bone and tuck and ruck or whatever the term be for nothing it be gorgeous dress slight mourning can not describe it
but it be what the queen call creation”--a thing that hit you straight between the eye and make you gasp she have not much heart for what she be go to do but as she glance at the long mirror she have the satisfaction of know that she have never look so well in her life she be large blonde and when she choose carry herself superbly after the dinner at the longmore she go on to the dance little late and encounter bremmil with mrs hauksbee on his arm that make she flush and as the man crowd round she for dance she look magnificent she fill up all her dance except three and those she leave blank mrs hauksbee catch her eye once
and she know it be war real war between they she start handicap in the struggle for she have order bremmil about just the least little bit in the world too much and he be begin to resent it moreover he have never see his wife look so lovely he star at she from doorway and glare at she from passage as she go about with her partner and the more he star the more take be he he could scarcely believe that this be the woman with the red eye and the black stuff gown who use to weep over the egg at breakfast mrs hauksbee do she good to hold he in play but after two dance he cross over to his wife and ask for dance be afraid you have come too late mister bremmil she say with her eye twinkle
then he beg she to give he dance and as great favor she allow he the fifth waltz luckily stand vacant on his programme they dance it together and there be little flutter round the room bremmil have sort of notion that his wife could dance but he never know she dance so divinely at the end of that waltz he ask for another as favor not as right and mrs bremmil say show your programme dear he show it as naughty little schoolboy hand up contraband sweet to master there be fair sprinkling of on it besides at supper mrs bremmil say nothing

silly silly boy mrs hauksbee hear that and she own as much feel that she have the bad of it bremmil accept and gratefully they dance and sit out in one of the little tent what bremmil say and what mrs bremmil say be no concern of any one be when the band strike up the roast beef of old england the two go out into the verandah and bremmil begin look for his wife 's dandy this be before rickshaw day while she go into the cloak room mrs hauksbee come up and say you take in to supper think mr bremmil bremmil turn red and look foolish ah h'm be go home with my wife
mrs hauksbee think there have be little mistake be man he speak as though mrs hauksbee be entirely responsible mrs bremmil come out of the cloak room in swansdown cloak with white cloud round her head she look radiant and she have right to the couple go off in the darkness together bremmil rid very close to the dandy then say mrs hauksbee to she look trifle faded and jaded in the lamplight take my word for it the silly woman can manage clever man
but it need very clever woman to manage fool then we go in to supper throw away and some be sulky while some will plunge
so ho steady stand still you some you must gentle and some you must lunge who want to kill you some there be loss in every trade-- will break their heart ere bit and make will fight like fiend as the rope cut hard and die dumb mad in the breaking yard toolungala stockyard chorus to rear boy under what parent call the sheltered life system be if the boy must go into the world and fend for himself not wise unless he be one in thousand he have certainly to pass through many unnecessary trouble and may possibly come to extreme grief simply from ignorance of the proper proportion of thing let puppy eat the soap in the bath room or chew newly black boot he chew and chuckle until by and by he find out that black and old brown windsor make he very sick
