so he argue that soap and boot be not wholesome any old dog about the house will soon show he the unwisdom of bite big dog ear be young he remember and go abroad at six month well mannered little beast with chasten appetite if he have be keep away from boot and soap and big dog till he come to the trinity full grow and with develop tooth just consider how fearfully sick and thrash he would be apply that motion to the shelter life and see how it work it do not sound pretty but it be the well of two evil there be boy once who have be bre up under the sheltered life theory and the theory kill he dead he stay with his people all his day from the hour he be bear till the hour he go into sandhurst nearly at the top of the list he be beautifully teach in all that win mark by private tutor and carry the extra weight of never having give his parent an hour 's anxiety in his life what he learn at sandhurst beyond the regular routine be of no great consequence he look about he and he find soap and black so to speak very good he eat little and come out of sandhurst not so high as he go in then there be an interval and scene with his people who expect much from he next year of live unspotted from the world in third rate depot battalion where all the junior be child and all the senior old woman and lastly he come out to india where he be cut off from the support of his parent and have no one to fall back on in time of trouble except himself now india be place beyond all other where one must not take thing too seriously the midday sun always except too much work and too much energy kill man just as effectively as too much assorted vice or too much drink flirtation do not matter because every one be be transfer and either you
or she leave the station and never return good work do not matter because man be judge by his bad output and another man take all the credit of his good as rule bad work do not matter because other man do bad and incompetent hang on long in india than anywhere else amusement do not matter because you must repeat they as soon as you have accomplish they once and most amusement only mean try to win another person 's money sickness do not matter because it be all in the day 's work and if you die another man take over your place and your office in the eight hour between death and burial nothing matter except home furlough and act allowance and these only because they be scarce this be slack kutcha country where all man work with imperfect instrument and the wise thing be to take no one and nothing in earnest but to escape as soon as ever you can to some place where amusement be amusement and reputation worth the have but this boy the tale be as old as the hill come out and take all thing seriously he be pretty and be pet he take the petting seriously and fret over woman not worth saddle pony to call upon he find his new free life in india very good it do look attractive in the beginning from subaltern 's point of view all pony partner dancing and so on he taste it as the puppy taste the soap only he come late to the eating with grow set of tooth he have no sense of balance just like the puppy and could not understand why he be not treat with the consideration he receive under his father 's roof this hurt his feeling he quarrel with other boy and be sensitive to the marrow remember these quarrel and they excite he he find whist and gymkhana and thing of that kind mean to amuse one after office
but he take they seriously too just as he take the head that follow after drink he lose his money over whist and gymkhanas because they be new to he he take his loss seriously and waste as much energy and interest over two goldmohur race for maiden ekka pony with their mane hog as if it have be the derby one half of this come from inexperience much as the puppy squabble with the corner of the hearth rug and the other half from the dizziness breed by stumble out of his quiet life into the glare and excitement of lively one no one tell he about the soap and the blacking because an average man take it for grant that an average man be ordinarily careful in regard to they it be pitiful to watch the boy knock himself to piece as an over handle colt fall down and cut himself when he get away from the groom this unbridled license in amusement not worth the trouble of break line for much less rioting over endure for six month all through one cold weather
and then we think that the heat and the knowledge of having lose his money and health and lam his horse would sober the boy down and he would stand steady in ninety nine case out of hundred this would have happen you can see the principle work in any indian station
but this particular case fall through because the boy be sensitive and take thing seriously as may have say some seven time before of course we could not tell how his excess strike he personally they be nothing very heart break or above the average he might be crippled for life financially and want little nursing still the memory of his performance would wither away in one hot weather and the shroff would help he to tide over the money trouble but he must have take another view altogether and have believe himself ruin beyond redemption his colonel talk to he severely when the cold weather end that make he more wretched than ever and it be only an ordinary colonel 's wigging what follow be curious instance of the fashion in which we be all link together and make responsible for one another the thing that kick the beam in the boy 's mind be remark that woman make when he be talk to she there be no use in repeat it for it be only cruel little sentence rap out before think that make he flush to the root of his hair he keep himself to himself for three day and then put in for two day leave to go shoot near canal engineer 's rest house about thirty mile out he get his leave and that night at mess be noisy and more offensive than ever he say that he be go to shoot big game and leave at half past ten o'clock in an ekka partridge which be the only thing man could get near the rest house be not big game so every one laugh next morning one of the major come in from short leave and hear that the boy have go out to shoot big game the major have take an interest in the boy and have more than once try to check he in the cold weather the major put up his eyebrow when he hear of the expedition and go to the boy 's room where he rummage presently he come out and find leave card on the mess there be no one else in the ante room he say the boy have go out shooting do man shoot tetur with revolver and writing case say nonsense major for see what be in his mind he say nonsense or nonsense be go to the canal now at once do not feel easy then he think for minute and say can you lie you know well answer it be my profession very well say the major you must come out with now at once in an ekka to the canal to shoot black buck go and put on shikar kit quick and drive here with gun the major be masterful man and know that he would not give order for nothing so obey and on return find the major pack up in an ekka gun case and food sling below all ready for shoot trip he dismiss the driver and drive himself we jog along quietly while in the station but as soon as we get to the dusty road across the plain he make that pony fly country breed can do nearly anything at pinch we cover the thirty mile in under three hour
but the poor brute be nearly dead once say what be the blaze hurry major he say quietly the boy have be alone by himself for one two five fourteen hour now tell you do not feel easy this uneasiness spread itself to and help to beat the pony when we come to the canal engineer 's rest house the major call for the boy 's servant but there be no answer then we go up to the house call for the boy by name but there be no answer
oh he be out shoot say i. just then see through one of the window little hurricane lamp burn this be at four in the afternoon we both stop dead in the verandah hold our breath to catch every sound and we hear inside the room the brr brr brr of multitude of fly the major say nothing
but he take off his helmet and we enter very softly the boy be dead on the charpoy in the centre of the bare lime wash room he have shoot his head nearly to piece with his revolver the gun case be still strapped so be the bedding and on the table lie the boy 's writing case with photograph he have go away to die like poison rat the major say to himself softly poor boy poor poor devil then he turn away from the bed and say want your help in this business know the boy be dead by his own hand see exactly what that help would be so pass over to the table take chair light cheroot and begin to go through the writing case the major look over my shoulder and repeat to himself we come too late!--like rat in hole!--poor poor devil the boy must have spend half the night in write to his people and to his colonel and to girl at home and as soon as he have finish must have shoot himself for he have be dead long time when we come in read all that he have write and pass over each sheet to the major as finish it we see from his account how very seriously he have take everything he write about disgrace which he be unable to bear”--“indelible shame”--“criminal folly”--“wasted life and so on besides lot of private thing to his father and mother too much too sacred to put into print the letter to the girl at home be the most pitiful of all and choke as read it the major make no attempt to keep dry eyed respect he for that he read and rock himself to and fro and simply cry like woman without care to hide it the letter be so dreary and hopeless and touching we forget all about the boy 's folly and only thought of the poor thing on the charpoy and the scrawl sheet in our hand it be utterly impossible to let the letter go home they would have break his father 's heart and kill his mother after kill her belief in her son at last the major dry his eye openly and say nice sort of thing to spring on an english family what shall we do say know what the major have bre but for the boy die of cholera we be with he at the time we can not commit ourselves to half measure come along then begin one of the most grimy comic scene have ever take part in the concoction of big write lie bolster with evidence to soothe the boy 's people at home begin the rough draft of letter the major throw in hint here and there while he gather up all the stuff that the boy have write and burn it in the fireplace it be hot still evening when we begin and the lamp burn very badly in due course get the draft to my satisfaction set forth how the boy be the pattern of all virtue belove by his regiment with every promise of great career before he and so on how we have help he through the sickness it be no time for little lie you will understand and how he have die without pain choke while be put down these thing and thinking of the poor people who would read they then laugh at the grotesqueness of the affair and the laughter mix itself up with the choke and the major say that we both want drink be afraid to say how much whiskey we drink before the letter be finish it have not the least effect on we then we take off the boy 's watch locket and ring lastly the major say we must send lock of hair too woman value that
but there be reason why we could not find lock fit to send the boy be black haired and so be the major luckily cut off piece of the major 's hair above the temple with knife and put it into the packet we be make the laugh fit and the choke get hold of again and have to stop the major be nearly as bad and we both know that the bad part of the work be to come we seal up the packet photograph locket seal ring letter and lock of hair with the boy 's seal wax and the boy 's seal then the major say for god 's sake let we get outside away from the room and think we go outside and walk on the bank of the canal for an hour eat and drink what we have with we until the moon rise know now exactly how murderer feel finally we force ourselves back to the room with the lamp and the other thing in it and begin to take up the next piece of work be not go to write about this it be too horrible we burn the bedstead and drop the ash into the canal we take up the matting of the room and treat that in the same way go off to village and borrow two big hoe do not want the villager to help while the major arrange the other matter it take we four hour hard work to make the grave as we work we argue out whether it be right to say as much as we remember of the burial of the dead we compromise thing by say the lord 's prayer with private unofficial prayer for the peace of the soul of the boy then we fill in the grave and go into the verandah not the house to lie down to sleep we be dead tired when we wake the major say wearily we can not go back till to morrow we must give he decent time to die in he die early this morning remember that seem more natural so the major must have be lie awake all the time think say then why do not we bring the body back to the cantonment the major think for minute:--“because the people bolt when they hear of the cholera and the ekka have go that be strictly true we have forget all about the ekka pony and he have go home so we be leave there alone all that stifle day in the canal rest house testing and re test our story of the boy 's death to see if it be weak at any point native turn up in the afternoon
but we say that sahib be dead of cholera and he run away as the dusk gather the major tell all his fear about the boy and awful story of suicide or nearly carry out suicide tale that make one 's hair crisp he say that he himself have once go into the same valley of the shadow as the boy when he be young and new to the country so he understand how thing fight together in the boy 's poor jumbled head he also say that youngster in their repentant moment consider their sin much more serious and ineffaceable than they really be we talk together all through the evening and rehearse the story of the death of the boy as soon as the moon be up and the boy theoretically just bury we strike across country for the station we walk from eight till six o'clock in the morning
but though we be dead tired we do not forget to go to the boy 's room and put away his revolver with the proper amount of cartridge in the pouch also to set his writing case on the table we find the colonel and report the death feel more like murderer than ever then we go to bed and sleep the clock round for there be no more in we the tale have credence as long as be necessary for every one forget about the boy before fortnight be over many people however find time to say that the major have behave scandalously in not bring in the body for regimental funeral the sad thing of all be letter from the boy 's mother to the major and with big inky blister all over the sheet she write the sweet possible thing about our great kindness and the obligation she would be under to we as long as she live all thing consider she be under an obligation but not exactly as she mean miss youghal 's sais when man and woman be agree what can the kazi do mahomedan proverb some people say that there be no romance in india those people be wrong our life hold quite as much romance as be good for we sometimes more strickland be in the police and people do not understand he so they say he be doubtful sort of man and pass by on the other side strickland have himself to thank for this he hold the extraordinary theory that policeman in india should try to know as much about the native as the native themselves now in the whole of upper india there be only one man who can pass for hindu or mohammedan chamar or faquir as he please he be fear and respect by the native from the ghor kathri to the jamma musjid and he be suppose to have the gift of invisibility and executive control over many devil but what good have this do he with the government none in the world he have never get simla for his charge and his name be almost unknown to englishmen strickland be foolish enough to take that man for his model and follow out his absurd theory dabble in unsavory place no respectable man would think of explore all among the native riff raff he educate himself in this peculiar way for seven year and people could not appreciate it he be perpetually go fantee among the native which of course no man with any sense believe in he be initiate into the sat bhai at allahabad once when he be on leave he know the lizard song of the sansis and the halli hukk dance which be religious can can of startling kind when man know who dance the halli hukk and how and when and where he know something to be proud of he have go deep than the skin but strickland be not proud though he have help once at jagadhri at the painting of the death bull which no englishman must even look upon have master the thieves'-patter of the changar have take eusufzai horse thief alone near attock and have stand under the mimbar board of border mosque and conduct service in the manner of sunni mollah his crowning achievement be spend eleven day as faquir in the garden of baba atal at amritsar and there pick up the thread of the great nasiban murder case but people say justly enough why on earth can not strickland sit in his office and write up his diary and recruit and keep quiet instead of show up the incapacity of his senior so the nasiban murder case do he no good departmentally but after his first feeling of wrath he return to his outlandish custom of pry into native life by the way when man once acquire taste for this particular amusement it abide with he all his day it be the most fascinating thing in the world love not except where other man take ten day to the hill strickland take leave for what he call shikar put on the disguise that appeal to he at the time step down into the brown crowd and be swallow up for while he be quiet dark young fellow spare black eye and when he be not think of something else very interesting companion strickland on native progress as he have see it be worth hear native hat strickland
but they be afraid of he he know too much when the youghal come into the station strickland very gravely as he do everything fall in love with miss youghal and she after while fall in love with he because she could not understand he then strickland tell the parent but
mrs youghal
say she be not go to throw her daughter into the bad pay department in the empire and old youghal say in so many word that he mistrust strickland 's way and work and would thank he not to speak or write to his daughter any more very well say strickland for he do not wish to make his lady love 's life burden after one long talk with miss youghal he drop the business entirely the youghal go up to simla in april in july strickland secure three month leave on urgent private affair he lock up his house though not native in the providence would wittingly have touch estreekin sahib 's gear for the world and go down to see friend of his an old dyer at tarn taran here all trace of he be lose until sais meet on the simla mall with this extraordinary note dear old man please give bearer box of cheroot super no for preference they be fresh at the club will repay when reappear but at present be out of society yours e. strickland order two box and hand they over to the sais with my love that sais be strickland and he be in old youghal 's employ attach to miss youghal 's arab the poor fellow be suffer for an english smoke and know that whatever happen should hold my tongue till the business be over later on mrs youghal who be wrap up in her servant begin talk at house where she call of her paragon among saise the man who be never too busy to get up in the morning and pick flower for the breakfast table and who black actually black the hoofs of his horse like london coachman the turnout of miss youghal 's arab be wonder and delight strickland dulloo mean find his reward in the pretty thing that miss youghal say to he when she go out rid her parent be pleased to find she have forget all her foolishness for young strickland and say she be good girl strickland vow that the two month of his service be the most rigid mental discipline he have ever go through quite apart from the little fact that the wife of one of his fellow saise fall in love with he and then try to poison he with arsenic because he would have nothing to do with she he have to school himself into keep quiet when miss youghal go out rid with some man who try to flirt with she and he be force to trot behind carry the blanket and hear every word also he have to keep his temper when he be slang in benmore porch by policeman especially once when he be abuse by naik he have himself recruit from isser jang village or bad still when young subaltern call he pig for not make way quickly enough but the life have its compensation he obtain great insight into the way and theft of saise enough he say to have summarily convict half the chamar population of the punjab if he have be on business he become one of the lead player at knuckle bone which all jhampani and many saise play while they be wait outside the government house or the gaiety theatre of night he learn to smoke tobacco that be three fourth cowdung and he hear the wisdom of the grizzled jemadar of the government house saise whose word be valuable he see many thing which amuse he and he state on honor that no man can appreciate simla properly till he have see it from the sais 's point of view he also say that if he choose to write all he see his head would be break in several place strickland 's account of the agony he endure on wet night hear the music and see the light in benmore with his toe tingle for waltz and his head in horse blanket be rather amusing one of these day strickland be go to write little book on his experience that book will be worth buy and even more worth suppress thus he serve faithfully as jacob serve for rachel and his leave be nearly at an end when the explosion come he have really do his good to keep his temper in the hearing of the flirtation have mention
but he break down at last an old and very distinguished general take miss youghal for ride and begin that specially offensive you be only little girl sort of flirtation most difficult for woman to turn aside deftly and most maddening to listen to miss youghal be shake with fear at the thing he say in the hearing of her sais dulloo strickland stand it as long as he could then he catch hold of the general 's bridle and in most fluent english invite he to step off and be heave over the cliff next minute miss youghal begin cry and strickland see that he have hopelessly give himself away and everything be over the general nearly have fit while miss youghal be sob out the story of the disguise and the engagement that be not recognize by the parent strickland be furiously angry with himself and more angry with the general for force his hand
so he say nothing but hold the horse 's head and prepare to thrash the general as some sort of satisfaction but when the general have thoroughly grasp the story and know who strickland be he begin to puff and blow in the saddle and nearly roll off with laugh he say strickland deserve v. c. if it be only for put on sais 's blanket then he call himself name and vow that he deserve thrash but he be too old to take it from strickland then he compliment miss youghal on her lover the scandal of the business never strike he for he be nice old man with weakness for flirtation then he laugh again and say that old youghal be fool strickland let go of the cob 's head and suggest that the general have well help they if that be his opinion strickland know youghal 's weakness for man with title and letter after their name and high official position it be rather like forty minute farce say the general but begad will help if it be only to escape that tremendous thrashing deserve go along to your home my sais policeman and change into decent kit and will attack mr youghal miss youghal may ask you to canter home and wait about seven minute later there be wild hurroosh at the club sais with blanket and head rope be ask all the man he know for heaven 's sake lend decent clothe as the man do not recognize he there be some peculiar scene before strickland could get hot bath with soda in it in one room shirt here collar there pair of trouser elsewhere and so on he gallop off with half the club wardrobe on his back and an utter stranger 's pony under he to the house of old youghal the general array in purple and fine linen be before he what the general have say strickland never know but youghal receive strickland with moderate civility and mrs youghal touch by the devotion of the transform dulloo be almost kind the general beam and chuckle and miss youghal come in and almost before old youghal know where he be the parental consent have be wrench out and strickland have depart with miss youghal to the telegraph office to wire for his kit the final embarrassment be when an utter strange attack he on the mall and ask for the steal pony so in the end strickland and miss youghal be marry on the strict understanding that strickland should drop his old way and stick to departmental routine which pay good and lead to simla strickland be far too fond of his wife just then to break his word
but it be sore trial to he for the street and the bazar and the sound in they be full of meaning to strickland and these call to he to come back and take up his wander and his discovery some day will tell you how he break his promise to help friend that be long since and he have by this time be nearly spoilt for what he would call shikar he be forget the slang and the beggar 's can not and the mark and the sign and the drift of the undercurrent which if man would master he must always continue to learn but he fill in his departmental return beautifully yoke with an unbeliever be die for you and you be die for another punjabi proverb when the gravesend tender leave the p. o. steamer for bombay and go back to catch the train to town there be many people in it cry but the one who weep most and most openly be miss agnes laiter she have reason to cry because the only man she ever love or ever could love so she say be go out to india and india as every one know be divide equally between jungle tiger cobra cholera and sepoy phil garron lean over the side of the steamer in the rain feel very unhappy too
but he do not cry he be send out to tea what tea mean he have not the vague idea but fancy that he would have to ride on prance horse over hill cover with tea vine and draw sumptuous salary for do
so and he be very grateful to his uncle for get he the berth he be really go to reform all his slack shiftless way save large proportion of his magnificent salary yearly and in very short time return to marry agnes laiter phil garron have be lie loose on his friend hand for three year and as he have nothing to do he naturally fall in love he be very nice
but he be not strong in his view and opinion and principle and though he never come to actual grief his friend be thankful when he say good bye and go out to this mysterious tea business near darjile they said:--“god bless you dear boy let we never see your face
again,”--or at least that be what phil be give to understand when he sail he be very full of great plan to prove himself several hundred time well than any one have give he credit for to work like horse and triumphantly marry agnes laiter he have many good point besides his good look his only fault be that he be weak the least little bit in the world weak he have as much notion of economy as the morning sun and yet you could not lay your hand on any one item and say herein phil garron be extravagant or reckless nor could you point out any particular vice in his character but he be unsatisfactory and as workable as putty agnes laiter go about her duty at home her family object to the engagement with red eye while phil be sail to darjiling--“a port on the bengal ocean as his mother use to tell her friend he be popular enough on board ship make many acquaintance and moderately large liquor bill and send off huge letter to agnes laiter at each port then he fall to work on this plantation somewhere between darjiling and kangra and though the salary and the horse and the work be not quite all he have fancy he succeed fairly well and give himself much unnecessary credit for his perseverance in the course of time as he settle more into collar and his work grow fix before he the face of agnes laiter go out of his mind and only come when he be at leisure which be not often he would forget all about she for fortnight and remember she with start like school boy who have forget to learn his lesson she do not forget phil because she be of the kind that never forget only another man really desirable young man present himself before mrs laiter and the chance of marriage with phil be as far off as ever and his letter be so unsatisfactory and there be certain amount of domestic pressure bring to bear on the girl and the young man really be an eligible person as income go and the end of all thing be that agnes marry he and write tempestuous whirlwind of letter to phil in the wild of darjiling and say she should never know happy moment all the rest of her life which be true prophecy phil get that letter and hold himself ill treat this be two year after he have come out but by dint of think fixedly of agnes laiter and look at her photograph and pat himself on the back for be one of the most constant lover in history and warm to the work as he go on he really fancy that he have be very hardly use he sit down and write one final letter really pathetic world without end
amen epistle explain how he would be true to eternity and that all woman be very much alike and he would hide his broken heart etc etc but if at any future time etc etc he could afford to wait etc etc unchanged affection etc etc return to her old love etc etc for eight closely write page from an artistic point of view it be very neat work but an ordinary philistine who know the state of phil 's real feeling not the one he rise to as he go on writing would have call it the thoroughly mean and selfish work of thoroughly mean and selfish weak man
but this verdict would have be incorrect phil pay for the postage and feel every word he have write for at least two day and half it be the last flicker before the light go out that letter make agnes laiter very unhappy
and she cry and put it away in her desk and become mrs
somebody else for the good of her family which be the first duty of every christian maid phil go his way and think no more of his letter except as an artist think of neatly touch in sketch his way be not bad but they be not altogether good until they bring he across dunmaya the daughter of rajput ex subadar major of our native army the girl have strain of hill blood in she and like the hill woman be not purdah nashin where phil meet she or how he hear of she do not matter she be good girl and handsome and in her way very clever and shrewd though of course little hard it be to be remember that phil be live very comfortably deny himself no small luxury never put by an anna very satisfied with himself and his good intention be drop all his english correspondent one by one and begin more and more to look upon this land as his home some man fall this way and they be of no use afterwards the climate where he be station be good and it really do not seem to he that there be anything to go home for he do what many planter have do before he that be to say he make up his mind to marry hill girl and settle down he be seven and twenty then with long life before he but no spirit to go through with it
so he marry dunmaya by the form of the english church and some fellow planter say he be fool and some say he be wise man dunmaya be thoroughly honest girl and in spite of her reverence for an englishman have reasonable estimate of her husband 's weakness she manage he tenderly and become in less than year very passable imitation of an english lady in dress and carriage it be curious to think that hill man after lifetime 's education be hill man still but hill woman can in six month master most of the way of her english sister there be coolie woman once but that be another story dunmaya dress by preference in black and yellow and look well meantime the letter lie in agnes 's desk and now and again she would think of poor resolute hard work phil among the cobra and tiger of darjiling toil in the vain hope that she might come back to he her husband be worth ten phil except that he have rheumatism of the heart three year after he be marry and after he have try nice and algeria for his complaint he go to bombay where he die and set agnes free be devout woman she look on his death and the place of it as direct interposition of providence and when she have recover from the shock she take out and reread phil 's letter with the etc etc and the big dash and the little dash and kiss it several time no one know she in bombay she have her husband 's income which be large one and phil be close at hand it be wrong and improper of course
but she decide as heroine do in novel to find her old lover to offer he her hand and her gold and with he spend the rest of her life in some spot far from unsympathetic soul she sit for two month alone in watson 's hotel elaborate this decision and the picture be pretty one then she set out in search of phil garron assistant on tea plantation with more than usually unpronounceable name she find he she spend month over it for his plantation be not in the darjiling district at all but near kangra phil be very little altered and dunmaya be very nice to she now the particular sin and shame of the whole business be that phil who really be not worth think of twice be and be love by dunmaya and more than love by agnes the whole of whose life he seem to have spoil bad of all dunmaya be make decent man of he and he will be ultimately save from perdition through her training which be manifestly unfair false dawn to night god know what thing shall tide the earth be rack and faint-- expectant sleepless open eyed and we who from the earth be make thrill with our mother 's pain in durance no man will ever know the exact truth of this story though woman may sometimes whisper it to one another after dance when they be put up their hair for the night and compare list of victim man of course can not assist at these function
so the tale must be tell from the outside in the dark all wrong never praise sister to sister in the hope of your compliment reach the proper ear and so prepare the way for you later on sister be woman first and sister afterwards and you will find that you do yourself harm saumarez know this when he make up his mind to propose to the eld miss copleigh saumarez be strange man with few merit so far as man could see though he be popular with woman and carry enough conceit to stock viceroy 's council and leave little over for the commander in chief 's staff he be civilian very many woman take an interest in saumarez perhaps because his manner to they be offensive if you hit pony over the nose at the outset of your acquaintance he may not love you
but he will take deep interest in your movement ever afterwards the elder miss copleigh be nice plump win and pretty the young be not so pretty and from man disregard the hint set forth above her style be repellant and unattractive both girl have practically the same figure and there be strong likeness between they in look and voice though no one could doubt for an instant which be the nice of the two saumarez make up his mind as soon as they come into the station from behar to marry the eld one at least we all make sure that he would which come to the same thing she be two and twenty and he be thirty three with pay and allowance of nearly fourteen hundred rupee month so the match as we arrange it be in every way good one saumarez be his name and summary be his nature as man once say having draft his resolution he form select committee of one to sit upon it and resolve to take his time in our unpleasant slang the copleigh girl hunt in couple that be to say you could do nothing with one without the other they be very love sister but their mutual affection be sometimes inconvenient saumarez hold the balance hair true between they and none but himself could have say to which side his heart incline though every one guess he ride with they good deal and dance with they
but he never succeed in detach they from each other for any length of time woman say that the two girl keep together through deep mistrust each fear that the other would steal march on she but that have nothing to do with man saumarez be silent for good or bad and as business likely attentive as he could be have due regard to his work and his polo
beyond doubt both girl be fond of he as the hot weather draw near and saumarez make no sign woman say that you could see their trouble in the eye of the girl that they be look strained anxious and irritable man be quite blind in these matter unless they have more of the woman than the man in their composition in which case it do not matter what they say or think maintain it be the hot april day that take the color out of the copleigh girl cheek they should have be send to the hill early no one man or woman feel an angel when the hot weather be approach the young sister grow more cynical not to say acid in her way and the winningness of the elder wear thin there be more effort in it now the station wherein all these thing happen be though not little one off the line of rail and suffer through want of attention there be no garden or band or amusement worth speak of and it be nearly day 's journey to come into lahore for dance people be grateful for small thing to interest they about the beginning of may and just before the final exodus of hill goer when the weather be very hot and there be not more than twenty people in the station saumarez give moonlight rid picnic at an old tomb six mile away near the bed of the river it be noah 's ark picnic and there be to be the usual arrangement of quarter mile interval between each couple on account of the dust six couple come altogether include chaperon moonlight picnic be useful just at the very end of the season before all the girl go away to the hill they lead to understanding and should be encourage by chaperone especially those whose girl look sweetish in rid habit know case once but that be another story that picnic be call the great pop picnic because every one know saumarez would propose then to the eld miss copleigh and beside his affair there be another which might possibly come to happiness the social atmosphere be heavily charge and want clear we meet at the parade ground at ten the night be fearfully hot the horse sweat even at walk pace but anything be well than sit still in our own dark house when we move off under the full moon we be four couple one triplet and mr saumarez ride with the copleigh girl and loiter at the tail of the procession wonder with whom saumarez would ride home every one be happy and contented but we all feel that thing be go to happen we ride slowly and it be nearly midnight before we reach the old tomb face the ruin tank in the decayed garden where we be go to eat and drink be late in come up and before go into the garden see that the horizon to the north carry faint dun color feather but no one would have thank for spoil so well manage an entertainment as this picnic and dust storm more or less do no great harm we gather by the tank some one have bre out banjo which be most sentimental instrument and three or four of we sing you must not laugh at this our amusement in out of the way station be very few indeed then we talk in group or together lie under the tree with the sun bake rise drop their petal on our foot until supper be ready it be beautiful supper as cold and as iced as you could wish and we stay long over it have feel that the air be grow hot and hot but nobody seem to notice it until the moon go out and burn hot wind begin lash the orange tree with sound like the noise of the sea before we know where we be the dust storm be on we and everything be roar whirl darkness the supper table be blow bodily into the tank we be afraid of stay anywhere near the old tomb for fear it might be blow down so we feel our way to the orange tree where the horse be picket and wait for the storm to blow over then the little light that be leave vanish and you could not see your hand before your face the air be heavy with dust and sand from the bed of the river that fill boot and pocket and drift down neck and coat eyebrow and moustache it be one of the bad dust storm of the year we be all huddle together close to the tremble horse with the thunder clatter overhead and the lightning spurt like water from sluice all way at once there be no danger of course unless the horse break loose be stand with my head downward and my hand over my mouth hear the tree thrash each other could not see who be next till the flash come then find that be pack near saumarez and the eld miss copleigh with my own horse just in front of recognize the eld miss copleigh because she have pagri round her helmet and the young have not all the electricity in the air have go into my body and be quivering and tingle from head to foot exactly as corn shoot and tingle before rain it be grand storm the wind seem to be pick up the earth and pitch it to leeward in great heap and the heat beat up from the ground like the heat of the day of judgment the storm lull slightly after the first half hour and hear despairing little voice close to my ear say to itself quietly and softly as if some lose soul be fly about with the wind my god then the young miss copleigh stumble into my arm say where be my horse get my horse want to go home want to go home take home think that the lightning and the black darkness have frighten she so say there be no danger
but she must wait till the storm blow over she answer it be not that it be not that want to go home take away from here say that she could not go till the light come but feel her brush past and go away it be too dark to see where then the whole sky be split open with one tremendous flash as if the end of the world be come and all the woman shriek almost directly after this feel man 's hand on my shoulder and hear saumarez bellow in my ear through the rattling of the tree and howling of the wind do not catch his word at once but at last hear he say have propose to the wrong one what shall do saumarez have no occasion to make this confidence to be never friend of his nor be now but fancy neither of we be ourselves just then he be shake as he stand with excitement and be feel queer all over with the electricity could not think of anything to say except:--“more fool you for propose in dust storm but do not see how that would improve the mistake then he shout where be edith edith copleigh edith be the young sister answer out of my astonishment:--“what
do you want with she would you believe it for the next two minute he and be shout at each other like maniac he vow that it be the young sister he have mean to propose to all along and tell he till my throat be hoarse that he must have make mistake can not account for this except again by the fact that we be neither of we ourselves everything seem to like bad dream from the stamping of the horse in the darkness to saumarez tell the story of his love edith copleigh since the first he be still claw my shoulder and beg to tell he where edith copleigh be when another lull come and bring light with it and we see the dust cloud form on the plain in front of we
so we know the bad be over the moon be low down and there be just the glimmer of the false dawn that come about an hour before the real one but the light be very faint and the dun cloud roar like bull wonder where edith copleigh have go and as be wonder see three thing together first maud copleigh 's face come smile out of the darkness and move towards saumarez who be stand by hear the girl whisper george and slide her arm through the arm that be not claw my shoulder and see that look on her face which only come once or twice in lifetime when woman be perfectly happy and the air be full of trumpet and gorgeous color fire and the earth turn into cloud because she love and be love at the same time see saumarez 's face as he hear maud copleigh 's voice and fifty yard away from the clump of orange tree see brown holland habit get upon horse it must have be my state of over excitement that make so quick to meddle with what do not concern saumarez be move off to the habit but push he back and said:--“stop here and explain will fetch she back and run out to get at my own horse have perfectly unnecessary notion that everything must be do decently and in order and that saumarez 's first care be to wipe the happy look out of maud copleigh 's face all the time be link up the curb chain wonder how he would do it canter after edith copleigh think to bring she back slowly on some pretence or another but she gallop away as soon as she see and be force to ride after she in earnest she call back over her shoulder--“go away be go home
oh go away two or three time but my business be to catch she first and argue later the ride just fit in with the rest of the evil dream the ground be very bad and now and again we rush through the whirl choke dust devil in the skirt of the fly storm there be burn hot wind blow that bring up stench of stale brick kiln with it and through the half light and through the dust devil across that desolate plain flicker the brown holland habit on the gray horse she head for the station at first then she wheel round and set off for the river through bed of burn down jungle grass bad even to ride pig over in cold blood should never have dream of go over such country at night
but it seem quite right and natural with the lightning crackling overhead and reek like the smell of the pit in my nostril ride and shout
and she bend forward and lash her horse and the aftermath of the dust storm come up and catch we both and drive we downwind like piece of paper do not know how far we ride but the drumming of the horse hoofs and the roar of the wind and the race of the faint blood red moon through the yellow mist seem to have go on for year and year and be literally drench with sweat from my helmet to my gaiter when the gray stumble recover himself and pull up dead lame my brute be use up altogether edith copleigh be in sad state plaster with dust her helmet off and cry bitterly why can not you let alone she say only want to get away and go home
oh please let go you have get to come back with miss copleigh saumarez have something to say to you it be foolish way of put
but hardly know miss copleigh and though be play providence at the cost of my horse could not tell she in as many word what saumarez have tell think he could do that well himself all her pretence about be tired and want to go home break down and she rock herself to and fro in the saddle as she sob and the hot wind blow her black hair to leeward be not go to repeat what she say because she be utterly unstrung this if you please be the cynical miss copleigh here be almost an utter stranger to she try to tell she that saumarez love she
and she be to come back to hear he say so believe make myself understand for she gather the gray together and make he hobble somehow and we set off for the tomb while the storm go thunder down to umballa and few big drop of warm rain fall find out that she have be stand close to saumarez when he propose to her sister and have want to go home and cry in peace as an english girl should she dabble her eye with her pocket handkerchief as we go along and babble to out of sheer lightness of heart and hysteria that be perfectly unnatural and yet it seem all right at the time and in the place all the world be only the two copleigh girl saumarez and ring in with the lightning and the dark and the guidance of this misguided world seem to lie in my hand when we return to the tomb in the deep dead stillness that follow the storm the dawn be just break and nobody have go away they be wait for our return saumarez most of all his face be white and draw as miss copleigh and limp up he come forward to meet we and when he help she down from her saddle he kiss she before all the picnic it be like scene in theatre and the likeness be heighten by all the dust white ghostly look man and woman under the orange tree clap their hand as if they be watch play at saumarez 's choice never know anything
so un english in my life lastly saumarez say we must all go home or the station would come out to look for we and would be good enough to ride home with maud copleigh nothing would give great pleasure say so we form up six couple in all and go back two by two saumarez walk at the side of edith copleigh who be rid his horse the air be clear and little by little as the sun rise feel we be all drop back again into ordinary man and woman and that the great pop picnic be thing altogether apart and out of the world never to happen again it have go with the dust storm and the tingle in the hot air feel tired and limp and good deal ashamed of myself as go in for bath and some sleep there be woman 's version of this story but it will never be write unless maud copleigh care to try the rescue of pluffle thus for season they fight it fair--
she and his cousin may-- tactful talented debonnaire decorous foe be they but never can battle of man compare with merciless feminine fray two and one mrs hauksbee be sometimes nice to her own sex here be story to prove this and you can believe just as much as ever you please pluffle be subaltern in the unmentionable he be callow even for subaltern he be callow all over like canary that have not finish fledge itself the bad of it be he have three time as much money as be good for he pluffle papa be rich man and pluffle be the only son pluffle mamma adore he she be only little less callow than pluffle and she believe everything he say pluffle weakness be not believe what people say he prefer what he call trust to his own judgment he have as much judgment as he have seat or hand and this preference tumble he into trouble once or twice but the big trouble pluffle ever manufacture come about at simla some year ago when he be four and twenty he begin by trust to his own judgment as usual and the result be that after time he be bind hand and foot to mrs reiver 's rickshaw wheel there be nothing good about mrs reiver unless it be her dress she be bad from her hair which start life on brittany 's girl 's head to her boot heel which be two and three eighth inch high she be not honestly mischievous like mrs hauksbee she be wicked in business like way there be never any scandal she have not generous impulse enough for that she be the exception which prove the rule that anglo indian lady be in every way as nice as their sister at home she spend her life in prove that rule mrs hauksbee
and she hat each other fervently they hear far too much to clash
but the thing they say of each other be startling not to say original mrs hauksbee be honest honest as her own front tooth and but for her love of mischief would have be woman 's woman there be no honesty about mrs reiver nothing but selfishness and at the beginning of the season poor little pluffle fall prey to she she lie herself out to that end and who be pluffle to resist he go on trust to his judgment and he get judge have see hayes argue with tough horse have see tonga driver coerce stubborn pony have see riotous setter break to gun by hard keeper but the breaking in of pluffle of the unmentionable be beyond all these he learn to fetch and carry like dog and to wait like one too for word from mrs reiver he learn to keep appointment which mrs reiver have no intention of keep he learn to take thankfully dance which mrs reiver have no intention of give he he learn to shiver for an hour and quarter on the windward side of elysium while mrs reiver be make up her mind to come for ride he learn to hunt for rickshaw in light dress suit under pelt rain and to walk by the side of that rickshaw when he have find it he learn what it be to be speak to like coolie and order about like cook he learn all this and many other thing besides and he pay for his schooling perhaps in some hazy way he fancy that it be fine and impressive that it give he status among man and be altogether the thing to do it be nobody 's business to warn pluffle that he be unwise the pace that season be too good to inquire and meddle with another man 's folly be always thankless work pluffle colonel should have order he back to his regiment when he hear how thing be go but pluffle have get himself engage to girl in england the last time he go home
and if there be one thing more than another which the colonel detest it be marry subaltern he chuckle when he hear of the education of pluffle and say it be good training for the boy
but it be not good training in the least it lead he into spend money beyond his mean which be good above that the education spoil an average boy and make it tenth rate man of an objectionable kind he wander into bad set and his little bill at hamilton 's be thing to wonder at then mrs hauksbee rise to the occasion she play her game alone know what people would say of she and she play it for the sake of girl she have never see pluffle fiancee be to come out under the chaperonage of an aunt in october to be married to pluffle at the beginning of august mrs hauksbee discover that it be time to interfere man who rid much know exactly what horse be go to do next before he do it in the same way woman of mrs hauksbee 's experience know accurately how boy will behave under certain circumstance notably when he be infatuate with one of mrs reiver 's stamp she say that soon or later little pluffle would break off that engagement for nothing at all simply to gratify mrs reiver who in return would keep he at her foot and in her service just so long as she find it worth she while she say she know the sign of these thing if she do not no one else could then she go forth to capture pluffle under the gun of the enemy just as mrs cusack bremmil carry away bremmil under mrs hauksbee 's eye this particular engagement last seven week we call it the seven week war and be fight out inch by inch on both side detailed account would fill book and would be incomplete then any one who know about these thing can fit in the detail for himself it be superb fight there will never be another like it as long as jakko stand and pluffle be the prize of victory people say shameful thing about mrs hauksbee they do not know what she be play for mrs reiver fight partly because pluffle be useful to she but mainly because she hat mrs hauksbee and the matter be trial of strength between they no one know what pluffle think he have not many idea at the good of time and the few he possess make he conceited mrs hauksbee said:--“the boy must be catch and the only way of catch he be by treat he well
so she treat he as man of the world and of experience so long as the issue be doubtful little by little pluffle fall away from his old allegiance and come over to the enemy by whom he be make much of he be never send on out post duty after rickshaw any more nor be he give dance which never come off nor be the drain on his purse continue mrs hauksbee hold he on the snaffle and after his treatment at mrs reiver 's hand he appreciate the change mrs reiver have break he of talk about himself and make he talk about her own merit mrs hauksbee act otherwise and win his confidence till he mention his engagement to the girl at home speak of it in high and mighty way as piece of boyish folly this be when he be take tea with her one afternoon and discourse in what he consider gay and fascinating
style mrs hauksbee have see an early generation of his stamp bud and blossom and decay into fat captain and tubby major at moderate estimate there be about three and twenty side to that lady 's character some man say more she begin to talk to pluffle after the manner of mother and as if there have be three hundred year instead of fifteen between they she speak with sort of throaty quaver in her voice which have soothing effect though what she say be anything but soothe she point out the exceed folly not to say meanness of pluffle conduct and the smallness of his view then he stammer something about trust to his own judgment as man of the world and this pave the way for what she want to say next it would have wither up pluffle have it come from any other woman but in the soft coo style in which mrs hauksbee put it it only make he feel limp and repentant as if he have be in some superior kind of church little by little very softly and pleasantly she begin take the conceit out of pluffle as you take the rib out of an umbrella before re cover it she tell he what she think of he and his judgment and his knowledge of the world and how his performance have make he ridiculous to other people and how it be his intention to make love to herself if she give he the chance then she say that marriage would be the making of he and draw pretty little picture all rise and opal of the mrs pluffle of the future go through life rely on the judgment and knowledge of the world of husband who have nothing to reproach himself with how she reconcile these two statement she alone know
but they do not strike pluffle as conflict her be perfect little homily much well than any clergyman could have give and it end with touch allusion to pluffles mamma and papa and the wisdom of take his bride home
then she send pluffle out for walk to think over what she have say pluffle leave blow his nose very hard and hold himself very straight mrs hauksbee laugh what pluffle have intend to do in the matter of the engagement only mrs reiver
and she keep her own counsel to her death she would have like it spoil as compliment fancy pluffle enjoy many talk with mrs hauksbee during the next few day they be all to the same end and they help pluffle in the path of virtue mrs hauksbee want to keep he under her wing to the last therefore she discountenance his go down to bombay to get marry goodness only know what might happen by the way she say pluffle be curse with the curse of reuben and india be no fit place for he in the end the fiancee arrive with her aunt and pluffle having reduce his affair to some sort of order here again mrs hauksbee help he be marry
mrs hauksbee give sigh of relief when both the will have be say and go her way pluffie take her advice about go home he leave the service and be now raise speckled cattle inside green paint fence somewhere at home believe he do this very judiciously he would have come to extreme grief out here for these reason if any one say anything more than usually nasty about mrs hauksbee tell he the story of the rescue of pluffle cupid 's arrow pit where the buffalo cool his hide by the hot sun empty and blister and dry log in the reh grass hide and alone bund where the earth rat 's mound be strown cave in the bank where the sly stream steal aloe that stab at the belly and heel jump if you dare on steed untried-- safe
it be to go wide go wide hark from in front where the good man ride:-- pull to the off boy go wide the peora hunt once upon time there live at simla very pretty girl the daughter of poor but honest district and session judge she be good girl but could not help know her power and use it her mamma be very anxious about her daughter 's future as all good mamma should be when man be commissioner and bachelor and have the right of wear open work jam tart jewel in gold and enamel on his clothe and of go through door before every one except member of council lieutenant governor or viceroy he be worth marry at least that be what lady say there be commissioner in simla in those day who be and wear and do all have say he be plain man an ugly man the ugly man in asia with two exception his be face to dream about and try to carve on pipe head afterwards his name be saggott barr saggott anthony barr saggott and six letter to follow departmentally he be one of the good man the government of india own
socially he be like blandish gorilla when he turn his attention to miss beighton believe that mrs beighton weep with delight at the reward providence have send she in her old age mr beighton hold his tongue he be an easy go man now commissioner be very rich his pay be beyond the dream of avarice be so enormous that he can afford to save and scrape in way that would almost discredit member of council most commissioner be mean but barr saggott be an exception he entertain royally he horse himself
well he give dance he be power in the land and he behave as such consider that everything be write of take place in an almost pre historic era in the history of british india some folk may remember the year before lawn tennis be bear when we all play croquet there be season before that if you will believe when even croquet have not be invent and archery which be revive in england in -was as great pest as lawn tennis be now people talk learnedly about hold and loose stele reflexe bow pound bow back or self yew bow as we talk about rally volley smash return and ounce racket miss beighton shoot divinely over lady distance--60
yard that be and be acknowledge the good lady archer in simla man call she diana of tara devi barr saggott pay she great attention and as have say the heart of her mother be uplift in consequence kitty beighton take matter more calmly it be pleasant to be single out by commissioner with letter after his name and to fill the heart of other girl with bad feeling but there be no deny the fact that barr saggott be phenomenally ugly and all his attempt to adorn himself only make he more grotesque he be not christen the langur”--which mean gray ape for nothing it be pleasant kitty think to have he at her foot
but it be well to escape from he and ride with the graceless cubbon the man in dragoon regiment at umballa the boy with handsome face and no prospect kitty like cubbon more than little he never pretend for moment the he be anything less than head over heel in love with she for he be an honest boy
so kitty flee now and again from the stately wooing of barr saggott to the company of young cubbon and be scold by her mamma in consequence but mother she say mr saggot be such such be so fearfully ugly you know my dear say mrs beighton piously we can not be other than an all rule providence have make we besides you will take precedence of your own mother you know think of that and be reasonable then kitty put up her little chin and say irreverent thing about precedence and commissioner and matrimony mr beighton rub the top of his head for he be an easy go man late in the season when he judge that the time be ripe barr saggott develop plan which do great credit to his administrative power he arrange an archery tournament for lady with most sumptuous diamond stud bracelet as prize he draw up his term skilfully and every one see that the bracelet be gift to miss beighton the acceptance carry with it the hand and the heart of commissioner barr saggott the term be st leonard 's round thirty six shot at sixty yard under the rule of the simla toxophilite society all simla be invite there be beautifully arrange tea table under the deodar at annandale where the grand stand be now and alone in its glory wink in the sun sit the diamond bracelet in blue velvet case miss beighton be anxious almost too anxious to compete on the appoint afternoon all simla ride down to annandale to witness the judgment of paris turn upside down kitty ride with young cubbon and it be easy to see that the boy be trouble in his mind he must be hold innocent of everything that follow kitty be pale and nervous and look long at the bracelet barr saggott be gorgeously dress even more nervous than kitty and more hideous than ever mrs beighton smile condescendingly as befit the mother of potential commissioneress and the shooting begin all the world stand in semicircle as the lady come out one after the other nothing be so tedious as an archery competition they shoot and they shoot and they keep on shoot till the sun leave the valley and little breeze get up in the deodar and people wait for miss beighton to shoot and win cubbon be at one horn of the semicircle round the shooter and barr saggott at the other miss beighton be last on the list the scoring have be weak and the bracelet plus commissioner barr saggott be hers to certainty the commissioner string her bow with his own sacred hand she step forward look at the bracelet and her first arrow go true to hair full into the heart of the gold”--counte nine point young cubbon on the leave turn white and his devil prompt barr saggott to smile now horse use to shy when barr saggott smile kitty see that smile she look to her leave front give an almost imperceptible nod to cubbon and go on shooting wish could describe the scene that follow it be out of the ordinary and most improper miss kitty fit her arrow with immense deliberation so that every one might see what she be do she be perfect shot and her pound bow suit she to nicety she pin the wooden leg of the target with great care four successive time she pin the wooden top of the target once and all the lady look at each other then she begin some fancy shooting at the white which if you hit it count exactly one point she put five arrow into the white it be wonderful archery but see that her business be to make gold and win the bracelet barr saggott turn delicate green like young water grass next she shoot over the target twice then wide to the left twice always with the same deliberation while chilly hush fall over the company and
mrs beighton take out her handkerchief then kitty shoot at the ground in front of the target and split several arrow then she make red or seven point just to show what she could do if she like and finish up her amazing performance with some more fancy shooting at the target support here be her score as it be pick off:-- gold red blue black total hit total score miss beighton barr saggott look as if the last few arrowhead have be drive into his leg instead of the target 's and the deep stillness be break by little snubby mottled half grow girl say in shrill voice of triumph
then have win
mrs beighton
do she good to bear up but she weep in the presence of the people no training could help she through such disappointment kitty unstre her bow with vicious jerk and go back to her place while barr saggott be try to pretend that he enjoy snap the bracelet on the snubby girl 's raw red wrist it be an awkward scene most awkward every one try to depart in body and leave kitty to the mercy of her mamma but cubbon take she away instead and the rest be not worth print his chance in life then pile of head be laid-- thirty thousand heap on high-- all to please the kafir maid where the oxus ripple by grimly spake atulla khan:-- love hath make this thing man oatta 's story if you go straight away from levee and government house list past trade ball far beyond everything and everybody you ever know in your respectable life you cross in time the border line where the last drop of white blood end and the full tide of black set in it would be easy to talk to new make duchess on the spur of the moment than to the borderline folk without violate some of their convention or hurt their feeling the black and the white mix very quaintly in their way sometimes the white show in spurt of fierce childish pride which be pride of race run crooked and sometimes the black in still fierce abasement and humility half heathenish custom and strange unaccountable impulse to crime one of these day this people understand they be far low than the class whence derozio the man who imitate byron sprung will turn out writer or poet and then we shall know how they live and what they feel in the meantime any story about they can not be absolutely correct in fact or inference miss vezzis come from across the borderline to look after some child who belong to lady until regularly ordain nurse could come out the lady say miss vezzis be bad dirty nurse and inattentive it never strike she that miss vezzi have her own life to lead and her own affair to worry over and that these affair be the most important thing in the world to miss vezzi very few mistress admit this sort of reasoning miss vezzis be as black as boot and to our standard of taste hideously ugly she wear cotton print gown and bulge shoe and when she lose her temper with the child she abuse they in the language of the borderline which be part english part portuguese and part native she be not attractive but she have her pride and she prefer be call miss vezzi every sunday she dress herself wonderfully and go to see her mamma who live for the most part on an old cane chair in greasy tussur silk dress gown and big rabbit warren of house full of vezzise pereiras ribieras lisboas and gansalvese and float population of loafer besides fragment of the day 's bazar garlic stale incense clothe throw on the floor petticoat hang on string for screen old bottle pewter crucifix dry immortelle pariah puppy plaster image of the virgin and hat without crown miss vezzis draw twenty rupee month for act as nurse and she squabble weekly with her mamma as to the percentage to be give towards housekeeping when the quarrel be over michele d'cruze use to shamble across the low mud wall of the compound and make love to miss vezzis after the fashion of the borderline which be hedge about with much ceremony michele be poor sickly weed and very black
but he have his pride he would not be see smoke huqa for anything
and he look down on native as only man with seven eighth native blood in his vein can the vezzis family have their pride too they trace their descent from mythical plate layer who have work on the sone bridge when railway be new in india and they value their english origin michele be telegraph signaller on month the fact that he be in government employ make mrs vezzis lenient to the shortcoming of his ancestor there be compromise legend dom anna
the tailor bring it from poonani that black jew of cochin have once marry into the d'cruze family while it be an open secret that an uncle of mrs d'cruze be at that very time do menial work connect with cook for club in southern india he send mrs d'cruze seven rupee eight annas month
but she feel the disgrace to the family very keenly all the same however in the course of few sunday mrs vezzi bring herself to overlook these blemish and give her consent to the marriage of her daughter with michele on condition that michele should have at least fifty rupee month to start married life upon this wonderful prudence must have be linger touch of the mythical plate layer 's yorkshire blood for across the borderline people take pride in marry when they please not when they can have regard to his departmental prospect miss vezzis might as well have ask michele to go away and come back with the moon in his pocket
but michele be deeply in love with miss vezzis and that help he to endure he accompany miss vezzis to mass one sunday and after mass walk home through the hot stale dust with her hand in his he swear by several saint whose name would not interest you never to forget miss vezzis
and she swear by her honor and the saint the oath run rather curiously in nomine sanctissimae-- whatever the name of the she saint be and so forth end with kiss on the forehead kiss on the left cheek and kiss on the mouth never to forget michele next week michele be transfer and miss vezzis drop tear upon the window sash of the intermediate compartment as he leave the station if you look at the telegraph map of india you will see long line skirt the coast from backergunge to madras michele be order to tibasu little sub office one third down this line to send message on from berhampur to chicacola and to think of miss vezzi and his chance of get fifty rupee month out of office hour he have the noise of the bay of bengal and bengali babu for company nothing more he send foolish letter with cross tuck inside the flap of the envelope to miss vezzi when he have be at tibasu for nearly three week his chance come never forget that unless the outward and visible sign of our authority be always before native he be as incapable as child of understand what authority mean or where be the danger of disobey it tibasu be forget little place with few orissa mohamedan in it these hear nothing of the collector sahib for some time and heartily despise the hindu sub judge arrange to start little mohurrum riot of their own but the hindus turn out and break their head when find lawlessness pleasant hindus and mahomedan together raise an aimless sort of donnybrook just to see how far they could go they loot each other 's shop and pay off private grudge in the regular way it be nasty little riot but not worth put in the newspaper michele be work in his office when he hear the sound that man never forget all his life the ah yah of an angry crowd when that sound drop about three tone and change to thick drone ut the man who hear it have well go away if he be alone the native police inspector run in and tell michele that the town be in an uproar and come to wreck the telegraph office the babu put on his cap and quietly drop out of the window while the police inspector afraid but obey the old race instinct which recognize drop of white blood as far as it can be dilute said:--“what order do the sahib give the sahib decide michele though horribly frighten he feel that for the hour he the man with the cochin jew and the menial uncle in his pedigree be the only representative of english authority in the place then he think of miss vezzis and the fifty rupee and take the situation on himself there be seven native policeman in tibasu and four crazy smooth bore musket among they all the man be gray with fear but not beyond lead michele drop the key of the telegraph instrument and go out at the head of his army to meet the mob as the shout crew come round corner of the road he drop and fire the man behind he loose instinctively at the same time the whole crowd cur to the backbone yell and run leave one man dead and another die in the road michele be sweat with fear
but he keep his weakness under and go down into the town past the house where the sub judge have barricade himself the street be empty tibasu be more frightened than michele for the mob have be take at the right time michele return to the telegraph office and send message to chicacola ask for help before an answer come he receive deputation of the elder of tibasu tell he that the sub judge say his action generally be unconstitutional and try to bully he but the heart of michele d'cruze be big and white in his breast because of his love for miss vezzi the nurse girl and because he have taste for the first time responsibility and success those two make an intoxicating drink and have ruin more man than ever have whiskey michele answer that the sub judge might say what he please but until the assistant collector come the telegraph signaller be the government of india in tibasu and the elder of the town would be hold accountable for further rioting
then they bow their head and say show mercy or word to that effect and go back in great fear each accuse the other of having begin the rioting early in the dawn after night 's patrol with his seven policeman michele go down the road musket in hand to meet the assistant collector who have ride in to quell tibasu but in the presence of this young englishman michele feel himself slip back more and more into the native and the tale of the tibasu riot end with the strain on the teller in an hysterical outburst of tear breed by sorrow that he have kill man shame that he could not feel as uplifted as he have feel through the night and childish anger that his tongue could not do justice to his great deed it be the white drop in michele 's vein die out though he do not know it but the englishman understand and after he have school those man of tibasu and have confer with the sub judge till that excellent official turn green he find time to draught an official letter describe the conduct of michele which letter filter through the proper channel and end in the transfer of michele up country once more on the imperial salary of sixty six rupee month so he and miss vezzis be marry with great state and ancientry and now there be several little d'cruze sprawl about the verandah of the central telegraph office but if the whole revenue of the department he serve be to be his reward michele could never never repeat what he do at tibasu for the sake of miss vezzi the nurse girl which prove that when man do good work out of all proportion to his pay in seven case out of nine there be woman at the back of the virtue the two exception must have suffer from sunstroke watch of the night what be in the brahmin 's book that be in the brahmin 's heart neither you nor know there be so much evil in the world hindu proverb this begin in practical joke but it have go far enough now and be get serious platte
the subaltern be poor have waterbury watch and plain leather guard the colonel have waterbury watch also and for guard the lip strap of curb chain lip strap make the good watch guard they be strong and short between lip strap and an ordinary leather guard there be no great difference between one waterbury watch and another there be none at all every one in the station know the colonel 's lip strap he be not horsey man
but he like people to believe he have be on once
