and he wove fantastic story of the hunting bridle to which this particular lip strap have belong otherwise he be painfully religious platte and the colonel be dress at the club both late for their engagement and both in hurry that be kismet the two watch be on shelf below the look glass guard hang down that be carelessness platte change first snatch watch look in the glass settle his tie and run forty second later the colonel do exactly the same thing each man take the other 's watch you may have notice that many religious people be deeply suspicious they seem for purely religious purpose of course to know more about iniquity than the unregenerate perhaps they be specially bad before they become convert at any rate in the imputation of thing evil and in put the bad construction on thing innocent certain type of good people may be trust to surpass all other the colonel and his wife be of that type but the colonel 's wife be the bad she manufacture the station scandal and talk to her ayah nothing more need be say the colonel 's wife break up the laplace 's home the colonel 's wife stop the ferris haughtrey engagement the colonel 's wife induce young buxton to keep his wife down in the plain through the first year of the marriage whereby little mrs buxton die and the baby with she these thing will be remember against the colonel 's wife so long as there be regiment in the country but to come back to the colonel and platte they go their several way from the dressing room the colonel din with two chaplain while platte go to bachelor party and whist to follow mark how thing happen if platte 's sais have put the new saddle pad on the mare the butt of the territ would not have work through the wear leather and the old pad into the mare 's wither when she be come home at two o'clock in the morning she would not have rear bolt fall into ditch upset the cart and send platte fly over an aloe hedge on to mrs larkyn 's well keep lawn and this tale would never have be write
but the mare do all these thing and while platte be roll over and over on the turf like shot rabbit the watch and guard fly from his waistcoat as an infantry major 's sword hop out of the scabbard when they be fire feu de joie and roll and roll in the moonlight till it stop under window platte stuff his handkerchief under the pad put the cart straight and go home mark again how kismet work this would not happen once in hundred year towards the end of his dinner with the two chaplain the colonel let out his waistcoat and lean over the table to look at some mission report the bar of the watch guard work through the buttonhole and the watch platte 's watch slide quietly on to the carpet where the bearer find it next morning and keep it then the colonel go home to the wife of his bosom but the driver of the carriage be drunk and lose his way
so the colonel return at an unseemly hour and his excuse be not accept if the colonel 's wife have be an ordinary vessel of wrath appoint for destruction she would have know that when man stay away on purpose his excuse be always sound and original the very baldness of the colonel 's explanation prove its truth see once more the working of kismet the colonel 's watch which come with platte hurriedly on to mrs larkyn 's lawn choose to stop just under mrs larkyn 's window where she see it early in the morning recognize it and pick it up she have hear the crash of platte 's cart at two o'clock that morning and his voice call the mare name she know platte and like he that day she show he the watch and hear his story he put his head on one side wink and said:--“how disgusting shocking old man with his religious training too should send the watch to the colonel 's wife and ask for explanation mrs larkyn think for minute of the laplace whom she have know when laplace and his wife believe in each other and answered:--“i will send it think it will do she good but remember we must never tell she the truth platte guess that his own watch be in the colonel 's possession and think that the return of the lip strap waterbury with soothe note from mrs larkyn would merely create small trouble for few minute mrs larkyn know well she know that any poison drop would find good hold ground in the heart of the colonel 's wife the packet and note contain few remark on the colonel 's calling hour be send over to the colonel 's wife who weep in her own room and take counsel with herself if there be one woman under heaven whom the colonel 's wife hat with holy fervor it be mrs larkyn mrs larkyn be frivolous lady and call the colonel 's wife old cat the colonel 's wife say that somebody in revelation be remarkably like mrs larkyn she mention other scripture people as well from the old testament
but the colonel 's wife be the only person who care or dare to say anything against mrs larkyn every one else accept she as an amusing honest little body wherefore to believe that her husband have be shed watch under that thing 's window at ungodly hour couple with the fact of his late arrival on the previous night be at this point she rise up and seek her husband he deny everything except the ownership of the watch she beseech he for his soul 's sake to speak the truth he deny afresh with two bad word then stony silence hold the colonel 's wife while man could draw his breath five time the speech that follow be no affair of mine or

bad wicked gray haired profligate this may sound too sudden revulsion for long we wife but it be venerable fact that if man or woman make practice of and take delight in believe and spread evil of people indifferent to he or she he or she will end in believe evil of folk very near and dear you may think also that the mere incident of the watch be too small and trivial to raise this have go far enough move we tell the colonel 's wife how it happen mrs larkyn shut her lip and shake her head and vow that the colonel 's wife must bear her punishment as good she could now mrs larkyn be frivolous woman in whom none would have suspect deep hate

but it never quite kill he understand do not blame schriederling he be good husband accord to his light and his temper only fail he when he be be nurse which be some seventeen day in each month he be almost generous to his wife about money matter and that for he be concession still mrs schreiderling be not happy they marry she when she be this side of twenty and have give all her poor little heart to another man have forget his name
but we will call he the other man he have no money and no prospect he be not even good look and think he be in the commissariat or transport but in spite of all these thing she love he very madly and there be some sort of an engagement between the two when schreiderling appear and tell mrs gaurey that he wish to marry her daughter then the other engagement be break off wash away by mrs gaurey 's tear for that lady govern her house by weep over disobedience to her authority and the lack of reverence she receive in her old age the daughter do not take after her mother she never cry not even at the wedding the other man bear his loss quietly and be transfer to as bad station as he could find perhaps the climate console he he suffer from intermittent fever and that may have distract he from his other trouble he be weak about the heart also both way one of the valve be affect and the fever make it bad this show itself later on then many month pass and mrs schreiderling take to be ill she do not pine away like people in story book
but she seem to pick up every form of illness that go about station from simple fever upwards she be never more than ordinarily pretty at the good of time and the illness make she ugly schreiderling say so he pride himself on speak his mind when she cease be pretty he leave she to her own device and go back to the lair of his bachelordom she use to trot up and down simla mall in forlorn sort of way with gray terai hat well on the back of her head and shocking bad saddle under she schreiderling 's generosity stop at the horse he say that any saddle would do for woman as nervous as mrs schreiderling she never be ask to dance because she do not dance well and she be so dull and uninteresting that her box very seldom have any card in it schreiderling say that if he have know that she be go to be such scare crow after her marriage he would never have marry she he always pride himself on speak his mind do schreiderle he leave she at simla one august and go down to his regiment then she revive little
but she never recover her look find out at the club that the other man be come up sick very sick on an off chance of recovery the fever and the heart valve have nearly kill he she know that too and she know what have no interest in know when he be come up suppose he write to tell she they have not see each other since month before the wedding and here come the unpleasant part of the story late call keep down at the dovedell hotel till dusk
one evening mrs schreidlerling have be flit up and down the mall all the afternoon in the rain come up along the cart road tonga pass and my pony tired with stand so long set off at canter just by the road down to the tonga office mrs schreiderling drip from head to foot be wait for the tonga turn up hill as the tonga be no affair of mine and just then she begin to shriek go back at once and see under the tonga office lamp mrs schreiderling kneel in the wet road by the back seat of the newly arrive tonga scream hideously then she fall face down in the dirt as come up sit in the back seat very square and firm with one hand on the awning stanchion and the wet pour off his hat and moustache be the other man dead the sixty mile up hill jolt have be too much for his valve suppose the tonga driver said:--“the sahib die two stage out of solon therefore tie he with rope lest he should fall out by the way and so come to simla will the sahib give bukshish it point to the other man should have give one rupee the other man sit with grin on his face as if he enjoy the joke of his arrival and
mrs schreiderling in the mud begin to groan there be no one except we four in the office and it be rain heavily the first thing be to take mrs schreiderling home and the second be to prevent her name from be mix up with the affair the tonga driver receive five rupee to find bazar rickshaw for mrs schreiderling he be to tell the tonga babu afterwards of the other man and the babu be to make such arrangement as seem well mrs schreiderling be carry into the shed out of the rain and for three quarter of an hour we two wait for the rickshaw the other man be leave exactly as he have arrive mrs schreiderling would do everything but cry which might have help she she try to scream as soon as her sen come back
and then she begin pray for the other man 's soul have she not be as honest as the day she would have pray for her own soul too wait to hear she do this
but she do not then try to get some of the mud off her habit lastly the rickshaw come and get she away partly by force it be terrible business from begin to end
but most of all when the rickshaw have to squeeze between the wall and the tonga
and she see by the lamp light that thin yellow hand grasp the awning stanchion she be take home just as every one be go to dance at viceregal lodge--“peterhoff it be then and the doctor find that she have fall from her horse that have pick she up at the back of jakko and really deserve great credit for the prompt manner in which have secure medical aid she do not die man of schreiderling 's stamp marry woman who do not die easily they live and grow ugly she never tell of her one meeting since her marriage with the other man and when the chill and cough follow the exposure of that evening allow she abroad
she never by word or sign allude to having meet by the tonga office perhaps she never know she use to trot up and down the mall on that shocking bad saddle look as if she expect to meet some one round the corner every minute two year afterward she go home and die at bournemouth think schreiderling when he grow maudlin at mess use to talk about my poor dear wife he always set great store on speak his mind
do schreiderling consequence rosicrucian subtletie in the orient have rise ye may find their teacher still under jacatala 's hill seek ye bombast paracelsus read what flood the seeker tell we of the dominant that run through the cycle of the suns-- read my story last and see luna at her apogee there be yearly appointment and two yearly appointment and five yearly appointment at simla and there be or use to be permanent appointment whereon you stay up for the term of your natural life and secured red cheek and nice income of course you could descend in the cold weather for simla be rather dull then tarrion come from goodness know where all away and away in some forsake part of central india where they call pachmari sanitarium and drive behind trot bullock believe he belong to regiment but what he really want to do be to escape from his regiment and live in simla forever and ever he have no preference for anything in particular beyond good horse and nice partner he think he could do everything well which be beautiful belief when you hold it with all your heart he be clever in many way and good to look at and always make people round he comfortable even in central india so he go up to simla and because he be clever and amusing he gravitate naturally to mrs hauksbee who could forgive everything but stupidity once he do her great service by change the date on an invitation card for big dance which mrs hauksbee wish to attend but could not because she have quarrel with the a.-d.-c
who take care be mean man to invite she to small dance on the 6th instead of the big ball of the 26th it be very clever piece of forgery and when mrs hauksbee show the a.-d.-c
her invitation card and chaff he mildly for not well manage his vendetta he really think he have make mistake and which be wise realize that it be no use to fight with mrs hauksbee she be grateful to tarrion and ask what she could do for he he say simply be freelance up here on leave and on the lookout for what can loot have not square inch of interest in all simla my name be not know to any man with an appointment in his gift and want an appointment good sound pukka one believe you can do anything you turn yourself to do will you help mrs hauksbee think for minute and pass the last of her rid whip through her lip as be her custom when think then her eye sparkle and she said:--“i will
and she shake hand on it tarrion have perfect confidence in this great woman take no further thought of the business at all except to wonder what sort of an appointment he would win mrs hauksbee begin calculate the price of all the head of department and member of council she know and the more she think the more she laugh because her heart be in the game and it amuse she then she take civil list and run over few of the appointment there be some beautiful appointment in the civil list eventually she decide that though tarrion be too good for the political department she have well begin by try to get he in there what be her own plan to this end do not matter in the least for luck or fate play into her hand and she have nothing to do but to watch the course of event and take the credit of they all viceroy when they first come out pass through the diplomatic secrecy craze it wear off in time but they all catch it in the beginning because they be new to the country the particular viceroy who be suffer from the complaint just then this be long time ago before lord dufferin ever come from canada or lord ripon from the bosom of the english church have it very badly and the result be that man who be new to keep official secret go about look unhappy and the viceroy plume himself on the way in which he have instill notion of reticence into his staff now the supreme government have careless custom of commit what they do to print paper these paper deal with all sort of thing from the payment of to secret service native up to rebuke administer to vakil and motamid of native state and rather brusque letter to native prince tell they to put their house in order to refrain from kidnap woman or fill offender with pound red pepper and eccentricity of that kind of course these thing could never be make public because native prince never err officially and their state be officially as well administer as our territory also the private allowance to various queer people be not exactly matter to put into newspaper though they give quaint reading sometimes when the supreme government be at simla these paper be prepare there and go round to the people who ought to see they in office box or by post the principle of secrecy be to that viceroy quite as important as the practice and he hold that benevolent despotism like ours should never allow even little thing such as appointment of subordinate clerk to leak out till the proper time he be always remarkable for his principle there be very important batch of paper in preparation at that time it have to travel from one end of simla to the other by hand it be not put into an official envelope but large square pale pink one the matter be in ms on soft crinkley paper it be address to the head clerk etc etc now between the head clerk etc etc and mrs hauksbee and flourish be no very great difference if the address be write in very bad hand as this be the chaprassi who take the envelope be not more of an idiot than most chaprassis he merely forget where this most unofficial cover be to be deliver and so ask the first englishman he meet who happen to be man rid down to annandale in great hurry the englishman hardly look say hauksbee sahib ki mem and go on so do the chaprasss because that letter be the last in stock and he want to get his work over there be no book to sign he thrust the letter into mrs hauksbee 's bearer 's hand and go off to smoke with friend mrs hauksbee be expect some cut out pattern thing in flimsy paper from friend as soon as she get the big square packet therefore she say oh the dear creature and tear it open with paper knife and all the ms enclosure tumble out on the floor mrs hauksbee begin read have say the batch be rather important that be quite enough for you to know it refer to some correspondence two measure peremptory order to native chief and two dozen other thing mrs hauksbee gasp as she read for the first glimpse of the naked machinery of the great indian government strip of its casing and lacquer and paint and guard rail impress even the most stupid man and
mrs hauksbee be clever woman she be little afraid at first and feel as if she have lie hold of lightning flash by the tail and do not quite know what to do with it there be remark and initial at the side of the paper and some of the remark be rather more severe than the paper the initial belong to man who be all dead or go now but they be great in their day mrs hauksbee read on and think calmly the government send out weird civilian now and again but mcgoggin be the queer export for long time he be clever brilliantly clever but his cleverness work the wrong way instead of keep to the study of the vernacular he have read some book write by man call comte think and man call spencer and professor clifford you will find these book in the library they deal with people be inside from the above his work it be not much of creed it only prove that man have no soul and there be no god and no hereafter and that you must worry along somehow for the good of humanity one of its minor tenet seem to be that the one thing more sinful than give an order be obey it at least that be what mcgoggin say but suspect he have misread his primer do not say word against this creed it be make up in town where there be nothing but machinery and asphalt and build all shut in by the fog naturally man grow to think that there be no one high than himself and that the metropolitan board of work make everything but in this country where you really see humanity raw brown naked humanity with nothing between it and the blaze sky and only the use up over handle earth underfoot the notion somehow die away and most folk come back to simple theory life in india be not long enough to waste in prove that there be no one in particular at the head of affair for this reason the deputy be above the assistant the commissioner above the deputy the lieutenant governor above the commissioner and the viceroy above all four under the order of the secretary of state who be responsible to the empress if the empress be not responsible to her maker if there be no maker for she to be responsible to the entire system of our administration must be wrong which be manifestly impossible at home man be to be excuse they be stall up good deal and get intellectually beany when you take gross beany horse to exercise he slaver and slobber over the bit till you can not see the horn

so do golightly 's khaki gaiter but he keep on steadily and try to think how pleasant the coolth be his next pony be rather brute at start and golightly 's hand be slippery with the rain contrive to get rid of golightly at corner he chase the animal catch it and go ahead briskly the spill have not improve his clothe or his temper and he have lose one spur he keep the other one employ by the time that stage be end the pony have have as much exercise as he want and in spite of the rain golightly be sweat freely at the end of another miserable half hour
golightly find the world disappear before his eye in clammy pulp the rain have turn the pith of his huge and snowy solah topee into an evil smell dough and it have close on his head like half open mushroom also the green lining be begin to run golightly do not say anything worth record here he tear off and squeeze up as much of the brim as be in his eye and plough on the back of the helmet be flap on his neck and the side stick to his ear but the leather band and green lining keep thing roughly together so that the hat do not actually melt away where it flap presently the pulp and the green stuff make sort of slimy mildew which run over golightly in several direction down his back and bosom for choice the khaki color run too it be really shockingly bad dye and section of golightly be brown and patch be violet and contour be ochre and streak be ruddy red and blotch be nearly white accord to the nature and peculiarity of the dye when he take out his handkerchief to wipe his face and the green of the hat lining and the purple stuff that have soak through on to his neck from the tie become thoroughly mix the effect be amazing near dhar the rain stop and the evening sun come out and dry he up slightly it fix the color too three mile from pathankote the last pony fall dead lame and golightly be force to walk he push on into pathankote to find his servant he do not know then that his khitmatgar have stop by the roadside to get drunk and would come on the next day say that he have sprain his ankle when he get into pathankote he could not find his servant his boot be stiff and ropy with mud and there be large quantity of dirt about his body the blue tie have run as much as the khaki
so he take it off with the collar and throw it away then he say something about servant generally and try to get peg he pay eight anna for the drink and this reveal to he that he have only six anna more in his pocket or in the world as he stand at that hour he go to the station master to negotiate for first class ticket to khasa where he be station the booking clerk say something to the station master the station master say something to the telegraph clerk and the three look at he with curiosity they ask he to wait for half an hour while they telegraph to umritsar for authority so he wait and four constable come and group themselves picturesquely round he just as he be prepare to ask they to go away the station master say that he would give the sahib ticket to umritsar if the sahib would kindly come inside the booking office golightly step inside and the next thing he know be that constable be attach to each of his leg and arm while the station master be try to cram mailbag over his head there be very fair scuffle all round the booking office and golightly receive nasty cut over his eye through fall against table
but the constable be too much for he and they and the station master handcuff he securely as soon as the mail bag be slip he begin express his opinion and the head constable said:--“without
doubt this be the soldier englishman we require listen to the abuse then golightly ask the station master what the this and the that the proceeding mean the station master tell he he be private john binkle of the regiment ft in fair hair gray eye and dissipate appearance no mark on the body who have desert fortnight ago
golightly begin explain at great length and the more he explain the less the station master believe he he say that no lieutenant could look such ruffian as do golightly and that his instruction be to send his capture under proper escort to umritsar golightly be feel very damp and uncomfortable and the language he use be not fit for publication even in an expurgate form the four constable see he safe to umritsar in an intermediate compartment and he spend the four hour journey in abuse they as fluently as his knowledge of the vernacular allow at umritsar
he be bundle out on the platform into the arm of corporal and two man of the regiment golightly draw himself up and try to carry off matter jauntily he do not feel too jaunty in handcuff with four constable behind he and the blood from the cut on his forehead stiffen on his left cheek the corporal be not jocular either golightly get as far as--“thi be very absurd mistake my man when the corporal tell he to stow his lip and come along
golightly do not want to come along he desire to stop and explain he explain very well indeed until the corporal cut in with:--“you orficer it be the like you as bring disgrace on the like of we bloom in fine orficer you be know your regiment the rogue 's march be the quickstep where you come from you be black shame to the service golightly keep his temper and begin explain all over again from the beginning then he be march out of the rain into the refreshment room and tell not to make qualified fool of himself the man be go to run he up to fort govindghar and run up be performance almost as undignified as the frog march golightly be nearly hysterical with rage and the chill and the mistake and the handcuff and the headache that the cut on his forehead have give he he really lie himself out to express what be in his mind when he have quite finish and his throat be feel dry one of the man said:--“i've eard few beggar in the click blind stiff and crack on bit but have never eard any one to touch this ere orficer they be not angry with he they rather admire he they have some beer at the refreshment room and offer golightly some too because he have swear won'erful they ask he to tell they all about the adventure of private john binkle while he be loose on the countryside and that make golightly wild than ever if he have keep his wit about he he would have keep quiet until an officer come but he attempt to run now the butt of martini in the small of your back hurt great deal and rotten rain soak khaki tear easily when two man be jerk at your collar golightly rise from the floor feel very sick and giddy with his shirt rip open all down his breast and nearly all down his back he yield to his luck and at that point the down train from lahore come in carry one of golightly 's major this be the major 's evidence in full:-- there be the sound of scuffle in the second class refreshment room so go in and see the most villainous loafer that ever set eye on his boot and breech be plaster with mud and beer stain he wear muddy white dunghill sort of thing on his head and it hang down in slip on his shoulder which be good deal scratch he be half in and half out of shirt as nearly in two piece as it could be and he be beg the guard to look at the name on the tail of it as he have ruck the shirt all over his head could not at first see who he be but fancy that he be man in the first stage of d. t. from the way he swear while he wrestle with his rag when he turn round and have make allowance for lump as big as pork pie over one eye and some green war paint on the face and some violet stripe round the neck see that it be golightly he be very glad to see say the major
and he hop would not tell the mess about it do
but you can if you like now that golightly have go
home golightly spend the great part of that summer in try to get the corporal and the two soldier try by court martial for arrest an officer and gentleman they be of course very sorry for their error but the tale leak into the regimental canteen and thence run about the province the house of suddhoo stone 's throw out on either hand from that well order road we tread and all the world be wild and strange churel and ghoul and djinn and sprite shall bear us company to night for we have reach the old land wherein the power of darkness range from the dusk to the dawn the house of suddhoo near the taksali gate be two storied with four carve window of old brown wood and flat roof you may recognize it by five red hand print arrange like the five of diamond on the whitewash between the upper window bhagwan dass the bunnia and man who say he get his living by seal cut live in the low story with troop of wife servant friend and retainer the two upper room use to be occupy by janoo and azizun and little black and tan terrier that be steal from an englishman 's house and give to janoo by soldier to day only janoo live in the upper room suddhoo sleep on the roof generally except when he sleep in the street he use to go to peshawar in the cold weather to visit his son who sell curiosity near the edwarde gate and then he sleep under real mud roof suddhoo be great friend of mine because his cousin have son who secure thank to my recommendation the post of head messenger to big firm in the station suddhoo say that god will make lieutenant governor one of these day daresay his prophecy will come true he be very very old with white hair and no tooth worth show
and he have outlive his wit outlive nearly everything except his fondness for his son at peshawar janoo and azizun be kashmiris lady of the city and theirs be an ancient and more or less honorable profession but azizun have since marry medical student from the north west and have settle down to most respectable life somewhere near bareilly
bhagwan dass be an extortionate and an adulterator he be very rich the man who be suppose to get his living by seal cut pretend to be very poor this let you know as much as be necessary of the four principal tenant in the house of suddhoo then there be of course but be only the chorus that come in at the end to explain thing so do not count suddhoo be not clever the man who pretend to cut seal be the clever of they all bhagwan dass only know how to lie except janoo she be also beautiful but that be her own affair suddhoo 's son at peshawar be attack by pleurisy and old suddhoo be trouble the seal cutter man hear of suddhoo 's anxiety and make capital out of it he be abreast of the time he get friend in peshawar to telegraph daily account of the son 's health and here the story begin suddhoo 's cousin 's son tell one evening that suddhoo want to see that he be too old and feeble to come personally and that should be confer an everlasting honor on the house of suddhoo if go to he go but think see how well off suddhoo be then that he might have send something well than an ekka which jolt fearfully to haul out future lieutenant governor to the city on muggy april evening the ekka do not run quickly it be full dark when we pull up opposite the door of ranjit singh 's tomb near the main gate of the fort here be suddhoo and he say that by reason of my condescension it be absolutely certain that should become lieutenant governor while my hair be yet black then we talk about the weather and the state of my health and the wheat crop for fifteen minute in the huzuri bagh under the star suddhoo come to the point at last he say that janoo have tell he that there be an order of the sirkar against magic because it be fear that magic might one day kill the empress of india do not know anything about the state of the law but fancy that something interesting be go to happen say that so far from magic be discourage by the government it be highly commend the great official of the state practice it themselves if the financial statement be not magic do not know what be then to encourage he far say that if there be any jadoo afoot have not the least objection to give it my countenance and sanction and to see that it be clean jadoo white magic as distinguish from the unclean jadoo which kill folk it take long time before suddhoo admit that this be just what he have ask to come for then he tell in jerk and quaver that the man who say he cut seal be sorcerer of the clean kind that every day he give suddhoo news of the sick son in peshawar more quickly than the lightning could fly and that this news be always corroborate by the letter far that he have tell suddhoo how great danger be threaten his son which could be remove by clean jadoo and of course heavy payment begin to see how the land lie and tell suddhoo that also understand little jadoo in the western line and would go to his house to see that everything be do decently and in order we set off together and on the way suddhoo tell he have pay the seal cutter between one hundred and two hundred rupee already and the jadoo of that night would cost two hundred more which be cheap he say consider the greatness of his son 's danger but do not think he mean it the light be all cloak in the front of the house when we arrive could hear awful noise from behind the seal cutter 's shop front as if some one be groan his soul out suddhoo shake all over and while we grope our way upstairs tell that the jadoo have begin janoo and azizun meet we at the stair head and tell we that the jadoo work be come off in their room because there be more space there janoo be lady of freethinke turn of mind she whisper that the jadoo be an invention to get money out of suddhoo and that the seal cutter would go to hot place when he die suddhoo be nearly cry with fear and old age he keep walk up and down the room in the half light repeat his son 's name over and over again and ask azizun if the seal cutter ought not to make reduction in the case of his own landlord janoo pull over to the shadow in the recess of the carve bow window the board be up and the room be only light by one tiny lamp there be no chance of my be see if stay still presently the groan below cease
and we hear step on the staircase that be the seal cutter he stop outside the door as the terrier bark and azizun fumble at the chain
and he tell suddhoo to blow out the lamp this leave the place in jet darkness except for the red glow from the two huqas that belong to janoo and azizun the seal cutter come in and hear suddhoo throw himself down on the floor and groan azizun catch her breath and janoo back to one of the bed with shudder there be clink of something metallic and then shoot up pale blue green flame near the ground the light be just enough to show azizun press against one corner of the room with the terrier between her knee janoo with her hand clasp lean forward as she sit on the bed suddhoo face down quiver and the seal cutter hope may never see another man like that seal cutter he be strip to the waist with wreath of white jasmine as thick as my wrist round his forehead salmon color loin cloth round his middle and steel bangle on each ankle this be not awe inspire it be the face of the man that turn cold it be blue gray in the first place in the second the eye be roll back till you could only see the white of they and in the third the face be the face of demon ghoul anything you please except of the sleek oily old ruffian who sit in the day time over his turn lathe downstairs he be lie on his stomach with his arm turn and cross behind he as if he have be throw down pinion his head and neck be the only part of he off the floor they be nearly at right angle to the body like the head of cobra at spring it be ghastly in the centre of the room on the bare earth floor stand big deep brass basin with pale blue green light float in the centre like night light round that basin the man on the floor wriggle himself three time how he do it do not know could see the muscle ripple along his spine and fall smooth again but could not see any other motion the head seem the only thing alive about he except that slow curl and uncurl of the labor back muscle janoo from the bed be breathe seventy to the minute azizun hold her hand before her eye and old suddhoo fingering at the dirt that have get into his white beard be cry to himself the horror of it be that the creep crawly thing make no sound only crawl and remember this last for ten minute while the terrier whine and azizun shudder and janoo gasp and suddhoo cry feel the hair lift at the back of my head and my heart thump like thermantidote paddle luckily the seal cutter betray himself by his most impressive trick and make calm again after he have finish that unspeakable triple crawl he stretch his head away from the floor as high as he could and send out jet of fire from his nostril now know how fire spout be do can do it myself so feel at ease the business be fraud if he have only keep to that crawl without try to raise the effect goodness know what might not have think both the girl shriek at the jet of fire and the head drop chin down on the floor with thud the whole body lie then like corpse with its arm truss there be pause of five full minute after this and the blue green flame die down janoo stoop to settle one of her anklet while azizun turn her face to the wall and take the terrier in her arm suddhoo put out an arm mechanically to janoo 's huqa and she slide it across the floor with her foot directly above the body and on the wall be couple of flame portrait in stamp paper frame of the queen and the prince of wales they look down on the performance and to my thinking seem to heighten the grotesqueness of it all just when the silence be get unendurable the body turn over and roll away from the basin to the side of the room where it lie stomach up there be faint plop from the basin exactly like the noise fish make when it take fly and the green light in the centre revive look at the basin and see bob in the water the dry shrivelled black head of native baby open eye open mouth and shave scalp it be bad be so very sudden than the crawl exhibition we have no time to say anything before it begin to speak read poe 's account of the voice that come from the mesmerize die man
and you will realize less than one half of the horror of that head 's voice there be an interval of second or two between each word and sort of ring ring ring in the note of the voice like the timbre of bell it peal slowly as if talk to itself for several minute before get rid of my cold sweat then the blessed solution strike look at the body lie near the doorway and see just where the hollow of the throat join on the shoulder muscle that have nothing to do with any man 's regular breathing twitch away steadily the whole thing be careful reproduction of the egyptian teraphin that one read about sometimes and the voice be as clever and as appal piece of ventriloquism as one could wish to hear all this time the head be lip lip lap against the side of the basin and speak it tell suddhoo on his face again whine of his son 's illness and of the state of the illness up to the evening of that very night always shall respect the seal cutter for keep so faithfully to the time of the peshawar telegram it go on to say that skilled doctor be night and day watch over the man 's life and that he would eventually recover if the fee to the potent sorcerer whose servant be the head in the basin be double here the mistake from the artistic point of view come in to ask for twice your stipulate fee in voice that lazarus might have use when he rise from the dead be absurd janoo who be really woman of masculine intellect see this as quickly as do hear she say asli nahin scornfully under her breath and just as she say so the light in the basin die out the head stop talk
and we hear the room door creak on its hinge then janoo strike match light the lamp
and we see that head basin and seal cutter be go suddhoo be wring his hand and explain to any one who care to listen that if his chance of eternal salvation depend on it he could not raise another two hundred rupee azizun be nearly in hysteric in the corner while janoo sit down composedly on one of the bed to discuss the probability of the whole thing be bunao or make up explain as much as know of the seal cutter 's way of jadoo but her argument be much more simple:--“the magic that be always demand gift be no true magic say she my mother tell that the only potent love spell be those which be tell you for love this seal cutter man be liar and devil dare not tell do anything or get anything do because be in debt to bhagwan dass the bunnia for two gold ring and heavy anklet must get my food from his shop the seal cutter be the friend of bhagwan dass and he would poison my food fool 's jadoo have be go on for ten day and have cost suddhoo many rupee each night the seal cutter use black hen and lemon and mantra before he never show we anything like this till to night azizun be fool and will be pur dahnashin soon suddhoo have lose his strength and his wit see now have hop to get from suddhoo many rupee while he live and many more after his death and behold he be spend everything on that offspring of devil and she ass the seal cutter
said:--“but what induce suddhoo to drag into the business of course can speak to the seal cutter and he shall refund the whole thing be child 's talk shame and senseless suddhoo be an old child say janoo he have live on the roof these seventy year and be as senseless as milch goat he bring you here to assure himself that he be not break any law of the sirkar whose salt he eat many year ago he worship the dust off the foot of the seal cutter and that cow devourer have forbid he to go and see his son what do suddhoo know of your law or the lightning post have to watch his money go day by day to that lie beast below janoo stamp her foot on the floor and nearly cry with vexation while suddhoo be whimper under blanket in the corner and azizun be try to guide the pipe stem to his foolish old mouth now the case stand thus unthinkingly have lie myself open to the charge of aid and abet the seal cutter in obtain money under false pretence which be forbid by section of the indian penal code be helpless in the matter for these reason can not inform the police what witness would support my statement janoo refuse flatly azizun be veil woman somewhere near bareilly lose in this big india of our can not again take the law into my own hand and speak to the seal cutter for certain be that not only would suddhoo disbelieve but this step would end in the poisoning of janoo who be bind hand and foot by her debt to the bunnia suddhoo be an old dotard and whenever we meet mumble my idiotic joke that the sirkar rather patronize the black art than otherwise his son be well now but suddhoo be completely under the influence of the seal cutter by whose advice he regulate the affair of his life janoo watch daily the money that she hop to wheedle out of suddhoo take by the seal cutter and the man we hunt cain some century ago across the world that breed the fear our own misdeed maintain to day vibart 's morality shakespeare say something about worm or it may be giant or beetle turn if you tread on they too severely the safe plan be never to tread on worm not even on the last new subaltern from home with his button hardly out of their tissue paper and the red of sappy english beef in his cheek this be the story of the worm that turn for the sake of brevity we will call henry augustus ramsay faizanne the worm although he really be an exceedingly pretty boy without hair on his face and with waist like girl be when he come out to the second shikarri and be make unhappy in several way the shikarris be high caste regiment and you must be able to do thing well play banjo or ride more than little or sing or act to get on with they the worm do nothing except fall off his pony and knock chip out of gate post with his trap even that become monotonous after time he object to whist cut the cloth at billiard sing out of tune keep very much to himself and write to his mamma and sister at home four of these five thing be vice which the shikarris object to and set themselves to eradicate every one know how subaltern be by brother subaltern soften and not permit to be ferocious it be good and wholesome and do no one any harm unless temper be lose and then there be trouble there be man once but that be another story the shikarris shikar the worm very much and he bear everything without wink he be so good and so anxious to learn and flush so pink that his education be cut short and he be leave to his own device by every one except the senior subaltern who continue to make life burden to the worm the senior subaltern mean no harm but his chaff be coarse and he do not quite understand where to stop he have be wait too long for his company and that always sour man also he be in love which make he bad one day after he have borrow the worm 's trap for lady who never exist have use it himself all the afternoon have send note to the worm purport to come from the lady and be tell the mess all about it the worm rise in his place and say in his quiet ladylike voice that be very pretty sell but will lay you month 's pay to month 's pay when you get your step that work sell on you that you will remember for the rest of your day and the regiment after you when you be dead or break the worm be not angry in the least and the rest of the mess shout then the senior subaltern look at the worm from the boot upwards and down again and say do baby the worm take the rest of the mess to witness that the bet have be take and retire into book with sweet smile two month pass and the senior subaltern still educate the worm who begin to move about little more as the hot weather come on have say that the senior subaltern be in love the curious thing be that girl be in love with the senior subaltern though the colonel say awful thing and the major snort and married captain look unutterable wisdom and the junior scoff those two be engage the senior subaltern be so pleased with get his company and his acceptance at the same time that he forget to bother the worm the girl be pretty girl and have money of her own she do not come into this story at all one night at the beginning of the hot weather all the mess except the worm who have go to his own room to write home letter be sit on the platform outside the mess house the band have finish play but no one want to go in and the captain wife be there also the folly of man in love be unlimited the senior subaltern have be hold forth on the merit of the girl he be engage to and the lady be purr approval while the man yawn when there be rustle of skirt in the dark and tired faint voice lift itself where be my husband do not wish in the least to reflect on the morality of the shikarris but it be on record that four man jump up as if they have be shoot three of they be marry man perhaps they be afraid that their wife have come from home unbeknownst the fourth say that he have act on the impulse of the moment he explain this afterwards then the voice cried:--“oh lionel lionel be the senior subaltern 's name woman come into the little circle of light by the candle on the peg table stretch out her hand to the dark where the senior subaltern be and sob we rise to our foot feel that thing be go to happen and ready to believe the bad in this bad small world of our one know so little of the life of the next man which after all be entirely his own concern that one be not surprise when crash come anything might turn up any day for any one perhaps the senior subaltern have be trap in his youth man be crippled that way occasionally we do not know we want to hear and the captain wife be as anxious as we if he have be trap he be to be excuse for the woman from nowhere in the dusty shoe and gray travel dress be very lovely with black hair and great eye full of tear she be tall with fine figure and her voice have run sob in it pitiful to hear as soon as the senior subaltern stand up she throw her arm round his neck and call he my darling and say she could not bear wait alone in england and his letter be so short and cold and she be his to the end of the world and would he forgive she this do not sound quite like lady 's way of speak it be too demonstrative thing seem black indeed and the captain wife peer under their eyebrow at the senior subaltern and the colonel 's face set like the day of judgment frame in gray bristle and no one speak for while next the colonel say very shortly:--“well sir and the woman sob afresh the senior subaltern be half choke with the arm round his neck but he gasp out:--“it 's d----d lie never have wife in my life do not swear say the colonel come into the mess we must sift this clear somehow and he sigh to himself for he believe in his shikarris do the colonel we troop into the ante room under the full light and there we see how beautiful the woman be she stand up in the middle of we all sometimes choke with cry then hard and proud and then hold out her arm to the senior subaltern it be like the fourth act of tragedy she tell we how the senior subaltern have marry she when he be home on leave eighteen month before and she seem to know all that we know and more too of his people and his past life he be white and ashy gray try now and again to break into the torrent of her word and we note how lovely she be and what criminal he look esteem he beast of the bad kind we feel sorry for he though shall never forget the indictment of the senior subaltern by his wife nor will he it be so sudden rush out of the dark unannounced into our dull life the captain wife stand back but their eye be alight and you could see that they have already convict and sentence the senior subaltern the colonel seem five year old one major be shade his eye with his hand and watch the woman from underneath it another be chew his moustache and smile quietly as if he be witness play full in the open space in the centre by the whist table the senior subaltern 's terrier be hunt for flea remember all this as clearly as though photograph be in my hand remember the look of horror on the senior subaltern 's face it be rather like see man hang but much more interesting finally the woman wind up by say that the senior subaltern carry double f. m. in tattoo on his left shoulder we all know that and to our innocent mind it seem to clinch the matter but one of the bachelor major say very politely:--“i presume that your marriage certificate would be more to the purpose that rouse the woman she stand up and sneer at the senior subaltern for cur and abuse the major and the colonel and all the rest then she weep
and then she pull paper from her breast say imperially:--“take that and let my husband my lawfully we husband read it aloud if he dare there be hush and the man look into each other 's eye as the senior subaltern come forward in dazed and dizzy way and take the paper we be wonder as we star whether there be anything against any one of we that might turn up later on the senior subaltern 's throat be dry but as he run his eye over the paper he break out into hoarse cackle of relief and say to the woman:--“you young blackguard
but the woman have flee through door and on the paper be written:--“this be to certify that the worm have pay in full my debt to the senior subaltern and far that the senior subaltern be my debtor by agreement on the 23d of february as by the mess attest to the extent of one month 's captain 's pay in the lawful currency of the india empire then deputation set off for the worm 's quarter and find he betwixt and between unlace his stay with the hat wig serge dress etc on the bed he come over as he be and the shikarri shout till the gunner mess send over to know if they might have share of the fun think we be all except the colonel and
the senior subaltern little disappointed that the scandal have come to nothing but that be human nature there could be no two word about the worm 's act it lean as near to nasty tragedy as anything to act at home with my sister but no acting with girl could account for the worm 's display that night personally think it be in bad taste besides be dangerous there be no sort of use in play with
