while the big beam tilt or the last bell ring while horse be horse to train and to race then woman and wine take second place for for me-- while short ten three have field to squander or fence to face song of the g. r. there be more way of run horse to suit your book than pull his head off in the straight some man forget this understand clearly that all racing be rotten as everything connect with lose money must be out here in addition to its inherent rottenness it have the merit of be two third sham look pretty on paper only every one know every one else far too well for business purpose how on earth can you rack and harry and post man for his losing when you be fond of his wife and live in the same station with he he say on the monday follow can not settle just yet you say all right old man and think your self lucky if you pull off nine hundred out of two thousand rupee debt any way you look at it indian racing be immoral and expensively immoral which be much bad if man want your money he ought to ask for it or send round subscription list instead of juggle about the country with an australian larrikin brumby with as much breed as the boy brace of chumar in gold lace cap three or four ekka pony with hogged mane and switch tail demirep of mare call arab because she have kink in her flag racing lead to the shroff quick than anything else
but if you have no conscience and no sentiment and good hand and some knowledge of pace and ten year experience of horse and several thousand rupee month believe that you can occasionally contrive to pay your shoeing bill do you ever know shackle b. w. g. 15.13.8
-coarse loose mule like ear barrel as long as gate post tough as telegraph wire and the queer brute that ever look through bridle he be of no brand be one of an ear nick mob take into the bucephalus at 4l.-10s head to make up freight and sell raw and out of condition at calcutta for people who lose money on he call he brumby
but if ever any horse have harpoon 's shoulder and the gin 's temper shackle be that horse two mile be his own particular distance he train himself run himself and ride himself and if his jockey insult he by give he hint he shut up at once and buck the boy off he object to dictation two or three of his owner do not understand this and lose money in consequence at last he be buy by man who discover that if race be to be win shackle and shackle only would win it in his own way so long as his jockey sit still this man have rid boy call brunt lad from perth west australia
and he teach brunt with trainer 's whip the hard thing jock can learn to sit still to sit still and to keep on sit still when brunt fairly grasp this truth shackle devastate the country no weight could stop he at his own distance and the fame of shackle spread from ajmir in the south to chedputter in the north there be no horse like shackle so long as he be allow to do his work in his own way
but he be beat in the end and the story of his fall be enough to make angel weep at the low end of the chedputter racecourse just before the turn into the straight the track pass close to couple of old brick mound enclose funnel shape hollow the big end of the funnel be not six foot from the railing on the off side the astounding peculiarity of the course be that if you stand at one particular place about half mile away inside the course and speak at an ordinary pitch your voice just hit the funnel of the brick mound and make curious whining echo there man discover this one morning by accident while out training with friend he mark the place to stand and speak from with couple of brick
and he keep his knowledge to himself every peculiarity of course be worth remember in country where rat play the mischief with the elephant litter and steward build jump to suit their own stable this man run very fairish country breed long rack high mare with the temper of fiend and the pace of an airy wander seraph drifty glidy stretch the mare be as delicate tribute to mrs reiver call the lady regula baddun”--or for short regula baddun shackle jockey brunt be quiet well behave boy
but his nerve have be shake he begin his career by rid jump race in melbourne where few steward want lynching and be one of the jockey who come through the awful butchery perhaps you will recollect it of the maribyrnong plate the wall be colonial rampart log of jarrak spike into masonry with wing as strong as church buttress once in his stride horse have to jump or fall he could not run out in the maribyrnong plate twelve horse be jam at the second wall red hat lead fall this side and throw out the glen and the ruck come up behind and the space between wing and wing be one struggle scream kick shamble four jockey be take out dead three be very badly hurt and brunt be among the three he tell the story of the maribyrnong plate sometimes and when he describe how whalley on red hat say as the mare fall under him:--“god ha mercy be do for and how next instant sithee there and white otter have crush the life out of poor whalley and the dust hide small hell of man and horse no one marvel that brunt have drop jump race and australia together regula baddun 's owner know that story by heart brunt never vary it in the telling he have no education shackle come to the chedputter autumn race one year and his owner walk about insult the sportsman of chedputter generally till they go to the honorary secretary in body and said:--“appoint handicapper and arrange race which shall break shackle and humble the pride of his owner the district rise against shackle and send up of their good ousel who be suppose to be able to do his mile in petard the stud breed train by cavalry regiment who know how to train gringalet the ewe lamb of the 75th bobolink the pride of peshawar and many other they call that race the break link handicap because it be to smash shackle and the handicapper pile on the weight and the fund give eight hundred rupee and the distance be round the course for all horse shackle owner said:--“you can arrange the race with regard to shackle only so long as you do not bury he under weight cloth do not mind regula baddun 's owner said:--“i throw in my mare to fret ousel six furlong be regula 's distance
and she will then lie down and die so also will ousel for his jockey do not understand wait race now this be lie for regula have be in work for two month at dehra and her chance be good always suppose that shackle break blood vessel or brunt move on he the plunging in the lottery be fine they fill eight thousand rupee lottery on the break link handicap and the account in the pioneer say that favoritism be divide in plain english the various contingent be wild on their respective horse for the handicapper have do their work well the honorary secretary shout himself hoarse through the din and the smoke of the cheroot be like the smoke and the rattling of the dice box like the rattle of small arm fire ten horse start very level and regula baddun 's owner canter out on his back to place inside the circle of the course where two brick have be throw he face towards the brick mound at the low end of the course and wait the story of the running be in the pioneer at the end of the first mile shackle creep out of the ruck well on the outside ready to get round the turn lie hold of the bit and spin up the straight
before the other know he have get away brunt be sit still perfectly happy listen to the drum drum drum of the hoofs behind and know that in about twenty stride shackle would draw one deep breath and go up the last half mile like the fly dutchman as shackle go short to take the turn and come abreast of the brick mound brunt hear above the noise of the wind in his ear whine wail voice on the offside saying:--“god ha mercy be do for in one stride brunt see the whole seethe smash of the maribyrnong plate before he start in his saddle and give yell of terror the start bring the heel into shackle side and the scream hurt shackle feeling he could not stop dead
but he put out his foot and slide along for fifty yard and then very gravely and judicially buck off brunt shake terror stricken lump while regula baddun make neck and neck race with bobolink up the straight and win by short head petard bad third shackle owner in the stand try to think that his field glass have go wrong regula baddun 's owner wait by the two brick give one deep sigh of relief and canter back to the stand he have win in lottery and bet about fifteen thousand it be break link handicap with vengeance it break nearly all the man concern and nearly break the heart of shackle owner he go down to interview brunt the boy lie livid and gasp with fright where he have tumble off the sin of lose the race never seem to strike he all he know be that whalley have call he that the call be warn and be he cut in two for it he would never get up again his nerve have go altogether and he only ask his master to give he good thrashing and let he go he be fit for nothing he say he get his dismissal and creep up to the paddock white as chalk with blue lip his knee give way under he people say nasty thing in the paddock but brunt never heed he change into tweed take his stick and go down the road still shake with fright and mutter over and over again:--“god
ha mercy be do for to the good of my knowledge and belief he speak the truth
so now you know how the break link handicap be run and win of course you do not believe it you would credit anything about russia 's design on india or the recommendation of the currency commission but little bit of sober fact be more than you can stand beyond the pale love heed not caste nor sleep break bed go in search of love and lose myself hindu proverb man should whatever happen keep to his own caste race and breed let the white go to the white and the black to the black then whatever trouble fall be in the ordinary course of thing neither sudden alien nor unexpected this be the story of man who wilfully step beyond the safe limit of decent every day society and pay for it heavily he know too much in the first instance and he see too much in the second he take too deep an interest in native life but he will never do so again deep away in the heart of the city behind jitha megji 's bustee lie amir nath 's gully which end in dead wall pierce by one grated window at the head of the gully be big cow byre and the wall on either side of the gully be without window neither suchet singh nor gaur chand approve of their woman folk look into the world if durga charan have be of their opinion he would have be happy man to day and little biessa would have be able to knead her own bread her room look out through the grated window into the narrow dark gully where the sun never come and where the buffalo wallow in the blue slime she be widow about fifteen year old
and she pray the god day and night to send she lover for she do not approve of live alone one day the man trejago his name be come into amir nath 's gully on an aimless wandering and after he have pas the buffalo stumble over big heap of cattle food then he see that the gully end in trap and hear little laugh from behind the grated window it be pretty little laugh and trejago know that for all practical purpose the old arabian night be good guide go forward to the window and whisper that verse of the love song of har dyal which begin can man stand upright in the face of the naked sun or lover in the presence of his beloved if my foot fail heart of my heart be to blame be blind by the glimpse of your beauty there come the faint tchink of woman 's bracelet from behind the grating and little voice go on with the song at the fifth verse
alas alas can the moon tell the lotus of her love when the gate of heaven be shut and the cloud gather for the rain they have take my beloved and drive she with the pack horse to the north there be iron chain on the foot that be set on my heart call to the bowman to make ready-- the voice stop suddenly and trejago walk out of amir nath 's gully wonder who in the world could have cap the love song of har dyal so neatly next morning as he be drive to the office an old woman throw packet into his dog cart in the packet be the half of break glass bangle one flower of the blood red dhak pinch of bhusa or cattle food and eleven cardamom that packet be letter not clumsy compromise letter but an innocent unintelligible lover 's epistle trejago know far too much about these thing as have say no englishman should be able to translate object letter but trejago spread all the trifle on the lid of his office box and begin to puzzle they out break glass bangle stand for hindu widow all india over because when her husband die woman 's bracelet be break on her wrist trejago see the meaning of the little bit of the glass the flower of the dhak mean diversely desire come write or danger accord to the other thing with it one cardamom mean jealousy but when any article be duplicate in an object letter it lose its symbolic meaning and stand merely for one of number indicate time or if incense curd or saffron be send also place the message run then:--“a widow dhak flower and bhusa at eleven o'clock the pinch of bhusa enlighten trejago he see this kind of letter leave much to instinctive knowledge that the bhusa refer to the big heap of cattle food over which he have fall in amir nath 's gully and that the message must come from the person behind the grating she be widow so the message run then:--“a widow in the gully in which be the heap of bhusa desire you to come at eleven o'clock trejago throw all the rubbish into the fireplace and laugh he know that man in the east do not make love under window at eleven in the forenoon nor do woman fix appointment week in advance
so he go that very night at eleven into amir nath 's gully clothe in boorka which cloak man as well as woman directly the gong in the city make the hour the little voice behind the grating take up the love song of har dyal at the verse where the panthan girl call upon har dyal to return the song be really pretty in the vernacular in english you miss the wail of it it run something like this:-- alone upon the housetop to the north turn and watch the lightning in the sky,-- the glamour of thy footstep in the north come back to beloved or die below my foot the still bazar be lie far far below the weary camel lie,-- the camel and the captive of thy raid come back to beloved or die my father 's wife be old and harsh with year and drudge of all my father 's house be i.-- my bread be sorrow and my drink be tear come back to beloved or die as the song stop trejago step up under the grating and whispered:--“i be here bisesa be good to look upon that night be the beginning of many strange thing and of double life so wild that trejago to day sometimes wonder if it be not all dream bisesa or her old handmaiden who have throw the object letter have detach the heavy grating from the brick work of the wall so that the window slide inside leave only square of raw masonry into which an active man might climb in the day time trejago drive through his routine of office work or put on his call clothe and call on the lady of the station wonder how long they would know he if they know of poor little bisesa at night when all the city be still come the walk under the evil smell boorka the patrol through jitha megji 's bustee the quick turn into amir nath 's gully between the sleeping cattle and the dead wall and then last of all bisesa and the deep even breathing of the old woman who sleep outside the door of the bare little room that durga charan allot to his sister 's daughter who or what durga charan be trejago never inquire and why in the world he be not discover and knife never occur to he till his madness be over and bisesa but this come later bisesa be an endless delight to trejago she be as ignorant as bird and her distorted version of the rumor from the outside world that have reach she in her room amuse trejago almost as much as her lisping attempt to pronounce his name--“christopher the first syllable be always more than she could manage and she make funny little gesture with her rose leaf hand as one throw the name away and then kneel before trejago ask he exactly as an englishwoman would do if he be sure he love she trejago swear that he love she more than any one else in the world which be true after month of this folly the exigency of his other life compel trejago to be especially attentive to lady of his acquaintance you may take it for fact that anything of this kind be not only notice and discuss by man 's own race but by some hundred and fifty native as well trejago have to walk with this lady and talk to she at the band stand and once or twice to drive with she never for an instant dream that this would affect his dear out of the way life but the news fly in the usual mysterious fashion from mouth to mouth till bisesa 's duenna hear of it and tell bisesa the child be so troubled that she do the household work evilly and be beat by durga charan 's wife in consequence week later bisesa tax trejago with the flirtation she understand no gradation and speak openly trejago laugh and bisesa stamp her little foot little foot light as marigold flower that could lie in the palm of man 's one hand much that be write about oriental passion and impulsiveness be exaggerate and compile at second hand but little of it be true and when an englishman find that little it be quite as startling as any passion in his own proper life bisesa rag and storm and finally threaten to kill herself if trejago do not at once drop the alien memsahib who have come between they trejago try to explain and to show she that she do not understand these thing from western standpoint bisesa draw herself up and say simply do not know only this it be not good that should have make you dear than my own heart to sahib you be an englishman be only black girl”--she be fair than bar gold in the mint--“and the widow of black man then she sob and say but on my soul and my mother 's soul love you there shall no harm come to you whatever happen to trejago argue with the child and try to soothe she
but she seem quite unreasonably disturb nothing would satisfy she save that all relation between they should end he be to go away at once
and he go as he drop out at the window she kiss his forehead twice and he walk away wonder week and then three week pass without sign from bisesa trejago think that the rupture have last quite long enough go down to amir nath 's gully for the fifth time in the three week hop that his rap at the sill of the shift grating would be answer he be not disappoint there be young moon and one stream of light fall down into amir nath 's gully and strike the grating which be draw away as he knock from the black dark bisesa hold out her arm into the moonlight both hand have be cut off at the wrist and the stump be nearly heal then as bisesa bow her head between her arm and sob some one in the room grunt like wild beast and something sharp knife sword or spear thrust at trejago in his boorka the stroke miss his body but cut into one of the muscle of the groin
and he limp slightly from the wound for the rest of his day the grating go into its place there be no sign whatever from inside the house nothing but the moonlight strip on the high wall and the blackness of amir nath 's gully behind the next thing trejago remember after rag and shout like madman between those pitiless wall be that he find himself near the river as the dawn be break throw away his boorka and go home bareheade what the tragedy be whether bisesa have in fit of causeless despair tell everything or the intrigue have be discover
and she torture to tell whether durga charan know his name and what become of bisesa trejago do not know to this day something horrible have happen and the thought of what it must have be come upon trejago in the night now and again and keep he company till the morning one special feature of the case be that he do not know where lie the front of durga charan 's house it may open on to courtyard common to two or more house or it may lie behind any one of the gate of jitha megji 's bustee trejago can not tell he can not get bisesa poor little bisesa back again he have lose she in the city where each man 's house be as guard and as unknowable as the grave and the grating that open into amir nath 's gully have be wall up but trejago pay his call regularly and be reckon very decent sort of man there be nothing peculiar about he except slight stiffness cause by rid strain in the right leg in error they burn corpse upon the sand-- the light shine out afar it guide home the plunge boat that beat from zanzibar spirit of fire where'er thy altar rise thou art light of guidance to our eye salsette boat song there be hope for man who get publicly and riotously drunk more often that he ought to do
but there be no hope for the man who drink secretly and alone in his own house the man who be never see to drink this be rule so there must be an exception to prove it moriarty 's case be that exception he be civil engineer and the government very kindly put he quite by himself in an out district with nobody but native to talk to and great deal of work to do he do his work well in the four year he be utterly alone but he pick up the vice of secret and solitary drinking and come up out of the wilderness more old and worn and haggard than the dead alive life have any right to make he you know the say that man who have be alone in the jungle for more than year be never quite sane all his life after people credit moriarty 's queerness of manner and moody way to the solitude and say it show how government spoil the future of its good man moriarty have build himself the plinth of very good reputation in the bridge dam girder line
but he know every night of the week that he be take step to undermine that reputation with l. l. l. and christopher and little nip of liqueur and filth of that kind he have sound constitution and great brain or else he would have break down and die like sick camel in the district as well man have do before he
government order he to simla after he have come out of the desert
and he go up meaning to try for post then vacant that season
mrs reiver perhaps you will remember she be in the height of her power and many man lie under her yoke everything bad that could be say have already be say about mrs reiver in another tale moriarty be heavily build and handsome very quiet and nervously anxious to please his neighbor when he be not sink in brown study he start good deal at sudden noise or if speak to without warn and when you watch he drink his glass of water at dinner you could see the hand shake little but all this be put down to nervousness and the quiet steady sip sip sip fill and sip sip sip again that go on in his own room when he be by himself be never know which be miraculous see how everything in man 's private life be public property out here moriarty be draw not into mrs reiver 's set because they be not his sort but into the power of mrs reiver and he fall down in front of she and make goddess of she this be due to his come fresh out of the jungle to big town he could not scale thing properly or see who be what because mrs reiver be cold and hard he say she be stately and dignified because she have no brain and could not talk cleverly he say she be reserve and shy
mrs reiver shy because she be unworthy of honor or reverence from any one he reverence she from distance and dower she with all the virtue in the bible and most of those in shakespeare this big dark abstracted man who be so nervous when pony canter behind he use to moon in the train of mrs reiver blush with pleasure when she throw word or two his way his admiration be strictly platonic even other woman see and admit this he do not move out in simla so he hear nothing against his idol which be satisfactory mrs reiver take no special notice of he beyond see that he be add to her list of admirer and go for walk with he now and then just to show that he be her property claimable as such moriarty must have do most of the talking for mrs reiver could not talk much to man of his stamp and the little she say could not have be profitable what moriarty believe in as he have good reason to be mrs reiver 's influence over he and in that belief set himself seriously to try to do away with the vice that only he himself know of his experience while he be fight with it must have be peculiar
but he never describe they sometimes he would hold off from everything except water for week then on rainy night when no one have ask he out to dinner and there be big fire in his room and everything comfortable he would sit down and make big night of it by add little nip to little nip plan big scheme of reformation meanwhile until he throw himself on his bed hopelessly drunk he suffer next morning one night the big crash come he be trouble in his own mind over his attempt to make himself worthy of the friendship of mrs reiver the past ten day have be very bad one and the end of it all be that he receive the arrear of two and three quarter year of sip in one attack of delirium treman of the subdued kind begin with suicidal depression go on to fit and start and hysteria and end with downright raving as he sit in chair in front of the fire or walk up and down the room pick handkerchief to piece you hear what poor moriarty really think of mrs reiver for he rave about she and his own fall for the most part though he ravel some
p. w.
d. account into the same skein of thought he talk and talk and talk in low dry whisper to himself and there be no stop he he seem to know that there be something wrong and twice try to pull himself together and confer rationally with the doctor but his mind run out of control at once and he fall back to whisper and the story of his trouble it be terrible to hear big man babble like child of all that man usually lock up and put away in the deep of his heart moriarty read out his very soul for the benefit of any one who be in the room between ten thirty that night and two forty five next morning from what he say one gather how immense an influence mrs reiver hold over he and how thoroughly he feel for his own lapse his whispering can not of course be put down here but they be very instructive as show the error of his estimate when the trouble be over and his few acquaintance be pity he for the bad attack of jungle fever that have so pull he down moriarty swear big oath to himself and go abroad again with mrs reiver till the end of the season adore she in quiet and deferential way as an angel from heaven later on he take to rid not hack but honest riding which be good proof that he be improve and you could slam door behind he without his jumping to his foot with gasp that again be hopeful how he keep his oath and what it cost he in the beginning nobody know he certainly manage to compass the hard thing that man who have drink heavily can do he take his peg and wine at dinner
but he never drink alone and never let what he drink have the least hold on he once he tell bosom friend the story of his great trouble and how the influence of pure honest woman and an angel as well have save he when the man startle at anything good be lie to mrs reiver 's door laugh it cost he moriarty 's friendship moriarty who be marry now to woman ten thousand time well than mrs reiver woman who believe that there be no man on earth as good and clever as her husband will go down to his grave vow and protest that mrs reiver save he from ruin in both world that she know anything of moriarty 's weakness nobody believe for moment that she would have cut he dead throw he over and acquaint all her friend with her discovery if she have know of it nobody who know she doubt for an instant moriarty think she something she never be and in that belief save himself which be just as good as though she have be everything that he have imagine but the question be what claim will mrs reiver have to the credit of moriarty 's salvation when her day of reckoning come bank fraud he drink strong water and his speech be coarse he purchase raiment and forebore to pay he strike trust junior with horse and win gymkhanas in doubtful way then twixt vice and folly turn aside to do good deed and straight to cloak they lie the mess room if reggie burke be in india now he would resent this tale be tell but as he be in hong kong and will not see it the telling be safe he be the man who work the big fraud on the sind and sialkote bank he be manager of an up country branch and sound practical man with large experience of native loan and insurance work he could combine the frivolity of ordinary life with his work and yet do well reggie burke ride anything that would let he get up dance as neatly as he ride and be want for every sort of amusement in the station
as he say himself and as many man find out rather to their surprise there be two burke both very much at your service reggie burke between four and ten ready for anything from hot weather gymkhana to rid picnic and between ten and four mr reginald burke manager of the sind and sialkote branch bank you might play polo with he one afternoon and hear he express his opinion when man cross and you might call on he next morning to raise two thousand rupee loan on five hundred pound insurance policy eighty pound pay in premium he would recognize you but you would have some trouble in recognize he the director of the bank it have its headquarters in calcutta and its general manager 's word carry weight with the government pick their man well they have test reggie up to fairly severe break strain they trust he just as much as director ever trust manager you must see for yourself whether their trust be misplace reggie 's branch be in big station and work with the usual staff one manager one accountant both english cashier and horde of native clerk besides the police patrol at night outside the bulk of its work for it be in thrive district be hoondi and accommodation of all kind fool have no grip of this sort of business and clever man who do not go about among his client and know more than little of their affair be bad than fool reggie be young look clean shave with twinkle in his eye and head that nothing short of gallon of the gunner madeira could make any impression on one day at big dinner he announce casually that the director have shift on to he natural curiosity from england in the accountant line he be perfectly correct mr silas riley accountant be most curious animal long gawky rawbone yorkshireman full of the savage self conceit that blossom be only in the good county in england arrogance be mild word for the mental attitude of mr s. riley he have work himself up after seven year to cashier 's position in huddersfield bank and all his experience lie among the factory of the north perhaps he would have do well on the bombay side where they be happy with one half per cent profit and money be cheap he be useless for upper india and wheat province where man want large head and touch of imagination if he be to turn out satisfactory balance sheet he be wonderfully narrow minded in business and be new to the country have no notion that indian banking be totally distinct from home work like most clever self make man he have much simplicity in his nature and somehow or other have construe the ordinarily polite term of his letter of engagement into belief that the director have choose he on account of his special and brilliant talent and that they set great store by he this notion grow and crystallize thus add to his natural north country conceit far he be delicate suffer from some trouble in his chest and be short in his temper you will admit that reggie have reason to call his new accountant natural curiosity the two man fail to hit it off at all riley consider reggie wild feather head idiot give to heaven only know what dissipation in low place call mess and totally unfit for the serious and solemn vocation of banking he could never get over reggie 's look of youth and you be damn air and he could not understand reggie 's friend clean build careless man in the army who ride over to big sunday breakfast at the bank and tell sultry story till riley get up and leave the room riley be always show reggie how the business ought to be conduct and reggie have more than once to remind he that seven year limited experience between huddersfield and beverly do not qualify man to steer big up country business then riley sulk and refer to himself as pillar of the bank and cherished friend of the director and reggie tear his hair if man 's english subordinate fail he in this country he come to hard time indeed for native help have strict limitation in the winter riley go sick for week at time with his lung complaint and this throw more work on reggie but he prefer it to the everlasting friction when riley be well one of the travel inspector of the bank discover these collapse and report they to the director now riley have be foist on the bank by an m. p. who want the support of riley 's father who again be anxious to get his son out to warm climate because of those lung the m. p. have an interest in the bank but one of the director want to advance nominee of his own and after riley 's father have die he make the rest of the board see that an accountant who be sick for half the year have well give place to healthy man if riley have know the real story of his appointment he might have behave well but know nothing his stretch of sickness alternate with restless persistent meddle irritation of reggie and all the hundred way in which conceit in subordinate situation can find play reggie use to call he striking and hair curl name behind his back as relief to his own feeling but he never abuse he to his face because he say riley be such frail beast that half of his loathsome conceit be due to pain in the chest late one april riley go very sick indeed the doctor punch he and thump he and tell he he would be well before long then the doctor go to reggie and said:--“do you know how sick your accountant be
no say reggie--“the bad
the well confound he he be clack nuisance when he be well will let you take away the bank safe if you can drug he silent for this hot weather
but the doctor do not laugh--“man be not joke he say will give he another three month in his bed and week or so more to die in on my honor and reputation that be all the grace he have in this world consumption have hold of he to the marrow reggie 's face change at once into the face of mr reginald burke and he answered:--“what can do nothing say the doctor for all practical purpose the man be dead already keep he quiet and cheerful and tell he he be go to recover that be all will look after he to the end of course the doctor go away and reggie sit down to open the evening mail his first letter be one from the director intimate for his information that mr riley be to resign under month 's notice by the term of his agreement tell reggie that their letter to riley would follow and advise reggie of the coming of new accountant man whom reggie know and like reggie light cheroot and before he have finish smoke he have sketch the outline of fraud he put away--“burked”--the director letter and go in to talk to riley who be as ungracious as usual and fret himself over the way the bank would run during his illness he never think of the extra work on reggie 's shoulder but solely of the damage to his own prospect of advancement then reggie assure he that everything would be well and that he reggie would confer with riley daily on the management of the bank riley be little soothe
but he hint in as many word that he do not think much of reggie 's business capacity reggie be humble and he have letter in his desk from the director that gilbarte or hardie might have be proud of the day pass in the big darken house and the director letter of dismissal to riley come and be put away by reggie who every evening bring the book to riley 's room and show he what have be go forward while riley snarl reggie do his good to make statement pleasing to riley but the accountant be sure that the bank be go to rack and ruin without he in june as the lying in bed tell on his spirit he ask whether his absence have be note by the director and reggie say that they have write most sympathetic letter hop that he would be able to resume his valuable service before long he show riley the letter and riley say that the director ought to have write to he direct few day later reggie open riley 's mail in the half light of the room and give he the sheet not the envelope of letter to riley from the director riley say he would thank reggie not to interfere with his private paper specially as reggie know he be too weak to open his own letter reggie apologize then riley 's mood change
and he lecture reggie on his evil way his horse and his bad friend of course lie here on my back mr burke can not keep you straight but when be well do hope you will pay some heed to my word reggie who have drop polo and dinner and tennis and all to attend to riley say that he be penitent and settle riley 's head on the pillow and hear he fret and contradict in hard dry hack whisper without sign of impatience this at the end of heavy day 's office work do double duty in the latter half of june when the new accountant come reggie tell he the fact of the case and announce to riley that he have guest stay with he riley say that he might have have more consideration than to entertain his doubtful friend at such time reggie make carron the new accountant sleep at the club in consequence carron 's arrival take some of the heavy work off his shoulder and he have time to attend to riley 's exaction to explain soothe invent and settle and resettle the poor wretch in bed and to forge complimentary letter from calcutta at the end of the first month riley wish to send some money home to his mother reggie send the draft at the end of the second month riley 's salary come in just the same reggie pay it out of his own pocket and with it write riley beautiful letter from the director riley be very ill indeed but the flame of his life burn unsteadily now
and then he would be cheerful and confident about the future sketch plan for go home and see his mother reggie listen patiently when the office work be over and encourage he at other time riley insist on reggie 's read the bible and grim methody tract to he out of these tract he point moral direct at his manager
but he always find time to worry reggie about the working of the bank and to show he where the weak point lay this in door sick room life and constant strain wear reggie down good deal and shake his nerve and lower his billiard play by forty point
but the business of the bank and the business of the sick room have to go on though the glass be degree in the shade at the end of the third month riley be sink fast and have begin to realize that he be very sick but the conceit that make he worry reggie keep he from believe the bad he want some sort of mental stimulant if he be to drag on say the doctor keep he interested in life if you care about his living
so riley contrary to all the law of business and the finance receive per cent rise of salary from the director the mental stimulant succeed
beautifully riley be happy and cheerful and as be often the case in consumption healthy in mind when the body be weak he linger for full month snarl and fret about the bank talk of the future hear the bible read lecture reggie on sin and wonder when he would be able to move abroad but at the end of september one mercilessly hot evening he rise up in his bed with little gasp and say quickly to reggie:--“mr burke be go to die know it in myself my chest be all hollow inside and there be nothing to breathe with to the good of my knowledge have do nowt”--he be return to the talk of his boyhood--“to lie heavy on my conscience god be thank have be preserve from the gross form of sin and counsel you mr burke here his voice die down and reggie stoop over he send my salary for september to my mother do great thing with the bank if have be spar mistaken policy no fault of mine then he turn his face to the wall and die reggie draw the sheet over its face and go out into the verandah with his last mental stimulant”--a letter of condolence and sympathy from the director unused in his pocket if would be only ten minute early think reggie might have hearten he up to pull through another day tod 's amendment the world hath set its heavy yoke upon the old white bearded folk who strive to please the king god 's mercy be upon the young god 's wisdom in the baby tongue that fear not anything the parable of chajju bhagat now tods mamma be singularly charming woman and every one in simla know tod most man have save he from death on occasion he be beyond his ayah 's control altogether and perille his life daily to find out what would happen if you pull mountain battery mule 's tail he be an utterly fearless young pagan about six year old and the only baby who ever break the holy calm of the supreme legislative council it happen this way tods pet kid get loose and flee up the hill off the boileaugunge road tod after it until it burst into the viceregal lodge lawn then attach to peterhoff the council be sit at the time and the window be open because it be warm the red lancer in the porch tell tod to go away but tod know the red lancer and most of the member of council personally moreover he have firm hold of the kid 's collar and be be drag all across the flower bed give my salaam to the long councillor sahib and ask he to help take moti back gasp tod the council hear the noise through the open window and after an interval be see the shocking spectacle of legal member and lieutenant governor help under the direct patronage of commander in chief and viceroy one small and very dirty boy in sailor 's suit and tangle of brown hair to coerce lively and rebellious kid they head it off down the path to the mall and tod go home in triumph and tell his mamma that all the councillor sahib have be help he to catch moti whereat his mamma smack tod for interfere with the administration of the empire but tod meet the legal member the next day and tell he in confidence that if the legal member ever want to catch goat he tod would give he all the help in his power thank you tod say the legal member tod be the idol of some eighty jhampani and half as many saise he salute they all as brother it never enter his head that any live human being could disobey his order and he be the buffer between the servant and his mamma 's wrath the working of that household turn on tod who be adore by every one from the dhoby to the dog boy even futteh khan the villainous loafer khit from mussoorie shirk risk tod displeasure for fear his co mate should look down on he so tod have honor in the land from boileaugunge to chota simla and rule justly accord to his light of course he speak urdu
but he have also master many queer side speech like the chotee bolee of the woman and hold grave converse with shopkeeper and hill cooly alike he be precocious for his age and his mix with native have teach he some of the more bitter truth of life the meanness and the sordidness of it he use over his bread and milk to deliver solemn and serious aphorism translate from the vernacular into the english that make his mamma jump and vow that tod must go home next hot weather just when tod be in the bloom of his power the supreme legislature be hack out bill for the sub montane tract revision of the then act small than the punjab land bill but affect few hundred thousand people none the less the legal member have build and bolster and embroider and amend that bill till it look beautiful on paper then the council begin to settle what they call the minor detail as if any englishman legislate for native know enough to know which be the minor and which be the major point from the native point of view of any measure that bill be triumph of safe guard the interest of the tenant one clause provide that land should not be lease on long term than five year at stretch because if the landlord have tenant bind down for say twenty year he would squeeze the very life out of he the notion be to keep up stream of independent cultivator in the sub montane tract and ethnologically and politically the notion be correct the only drawback be that it be altogether wrong native 's life in india imply the life of his son wherefore you can not legislate for one generation at time you must consider the next from the native point of view curiously enough the native now and then and in northern india more particularly hat be over protect against himself there be naga village once where they live on dead and bury commissariat mule but that be another story for many reason to be explain later the people concern object to the bill the native member in council know as much about punjabi as he know about charing cross he have say in calcutta that the bill be entirely in accord with the desire of that large and important class the cultivator and so on and so on the legal member 's knowledge of native be limit to english speak durbaris and his own red chaprassis the sub montane tract concern no one in particular the deputy commissioner be good deal too drive to make representation and the measure be one which deal with small landholder only nevertheless the legal member pray that it might be correct for he be nervously conscientious man he do not know that no man can tell what native think unless he mix with they with the varnish off and not always then
but he do the good he know and the measure come up to the supreme council for the final touch while tod patrol the burra simla bazar in his morning ride and play with the monkey belong to ditta mull the bunnia and listen as child listen to all the stray talk about this new freak of the lat sahib 's one day there be dinner party at the house of tods mamma and the legal member come tod be in bed

bear ditta mull have one daughter now but he say he will have son soon
and he say at the end of five year by this new bundobust must go if do not go must get fresh seal and takkus stamp on the paper perhaps in the middle of the harvest and to go to the law court once be wisdom but to go twice be jehannum that be quite true explain tod gravely all my friend say so and ditta mull says:--'alway fresh takkus and pay money to vakil and chaprassis and law court every five year or else the landlord make go why do want to go be fool if be fool and do not know after forty year good land when see it let die but if the new bundobust say for fifteen year then it be good and wise my little son be man and be burn
and he take the ground or another ground pay only once for the takkus stamp on the paper and his little son be bear and at the end of fifteen year be man too but what profit be there in five year and fresh paper nothing but dikh trouble dikh we be not young man who take these land but old one not jais but tradesman with little money and for fifteen year we shall have peace nor be we child that the sirkar should treat we so here tod stop short for the whole table be listen the legal member say to tod be that all all can remember say tod

whimper pinecoffin do not mind the trouble so much though that be bad enough but what resent be this show up in print it will stick to like burr all through my service and do do my good for your interminable swine it be too bad of you on my soul it be do not know say nafferton have you ever be stick with horse it be not the money mind though that be bad enough but what resent be the chaff that follow especially from the boy who stick but think we will cry quit now pinecoffin find nothing to say save bad word and nafferton smile ever so sweetly and ask he to dinner the rout of the white hussar it be not in the open fight we throw away the sword but in the lonely watching in the darkness by the ford the water lap the night wind blow full armed the fear be bear and grow and we be fly
ere we know from panic in the night beoni bar some people hold that an english cavalry regiment can not run this be mistake have see four hundred and thirty seven sabre fly over the face of the country in abject terror have see the good regiment that ever draw bridle wipe off the army list for the space of two hour if you repeat this tale to the white hussar they will in all probability treat you severely they be not proud of the incident you may know the white hussar by their side which be great than that of all the cavalry regiment on the roster if this be not sufficient mark you may know they by their old brandy it have be sixty year in the mess and be worth go far to taste ask for the mcgaire old brandy and see that you get it if the mess sergeant think that you be uneducated and that the genuine article will be lose on you he will treat you accordingly he be good man
but when you be at mess you must never talk to your host about force march or long distance ride the mess be very sensitive and if they think that you be laugh at they will tell you so as the white hussar say it be all the colonel 's fault he be new man and he ought never to have take the command he say that the regiment be not smart enough this to the white hussar who know they could walk round any horse and through any gun and over any foot on the face of the earth that insult be the first cause of offence then the colonel cast the drum horse the drum horse of the white hussar perhaps you do not see what an unspeakable crime he have commit will try to make it clear the soul of the regiment live in the drum horse who carry the silver kettle drum he be nearly always big piebald waler that be point of honor and regiment will spend anything you please on piebald he be beyond the ordinary law of cast his work be very light and he only manoeuvre at foot pace
wherefore so long as he can step out and look handsome his well be be assure he know more about the regiment than the adjutant and could not make mistake if he try the drum horse of the white hussar be only eighteen year old and perfectly equal to his duty he have at least six year more work in he and carry himself with all the pomp and dignity of drum major of the guard the regiment have pay rs 1,200 for he
but the colonel say that he must go and he be cast in due form and replace by washy bay beast as ugly as mule with ewe neck rat tail and cow hock the drummer detest that animal and the good of the band horse put back their ear and show the white of their eye at the very sight of he they know he for an upstart and no gentleman fancy that the colonel 's idea of smartness extend to the band and that he want to make it take part in the regular parade movement cavalry band be sacred thing it only turn out for command officer parade and the band master be one degree more important than the colonel he be high priest and the keel row be his holy song the keel row be the cavalry trot and the man who have never hear that tune rise high and shrill above the rattle of the regiment go past the salute base have something yet to hear and understand when the colonel cast the drum horse of the white hussar there be nearly mutiny the officer be angry the regiment be furious and the bandsman swear like trooper the drum horse be go to be put up to auction public auction to be buy perhaps by parsee and put into cart it be bad than expose the inner life of the regiment to the whole world or sell the mess plate to jew black jew the colonel be mean man and bully he know what the regiment think about his action and when the trooper offer to buy the drum horse he say that their offer be mutinous and forbid by the regulation but one of the subaltern hogan yale an irishman buy the drum horse for at the sale and the colonel be wroth yale profess repentance he be unnaturally submissive and say that as he have only make the purchase to save the horse from possible ill treatment and starvation he would now shoot he and end the business this appear to soothe the colonel for he want the drum horse dispose of he feel that he have make mistake and could not of course acknowledge it meantime the presence of the drum horse be an annoyance to he yale take to himself glass of the old brandy three cheroot and his friend martyn and they all leave the mess together yale and martyn confer for two hour in yale 's quarter but only the bull terrier who keep watch over yale 's boot tree know what they say horse hood and sheet to his ear leave yale 's stable and be take very unwillingly into the civil line yale 's groom go with he two man break into the regimental theatre and take several paint pot and some large scenery brush then night fall over the cantonment and there be noise as of horse kick his loose box to piece in yale 's stable yale have big old white waler trap horse the next day be thursday and the man hear that yale be go to shoot the drum horse in the evening determine to give the beast regular regimental funeral fine one than they would have give the colonel have he die just then they get bullock cart and some sacking and mound and mound of rose and the body under sack be carry out to the place where the anthrax case be cremate two third of the regiment follow there be no band but they all sing the place where the old horse die as something respectful and appropriate to the occasion when the corpse be dump into the grave and the man begin throw down armful of rise to cover it the farrier sergeant rip out an oath and say aloud:--“why it be not the drum horse any more than it be the troop sergeant major ask he whether he have leave his head in the canteen the farrier sergeant say that he know the drum horse 's foot as well as he know his own but he be silence when he see the regimental number burn in on the poor stiff upturne near fore thus be the drum horse of the white hussar bury the farrier sergeant grumble the sacking that cover the corpse be smear in place with black paint and the farrier sergeant draw attention to this fact but the troop sergeant major of troop kick he severely on the shin and tell he that he be undoubtedly drunk on the monday follow the burial the colonel seek revenge on the white hussar unfortunately be at that time temporarily in command of the station he order brigade field day he say that he wish to make the regiment sweat for their damned insolence and he carry out his notion thoroughly that monday be one of the hard day in the memory of the white hussar they be throw against skeleton enemy and push forward and withdraw and dismount and scientifically handle in every possible fashion over dusty country till they sweat profusely their only amusement come late in the day when they fall upon the battery of horse artillery and chase it for two mile 's this be personal question and most of the trooper have money on the event the gunner say openly that they have the leg of the white hussar they be wrong march past conclude the campaign and when the regiment get back to their line the man be coat with dirt from spur to chin strap the white hussar have one great and peculiar privilege they win it at fontenoy think many regiment possess special right such as wear collar with undress uniform or bow of ribbon between the shoulder or red and white rise in their helmet on certain day of the year some right be connect with regimental saint and some with regimental success all be value highly but none so highly as the right of the white hussar to have the band play when their horse be be water in the line only one tune be play and that tune never vary do not know its real name but the white hussar call it:--“take to london again it sound be very pretty the regiment would soon be strike off the roster than forego their distinction after the dismiss be sound the officer ride off home to prepare for stable and the man file into the line rid easy that be to say they open their tight button shift their helmet and begin to joke or to swear as the humor take they the more careful slip off and ease girth and curb good trooper value his mount exactly as much as he value himself and believe or should believe that the two together be irresistible where woman or man girl 's or gun 's be concern then the orderly officer give the order:--“water horse and the regiment loaf off to the squadron trough which be in rear of the stable and between these and the barrack there be four huge trough one for each squadron arrange en echelon so that the whole regiment could water in ten minute if it like but it linger for seventeen as rule while the band play the band strike up as the squadron file off the trough and the man slip their foot out of the stirrup and chaff each other the sun be just set in big hot bed of red cloud and the road to the civil line seem to run straight into the sun 's eye there be little dot on the road it grow and grow till it show as horse with sort of gridiron thing on his back the red cloud glare through the bar of the gridiron some of the trooper shade their eye with their hand and said:--“what the mischief as that there orse get on in another minute they hear neigh that every soul horse and man in the regiment know and see head straight towards the band the dead drum horse of the white hussar on his wither bang and bump the kettle drum drape in crape and on his back very stiff and soldierly sit bare head skeleton the band stop play and for moment there be hush then some one in troop man say it be the troop sergeant major swing his horse round and yell no one can account exactly for what happen afterwards but it seem that at least one man in each troop set an example of panic and the rest follow like sheep the horse that have barely put their muzzle into the trough 's rear and capered but as soon as the band break which it do when the ghost of the drum horse be about furlong distant all hoof follow suit and the clatter of the stampede quite different from the orderly throb and roar of movement on parade or the rough horse play of watering in camp make they only more terrified they feel that the man on their back be afraid of something when horse once know that all be over except the butchery troop after troop turn from the trough and run anywhere and everywhere like spit quicksilver it be most extraordinary spectacle for man and horse be in all stage of easiness and the carbine bucket flop against their side urge the horse on man be shout and curse and try to pull clear of the band which be be chase by the drum horse whose rider have fall forward and seem to be spur for wager the colonel have go over to the mess for drink most of the officer be with he and the subaltern of the day be prepare to go down to the line and receive the watering report from the troop sergeant major when take to london again stop after twenty bar every one in the mess said:--“what on earth have happen minute later they hear unmilitary noise and see far across the plain the white hussar scatter and break and fly the colonel be speechless with rage for he think that the regiment have rise against he or be unanimously drunk the band disorganized mob tear past and at it 's heel labor the drum horse the dead and bury drum horse with the jolting clatter skeleton hogan yale whisper softly to martyn:--“no wire will stand that treatment and the band which have double like hare come back again but the rest of the regiment be go be riot all over the province for the dusk have shut in and each man be howl to his neighbor that the drum horse be on his flank troop horse be far too tenderly treat as rule they can on emergency do great deal even with seventeen stone on their back as the trooper find out how long this panic last can not say believe that when the moon rise the man see they have nothing to fear and by two and three and half troop creep back into cantonment very much ashamed of themselves meantime the drum horse disgust at his treatment by old friend pull up wheel round and trot up to the mess verandah step for bread no one like to run but no one care to go forward till the colonel make movement and lay hold of the skeleton 's foot the band have halt some distance away and now come back slowly the colonel call it individually and collectively every evil name that occur to he at the time for he have set his hand on the bosom of the drum horse and find flesh and blood then he beat the kettle drum with his clenched fist and discover that they be but make of silvered paper and bamboo next still swear he try to drag the skeleton out of the saddle but find that it have be wire into the cantle the sight of the colonel with his arm round the skeleton 's pelvi and his knee in the old drum horse 's stomach be strike not to say amusing he worry the thing off in minute or two and throw it down on the ground say to the band:--“here
you cur that be what you be afraid of the skeleton do not look pretty in the twilight the band sergeant seem to recognize it for he begin to chuckle and choke shall take it away sir say the band sergeant yes say the colonel take it to hell and ride there yourself the band sergeant salute hoist the skeleton across his saddle bow and lead off to the stable then the colonel begin to make inquiry for the rest of the regiment and the language he use be wonderful he would disband the regiment he would court martial every soul in it he would not command such set of rabble and so on and so on as the man drop in his language grow wild until at last it exceed the utmost limit of free speech allow even to colonel of horse martyn take hogan yale aside and suggest compulsory retirement from the service as necessity when all be discover martyn be the weak man of the two hogan yale put up his eyebrow and remark firstly that he be the son of lord and secondly that he be as innocent as the babe unborn of the theatrical resurrection of the drum horse my instruction say yale with singularly sweet smile be that the drum horse should be send back as impressively as possible ask you be responsible if mule head friend send he back in such manner as to disturb the peace of mind of regiment of her majesty 's cavalry
martyn said:--“you be great man and will in time become general but would give my chance of troop to be safe out of this affair providence save martyn and hogan yale the second in command lead the colonel away to the little curtained alcove wherein the subaltern of the white hussar be accustom to play poker of night and there after many oath on the colonel 's part they talk together in low tone fancy that the second in command must have represent the scare as the work of some trooper whom it would be hopeless to detect and know that he dwell upon the sin and the shame of make public laughingstock of the scare they will call we say the second in command who have really fine imagination they will call we the fly by night they will call we the ghost hunter they will nickname we from one end of the army list to the other all the explanation in the world will not make outsider understand that the officer be away when the panic begin for the honor of the regiment and for your own sake keep this thing quiet the colonel be so exhausted with anger that soothe he down be not so difficult as might be imagine he be make to see gently and by degree that it be obviously impossible to court martial the whole regiment and equally impossible to proceed against any subaltern who in his belief have any concern in the hoax but the beast 's alive he be never be shoot at all shout the colonel it be flat flagrant disobedience have know man break for less d----d sight less they be mock tell you mutman they be mock once more the second in command set himself to sooth the colonel and wrestle with he for half an hour at the end of that time the regimental sergeant major report himself the situation be rather novel tell to he but he be not man to be put out by circumstance he salute and say regiment all come back sir then to propitiate the colonel:--“an none of the horse any the bad sir the colonel only snort and answered:--“you'd well tuck the man into their cot then and see that they do not wake up and cry in the night the sergeant withdraw his little stroke of humor please the colonel
and far he feel slightly ashamed of the language he have be use the second in command worry he again and the two sit talk far into the night next day but one there be command officer 's parade and the colonel harangue the white hussar vigorously the pith of his speech be that since the drum horse in his old age have prove himself capable of cut up the whole regiment he should return to his post of pride at the head of the band but the regiment be set of ruffian with bad conscience the white hussar shout and throw everything movable about they into the air and when the parade be over they cheer his resurrection he do not take kindly to the skeleton at all the bronckhorst divorce case in the daytime when she move about in the night when she be sleep at my side,-- be weary be weary of her presence day by day and night by night grow to hate her-- would to god that
she or have die confession there be man call bronckhorst three cornered middle aged man in the army gray as badger and some people say with touch of country blood in he that however can not be prove mrs bronckhorst be not exactly young though fifteen year young than her husband she be large pale quiet woman with heavy eyelid over weak eye and hair that turn red or yellow as the light fall on it bronckhorst be not nice in any way he have no respect for the pretty public and private lie that make life little less nasty than it be his manner towards his wife be coarse there be many thing include actual assault with the clenched fist that wife will endure but seldom wife can bear as mrs bronckhorst bear with long course of brutal hard chaff make light of her weakness her headache her small fit of gayety her dress her queer little attempt to make herself attractive to her husband when she know that she be not what she have be and bad of all the love that she spend on her child that particular sort of heavy handed jest be specially dear to bronckhorst suppose that he have first slip into it mean no harm in the honeymoon when folk find their ordinary stock of endearment run short and so go to the other extreme to express their feeling similar impulse make be man say:--“hutt you old beast when favorite horse nuzzle his coat front unluckily when the reaction of marriage set in the form of speech remain and the tenderness having die out hurt the wife more than she care to say
but mrs bronckhorst be devoted to her teddy as she call he perhaps that be why he object to she perhaps this be only theory to account for his infamous behavior later on he give way to the queer savage feeling that sometimes take by the throat husband twenty year marry when he see across the table the same face of his we wife and know that as he have sit face it
so must he continue to sit until day of its death or his own most man and all woman know the spasm it only last for three breath as rule must be throw back to time when man and woman be rather bad than they be now and be too unpleasant to be discuss dinner at the bronckhorst be be an infliction few man care to undergo bronckhorst take pleasure in say thing that make his wife wince when their little boy come in at dessert bronckhorst use to give he half glass of wine and naturally enough the poor little mite get first riotous next miserable and be remove scream bronckhorst ask if that be the way teddy usually behave and whether mrs bronckhorst could not spare some of her time to teach the little beggar decency mrs bronckhorst who love the boy more than her own life try not to cry her spirit seem to have be break by her marriage lastly bronckhorst use to say:--“there that will do that will do for god 's sake try to behave like rational woman go into the drawing room mrs bronckhorst would go try to carry it all off with smile and the guest of the evening would feel angry and uncomfortable after three year of this cheerful life for mrs bronckhorst have no woman friend to talk to the station be startle by the news that bronckhorst have institute proceed on the criminal count against man call biel who certainly have be rather attentive to mrs
bronckhorst whenever she have appear in public the utter want of reserve with which bronckhorst treat his own dishonor help we to know that the evidence against biel would be entirely circumstantial and native there be no letter but bronckhorst say openly that he would rack heaven and earth until he see biel superintend the manufacture of carpet in the central jail
mrs bronckhorst keep entirely to her house and let charitable folk say what they please opinion be divide some two third of the station jump at once to the conclusion that biel be guilty but dozen man who know and like he hold by he biel be furious and surprise he deny the whole thing and vow that he would thrash bronckhorst within an inch of his life no jury we know could convict man on the criminal count on native evidence in land where you can buy murder charge include the corpse all complete for fifty four rupee but biel do not care to scrape through by the benefit of doubt he want the whole thing clear but as he say one night:--“he can prove anything with servant evidence and have only my bare word this be about month before the case come on and beyond agree with biel we could do little all that we could be sure of be that the native evidence would be bad enough to blast biel 's character for the rest of his service for when native begin perjury he perjure himself thoroughly he do not boggle over detail some genius at the end of the table whereat the affair be be talk over said:--“look here do not believe lawyer be any good get man to wire to strickland and beg he to come down and pull we through strickland be about hundred and eighty mile up the line he have not long be married to miss youghal but he scent in the telegram chance of return to the old detective work that his soul lust after and next night he come in and hear our story he finish his pipe and say oracularly:--“we must get at the evidence oorya bearer mussalman khit and methraniayah suppose be the pillar of the charge be on in this piece but be afraid be get rusty in my talk he rise and go into biel 's bedroom where his trunk have be put and shut the door an hour later we hear he say:--“i have not the heart to part with my old makeup when marry will this do there be lothely faquir salaam in the doorway now lend fifty rupee say strickland and give your word of honor that you will not tell my wife he get all that he ask for and leave the house while the table drink his health what he do only he himself know faquir hang about bronckhorst 's compound for twelve day then mehter appear and when biel hear of he he say that strickland be an angel full fledge whether the mehter make love to janki mrs bronckhorst 's ayah be question which concern strickland exclusively he come back at the end of three week and say quietly:--“you speak the truth biel the whole business be put up from begin to end it almost astonish that bronckhorst beast be not fit to live there be uproar and shout and biel said:--“how be you go to prove it you can not say that you have be trespass on bronckhorst 's compound in disguise no say strickland tell your lawyer fool whoever he be to get up something strong about inherent improbability and discrepancy of evidence he will not have to speak but it will make he happy be go to run this business biel hold his tongue and the other man wait to see what would happen they trust strickland as man trust quiet man when the case come off the court be crowd strickland hang about in the verandah of the court till he meet the mohammedan khitmatgar then he murmur faquir 's blessing in his ear and ask he how his second wife do the man spin round and as he look into the eye of estreeken sahib his jaw drop you must remember that before strickland be marry he be as have tell you already power among native strickland whisper rather coarse vernacular proverb to the effect that he be abreast of all that be go on and go into the court arm with gut trainer 's whip the mohammedan be the first witness and strickland beam upon he from the back of the court the man moisten his lip with his tongue and in his abject fear of estreeken sahib the faquir go back on every detail of his evidence say he be poor man and god be his witness that he have forget every thing that bronckhorst sahib have tell he to say between his terror of strickland the judge and bronckhorst he collapse weep then begin the panic among the witness
janki the ayah leer chastely behind her veil turn gray and the bearer leave the court he say that his mamma be die and that it be not wholesome for any man to lie unthriftily in the presence of estreeken sahib biel say politely to bronckhorst:--“your witness do not seem to work have not you any forge letter to produce but bronckhorst be sway to and fro in his chair and there be dead pause after biel have be call to order bronckhorst 's counsel see the look on his client 's face and without more ado pitch his paper on the little green baize table and mumble something about having be misinform the whole court applaud wildly like soldier at theatre and the judge begin to say what he think biel come out of the place and strickland drop gut trainer 's whip in the verandah ten minute later biel be cut bronckhorst into ribbon behind the old court cell quietly and without scandal what be leave of bronckhorst be send home in carriage and his wife weep over it and nurse it into man again later on after biel have manage to hush up the counter charge against bronckhorst of fabricate false evidence
mrs bronckhorst with her faint watery smile say that there have be mistake but it be not her teddy 's fault altogether she would wait till her teddy come back to she perhaps he have grow tired of she or she have try his patience and perhaps we would not cut she any more and perhaps the mother would let their child play with little teddy again he be so lonely then the station invite mrs bronckhorst
