so he have tell shere khan be the tiger who live near the waingunga river twenty mile away he have no right father wolf begin angrily--“by the law of the jungle he have no right to change his quarter without due warn he will frighten every head of game within ten mile and have to kill for two these day his mother do not call he lungri the lame one for nothing say mother wolf quietly he have be lame in one foot from his birth that be why he have only kill cattle now the villager of the waingunga be angry with he and he have come here to make our villager angry they will scour the jungle for he when he be far away
and we and our child must run when the grass be set alight indeed we be very grateful to shere khan shall tell he of your gratitude say tabaqui out snap father wolf out and hunt with thy master thou hast do harm enough for one night
go say tabaqui quietly ye can hear shere khan below in the thicket might have save myself the message father wolf listen and below in the valley that run down to little river he hear the dry angry snarly singsong whine of tiger who have catch nothing and do not care if all the jungle know it the fool say father wolf to begin night ’s work with that noise do he think that our buck be like his fat waingunga bullock h’sh it be neither bullock nor buck he hunt to night say mother wolf it be man the whine have change to sort of hum purr that seem to come from every quarter of the compass it be the noise that bewilder woodcutter and gypsy sleep in the open and make they run sometimes into the very mouth of the tiger man say father wolf show all his white tooth be there not enough beetle and frog in the tank that he must eat man and on our ground too the law of the jungle which never order anything without reason forbid every beast to eat man except when he be kill to show his child how to kill
and then he must hunt outside the hunting ground of his pack or tribe the real reason for this be that man kill mean soon or later the arrival of white man on elephant with gun and hundred of brown man with gong and rocket and torch then everybody in the jungle suffer the reason the beast give among themselves be that man be the weak and most defenseless of all live thing and it be unsportsmanlike to touch he they say too and it be true that man eater become mangy and lose their tooth the purr grow louder and end in the full throate aaarh of the tiger ’s charge then there be howl an untigerish howl from shere khan he have miss say mother wolf what be it father wolf run out few pace and hear shere khan mutter and mumble savagely as he tumble about in the scrub the fool have have no more sense than to jump at woodcutter ’s campfire and have burn his foot say father wolf with grunt tabaqui be with he something be come uphill say mother wolf twitch one ear get ready the bush rustle little in the thicket and father wolf drop with his haunch under he ready for his leap then if you have be watch you would have see the most wonderful thing in the world the wolf check in mid spring he make his bind before he see what it be he be jump at and then he try to stop himself the result be that he shoot up straight into the air for four or five foot land almost where he leave ground he snap man ’s cub look directly in front of he hold on by low branch stand naked brown baby who could just walk as soft and as dimpled little atom as ever come to wolf ’s cave at night he look up into father wolf ’s face and laugh be that man ’s cub say mother wolf have never see one bring it here wolf accustomed to move his own cub can if necessary mouth an egg without break it and though father wolf ’s jaw close right on the child ’s back not tooth even scratch the skin as he lie it down among the cub how little how naked and how bold say mother wolf softly the baby be push his way between the cub to get close to the warm hide he be take his meal with the other and so this be man ’s cub now be there ever wolf that could boast of man ’s cub among her child have hear now and again of such thing but never in our pack or in my time say father wolf he be altogether without hair and could kill he with touch of my foot but see he look up and be not afraid the moonlight be block out of the mouth of the cave for shere khan ’s great square head and shoulder be thrust into the entrance tabaqui behind he be squeak my lord my lord it go in here
shere khan do we great honor say father wolf but his eye be very angry what do shere khan need my quarry man ’s cub go this way say shere khan its parent have run off give it to shere khan have jump at woodcutter ’s campfire as father wolf have say and be furious from the pain of his burn foot but father wolf know that the mouth of the cave be too narrow for tiger to come in by even where he be shere khan ’s shoulder and forepaw be cramp for want of room as man ’s would be if he try to fight in barrel the wolf be free people say father wolf they take order from the head of the pack and not from any striped cattle killer the man ’s cub be our to kill if we choose ye choose and ye do not choose what talk be this of choose by the bull that kill be to stand nose into your dog ’s den for my fair due it be shere khan who speak the tiger ’s roar fill the cave with thunder mother wolf shake herself clear of the cub and spring forward her eye like two green moon in the darkness face the blaze eye of shere khan and it be raksha the demon who answer the man ’s cub be mine lungri mine to he shall not be kill he shall live to run with the pack and to hunt with the pack and in the end
look you hunter of little naked cub frog eater fish killer he shall hunt thee now get hence or by the sambhur that kill eat no starve cattle back thou goest to thy mother burn beast of the jungle lamer than ever thou camest into the world father wolf look on amazed he have almost forget the day when he win mother wolf in fair fight from five other wolf when she run in the pack and be not call the demon for compliment ’s sake shere khan might have face father wolf
but he could not stand up against mother wolf for he know that where he be she have all the advantage of the ground and would fight to the death so he back out of the cave mouth growl and when he be clear he shout each dog bark in his own yard we will see what the pack will say to this fostering of man cub the cub be mine and to my tooth he will come in the end bush tail thief mother wolf throw herself down pant among the cub and father wolf say to she gravely shere khan speak this much truth the cub must be show to the pack wilt thou still keep he mother keep he she gasp he come naked by night alone and very hungry yet he be not afraid look he have push one of my babe to one side already and that lame butcher would have kill he and would have run off to the waingunga while the villager here hunt through all our lair in revenge keep he assuredly will keep he lie still little frog thou mowgli for mowgli the frog will call thee the time will come when thou wilt hunt shere khan as he have hunt thee but what will our pack say say father wolf the law of the jungle lie down very clearly that any wolf may when he marry withdraw from the pack he belong to but as soon as his cub be old enough to stand on their foot he must bring they to the pack council which be generally hold once month at full moon in order that the other wolf may identify they after that inspection the cub be free to run where they please and until they have kill their first buck no excuse be accept if grow wolf of the pack kill one of they the punishment be death where the murderer can be find and if you think for minute you will see that this must be so father wolf wait till his cub could run little and then on the night of the pack meeting take they and mowgli and mother wolf to the council rock hilltop cover with stone and boulder where hundred wolf could hide akela the great gray lone wolf who lead all the pack by strength and cunning lie out at full length on his rock and below he sit forty or more wolf of every size and color from badger color veteran who could handle buck alone to young black three year old who think they could the lone wolf have lead they for year now he have fall twice into wolf trap in his youth and once he have be beat and leave for dead
so he know the manner and custom of man there be very little talk at the rock the cub tumble over each other in the center of the circle where their mother and father sit and now and again senior wolf would go quietly up to cub look at he carefully and return to his place on noiseless foot sometimes mother would push her cub far out into the moonlight to be sure that he have not be overlook akela from his rock would cry ye know the law ye know the law look well wolf and the anxious mother would take up the call look look well wolf at last and mother wolf ’s neck bristle lift as the time come father wolf push mowgli the frog as they call he into the center where he sit laugh and play with some pebble that glisten in the moonlight akela never raise his head from his paw but go on with the monotonous cry look well muffled roar come up from behind the rock the voice of shere khan cry the cub be mine give he to what have the free people to do with man ’s cub akela never even twitch his ear all he say be look well wolf what have the free people to do with the order of any save the free people look well there be chorus of deep growl and young wolf in his fourth year fling back shere khan ’s question to akela what have the free people to do with man ’s cub now the law of the jungle lie down that if there be any dispute as to the right of cub to be accept by the pack he must be speak for by at least two member of the pack who be not his father and mother who speak for this cub say akela among the free people who speak there be no answer and mother wolf get ready for what she know would be her last fight if thing come to fight then the only other creature who be allow at the pack council baloo the sleepy brown bear who teach the wolf cub the law of the jungle old baloo who can come and go where he please because he eat only nut and root and honey rise upon his hind quarter and grunt the man ’s cub the man ’s cub he say speak for the man ’s cub there be no harm in man ’s cub have no gift of word but speak the truth let he run with the pack and be enter with the other myself will teach he we need yet another say akela baloo have speak and he be our teacher for the young cub who speak besides baloo black shadow drop down into the circle it be bagheera the black panther inky black all over but with the panther marking show up in certain light like the pattern of water silk everybody know bagheera and nobody care to cross his path for he be as cunning as tabaqui as bold as the wild buffalo and as reckless as the wound elephant but he have voice as soft as wild honey drip from tree and skin soft than down akela and ye the free people he purr have no right in your assembly but the law of the jungle say that if there be doubt which be not kill matter in regard to new cub the life of that cub may be buy at price and the law do not say who may or may not pay that price be right good
good say the young wolf who be always hungry listen to bagheera the cub can be buy for price it be the law know that have no right to speak here ask your leave speak then cry twenty voice to kill naked cub be shame besides he may make well sport for you when he be grow baloo have speak in his behalf now to baloo ’s word will add one bull and fat one newly kill not half mile from here if ye will accept the man ’s cub accord to the law be it difficult there be clamor of score of voice say what matter he will die in the winter rain he will scorch in the sun what harm can naked frog do we let he run with the pack where be the bull bagheera let he be accept and then come akela ’s deep bay cry look well look
well wolf mowgli be still deeply interested in the pebble and he do not notice when the wolf come and look at he one by one at last they all go down the hill for the dead bull and only akela bagheera baloo and mowgli ’s own wolf be leave shere khan roar still in the night for he be very angry that mowgli have not be hand over to he
ay roar well say bagheera under his whisker for the time will come when this naked thing will make thee roar to another tune or know nothing of man it be well do say akela man and their cub be very wise he may be help in time truly help in time of need for none can hope to lead the pack forever say bagheera akela say nothing he be think of the time that come to every leader of every pack when his strength go from he and he get feeble and feeble till at last he be kill by the wolf and new leader come up to be kill in his turn take he away he say to father wolf and train he as befit one of the free people and that be how mowgli be enter into the seeonee wolf pack for the price of bull and on baloo ’s good word now you must be content to skip ten or eleven whole year and only guess at all the wonderful life that mowgli lead among the wolf because if it be write out it would fill ever so many book he grow up with the cub though they of course be grow wolf almost before he be child and father wolf teach he his business and the meaning of thing in the jungle till every rustle in the grass every breath of the warm night air every note of the owl above his head every scratch of bat ’s claws as it roost for while in tree and every splash of every little fish jump in pool mean just as much to he as the work of his office mean to business man when he be not learn he sit out in the sun and sleep and eat and go to sleep again when he feel dirty or hot he swim in the forest pool and when he want honey baloo tell he that honey and nut be just as pleasant to eat as raw meat he climb up for it and that bagheera show he how to do bagheera would lie out on branch and call come along little brother and at first mowgli would cling like the sloth but afterward he would fling himself through the branch almost as boldly as the gray ape he take his place at the council rock too when the pack meet and there he discover that if he star hard at any wolf the wolf would be force to drop his eye
and so he use to stare for fun at other time he would pick the long thorn out of the pad of his friend for wolf suffer terribly from thorn and bur in their coat he would go down the hillside into the cultivate land by night and look very curiously at the villager in their hut but he have mistrust of man because bagheera show he square box with drop gate so cunningly hide in the jungle that he nearly walk into it and tell he that it be trap he love well than anything else to go with bagheera into the dark warm heart of the forest to sleep all through the drowsy day and at night see how bagheera do his killing bagheera kill right and leave as he feel hungry and so do mowgli with one exception as soon as he be old enough to understand thing bagheera tell he that he must never touch cattle because he have be buy into the pack at the price of bull ’s life all the jungle be thine say bagheera and thou canst kill everything that thou art strong enough to kill but for the sake of the bull that buy thee thou must never kill or eat any cattle young or old that be the law of the jungle mowgli obey faithfully and he grow and grow strong as boy must grow who do not know that he be learn any lesson and who have nothing in the world to think of except thing to eat mother wolf tell he once or twice that shere khan be not creature to be trust and that some day he must kill shere khan but though young wolf would have remember that advice every hour mowgli forget it because he be only boy though he would have call himself wolf if he have be able to speak in any human tongue shere khan be always cross his path in the jungle for as akela grow old and feebler the lame tiger have come to be great friend with the young wolf of the pack who follow he for scrap thing akela would never have allow if he have dare to push his authority to the proper bind then shere khan would flatter they and wonder that such fine young hunter be content to be lead by die wolf and man ’s cub they tell shere khan would say that at council ye dare not look he between the eye and the young wolf would growl and bristle bagheera who have eye and ear everywhere know something of this and once or twice he tell mowgli in so many word that shere khan would kill he some day mowgli would laugh and answer have the pack and have thee and baloo though he be so lazy might strike blow or two for my sake why should be afraid it be one very warm day that new notion come to bagheera bear of something that he have hear perhaps ikki the porcupine have tell he but he say to mowgli when they be deep in the jungle as the boy lie with his head on bagheera ’s beautiful black skin little brother how often have tell thee that shere khan be thy enemy as many time as there be nuts on that palm
say mowgli who naturally could not count what of it be sleepy bagheera and shere khan be all long tail and loud talk like mao the peacock but this be no time for sleep baloo know it know it the pack know it and even the foolish foolish deer know tabaqui have tell thee
too ho ho say mowgli tabaqui come to not long ago with some rude talk that be naked man ’s cub and not fit to dig pig nut but catch tabaqui by the tail and swing he twice against palm tree to teach he well manner that be foolishness for though tabaqui be mischief maker he would have tell thee of something that concern thee closely open those eye little brother shere khan dare not kill thee in the jungle
but remember akela be very old and soon the day come when he can not kill his buck
and then he will be leader no more many of the wolf that look thee over when thou wast bring to the council first be old too and the young wolf believe as shere khan have teach they that man cub have no place with the pack in little time thou wilt be man and what be man that he should not run with his brother say mowgli be bear in the jungle have obey the law of the jungle and there be no wolf of our from whose paw have not pull thorn surely they be my brother bagheera stretch himself at full length and half shut his eye little brother say he feel under my jaw mowgli put up his strong brown hand and just under bagheera ’s silky chin where the giant roll muscle be all hide by the glossy hair he come upon little bald spot there be no one in the jungle that know that bagheera carry that mark the mark of the collar and yet little brother be bear among man
and it be among man that my mother die in the cage of the king ’s palace at oodeypore it be because of this that pay the price for thee at the council when thou wast little naked cub yes too be bear among man have never see the jungle they feed behind bar from an iron pan till one night feel that be bagheera the panther and no man plaything and break the silly lock with one blow of my paw and come away and because have learn the way of man become more terrible in the jungle than shere khan be it not
so yes say mowgli all the jungle fear bagheera all except mowgli
oh thou art man ’s cub say the black panther very tenderly and even as return to my jungle so thou must go back to man at last to the man who be thy brother if thou art not kill in the council
but why but why should any wish to kill say mowgli look at say bagheera and mowgli look at he steadily between the eye the big panther turn his head away in half minute that be why he say shift his paw on the leaf not even can look thee between the eye and be bear among man and love thee little brother the other
they hate thee because their eye can not meet thine because thou art wise because thou hast pull out thorn from their foot because thou art man do not know these thing say mowgli sullenly and he frown under his heavy black eyebrow what be the law of the jungle strike first and then give tongue by thy very carelessness they know that thou art man but be wise it be in my heart that when akela miss his next kill and at each hunt it cost he more to pin the buck the pack will turn against he and against thee they will hold jungle council at the rock and then and then have it say bagheera leap up go thou down quickly to the man ’s hut in the valley and take some of the red flower which they grow there so that when the time come thou mayest have even strong friend than or baloo or those of the pack that love thee get
the red flower by red flower bagheera mean fire only no creature in the jungle will call fire by its proper name every beast live in deadly fear of it and invent hundred way of describe it the red flower say mowgli that grow outside their hut in the twilight will get some there speak the man ’s cub say bagheera proudly remember that it grow in little pot get one swiftly and keep it by thee for time of need good say mowgli but art thou sure my bagheera”--he slip his arm around the splendid neck and look deep into the big eyes--“art thou sure that all this be shere khan ’s do by the broken lock that free be sure little brother then by the bull that buy will pay shere khan full tale for this and it may be little over say mowgli
and he bind away that be man that be all man say bagheera to himself lie down again
oh shere khan never be black hunting than that frog hunt of thine
ten year ago mowgli be far and far through the forest run hard and his heart be hot in he he come to the cave as the evening mist rise and draw breath and look down the valley the cub be out but mother wolf at the back of the cave know by his breathing that something be trouble her frog what be it son she say some bat ’s chatter of shere khan he call back hunt among the plow field tonight and he plunge downward through the bush to the stream at the bottom of the valley there he check for he hear the yell of the pack hunting hear the bellow of hunt sambhur and the snort as the buck turn at bay then there be wicked bitter howl from the young wolf akela akela let the lone wolf show his strength room for the leader of the pack spring akela the lone wolf must have spring and miss his hold for mowgli hear the snap of his tooth and then yelp as the sambhur knock he over with his forefoot he do not wait for anything more but dash on and the yell grow faint behind he as he run into the cropland where the villager live bagheera speak truth he pant as he nestle down in some cattle fodder by the window of hut to morrow be one day both for akela and for then he press his face close to the window and watch the fire on the hearth he see the husbandman ’s wife get up and feed it in the night with black lump and when the morning come and the mist be all white and cold he see the man ’s child pick up wicker pot plaster inside with earth fill it with lump of red hot charcoal put it under his blanket and go out to tend the cow in the byre be that all say mowgli if cub can do it there be nothing to fear so he stride round the corner and meet the boy take the pot from his hand and disappear into the mist while the boy howl with fear they be very like say mowgli blow into the pot as he have see the woman do this thing will die if do not give it thing to eat and he drop twig and dry bark on the red stuff halfway up the hill he meet bagheera with the morning dew shin like moonstone on his coat akela have miss say the panther they would have kill he last night but they need thee also they be look for thee on the hill be among the plow land be
ready mowgli hold up the fire pot now have see man thrust dry branch into that stuff and presently the red flower blossom at the end of it art thou not afraid no why should fear remember now if it be not dream how before be wolf lie beside the red flower and it be warm and pleasant all that day mowgli sit in the cave tend his fire pot and dip dry branch into it to see how they look he find branch that satisfy he and in the evening when tabaqui come to the cave and tell he rudely enough that he be want at the council rock he laugh till tabaqui run away then mowgli go to the council still laugh akela the lone wolf lie by the side of his rock as sign that the leadership of the pack be open and shere khan with his following of scrap feed wolf walk to and fro openly be flatter bagheera lie close to mowgli and the fire pot be between mowgli ’s knee when they be all gather together shere khan begin to speak thing he would never have dare to do when akela be in his prime he have no right whisper bagheera say so he be dog ’s son he will be frighten mowgli spring to his foot free people he cry do shere khan lead the pack what have tiger to do with our leadership see that the leadership be yet open and be ask to speak-- shere khan begin by whom say mowgli be we all jackal to fawn on this cattle butcher the leadership of the pack be with the pack alone there be yell of silence thou man ’s cub let he speak he have keep our law and at last the senior of the pack thunder let the dead wolf speak when leader of the pack have miss his kill he be call the dead wolf as long as he live which be not long akela raise his old head wearily:-- free people and ye too jackal of shere khan for twelve season have lead ye to and from the kill and in all that time not one have be trap or maim now have miss
my kill ye know how that plot be make ye know how ye bring up to an untried buck to make my weakness know it be cleverly do your right be to kill here on the council rock now therefore ask who come to make an end of the lone wolf for it be my right by the law of the jungle that ye come one by one there be long hush for no single wolf care to fight akela to the death then shere khan roar bah what have we to do with this toothless fool he be doom to die it be the man cub who have live too long free people he be my meat from the first give he to be weary of this man wolf folly he have trouble the jungle for ten season give the man cub or will hunt here always and not give you one bone he be man man ’s child and from the marrow of my bone hate he then more than half the pack yell man what have man to do with we let he go to his own place and turn all the people of the village against we clamor shere khan no give he to he be man and none of we can look he between the eye akela lift his head again and say he have eat our food he have sleep with we he have drive game for we he have break no word of the law of the jungle also pay for he with bull when he be accept the worth of bull be little but bagheera ’s honor be something that he will perhaps fight for say bagheera in his gentle voice bull pay ten year ago the pack snarl what do we care for bone ten year old or for pledge say bagheera his white tooth bar under his lip well be ye call the free people no man ’s cub can run with the people of the jungle howl shere khan give he to he be our brother in all but blood akela go on and ye would kill he here in truth have live too long some of ye be eater of cattle and of other have hear that under shere khan ’s teaching ye go by dark night and snatch child from the villager ’s doorstep therefore know ye to be coward and it be to coward speak it be certain that must die and my life be of no worth or would offer that in the man cub ’s place but for the sake of the honor of the pack,--a little matter that by be without leader ye have forgotten,--i promise that if ye let the man cub go to his own place will not when my time come to die bare one tooth against ye will die without fight that will at least save the pack three life more can not do
but if ye will can save ye the shame that come of kill brother against whom there be no fault brother speak for and buy into the pack accord to the law of the jungle he be man man man snarl the pack and most of the wolf begin to gather round shere khan whose tail be begin to switch now the business be in thy hand say bagheera to mowgli we can do no more except fight mowgli stand upright the fire pot in his hand then he stretch out his arm and yawn in the face of the council but he be furious with rage and sorrow for wolflike the wolf have never tell he how they hat he listen you he cry there be no need for this dog ’s jabber ye have tell so often tonight that be man and indeed would have be wolf with you to my life ’s end that feel your word be true so do not call ye my brother any more but sag dog as man should what ye will do and what ye will not do be not yours to say that matter be with and that we may see the matter more plainly the man have bre here little of the red flower which ye dog fear he fling the fire pot on the ground and some of the red coal light tuft of dry moss that flare up as all the council draw back in terror before the leap flame mowgli thrust his dead branch into the fire till the twig light and crackle and whirl it above his head among the cower wolf thou art the master say bagheera in an undertone save akela from the death he be ever thy friend akela the grim old wolf who have never ask for mercy in his life give one piteous look at mowgli as the boy stand all naked his long black hair toss over his shoulder in the light of the blaze branch that make the shadow jump and
quiver good say mowgli star round slowly see that ye be dog go from you to my own people if they be my own people the jungle be shut to and must forget your talk and your companionship but will be more merciful than ye be because be all but your brother in blood promise that when be man among man will not betray ye to man as ye have betray he kick the fire with his foot and the spark fly up there shall be no war between any of we in the pack but here be debt to pay before go he stride forward to where shere khan sit blink stupidly at the flame and catch he by the tuft on his chin bagheera follow in case of accident up dog mowgli cry up when man speak or will set that coat
ablaze shere khan ’s ear lie flat back on his head and he shut his eye for the blaze branch be very near this cattle killer say he would kill in the council because he have not kill when be cub thus and thus then do we beat dog when we be man stir whisker lungri and ram the red flower down thy gullet he beat shere khan over the head with the branch and the tiger whimper and whine in an agony of fear sing jungle cat go now but remember when next come to the council rock as man should come it will be with shere khan ’s hide on my head for the rest akela go free to live as he please ye will not kill he because that be not my will nor do think that ye will sit here any long loll out your tongue as though ye be somebody instead of dog whom drive out thus the fire be burn furiously at the end of the branch and mowgli strike right and leave round the circle and the wolf run howl with the spark burn their fur at last there be only akela bagheera and perhaps ten wolf that have take mowgli ’s part then something begin to hurt mowgli inside he as he have never be hurt in his life before and he catch his breath and sob and the tear run down his face what be it what be it he say do not wish to leave the jungle and do not know what this be be die bagheera
no little brother that be only tear such as man use say bagheera now know thou art man and man ’s cub no long the jungle be shut indeed to thee henceforward let they fall mowgli they be only tear
so mowgli sit and cry as though his heart would break and he have never cry in all his life before now he say will go to man but first must say farewell to my mother and he go to the cave where she live with father wolf and he cry on her coat while the four cub howl miserably ye will not forget say mowgli never while we can follow trail say the cub come to the foot of the hill when thou art man and we will talk to thee and we will come into the cropland to play with thee by night come soon say father wolf
oh wise little frog come again soon for we be old thy mother and i. come soon say mother wolf little naked son of mine for listen child of man love thee more than ever love my cub will surely come say mowgli and when come it will be to lay out shere khan ’s hide upon the council rock do not forget tell they in the jungle never to forget the dawn be begin to break when mowgli go down the hillside alone to meet those mysterious thing that be call man hunting song of the seeonee pack as the dawn be break the sambhur bell once twice and again and doe leap up and doe leap up from the pond in the wood where the wild deer sup this scout alone behold once twice and again as the dawn be break the sambhur bell once twice and again and wolf steal back and wolf steal back to carry the word to the wait pack and we seek and we find
and we bay on his track once twice and again as the dawn be break the wolf pack yell once twice and again foot in the jungle that leave no mark eye that can see in the dark the dark tongue give tongue to it hark once twice and again
kaa ’s hunt his spot be the joy of the leopard his horn be the buffalo ’s pride be clean for the strength of the hunter be know by the gloss of his hide if ye find that the bullock can toss you or the heavy browed sambhur can gore ye need not stop work to inform we we know it ten season before oppress not the cub of the stranger but hail they as sister and brother for though they be little and fubsy it may be the bear be their mother
there be none like to say the cub in the pride of his early kill but the jungle be large and the cub he be small let he think and be still maxim of baloo all that be tell here happen some time before mowgli be turn out of the seeonee wolf pack or revenge himself on shere khan the tiger it be in the day when baloo be teach he the law of the jungle the big serious old brown bear be delighted to have so quick pupil for the young wolf will only learn as much of the law of the jungle as apply to their own pack and tribe and run away as soon as they can repeat the hunting verse--“feet that make no noise eye that can see in the dark ear that can hear the wind in their lair and sharp white tooth all these thing be the mark of our brother except tabaqui the jackal and the hyaena whom we hate but mowgli as man cub have to learn great deal more than this sometimes bagheera the black panther would come lounge through the jungle to see how his pet be get on and would purr with his head against tree while mowgli recite the day ’s lesson to baloo the boy could climb almost as well as he could swim and swim almost as well as he could run
so baloo the teacher of the law teach he the wood and water law how to tell rotten branch from sound one how to speak politely to the wild bee when he come upon hive of they fifty foot above ground what to say to mang the bat when he disturb he in the branch at midday and how to warn the water snake in the pool before he splash down among they none of the jungle people like be disturb and all be very ready to fly at an intruder then too
mowgli be teach the stranger hunt call which must be repeat aloud till it be answer whenever one of the jungle people hunt outside his own ground it mean translate give leave to hunt here because be hungry and the answer be hunt then for food but not for pleasure all this will show you how much mowgli have to learn by heart and he grow very tired of say the same thing over hundred time but as baloo say to bagheera one day when mowgli have be cuff and run off in temper man ’s cub be man ’s cub and he must learn all the law of the jungle but think how small he be say the black panther who would have spoil mowgli if he have have his own way how can his little head carry all thy long talk be there anything in the jungle too little to be kill that be why teach he these thing and that be why hit he very softly when he forget what dost thou know of softness old iron foot bagheera grunt his face be all bruise today by thy softness
ugh well he should be bruise from head to foot by who love he than that he should come to harm through
ignorance baloo answer very earnestly be now teach he the master word of the jungle that shall protect he with the bird and the snake people and all that hunt on four foot except his own pack he can now claim protection if he will only remember the word from all in the jungle be not that worth little beat well look to it then that thou dost not kill the man cub he be no tree trunk to sharpen thy blunt claws upon but what be those master word be more likely to give help than to ask it”--bagheera stretch out one paw and admire the steel blue rip chisel talon at the end of it--“still should like to know will call mowgli and he shall say they if he will come little brother my head be ring like bee tree say sullen little voice over their head and mowgli slide down tree trunk very angry and have teach thee this day master word for which people say mowgli delight to show off the jungle have many tongue know they all little thou know but not much see bagheera they never thank their teacher not one small wolfling have ever come back to thank old baloo for his teaching say the word for the hunt people then great scholar we be of one blood ye and say mowgli give the word the bear accent their leader some day they have no leader say bagheera they lie they have always lie they be very kind and bid come again why have never be take among the monkey people they stand on their foot as do they do not hit with their hard paw they play all day let get up bad baloo let up will play with they again listen man cub say the bear and his voice rumble like thunder on hot night have teach thee all the law of the jungle for all the people of the jungle except the monkey folk who live in the tree they have no law they be outcast they have no speech of their own but use the steal word which they overhear when they listen and peep and wait up above in the branch their way be not our way they be without leader they have no remembrance they boast and chatter and pretend that they be great people about to do great affair in the jungle but the fall of nut turn their mind to laughter and all be forget we of the jungle have no deal with they we do not drink where the monkey drink we do not go where the monkey go we do not hunt where they hunt we do not die where they die hast thou ever hear speak of the bandar log till today no say mowgli in whisper for the forest be very still now but still think baloo should have warn thee against they how be to guess he would play with such dirt the monkey people fresh shower come down on their head and the two trot away take mowgli with they what baloo have say about the monkey be perfectly true they belong to the tree top and as beast very seldom look up there be no occasion for the monkey and the jungle people to cross each other ’s path
but whenever they find sick wolf or wound tiger or bear the monkey would torment he and would throw stick and nut at any beast for fun and in the hope of be notice then they would howl and shriek senseless song and invite the jungle people to climb up their tree and fight they or would start furious battle over nothing among themselves and leave the dead monkey where the jungle people could see they they be always just go to have leader and law and custom of their own
but they never do because their memory would not hold over from day to day
and so they compromise thing by make up say what the bandar log think now the jungle will think later and that comfort they great deal none of the beast could reach they but on the other hand none of the beast would notice they and that be why they be so pleased when mowgli come to play with they and they hear how angry baloo be they never mean to do any more the bandar log never mean anything at all but one of they invent what seem to he brilliant idea
and he tell all the other that mowgli would be useful person to keep in the tribe because he could weave stick together for protection from the wind so if they catch he they could make he teach they of course mowgli as woodcutter ’s child inherit all sort of instinct and use to make little hut of fall branch without think how he come to do it the monkey people watch in the tree consider his play most wonderful this time they say they be really go to have leader and become the wise people in the jungle so wise that everyone else would notice and envy they therefore they follow baloo and bagheera and mowgli through the jungle very quietly till it be time for the midday nap and mowgli who be very much ashamed of himself sleep between the panther and the bear resolve to have no more to do with the monkey people the next thing he remember be feel hand on his leg and arm hard strong little hand and then swash of branch in his face
and then he be star down through the sway bough as baloo wake the jungle with his deep cry and bagheera bind up the trunk with every tooth bar the bandar log howl with triumph and scuffle away to the upper branch where bagheera dare not follow shout he have notice we bagheera have notice we all the jungle people admire we for our skill and our cunning then they begin their flight and the flight of the monkey people through tree land be one of the thing nobody can describe they have their regular road and crossroad up hill and down hill all lie out from fifty to seventy or hundred foot above ground and by these they can travel even at night if necessary two of the strong monkey catch mowgli under the arm and swing off with he through the treetop twenty foot at bind have they be alone they could have go twice as fast
but the boy ’s weight hold they back sick and giddy as mowgli be he could not help enjoy the wild rush though the glimpse of earth far down below frighten he and the terrible check and jerk at the end of the swing over nothing but empty air bring his heart between his tooth his escort would rush he up tree till he feel the thin topmost branch crackle and bend under they and then with cough and whoop would fling themselves into the air outward and downward and bring up hang by their hand or their foot to the low limb of the next tree sometimes he could see for mile and mile across the still green jungle as man on the top of mast can see for mile across the sea and then the branch and leaf would lash he across the face and he and his two guard would be almost down to earth again so bounding and crash and whoop and yell the whole tribe of bandar log sweep along the tree road with mowgli their prisoner for time he be afraid of be drop then he grow angry but know well than to struggle
and then he begin to think the first thing be to send back word to baloo and bagheera for at the pace the monkey be go he know his friend would be leave far behind it be useless to look down for he could only see the topside of the branch so he star upward and see far away in the blue rann the kite balancing and wheeling as he keep watch over the jungle wait for thing to die rann see that the monkey be carry something and drop few hundred yard to find out whether their load be good to eat he whistle with surprise when he see mowgli be drag up to treetop and hear he give the kite call for--“we be of one blood thou and i. the wave of the branch close over the boy but rann balance away to the next tree in time to see the little brown face come up again mark

many goat he sleep for full month after he have once eat he may be asleep now and even be he awake what if he would rather kill his own goat bagheera who do not know much about kaa be naturally suspicious then in that case thou and together old hunter might make he see reason here baloo rub his fade brown shoulder against the panther and they go off to look for kaa the rock python they find he stretch out on warm ledge in the afternoon sun admire his beautiful new coat for he have be in retirement for the last ten day change his skin and now he be very splendid dart his big blunt nosed head along the ground and twist the thirty foot of his body into fantastic knot and curve and lick his lip as he think of his dinner to come he have not eat say baloo with grunt of relief as soon as he see the beautifully mottled brown and yellow jacket be careful bagheera he be always little blind after he have change his skin and very quick to strike kaa be not poison snake in fact he rather despise the poison snake as coward but his strength lie in his hug and when he have once lap his huge coil round anybody there be no more to be say good hunting cry baloo sit up on his haunch like all snake of his breed kaa be rather deaf and do not hear the call at first then he curl up ready for any accident his head low good hunting for we all he answer
oho baloo what dost thou do here good hunting bagheera one of we at least need food be there any news of game afoot doe now or even young buck be as empty as dry well we be hunt say baloo carelessly he know that you must not hurry kaa he be too big give permission to come with you say kaa blow more or less be nothing to thee bagheera or baloo but have to wait and wait for day in wood path and climb half night on the mere chance of young ape the branch be not what they be when be young rotten twig and dry bough be they all maybe thy great weight have something to do with the matter say baloo be fair length fair length say kaa with little pride but for all that it be the fault of this new grow timber come very near to fall on my last hunt very near indeed and the noise of my slip for my tail be not tight wrap around the tree wake the bandar log

say baloo but the word stick in his throat for that be the first time in his memory that one of the jungle people have own to be interested in the doing of the monkey beyond doubt then it be no small thing that take two such hunter leader in their own jungle be certain on the trail of the bandar log kaa reply courteously as he swell with curiosity indeed baloo begin be no more than the old and sometimes very foolish teacher of the law to the seeonee wolf cub and bagheera here-- be bagheera say the black panther and his jaw shut with snap for he do not believe in be humble the trouble be this kaa those nut stealer and picker of palm leaf have steal away our man cub of whom thou hast perhaps hear hear some news from ikki his quill make he presumptuous of man thing that be enter into wolf pack but do not believe ikki be full of story half hear and very badly tell
but it be true he be such man cub as never be say baloo the good and wise and bold of man cub my own pupil who shall make the name of baloo famous through all the jungle and besides we love he kaa ts ts say kaa weave his head to and fro also have know what love be there be tale could tell that-- that need clear night when we be all well feed to praise properly say bagheera quickly our man cub be in the hand of the bandar log now and we know that of all the jungle people they fear kaa alone they fear alone they have good reason say kaa chatter foolish vain vain foolish and chatter be the monkey but man thing in their hand be in no good luck they grow tired of the nut they pick and throw they down they carry branch half day mean to do great thing with it

drive into he say bagheera but be proud of he and now we must go to the cold lair they all know where that place be but few of the jungle people ever go there because what they call the cold lair be an old deserted city lose and bury in the jungle and beast seldom use place that man have once use the wild boar will but the hunt tribe do not besides the monkey live there as much as they could be say to live anywhere and no self respect animal would come within eyeshot of it except in time of drought when the half ruin tank and reservoir hold little water will go as fast as can he say anxiously we dare not wait for thee follow baloo we must go on the quick foot kaa and i. foot or no foot can keep abreast of all thy four say kaa shortly baloo make one effort to hurry but have to sit down pant and so they leave he to come on later while bagheera hurry forward at the quick panther canter kaa say nothing but strive as bagheera might the huge rock python hold level with he when they come to hill stream bagheera gain because he bind across while kaa swim his head and two foot of his neck clear the water but on level ground kaa make up the distance by the broken lock that free say bagheera when twilight have fall thou art no slow goer be hungry say kaa besides they call speckled frog worm earth worm and yellow to boot all one let we go on and kaa seem to pour himself along the ground find the short road with his steady eye and keep to it in the cold lair the monkey people be not think of mowgli ’s friend at all they have bring the boy to the lose city and be very much pleased with themselves for the time mowgli have never see an indian city before and though this be almost heap of ruin it seem very wonderful and splendid some king have build it long ago on little hill you could still trace the stone causeway that lead up to the ruin gate where the last splinter of wood hang to the wear rust hinge tree have grow into and out of the wall the battlement be tumble down and decayed and wild creeper hang out of the window of the tower on the wall in bushy hang clump great roofless palace crown the hill and the marble of the courtyard and the fountain be split and stain with red and green and the very cobblestone in the courtyard where the king ’s elephant use to live have be thrust up and apart by grass and young tree from the palace you could see the row and row of roofless house that make up the city look like empty honeycomb fill with blackness the shapeless block of stone that have be an idol in the square where four road meet the pit and dimple at street corner where the public well once stand and the shatter dome of temple with wild fig sprout on their side the monkey call the place their city and pretend to despise the jungle people because they live in the forest and yet they never know what the building be make for nor how to use they they would sit in circle on the hall of the king ’s council chamber and scratch for flea and pretend to be man or they would run in and out of the roofless house and collect piece of plaster and old brick in corner and forget where they have hide they and fight and cry in scuffle crowd and then break off to play up and down the terrace of the king ’s garden where they would shake the rose tree and the orange in sport to see the fruit and flower fall they explore all the passage and dark tunnel in the palace and the hundred of little dark room but they never remember what they have see and what they have not and so drift about in one and two or crowd tell each other that they be do as man do they drink at the tank and make the water all muddy
and then they fight over it
and then they would all rush together in mob and shout there be no one in the jungle so wise and good and clever and strong and gentle as the bandar log then all would begin again till they grow tired of the city and go back to the tree top hop the jungle people would notice they mowgli who have be train under the law of the jungle do not like or understand this kind of life the monkey drag he into the cold lair late in the afternoon and instead of go to sleep as mowgli would have do after long journey they join hand and dance about and sing their foolish song one of the monkey make speech and tell his companion that mowgli ’s capture mark new thing in the history of the bandar log for mowgli be go to show they how to weave stick and cane together as protection against rain and cold mowgli pick up some creeper and begin to work they in and out and the monkey try to imitate but in very few minute they lose interest and begin to pull their friend tail or jump up and down on all four cough wish to eat say mowgli be strange in this part of the jungle bring food or give leave to hunt here twenty or thirty monkey bind away to bring he nut and wild pawpaw
but they fall to fight on the road and it be too much trouble to go back with what be leave of the fruit mowgli be sore and angry as well as hungry and he roam through the empty city give the stranger hunt call from time to time
but no one answer he and mowgli feel that he have reach very bad place indeed all that baloo have say about the bandar log be true he think to himself they have no law no hunting call and no leader nothing but foolish word and little pick thievish hand
so if be starve or kill here it will be all my own fault but must try to return to my own jungle baloo will surely beat but that be well than chase silly rose leaf with the bandar log
no soon have he walk to the city wall than the monkey pull he back tell he that he do not know how happy he be and pinch he to make he grateful he set his tooth and say nothing but go with the shout monkey to terrace above the red sandstone reservoir that be half full of rain water there be ruin summer house of white marble in the center of the terrace build for queen dead hundred year ago the domed roof have half fall in and block up the underground passage from the palace by which the queen use to enter but the wall be make of screen of marble tracery beautiful milk white fretwork set with agate and cornelian and jasper and lapis lazuli and as the moon come up behind the hill it shine through the open work cast shadow on the ground like black velvet embroidery sore sleepy and hungry as he be mowgli could not help laugh when the bandar log begin twenty at time to tell he how great and wise and strong and gentle they be and how foolish he be to wish to leave they we be great we be free we be wonderful we be the most wonderful people in all the jungle we all say so and so it must be true they shout now as you be new listener and can carry our word back to the jungle people so that they may notice we in future we will tell you all about our most excellent self mowgli make no objection and the monkey gather by hundred and hundred on the terrace to listen to their own speaker sing the praise of the bandar log and whenever speaker stop for want of breath they would all shout together this be true we all say so mowgli nod and blink and say yes when they ask he question and his head spin with the noise tabaqui the jackal must have bite all these people he say to himself and now they have madness certainly this be dewanee the madness do they never go to sleep now there be cloud come to cover that moon if it be only big enough cloud might try to run away in the darkness but be tired that same cloud be be watch by two good friend in the ruin ditch below the city wall for bagheera and kaa know well how dangerous the monkey people be in large number do not wish to run any risk the monkey never fight unless they be hundred to one and few in the jungle care for those odd will go to the west wall kaa whisper and come down swiftly with the slope of the ground in my favor they will not throw themselves upon my back in their hundred but-- know it say bagheera would that baloo be here but we must do what we can when that cloud cover the moon shall go to the terrace they hold some sort of council there over the boy good hunting say kaa grimly and glide away to the west wall that happen to be the least ruin of any and the big snake be delay awhile before he could find way up the stone the cloud hide the moon and as mowgli wonder what would come next he hear bagheera ’s light foot on the terrace the black panther have race up the slope almost without sound and be strike he know well than to waste time in bite right and leave among the monkey who be seat round mowgli in circle fifty and sixty deep there be howl of fright and rage and then as bagheera trip on the rolling kicking body beneath he monkey shout there be only one here kill he scuffle mass of monkey bite scratch tear and pull close over bagheera while five or six lay hold of mowgli drag he up the wall of the summerhouse and push he through the hole of the broken dome man train boy would have be badly bruise for the fall be good fifteen foot
but mowgli fall as baloo have teach he to fall and land on his foot stay there shout the monkey till we have kill thy friend and later we will play with thee if the poison people leave thee alive we be of one blood ye and say mowgli quickly give the snake ’s call he could hear rustle and hiss in the rubbish all round he and give the call second time to make sure even ssso down hood all say half dozen low voice every ruin in india become soon or later dwelling place of snake and the old summerhouse be alive with cobra stand still little brother for thy foot may do we harm mowgli stand as quietly as he could peer through the open work and listen to the furious din of the fight round the black panther the yell and chatter and scuffling and bagheera ’s deep hoarse cough as he back and buck and twisted and plunge under the heap of his enemy for the first time since he be bear bagheera be fight for his life baloo must be at hand bagheera would not have come alone mowgli think
and then he call aloud to the tank bagheera roll to the water tank roll and plunge get to the water bagheera hear and the cry that tell he mowgli be safe give he new courage he work his way desperately inch by inch straight for the reservoir halt in silence then from the ruined wall nearest the jungle rise up the rumble war shout of baloo the old bear have do his good but he could not come before bagheera he shout be here the stone slip under my foot wait my come most infamous bandar log he pant up the terrace only to disappear to the head in wave of monkey but he throw himself squarely on his haunch and spread out his forepaw hug as many as he could hold and then begin to hit with regular bat bat bat like the flip stroke of paddle wheel crash and splash tell mowgli that bagheera have fight his way to the tank where the monkey could not follow the panther lie gasp for breath his head just out of the water while the monkey stand three deep on the red step dance up and down with rage ready to spring upon he from all side if he come out to help baloo it be then that bagheera lift up his dripping chin and in despair give the snake ’s call for protection--“we be of one blood ye and i”--for he believe that kaa have turn tail at the last minute even baloo half smother under the monkey on the edge of the terrace could not help chuckle as he hear the black panther ask for help kaa have only just work his way over the west wall land with wrench that dislodge cop stone into the ditch he have no intention of lose any advantage of the ground and coil and uncoil himself once or twice to be sure that every foot of his long body be in work order all that while the fight with baloo go on and the monkey yell in the tank round bagheera and mang the bat fly to and fro carry the news of the great battle over the jungle till even hathi the wild elephant trumpet and far away scatter band of the monkey folk wake and come leap along the tree road to help their comrade in the cold lair and the noise of the fight rouse all the day bird for mile round then kaa come straight quickly and anxious to kill the fighting strength of python be in the drive blow of his head back by all the strength and weight of his body if you can imagine lance or batter ram or hammer weigh nearly half ton drive by cool quiet mind live in the handle of it you can roughly imagine what kaa be like when he fight python four or five foot long can knock man down if he hit he fairly in the chest and kaa be thirty foot long as you know his first stroke be deliver into the heart of the crowd round baloo it be send home with shut mouth in silence and there be no need of second the monkey scatter with cry of--“kaa it be kaa run run generation of monkey have be scar into good behavior by the story their elder tell they of kaa the night thief who could slip along the branch as quietly as moss grow and steal away the strong monkey that ever live of old kaa who could make himself look so like dead branch or rotten stump that the wise be deceive till the branch catch they kaa be everything that the monkey fear in the jungle for none of they know the limit of his power none of they could look he in the face and none have ever come alive out of his hug and so they run stammer with terror to the wall and the roof of the house and baloo draw deep breath of relief his fur be much thick than bagheera ’s
but he have suffer sorely in the fight then kaa open his mouth for the first time and speak one long hiss word and the far away monkey hurry to the defense of the cold lair stay where they be cower till the load branch bend and crackle under they the monkey on the wall and the empty house stop their cry and in the stillness that fall upon the city mowgli hear bagheera shake his wet side as he come up from the tank then the clamor break out again the monkey leap higher up the wall they cling around the neck of the big stone idol and shriek as they skip along the battlement while mowgli dance in the summerhouse put his eye to the screenwork and hoot owl fashion between his front tooth to show his derision and contempt get the man cub out of that trap can do no more bagheera gasp let we take the man cub and go they may attack again they will not move till order they stay you sssso kaa hiss and the city be silent once more could not come before brother but think hear thee call”--this be to bagheera may have cry out in the battle bagheera answer baloo art thou hurt be not sure that they do not pull into hundred little bearling say baloo gravely shake one leg after the other be sore kaa we owe thee think our life bagheera and i. no matter where be the manling here in trap can not climb out cry mowgli the curve of the break dome be above his head take he away he dance like mao the peacock he will crush our young say the cobra inside
hah say kaa with chuckle he have friend everywhere this manle stand back manle and hide you poison people break down the wall kaa look carefully till he find discolor crack in the marble tracery show weak spot make two or three light tap with his head to get the distance and then lift up six foot of his body clear of the ground send home half dozen full power smash blow nose first the screen work break and fall away in cloud of dust and rubbish and mowgli leap through the opening and fling himself between baloo and bagheera an arm around each big neck art thou hurt
say baloo hug he softly be sore hungry and not little bruise
but oh they have handle ye grievously my brother ye bleed other also say bagheera lick his lip and look at the monkey dead on the terrace and round the tank it be nothing it be nothing if thou art safe
oh my pride of all little frog whimper baloo of that we shall judge later say bagheera in dry voice that mowgli do not at all like but here be kaa to whom we owe the battle and thou owest thy life thank he accord to our custom mowgli mowgli turn and see the great python ’s head sway foot above his own so this be the manling say kaa very soft be his skin and he be not unlike the bandar log have care manling that do not mistake thee for monkey some twilight when have newly change my coat we be one blood thou and mowgli answer take my life from thee tonight my kill shall be thy kill if ever thou art hungry kaa all thank little brother say kaa though his eye twinkle and what may so bold hunter kill ask that may follow when next he go abroad kill nothing,--i be too little,--but drive goat toward such as can use they when thou art empty come to and see if speak the truth have some skill in these he hold out his hand and if ever thou art in trap may pay the debt which owe to thee to bagheera and to baloo here good hunting to ye all my master well say growl baloo for mowgli have return thank very prettily the python drop his head lightly for minute on mowgli ’s shoulder brave heart and courteous tongue say he they shall carry thee far through the jungle manle but now go hence quickly with thy friend go and sleep for the moon set and what follow it be not well that thou shouldst see the moon be sink behind the hill and the line of tremble monkey huddle together on the wall and battlement look like ragged shaky fringe of thing baloo go down to the tank for drink and bagheera begin to put his fur in order as kaa glide out into the center of the terrace and bring his jaw together with ring snap that draw all the monkey eye upon he the moon set he say be there yet light enough to see from the wall come moan like the wind in the tree tops--“we see kaa begin now the dance the dance of the hunger of kaa sit still and watch he turn twice or thrice in big circle weave his head from right to leave then he begin make loop and figure of eight with his body and soft oozy triangle that melt into square and five sided figure and coil mound never rest never hurry and never stop his low hum song it grow dark and dark till at last the drag shift coil disappear
but they could hear the rustle of the scale baloo and bagheera stand still as stone growl in their throat their neck hair bristle and mowgli watch and wonder bandar log say the voice of kaa at last can ye stir foot or hand without my order without thy order we can not stir foot or hand kaa come all one pace near to the line of the monkey sway forward helplessly and baloo and bagheera take one stiff step forward with they nearer hiss kaa
and they all move again mowgli lie his hand on baloo and bagheera to get they away and the two great beast start as though they have be wake from dream keep thy hand on my shoulder bagheera whisper keep it there or must go back must go back to kaa it be only old kaa make circle on the dust say mowgli let we go and the three slip off through gap in the wall to the jungle whoof say baloo when he stand under the still tree again never more will make an ally of kaa and he shake himself all over he know more than we say bagheera tremble in little time have stay should have walk down his throat many will walk by that road before the moon rise again say baloo he will have good hunting after his own fashion but what be the meaning of it all say mowgli who do not know anything of python ’s power of fascination see no more than big snake make foolish circle till the dark come and his nose be all sore ho ho mowgli say bagheera angrily his nose be sore on thy account as my ear and side and paw and baloo ’s neck and shoulder be bite on thy account neither baloo nor bagheera will be able to hunt with pleasure for many day it be nothing say baloo we have the man cub again true
but he have cost we heavily in time which might have be spend in good hunting in wound in hair be half pluck along my back and last of all in honor for remember mowgli who be the black panther be force to call upon kaa for protection and baloo and be both make stupid as little bird by the hunger dance all this man cub come of thy playing with the bandar log true it be true say mowgli sorrowfully be an evil man cub and my stomach be sad in mf what say the law of the jungle baloo baloo do not wish to bring mowgli into any more trouble
but he could not tamper with the law so he mumble sorrow never stay punishment but remember bagheera he be very little will remember
but he have do mischief and blow must be deal now mowgli hast thou anything to say nothing do wrong baloo and thou be wind it be just bagheera give he half dozen love tap from panther ’s point of view they would hardly have wake one of his own cub but for seven year old boy they amount to as severe beating as you could wish to avoid when it be all over mowgli sneeze and pick himself up without word now say bagheera jump on my back little brother and we will go home one of the beauty of jungle law be that punishment settle all score there be no nagging afterward mowgli lie his head down on bagheera ’s back and sleep so deeply that he never wake when he be put down in the home cave road song of the bandar log here we go in flung festoon half way up to the jealous moon do not you envy our pranceful band do not you wish you have extra hand would not you like if your tail be so-- curve in the shape of cupid ’s bow now you ’re angry but never mind brother thy tail hang down behind here we sit in branchy row thinking of beautiful thing we know dream of deed that we mean to do all complete in minute or two-- something noble and wise and good do by merely wish we could we ve forget but never mind brother thy tail hang down behind all the talk we ever have hear utter by bat or beast or bird-- hide or fin or scale or feather-- jabber it quickly and all together once again now we be talk just like man let ’s pretend we be never mind brother thy tail hang down behind this be the way of the monkey kind then join our leap line that scumfish through the pine that rocket by where light and high the wild grape swing by the rubbish in our wake and the noble noise we make be sure be sure we ’re go to do some splendid thing what of the hunting hunter bold brother the watch be long and cold what of the quarry ye go to kill brother he crop in the jungle still where be the power that make your pride brother it ebb from my flank and side where be the haste that ye hurry by brother go to my lair to die now we must go back to the first tale when mowgli leave the wolf ’s cave after the fight with the pack at the council rock he go down to the plow land where the villager live but he would not stop there because it be too near to the jungle
and he know that he have make at least one bad enemy at the council so he hurry on keep to the rough road that run down the valley and follow it at steady jog trot for nearly twenty mile till he come to country that he do not know the valley open out into great plain dot over with rock and cut up by ravine at one end stand little village and at the other the thick jungle come down in sweep to the graze ground and stop there as though it have be cut off with hoe all over the plain cattle and buffalo be graze and when the little boy in charge of the herd see mowgli they shout and run away and the yellow pariah dog that hang about every indian village bark mowgli walk on for he be feel hungry and when he come to the village gate he see the big thorn bush that be draw up before the gate at twilight push to one side he say for he have come across more than one such barricade in his night ramble after thing to eat
so man be afraid of the people of the jungle here also he sit down by the gate and when man come out he stand up open his mouth and point down it to show that he want food the man star and run back up the one street of the village shout for the priest who be big fat man dress in white with red and yellow mark on his forehead the priest come to the gate and with he at least hundred people who star and talk and shout and point at mowgli they have no manner these man folk say mowgli to himself only the gray ape would behave as they do so he throw back his long hair and frown at the crowd what be there to be afraid of say the priest look at the mark on his arm and leg they be the bite of wolf he be but wolf child run away from the jungle of course in play together the cub have often nip mowgli hard than they intend and there be white scar all over his arm and leg
but he would have be the last person in the world to call these bite for he know what real biting mean arre say two or three woman together to be bite by wolf poor child he be handsome boy he have eye like red fire by my honor messua he be not unlike thy boy that be take by the tiger let look say woman with heavy copper ring on her wrist and ankle
and she peer at mowgli under the palm of her hand indeed he be not he be thin
but he have the very look of my boy the priest be clever man
and he know that messua be wife to the rich villager in the place so he look up at the sky for minute and say solemnly what the jungle have take the jungle have restore take the boy into thy house my sister and forget not to honor the priest who see so far into the life of man by the bull that buy
say mowgli to himself but all this talk be like another look over by the pack
well if be man man must become the crowd part as the woman beckon mowgli to her hut where there be red lacquer bedstead great earthen grain chest with funny raise pattern on it half dozen copper cooking pot an image of hindu god in little alcove and on the wall real look glass such as they sell at the country fair she give he long drink of milk and some bread
and then she lie her hand on his head and look into his eye for she think perhaps that he might be her real son come back from the jungle where the tiger have take he
so she say nathoo nathoo mowgli do not show that he know the name dost thou not remember the day when give thee thy new shoe she touch his foot and it be almost as hard as horn
no she say sorrowfully those foot have never wear shoe but thou art very like my nathoo and thou shalt be my son mowgli be uneasy because he have never be under roof before
but as he look at the thatch he see that he could tear it out any time if he want to get away and that the window have no fastening what be the good of man he say to himself at last if he do not understand man ’s talk now be as silly and dumb as man would be with we in the jungle must speak their talk it be not for fun that he have learn while he be with the wolf to imitate the challenge of buck in the jungle and the grunt of the little wild pig so as soon as messua pronounce word mowgli would imitate it almost perfectly and before dark he have learn the name of many thing in the hut there be difficulty at bedtime because mowgli would not sleep under anything that look so like panther trap as that hut and when they shut the door he go through the window give he his will say messua ’s husband remember he can never till now have sleep on bed if he be indeed send in the place of our son he will not run away
so mowgli stretch himself in some long clean grass at the edge of the field but before he have close his eye soft gray nose poke he under the chin phew say gray brother he be the eld of mother wolf ’s cub this be poor reward for follow thee twenty mile thou smell of wood smoke and cattle altogether like man already wake little brother bring news be all well in the jungle
say mowgli hug he all except the wolf that be burn with the red flower now listen shere khan have go away to hunt far off till his coat grow again for he be badly sing when he return he swear that he will lay thy bone in the waingunga there be two word to that also have make little promise but news be always good be tired to night,--very tired with new thing gray brother,--but bring the news always thou wilt not forget that thou art wolf man will not make thee forget say gray brother anxiously will always remember that love thee and all in our cave but also will always remember that have be cast out of the pack and that thou mayest be cast out of another pack man be only man little brother and their talk be like the talk of frog in pond when come down here again will wait for thee in the bamboo at the edge of the graze ground for three month after that night mowgli hardly ever leave the village gate he be so busy learn the way and custom of man first he have to wear cloth round he which annoy he horribly
and then he have to learn about money which he do not in the least understand and about plow of which he do not see the use then the little child in the village make he very angry luckily the law of the jungle have teach he to keep his temper for in the jungle life and food depend on keep your temper
but when they make fun of he because he would not play game or fly kite or because he mispronounce some word only the knowledge that it be unsportsmanlike to kill little naked cub keep he from pick they up and break they in two he do not know his own strength in the least in the jungle he know he be weak compare with the beast but in the village people say that he be as strong as bull and mowgli have not the faint idea of the difference that caste make between man and man when the potter ’s donkey slip in the clay pit mowgli haul it out by the tail and help to stack the pot for their journey to the market at khanhiwara that be very shocking too for the potter be low caste man and his donkey be bad when the priest scold he mowgli threaten to put he on the donkey too and the priest tell messua ’s husband that mowgli have well be set to work as soon as possible and the village head man tell mowgli that he would have to go out with the buffalo next day and herd they while they graze no one be more pleased than mowgli and that night because he have be appoint servant of the village as it be he go off to circle that meet every evening on masonry platform under great fig tree it be the village club and the head man and the watchman and the barber who know all the gossip of the village and old buldeo the village hunter who have tower musket meet and smoke the monkey sit and talk in the upper branch and there be hole under the platform where cobra live and he have his little platter of milk every night because he be sacred and the old man sit around the tree and talk and pull at the big huqas the water pipe till far into the night they tell wonderful tale of god and man and ghost and buldeo tell even more wonderful one of the way of beast in the jungle till the eye of the child sit outside the circle bulge out of their head most of the tale be about animal for the jungle be always at their door the deer and the wild pig grub up their crop and now and again the tiger carry off man at twilight within sight of the village gate mowgli who naturally know something about what they be talk of have to cover his face not to show that he be laugh while buldeo the tower musket across his knee climb on from one wonderful story to another and mowgli ’s shoulder shake buldeo be explain how the tiger that have carry away messua ’s son be ghost tiger and his body be inhabit by the ghost of wicked old money lender who have die some year ago and know that this be true he say because purun dass always limp from the blow that he get in riot when his account book be burn and the tiger that speak of he limp too for the track of his pad be unequal true true that must be the truth say the gray beard nod together be all these tale such cobwebs and moontalk say mowgli that tiger limp because he be bear lame as everyone know to talk of the soul of money lender in beast that never have the courage of jackal be child ’s talk buldeo be speechless with surprise for moment and the head man star it be the jungle brat be it say buldeo if thou art so wise well bring his hide to khanhiwara for the government have set hundred rupee on his life well still talk not when thy elder speak mowgli rise to go all the evening have lie here listen he call back over his shoulder and except once or twice buldeo have not say one word of truth concern the jungle which be at his very door how then shall believe the tale of ghost and god and goblin which he say he have see it be full time that boy go to herd say the head man while buldeo puff and snort at mowgli ’s impertinence the custom of most indian village be for few boy to take the cattle and buffalo out to graze in the early morning and bring they back at night the very cattle that would trample white man to death allow themselves to be bang and bully and shout at by child that hardly come up to their nose so long as the boy keep with the herd they be safe for not even the tiger will charge mob of cattle
