but if they straggle to pick flower or hunt lizard they be sometimes carry off mowgli go through the village street in the dawn sit on the back of rama the great herd bull the slaty blue buffalo with their long backward sweep horn and savage eye rise out their byre one by one and follow he and mowgli make it very clear to the child with he that he be the master he beat the buffalo with long polished bamboo and tell kamya one of the boy to graze the cattle by themselves while he go on with the buffalo and to be very careful not to stray away from the herd an indian grazing ground be all rock and scrub and tussock and little ravine among which the herd scatter and disappear the buffalo generally keep to the pool and muddy place where they lie wallow or bask in the warm mud for hour mowgli drive they on to the edge of the plain where the waingunga come out of the jungle then he drop from rama ’s neck trot off to bamboo clump and find gray brother ah say gray brother have wait here very many day what be the meaning of this cattle herd work it be an order say mowgli be village herd for while what news of shere khan he have come back to this country and have wait here long time for thee now he have go off again for the game be scarce
but he mean to kill thee very good say mowgli so long as he be away do thou or one of the four brother sit on that rock so that can see thee as come out of the village when he come back wait for in the ravine by the dhâk tree in the center of the plain we need not walk into shere khan ’s mouth then mowgli pick out shady place and lie down and sleep while the buffalo graze round he herd in india be one of the lazy thing in the world the cattle move and crunch and lie down and move on again and they do not even low they only grunt and the buffalo very seldom say anything but get down into the muddy pool one after another and work their way into the mud till only their nose and star china blue eye show above the surface
and then they lie like log the sun make the rock dance in the heat and the herd child hear one kite never any more whistle almost out of sight overhead
and they know that if they die or cow die that kite would sweep down and the next kite mile away would see he drop and follow and the next and the next and almost before they be dead there would be score of hungry kite come out of nowhere then they sleep and wake and sleep again and weave little basket of dry grass and put grasshopper in they or catch two pray mantis and make they fight or string necklace of red and black jungle nut or watch lizard bask on rock or snake hunt frog near the wallow then they sing long long song with odd native quaver at the end of they and the day seem long than most people ’s whole life and perhaps they make mud castle with mud figure of man and horse and buffalo and put reed into the man ’s hand and pretend that they be king and the figure be their army or that they be god to be worship then evening come and the child call and the buffalo lumber up out of the sticky mud with noise like gunshot go off one after the other and they all string across the gray plain back to the twinkle village light day after day mowgli would lead the buffalo out to their wallow and day after day he would see gray brother ’s back mile and half away across the plain
so he know that shere khan have not come back and day after day he would lie on the grass listen to the noise round he and dream of old day in the jungle if shere khan have make false step with his lame paw up in the jungle by the waingunga mowgli would have hear he in those long still morning at last day come when he do not see gray brother at the signal place and he laugh and head the buffalo for the ravine by the dhk tree which be all cover with golden red flower there sit gray brother every bristle on his back lift he have hide for month to throw thee off thy guard he cross the range last night with tabaqui hot foot on thy trail say the wolf panting mowgli frown be not afraid of shere khan but tabaqui be very cunning have no fear say gray brother lick his lip little meet tabaqui in the dawn now he be tell all his wisdom to the kite
but he tell everything before break his back shere khan ’s plan be to wait for thee at the village gate this evening for thee and for no one else he be lie up now in the big dry ravine of the waingunga have he eat today or do he hunt empty say mowgli for the answer mean life and death to he he kill at dawn,--a pig,--and he have drunk too remember shere khan could never fast even for the sake of revenge
oh fool fool what cub ’s cub it be eat and drunk too
and he think that shall wait till he have sleep now where do he lie up if there be but ten of we we might pull he down as he lie these buffalo will not charge unless they wind he and can not speak their language can we get behind his track so that they may smell it he swim far down the waingunga to cut that off say gray brother tabaqui tell he that know he would never have think of it
alone mowgli stand with his finger in his mouth think the big ravine of the waingunga that open out on the plain not half mile from here can take the herd round through the jungle to the head of the ravine and then sweep down but he would slink out at the foot we must block that end gray brother canst thou cut the herd in two for not perhaps but have bre wise helper gray brother trot off and drop into hole then there lift up huge gray head that mowgli know well and the hot air be fill with the most desolate cry of all the jungle the hunting howl of wolf at midday
akela akela say mowgli clap his hand might have know that thou wouldst not forget we have big work in hand cut the herd in two akela keep the cow and calf together and the bull and the plow buffalo by themselves the two wolf run ladies’-chain fashion in and out of the herd which snort and throw up its head and separate into two clump in one the cow buffalo stand with their calf in the center and glare and paw ready if wolf would only stay still to charge down and trample the life out of he in the other the bull and the young bull snort and stamp
but though they look more impose they be much less dangerous for they have no calf to protect no six man could have divide the herd so neatly what order pant akela they be try to join again
mowgli slip on to rama ’s back drive the bull away to the left akela gray brother when we be go hold the cow together and drive they into the foot of the ravine how far say gray brother pant and snap till the side be high than shere khan can jump shout
mowgli keep they there till we come down the bull sweep off as akela bayed and gray brother stop in front of the cow they charge down on he and he run just before they to the foot of the ravine as akela drive the bull far to the left
well do another charge and they be fairly start careful now careful akela snap too much and the bull will charge this be wild work than drive black buck didst thou think these creature could move
so swiftly mowgli call have have hunt these too in my time gasp akela in the dust shall turn they into the jungle ay turn swiftly turn they rama be mad with rage
oh if could only tell he what need of he to day the bull be turn to the right this time and crash into the stand thicket the other herd child watch with the cattle half mile away hurry to the village as fast as their leg could carry they cry that the buffalo have go mad and run away
but mowgli ’s plan be simple enough all he want to do be to make big circle uphill and get at the head of the ravine and then take the bull down it and catch shere khan between the bull and the cow for he know that after meal and full drink shere khan would not be in any condition to fight or to clamber up the side of the ravine he be soothe the buffalo now by voice and akela have drop far to the rear only whimpering once or twice to hurry the rear guard it be long long circle for they do not wish to get too near the ravine and give shere khan warning at last mowgli round up the bewildered herd at the head of the ravine on grassy patch that slop steeply down to the ravine itself from that height you could see across the top of the tree down to the plain below but what mowgli look at be the side of the ravine and he see with great deal of satisfaction that they run nearly straight up and down while the vine and creeper that hang over they would give no foothold to tiger who want to get out let they breathe akela he say hold up his hand they have not wind he yet let they breathe must tell shere khan who come we have he in the trap he put his hand to his mouth and shout down the ravine it be almost like shout down tunnel and the echo jump from rock to rock after long time there come back the drawl sleepy snarl of full feed tiger just waken who call say shere khan and splendid peacock flutter up out of the ravine screech cattle thief it be time to come to the council rock down hurry they down akela down rama down the herd pause for an instant at the edge of the slope but akela give tongue in the full hunting yell
and they pitch over one after the other just as steamer shoot rapid the sand and stone spurt up round they once start there be no chance of stop and before they be fairly in the bed of the ravine rama wind shere khan and bellow
ha ha say mowgli on his back now thou know and the torrent of black horn foam muzzle and star eye whirl down the ravine just as boulder go down in floodtime the weak buffalo be shoulder out to the side of the ravine where they tear through the creeper they know what the business be before they the terrible charge of the buffalo herd against which no tiger can hope to stand shere khan hear the thunder of their hoofs pick himself up and lumber down the ravine look from side to side for some way of escape but the wall of the ravine be straight and he have to hold on heavy with his dinner and his drink willing to do anything rather than fight the herd splash through the pool he have just leave bellow till the narrow cut ring mowgli hear an answer bellow from the foot of the ravine see shere khan turn the tiger know if the bad come to the bad it be well to meet the bull than the cow with their calf and then rama trip stumble and go on again over something soft and with the bull at his heel crash full into the other herd while the weak buffalo be lift clean off their foot by the shock of the meeting that charge carry both herd out into the plain goring and stamping and snort mowgli watch his time and slip off rama ’s neck lie about he right and leave with his stick quick akela break they up scatter they or they will be fight one another drive they away akela hai rama hai hai hai my child softly now softly it be all over akela and gray brother run to and fro nip the buffalo leg and though the herd wheel once to charge up the ravine again mowgli manage to turn rama and the other follow he to the wallow shere khan need no more trampling he be dead and the kite be come for he already brother that be dog ’s death say mowgli feel for the knife he always carry in sheath round his neck now that he live with man
but he would never have show fight his hide will look well on the council rock we must get to work swiftly
boy train among man would never have dream of skin ten foot tiger alone
but mowgli know well than anyone else how an animal ’s skin be fit on and how it can be take off
but it be hard work and mowgli slash and tear and grunt for an hour while the wolf loll out their tongue or come forward and tug as he order they presently hand fall on his shoulder and look up he see buldeo with the tower musket the child have tell the village about the buffalo stampede and buldeo go out angrily only too anxious to correct mowgli for not take well care of the herd the wolf drop out of sight as soon as they see the man come what be this folly say buldeo angrily to think that thou canst skin tiger where do the buffalo kill he it be the lame tiger too and there be hundred rupee on his head
well well we will overlook thy let the herd run off and perhaps will give thee one of the rupee of the reward when have take the skin to khanhiwara he fumble in his waist cloth for flint and steel and stoop down to singe shere khan ’s whisker most native hunter always singe tiger ’s whisker to prevent his ghost from haunt they hum say mowgli half to himself as he rip back the skin of forepaw so thou wilt take the hide to khanhiwara for the reward and perhaps give one rupee now it be in my mind that need the skin for my own use old man take away that fire what talk be this to the chief hunter of the village thy luck and the stupidity of thy buffalo have help thee to this kill the tiger have just feed or he would have go twenty mile by this time thou canst not even skin he properly little beggar brat and forsooth buldeo must be tell not to singe his whisker mowgli will not give thee one anna of the reward but only very big beating leave the carcass by the bull that buy say mowgli who be try to get at the shoulder must stay babble to an old ape all noon here akela this man plague buldeo who be still stoop over shere khan ’s head find himself sprawl on the grass with gray wolf stand over he while mowgli go on skin as though he be alone in all india
ye es he say between his tooth thou art altogether right buldeo thou wilt never give one anna of the reward there be an old war between this lame tiger and myself very old war and have win to do buldeo justice if he have be ten year young he would have take his chance with akela have he meet the wolf in the wood but wolf who obey the order of this boy who have private war with man eat tiger be not common animal it be sorcery magic of the bad kind think buldeo
and he wonder whether the amulet round his neck would protect he he lie as still as still expect every minute to see mowgli turn into tiger too great king he say at last in husky whisper
yes say mowgli without turn his head chuckle little be an old man do not know that thou wast anything more than herdsboy may rise up and go away or will thy servant tear to piece go and peace go with thee only another time do not meddle with my game
let he go akela buldeo hobble away to the village as fast as he could look back over his shoulder in case mowgli should change into something terrible when he get to the village he tell tale of magic and enchantment and sorcery that make the priest look
very grave mowgli go on with his work but it be nearly twilight before he and the wolf have draw the great gay skin clear of the body now we must hide this and take the buffalo home help to herd they akela the herd round up in the misty twilight and when they get near the village mowgli see light and hear the conch and bell in the temple blow and bang half the village seem to be wait for he by the gate that be because have kill shere khan he say to himself but shower of stone whistle about his ear and the villager shout sorcerer wolf ’s brat jungle demon go away get hence quickly or the priest will turn thee into wolf again shoot buldeo shoot the old tower musket go off with bang and young buffalo bellow in pain more sorcery shout the villager he can turn bullet buldeo that be thy buffalo now what be this say mowgli bewilder as the stone fly thick they be not unlike the pack these brother of thine
say akela sit down composedly it be in my head that if bullet mean anything they would cast thee out wolf wolf ’s cub go away shout the priest wave sprig of the sacred tulsi plant last time it be because be man this time it be because be wolf let we go akela woman it be messua run across to the herd and cry
oh my son my son they say thou art sorcerer who can turn himself into beast at will do not believe but go away or they will kill thee buldeo say thou art wizard but know thou hast avenge nathoo ’s death come back messua shout the crowd come back or we will stone thee mowgli laugh little short ugly laugh for stone have hit he in the mouth run back messua this be one of the foolish tale they tell under the big tree at dusk have at least pay for thy son ’s life farewell and run quickly for shall send the herd in more swiftly than their brickbat be no wizard messua now once more akela he cry bring the herd in the buffalo be anxious enough to get to the village they hardly need akela ’s yell but charge through the gate like whirlwind scatter the crowd right and leave keep count shout mowgli scornfully it may be that have steal one of they keep count for will do your herd no more fare you well child of man and thank messua that do not come in with my wolf and hunt you up and down your street he turn on his heel and walk away with the lone wolf and as he look up at the star he feel happy no more sleeping in trap for akela let we get shere khan ’s skin and go away
no we will not hurt the village for messua be kind to when the moon rise over the plain make it look all milky the horrify villager see mowgli with two wolf at his heel and bundle on his head trot across at the steady wolf ’s trot that eat up the long mile like fire then they bang the temple bell and blow the conch louder than ever and messua cry and buldeo embroider the story of his adventure in the jungle till he end by say that akela stand up on his hind leg and talk like man the moon be just go down when mowgli and the two wolf come to the hill of the council rock and they stop at mother wolf ’s cave they have cast out from the man pack mother shout mowgli but come with the hide of shere khan to keep my word mother wolf walk stiffly from the cave with the cub behind she and her eye glow as she see the skin tell he on that day when he cram his head and shoulder into this cave hunt for thy life little frog tell he that the hunter would be the hunt it be well do little brother it be well do say deep voice in the thicket we be lonely in the jungle without thee and bagheera come run to mowgli ’s bare foot they clamber up the council rock together and mowgli spread the skin out on the flat stone where akela use to sit and peg it down with four sliver of bamboo and akela lie down upon it and call the old call to the council look look well wolf exactly as he have call when mowgli be first bring there ever since akela have be depose the pack have be without leader hunting and fight at their own pleasure
but they answer the call from habit and some of they be lame from the trap they have fall into and some limp from shot wound and some be mangy from eat bad food and many be miss
but they come to the council rock all that be leave of they and see shere khan ’s striped hide on the rock and the huge claw dangle at the end of the empty dangle foot it be then that mowgli make up song that come up into his throat all by itself and he shout it aloud leap up and down on the rattle skin and beat time with his heel till he have no more breath leave while gray brother and akela howl between the verse look well wolf have keep my word say mowgli and the wolf bay yes and one tattered wolf howl lead we again akela lead we again man cub for we be sick of this lawlessness and we would be the free people once more nay purr bagheera that may not be when ye be full feed the madness may come upon you again not for nothing be ye call the free people ye fight for freedom and it be yours eat it wolf man pack and wolf pack have cast out say mowgli now will hunt alone in the jungle and we will hunt with thee say the four cub
so mowgli go away and hunt with the four cub in the jungle from that day on but he be not always alone because year afterward he become man and marry but that be story for grow up mowgli ’s song that he sing at the council rock when he dance on shere khan ’s hide the song of mowgli mowgli be sing let the jungle listen to the thing have do shere khan say he would kill would kill at the gate in the twilight he would kill mowgli the frog he eat and he drink drink deep shere khan for when wilt thou drink again sleep and dream of the kill be alone on the graze ground gray brother come to come to lone wolf for there be big game afoot bring up the great bull buffalo the blue skin herd bull with the angry eye drive they to and fro as order sleepest thou still shere khan wake
oh wake here come and the bull be behind rama the king of the buffalo stamp with his foot water of the waingunga whither go shere khan he be not ikki to dig hole nor mao the peacock that he should fly he be not mang the bat to hang in the branch little bamboo that creak together tell where he run he be there he be there under the foot of rama lie the lame one shere khan up and kill here be meat break the neck of the bull hsh he be asleep we will not wake he for his strength be very great the kite have come down to see it the black ant have come up to know it there be great assembly in his honor have no cloth to wrap the kite will see that be naked be ashamed to meet all these people lend thy coat shere khan lend thy gay striped coat that may go to the council rock by the bull that buy make promise little promise only thy coat be lack before keep my word with the knife with the knife that man use with the knife of the hunter will stoop down for my gift water of the waingunga shere khan give his coat for the love that he bear pull gray brother pull akela heavy be the hide of shere khan the man pack be angry they throw stone and talk child ’s talk my mouth be blee let run away through the night through the hot night run swiftly with my brother we will leave the light of the village and go to the low moon water of the waingunga the man pack have cast out do they no harm
but they be afraid of why wolf pack ye have cast out too the jungle be shut to and the village gate be shut why as mang fly between the beast and bird so fly between the village and the jungle why dance on the hide of shere khan but my heart be very heavy my mouth be cut and wound with the stone from the village but my heart be very light because have come back to the jungle why these two thing fight together in as the snake fight in the spring the water come out of my eye yet laugh while it fall why be two mowgli but the hide of shere khan be under my foot all the jungle know that have kill shere khan look look well wolf ahae my heart be heavy with the thing that do not understand the white seal
oh hush thee my baby the night be behind we and black be the water that sparkle so green the moon o’er the comber look downward to find we at rest in the hollow that rustle between where billow meet billow then soft be thy pillow ah weary wee flipperling curl at thy ease the storm shall not wake thee nor shark overtake thee asleep in the arm of the slow swing sea seal lullaby all these thing happen several year ago at place call novastoshnah or north east point on the island of st paul away and away in the bering sea limmershin the winter wren tell the tale when he be blow on to the rigging of steamer go to japan and take he down into my cabin and warm and feed he for couple of day till he be fit to fly back to st paul ’s again limmershin be very quaint little bird
but he know how to tell the truth nobody come to novastoshnah except on business and the only people who have regular business there be the seal they come in the summer month by hundred and hundred of thousand out of the cold gray sea for novastoshnah beach have the fine accommodation for seal of any place in all the world sea catch know that and every spring would swim from whatever place he happen to be in would swim like torpedo boat straight for novastoshnah and spend month fight with his companion for good place on the rock as close to the sea as possible sea catch be fifteen year old huge gray fur seal with almost mane on his shoulder and long wicked dog tooth when he heave himself up on his front flipper he stand more than four foot clear of the ground and his weight if anyone have be bold enough to weigh he be nearly seven hundred pound he be scar all over with the mark of savage fight
but he be always ready for just one fight more he would put his head on one side as though he be afraid to look his enemy in the face then he would shoot it out like lightning and when the big tooth be firmly fix on the other seal ’s neck the other seal might get away if he could but sea catch would not help he yet sea catch never chase beat seal for that be against the rule of the beach he only want room by the sea for his nursery
but as there be forty or fifty thousand other seal hunt for the same thing each spring the whistle bellow roar and blow on the beach be something frightful from little hill call hutchinson ’s hill you could look over three and half mile of ground cover with fight seal and the surf be dot all over with the head of seal hurry to land and begin their share of the fighting they fight in the breaker they fight in the sand and they fight on the smooth wear basalt rock of the nursery for they be just as stupid and unaccommodating as man their wife never come to the island until late in may or early in june for they do not care to be tear to piece and the young two- three- and four year old seal who have not begin housekeeping go inland about half mile through the rank of the fighter and play about on the sand dune in drove and legion and rub off every single green thing that grow they be call the holluschickie the bachelor and there be perhaps two or three hundred thousand of they at novastoshnah alone sea catch have just finish his forty fifth fight one spring when matkah his soft sleek gentle eyed wife come up out of the sea and he catch she by the scruff of the neck and dump she down on his reservation say gruffly late as usual where have you be it be not the fashion for sea catch to eat anything during the four month he stay on the beach and so his temper be generally bad matkah know well than to answer back she look round and coo how thoughtful of you you ve take the old place again should think have say sea catch look at he be scratch and bleed in twenty place one eye be almost out and his side be tear to ribbon
oh you man you man matkah say fan herself with her hind flipper why can not you be sensible and settle your place quietly you look as though you have be fight with the killer whale have not be do anything but fight since the middle of may the beach be disgracefully crowd this season ve meet at least hundred seal from lukannon beach house hunting why can not people stay where they belong ve often think we should be much happy if we haul out at otter island instead of this crowd place say matkah only the holluschickie go to otter island if we go there they would say we be afraid we must preserve appearance my dear sea catch sink his head proudly between his fat shoulder and pretend to go to sleep for few minute but all the time he be keep sharp lookout for fight now that all the seal and their wife be on the land you could hear their clamor mile out to sea above the loud gale at the low counting there be over million seal on the beach old seal mother seal tiny baby and holluschickie fighting scuffle bleat crawl and play together go down to the sea and come up from it in gang and regiment lie over every foot of ground as far as the eye could reach and skirmish about in brigade through the fog it be nearly always foggy at novastoshnah except when the sun come out and make everything look all pearly and rainbow color for little while kotick matkah ’s baby be bear in the middle of that confusion and he be all head and shoulder with pale watery blue eye as tiny seal must be but there be something about his coat that make his mother look at he very closely sea catch she say at last our baby ’s go to be white empty clam shell and dry seaweed snort sea catch there never have be such thing in the world as white seal can not help that say matkah there go to be now and she sing the low croon seal song that all the mother seal sing to their baby you must not swim till you ’re six week old or your head will be sink by your heel and summer gale and killer whale be bad for baby seal be bad for baby seal dear rat as bad as bad can be but splash and grow strong and you can not be wrong child of the open sea of course the little fellow do not understand the word at first he paddle and scramble about by his mother ’s side and learn to scuffle out of the way when his father be fight with another seal and the two roll and roar up and down the slippery rock matkah use to go to sea to get thing to eat and the baby be feed only once in two day
but then he eat all he could and throve upon it the first thing he do be to crawl inland and there he meet ten of thousand of baby of his own age and they play together like puppy go to sleep on the clean sand and play again the old people in the nursery take no notice of they and the holluschickie keep to their own ground and the baby have beautiful playtime when matkah come back from her deep sea fishing she would go straight to their playground and call as sheep call for lamb and wait until she hear kotick bleat then she would take the straight of straight line in his direction strike out with her fore flipper and knock the youngster head over heel right and leave there be always few hundred mother hunt for their child through the playground and the baby be keep lively but as matkah tell kotick so long as you do not lie in muddy water and get mange or rub the hard sand into cut or scratch and so long as you never go swim when there be heavy sea nothing will hurt you here little seal can no more swim than little child
but they be unhappy till they learn the first time that kotick go down to the sea wave carry he out beyond his depth and his big head sink and his little hind flipper fly up exactly as his mother have tell he in the song and if the next wave have not throw he back again he would have drown after that he learn to lie in beach pool and let the wash of the wave just cover he and lift he up while he paddle
but he always keep his eye open for big wave that might hurt he be two week learn to use his flipper and all that while he flounder in and out of the water and cough and grunt and crawl up the beach and take catnap on the sand and go back again until at last he find that he truly belong to the water then you can imagine the time that he have with his companion duck under the roller or come in on top of comber and land with swash and splutter as the big wave go whirl far up the beach or stand up on his tail and scratch his head as the old people do or play the king of the castle on slippery weedy rock that just stick out of the wash now
and then he would see thin fin like big shark ’s fin drift along close to shore
and he know that that be the killer whale the grampus who eat young seal when he can get they and kotick would head for the beach like an arrow and the fin would jig off slowly as if it be look for nothing at all late in october the seal begin to leave st paul ’s for the deep sea by family and tribe and there be no more fighting over the nursery and the holluschickie play anywhere they like next year say matkah to kotick you will be holluschickie but this year you must learn how to catch fish they set out together across the pacific and matkah show kotick how to sleep on his back with his flipper tuck down by his side and his little nose just out of the water no cradle be so comfortable as the long rock swell of the pacific when kotick feel his skin tingle all over matkah tell he he be learn the feel of the water and that tingly prickly feeling mean bad weather come
and he must swim hard and get away in little time she say you ’ll know where to swim to
but just now we ’ll follow sea pig the porpoise for he be very wise school of porpoise be duck and tear through the water and little kotick follow they as fast as he could how do you know where to go to he pant the leader of the school roll his white eye and duck under my tail tingle youngster he say that mean there gale behind come along when you ’re south of the sticky water he mean the equator and your tail tingle that mean there gale in front of you and you must head north come along the water feel bad here this be one of very many thing that kotick learn and he be always learn matkah teach he to follow the cod and the halibut along the under sea bank and wrench the rockling out of his hole among the weed how to skirt the wreck lie hundred fathom below water and dart like rifle bullet in at one porthole and out at another as the fish run how to dance on the top of the wave when the lightning be race all over the sky and wave his flipper politely to the stumpy tailed albatross and the man of war hawk as they go down the wind how to jump three or four foot clear of the water like dolphin flipper close to the side and tail curve to leave the fly fish alone because they be all bony to take the shoulder piece out of cod at full speed ten fathom deep and never to stop and look at boat or ship but particularly row boat at the end of six month what kotick do not know about deep sea fishing be not worth the knowing and all that time he never set flipper on dry ground one day however as he be lie half asleep in the warm water somewhere off the island of juan fernandez he feel faint and lazy all over just as human people do when the spring be in their leg and he remember the good firm beach of novastoshnah seven thousand mile away the game his companion play the smell of the seaweed the seal roar and the fighting that very minute he turn north swim steadily and as he go on he meet score of his mate all bind for the same place and they say greet kotick this year we be all holluschickie
and we can dance the fire dance in the breaker off lukannon and play on the new grass but where do you get that coat kotick ’s fur be almost pure white now and though he feel very proud of it he only say swim quickly my bone be ache for the land
and so they all come to the beach where they have be bear and hear the old seal their father fight in the rolling mist that night kotick dance the fire dance with the yearling seal the sea be full of fire on summer night all the way down from novastoshnah to lukannon and each seal leave wake like burn oil behind he and flame flash when he jump and the wave break in great phosphorescent streak and swirl then they go inland to the holluschickie ground and roll up and down in the new wild wheat and tell story of what they have do while they have be at sea they talk about the pacific as boy would talk about wood that they have be nut in and if anyone have understand they he could have go away and make such chart of that ocean as never be the three- and four year old holluschickie romp down from hutchinson ’s hill cry out of the way youngster the sea be deep and you do not know all that in it yet wait till you ve round the horn hi you yearle where do you get that white coat do not get it say kotick it grow and just as he be go to roll the speaker over couple of black haired man with flat red face come from behind sand dune and kotick who have never see man before cough and lower his head the holluschickie just bundle off few yard and sit star stupidly the man be no less than kerick booterin the chief of the seal hunter on the island and patalamon his son they come from the little village not half mile from the sea nursery and they be decide what seal they would drive up to the kill pen for the seal be drive just like sheep to be turn into seal skin jacket later on ho say patalamon there white seal kerick booterin turn nearly white under his oil and smoke for he be an aleut and aleut be not clean people then he begin to mutter prayer do not touch he patalamon there have never be white seal since since be bear perhaps it be old zaharrof ’s ghost he be lose last year in the big gale not go near he say patalamon he unlucky do you really think he be old zaharrof come back owe he for some gull egg do not look at he say kerick head off that drive of four year old the man ought to skin two hundred to day
but it the beginning of the season
and they be new to the work hundred will do patalamon rattle pair of seal ’s shoulder bone in front of herd of holluschickie and they stop dead puff and blow then he step near and the seal begin to move and kerick head they inland and they never try to get back to their companion hundred and hundred of thousand of seal watch they be drive
but they go on play just the same kotick be the only one who ask question and none of his companion could tell he anything except that the man always drive seal in that way for six week or two month of every year be go to follow he say and his eye nearly pop out of his head as he shuffle along in the wake of the herd the white seal be come after we cry patalamon that the first time seal have ever come to the kill ground alone do not look behind you say kerick it be zaharrof ’s ghost must speak to the priest about this the distance to the kill ground be only half mile
but it take an hour to cover because if the seal go too fast kerick know that they would get heat and then their fur would come off in patch when they be skin so they go on very slowly past sea lion ’s neck past webster house till they come to the salt house just beyond the sight of the seal on the beach kotick follow pant and wonder he think that he be at the world ’s end but the roar of the seal nursery behind he sound as loud as the roar of train in tunnel then kerick sit down on the moss and pull out heavy pewter watch and let the drive cool off for thirty minute and kotick could hear the fog dew drip off the brim of his cap then ten or twelve man each with an iron bind club three or four foot long come up and kerick point out one or two of the drove that be bite by their companion or too hot and the man kick those aside with their heavy boot make of the skin of walrus ’s throat and then kerick say let go and then the man club the seal on the head as fast as they could ten minute later little kotick do not recognize his friend any more for their skin be rip off from the nose to the hind flipper whip off and throw down on the ground in pile that be enough for kotick he turn and gallop seal can gallop very swiftly for short time back to the sea his little new mustache bristle with horror at sea lion ’s neck where the great sea lion sit on the edge of the surf he fling himself flipper overhead into the cool water and rock there gasp miserably what here say sea lion gruffly for as rule the sea lion keep themselves to themselves ochen scoochnie lonesome very lonesome say kotick they ’re kill all the holluschickie on all the beach the sea lion turn his head inshore he say your friend be make as much noise as ever you must have see old kerick polish off drive he do that for thirty year it horrible say kotick back water as wave go over he and steady himself with screw stroke of his flipper that bring he all stand within three inch of jagged edge of rock well do for yearling say the sea lion who could appreciate good swimming suppose it be rather awful from your way of look at it
but if you seal will come here year after year of course the man get to know of it and unless you can find an island where no man ever come you will always be drive be not there any such island begin kotick ve follow the poltoos the halibut for twenty year and can not say ve find it yet but look here you seem to have fondness for talk to your well suppose you go to walrus islet and talk to sea vitch he may know something do not flounce off like that it six mile swim and if be you should haul out and take nap first little one kotick think that that be good advice so he swim round to his own beach haul out and sleep for half an hour twitch all over as seal will then he head straight for walrus islet little low sheet of rocky island almost due northeast from novastoshnah all ledge and rock and gull nest where the walrus herd by themselves he land close to old sea vitch the big ugly bloated pimpled fat necked long tusked walrus of the north pacific who have no manner except when he be asleep as he be then with his hind flipper half in and half out of the surf wake up bark kotick for the gull be make great noise
hah ho hmph what that say sea vitch and he strike the next walrus blow with his tusk and wake he up and the next strike the next and so on till they be all awake and star in every direction but the right one it say kotick bob in the surf and look like little white slug may be skin say sea vitch
and they all look at kotick as you can fancy club full of drowsy old gentleman would look at little boy kotick do not care to hear any more about skin just then he have see enough of it
so he call out be not there any place for seal to go where man do not ever come go and find out say sea vitch shut his eye run away we ’re busy here kotick make his dolphin jump in the air and shout as loud as he could clam eater clam eater he know that sea vitch never catch fish in his life but always root for clam and seaweed though he pretend to be very terrible person naturally the chickie and the gooverooskie and the epatka the burgomaster gull and the kittiwake and the puffin who be always look for chance to be rude take up the cry and so limmershin tell for nearly five minute you could not have hear gun fire on walrus islet all the population be yell and scream clam eater stareek old man while sea vitch roll from side to side grunt and cough now will you tell say kotick all out of breath go and ask sea cow say sea vitch if he be live still he ’ll be able to tell you how shall know sea cow when meet he say kotick sheer off he the only thing in the sea ugly than sea vitch scream burgomaster gull wheel under sea vitch ’s nose ugly and with bad manner kotick swim back to novastoshnah leave the gull to scream there he find that no one sympathize with he in his little attempt to discover quiet place for the seal they tell he that man have always drive the holluschickie it be part of the day ’s work and that if he do not like to see ugly thing he should not have go to the kill ground but none of the other seal have see the killing and that make the difference between he and his friend besides kotick be white seal what you must do say old sea catch after he have hear his son ’s adventure be to grow up and be big seal like your father and have nursery on the beach and then they will leave you alone in another five year you ought to be able to fight for yourself even gentle matkah
his mother say you will never be able to stop the killing go and play in the sea kotick and kotick go off and dance the fire dance with very heavy little heart that autumn
he leave the beach as soon as he could and set off alone because of notion in his bullet head he be go to find sea cow if there be such person in the sea and he be go to find quiet island with good firm beach for seal to live on where man could not get at they so he explore and explore by himself from the north to the south pacific swim as much as three hundred mile in day and night he meet with more adventure than can be tell and narrowly escape be catch by the bask shark and the spot shark and the hammerhead and he meet all the untrustworthy ruffian that loaf up and down the sea and the heavy polite fish and the scarlet spot scallop that be moor in one place for hundred of year and grow very proud of it
but he never meet sea cow and he never find an island that he could fancy if the beach be good and hard with slope behind it for seal to play on there be always the smoke of whaler on the horizon boil down blubber and kotick know what that mean or else he could see that seal have once visit the island and be kill off and kotick know that where man have come once they would come again he pick up with an old stumpy tailed albatross who tell he that kerguelen island be the very place for peace and quiet and when kotick go down there he be all but smash to piece against some wicked black cliff in heavy sleet storm with lightning and thunder yet as he pull out against the gale he could see that even there have once be seal nursery and it be so in all the other island that he visit limmershin give long list of they for he say that kotick spend five season explore with four month rest each year at novastoshnah when the holluschickie use to make fun of he and his imaginary island he go to the gallapago horrid dry place on the equator where he be nearly bake to death he go to the georgia islands the orkneys emerald island little nightingale island gough ’s island bouvet ’s island the crosset and even to little speck of an island south of the cape of good hope but everywhere the people of the sea tell he the same thing seal have come to those island once upon time but man have kill they all off even when he swim thousand of mile out of the pacific and get to place call cape corriente that be when he be come back from gough ’s island he find few hundred mangy seal on rock and they tell he that man come there too that nearly break his heart and he head round the horn back to his own beach and on his way north he haul out on an island full of green tree where he find an old old seal who be die and kotick catch fish for he and tell he all his sorrow now say kotick be go back to novastoshnah and if be drive to the kill pen with the holluschickie shall not care the old seal say try once more be the last of the lost rookery of masafuera and in the day when man kill we by the hundred thousand there be story on the beach that some day white seal would come out of the north and lead the seal people to quiet place be old and shall never live to see that day but other will try once more and kotick curl up his mustache it be beauty and say be the only white seal that have ever be bear on the beach and be the only seal black or white who ever think of look for new island this cheer he immensely and when he come back to novastoshnah that summer matkah his mother beg he to marry and settle down for he be no long holluschick but full grow sea catch with curly white mane on his shoulder as heavy as big and as fierce as his father give another season he say remember mother it be always the seventh wave that go farthest up the beach curiously enough there be another seal who think that she would put off marry till the next year and kotick dance the fire dance with she all down lukannon beach the night before he set off on his last exploration this time he go westward because he have fall on the trail of great shoal of halibut and he need at least one hundred pound of fish day to keep he in good condition he chase they till he be tired and then he curl himself up and go to sleep on the hollow of the ground swell that set in to copper island he know the coast perfectly well so about midnight when he feel himself gently bump on weed bed he say hm tide ’s run strong tonight and turn over under water open his eye slowly and stretch then he jump like cat for he see huge thing nose about in the shoal water and browse on the heavy fringe of the weed by the great comber of magellan he say beneath his mustache who in the deep sea be these people they be like no walrus sea lion seal bear whale shark fish squid or scallop that kotick have ever see before they be between twenty and thirty foot long and they have no hind flipper but shovel like tail that look as if it have be whittle out of wet leather their head be the most foolish look thing you ever see and they balance on the end of their tail in deep water when they be not graze bow solemnly to each other and wave their front flipper as fat man wave his arm ahem say kotick good sport gentleman the big thing answer by bow and wave their flipper like the frog footman when they begin feed again kotick see that their upper lip be split into two piece that they could twitch apart about foot and bring together again with whole bushel of seaweed between the split they tuck the stuff into their mouth and chumpe solemnly messy style of feed that say kotick they bow again and kotick begin to lose his temper very good he say if you do happen to have an extra joint in your front flipper you need not show off so see you bow gracefully but should like to know your name the split lip move and twitch and the glassy green eye star
but they do not speak well say kotick you ’re the only people ve ever meet ugly than sea vitch and with bad manner then he remember in flash what the burgomaster gull have scream to he when he be little yearling at walrus islet and he tumble backward in the water for he know that he have find sea cow at last the sea cow go on schloope and graze and chumpe in the weed and kotick ask they question in every language that he have pick up in his travel and the sea people talk nearly as many language as human be but the sea cow do not answer because sea cow can not talk he have only six bone in his neck where he ought to have seven and they say under the sea that that prevent he from speak even to his companion but as you know he have an extra joint in his foreflipper and by wave it up and down and about he make what answer to sort of clumsy telegraphic code by daylight kotick ’s mane be stand on end and his temper be go where the dead crab go then the sea cow begin to travel northward very slowly stop to hold absurd bow council from time to time and kotick follow they say to himself people who be such idiot as these be would have be kill long ago if they have not find out some safe island and what be good enough for the sea cow be good enough for the sea catch all the same wish they ’d hurry it be weary work for kotick the herd never go more than forty or fifty mile day and stop to feed at night and keep close to the shore all the time while kotick swim round they and over they and under they
but he could not hurry they up one half mile as they go far north they hold bow council every few hour and kotick nearly bit off his mustache with impatience till he see that they be follow up warm current of water
and then he respect they more one night they sink through the shiny water sink like stone and for the first time since he have know they begin to swim quickly kotick follow and the pace astonish he for he never dream that sea cow be anything of swimmer they head for cliff by the shore cliff that run down into deep water and plunge into dark hole at the foot of it twenty fathom under the sea it be long long swim and kotick badly want fresh air before he be out of the dark tunnel they lead he through my wig he say when he rise gasp and puff into open water at the farther end it be long dive
but it be worth it the sea cow have separate and be browse lazily along the edge of the fine beach that kotick have ever see there be long stretch of smooth wear rock run for mile exactly fit to make seal nursery and there be play ground of hard sand slop inland behind they and there be roller for seal to dance in and long grass to roll in and sand dune to climb up and down and good of all kotick know by the feel of the water which never deceive true sea catch that no man have ever come there the first thing he do be to assure himself that the fishing be good
and then he swim along the beach and count up the delightful low sandy island half hide in the beautiful rolling fog away to the northward out to sea run line of bar and shoal and rock that would never let ship come within six mile of the beach and between the island and the mainland be stretch of deep water that run up to the perpendicular cliff and somewhere below the cliff be the mouth of the tunnel it novastoshnah over again but ten time well say kotick sea cow must be wise than think man can not come down the cliff even if there be any man and the shoal to seaward would knock ship to splinter if any place in the sea be safe this be it he begin to think of the seal he have leave behind he
but though he be in hurry to go back to novastoshnah he thoroughly explore the new country so that he would be able to answer all question then he dive and make sure of the mouth of the tunnel and race through to the southward no one but sea cow or seal would have dream of there be such place and when he look back at the cliff even kotick could hardly believe that he have be under they he be six day go home though he be not swim slowly and when he haul out just above sea lion ’s neck the first person he meet be the seal who have be wait for he
and she see by the look in his eye that he have find his island at last
but the holluschickie and sea catch his father and all the other seal laugh at he when he tell they what he have discover and young seal about his own age say this be all very well kotick but you can not come from no one know where and order we off like this remember we ve be fight for our nursery and that thing you never do you prefer prowl about in the sea the other seal laugh at this and the young seal begin twist his head from side to side he have just marry that year and be make great fuss about it ve no nursery to fight for say kotick only want to show you all place where you will be safe what the use of fight
oh if you ’re try to back out of course ve no more to say say the young seal with an ugly chuckle will you come with if win say kotick and green light come into his eye for he be very angry at have to fight at all very good say the young seal carelessly if you win ’ll come he have no time to change his mind for kotick ’s head be out and his tooth sink in the blubber of the young seal ’s neck then he throw himself back on his haunch and haul his enemy down the beach shake he and knock he over then kotick roar to the seal ve do my good for you these five season past ve find you the island where you ’ll be safe but unless your head be drag off your silly neck you will not believe go to teach you now look out for yourself limmershin tell that never in his life and limmershin see ten thousand big seal fight every year never in all his little life do he see anything like kotick ’s charge into the nursery he fling himself at the big sea catch he could find catch he by the throat choke he and bump he and bang he till he grunt for mercy and then throw he aside and attack the next you see kotick have never fast for four month as the big seal do every year and his deep sea swimming trip keep he in perfect condition and good of all he have never fight before his curly white mane stand up with rage and his eye flame and his big dog tooth glisten and he be splendid to look at old sea catch his father see he tear past haul the grizzled old seal about as though they have be halibut and upset the young bachelor in all direction and sea catch give roar and shout he may be fool
but he be the good fighter on the beach do not tackle your father my son he with you kotick roar in answer and old sea catch waddle in with his mustache on end blow like locomotive while matkah and the seal that be go to marry kotick cower down and admire their man folk it be gorgeous fight for the two fight as long as there be seal that dare lift up his head and when there be none they parade grandly up and down the beach side by side bellow at night just as the northern light be wink and flash through the fog kotick climb bare rock and look down on the scatter nursery and the tear and bleed seal now he say ve teach you your lesson my wig say old sea catch boost himself up stiffly for he be fearfully maul the killer whale himself could not have cut they up bad son proud of you and what more ’ll come with you to your island if there be such place hear you fat pig of the sea who come with to the sea cow ’s tunnel answer or shall teach you again roar kotick there be murmur like the ripple of the tide all up and down the beach we will come say thousand of tired voice we will follow kotick the white seal then kotick drop his head between his shoulder and shut his eye proudly he be not white seal any more but red from head to tail all the same he would have scorn to look at or touch one of his wound week later he and his army nearly ten thousand holluschickie and old seal go away north to the sea cow ’s tunnel kotick lead they and the seal that stay at novastoshnah call they idiot but next spring when they all meet off the fishing bank of the pacific kotick ’s seal tell such tale of the new beach beyond sea cow ’s tunnel that more and more seal leave novastoshnah of course it be not all do at once for the seal be not very clever and they need long time to turn thing over in their mind but year after year more seal go away from novastoshnah and lukannon and the other nursery to the quiet sheltered beach where kotick sit all the summer through get big and fat and strong each year while the holluschickie play around he in that sea where no man come lukannon this be the great deep sea song that all the st paul seal sing when they be head back to their beach in the summer it be sort of very sad seal national anthem meet my mate in the morning
oh but be old where roar on the ledge the summer ground swell roll hear they lift the chorus that drown the breaker song the beach of lukannon two million voice strong the song of pleasant station beside the salt lagoon the song of blow squadron that shuffle down the dune the song of midnight dance that churn the sea to flame the beach of lukannon before the sealer come meet my mate in the morning ’ll never meet they more they come and go in legion that darken all the shore and o’er the foam flecked offing as far as voice could reach we hail the landing party and we sing they up the beach the beach of lukannon the winter wheat so tall the dripping crinkled lichen and the sea fog drenching all the platform of our playground all shin smooth and wear the beach of lukannon the home where we be bear meet my mate in the morning break scatter band man shoot we in the water and club we on the land man drive we to the salt house like silly sheep and tame and still we sing lukannon before the sealer come wheel down wheel down to southward
oh gooverooska go and tell the deep sea viceroy the story of our woe ere empty as the shark ’s egg the tempest fling ashore the beach of lukannon shall know their son no more rikki tikki tavi at the hole where he go in red eye call to wrinkle skin hear what little red eye
saith nag come up and dance with death eye to eye and head to head keep the measure nag this shall end when one be dead at thy pleasure nag turn for turn and twist for twist run and hide thee nag the hooded death have miss woe betide thee nag this be the story of the great war that rikki tikki tavi fight single hand through the bath room of the big bungalow in segowlee cantonment darzee the tailorbird help he and chuchundra the muskrat who never come out into the middle of the floor but always creep round by the wall give he advice but rikki tikki do the real fighting he be mongoose rather like little cat in his fur and his tail but quite like weasel in his head and his habit his eye and the end of his restless nose be pink he could scratch himself anywhere he please with any leg front or back that he choose to use he could fluff up his tail till it look like bottle brush and his war cry as he scuttle through the long grass be rikk tikk tikki tikki tchk one day high summer flood wash he out of the burrow where he live with his father and mother and carry he kick and cluck down roadside ditch he find little wisp of grass float there and cling to it till he lose his sen when he revive he be lie in the hot sun on the middle of garden path very draggle indeed and small boy be say here dead mongoose let ’s have funeral no say his mother let ’s take he in and dry he perhaps he be not really dead they take he into the house and big man pick he up between his finger and thumb and say he be not dead but half choke so they wrap he in cotton wool and warm he over little fire and he open his eye and sneeze now say the big man he be an englishman who have just move into the bungalow do not frighten he
and we ’ll see what he ’ll do it be the hard thing in the world to frighten mongoose because he be eat up from nose to tail with curiosity the motto of all the mongoose family be run and find out and rikki tikki be true mongoose he look at the cotton wool decide that it be not good to eat run all round the table sit up and put his fur in order scratch himself and jump on the small boy ’s shoulder do not be frightened teddy say his father that his way of make friend he ’s tickle under my chin say teddy rikki tikki look down between the boy ’s collar and neck snuff at his ear and climb down to the floor where he sit rub his nose good gracious say teddy ’s mother and that wild creature suppose he so tame because we ve be kind to he all mongoose be like that say her husband if teddy do not pick he up by the tail or try to put he in cage he ’ll run in and out of the house all day long let ’s give he something to eat they give he little piece of raw meat rikki tikki like it immensely and when it be finish he go out into the veranda and sit in the sunshine and fluff up his fur to make it dry to the root then he feel well there be more thing to find out about in this house he say to himself than all my family could find out in all their life shall certainly stay and find out he spend all that day roam over the house he nearly drown himself in the bath tub put his nose into the ink on write table and burn it on the end of the big man ’s cigar for he climb up in the big man ’s lap to see how writing be do at nightfall he run into teddy ’s nursery to watch how kerosene lamp be light and when teddy go to bed rikki tikki climb up too
but he be restless companion because he have to get up and attend to every noise all through the night and find out what make it teddy ’s mother and father come in the last thing to look at their boy and rikki tikki be awake on the pillow do not like that say teddy ’s mother he may bite the child he ’ll do no such thing say the father teddy safe with that little beast than if he have bloodhound to watch he if snake come into the nursery now--
but teddy ’s mother would not think of anything so awful early in the morning rikki tikki come to early breakfast in the veranda rid on teddy ’s shoulder and they give he banana and some boil egg he sit on all their lap one after the other because every well bring up mongoose always hop to be house mongoose some day and have room to run about in and rikki tikki ’s mother she use to live in the general ’s house at segowlee have carefully tell rikki what to do if ever he come across white man then rikki tikki go out into the garden to see what be to be see it be large garden only half cultivate with bush as big as summer house of marshal niel rise lime and orange tree clump of bamboo and thicket of high grass rikki tikki lick his lip this be splendid hunting ground he say and his tail grow bottle brushy at the thought of it and he scuttle up and down the garden snuff here and there till he hear very sorrowful voice in thorn bush it be darzee the tailorbird and his wife they have make beautiful nest by pull two big leaf together and stitch they up the edge with fiber and have fill the hollow with cotton and downy fluff the nest sway to and fro as they sit on the rim and cry what be the matter ask rikki tikki we be very miserable say darzee one of our baby fall out of the nest yesterday and nag eat he h’m say rikki tikki that be very sad but be strange here who be nag darzee and his wife only cower down in the nest without answer for from the thick grass at the foot of the bush there come low hiss horrid cold sound that make rikki tikki jump back two clear foot then inch by inch out of the grass rise up the head and spread hood of nag the big black cobra
and he be five foot long from tongue to tail when he have lift one third of himself clear of the ground he stay balance to and fro exactly as dandelion tuft balance in the wind and he look at rikki tikki with the wicked snake ’s eye that never change their expression whatever the snake may be think of who be nag say he be nag the great god brahm put his mark upon all our people when the first cobra spread his hood to keep the sun off brahm as he sleep look and be afraid he spread out his hood more than ever and rikki tikki see the spectacle mark on the back of it that look exactly like the eye part of hook and eye fastening he be afraid for the minute but it be impossible for mongoose to stay frightened for any length of time and though rikki tikki have never meet live cobra before his mother have feed he on dead one
and he know that all grow mongoose ’s business in life be to fight and eat snake nag know that too and at the bottom of his cold heart he be afraid well say rikki tikki and his tail begin to fluff up again mark or no mark do you think it be right for you to eat fledgling out of nest nag be think to himself and watch the least little movement in the grass behind rikki tikki he know that mongoose in the garden mean death soon or later for he and his family
but he want to get rikki tikki off his guard so he drop his head little and put it on one side let we talk he say you eat egg why should not eat bird behind you look behind you sing darzee rikki tikki know
well than to waste time in star he jump up in the air as high as he could go and just under he whiz by the head of nagaina nag ’s wicked wife she have creep up behind he as he be talk to make an end of he he hear her savage hiss as the stroke miss he come down almost across she back and if he have be an old mongoose he would have know that then be the time to break she back with one bite
but he be afraid of the terrible lashing return stroke of the cobra he bite indeed but do not bite long enough and he jump clear of the whisk tail leave nagaina tear and angry wicked wicked darzee say nag lash up as high as he could reach toward the nest in the thorn bush but darzee have build it out of reach of snake and it only sway to and fro rikki tikki feel his eye grow red and hot when mongoose ’s eye grow red he be angry and he sit back on his tail and hind leg like little kangaroo and look all round he and chatter with rage but nag and nagaina have disappear into the grass when snake miss its stroke it never say anything or give any sign of what it mean to do next rikki tikki do not care to follow they for he do not feel sure that he could manage two snake at once so he trot off to the gravel path near the house and sit down to think it be serious matter for he if you read the old book of natural history you will find they say that when the mongoose fight the snake and happen to get bite he run off and eat some herb that cure he that be not true the victory be only matter of quickness of eye and quickness of foot snake ’s blow against mongoose ’s jump and as no eye can follow the motion of snake ’s head when it strike this make thing much more wonderful than any magic herb rikki
tikki know he be young mongoose and it make he all the more pleased to think that he have manage to escape blow from behind it give he confidence in himself and when teddy come run down the path rikki tikki be ready to be pet but just as teddy be stoop something wriggle little in the dust and tiny voice say be careful be death it be karait the dusty brown snakeling that lie for choice on the dusty earth and his bite be as dangerous as the cobra ’s
but he be so small that nobody think of he
and so he do the more harm to people rikki tikki ’s eye grow red again
and he dance up to karait with the peculiar rock sway motion that he have inherit from his family it look very funny
but it be so perfectly balanced gait that you can fly off from it at any angle you please and in deal with snake this be an advantage if rikki tikki have only know he be do much more dangerous thing than fight nag for karait be so small and can turn so quickly that unless rikki bite he close to the back of the head he would get the return stroke in his eye or his lip but rikki do not know his eye be all red and he rock back and forth look for good place to hold karait strike out rikki jump sideways and try to run in but the wicked little dusty gray head lash within fraction of his shoulder and he have to jump over the body and the head follow his heel close teddy shout to the house
oh look here our mongoose be kill snake and rikki tikki hear scream from teddy ’s mother his father run out with stick but by the time he come up karait have lunge out once too far and rikki tikki have spring jump on the snake ’s back drop his head far between his foreleg bite as high up the back as he could get hold and roll away that bite paralyze karait and rikki tikki be just go to eat he up from the tail after the custom of his family at dinner when he remember that full meal make slow mongoose and if he want all his strength and quickness ready he must keep himself thin he go away for dust bath under the castor oil bush while teddy ’s father beat the dead karait what be the use of that think rikki tikki have settle it all and then teddy ’s mother pick he up from the dust and hug he cry that he have save teddy from death and teddy ’s father say that he be providence and teddy look on with big scared eye rikki tikki be rather amuse at all the fuss which of course he do not understand teddy ’s mother might just as well have pet teddy for play in the dust rikki be thoroughly enjoy himself that night at dinner walk to and fro among the wine glass on the table he might have stuff himself three time over with nice thing
but he remember nag and nagaina and though it be very pleasant to be pat and pet by teddy ’s mother and to sit on teddy ’s shoulder his eye would get red from time to time and he would go off into his long war cry of rikk tikk tikki tikki tchk teddy carry he off to bed and insist on rikki tikki sleep under his chin rikki tikki be too well breed to bite or scratch
but as soon as teddy be asleep he go off for his nightly walk round the house and in the dark he run up against chuchundra the muskrat creep around by the wall chuchundra be break hearted little beast he whimper and cheep all the night try to make up his mind to run into the middle of the room
but he never get there do not kill say chuchundra almost weep rikki tikki do not kill do you think snake killer kill muskrat say rikki tikki scornfully those who kill snake get kill by snake say chuchundra more sorrowfully than ever and how be to be sure that nag will not mistake for you some dark night there not the least danger say rikki tikki but nag be in the garden and know you do not go there my cousin chua the rat tell me-- say chuchundra
and then he stop tell you what h’sh nag be everywhere rikki tikki you should have talk to chua in the garden do not so you must tell quick chuchundra or ’ll bite you chuchundra sit down and cry till the tear roll off his whisker be very poor man he sob never have spirit enough to run out into the middle of the room must not tell you anything can not you hear rikki tikki rikki tikki listen the house be as still as still
but he think he could just catch the faint scratch scratch in the world noise as faint as that of wasp walk on window pane the dry scratch of snake ’s scale on brick work that nag or nagaina he say to himself and he be crawl into the bath room sluice you ’re right chuchundra should have talk to chua he steal off to teddy ’s bath room but there be nothing there and then to teddy ’s mother ’s bathroom at the bottom of the smooth plaster wall there be brick pull out to make sluice for the bath water and as rikki tikki steal in by the masonry curb where the bath be put he hear nag and nagaina whisper together outside in the moonlight when the house be empty of people say nagaina to her husband he will have to go away
and then the garden will be our own again go in quietly and remember that the big man who kill karait be the first one to bite then come out and tell and we will hunt for rikki tikki together but be you sure that there be anything to be gain by kill the people say nag everything when there be no people in the bungalow do we have any mongoose in the garden so long as the bungalow be empty we be king and queen of the garden and remember that as soon as our egg in the melon bed hatch as they may tomorrow our child will need room and quiet have not think of that say nag will go but there be no need that we should hunt for rikki tikki afterward will kill the big man and his wife and the child if can and come away quietly then the bungalow will be empty and rikki tikki will go rikki tikki tingle all over with rage and hatred at this and then nag ’s head come through the sluice and his five foot of cold body follow it angry as he be rikki tikki be very frightened as he see the size of the big cobra nag coil himself up raise his head and look into the bathroom in the dark and rikki could see his eye glitter now if kill he here nagaina will know and if fight he on the open floor the odd be in his favor what be to do say rikki tikki tavi nag wave to and fro and then rikki tikki hear he drink from the big water jar that be use to fill the bath that be good
say the snake now when karait be kill the big man have stick he may have that stick still
but when he come in to bathe in the morning he will not have stick shall wait here till he come nagaina do you hear me?--i shall wait here in the cool till daytime there be no answer from outside so rikki tikki know nagaina have go away nag coil himself down coil by coil round the bulge at the bottom of the water jar and rikki tikki stay still as death after an hour he begin to move muscle by muscle toward the jar nag be asleep and rikki tikki look at his big back wonder which would be the good place for good hold if do not break his back at the first jump say rikki he can still fight and if he fight rikki he look at the thickness of the neck below the hood but that be too much for he and bite near the tail would only make nag savage it must be the head he say at last the head above the hood and when be once there must not let go then he jump the head be lie little clear of the water jar under the curve of it and as his tooth meet rikki brace his back against the bulge of the red earthenware to hold down the head this give he just one second ’s purchase and he make the most of it then he be batter to and fro as rat be shake by dog to and fro on the floor up and down and around in great circle but his eye be red and he hold on as the body cart whip over the floor upset the tin dipper and the soap dish and the flesh brush and bang against the tin side of the bath as he hold he close his jaw tight and tight for he make sure he would be bang to death and for the honor of his family he prefer to be find with his tooth lock he be dizzy ache and feel shake to piece when something go off like thunderclap just behind he hot wind knock he senseless and red fire sing his fur the big man have be waken by the noise and have fire both barrel of shotgun into nag just behind the hood rikki tikki hold on with his eye shut for now he be quite sure he be dead
but the head do not move and the big man pick he up and say it the mongoose again alice the little chap have save our life now then teddy ’s mother come in with very white face and see what be leave of nag and rikki tikki drag himself to teddy ’s bedroom and spend half the rest of the night shake himself tenderly to find out whether he really be break into forty piece as he fancy when morning come he be very stiff but well pleased with his doing now have nagaina to settle with and she will be bad than five nag and there no knowing when the egg she speak of will hatch must go and see darzee he say without wait for breakfast rikki tikki run to the thornbush where darzee be sing song of triumph at the top of his voice the news of nag ’s death be all over the garden for the sweeper have throw the body on the rubbish heap
oh you stupid tuft of feather say rikki tikki angrily be this the time to sing nag be dead be dead be dead sing darzee the valiant rikki tikki catch he by the head and hold fast the big man bring the bang stick and nag fall in two piece he will never eat my baby again all that true enough but where nagaina say rikki tikki look carefully round he nagaina come to the bathroom sluice and call for nag darzee go on and nag come out on the end of stick the sweeper pick he up on the end of stick and throw he upon the rubbish heap let we sing about the great the red eyed rikki tikki and darzee fill his throat and sing if could get up to your nest ’d roll your baby out say rikki tikki you do not know when to do the right thing at the right time you ’re safe enough in your nest there but it war for down here stop sing minute darzee for the great the beautiful rikki tikki ’s sake will stop say darzee what be it killer of the terrible nag where be nagaina for the third time on the rubbish heap by the stable mourn for nag great be rikki tikki with the white tooth bother my white tooth have you ever hear where she keep her egg in the melon bed on the end nearest the wall where the sun strike nearly all day she hide they there week ago
and you never think it worth while to tell the end nearest the wall you say rikki tikki you be not go to eat her egg not eat exactly no darzee if you have grain of sense you will fly off to the stable and pretend that your wing be break and let nagaina chase you away to this bush must get to the melon bed and if go there now she ’d see darzee be feather brained little fellow who could never hold more than one idea at time in his head and just because he know that nagaina ’s child be bear in egg like his own he do not think at first that it be fair to kill they
but his wife be sensible bird
and she know that cobra ’s egg mean young cobra later on so she fly off from the nest and leave darzee to keep the baby warm and continue his song about the death of nag darzee be very like man in some way she flutter in front of nagaina by the rubbish heap and cry out
oh my wing be break the boy in the house throw stone at and break it then she flutter more desperately than ever nagaina lift up her head and hiss you warn rikki tikki when would have kill he indeed and truly you ve choose bad place to be lame in and she move toward darzee ’s wife slip along over the dust the boy break it with stone shriek darzee ’s wife it may be some consolation to you when you ’re dead to know that shall settle account with the boy my husband lie on the rubbish heap this morning but before night the boy in the house will lie very still what be the use of run away be sure to catch you little fool look at darzee ’s wife know well than to do that for bird who look at snake ’s eye get so frightened that she can not move darzee ’s wife flutter on pip sorrowfully and never leave the ground and nagaina quicken her pace rikki tikki hear they go up the path from the stable and he race for the end of the melon patch near the wall there in the warm litter above the melon very cunningly hide he find twenty five egg about the size of bantam ’s egg but with whitish skin instead of shell be not day too soon he say for he could see the baby cobra curl up inside the skin and he know that the minute they be hatch they could each kill man or mongoose he bite off the top of the egg as fast as he could take care to crush the young cobra and turn over the litter from time to time to see whether he have miss any at last there be only three egg leave and rikki tikki begin to chuckle to himself when he hear darzee ’s wife scream rikki tikki lead nagaina toward the house and she have go into the veranda
