and oh come quickly she mean kill rikki tikki smash two egg and tumble backward down the melon bed with the third egg in his mouth and scuttle to the veranda as hard as he could put foot to the ground teddy and his mother and father be there at early breakfast but rikki tikki see that they be not eat anything they sit stone still and their face be white nagaina be coil up on the matting by teddy ’s chair within easy striking distance of teddy ’s bare leg and she be sway to and fro sing song of triumph son of the big man that kill
nag she hiss stay still be not ready yet wait little keep very still all you three if you move strike and if you do not move strike
oh foolish people who kill my nag teddy ’s eye be fix on his father and all his father could do be to whisper sit still teddy you must not move teddy keep still then rikki tikki come up and cry turn round nagaina turn and fight all in good time say she without move her eye will settle my account with you presently look at your friend rikki tikki they be still and white they be afraid they dare not move and if you come step near strike look at your egg say rikki tikki in the melon bed near the wall go and look nagaina the big snake turn half around and see the egg on the veranda ah give it to she say rikki tikki put his paw one on each side of the egg and his eye be blood red what price for snake ’s egg for young cobra for young king cobra for the last the very last of the brood the ant be eat all the other down by the melon bed nagaina spin clear round forget everything for the sake of the one egg rikki tikki see teddy ’s father shoot out big hand catch teddy by the shoulder and drag he across the little table with the tea cup safe and out of reach of nagaina trick rikk tck tck chuckle rikki tikki the boy be safe and it be that catch nag by the hood last night in the bathroom then he begin to jump up and down all four foot together his head close to the floor he throw to and fro
but he could not shake off he be dead before the big man blow he in two do it rikki tikki tck tck come then nagaina come and fight with you shall not be widow long nagaina see that she have lose her chance of kill teddy and the egg lie between rikki tikki ’s paw give the egg rikki tikki give the last of my egg and will go away and never come back she say lower her hood
yes you will go away and you will never come back for you will go to the rubbish heap with nag fight widow the big man have go for his gun fight rikki tikki be bind all round nagaina keep just out of reach of her stroke his little eye like hot coal nagaina gather herself together and fling out at he rikki tikki jump up and backward again and again and again she strike and each time her head come with whack on the matting of the veranda and she gather herself together like watch spring then rikki tikki dance in circle to get behind she and nagaina spin round to keep her head to his head so that the rustle of her tail on the matting sound like dry leaf blow along by the wind he have forget the egg it still lie on the veranda and nagaina come near and near to it till at last while rikki tikki be draw breath
she catch it in her mouth turn to the veranda step and fly like an arrow down the path with rikki tikki behind she when the cobra run for her life she go like whip lash flick across horse ’s neck rikki tikki know that he must catch she or all the trouble would begin again she head straight for the long grass by the thorn bush and as he be run
rikki tikki hear darzee still sing his foolish little song of triumph but darzee ’s wife be wise she fly off her nest as nagaina come along and flap her wing about nagaina ’s head if darzee have help they might have turn she but nagaina only lower her hood and go on still the instant ’s delay bring rikki tikki up to she and as she plunge into the rat hole where she and nag use to live his little white tooth be clench on her tail and he go down with she and very few mongoose however wise and old they may be care to follow cobra into its hole it be dark in the hole and rikki tikki never know when it might open out and give nagaina room to turn and strike at he he hold on savagely and stick out his foot to act as brake on the dark slope of the hot moist earth then the grass by the mouth of the hole stop wave
and darzee say it be all over with rikki tikki we must sing his death song valiant rikki tikki be dead for nagaina will surely kill he underground so he sing very mournful song that he make up on the spur of the minute and just as he get to the most touching part the grass quiver again and rikki tikki cover with dirt drag himself out of the hole leg by leg lick his whisker darzee stop with little shout rikki tikki shake some of the dust out of his fur and sneeze it be all over he say the widow will never come out again and the red ant that live between the grass stem hear he and begin to troop down one after another to see if he have speak the truth rikki tikki curl himself up in the grass and sleep where he be sleep and sleep till it be late in the afternoon for he have do hard day ’s work now he say when he awake will go back to the house tell the coppersmith darzee and he will tell the garden that nagaina be dead the coppersmith be bird who make noise exactly like the beating of little hammer on copper pot and the reason he be always make it be because he be the town crier to every indian garden and tell all the news to everybody who care to listen as rikki tikki go up the path he hear his attention note like tiny dinner gong and then the steady ding dong tock nag be dead dong nagaina be dead ding dong tock that set all the bird in the garden singing and the frog croak for nag and nagaina use to eat frog as well as little bird when rikki get to the house teddy and teddy ’s mother she look very white still for she have be faint and teddy ’s father come out and almost cry over he and that night he eat all that be give he till he could eat no more and go to bed on teddy ’s shoulder where teddy ’s mother see he when she come to look late at night he save our life and teddy ’s life she say to her husband just think he save all our life rikki tikki wake up with jump for the mongoose be light sleeper

you say he what be you bother for all the cobra be dead and if they be not here rikki tikki have right to be proud of himself
but he do not grow too proud and he keep that garden as mongoose should keep it with tooth and jump and spring and bite till never cobra dare show its head inside the wall darzee ’s chant sing in honor of rikki tikki tavi singer and tailor be i-- double the joy that know-- proud of my lilt to the sky proud of the house that sew-- over and under so weave my music so weave the house that sew sing to your fledgling again mother

will remember what be be sick of rope and chain-- will remember my old strength and all my forest affair will not sell my back to man for bundle of sugar cane will go out to my own kind and the wood folk in their lair will go out until the day until the morning break-- out to the wind ’s untainted kiss the water ’s clean caress will forget my ankle ring and snap my picket stake will revisit my lost love and playmate masterless kala nag which mean black snake have serve the indian government in every way that an elephant could serve it for forty seven year and as he be fully twenty year old when he be catch that make he nearly seventy ripe age for an elephant he remember push with big leather pad on his forehead at gun stick in deep mud and that be before the afghan war of and he have not then come to his full strength his mother radha pyari,--radha the darling,--who have be catch in the same drive with kala nag tell he before his little milk tusk have drop out that elephant who be afraid always get hurt kala nag know that that advice be good for the first time that he see shell burst he back scream into stand of pile rifle and the bayonet prick he in all his soft place so before he be twenty five he give up be afraid and so he be the well love and the well look after elephant in the service of the government of india he have carry tent twelve hundred pound weight of tent on the march in upper india he have be hoist into ship at the end of steam crane and take for day across the water and make to carry mortar on his back in strange and rocky country very far from india and have see the emperor theodore lie dead in magdala and have come back again in the steamer entitle so the soldier say to the abyssinian war medal he have see his fellow elephant die of cold and epilepsy and starvation and sunstroke up at place call ali musjid ten year later and afterward he have be send down thousand of mile south to haul and pile big balk of teak in the timberyard at moulmein there he have half kill an insubordinate young elephant who be shirk his fair share of work after that he be take off timber hauling and employ with few score other elephant who be train to the business in help to catch wild elephant among the garo hill elephant be very strictly preserve by the indian government there be one whole department which do nothing else but hunt they and catch they and break they in and send they up and down the country as they be need for work kala nag stand ten fair foot at the shoulder and his tusk have be cut off short at five foot and bind round the end to prevent they split with band of copper but he could do more with those stump than any untrained elephant could do with the real sharpen one when after week and week of cautious driving of scatter elephant across the hill the forty or fifty wild monster be drive into the last stockade and the big drop gate make of tree trunk lash together jar down behind they kala nag at the word of command would go into that flare trumpet pandemonium generally at night when the flicker of the torch make it difficult to judge distance and pick out the big and wild tusker of the mob would hammer he and hustle he into quiet while the man on the back of the other elephant rope and tie the small one there be nothing in the way of fight that kala nag the old wise black snake do not know for he have stand up more than once in his time to the charge of the wound tiger and curl up his soft trunk to be out of harm ’s way have knock the spring brute sideways in mid air with quick sickle cut of his head that he have invent all by himself have knock he over and kneel upon he with his huge knee till the life go out with gasp and howl and there be only fluffy striped thing on the ground for kala nag to pull by the tail
yes say big toomai his driver the son of black toomai who have take he to abyssinia and grandson of toomai of the elephant who have see he catch there be nothing that the black snake fear except he have see three generation of we feed he and groom he and he will live to see four he be afraid of also say little toomai stand up to his full height of four foot with only one rag upon he he be ten year old the eld son of big toomai and accord to custom he would take his father ’s place on kala nag ’s neck when he grow up and would handle the heavy iron ankus the elephant goad that have be wear smooth by his father and his grandfather and his great grandfather he know what he be talk of for he have be bear under kala nag ’s shadow have play with the end of his trunk before he could walk have take he down to water as soon as he could walk and kala nag would no more have dream of disobey his shrill little order than he would have dream of kill he on that day when big toomai carry the little brown baby under kala nag ’s tusk and tell he to salute his master that be to be yes say little toomai he be afraid of and he take long stride up to kala nag call he fat old pig and make he lift up his foot one after the other wah say little toomai thou art big elephant
and he wag his fluffy head quote his father the government may pay for elephant
but they belong to we mahout when thou art old kala nag there will come some rich rajah and he will buy thee from the government on account of thy size and thy manner and then thou wilt have nothing to do but to carry gold earring in thy ear and gold howdah on thy back and red cloth cover with gold on thy side and walk at the head of the procession of the king then shall sit on thy neck kala nag with silver ankus and man will run before we with golden stick cry room for the king ’s elephant that will be good kala nag but not so good as this hunting in the jungle umph say big toomai thou art boy and as wild as buffalo calf this run up and down among the hill be not the good government service be get old and do not love wild elephant give brick elephant line one stall to each elephant and big stump to tie they to safely and flat broad road to exercise upon instead of this come and go camp
aha the cawnpore barrack be good there be bazaar close by and only three hour work day little toomai remember the cawnpore elephant line and say nothing he very much prefer the camp life and hat those broad flat road with the daily grubbing for grass in the forage reserve and the long hour when there be nothing to do except to watch kala nag fidget in his picket what little toomai like be to scramble up bridle path that only an elephant could take the dip into the valley below the glimpse of the wild elephant browse mile away the rush of the frightened pig and peacock under kala nag ’s foot the blind warm rain when all the hill and valley smoke the beautiful misty morning when nobody know where they would camp that night the steady cautious drive of the wild elephant and the mad rush and blaze and hullabaloo of the last night ’s drive when the elephant pour into the stockade like boulder in landslide find that they could not get out and fling themselves at the heavy post only to be drive back by yell and flare torch and volley of blank cartridge even little boy could be of use there and toomai be as useful as three boy he would get his torch and wave it and yell with the good but the really good time come when the drive out begin and the keddah that be the stockade look like picture of the end of the world and man have to make sign to one another because they could not hear themselves speak then little toomai would climb up to the top of one of the quivering stockade post his sun bleach brown hair fly loose all over his shoulder and he look like goblin in the torch light and as soon as there be lull you could hear his high pitch yell of encouragement to kala nag above the trumpeting and crash and snap of rope and groan of the tether elephant
mael mael kala nag go on go on black snake dant do give he the tusk careful careful hit he hit he mind the post

ah he would shout and the big fight between kala nag and the wild elephant would sway to and fro across the keddah and the old elephant catcher would wipe the sweat out of their eye and find time to nod to little toomai wriggle with joy on the top of the post he do more than wriggle one night he slide down from the post and slip in between the elephant and throw up the loose end of rope which have drop to driver who be try to get purchase on the leg of kick young calf calf always give more trouble than full grow animal kala nag see he catch he in his trunk and hand he up to big toomai who slap he then and there and put he back on the post next morning he give he scold and say be not good brick elephant line and little tent carry enough that thou must need go elephant catch on thy own account little worthless now those foolish hunter whose pay be less than my pay have speak to petersen sahib of the matter little toomai be frighten he do not know much of white man but petersen sahib be the great white man in the world to he he be the head of all the keddah operation the man who catch all the elephant for the government of india and who know more about the way of elephant than any live man what what will happen say little toomai the bad that can happen petersen sahib be madman else why should he go hunt these wild devil he may even require thee to be an elephant catcher to sleep anywhere in these fever fill jungle and at last to be trample to death in the keddah it be well that this nonsense end safely next week the catching be over and we of the plain be send back to our station then we will march on smooth road and forget all this hunting but son be angry that thou shouldst meddle in the business that belong to these dirty assamese jungle folk kala nag will obey none but so must go with he into the keddah
but he be only fight elephant
and he do not help to rope they so sit at my ease as befit mahout,--not mere hunter,--a mahout say and man who get pension at the end of his service be the family of toomai of the elephant to be tread underfoot in the dirt of keddah bad one wicked one worthless son go and wash kala nag and attend to his ear and see that there be no thorn in his foot or else petersen sahib will surely catch thee and make thee wild hunter follower of elephant ’s foot track jungle bear little toomai go off without say word
but he tell kala nag all his grievance while he be examine his foot no matter say little toomai turn up the fringe of kala nag ’s huge right ear they have say my name to petersen sahib and perhaps and perhaps and perhaps who know that be big thorn that have pull out the next few day be spend in get the elephant together in walk the newly catch wild elephant up and down between couple of tame one to prevent they give too much trouble on the downward march to the plain and in take stock of the blanket and rope and thing that have be wear out or lose in the forest petersen sahib come in on his clever she elephant pudmini he have be pay off other camp among the hill for the season be come to an end and there be native clerk sit at table under tree to pay the driver their wage as each man be pay he go back to his elephant and join the line that stand ready to start the catcher and hunter and beater the man of the regular keddah who stay in the jungle year in and year out sit on the back of the elephant that belong to petersen sahib ’s permanent force or lean against the tree with their gun across their arm and make fun of the driver who be go away and laugh when the newly catch elephant break the line and run about big toomai go up to the clerk with little toomai behind he and machua appa the head tracker say in an undertone to friend of his there go one piece of good elephant stuff at least tis pity to send that young jungle cock to molt in the plain now petersen sahib have ear all over he as man must have who listen to the most silent of all live thing the wild elephant he turn where he be lie all along on pudmini ’s back and say what be that do not know of man among the plain driver who have wit enough to rope even dead elephant this be not man but boy he go into the keddah at the last drive and throw barmao there the rope when we be try to get that young calf with the blotch on his shoulder away from his mother machua appa point at little toomai and petersen sahib look and little toomai bow to the earth he throw rope he be small than picket pin little one what be thy name say petersen sahib little toomai be too frightened to speak
but kala nag be behind he and toomai make sign with his hand and the elephant catch he up in his trunk and hold he level with pudmini ’s forehead in front of the great petersen sahib then little toomai cover his face with his hand for he be only child and except where elephant be concern he be just as bashful as child could be oho say petersen sahib smile underneath his mustache and why didst thou teach thy elephant that trick be it to help thee steal green corn from the roof of the house when the ear be put out to dry not green corn protector of the poor,--melon say little toomai and all the man sit about break into roar of laughter most of they have teach their elephant that trick when they be boy little toomai be hang eight foot up in the air
and he wish very much that he be eight foot underground he be toomai my son sahib say big toomai scowl he be very bad boy
and he will end in jail sahib of that have my doubt say petersen sahib boy who can face full keddah at his age do not end in jail see little one here be four annas to spend in sweetmeat because thou hast little head under that great thatch of hair in time thou mayest become hunter too big toomai scowl more than ever remember though that keddah be not good for child to play in petersen sahib go on must never go there sahib ask little toomai with big gasp petersen sahib smile again when thou hast see the elephant dance that be the proper time come to when thou hast see the elephant dance and then will let thee go into all the keddah there be another roar of laughter for that be an old joke among elephant catcher
and it mean just never there be great clear flat place hide away in the forest that be call elephant ball room
but even these be only find by accident and no man have ever see the elephant dance when driver boast of his skill and bravery the other driver say and when didst thou see the elephant dance kala nag put little toomai down and he bow to the earth again and go away with his father and give the silver four anna piece to his mother who be nurse his baby brother and they all be put up on kala nag ’s back and the line of grunt squeal elephant roll down the hill path to the plain it be very lively march on account of the new elephant who give trouble at every ford and need coax or beat every other minute big toomai prod kala nag spitefully for he be very angry but little toomai be too happy to speak petersen sahib have notice he and give he money
so he feel as private soldier would feel if he have be call out of the rank and praise by his commander in chief what do petersen sahib mean by the elephant dance he say at last softly to his mother big toomai hear he and grunt that thou shouldst never be one of these hill buffalo of tracker that be what he mean
oh you in front what be block the way an assamese driver two or three elephant ahead turn round angrily cry bring up kala nag and knock this youngster of mine into good behavior why should petersen sahib have choose to go down with you donkey of the rice field lie your beast alongside toomai and let he prod with his tusk by all the god of the hill these new elephant be possess or else they can smell their companion in the jungle kala nag hit the new elephant in the rib and knock the wind out of he as big toomai
say we have sweep the hill of wild elephant at the last catch it be only your carelessness in drive must keep order along the whole line hear he say the other driver we have sweep the hill ho ho you be very wise
you plain people anyone but mud head who never see the jungle would know that they know that the drive be end for the season therefore all the wild elephant to night will but why should waste wisdom on river turtle what will they do little toomai call out ohe little one art thou there well will tell thee for thou hast cool head they will dance and it behoove thy father who have sweep all the hill of all the elephant to double chain his picket to night what talk be this say big toomai for forty year father and son we have tend elephant and we have never hear such moonshine about dance yes but plainsman who live in hut know only the four wall of his hut well leave thy elephant unshackle tonight and see what come as for their dancing have see the place where bapree bap how many winding have the dihang river here be another ford and we must swim the calf stop still you behind there and in this way talk and wrangle and splash through the river they make their first march to sort of receive camp for the new elephant but they lose their temper long before they get there then the elephant be chain by their hind leg to their big stump of picket and extra rope be fit to the new elephant and the fodder be pile before they and the hill driver go back to petersen sahib through the afternoon light tell the plain driver to be extra careful that night and laugh when the plain driver ask the reason little toomai attend to kala nag ’s supper and as evening fall wander through the camp unspeakably happy in search of tom tom when an indian child ’s heart be full he do not run about and make noise in an irregular fashion he sit down to sort of revel all by himself and little toomai have be speak to by petersen sahib if he have not find what he want believe he would have be ill but the sweetmeat seller in the camp lend he little tom tom drum beat with the flat of the hand and he sit down cross legged before kala nag as the star begin to come out the tom tom in his lap and he thump and he thump and he thump and the more he think of the great honor that have be do to he the more he thump all alone among the elephant fodder there be no tune and no word but the thumping make he happy the new elephant strain at their rope and squeal and trumpet from time to time and he could hear his mother in the camp hut put his small brother to sleep with an old old song about the great god shiv who once tell all the animal what they should eat it be very soothing lullaby and the first verse say shiv who pour the harvest and make the wind to blow sit at the doorway of day of long ago give to each his portion food and toil and fate from the king upon the guddee to the beggar at the gate all thing make he shiva the preserver he make all-- thorn for the camel fodder for the kine and mother ’s heart for sleepy head little son of mine little toomai come in with joyous tunk tunk at the end of each verse till he feel sleepy and stretch himself on the fodder at kala nag ’s side at last the elephant begin to lie down one after another as be their custom till only kala nag at the right of the line be leave stand up and he rock slowly from side to side his ear put forward to listen to the night wind as it blow very slowly across the hill the air be full of all the night noise that take together make one big silence the click of one bamboo stem against the other the rustle of something alive in the undergrowth the scratch and squawk of half wake bird bird be awake in the night much more often than we imagine and the fall of water ever so far away little toomai sleep for some time and when he wake it be brilliant moonlight and kala nag be still stand up with his ear cocked little toomai turn rustle in the fodder and watch the curve of his big back against half the star in heaven and while he watch he hear so far away that it sound no more than pinhole of noise prick through the stillness the hoot toot of wild elephant all the elephant in the line jump up as if they have be shoot and their grunt at last wake the sleep mahout and they come out and drive in the picket peg with big mallet and tighten this rope and knot that till all be quiet one new elephant have nearly grub up his picket and big toomai take off kala nag ’s leg chain and shackle that elephant fore foot to hind foot but slip loop of grass string round kala nag ’s leg and tell he to remember that he be tie fast he know that he and his father and his grandfather have do the very same thing hundred of time before kala nag do not answer to the order by gurgle as he usually do he stand still look out across the moonlight
his head little raise
and his ear spread like fan up to the great fold of the garo hill tend to he if he grow restless in the night say big toomai to little toomai and he go into the hut and sleep little toomai be just go to sleep too when he hear the coir string snap with little tang and kala nag roll out of his picket as slowly and as silently as cloud roll out of the mouth of valley little toomai patter after he barefoote down the road in the moonlight call under his breath kala nag kala nag take with you kala nag the elephant turn without sound take three stride back to the boy in the moonlight put down his trunk swing he up to his neck and almost before little toomai have settle his knee slip into the forest there be one blast of furious trumpeting from the line and then the silence shut down on everything and kala nag begin to move sometimes tuft of high grass wash along his side as wave wash along the side of ship and sometimes cluster of wild pepper vine would scrape along his back or bamboo would creak where his shoulder touch it but between those time he move absolutely without any sound drift through the thick garo forest as though it have be smoke he be go uphill but though little toomai watch the star in the rift of the tree he could not tell in what direction then kala nag reach the crest of the ascent and stop for minute and little toomai could see the top of the tree lie all speckled and furry under the moonlight for mile and mile and the blue white mist over the river in the hollow toomai lean forward and look and he feel that the forest be awake below he awake and alive and crowd big brown fruit eat bat brush past his ear porcupine ’s quill rattle in the thicket and in the darkness between the tree stem he hear hog bear dig hard in the moist warm earth and snuff as it digge then the branch close over his head again and kala nag begin to go down into the valley not quietly this time but as runaway gun go down steep bank in one rush the huge limb move as steadily as piston eight foot to each stride and the wrinkled skin of the elbow point rustle the undergrowth on either side of he rip with noise like torn canvas and the sapling that he heave away right and leave with his shoulder spring back again and bang he on the flank and great trail of creeper all mat together hang from his tusk as he throw his head from side to side and plow out his pathway then little toomai lie himself down close to the great neck lest swing bough should sweep he to the ground and he wish that he be back in the line again the grass begin to get squashy and kala nag ’s foot suck and squelch as he put they down and the night mist at the bottom of the valley chill little toomai there be splash and trample and the rush of run water and kala nag stride through the bed of river feel his way at each step above the noise of the water as it swirl round the elephant ’s leg little toomai could hear more splashing and some trumpet both upstream and down great grunt and angry snorting and all the mist about he seem to be full of rolling wavy shadow he say half aloud his tooth chatter the elephant folk be out tonight it be the dance then kala nag swash out of the water blow his trunk clear and begin another climb but this time he be not alone and he have not to make his path that be make already six foot wide in front of he where the bent jungle grass be try to recover itself and stand up many elephant must have go that way only few minute before little toomai look back and behind he great wild tusker with his little pig ’s eye glow like hot coal be just lift himself out of the misty river then the tree close up again and they go on and up with trumpet and crash and the sound of break branch on every side of they at last kala nag stand still between two tree trunk at the very top of the hill they be part of circle of tree that grow round an irregular space of some three or four acre and in all that space as little toomai could see the ground have be trample down as hard as brick floor some tree grow in the center of the clearing but their bark be rub away and the white wood beneath show all shiny and polished in the patch of moonlight there be creeper hang from the upper branch and the bell of the flower of the creeper great waxy white thing like convolvulus hang down fast asleep but within the limit of the clearing there be not single blade of green nothing but the trample earth the moonlight show it all iron gray except where some elephant stand upon it and their shadow be inky black little toomai look hold his breath with his eye start out of his head and as he look more and more and more elephant swing out into the open from between the tree trunk little toomai could only count up to ten and he count again and again on his finger till he lose count of the ten and his head begin to swim outside the clearing he could hear they crash in the undergrowth as they work their way up the hillside but as soon as they be within the circle of the tree trunk they move like ghost there be white tusked wild male with fallen leaf and nut and twig lie in the wrinkle of their neck and the fold of their ear fat slow footed she elephant with restless little pinky black calf only three or four foot high run under their stomach young elephant with their tusk just begin to show and very proud of they lanky scraggy old maid elephant with their hollow anxious face and trunk like rough bark savage old bull elephant scar from shoulder to flank with great weal and cut of bygone fight and the cake dirt of their solitary mud bath drop from their shoulder and there be one with break tusk and the mark of the full stroke the terrible drawing scrape of tiger ’s claws on his side they be stand head to head or walk to and fro across the ground in couple or rock and sway all by themselves score and score of elephant toomai know that so long as he lie still on kala nag ’s neck nothing would happen to he for even in the rush and scramble of keddah drive wild elephant do not reach up with his trunk and drag man off the neck of tame elephant and these elephant be not think of man that night once they start and put their ear forward when they hear the chinking of leg iron in the forest but it be pudmini petersen sahib ’s pet elephant her chain snap short off grunt snuffle up the hillside she must have break her picket and come straight from petersen sahib ’s camp and little toomai see another elephant one that he do not know with deep rope gall on his back and breast he too must have run away from some camp in the hill about at last there be no sound of any more elephant move in the forest and kala nag roll out from his station between the tree and go into the middle of the crowd cluck and gurgle and all the elephant begin to talk in their own tongue and to move about still lie down little toomai look down upon score and score of broad back and wag ear and toss trunk and little rolling eye he hear the click of tusk as they cross other tusk by accident and the dry rustle of trunk twin together and the chafing of enormous side and shoulder in the crowd and the incessant flick and hissh of the great tail then cloud come over the moon and he sit in black darkness but the quiet steady hustling and push and gurgle go on just the same he know that there be elephant all round kala nag and that there be no chance of back he out of the assembly so he set his tooth and shiver in keddah at least there be torchlight and shout
but here he be all alone in the dark and once trunk come up and touch he on the knee then an elephant trumpet and they all take it up for five or ten terrible second the dew from the tree above spatter down like rain on the unseen back and dull booming noise begin not very loud at first and little toomai could not tell what it be but it grow and grow and kala nag lift up one forefoot and then the other and bring they down on the ground one two one two as steadily as trip hammer the elephant be stamp all together now
and it sound like war drum beat at the mouth of cave the dew fall from the tree till there be no more leave to fall and the boom go on and the ground rock and shiver and little toomai put his hand up to his ear to shut out the sound
but it be all one gigantic jar that run through he this stamp of hundred of heavy foot on the raw earth once or twice he could feel kala nag and all the other surge forward few stride
and the thumping would change to the crush sound of juicy green thing be bruise
but in minute or two the boom of foot on hard earth begin again tree be creak and groan somewhere near he he put out his arm and feel the bark but kala nag move forward still tramp
and he could not tell where he be in the clearing there be no sound from the elephant except once when two or three little calf squeak together then he hear thump and shuffle and the boom go on it must have last fully two hour and little toomai ache in every nerve
but he know by the smell of the night air that the dawn be come the morning break in one sheet of pale yellow behind the green hill and the boom stop with the first ray as though the light have be an order before little toomai have get the ringing out of his head before even he have shift his position there be not an elephant in sight except kala nag pudmini and the elephant with the rope gall and there be neither sign nor rustle nor whisper down the hillside to show where the other have go little toomai star again and again the clearing as he remember it have grow in the night more tree stand in the middle of it but the undergrowth and the jungle grass at the side have be roll back little toomai star once more now he understand the trampling the elephant have stamp out more room have stamp the thick grass and juicy cane to trash the trash into sliver the sliver into tiny fiber and the fiber into hard earth wah say little toomai and his eye be very heavy kala nag my lord let we keep by pudmini and go to petersen sahib ’s camp or shall drop from thy neck the third elephant watch the two go away snort wheel round and take his own path he may have belong to some little native king ’s establishment fifty or sixty or hundred mile away two hour later as petersen sahib be eat early breakfast his elephant who have be double chain that night begin to trumpet and pudmini mire to the shoulder with kala nag very footsore shamble into the camp
little toomai ’s face be gray and pinch and his hair be full of leaf and drench with dew but he try to salute petersen sahib and cry faintly the dance the elephant dance have see it and die as kala nag sit down he slide off his neck in dead faint but since native child have no nerve worth speak of in two hour he be lie very contentedly in petersen sahib ’s hammock with petersen sahib ’s shoot coat under his head and glass of warm milk little brandy with dash of quinine inside of he and while the old hairy scarred hunter of the jungle sit three deep before he look at he as though he be spirit he tell his tale in short word as child will and wound up with now if lie in one word send man to see and they will find that the elephant folk have trample down more room in their dance room and they will find ten and ten and many time ten track lead to that dance room they make more room with their foot have see it kala nag take and see also kala nag be very leg weary little toomai lie back and sleep all through the long afternoon and into the twilight and while he sleep petersen sahib and machua appa follow the track of the two elephant for fifteen mile across the hill petersen sahib have spend eighteen year in catch elephant and he have only once before find such dance place machua appa have no need to look twice at the clearing to see what have be do there or to scratch with his toe in the pack rammed earth the child speak truth say he all this be do last night and have count seventy track cross the river see sahib where pudmini ’s leg iron cut the bark of that tree
yes she be there too they look at one another and up and down and they wonder for the way of elephant be beyond the wit of any man black or white to fathom forty year and five say machua appa have follow my lord the elephant but never have hear that any child of man have see what this child have see by all the god of the hill it be what can we say
and he shake his head when they get back to camp it be time for the evening meal petersen sahib eat alone in his tent
but he give order that the camp should have two sheep and some fowl as well as double ration of flour and rice and salt for he know that there would be feast big toomai have come up hotfoot from the camp in the plain to search for his son and his elephant and now that he have find they he look at they as though he be afraid of they both and there be feast by the blaze campfire in front of the line of picket elephant and little toomai be the hero of it all and the big brown elephant catcher the tracker and driver and roper and the man who know all the secret of break the wild elephant pass he from one to the other and they mark his forehead with blood from the breast of newly kill jungle cock to show that he be forester initiate and free of all the jungle and at last when the flame die down and the red light of the log make the elephant look as though they have be dip in blood too machua appa the head of all the driver of all the keddah machua appa petersen sahib ’s other self who have never see make road in forty year machua appa who be so great that he have no other name than machua appa,--leape to his foot with little toomai hold high in the air above his head and shout listen my brother listen too you my lord in the line there for machua appa be speak this little one shall no more be call little toomai but toomai of the elephant as his great grandfather be call before he what never man have see he have see through the long night and the favor of the elephant folk and of the god of the jungle be with he he shall become great tracker he shall become great than even machua appa he shall follow the new trail and the stale trail and the mixed trail with clear eye he shall take no harm in the keddah when he run under their belly to rope the wild tusker and if he slip before the foot of the charge bull elephant the bull elephant shall know who he be and shall not crush he my lord in the chains,”--he whirl up the line of pickets--“here be the little one that have see your dance in your hide places,--the sight that never man see give he honor
my lord salaam karo
my child make your salute to toomai of the elephant gunga pershad ahaa hira guj birchi guj kuttar guj ahaa pudmini,--thou hast see he at the dance and thou too kala nag my pearl among elephants!--ahaa to toomai of the elephant and at that last wild yell the whole line fling up their trunk till the tip touch their forehead and break out into the full salute the crash trumpet peal that only the viceroy of india hear the salaamut of the keddah
but it be all for the sake of little toomai who have see what never man have see before the dance of the elephant at night and alone in the heart of the garo hill shiv and the grasshopper the song that toomai ’s mother sing to the baby shiv who pour the harvest and make the wind to blow sit at the doorway of day of long ago give to each his portion food and toil and fate from the king upon the guddee to the beggar at the gate all thing make he shiva the preserver he make all,-- thorn for the camel fodder for the kine and mother ’s heart for sleepy head little son of mine wheat he give to rich folk millet to the poor break scrap for holy man that beg from door to door battle to the tiger carrion to the kite and rag and bone to wicked wolf without the wall at night
naught he find too lofty none he see too low-- parbati
beside he watch they come and go think to cheat her husband turn shiv to jest-- steal the little grasshopper and hide it in her breast
so she trick he shiva the preserver turn and see tall be the camel heavy be the kine but this be least of little thing little son of mine when the dole be end
laughingly she say master of million mouth be not one unfed laugh shiv make answer all have have their part even he the little one hide neath thy heart from her breast she pluck it parbati the thief see the least of little thing gnaw new grown leaf see and fear and wonder make prayer to shiv who hath surely give meat to all that live all thing make he shiva the preserver he make all,-- thorn for the camel fodder for the kine and mother ’s heart for sleepy head little son of mine her majesty ’s servant you can work it out by fraction or by simple rule of three but the way of tweedle dum be not the way of tweedle dee you can twist it you can turn it you can plait it till you drop but the way of pilly winky not the way of winkie pop it have be rain heavily for one whole month rain on camp of thirty thousand man and thousand of camel elephant horse bullock and mule all gather together at place call rawal pindi to be review by the viceroy of india he be receive visit from the amir of afghanistan wild king of very wild country the amir have bre with he for bodyguard eight hundred man and horse who have never see camp or locomotive before in their life savage man and savage horse from somewhere at the back of central asia every night mob of these horse would be sure to break their heel rope and stampede up and down the camp through the mud in the dark or the camel would break loose and run about and fall over the rope of the tent
and you can imagine how pleasant that be for man try to go to sleep my tent lie far away from the camel line and think it be safe but one night man pop his head in and shout get out quick they ’re come my tent go know who they be so put on my boot and waterproof and scuttle out into the slush little vixen my fox terrier go out through the other side
and then there be roar and grunt and bubble and see the tent cave in as the pole snap and begin to dance about like mad ghost camel have blunder into it and wet and angry as be could not help laugh then run on because do not know how many camel might have get loose and before long be out of sight of the camp plow my way through the mud at last fall over the tail end of gun and by that know be somewhere near the artillery line where the cannon be stack at night as do not want to plowter about any more in the drizzle and the dark put my waterproof over the muzzle of one gun and make sort of wigwam with two or three rammer that find and lie along the tail of another gun wonder where vixen have get to and where might be just as be get ready to go to sleep hear jingle of harness and grunt and mule pass shake his wet ear he belong to screw gun battery for could hear the rattle of the strap and ring and chain and thing on his saddle pad the screw gun be tiny little cannon make in two piece that be screw together when the time come to use they they be take up mountain anywhere that mule can find road and they be very useful for fight in rocky country behind the mule there be camel with his big soft foot squelch and slip in the mud and his neck bob to and fro like stray hen ’s luckily know enough of beast language not wild beast language but camp beast language of course from the native to know what he be say he must have be the one that flop into my tent for he call to the mule what shall do where shall go have fight with white thing that wave
and it take stick and hit on the neck that be my broken tent pole and be very glad to know it shall we run on
oh it be you say the mule you and your friend that have be disturb the camp all right you ’ll be beat for this in the morning
but may as well give you something on account now hear the harness jingle as the mule back and catch the camel two kick in the rib that ring like drum another time he say you ’ll know well than to run through mule battery at night shout thief and fire sit down and keep your silly neck quiet the camel double up camel fashion like two foot rule and sit down whimpering there be regular beat of hoofs in the darkness and big troop horse canter up as steadily as though he be on parade jump gun tail and land close to the mule it disgraceful he say blow out his nostril those camel have racket through our line again the third time this week how horse to keep his condition if he be not allow to sleep who here the breech piece mule of number two gun of the first screw battery say the mule and the other ’s one of your friend he ’s wake up too who be you number fifteen troop ninth lancers dick cunliffe ’s horse stand over little there
oh beg your pardon say the mule it too dark to see much be not these camel too sickening for anything walk out of my line to get little peace and quiet here my lord say the camel humbly we dream bad dream in the night and we be very much afraid be only baggage camel of the 39th native infantry and be not as brave as you be my lord then why do not you stay and carry baggage for the 39th native infantry instead of run all round the camp say the mule they be such very bad dream say the camel be sorry what be that shall we run on again sit down say the mule or you ’ll snap your long stick leg between the gun he cock one ear and listen he say gun bullock on my word you and your friend have wake the camp very thoroughly it take good deal of prod to put up gun bullock hear chain drag along the ground and yoke of the great sulky white bullock that drag the heavy siege gun when the elephant will not go any near to the firing come shoulder along together and almost step on the chain be another battery mule call wildly for billy that one of our recruit say the old mule to the troop horse he ’s call for here youngster stop squeal the dark never hurt anybody yet the gun bullock lie down together and begin chew the cud but the young mule huddle close to billy he say fearful and horrible billy they come into our line while we be asleep d’you think they ’ll kill we ve very great mind to give you number one kicking say billy the idea of fourteen hand mule with your training disgrace the battery before this gentleman gently gently say the troop horse remember they be always like this to begin with the first time ever see man it be in australia when be three year old run for half day and if ’d see camel should have be run still nearly all our horse for the english cavalry be bre to india from australia and be break in by the trooper themselves true enough say billy stop shake youngster the first time they put the full harness with all its chain on my back stand on my foreleg and kick every bit of it off have not learn the real science of kicking then but the battery say they have never see anything like it
but this be not harness or anything that jingle say the young mule you know do not mind that now billy it be thing like tree and they fall up and down the line and bubble and my head rope break and could not find my driver and could not find you billy so run off with with these gentleman h’m say billy as soon as hear the camel be loose come away on my own account when battery screw gun mule call gun bullock gentleman he must be very badly shake up who be you fellow on the ground there the gun bullock roll their cud and answer both together the seventh yoke of the first gun of the big gun battery we be asleep when the camel come but when we be trample on we get up and walk away it be well to lie quiet in the mud than to be disturb on good bedding we tell your friend here that there be nothing to be afraid of
but he know so much that he think otherwise they go on chew that come of be afraid say billy you get laugh at by gun bullock hope you like it young un the young mule ’s tooth snap and hear he say something about not be afraid of any beefy old bullock in the world
but the bullock only click their horn together and go on chew now do not be angry after you ve be afraid that the bad kind of cowardice say the troop horse anybody can be forgive for be scared in the night think if they see thing they do not understand we ve break out of our picket again and again four hundred and fifty of we just because new recruit get to tell tale of whip snake at home in australia till we be scared to death of the loose end of our head rope that all very well in camp say billy not above stampede myself for the fun of the thing when have not be out for day or two but what do you do on active service
oh that quite another set of new shoe say the troop horse dick cunliffe on my back then and drive his knee into and all have to do be to watch where be put my foot and to keep my hind leg well under and be bridle wise what bridle wise say the young mule by the blue gum of the back block snort the troop horse do you mean to say that you be not teach to be bridle wise in your business how can you do anything unless you can spin round at once when the rein be press on your neck it mean life or death to your man and of course that life and death to you get round with your hind leg under you the instant you feel the rein on your neck if you have not room to swing round rear up little and come round on your hind leg that be bridle wise we be not teach that way say billy the mule stiffly we ’re teach to obey the man at our head step off when he say so and step in when he say so suppose it come to the same thing now with all this fine fancy business and rearing which must be very bad for your hock what do you do that depend say the troop horse generally have to go in among lot of yell hairy man with knife long shiny knife bad than the farrier ’s knife and have to take care that dick ’s boot be just touch the next man ’s boot without crush it can see dick ’s lance to the right of my right eye and know safe should not care to be the man or horse that stand up to dick and when we ’re in hurry do not the knife hurt say the young mule well get one cut across the chest once but that be not dick ’s fault-- lot should have care whose fault it be if it hurt say the young mule you must say the troop horse if you do not trust your man you may as well run away at once that what some of our horse do and do not blame they as be say it be not dick ’s fault the man be lie on the ground and stretch myself not to tread on he and he slash up at next time have to go over man lie down shall step on he hard h’m
say billy it sound very foolish knife be dirty thing at any time the proper thing to do be to climb up mountain with well balance saddle hang on by all four foot and your ear too and creep and crawl and wriggle along till you come out hundred of foot above anyone else on ledge where there just room enough for your hoofs then you stand still and keep quiet never ask man to hold your head young un keep quiet while the gun be be put together
and then you watch the little poppy shell drop down into the tree top ever so far below do not you ever trip say the troop horse they say that when mule trip you can split hen ’s ear say billy now and again perhaps badly pack saddle will upset mule
but it very seldom wish could show you our business it beautiful why it take three year to find out what the man be drive at the science of the thing be never to show up against the sky line because if you do you may get fire at remember that young un always keep hide as much as possible even if you have to go mile out of your way lead the battery when it come to that sort of climb fire at without the chance of run into the people who be fire say the troop horse think hard could not stand that should want to charge with dick
oh no you would not you know that as soon as the gun be in position they ’ll do all the charging that scientific and neat but knife pah the baggage camel have be bob his head to and fro for some time past anxious to get word in edgewise then hear he say as he clear his throat nervously have fight little but not in that climbing way or that running way now you mention it say billy you do not look as though you be make for climbing or run much well how be it old hay bale the proper way say the camel we all sit down--
oh my crupper and breastplate say the troop horse under his breath sit down we sit down hundred of we the camel go on in big square and the man pile our pack and saddle outside the square and they fire over our back the man do on all side of the square what sort of man any man that come along say the troop horse they teach we in rid school to lie down and let our master fire across we but dick cunliffe be the only man ’d trust to do that it tickle my girth and besides can not see with my head on the ground what do it matter who fire across you say the camel there be plenty of man and plenty of other camel close by and great many cloud of smoke be not frightened then sit still and wait and yet say billy you dream bad dream and upset the camp at night well well before ’d lie down not to speak of sit down and let man fire across my heel and his head would have something to say to each other do you ever hear anything so awful as that there be long silence and then one of the gun bullock lift up his big head and say this be very foolish indeed there be only one way of fight
oh go on say billy please do not mind suppose you fellow fight stand on your tail only one way say the two together they must have be twin this be that way to put all twenty yoke of we to the big gun as soon as two tail trumpet two tail be camp slang for the elephant what do two tail trumpet for say the young mule to show that he be not go any near to the smoke on the other side two tail be great coward then we tug the big gun all together heya hullah we do not climb like cat nor run like calf we go across the level plain twenty yoke of we till we be unyoke again and we graze while the big gun talk across the plain to some town with mud wall and piece of the wall fall out and the dust go up as though many cattle be come home
and you choose that time for graze say the young mule that time or any other eat be always good we eat till we be yoke up again and tug the gun back to where two tail be wait for it sometimes there be big gun in the city that speak back and some of we be kill
and then there be all the more grazing for those that be leave this be fate none the less two tail be great coward that be the proper way to fight we be brother from hapur our father be sacred bull of shiva we have speak well ve certainly learn something tonight say the troop horse do you gentleman of the screw gun battery feel inclined to eat when you be be fire at with big gun and two tail be behind you about as much as we feel inclined to sit down and let man sprawl all over we or run into people with knife never hear such stuff mountain ledge well balanced load driver you can trust to let you pick your own way and your mule but the other thing no say billy with stamp of his foot of course say the troop horse everyone be not make in the same way and can quite see that your family on your father ’s side would fail to understand great many thing never you mind my family on my father ’s side say billy angrily for every mule hat to be remind that his father be donkey my father be southern gentleman and he could pull down and bite and kick into rag every horse he come across remember that you big brown brumby brumby mean wild horse without any breeding imagine the feeling of sunol if car horse call she skate
and you can imagine how the australian horse feel see the white of his eye glitter in the dark see here you son of an import malaga jackass he say between his tooth ’d have you know that relate on my mother ’s side to carbine winner of the melbourne cup and where come from we be not accustomed to be ride over roughshod by any parrot mouthed pig head mule in pop gun pea shooter battery be you ready on your hind leg squeal billy they both rear up face each other and be expect furious fight when gurgly rumbly voice call out of the darkness to the right--“children what be you fight about there be quiet both beast drop down with snort of disgust for neither horse nor mule can bear to listen to an elephant ’s voice it two tail say the troop horse can not stand he tail at each end be not fair my feeling exactly say billy crowd into the troop horse for company we ’re very alike in some thing suppose we ve inherit they from our mother say the troop horse it not worth quarrel about two tail be you tie up yes say two tail with laugh all up his trunk picket for the night ve hear what you fellow have be say but do not be afraid not come over the bullock and the camel say half aloud afraid of two tail what nonsense and the bullock go on we be sorry that you hear
but it be true two tail why be you afraid of the gun when they fire well say two tail rub one hind leg against the other exactly like little boy say poem do not quite know whether you ’d understand we do not
but we have to pull the gun say the bullock know it and know you be good deal braver than you think you be
but it different with my battery captain call pachydermatous anachronism the other day that another way of fighting suppose say billy who be recover his spirit you do not know what that mean of course but do it mean betwixt and between and that be just where be can see inside my head what will happen when shell burst and you bullock can not can say the troop horse at least little bit try not to think about it can see more than you and do think about it know there great deal of to take care of and know that nobody know how to cure when sick all they can do be to stop my driver ’s pay till get well and can not trust my driver ah say the troop horse that explain it can trust dick you could put whole regiment of dick on my back without make feel any well know just enough to be uncomfortable and not enough to go on in spite of it we do not understand say the bullock know you do not not talk to you you do not know what blood be we do say the bullock it be red stuff that soak into the ground and smell the troop horse give kick and bind and snort do not talk of it he say can smell it now just think of it it make want to run when have not dick on my back
but it be not here say the camel and the bullock why be you so stupid it vile stuff say billy do not want to run but do not want to talk about it there you be say two tail wave his tail to explain yes we have be here all night say the bullock two tail stamp his foot till the iron ring on it jingle
oh not talk to you you can not see inside your head we see out of our four eye say the bullock we see straight in front of we if could do that and nothing else you would not be need to pull the big gun at all if be like my captain he can see thing inside his head before the firing begin and he shake all over but he know too much to run away if be like he could pull the gun but if be as wise as all that should never be here should be king in the forest as use to be sleep half the day and bath when like have not have good bath for month that all very fine say billy but give thing long name do not make it any well h’sh say the troop horse think understand what two tail mean you ’ll understand well in minute say two tail angrily now you just explain to why you do not like this he begin trumpet furiously at the top of his trumpet stop that say billy and the troop horse together and could hear they stamp and shiver an elephant ’s trumpeting be always nasty especially on dark night sha not stop say two tail will not you explain that please hhrrmph then he stop suddenly and hear little whimper in the dark and know that vixen have find at last she know as well as do that if there be one thing in the world the elephant be more afraid of than another it be little bark dog so she stop to bully two tail in his picket and yap round his big foot two tail shuffle and squeak go away little dog he say do not snuff at my ankle or ’ll kick at you good little dog nice little doggie then go home you yelp little beast
oh why do not someone take she away she ’ll bite in minute seem to say billy to the troop horse that our friend two tail be afraid of most thing now if have full meal for every dog ve kick across the parade ground should be as fat as two tail nearly whistle and vixen run up to muddy all over and lick my nose and tell long tale about hunt for all through the camp never let she know that understand beast talk or she would have take all sort of liberty
so button she into the breast of my overcoat and two tail shuffle and stamp and growl to himself most extraordinary he say it run in our family now where have that nasty little beast go to hear he feel about with his trunk we all seem to be affect in various way he go on blow his nose now you gentleman be alarmed believe when trumpet not alarm exactly say the troop horse
but it make feel as though have hornet where my saddle ought to be do not begin again frightened of little dog and the camel here be frighten by bad dream in the night it be very lucky for we that we have not all get to fight in the same way say the troop horse what want to know say the young mule who have be quiet for long time--“what want to know be why we have to fight at all because we ’re tell to say the troop horse with snort of contempt order say billy the mule and his tooth snap hukm hai it be an order say the camel with gurgle and two tail and the bullock repeat hukm hai
but who give the order say the recruit mule the man who walk at your head or sit on your back or hold the nose rope or twist your tail say billy and the troop horse and the camel and the bullock one after the other but who give they the order now you want to know too much young un say billy
and that be one way of get kick all you have to do be to obey the man at your head and ask no question he quite right say two tail can not always obey because betwixt and between but billy right obey the man next to you who give the order or you ’ll stop all the battery besides get thrash the gun bullock get up to go morning be come they say we will go back to our line it be true that we only see out of our eye and we be not very clever
but still we be the only people to night who have not be afraid good night you brave people nobody answer and the troop horse say to change the conversation where that little dog dog mean man somewhere about here be yap vixen under the gun tail with my man you big blunder beast of camel you you upset our tent my man
very angry phew say the bullock he must be white of course he be say vixen do you suppose look after by black bullock driver huah

and we ’ll go back to our line good night australia see you on parade to morrow suppose good night old hay bale!--try to control your feeling will not you good night two tail if you pass we on the ground tomorrow do not trumpet it spoil our formation billy the mule stump off with the swaggering limp of an old campaigner as the troop horse ’s head come nuzzle into my breast and give he biscuit while vixen who be most conceited little dog tell he fib about the score of horse that she and keep come to the parade to morrow in my dog cart she say where will you be on the left hand of the second squadron set the time for all my troop little lady he say politely now must go back to dick my tail all muddy

that afternoon and vixen
and have good place close to the viceroy and the amir of afghanistan with high big black hat of astrakhan wool and the great diamond star in the center the first part of the review be all sunshine and the regiment go by in wave upon wave of leg all move together and gun all in line till our eye grow dizzy then the cavalry come up to the beautiful cavalry canter of bonnie dundee and vixen cock her ear where she sit on the dog cart the second squadron of the lancer shoot by and there be the troop horse with his tail like spin silk his head pull into his breast one ear forward and one back set the time for all his squadron his leg go as smoothly as waltz music then the big gun come by and see two tail and two other elephant harness in line to forty pounder siege gun while twenty yoke of oxen walk behind the seventh pair have new yoke and they look rather stiff and tired last come the screw gun and billy the mule carry himself as though he command all the troop and his harness be oil and polished till it wink give cheer all by myself for billy the mule
but he never look right or leave the rain begin to fall again and for while it be too misty to see what the troop be do they have make big half circle across the plain and be spread out into line that line grow and grow and grow till it be three quarter of mile long from wing to wing one solid wall of man horse and gun then it come on straight toward the viceroy and the amir and as it get near the ground begin to shake like the deck of steamer when the engine be go fast unless you have be there you can not imagine what frightening effect this steady come down of troop have on the spectator even when they know it be only review look at the amir up till then he have not show the shadow of sign of astonishment or anything else
but now his eye begin to get big and big and he pick up the rein on his horse ’s neck and look behind he for minute it seem as though he be go to draw his sword and slash his way out through the english man and woman in the carriage at the back then the advance stop dead the ground stand still the whole line salute and thirty band begin to play all together that be the end of the review and the regiment go off to their camp in the rain and an infantry band strike up with-- the animal go in two by two hurrah the animal go in two by two the elephant and the battery mul
and they all get into the ark for to get out of the rain then hear an old grizzled long haired central asian chief who have come down with the amir ask question of native officer now say he in what manner be this wonderful thing do and the officer answer an order be give
and they obey but be the beast as wise as the man say the chief they obey as the man do mule horse elephant or bullock he obey his driver and the driver his sergeant and the sergeant his lieutenant and the lieutenant his captain and the captain his major and the major his colonel and the colonel his brigadier command three regiment and the brigadier the general who obey the viceroy who be the servant of the empress thus it be do would it be so in afghanistan say the chief for there we obey only our own will and for that reason say the native officer twirl his mustache your amir whom you do not obey must come here and take order from our viceroy parade song of the camp animal elephant of the gun team we lend to alexander the strength of hercules the wisdom of our forehead the cunning of our knee we bow our neck to service they ne’er be loose again,-- make way there way for the ten foot team of the forty pounder train gun bullock those hero in their harness avoid cannon ball and what they know of powder upset they one and all then we come into action and tug the gun again-- make way there way for the twenty yoke of the forty pounder train cavalry horse by the brand on my shoulder the fine of tune be play by the lancer hussar and dragoon and it sweet than stable or water to me-- the cavalry canter of bonnie dundee then feed we and break we and handle and groom and give we good rider and plenty of room and launch we in column of squadron and see the way of the war horse to bonnie dundee screw gun mule as and my companion be scramble up hill the path be lose in roll stone
but we go forward still for we can wriggle and climb my lad and turn up everywhere

good hunting!--loose the rain that break our water truce the law of the jungle which be by far the old law in the world have arrange for almost every kind of accident that may befall the jungle people till now its code be as perfect as time and custom can make it you will remember that mowgli spend great part of his life in the seeonee wolf pack learn the law from baloo the brown bear and it be baloo who tell he when the boy grow impatient at the constant order that the law be like the giant creeper because it drop across every one back and no one could escape when thou hast live as long as have little brother thou wilt see how all the jungle obey at least one law and that will be no pleasant sight say baloo this talk go in at one ear and out at the other for boy who spend his life eat and sleep do not worry about anything till it actually star he in the face but one year baloo ’s word come true and mowgli see all the jungle work under the law it begin when the winter rain fail almost entirely and ikki the porcupine meet mowgli in bamboo thicket tell he that the wild yam be dry up now everybody know that ikki be ridiculously fastidious in his choice of food and will eat nothing but the very good and ripe so mowgli laugh and say what be that to not much now say ikki rattle his quill in stiff uncomfortable way but later we shall see be there any more diving into the deep rock pool below the bee rock little brother no the foolish water be go all away and do not wish to break my head say mowgli who in those day be quite sure that he know as much as any five of the jungle people put together that be thy loss small crack might let in some wisdom ikki duck quickly to prevent mowgli from pull his nose bristle and mowgli tell baloo what ikki have say baloo look very grave and mumble half to himself if be alone would change my hunting ground now before the other begin to think and yet hunt among strange end in fighting and they might hurt the man cub we must wait and see how the mohwa bloom that spring the mohwa tree that baloo be so fond of never flower the greeny cream colour waxy blossom be heat kill before they be bear and only few bad smell petal come down when he stand on his hind leg and shake the tree then inch by inch the untempered heat creep into the heart of the jungle turn it yellow brown and at last black the green growth in the side of the ravine burn up to broken wire and curl film of dead stuff the hide pool sink down and cake over keep the last least footmark on their edge as if it have be cast in iron the juicy stem creeper fall away from the tree they cling to and die at their foot the bamboo wither clank when the hot wind blow and the moss peel off the rock deep in the jungle till they be as bare and as hot as the quivering blue boulder in the bed of the stream the bird and the monkey people go north early in the year for they know what be come and the deer and the wild pig break far away to the perish field of the village die sometimes before the eye of man too weak to kill they chil the kite stay and grow fat for there be great deal of carrion and evening after evening he bring the news to the beast too weak to force their way to fresh hunting ground that the sun be kill the jungle for three day flight in every direction mowgli who have never know what real hunger mean fall back on stale honey three year old scrap out of deserted rock hive honey black as sloe and dusty with dry sugar he hunt too for deep boring grub under the bark of the tree and rob the wasp of their new brood all the game in the jungle be no more than skin and bone and bagheera could kill thrice in night and hardly get full meal but the want of water be the bad for though the jungle people drink seldom they must drink deep and the heat go on and on and suck up all the moisture till at last the main channel of the waingunga be the only stream that carry trickle of water between its dead bank and when hathi the wild elephant who live for hundred year and more see long lean blue ridge of rock show dry in the very centre of the stream he know that he be look at the peace rock and then and there he lift up his trunk and proclaim the water truce as his father before he have proclaim it fifty year ago the deer wild pig and buffalo take up the cry hoarsely and chil the kite fly in great circle far and wide whistle and shriek the warning by the law of the jungle it be death to kill at the drinking place when once the water truce have be declare the reason of this be that drinking come before eat every one in the jungle can scramble along somehow when only game be scarce but water be water and when there be but one source of supply all hunt stop while the jungle people go there for their need in good season when water be plentiful those who come down to drink at the waingunga or anywhere else for that matter do so at the risk of their life and that risk make no small part of the fascination of the night ’s do to move down so cunningly that never leaf stir to wade knee deep in the roar shallow that drown all noise from behind to drink look backward over one shoulder every muscle ready for the first desperate bind of keen terror to roll on the sandy margin and return wet muzzle and well plump out to the admire herd be thing that all tall antlered young buck take delight in precisely because they know that at any moment bagheera or shere khan might leap upon they and bear they down but now all that life and death fun be end and the jungle people come up starve and weary to the shrunken river,--tiger bear deer buffalo and pig all together,--drank the fouled water and hang above they too exhausted to move off the deer and the pig have tramp all day in search of something well than dry bark and wither leaf the buffalo have find no wallow to be cool in and no green crop to steal the snake have leave the jungle and come down to the river in the hope of find stray frog they curl round wet stone and never offer to strike when the nose of root pig dislodge they the river turtle have long ago be kill by bagheera clever of hunter and the fish have bury themselves deep in the dry mud only the peace rock lie across the shallow like long snake and the little tired ripple hiss as they dry on its hot side it be here that mowgli come nightly for the cool and the companionship the most hungry of his enemy would hardly have care for the boy then his naked hide make he seem more lean and wretched than any of his fellow his hair be bleach to tow colour by the sun his rib stand out like the rib of basket and the lump on his knee and elbow where he be use to track on all four give his shrunken limb the look of knotted grass stem but his eye under his matted forelock be cool and quiet for bagheera be his adviser in this time of trouble and tell he to go quietly hunt slowly and never on any account to lose his temper it be an evil time say the black panther one furnace hot evening
but it will go if we can live till the end be thy stomach full man cub there be stuff in my stomach but get no good of it think you bagheera the rain have forget we and will never come again not we shall see the mohwa in blossom yet and the little fawn all fat with new grass come down to the peace rock and hear the news on my back little brother this be no time to carry weight can still stand alone
but indeed we be no fatted bullock
we two bagheera look along his ragged dusty flank and whisper last night kill bullock under the yoke so low be bring that think should not have dare to spring if he have be loose mowgli laugh
yes we be great hunter now say he be very bold to eat grub and the two come down together through the crackling undergrowth to the river bank and the lace work of shoal that run out from it in every direction the water can not live long say baloo join they look across yonder be trail like the road of man on the level plain of the farth bank the stiff jungle grass have die stand and die have mummie the beat track of the deer and the pig all head toward the river have strip that colourless plain with dusty gully drive through the ten foot grass and early as it be each long avenue be full of first comer hasten to the water you could hear the doe and fawn cough in the snuff like dust up stream at the bend of the sluggish pool round the peace rock and warden of the water truce stand hathi the wild elephant with his son gaunt and gray in the moonlight rock to and fro always rock
