below he little be the vanguard of the deer below these again the pig and the wild buffalo and on the opposite bank where the tall tree come down to the water ’s edge be the place set apart for the eater of flesh the tiger the wolf the panther the bear and the other we be under one law indeed say bagheera wad into the water and look across at the line of click horn and start eye where the deer and the pig push each other to and fro good hunting all you of my blood he add lie own at full length one flank thrust out of the shallow and then between his tooth but for that which be the law it would be very good hunting the quick spread ear of the deer catch the last sentence and frightened whisper run along the rank the truce remember the truce peace there peace gurgle hathi the wild elephant the truce hold bagheera this be no time to talk of hunting who should know well than bagheera answer roll his yellow eye up stream be an eater of turtle fisher of frog would could get good from chew branch we wish so very greatly bleat young fawn who have only be bear that spring and do not at all like it wretched as the jungle people be even hathi could not help chuckle while mowgli lie on his elbow in the warm water laugh aloud and beat up the scum with his foot well speak little bud horn bagheera purr when the truce end that shall be remember in thy favour
and he look keenly through the darkness to make sure of recognise the fawn again gradually the talking spread up and down the drinking place one could hear the scuffling snort pig ask for more room the buffalo grunt among themselves as they lurch out across the sand bar and the deer tell pitiful story of their long foot sore wander in quest of food now and again they ask some question of the eater of flesh across the river but all the news be bad and the roar hot wind of the jungle come and go between the rock and the rattle branch and scatter twig and dust on the water the man folk too they die beside their plough say young sambhur pass three between sunset and night they lie still and their bullock with they we also shall lie still in little the river have fall since last night say baloo hathi hast thou ever see the like of this drought it will pass it will pass say hathi squirt water along his back and side we have one here that can not endure long say baloo
and he look toward the boy he love say mowgli indignantly sit up in the water have no long fur to cover my bone
but but if thy hide be take off baloo---- hathi shake all over at the idea and baloo say severely man cub that be not seemly to tell teacher of the law never have be see without my hide nay mean no harm baloo but only that thou art as it be like the cocoanut in the husk and be the same cocoanut all naked now that brown husk of thine---- mowgli be sit cross legged and explain thing with his forefinger in his usual way when bagheera put out paddy paw and pull he over backward into the water bad and bad say the black panther as the boy rise splutter first baloo be to be skin and now he be cocoanut be careful that he do not do what the ripe cocoanut do and what be
that say
mowgli off his guard for the minute though that be one of the old catch in the jungle break thy head say bagheera quietly pull he under again it be not good to make jest of thy teacher say the bear when mowgli have be duck for the third time not good what would ye have that naked thing run to and fro make monkey jest of those who have once be good hunter and pull the good of we by the whisker for sport this be shere khan the lame tiger limp down to the water he wait little to enjoy the sensation he make among the deer on the opposite to lap growl the jungle have become whelp ground for naked cub now look at man cub mowgli look star rather as insolently as he know how and in minute
shere khan turn away uneasily man cub this and man cub that he rumble go on with his drink the cub be neither man nor cub or he would have be afraid next season shall have to beg his leave for drink augrh that may come too say bagheera look he steadily between the eye that may come too faugh shere khan!--what new shame hast thou bring here the lame tiger have dip his chin and jowl in the water and dark oily streak be float from it down stream man say shere khan coolly kill an hour since he go on purr and growl to himself the line of beast shake and waver to and fro and whisper go up that grow to cry man man he have kill man then all look towards hathi the wild elephant
but he seem not to hear hathi never do anything till the time come and that be one of the reason why he live so long at such season as this to kill man be no other game afoot say bagheera scornfully draw himself out of the tainted water and shake each paw cat fashion as he do so kill for choice not for food the horrified whisper begin again and hathi ’s watchful little white eye cock itself in shere khan ’s direction for choice shere khan drawl now come to drink and make clean again be there any to forbid bagheera ’s back begin to curve like bamboo in high wind but hathi lift up his trunk and speak quietly thy kill be from choice he ask and when hathi ask question it be good to answer even so it be my right and my night thou know hathi shere khan speak almost courteously
yes know hathi answer and after little silence hast thou drunk thy fill for to night yes go then the river be to drink and not to defile none but the lame tiger would so have boast of his right at this season when when we suffer together man and jungle people alike clean or unclean get to thy lair shere khan the last word ring out like silver trumpet and hathi ’s three son roll forward half pace though there be no need shere khan slink away not dare to growl for he know what every one else know that when the last come to the last hathi be the master of the jungle what be
this right shere khan speak of mowgli whisper in bagheera ’s ear to kill man be always shameful the law say so and yet hathi says---- ask he do not know little brother right or no right if hathi have not speak would have teach that lame butcher his lesson to come to the peace rock fresh from kill of man and to boast of it be jackal ’s trick besides he taint the good water mowgli wait for minute to pick up his courage because no one care to address hathi directly
and then he cry what be shere khan ’s right hathi both bank echo his word for all the people of the jungle be intensely curious and they have just see something that none except baloo who look very thoughtful seem to understand it be an old tale say hathi tale old than the jungle keep silence along the bank and will tell that tale there be minute or two of push shoulder among the pig and the buffalo and then the leader of the herd grunt one after another we wait and hathi stride forward till he be nearly knee deep in the pool by the peace rock lean and wrinkled and yellow tusked though he be he look what the jungle know he to be their master ye know child he begin that of all thing ye most fear man and there be mutter of agreement this tale touch thee little brother say bagheera to mowgli be of the pack hunter of the free people mowgli answer what have to do with man and ye do not know why ye fear man hathi go on this be the reason in the beginning of the jungle and none know when that be we of the jungle walk together have no fear of one another in those day there be no drought and leaf and flower and fruit grow on the same tree and we eat nothing at all except leaf and flower and grass and fruit and bark be glad be not bear in those day say bagheera bark be only good to sharpen claw and the lord of the jungle be tha the first of the elephant he draw the jungle out of deep water with his trunk and where he make furrow in the ground with his tusk there the river run and where he strike with his foot there rise pond of good water and when he blow through his trunk,--thus,--the tree fall that be the manner in which the jungle be make by tha
and so the tale be tell to it have not lose fat in the tell bagheera whisper and mowgli laugh behind his hand in those day there be no corn or melon or pepper or sugar cane nor be there any little hut such as ye have all see and the jungle people know nothing of man but live in the jungle together make one people
but presently they begin to dispute over their food though there be graze enough for all they be lazy each wish to eat where he lie as sometimes we can do now when the spring rain be good tha the first of the elephant be busy make new jungle and lead the river in their bed he could not walk in all place therefore he make the first of the tiger the master and the judge of the jungle to whom the jungle people should bring their dispute in those day the first of the tiger eat fruit and grass with the other he be as large as be and he be very beautiful in colour all over like the blossom of the yellow creeper there be never stripe nor bar upon his hide in those good day when this the jungle be new all the jungle people come before he without fear and his word be the law of all the jungle we be then remember ye one people yet upon night there be dispute between two buck graze quarrel such as ye now settle with the horn and the fore foot and it be say that as the two speak together before the first of the first of the tiger lie among the flower buck push he with his horn and the first of the tiger forget that he be the master and judge of the jungle and leap upon that buck break his neck till that night never one of we have die and the first of the tiger see what he have do and be make foolish by the scent of the blood run away into the marsh of the north and we of the jungle leave without judge fall to fight among ourselves and tha hear the noise of it and come back then some of we say this and some of we say that
but he see the dead buck among the flower and ask who have kill and we of the jungle would not tell because the smell of the blood make we foolish we run to and fro in circle caper and cry out and shake our head then tha give an order to the tree that hang low and to the trail creeper of the jungle that they should mark the killer of the buck so that he should know he again
and he say who will now be master of the jungle people then up leap the gray ape who live in the branch and say will now be master of the jungle at this tha laugh and say so be it and go away very angry child
ye know the gray ape he be then as he be now at the first he make wise face for himself but in little while he begin to scratch and to leap up and down and when tha come back he find the gray ape hang head down from bough mock those who stand below and they mock he again
and so there be no law in the jungle only foolish talk and senseless word then tha call we all together and say the first of your master have bre death into the jungle and the second shame now it be time there be law and law that ye must not break now ye shall know fear and when ye have find he ye shall know that he be your master and the rest shall follow then we of the jungle say what be fear and tha say seek till ye find so we go up and down the jungle seek for fear and
presently the buffaloes---- ugh say mysa the leader of the buffalo from their sand bank
yes mysa it be the buffalo they come back with the news that in cave in the jungle sit fear and that he have no hair and go upon his hind leg then we of the jungle follow the herd till we come to that cave and fear stand at the mouth of it
and he be as the buffalo have say hairless and he walk upon his hinder leg when he see we he cry out and his voice fill we with the fear that we have now of that voice when we hear it
and we run away tramp upon and tear each other because we be afraid that night so it be tell to we of the jungle do not lie down together as use to be our custom but each tribe draw off by itself the pig with the pig the deer with the deer horn to horn hoof to hoof,--like keeping to like and so lay shake in the jungle only the first of the tiger be not with we for he be still hide in the marsh of the north and when word be bre to he of the thing we have see in the cave he say will go to this thing and break his neck
so he run all the night till he come to the cave but the tree and the creeper on his path remember the order that tha have give let down their branch and mark he as he run draw their finger across his back his flank his forehead and his jowl wherever they touch he there be mark and stripe upon his yellow hide and those stripe do this child wear to this day when he come to the cave fear the hairless one put out his hand and call he the striped one that come by night and the first of the tiger be afraid of the hairless one and run back to the swamp howl mowgli chuckle quietly here his chin in the water so loud do he howl that tha hear he and say what be the sorrow and the first of the tiger lift up his muzzle to the new make sky which be now so old say give back my power tha be make ashamed before all the jungle and have run away from hairless one and he have call shameful name and why say tha because be smear with the mud of the marsh say the first of the tiger swim then and roll on the wet grass and if it be mud it will wash away say tha and the first of the tiger swim and roll and roll upon the grass till the jungle run round and round before his eye but not one little bar upon all his hide be change and tha watch he laugh then the first of the tiger say what have do that this come to tha say thou hast kill the buck and thou hast let death loose in the jungle and with death have come fear so that the people of the jungle be afraid one of the other as thou art afraid of the hairless one the first of the tiger say they will never fear for know they since the beginning tha say go and see and the first of the tiger run to and fro call aloud to the deer and the pig and the sambhur and the porcupine and all the jungle people and they all run away from he who have be their judge because they be afraid then the first of the tiger come back and his pride be break in he and beat his head upon the ground he tear up the earth with all his foot and say remember that be once the master of the jungle do not forget tha let my child remember that be once without shame or fear and tha say this much will do because thou and together see the jungle make for one night in each year it shall be as it be before the buck be kill for thee and for thy child in that one night if ye meet the hairless one and his name be man ye shall not be afraid of he but he shall be afraid of you as though ye be judge of the jungle and master of all thing show he mercy in that night of his fear for thou hast know what fear be then the first of the tiger answer be content but when next he drink he see the black stripe upon his flank and his side and he remember the name that the hairless one have give he and he be angry for year he live in the marsh wait till tha should keep his promise and upon night when the jackal of the moon the evening star stand clear of the jungle he feel that his night be upon he and he go to that cave to meet the hairless one then it happen as tha promise for the hairless one fall down before he and lie along the ground and the first of the tiger strike he and break his back for he think that there be but one such thing in the jungle and that he have kill fear then nose above the kill he hear tha come down from the wood of the north and presently the voice of the first of the elephant which be the voice that we hear now---- the thunder be roll up and down the dry scarred hill but it bring no rain only heat lightning that flicker along the ridge and hathi go on that be the voice he hear and it say be this thy mercy the first of the tiger lick his lip and say what matter have kill fear and tha say blind and foolish thou hast untie the foot of death and he will follow thy trail till thou diest thou hast teach man to kill the first of the tiger stand stiffly to his kill say he be as the buck be there be no fear now will judge the jungle people once more and tha say never again shall the jungle people come to thee they shall never cross thy trail nor sleep near thee nor follow after thee nor browse by thy lair only fear shall follow thee and with blow that thou canst not see he shall bid thee wait his pleasure he shall make the ground to open under thy foot and the creeper to twist about thy neck and the tree trunk to grow together about thee high than thou canst leap and at the last he shall take thy hide to wrap his cub when they be cold thou hast show he no mercy
none will he show thee the first of the tiger be very bold for his night be still on he and he say the promise of tha be the promise of tha he will not take away my night and tha say the one night be thine as have say but there be price to pay thou hast teach man to kill and he be no slow learner the first of the tiger say he be here under my foot and his back be break let the jungle know have kill fear then tha laugh and say thou hast kill one of many but thou thyself shalt tell the jungle for thy night be end so the day come and from the mouth of the cave go out another hairless one and he see the kill in the path and the first of the tiger above it and he take pointed stick---- they throw thing that cut now say ikki rustle down the bank for ikki be consider uncommonly good eating by the gond they call he ho igoo and he know something of the wicked little gondee axe that whirl across clear like dragon fly it be point stick such as they put in the foot of pit trap say hathi and throw it he strike the first of the tiger deep in the flank thus it happen as tha say for the first of the tiger run howl up and down the jungle till he tear out the stick and all the jungle know that the hairless one could strike from far off and they fear more than before so it come about that the first of the tiger teach the hairless one to kill and ye know what harm that have since do to all our people through the noose and the pitfall and the hide trap and the fly stick and the stinging fly that come out of white smoke hathi mean the rifle and the red flower that drive we into the open yet for one night in the year the hairless one fear the tiger as tha promise and never have the tiger give he cause to be less afraid where he find he there he kill he remember how the first of the tiger be make ashamed for the rest fear walk up and down the jungle by day and by night ahi aoo say the deer think of what it all mean to they and only when there be one great fear over all as there be now can we of the jungle lie aside our little fear and meet together in one place as we do now for one night only do man fear the tiger say mowgli for one night only say hathi
but we but all the jungle know that shere khan kill man twice and thrice in moon even so then he spring from behind and turn his head aside as he strike for he be full of fear if man look at he he would run but on his one night he go openly down to the village he walk between the house and thrust his head into the doorway and the man fall on their face and there he do his kill one kill in that night
oh say mowgli to himself roll over in the water now see why it be shere khan bid look at he he get no good of it for he could not hold his eye steady and and certainly do not fall down at his foot but then be not man be of the free people
umm say bagheera deep in his furry throat do the tiger know his night never till the jackal of the moon stand clear of the evening mist sometimes it fall in the dry summer and sometimes in the wet rain this one night of the tiger but for the first of the tiger this would never have be nor would any of we have know fear the deer grunt sorrowfully and bagheera ’s lip curl in wicked smile do man know this tale say he none know it except the tiger and we the elephant the child of tha now ye by the pool have hear it and have speak hathi dip his trunk into the water as sign that he do not wish to talk
but but say mowgli turn to baloo why do not the first of the tiger continue to eat grass and leaf and tree he do but break the buck ’s neck he do not eat what lead he to the hot meat the tree and the creeper mark he little brother and make he the striped thing that we see never again would he eat their fruit but from that day he revenge himself upon the deer and the other the eater of grass say baloo then thou know the tale why have never hear because the jungle be full of such tale if make beginning there would never be an end to they let go my ear little brother the law of the jungle just to give you an idea of the immense variety of the jungle law have translate into verse baloo always recite they in sort of sing song few of the law that apply to the wolf there be of course hundred and hundred more but these will do for specimen of the simple ruling now this be the law of the jungle as old and as true as the sky and the wolf that shall keep it may prosper but the wolf that shall break it must die as the creeper that girdle the tree trunk the law runneth forward and back-- for the strength of the pack be the wolf and the strength of the wolf be the pack wash daily from nose tip to tail tip drink deeply but never too deep and remember the night be for hunt and forget not the day be for sleep the jackal may follow the tiger but cub when thy whisker be grow remember the wolf be hunter go forth and get food of thine own keep peace with the lord of the jungle the tiger the panther the bear and trouble not hathi the silent and mock not the boar in his lair when pack meet with pack in the jungle and neither will go from the trail lie down till the leader have speak it may be fair word shall prevail when ye fight with wolf of the pack ye must fight he alone and afar lest other take part in the quarrel and the pack be diminish by war the lair of the wolf be his refuge and where he have make he his home not even the head wolf may enter not even the council may come the lair of the wolf be his refuge but where he have digge it too plain the council shall send he message
and so he shall change it again if ye kill before midnight be silent and wake not the wood with your bay lest ye frighten the deer from the crop and the brother go empty away ye may kill for yourself and your mate and your cub as they need and ye can but kill not for pleasure of killing and seven time never kill man if ye plunder his kill from weak devour not all in thy pride pack right be the right of the mean so leave he the head and the hide the kill of the pack be the meat of the pack ye must eat where it lie
and no one may carry away of that meat to his lair or he die the kill of the wolf be the meat of the wolf he may do what he will but till he have give permission the pack may not eat of that kill cub right be the right of the yearling from all of his pack he may claim full gorge when the killer have eat and none may refuse he the same lair right be the right of the mother from all of her year she may claim one haunch of each kill for her litter and none may deny she the same cave right be the right of the father to hunt by himself for his own he be free of all call to the pack he be judge by the council alone because of his age and his cunning because of his gripe and his paw in all that the law leaveth open the word of the head wolf be law now these be the law of the jungle and many and mighty be they but the head and the hoof of the law and the haunch and the hump be obey the miracle of purun bhagat the night we feel the earth would move we steal and pluck he by the hand because we love he with the love that know but can not understand and when the roar hillside break and all our world fall down in rain we save he we the little folk but he do not come again mourn now we save he for the sake of such poor love as wild one may mourn ye our brother will not wake and his own kind drive we away dirge of the langur there be once man in india who be prime minister of one of the semi independent native state in the north western part of the country he be brahmin so high caste that caste cease to have any particular meaning for he and his father have be an important official in the gay colour tag rag and bobtail of an old fashioned hindu court
but as purun dass grow up he feel that the old order of thing be change and that if any one wish to get on in the world he must stand well with the english and imitate all that the english believe to be good at the same time native official must keep his own master ’s favour this be difficult game but the quiet close mouthed young brahmin help by good english education at bombay university play it coolly and rise step by step to be prime minister of the kingdom that be to say he hold more real power than his master the maharajah when the old king who be suspicious of the english their railway and telegraph die purun dass stand high with his young successor who have be tutor by an englishman and between they though he always take care that his master should have the credit they establish school for little girl make road and start state dispensary and show of agricultural implement and publish yearly blue book on the moral and material progress of the state and the foreign office and the government of india be delight very few native state take up english progress altogether for they will not believe as purun dass show he do that what be good for the englishman must be twice as good for the asiatic the prime minister become the honoured friend of viceroy and governor and lieutenant governor and medical missionary and common missionary and hard rid english officer who come to shoot in the state preserve as well as of whole host of tourist who travel up and down india in the cold weather show how thing ought to be manage in his spare time he would endow scholarship for the study of medicine and manufacture on strictly english line and write letter to the pioneer the great indian daily paper explain his master ’s aim and object at last he go to england on visit and have to pay enormous sum to the priest when he come back for even so high caste brahmin as purun dass lose caste by cross the black sea in london he meet and talk with every one worth know man whose name go all over the world and see great deal more than he say he be give honorary degree by learn university and he make speech and talk of hindu social reform to english lady in evening dress till all london cry this be the most fascinating man we have ever meet at dinner since cloth be first lay when he return to india there be blaze of glory for the viceroy himself make special visit to confer upon the maharajah the grand cross of the star of india all diamond and ribbon and enamel and at the same ceremony while the cannon boom purun dass be make knight commander of the order of the indian empire so that his name stand sir purun dass k.c.i.e
that evening at dinner in the big viceregal tent he stand up with the badge and the collar of the order on his breast and reply to the toast of his master ’s health make speech few englishman could have well next month when the city have return to its sun bake quiet
he do thing no englishman would have dream of do for so far as the world ’s affair go he die the jewelled order of his knighthood go back to the indian government and new prime minister be appoint to the charge of affair and great game of general post begin in all the subordinate appointment the priest know what have happen and the people guess
but india be the one place in the world where man can do as he please and nobody ask why and the fact that dewan sir purun dass k.c.i.e have resign position palace and power and take up the beg bowl and ochre colour dress of sunnyasi or holy man be consider nothing extraordinary he have be as the old law recommend twenty year youth twenty year
he have never carry weapon in his life,--and twenty year head of household he have use his wealth and his power for what he know both to be worth he have take honour when it come his way he have see man and city far and near and man and city have stand up and honour he now he would let those thing go as man drop the cloak he
no long need behind he as he walk through the city gate an antelope skin and brass handle crutch under his arm and beg bowl of polished brown coco de mer in his hand barefoot alone with eye cast on the ground behind he they be fire salute from the bastion in honour of his happy successor purun dass nod all that life be end
and he bear it no more ill will or good will than man bear to colourless dream of the night he be sunnyasi houseless wander mendicant depend on his neighbour for his daily bread and so long as there be morsel to divide in india neither priest nor beggar starve he have never in his life taste meat and very seldom eat even fish five pound note would have cover his personal expense for food through any one of the many year in which he have be absolute master of million of money even when he be be lionise in london he have hold before he his dream of peace and quiet the long white dusty indian road print all over with bare foot the incessant slow move traffic and the sharp smell wood smoke curl up under the fig tree in the twilight where the wayfarer sit at their evening meal when the time come to make that dream true the prime minister take the proper step and in three day you might more easily have find bubble in the trough of the long atlantic sea than purun dass among the rove gathering separate million of india at night his antelope skin be spread where the darkness overtake he sometimes in sunnyasi monastery by the roadside sometimes by mud pillar shrine of kala pir where the jogis who be another misty division of holy man would receive he as they do those who know what caste and division be worth sometimes on the outskirt of little hindu village where the child would steal up with the food their parent have prepare and sometimes on the pitch of the bare graze ground where the flame of his stick fire wake the drowsy camel it be all one to purun dass or purun bhagat as he call himself now earth people and food be all one but unconsciously his foot draw he away northward and eastward from the south to rohtak from rohtak to kurnool from kurnool to ruin samanah and then up stream along the dry bed of the gugger river that fill only when the rain fall in the hill till one day he see the far line of the great himalayas then purun bhagat smile for he remember that his mother be of rajput brahmin birth from kulu way hill woman always home sick for the snow and that the least touch of hill blood draw man in the end back to where he belong yonder say purun bhagat breast the low slope of the sewaliks where the cactus stand up like seven branch candlesticks-“yonder shall sit down and get knowledge and the cool wind of the himalayas whistle about his ear as he tread the road that lead to simla the last time he have come that way it have be in state with clattering cavalry escort to visit the gentle and most affable of viceroy and the two have talk for an hour together about mutual friend in london and what the indian common folk really think of thing this time purun bhagat pay no call but lean on the rail of the mall watch that glorious view of the plain spread out forty mile below till native mohammedan policeman tell he he be obstruct traffic and purun bhagat salaam reverently to the law because he know the value of it and be seek for law of his own then he move on and sleep that night in an empty hut at chota simla which look like the very last end of the earth but it be only the beginning of his journey he follow the himalaya thibet road the little ten foot track that be blast out of solid rock or strut out on timber over gulfs thousand foot deep that dip into warm wet shut in valley and climb out across bare grassy hill shoulder where the sun strike like burn glass or turn through dripping dark forest where the tree fern dress the trunk from head to heel and the pheasant call to his mate and he meet thibetan herdsman with their dog and flock of sheep each sheep with little bag of borax on his back and wander wood cutter and cloak and blanket lama from thibet come into india on pilgrimage and envoy of little solitary hill state post furiously on ring streak and piebald pony or the cavalcade of rajah pay visit or else for long clear day he would see nothing more than black bear grunt and root below in the valley when he first start the roar of the world he have leave still re in his ear as the roar of tunnel ring long after the train have pas through but when he have put the mutteeanee pass behind he that be all do and purun bhagat be alone with himself walk wonder and think his eye on the ground and his thought with the cloud one evening he cross the high pass he have meet till then it have be two day ’s climb and come out on line of snow peak that band all the horizon mountain from fifteen to twenty thousand foot high look almost near enough to hit with stone though they be fifty or sixty mile away the pass be crown with dense dark forest deodar walnut wild cherry wild olive and wild pear but mostly deodar which be the himalayan cedar and under the shadow of the deodar stand deserted shrine to kali who be durga who be sitala who be sometimes worship against the smallpox purun dass sweep the stone floor clean smile at the grin statue make himself little mud fireplace at the back of the shrine spread his antelope skin on bed of fresh pine needle tuck his bairagi his brass handle crutch under his armpit and sit down to rest immediately below he the hillside fall away clean and clear for fifteen hundred foot where little village of stone walled house with roof of beat earth cling to the steep tilt all round it the tiny terraced field lie out like apron of patchwork on the knee of the mountain and cow no big than beetle graze between the smooth stone circle of the threshing floor look across the valley the eye be deceive by the size of thing and could not at first realise that what seem to be low scrub on the opposite mountain flank be in truth forest of hundred foot pine purun bhagat see an eagle swoop across the gigantic hollow but the great bird dwindle to dot
ere it be half way over few band of scatter cloud string up and down the valley catch on shoulder of the hill or rise up and die out when they be level with the head of the pass and here shall find peace say purun bhagat now hill man make nothing of few hundred foot up or down and as soon as the villager see the smoke in the deserted shrine the village priest climb up the terraced hillside to welcome the stranger when he meet purun bhagat ’s eye the eye of man use to control thousand he bow to the earth take the begging bowl without word and return to the village say we have at last holy man never have see such man he be of the plain but pale coloured brahmin of the brahmin then all the housewife of the village say think you he will stay with we and each do she good to cook the most savoury meal for the bhagat hill food be very simple but with buckwheat and indian corn and rice and red pepper and little fish out of the stream in the valley and honey from the flue like hive build in the stone wall and dry apricot and turmeric and wild ginger and bannock of flour devout woman can make good thing and it be full bowl that the priest carry to the bhagat be he go to stay ask the priest would he need chela disciple to beg for he have he blanket against the cold weather be the food good purun bhagat eat and thank the giver it be in his mind to stay that be sufficient say the priest let the beg bowl be place outside the shrine in the hollow make by those two twisted root and daily should the bhagat be feed for the village feel honour that such man he look timidly into the bhagat ’s face should tarry among they that day see the end of purun bhagat ’s wander he have come to the place appoint for he the silence and the space after this time stop
and he sit at the mouth of the shrine could not tell whether he be alive or dead man with control of his limb or part of the hill and the cloud and the shift rain and sunlight he would repeat name softly to himself hundred hundred time till at each repetition he seem to move more and more out of his body sweep up to the door of some tremendous discovery but just as the door be open his body would drag he back and with grief he feel he be lock up again in the flesh and bone of purun bhagat every morning the fill beg bowl be lie silently in the crutch of the root outside the shrine sometimes the priest bring it sometimes ladakhi trader lodging in the village and anxious to get merit trudge up the path but more often it be the woman who have cook the meal overnight
and she would murmur hardly above her breath speak for before the god bhagat speak for such one the wife of so and so now
and then some bold child would be allow the honour and purun bhagat would hear he drop the bowl and run as fast as his little leg could carry he but the bhagat never come down to the village it be lie out like map at his foot he could see the evening gathering hold on the circle of the threshing floor because that be the only level ground could see the wonderful unnamed green of the young rice the indigo blue of the indian corn the dock like patch of buckwheat and in its season the red bloom of the amaranth whose tiny seed be neither grain nor pulse make food that can be lawfully eat by hindus in time of fast when the year turn the roof of the hut be all little square of pure gold for it be on the roof that they lie out their cobs of the corn to dry hiving and harvest rice sowing and husking pass before his eye all embroider down there on the many sided plot of field
and he think of they all and wonder what they all lead to at the long last even in populated india man can not day sit still before the wild thing run over he as though he be rock and in that wilderness very soon the wild thing who know kali ’s shrine well come back to look at the intruder the langur the big gray whiskered monkey of the himalayas be naturally the first for they be alive with curiosity and when they have upset the beg bowl and roll it round the floor and try their tooth on the brass handle crutch and make face at the antelope skin they decide that the human being who sit so still be harmless at evening they would leap down from the pine and beg with their hand for thing to eat and then swing off in graceful curve they like the warmth of the fire too and huddle round it till purun bhagat have to push they aside to throw on more fuel and in the morning as often as not he would find furry ape share his blanket all day long one or other of the tribe would sit by his side star out at the snow croon and look unspeakably wise and sorrowful after the monkey come the barasingh that big deer which be like our red deer but strong he wish to rub off the velvet of his horn against the cold stone of kali ’s statue and stamp his foot when he see the man at the shrine but purun bhagat never move and little by little the royal stag edge up and nuzzle his shoulder purun bhagat slide one cool hand along the hot antler and the touch soothe the fret beast who bow his head and purun bhagat very softly rub and ravel off the velvet afterward the barasingh bring his doe and fawn gentle thing that mumble on the holy man ’s blanket or would come alone at night his eye green in the fire flicker to take his share of fresh walnut at last the musk deer the shy and almost the small of the deerlet come too her big rabbity ear erect even brindled silent mushick nabha must need find out what the light in the shrine mean and drop out her moose like nose into purun bhagat ’s lap come and go with the shadow of the fire purun bhagat call they all my brother and his low call of bhai bhai would draw they from the forest at noon if they be within ear shoot the himalayan black bear moody and suspicious sona who have the shape white mark under his chin pass that way more than once and since the bhagat show no fear sona show no anger but watch he and come close and beg share of the caress and dole of bread or wild berry often in the still dawn when the bhagat would climb to the very crest of the pass to watch the red day walk along the peak of the snow he would find sona shuffle and grunt at his heel thrust curious fore paw under fall trunk and bring it away with whoof of impatience or his early step would wake sona where he lie curl up and the great brute rise erect would think to fight till he hear the bhagat ’s voice and know his good friend nearly all hermit and holy man who live apart from the big city have the reputation of be able to work miracle with the wild thing but all the miracle lie in keep still in never make hasty movement and for long time at least in never look directly at visitor the villager see the outline of the barasingh stalking like shadow through the dark forest behind the shrine see the minaul the himalayan pheasant blaze in her good colour before kali ’s statue and the langur on their haunch inside play with the walnut shell some of the child too have hear sona sing to himself bear fashion behind the fall rock and the bhagat ’s reputation as miracle worker stand firm yet nothing be far from his mind than miracle he believe that all thing be one big miracle and when man know that much he know something to go upon he know for certainty that there be nothing great and nothing little in this world and day and night he strove to think out his way into the heart of thing back to the place whence his soul have come so think his untrimmed hair fall down about his shoulder the stone slab at the side of the antelope skin be dent into little hole by the foot of his brass handle crutch and the place between the tree trunk where the beg bowl rest day after day sink and wear into hollow almost as smooth as the brown shell itself and each beast know his exact place at the fire the field change their colour with the season the thresh floor fill and empty and fill again and again and again and again when winter come the langur frisk among the branch feather with light snow till the mother monkey bring their sad eyed little baby up from the warm valley with the spring there be few change in the village the priest be old and many of the little child who use to come with the begging dish send their own child now and when you ask of the villager how long their holy man have live in kali ’s shrine at the head of the pass they answer always then come such summer rain as have not be know in the hill for many season through three good month the valley be wrap in cloud and soak mist steady unrelenting downfall break off into thunder shower after thunder shower kali ’s shrine stand above the cloud for the most part and there be whole month in which the bhagat never catch glimpse of his village it be pack away under white floor of cloud that sway and shift and roll on itself and bulge upward but never break from its pier the stream flank of the valley all that time he hear nothing but the sound of million little water overhead from the tree and underfoot along the ground soak through the pine needle dripping from the tongue of draggled fern and spout in newly tear muddy channel down the slope then the sun come out and draw forth the good incense of the deodar and the rhododendron and that far off clean smell which the hill people call the smell of the snow the hot sunshine last for week and then the rain gather together for their last downpour and the water fall in sheet that flay off the skin of the ground and leap back in mud purun bhagat heap his fire high that night for he be sure his brother would need warmth but never beast come to the shrine though he call and call till he drop asleep wonder what have happen in the wood it be in the black heart of the night the rain drum like thousand drum that he be rouse by pluck at his blanket and stretch out feel the little hand of langur it be well here than in the tree he say sleepily loosen fold of blanket take it and be warm the monkey catch his hand and pull hard be it food then say purun bhagat wait awhile and will prepare some as he kneel to throw fuel on the fire the langur run to the door of the shrine croon and run back again pluck at the man ’s knee what be it what be thy trouble brother say purun bhagat for the langur ’s eye be full of thing that he could not tell unless one of thy caste be in trap and none set trap here will not go into that weather look brother even the barasingh come for shelter the deer ’s antler clash as he stride into the shrine clash against the grin statue of kali he lower they in purun bhagat ’s direction and stamp uneasily hiss through his half shut nostril hai hai hai say the bhagat snap his finger be this payment for night ’s lodging but the deer push he toward the door and as he do so purun bhagat hear the sound of something open with sigh and see two slab of the floor draw away from each other while the sticky earth below smack its lip now see say purun bhagat no blame to my brother that they do not sit by the fire to night the mountain be fall
and yet why should go his eye fall on the empty begging bowl and his face change they have give good food daily since since come and if be not swift to morrow there will not be one mouth in the valley indeed must go and warn they below back there brother let get to the fire the barasingh back unwillingly as purun bhagat drive pine torch deep into the flame twirl it till it be well light ah ye come to warn he say rise well than that we shall do well than that out now and lend thy neck brother for have but two foot he clutch the bristle wither of the barasingh with his right hand hold the torch away with his left and step out of the shrine into the desperate night there be no breath of wind but the rain nearly drown the flare as the great deer hurry down the slope slide on his haunch as soon as they be clear of the forest more of the bhagat ’s brother join they he hear though he could not see the langur press about he and behind they the uhh uhh of sona the rain mat his long white hair into rope the water splash beneath his bare foot and his yellow robe cling to his frail old body but he step down steadily lean against the barasingh he be no long holy man but sir purun dass k.c.i.e prime minister of no small state man accustom to command go out to save life down the steep plashy path they pour all together the bhagat and his brother down and down till the deer ’s foot click and stumble on the wall of threshing floor and he snort because he smell man now they be at the head of the one crooked village street and the bhagat beat with his crutch on the bar window of the blacksmith ’s house as his torch blaze up in the shelter of the eave up and out cry purun bhagat and he do not know his own voice for it be year since he have speak aloud to man the hill fall the hill be fall up and out
oh you within it be our bhagat say the blacksmith ’s wife he stand among his beast gather the little one and give the call it run from house to house while the beast cramp in the narrow way surge and huddle round the bhagat and sona puff impatiently the people hurry into the street they be no more than seventy soul all tell and in the glare of the torch they see their bhagat hold back the terrified barasingh while the monkey pluck piteously at his skirt and sona sit on his haunch and roar across the valley and up the next hill shout purun bhagat leave none behind we follow then the people run as only hill folk can run for they know that in landslip you must climb for the high ground across the valley they flee splash through the little river at the bottom and pant up the terraced field on the far side while the bhagat and his brother follow up and up the opposite mountain they climb call to each other by name the roll call of the village and at their heel toil the big barasingh weight by the fail strength of purun bhagat at last the deer stop in the shadow of deep pinewood five hundred foot up the hillside his instinct that have warn he of the come slide tell he he would he safe here purun bhagat drop faint by his side for the chill of the rain and that fierce climb be kill he but first he call to the scatter torch ahead stay and count your number then whisper to the deer as he see the light gather in cluster stay with brother stay till go there be sigh in the air that grow to mutter and mutter that grow to roar and roar that pass all sense of hearing and the hillside on which the villager stand be hit in the darkness and rock to the blow then note as steady deep and true as the deep of the organ drown everything for perhaps five minute while the very root of the pine quiver to it it die away and the sound of the rain fall on mile of hard ground and grass change to the muffled drum of water on soft earth that tell its own tale never villager not even the priest be bold enough to speak to the bhagat who have save their life they crouch under the pine and wait till the day when it come they look across the valley and see that what have be forest and terraced field and track thread graze ground be one raw red fan shape smear with few tree fling head down on the scarp that red run high up the hill of their refuge dam back the little river which have begin to spread into brick colour lake of the village of the road to the shrine of the shrine itself and the forest behind there be no trace for one mile in width and two thousand foot in sheer depth the mountain side have come away bodily plan clean from head to heel and the villager one by one creep through the wood to pray before their bhagat they see the barasingh stand over he who flee when they come near and they hear the langur wail in the branch and sona moan up the hill but their bhagat be dead sit cross legged his back against tree his crutch under his armpit and his face turn to the north east the priest say behold miracle after miracle for in this very attitude must all sunnyasis be bury therefore where he now be we will build the temple to our holy man they build the temple before year be end little stone and earth shrine
and they call the hill the bhagat ’s hill and they worship there with light and flower and offering to this day
but they do not know that the saint of their worship be the late sir purun dass k.c.i.e d.c.l
etc once prime minister of the progressive and enlightened state of mohiniwala and honorary or corresponding member of more learned and scientific society than will ever do any good in this world or the next song of kabir
oh light be the world that he weigh in his hand
oh heavy the tale of his fief and his land he have go from the guddee and put on the shroud and depart in guise of bairagi avow now the white road to delhi be mat for his foot the sal and the kikar must guard he from heat his home be the camp and the waste and the crowd-- he be seek the way as bairagi avow he have look upon man and his eyeball be clear there be one there be one and but one saith kabir the red mist of do have thin to cloud-- he have take the path for bairagi avow to learn and discern of his brother the clod of his brother the brute and his brother the god he have go from the council and put on the shroud can ye hear saith kabir bairagi avow let in the jungle veil they cover they wall they round-- blossom and creeper
and weed-- let we forget the sight and the sound the smell and the touch of the breed fat black ash by the altar stone here be the white foot rain and the do bring forth in the field unsown and none shall affright they again and the blind wall crumble unknown o’erthrown and none shall inhabit again you will remember that after mowgli have pin shere khan ’s hide to the council rock he tell as many as be leave of the seeonee pack that henceforward he would hunt in the jungle alone and the four child of mother and father wolf say that they would hunt with he but it be not easy to change one ’s life all in minute particularly in the jungle the first thing mowgli do when the disorderly pack have slink off be to go to the home cave and sleep for day and night then he tell mother wolf and father wolf as much as they could understand of his adventure among man and when he make the morning sun flicker up and down the blade of his skin knife,--the same
he have skin shere khan with,--they say he have learn something then akela and gray brother have to explain their share of the great buffalo drive in the ravine and baloo toil up the hill to hear all about it and bagheera scratch himself all over with pure delight at the way in which mowgli have manage his war it be long after sunrise
but no one dream of go to sleep and from time to time during the talk mother wolf would throw up her head and sniff deep snuff of satisfaction as the wind bring she the smell of the tiger skin on the council rock but for akela and gray brother here mowgli say at the end could have do nothing
oh mother mother if thou hadst see the black herd bull pour down the ravine or hurry through the gate when the man pack fling stone at be glad do not see that last say mother wolf stiffly it be not my custom to suffer my cub to be drive to and fro like jackal would have take price from the man pack but would have spar the woman who give thee the milk yes would have spar she alone peace peace raksha say father wolf lazily our frog have come back again so wise that his own father must lick his foot and what be cut more or less on the head leave man alone baloo and bagheera both echo leave man alone mowgli his head on mother wolf ’s side smile contentedly and say that for his own part he never wish to see or hear or smell man again but what say akela cock one ear--“but what if man do not leave thee alone little brother we be five say gray brother look round at the company and snap his jaw on the last word we also might attend to that hunting say bagheera with little switch switch of his tail look at baloo but why think of man now akela for this reason the lone wolf answer when that yellow chief ’s hide be hang up on the rock go back along our trail to the village step in my track turn aside and lie down to make mixed trail in case one should follow we but when have foul the trail so that myself hardly know it again mang the bat come hawk between the tree and hang up above say mang the village of the man pack where they cast out the man cub hum like hornet ’s nest it be big stone that throw chuckle mowgli who have often amuse himself by throw ripe paw paw into hornet ’s nest and race off to the near pool before the hornet catch he ask of mang what he have see he say that the red flower blossom at the gate of the village and man sit about it carry gun now know for have good cause,”--akela look down at the old dry scar on his flank and side,--“that man do not carry gun for pleasure presently little brother man with gun follow our trail if indeed he be not already on it
but why should he man have cast out what more do they need say mowgli angrily thou art man little brother akela return it be not for we the free hunter to tell thee what thy brother do or why he have just time to snatch up his paw as the skin knife cut deep into the ground below mowgli strike quick than an average human eye could follow but akela be wolf and even dog who be very far remove from the wild wolf his ancestor can be wake out of deep sleep by cart wheel touch his flank and can spring away unharmed before that wheel come on another time mowgli say quietly return the knife to its sheath speak of the man pack and of mowgli in two breath not one phff that be sharp tooth
say akela snuff at the blade ’s cut in the earth but live with the man pack have spoil thine eye little brother could have kill buck while thou wast striking bagheera spring to his foot thrust up his head as far as he could sniff and stiffen through every curve in his body gray brother follow his example quickly keep little to his left to get the wind that be blow from the right while akela bind fifty yard up wind and half crouch stiffen too mowgli look on enviously he could smell thing as very few human being could but he have never reach the hair trigger like sensitiveness of jungle nose and his three month in the smoky village have set he back sadly however he dampen his finger rub it on his nose and stand erect to catch the upper scent which though it be the faint be the true akela growl drop on his haunch buldeo say mowgli sit down he follow our trail and yonder be the sunlight on his gun it be no more than splash of sunlight for fraction of second on the brass clamp of the old tower musket but nothing in the jungle wink with just that flash except when the cloud race over the sky then piece of mica or little pool or even highly polish leaf will flash like heliograph but that day be cloudless and still know man would follow say akela triumphantly not for nothing have lead the pack the four cub say nothing but run down hill on their belly melt into the thorn and under brush as mole melt into lawn where go ye and without word mowgli call we roll his skull here before mid day gray brother answer back and wait man do not eat
man mowgli shriek who be wolf but now who drive the knife at for think he might be man say akela as the four wolf turn back sullenly and drop to heel be to give reason for all choose to do say mowgli furiously that be man there speak man bagheera mutter under his whisker even so do man talk round the king ’s cage at oodeypore we of the jungle know that man be wise of all if we trust our ear we should know that of all thing he be most foolish raise his voice he add the man cub be right in this man hunt in pack to kill one unless we know what the other will do be bad hunting come let we see what this man mean toward we we will not come gray brother growl hunt alone little brother we know our own mind the skull would have be ready to bring by now mowgli have be look from one to the other of his friend his chest heaving and his eye full of tear he stride forward to the wolf and drop on one knee say do not know my mind look at they look uneasily and when their eye wander he call they back again and again till their hair stand up all over their body and they tremble in every limb while mowgli star and star now say he of we five which be leader thou art leader
little brother say gray brother and he lick mowgli ’s foot follow then say mowgli and the four follow at his heel with their tail between their leg this come of live with the man pack say bagheera slip down after they there be more in the jungle now than jungle law baloo the old bear say nothing
but he think many thing mowgli cut across noiselessly through the jungle at right angle to buldeo ’s path till part the undergrowth he see the old man his musket on his shoulder run up the trail of overnight at dog trot you will remember that mowgli have leave the village with the heavy weight of shere khan ’s raw hide on his shoulder while akela and gray brother trot behind so that the triple trail be very clearly mark presently buldeo come to where akela as you know have go back and mix it all up then he sit down and cough and grunt and make little cast round and about into the jungle to pick it up again and all the time he could have throw stone over those who be watch he no one can be so silent as wolf when he do not care to be hear and mowgli though the wolf think he move very clumsily could come and go like shadow they ring the old man as school of porpoise ring steamer at full speed and as they ring he they talk unconcernedly for their speech begin below the low end of the scale that untrained human being can hear the other end be bind by the high squeak of mang the bat which very many people can not catch at all from that note all the bird and bat and insect talk take on this be well than any kill say gray brother as buldeo stoop and peer and puff he look like lose pig in the jungle by the river what do he say buldeo be mutter
savagely mowgli translate he say that pack of wolf must have dance round he say that he never see such trail in his life he say he be tired he will be rest before he pick it up again say bagheera coolly as he slip round tree trunk in the game of blindman ’s buff that they be play now what do the lean thing do eat or blow smoke out of his mouth man always play with their mouth
say mowgli and the silent trailer see the old man fill and light and puff at water pipe and they take good note of the smell of the tobacco so as to be sure of buldeo in the dark night if necessary then little knot of charcoal burner come down the path and naturally halt to speak to buldeo whose fame as hunter reach for at least twenty mile round they all sit down and smoke and bagheera and the other come up and watch while buldeo begin to tell the story of mowgli the devil child from one end to another with addition and invention how he himself have really kill shere khan and how mowgli have turn himself into wolf and fight with he all the afternoon and change into boy again and bewitch buldeo ’s rifle so that the bullet turn the corner when he point it at mowgli and kill one of buldeo ’s own buffalo and how the village know he to be the brave hunter in seeonee have send he out to kill this devil child but meantime the village have get hold of messua and her husband who be undoubtedly the father and mother of this devil child and have barricade they in their own hut and presently would torture they to make they confess they be witch and wizard and then they would be burn to death when say the charcoal burner because they would very much like to be present at the ceremony buldeo say that nothing would be do till he return because the village wish he to kill the jungle boy first after that they would dispose of messua and her husband and divide their land and buffalo among the village messua ’s husband have some remarkably fine buffalo too it be an excellent thing to destroy wizard buldeo think and people who entertain wolf child out of the jungle be clearly the bad kind of witch but say the charcoal burner what would happen if the english hear of it the english they have hear be perfectly mad people who would not let honest farmer kill witch in peace why say buldeo the head man of the village would report that messua and her husband have die of snake bite that be all arrange and the only thing now be to kill the wolf child they do not happen to have see anything of such creature the charcoal burner look round cautiously and thank their star they have not but they have no doubt that so brave man as buldeo would find he if any one could the sun be get rather low and they have an idea that they would push on to buldeo ’s village and see that wicked witch buldeo say that though it be his duty to kill the devil child he could not think of let party of unarmed man go through the jungle which might produce the wolf demon at any minute without his escort he therefore would accompany they and if the sorcerer ’s child appear well he would show they how the good hunter in seeonee deal with such thing the brahmin he say have give he charm against the creature that make everything perfectly safe what say he what say he what say he the wolf repeat every few minute and mowgli translate until he come to the witch part of the story which be little beyond he and then he say that the man and woman who have be so kind to he be trap do man trap man say bagheera so he say can not understand the talk they be all mad together what have messua and her man to do with that they should be put in trap and what be all this talk about the red flower must look to this whatever they would do to messua they will not do till buldeo return and so---- mowgli think hard with his finger play round the haft of the skin knife while buldeo and the charcoal burner go off very valiantly in single file go hot foot back to the man pack mowgli say at last and those say gray brother look hungrily after the brown back of the charcoal burner sing they home say mowgli with grin do not wish they to be at the village gate till it be dark can ye hold they gray brother bar his white tooth in contempt we can head they round and round in circle like tether goat if know man that do not need sing to they little lest they be lonely on the road and gray brother the song need not be of the sweet go with they bagheera and help make that song when night be shut down meet by the village gray brother know the place it be no light hunting to work for man cub when shall sleep say bagheera yawn though his eye show that he be delighted with the amusement to sing to naked man but let we try he lower his head so that the sound would travel and cry long long
good hunting”--a midnight call in the afternoon which be quite awful enough to begin with mowgli hear it rumble and rise and fall and die off in creepy sort of whine behind he and laugh to himself as he run through the jungle he could see the charcoal burner huddle in knot old buldeo ’s gun barrel wave like banana leaf to every point of the compass at once then gray brother give the ya la hi yalaha call for the buck drive when the pack drive the nilghai the big blue cow before they and it seem to come from the very end of the earth near and near and near till it end in shriek snap off short the other three answer till even mowgli could have vow that the full pack be in full cry and then they all break into the magnificent morning song in the jungle with every turn and flourish and grace note that deep mouthed wolf of the pack know this be rough rendering of the song but you must imagine what it sound like when it break the afternoon hush of the jungle:-- one moment past our body cast no shadow on the plain now clear and black they stride our track and we run home again in morning hush each rock and bush stand hard and high and raw then give the call good rest to all that keep the jungle law now horn and pelt our people melt in covert to abide now crouch and still to cave and hill our jungle baron glide now stark and plain man ’s oxen strain that draw the new yoke plough now strip and dread the dawn be red above the light talao ho get to lair the sun ’s aflare behind the breathing grass and crack through the young bamboo the warning whisper pass by day make strange the wood we range with blink eye we scan while down the sky the wild duck cry the day the day to man the dew be dry that drench our hide or wash about our way and where we drink the puddle bank be crisp into clay the traitor dark give up each mark of stretched or hooded claw then hear the call good rest to all that keep the jungle law but no translation can give the effect of it or the yelp scorn the four throw into every word of it as they hear the tree crash when the man hastily climb up into the branch and buldeo begin repeat incantation and charm then they lie down and sleep for like all who live by their own exertion they be of methodical cast of mind and no one can work well without sleep meantime mowgli be put the mile behind he nine to the hour swing on delighted to find himself so fit after all his cramped month among man the one idea in his head be to get messua and her husband out of the trap whatever it be for he have natural mistrust of trap later on he promise himself he would pay his debt to the village at large it be at twilight when he see the well remember graze ground and the dhak tree where gray brother have wait for he on the morning that he kill shere khan angry as he be at the whole breed and community of man something jump up in his throat and make he catch his breath when he look at the village roof he notice that every one have come in from the field unusually early and that instead of get to their evening cooking they gather in crowd under the village tree and chatter and shout man must always be make trap for man or they be not content say mowgli last night it be mowgli but that night seem many rain ago to night it be messua and her man to morrow and for very many night after it will be mowgli turn again he creep along outside the wall till he come to messua ’s hut and look through the window into the room there lie messua gag and bind hand and foot breathe hard and groan her husband be tie to the gaily paint bedstead the door of the hut that open into the street be shut fast and three or four people be sit with their back to it
mowgli know the manner and custom of the villager very fairly he argue that so long as they could eat and talk and smoke they would not do anything else
but as soon as they have feed they would begin to be dangerous buldeo would be come in before long and if his escort have do its duty buldeo would have very interesting tale to tell so he go in through the window and stoop over the man and the woman cut their thong pull out the gag and look round the hut for some milk messua be half wild with pain and fear she have be beat and stone all the morning and mowgli put his hand over her mouth just in time to stop scream her husband be only bewildered and angry and sit pick dust and thing out of his tear beard
know know he would come messua sob at last now do know that he be my son and she hug mowgli to her heart up to that time mowgli have be perfectly steady
but now he begin to tremble all over and that surprise he immensely why be these thong why have they tie thee he ask after pause to be put to the death for make son of thee what else say the man sullenly messua say nothing but it be at her wound that mowgli look and they hear he grit his tooth when he see the blood whose work be this say he there be price to pay the work of all the village be too rich have too many cattle therefore she and be witch because we give thee shelter do not understand let messua tell the tale give thee milk nathoo dost thou remember messua say timidly because thou wast my son whom the tiger take and because love thee very dearly they say that be thy mother the mother of devil and therefore worthy of death and what be devil say mowgli death have see the man look up gloomily but messua laugh she say to her husband know say that he be no sorcerer he be my son my son son or sorcerer what good will that do we the man answer we be as dead already yonder be the road to the jungle”--mowgli point through the window your hand and foot be free go now we do not know the jungle my son as as thou know messua begin do not think that could walk far and the man and woman would be upon our back and drag we here again say the husband h’m say mowgli
and he tickle the palm of his hand with the tip of his skin knife have no wish to do harm to any one of this village yet but do not think they will stay thee in little while they will have much else to think upon he lift his head and listen to shout and trample outside
so they have let buldeo come home at last he be send out this morning to kill thee messua cry didst thou meet he
yes we meet he he have tale to tell and while he be tell it there be time to do much but first will learn what they mean think where ye would go and tell when come back he bind through the window and run along again outside the wall of the village till he come within ear shoot of the crowd round the peepul tree buldeo be lie on the ground cough and groan and every one be ask he question his hair have fall about his shoulder his hand and leg be skin from climb up tree and he could hardly speak
but he feel the importance of his position keenly from time to time he say something about devil and sing devil and magic enchantment just to give the crowd taste of what be come then he call for water bah say mowgli chatter chatter talk talk man be blood brother of the bandar log now he must wash his mouth with water now he must blow smoke and when all that be do he have still his story to tell they be very wise people man they will leave no one to guard messua till their ear be stuff with buldeo ’s tale and grow as lazy as they he shake himself and glide back to the hut just as he be at the window he feel touch on his foot mother say he for he know that tongue
well what dost thou here hear my child sing through the wood and follow the one love well little frog have desire to see that woman who give thee milk say mother wolf all wet with the dew they have bind and mean to kill she have cut those tie and she go with her man through the jungle also will follow be old but not yet toothless mother wolf rear herself up on end and look through the window into the dark of the hut in minute she drop noiselessly and all she say be give thee thy first milk but bagheera speak truth man go to man at the last maybe say mowgli with very unpleasant look on his face but to night be very far from that trail wait here but do not let she see thou wast never afraid of little frog say mother wolf back into the high grass and blot herself out as she know how and now say mowgli cheerfully as he swing into the hut again they be all sit round buldeo who be say that which do not happen when his talk be finish they say they will assuredly come here with the red with fire and burn you both and then have speak to my man say messua khanhiwara be thirty mile from here but at khanhiwara we may find the english-- and what pack be they say mowgli do not know they be white and it be say that they govern all the land and do not suffer people to burn or beat each other without witness if we can get thither to night we live otherwise we die live then no man pass the gate to night
but what do he do messua ’s husband be on his hand and knee dig up the earth in one corner of the hut it be his little money say messua we can take nothing else ah yes the stuff that pass from hand to hand and never grow warm do they need it outside this place also say mowgli the man star angrily he be fool and no devil he mutter with the money can buy horse we be too bruise to walk far and the village will follow we in an hour say they will not follow till choose but horse be well think of for messua be tired her husband stand up and knot the last of the rupee into his waist cloth mowgli help messua through the window and the cool night air revive she but the jungle in the starlight look very dark and terrible
ye know the trail to khanhiwara mowgli whisper they nod remember now not to be afraid and there be no need to go quickly only only there may be some small singing in the jungle behind you and before think you we would have risk night in the jungle through anything less than the fear of burn it be well to be kill by beast than by man say messua ’s husband but messua look at mowgli and smile say mowgli go on just as though he be baloo repeat an old jungle law for the hundredth time to foolish cub--“i say that not tooth in the jungle be bar against you not foot in the jungle be lift against you neither man nor beast shall stay you till you come within eye shot of khanhiwara there will be watch about you he turn quickly to messua say he do not believe but thou wilt believe ay surely my son man ghost or wolf of the jungle believe he will be afraid when he hear my people sing thou wilt know and understand go now and slowly for there be no need of any haste the gate be shut messua fling herself sob at mowgli ’s foot
but he lift she very quickly with shiver then she hang about his neck and call he every name of blessing she could think of but her husband look enviously across his field and say if we reach khanhiwara and get the ear of the english will bring such lawsuit against the brahmin and old buldeo and the other as shall eat the village to the bone they shall pay twice over for my crop untilled and my buffalo unfed will have great justice mowgli laugh do not know what justice be but come next rain and see what be leave they go off toward the jungle and mother wolf leap from her place of hiding follow say mowgli and look to it that all the jungle know these two be safe give tongue little would call bagheera the long low howl rise and fall and mowgli see messua ’s husband flinch and turn half minded to run back to the hut go on mowgli call cheerfully say there might be sing that call will follow up to khanhiwara it be favour of the jungle messua urge her husband forward and the darkness shut down on they and mother wolf as bagheera rise up almost under mowgli ’s foot tremble with delight of the night that drive the jungle people wild be ashamed of thy brother he say purr do they not sing sweetly to buldeo
say mowgli too well too well they make even forget my pride and by the broken lock that free go sing through the jungle as though be out woo in the spring didst thou not hear we have other game afoot ask buldeo if he like the song but where be the four do not wish one of the man pack to leave the gate to night what need of the four then say bagheera shift from foot to foot his eye ablaze and purr louder than ever can hold they little brother be it kill at last the singing and the sight of the man climb up the tree have make very ready who be man that we should care for he the naked brown digger the hairless and toothless the eater of earth have follow he all day at noon in the white sunlight herd he as the wolf herd buck be bagheera bagheera bagheera as dance with my shadow
so dance with those man the great panther leap as kitten leap at dead leaf whirl overhead strike leave and right into the empty air that sing under the stroke land noiselessly and leap again and again while the half purr half growl gather head as steam rumble in boiler be bagheera in the jungle in the night and my strength be in who shall stay my stroke man cub with one blow of my paw could beat thy head flat as dead frog in the summer strike then say mowgli in the dialect of the village not the talk of the jungle and the human word bring bagheera to full stop fling back on haunch that quiver under he his head just at the level of mowgli ’s once more mowgli star as he have star at the rebellious cub full into the beryl green eye till the red glare behind their green go out like the light of lighthouse shut off twenty mile across the sea till the eye drop and the big head with they drop lower and low and the red rasp of tongue grate on mowgli ’s instep brother brother brother the boy whisper stroke steadily and lightly from the neck along the heaving back be still be still it be the fault of the night and no fault of thine it be the smell of the night say bagheera penitently this air cry aloud to but how dost thou know of course the air round an indian village be full of all kind of smell and to any creature who do nearly all his thinking through his nose smell be as maddening as music and drug be to human be mowgli gentle the panther for few minute long
and he lie down like cat before fire his paw tuck under his breast and his eye half shut thou art of the jungle and not of the jungle he say at last and be only black panther but love thee little brother they be very long at their talk under the tree mowgli say without notice the last sentence buldeo must have tell many tale they should come soon to drag the woman and her man out of the trap and put they into the red flower they will find that trap spring ho ho nay listen say bagheera the fever be out of my blood now let they find there few would leave their house after meet it be not the first time have be in cage and do not think they will tie with cord be wise then say mowgli laugh for he be begin to feel as reckless as the panther who have glide into the hut bagheera grunt this place be rank with man but here be just such bed as they give to lie upon in the king ’s cage at oodeypore now lie down
