mowgli hear the string of the cot crack under the great brute ’s weight by the broken lock that free they will think they have catch big game come and sit beside little brother we will give they good hunting together
no have another thought in my stomach the man pack shall not know what share have in the sport make thine own hunt do not wish to see they be it so say bagheera ah now they come the conference under the peepul tree have be grow noisy and noisy at the far end of the village it break in wild yell and rush up the street of man and woman wave club and bamboo and sickle and knife buldeo and the brahmin be at the head of it but the mob be close at their heel and they cry the witch and the wizard let we see if hot coin will make they confess burn the hut over their head we will teach they to shelter wolf devil nay beat they first more torch buldeo heat the gun barrel here be some little difficulty with the catch of the door it have be very firmly fasten but the crowd tear it away bodily and the light of the torch stream into the room where stretch at full length on the bed his paw cross and lightly hang down over one end black as the pit and terrible as demon be bagheera there be one half minute of desperate silence as the front rank of the crowd claw and tear their way back from the threshold and in that minute bagheera raise his head and yawn elaborately carefully and ostentatiously as he would yawn when he wish to insult an equal the fringed lip draw back and up the red tongue curl the low jaw drop and drop till you could see half way down the hot gullet and the gigantic dog tooth stand clear to the pit of the gum till they ring together upper and under with the snick of steel face ward shoot home round the edge of safe next instant the street be empty bagheera have leap back through the window and stand at mowgli ’s side while yell scream torrent scramble and tumble one over another in their panic haste to get to their own hut they will not stir till day come say bagheera quietly and now the silence of the afternoon sleep seem to have overtake the village but as they listen they could hear the sound of heavy grain box be drag over earthen floor and set down against door bagheera be quite right the village would not stir till daylight mowgli sit still and think and his face grow dark and dark what have do say bagheera at last come to his foot fawn nothing but great good watch they now till the day mowgli run off into the jungle and drop like dead man across rock and sleep and sleep the day round and the night back again when he wake bagheera be at his side and there be newly kill buck at his foot bagheera watch curiously while mowgli go to work with his skin knife eat and drink and turn over with his chin in his hand the man and the woman be come safe within eye shot of khanhiwara bagheera say thy lair mother send the word back by chil the kite they find horse before midnight of the night they be free and go very quickly be not that well that be well say mowgli and thy man pack in the village do not stir till the sun be high this morning then they eat their food and run back quickly to their house do they by chance see thee it may have be be roll in the dust before the gate at dawn and may have make also some small song to myself now little brother there be nothing more to do come hunt with and baloo he have new hive that he wish to show and we all desire thee back again as of old take off that look which make even afraid the man and woman will not be put into the red flower and all go well in the jungle be it not true let we forget the man pack they shall be forget in little while where do hathi feed to night where he choose who can answer for the silent one
but why what be there hathi can do which we can not bid he and his three son come here to but indeed and truly little brother it be not it be not seemly to say come and go to hathi remember he be the master of the jungle and before the man pack change the look on thy face he teach thee the master word of the jungle that be all one have master word for he now bid he come to mowgli the frog
and if he do not hear at first bid he come because of the sack of the field of bhurtpore the sack of the field of bhurtpore bagheera repeat two or three time to make sure hathi can but be angry at the bad and would give moon ’s hunt to hear master word that compel the silent one he go away leave mowgli stab furiously with his skin knife into the earth mowgli have never see human blood in his life before till he have see and what mean much more to he smell messua ’s blood on the thong that bind she and messua have be kind to he and so far as he know anything about love he love messua as completely as he hat the rest of mankind but deeply as he loathe they their talk their cruelty and their cowardice not for anything the jungle have to offer could he bring himself to take human life and have that terrible scent of blood back again in his nostril his plan be simple but much more thorough and he laugh to himself when he think that it be one of old buldeo ’s tale tell under the peepul tree in the evening that have put the idea into his head it be master word bagheera whisper in his ear they be feed by the river
and they obey as though they be bullock look
where they come now hathi and his three son have arrive in their usual way without sound the mud of the river be still fresh on their flank and hathi be thoughtfully chew the green stem of young plantain tree that he have gouge up with his tusk but every line in his vast body show to bagheera who could see thing when he come across they that it be not the master of the jungle speak to man cub but one who be afraid come before one who be not his three son roll side by side behind their father mowgli hardly lift his head as hathi give he good hunting he keep he swing and rock and shift from one foot to another for long time before he speak and when he open his mouth it be to bagheera not to the elephant will tell tale that be tell to by the hunter ye hunt to day say mowgli it concern an elephant old and wise who fall into trap and the sharpen stake in the pit scar he from little above his heel to the crest of his shoulder leave white mark mowgli throw out his hand and as hathi wheel the moonlight show long white scar on his slaty side as though he have be strike with red hot whip man come to take he from the trap mowgli continue but he break his rope for he be strong and go away till his wound be heal then come he angry by night to the field of those hunter and remember now that he have three son these thing happen many many rain ago and very far away among the field of bhurtpore what come to those field at the next reaping hathi they be reap by and by my three son say hathi and to the ploughing that follow the reaping say mowgli there be no ploughing say hathi and to the man that live by the green crop on the ground say mowgli they go away and to the hut in which the man sleep say mowgli we tear the roof to piece and the jungle swallow up the wall say hathi and what more say mowgli as much good ground as can walk over in two night from the east to the west and from the north to the south as much as can walk over in three night the jungle take we let in the jungle upon five village and in those village and in their land the graze ground and the soft crop ground there be not one man to day who take his food from the ground that be the sack of the field of bhurtpore which and my three son do and now ask man cub how the news of it come to thee say hathi man tell and now see even buldeo can speak truth it be well do hathi with the white mark but the second time it shall be do well for the reason that there be man to direct thou knowest the village of the man pack that cast out they be idle senseless and cruel they play with their mouth and they do not kill the weak for food but for sport when they be full feed they would throw their own breed into the red flower this have see it be not well that they should live here any more hate they kill then say the young of hathi ’s three son pick up tuft of grass dust it against his fore leg and throw it away while his little red eye glance furtively from side to side what good be white bone to mowgli answer angrily be the cub of wolf to play in the sun with raw head have kill shere khan and his hide rot on the council rock
but do not know whither shere khan be go and my stomach be still empty now will take that which can see and touch let in the jungle upon that village hathi bagheera shiver and cower down he could understand if the bad come to the bad quick rush down the village street and right and leave blow into crowd or crafty killing of man as they plough in the twilight but this scheme for deliberately blot out an entire village from the eye of man and beast frighten he now he see why mowgli have send for hathi no one but the long live elephant could plan and carry through such war let they run as the man run from the field of bhurtpore till we have the rain water for the only plough and the noise of the rain on the thick leaf for the pattering of their spindle till bagheera and lair in the house of the brahmin and the buck drink at the tank behind the temple let in the jungle hathi
but we have no quarrel with they and it need the red rage of great pain
ere we tear down the place where man sleep say hathi doubtfully be ye the only eater of grass in the jungle drive in your people let the deer and the pig and the nilghai look to it ye need never show hand ’s breadth of hide till the field be naked let in the jungle hathi there will be no kill my tusk be red at the sack of the field of bhurtpore and would not wake that smell again nor i. do not wish even their bone to lie on the clean earth let they go and find fresh lair they can not stay here have see and smell the blood of the woman that give food the woman whom they would have kill but for only the smell of the new grass on their door step can take away that smell it burn in my mouth let in the jungle hathi ah say hathi
so do the scar of the stake burn on my hide till we watch the village die under in the spring growth now see thy war shall be our war we will let in the jungle mowgli have hardly time to catch his breath he be shake all over with rage and hate before the place where the elephant have stand be empty and bagheera be look at he with terror by the broken lock that free say the black panther at last art thou the naked thing speak for in the pack when all be young master of the jungle when my strength go speak for speak for baloo speak for we all we be cub before thee snap twig under foot fawn that have lose their doe the idea of bagheera be stray fawn upset mowgli altogether and he laugh and catch his breath and sob and laugh again till he have to jump into pool to make himself stop then he swim round and round duck in and out of the bar of the moonlight like the frog his namesake by this time hathi and his three son have turn each to one point of the compass and be stride silently down the valley mile away they go on and on for two day march that be to say long sixty mile through the jungle and every step they take and every wave of their trunk be know and note and talk over by mang and chil and the monkey people and all the bird then they begin to feed and feed quietly for week or so hathi and his son be like kaa the rock python they never hurry till they have to at the end of that time and none know who have start it rumour go through the jungle that there be well food and water to be find in such and such valley the pig who of course will go to the end of the earth for full meal move first by company scuffle over the rock and the deer follow with the small wild fox that live on the dead and die of the herd and the heavy shouldered nilghai move parallel with the deer and the wild buffalo of the swamp come after the nilghai
the least little thing would have turn the scatter straggling drive that graze and saunter and drank and graze again but whenever there be an alarm some one would rise up and soothe they at one time it would be ikki the porcupine full of news of good feed just little far on at another mang would cry cheerily and flap down glade to show it be all empty or baloo his mouth full of root would shamble alongside waver line and half frighten half romp it clumsily back to the proper road very many creature break back or run away or lose interest but very many be leave to go forward at the end of another ten day or so the situation be this the deer and the pig and the nilghai be mill round and round in circle of eight or ten mile radius while the eater of flesh skirmish round its edge and the centre of that circle be the village and round the village the crop be ripen and in the crop sit man on what they call machan platform like pigeon perch make of stick at the top of four pole to scare away bird and other stealer then the deer be coax no more the eater of flesh be close behind they and force they forward and inward it be dark night when hathi and his three son slip down from the jungle and break off the pole of the machan with their trunk they fall as snap stalk of hemlock in bloom fall and the man that tumble from they hear the deep gurgling of the elephant in their ear then the vanguard of the bewildered army of the deer break down and flood into the village graze ground and the plough field and the sharp hoof root wild pig come with they and what the deer leave the pig spoil and from time to time an alarm of wolf would shake the herd and they would rush to and fro desperately tread down the young barley and cut flat the bank of the irrigating channel before the dawn break the pressure on the outside of the circle give way at one point the eater of flesh have fall back and leave an open path to the south and drive upon drove of buck flee along it other who be bold lie up in the thicket to finish their meal next night
but the work be practically do when the villager look in the morning they see their crop be lose and that mean death if they do not get away for they live year in and year out as near to starvation as the jungle be near to they when the buffalo be send to graze the hungry brute find that the deer have clear the graze ground and so wander into the jungle and drift off with their wild mate and when twilight fall the three or four pony that belong to the village lie in their stable with their head beat in only bagheera could have give those stroke and only bagheera would have think of insolently drag the last carcass to the open street the villager have no heart to make fire in the field that night so hathi and his three son go glean among what be leave and where hathi glean there be no need to follow the man decide to live on their store seed corn until the rain have fall and then to take work as servant till they could catch up with the lose year but as the grain dealer be think of his well fill crate of corn and the price he would levy at the sale of it hathi ’s sharp tusk be pick out the corner of his mud house and smash open the big wicker chest leepe with cow dung where the precious stuff lay when that last loss be discover it be the brahmin ’s turn to speak he have pray to his own god without answer it might be he say that unconsciously the village have offend some one of the god of the jungle for beyond doubt the jungle be against they so they send for the head man of the near tribe of wander gond little wise and very black hunter live in the deep jungle whose father come of the old race in india the aboriginal owner of the land they make the gond welcome with what they have and he stand on one leg his bow in his hand and two or three poison arrow stick through his top knot look half afraid and half contemptuously at the anxious villager and their ruin field they wish to know whether his god the old god be angry with they and what sacrifice should be offer the gond say nothing but pick up trail of the karela the vine that bear the bitter wild gourd and lace it to and fro across the temple door in the face of the staring red hindu image then he push with his hand in the open air along the road to khanhiwara and go back to his jungle and watch the jungle people drift through it he know that when the jungle move only white man can hope to turn it aside there be no need to ask his meaning the wild gourd would grow where they have worship their god and the soon they save themselves the well
but it be hard to tear village from its mooring they stay on as long as any summer food be leave to they and they try to gather nut in the jungle but shadow with glaring eye watch they and roll before they even at mid day and when they run back afraid to their wall on the tree trunk they have pas not five minute before the bark would be strip and chisel with the stroke of some great taloned paw the more they keep to their village the bold grow the wild thing that gambol and bellow on the graze ground by the waingunga they have no time to patch and plaster the rear wall of the empty byre that back on to the jungle the wild pig trample they down and the knotty root vine hurry after and throw their elbow over the new win ground and the coarse grass bristle behind the vine like the lance of goblin army follow retreat the unmarried man run away first and carry the news far and near that the village be doom who could fight they say against the jungle or the god of the jungle when the very village cobra have leave his hole in the platform under the peepul tree so their little commerce with the outside world shrink as the trodden path across the open grow few and faint at last the nightly trumpeting of hathi and his three son cease to trouble they for they have no more to be rob of the crop on the ground and the seed in the ground have be take the outlying field be already lose their shape and it be time to throw themselves on the charity of the english at khanhiwara native fashion they delay their departure from one day to another till the first rain catch they and the unmended roof let in flood and the graze ground stand ankle deep and all life come on with rush after the heat of the summer then they wad out man woman and child through the blind hot rain of the morning but turn naturally for one farewell look at their home they hear as the last burden family file through the gate crash of fall beam and thatch behind the wall they see shiny snaky black trunk lift for an instant scatter sodden thatch it disappear and there be another crash follow by squeal hathi have be pluck off the roof of the hut as you pluck water lily and rebound beam have prick he he need only this to unchain his full strength for of all thing in the jungle the wild elephant enrage be the most wantonly destructive he kick backward at mud wall that crumble at the stroke and crumble melt to yellow mud under the torrent of rain then he wheel and squeal and tear through the narrow street lean against the hut right and leave shiver the crazy door and crumple up the cave while his three son rag behind as they have rag at the sack of the field of bhurtpore the jungle will swallow these shell say quiet voice in the wreckage it be the outer wall that must lie down and mowgli with the rain sluice over his bare shoulder and arm leap back from wall that be settle like tired buffalo all in good time pant hathi
but my tusk be red at bhurtpore to the outer wall child with the head now the four push side by side the outer wall bulge split and fall and the villager dumb with horror see the savage clay streak head of the wrecker in the ragged gap then they flee houseless and foodless down the valley as their village shred and toss and trample melt behind they month later the place be dimpled mound cover with soft green young stuff and by the end of the rain there be the roar jungle in full blast on the spot that have be under plough not six month before mowgli ’s song against people will let loose against you the fleet footed vines-- will call in the jungle to stamp out your line the roof shall fade before it the house beam shall fall and the karela the bitter karela shall cover it all in the gate of these your council my people shall sing in the door of these your garner the bat folk shall cling and the snake shall be your watchman by hearthstone unswept for the karela the bitter karela shall fruit where ye sleep
ye shall not see my striker ye shall hear they and guess by night before the moon rise will send for my cess and the wolf shall be your herdsman by landmark remove for the karela the bitter karela shall seed where ye love will reap your field before you at the hand of host ye shall glean behind my reaper for the bread that be lose and the deer shall be your oxen by headland untilled for the karela the bitter karela shall leaf where ye build have untie against you the club footed vine have send in the jungle to swamp out your line the tree the tree be on you the house beam shall fall and the karela the bitter karela shall cover you all the undertaker when ye say to tabaqui my brother when ye call the hyena to meat ye may cry the full truce with jacala the belly that run on four foot jungle law respect the age it be thick voice muddy voice that would have make you shudder voice like something soft breaking in two there be quaver in it croak and whine respect the aged companion of the river respect the age nothing could be see on the broad reach of the river except little fleet of square sail wooden pin barge load with building stone that have just come under the railway bridge and be drive down stream they put their clumsy helm over to avoid the sand bar make by the scour of the bridge pier and as they pass three abreast the horrible voice begin again brahmin of the river respect the aged and infirm boatman turn where he sit on the gunwale lift up his hand say something that be not blessing and the boat creak on through the twilight the broad indian river that look more like chain of little lake than stream be as smooth as glass reflect the sandy red sky in mid channel but splash with patch of yellow and dusky purple near and under the low bank little creek run into the river in the wet season but now their dry mouth hang clear above water line on the left shore and almost under the railway bridge stand mud and brick and thatch and stick village whose main street full of cattle go back to their byre run straight to the river and end in sort of rude brick pier head where people who want to wash could wade in step by step that be the ghaut of the village of mugger ghaut night be fall fast over the field of lentil and rice and cotton in the low lie grind yearly flood by the river over the reed that fringe the elbow of the bend and the tangled jungle of the graze ground behind the still reed the parrot and crow who have be chatter and shout over their evening drink have fly inland to roost cross the out go battalion of the fly fox and cloud upon cloud of water bird come whistle and honk to the cover of the reed bed there be goose barrel head and black back teal widgeon mallard and sheldrake with curlews and here and there flamingo lumber adjutant crane bring up the rear fly as though each slow stroke would be his last respect the aged brahmin of the river respect the age the adjutant half turn his head sheer little in the direction of the voice and land stiffly on the sand bar below the bridge then you see what ruffianly brute he really be his back view be immensely respectable for he stand nearly six foot high and look rather like very proper bald head parson in front it be different for his ally sloper like head and neck have not feather to they and there be horrible raw skin pouch on his neck under his chin hold all for the thing his pick axe beak might steal his leg be long and thin and skinny but he move they delicately and look at they with pride as he preen down his ashy gray tail feather glance over the smooth of his shoulder and stiffen into stand at attention mangy little jackal who have be yap hungrily on low bluff cock up his ear and tail and scuttere across the shallow to join the adjutant he be the low of his caste not that the good of jackal be good for much but this one be peculiarly low be half beggar half criminal clean up of village rubbish heap desperately timid or wildly bold everlastingly hungry and full of cunning that never do he any good he say shake himself dolefully as he land may the red mange destroy the dog of this village have three bite for each flea upon and all because look only look mark you at an old shoe in cow byre can eat mud he scratch himself under his left ear hear say the adjutant in voice like blunt saw go through thick board--“i hear there be new bear puppy in that same shoe to hear be one thing to know be another say the jackal who have very fair knowledge of proverb pick up by listen to man round the village fire of an evening quite true so to make sure take care of that puppy while the dog be busy elsewhere they be very busy say the jackal well must not go to the village hunting for scrap yet awhile
and so there truly be blind puppy in that shoe it be here say the adjutant squint over his beak at his full pouch small thing but acceptable now that charity be dead in the world the world be iron in these day wail the jackal then his restless eye catch the least possible ripple on the water and he go on quickly life be hard for we all and doubt not that even our excellent master the pride of the ghaut and the envy of the river---- liar flatterer and jackal be all hatch out of the same egg say the adjutant to nobody in particular for he be rather fine sort of liar on his own account when he take the trouble yes the envy of the river the jackal repeat raise his voice even he doubt not find that since the bridge have be build good food be more scarce but on the other hand though would by no mean say this to his noble face he be so wise and so virtuous as alas be not---- when the jackal own he be gray how black must the jackal be mutter the adjutant he could not see what be come that his food never fail and in consequence---- there be soft grating sound as though boat have just touch in shoal water the jackal spin round quickly and face it be always good to face the creature he have be talk about it be twenty four foot crocodile case in what look like treble rivet boiler plate stud and keeled and crest the yellow point of his upper tooth just overhang his beautifully fluted low jaw it be the blunt nosed mugger of mugger ghaut old than any man in the village who have give his name to the village the demon of the ford before the railway bridge come murderer man eater and local fetish in one he lie with his chin in the shallow keep his place by an almost invisible rippling of his tail and well the jackal know that one stroke of that same tail in the water would carry the mugger up the bank with the rush of steam engine auspiciously meet protector of the poor he fawn back at every word delectable voice be hear
and we come in the hope of sweet conversation my tailless presumption while wait here lead indeed to speak of thee it be my hope that nothing be overhear now the jackal have speak just to be listen to for he know flattery be the good way of get thing to eat and the mugger know that the jackal have speak for this end and the jackal know that the mugger know and the mugger know that the jackal know that the mugger know
and so they be all very contented together the old brute push and pant and grunt up the bank mumble respect the aged and infirm and all the time his little eye burn like coal under the heavy horny eyelid on the top of his triangular head as he shove his bloated barrel body along between his crutche leg then he settle down and accustom as the jackal be to his way he could not help start for the hundredth time when he see how exactly the mugger imitate log adrift on the bar he have even take pain to lie at the exact angle naturally strand log would make with the water have regard to the current of the season at the time and place all this be only matter of habit of course because the mugger have come ashore for pleasure but crocodile be never quite full and if the jackal have be deceive by the likeness he would not have live to philosophise over it my child hear nothing say the mugger shut one eye the water be in my ear and also be faint with hunger since the railway bridge be build my people at my village have cease to love and that be break my heart ah shame say the jackal so noble heart too but man be all alike to my mind nay there be very great difference indeed the mugger answer gently some be as lean as boat pole other again be fat as young ja dog never would causelessly revile man they be of all fashion but the long year have show that one with another they be very good man woman and child have no fault to find with they and remember child he who rebuke the world be rebuke by the world flattery be bad than an empty tin can in the belly but that which we have just hear be wisdom say the adjutant bring down one foot consider though their ingratitude to this excellent one begin the jackal tenderly
nay nay not ingratitude the mugger say they do not think for other that be all but have notice lie at my station below the ford that the stair of the new bridge be cruelly hard to climb both for old people and young child the old indeed be not so worthy of consideration but be grieve be truly grieve on account of the fat child still think in little while when the newness of the bridge have wear away we shall see my people ’s bare brown leg bravely splash through the ford as before then the old mugger will be honour again but surely see marigold wreath float off the edge of the ghaut only this noon say the adjutant marigold wreath be sign of reverence all india over an error an error it be the wife of the sweetmeat seller she lose her eyesight year by year and can not tell log from the mugger of the ghaut see the mistake when she throw the garland for be lie at the very foot of the ghaut and have she take another step might have show she some little difference
yet she mean well and we must consider the spirit of the offering what good be marigold wreath when one be on the rubbish heap say the jackal hunting for flea but keep one wary eye on his protector of the poor true
but they have not yet begin to make the rubbish heap that shall carry five time have see the river draw back from the village and make new land at the foot of the street five time have see the village rebuild on the bank and shall see it build yet five time more be no faithless fish hunt gavial at kasi to day and prayag to morrow as the saying be but the true and constant watcher of the ford it be not for nothing child that the village bear my name and he who watch long as the saying be shall at last have his reward have watch long very long nearly all my life and my reward have be bite and blow say the jackal ho ho ho roar the adjutant in august be the jackal bear the rain fall in september now such fearful flood as this say he can not remember there be one very unpleasant peculiarity about the adjutant at uncertain time he suffer from acute attack of the fidget or cramp in his leg and though he be more virtuous to behold than any of the crane who be all immensely respectable he fly off into wild cripple stilt war dance half open his wing and bob his bald head up and down while for reason well know to himself he be very careful to time his bad attack with his nasty remark at the last word of his song he come to attention again ten time adjutaunter than before the jackal wince though he be full three season old but you can not resent an insult from person with beak yard long and the power of drive it like javelin the adjutant be most notorious coward but the jackal be bad we must live before we can learn say the mugger and there be this to say little jackal be very common child but such mugger as be be not common for all that be not proud since pride be destruction but take notice it be fate and against his fate no one who swim or walk or run should say anything at all be well contented with fate with good luck keen eye and the custom of consider whether creek or backwater have an outlet to it ere you ascend much may be do once hear that even the protector of the poor make mistake say the jackal viciously true but there my fate help it be before have come to my full growth before the last famine but three by the right and leave of gunga how full use the stream to be in those day yes be young and unthinking and when the flood come who so please as little make very happy then the village be deep in flood and swim above the ghaut and go far inland up to the rice field and they be deep in good mud remember also pair of bracelet glass they be and trouble not little that find that evening yes glass bracelet and if my memory serve well shoe should have shake off both shoe but be hungry learn well later and so feed and rest
but when be ready to go to the river again the flood have fall and walk through the mud of the main street who but come out all my people priest and woman and child and look upon they with benevolence the mud be not good place to fight in say boatman get axis and kill he for he be the mugger of the ford not so say the brahmin look he be drive the flood before he he be the godling of the village then they throw many flower at and by happy think one lead goat across the road how good how very good be goat say the jackal hairy too hairy and when find in the water more than likely to hide cross shape hook but that goat accept and go down to the ghaut in great honour later my fate send the boatman who have desire to cut off my tail with an axe his boat ground upon an old shoal which you would not remember we be not all jackal here say the adjutant be it the shoal make where the stone boat sink in the year of the great drouth long shoal that last three flood there be two say the mugger an upper and low shoal ay forget channel divide they and later dry up again say the adjutant who pride himself on his memory on the low shoal my well wisher ’s craft grind he be sleep in the bow and half awake leap over to his waist
no it be no more than to his knee to push off his empty boat go on and touch again below the next reach as the river run then follow because know man would come out to drag it ashore and do they do so say the jackal little awe stricken this be hunt on scale that impress he there and low down they do go no far but that give three in one day well feed manji boatman all and except in the case of the last then be careless never cry to warn those on the bank ah noble sport but what cleverness and great judgment it require say the jackal not cleverness child but only think little thought in life be like salt upon rice as the boatman say and have think deeply always the gavial my cousin the fish eater have tell how hard it be for he to follow his fish and how one fish differ from the other and how he must know they all both together and apart say that be wisdom but on the other hand my cousin the gavial live among his people my people do not swim in company with their mouth out of the water as rewa do nor do they constantly rise to the surface of the water and turn over on their side like mohoo and little chapta nor do they gather in shoal after flood like batchua and chilwa all be very good eating say the adjutant clatter his beak
so my cousin say and make great to do over hunt they but they do not climb the bank to escape his sharp nose my people be otherwise their life be on the land in the house among the cattle must know what they do and what they be about to do and add the tail to the trunk as the saying be make up the whole elephant be there green branch and an iron ring hang over doorway the old mugger know that boy have be bear in that house and must some day come down to the ghaut to play be maiden to be marry the old mugger know for he see the man carry gift back and forth and she too come down to the ghaut to bathe before her wedding and he be there have the river change its channel and make new land where there be only sand before the mugger know now of what use be that knowledge say the jackal the river have shift even in my little life indian river be nearly always move about in their bed and will shift sometimes as much as two or three mile in season drown the field on one bank and spread good silt on the other there be no knowledge so useful say the mugger for new land mean new quarrel the mugger know the mugger know as soon as the water have drain off he creep up the little creek that man think would not hide dog and there he wait presently come farmer say he will plant cucumber here and melon there in the new land that the river have give he he feel the good mud with his bare toe anon come another say he will put onion and carrot and sugar cane in such and such place they meet as boat adrift meet and each roll his eye at the other under the big blue turban the old mugger see and hear each call the other brother and they go to mark out the boundary of the new land the mugger hurry with they from point to point shuffle very low through the mud now they begin to quarrel now they say hot word now they pull turban now they lift up their lathis club and at last one fall backward into the mud and the other run away when he come back the dispute be settle as the iron bind bamboo of the loser witness yet they be not grateful to the mugger no they cry murder and their family fight with stick twenty side my people be good people upland jats malwai of the bet they do not give blow for sport and when the fight be do the old mugger wait far down the river out of sight of the village behind the kikar scrub yonder then come they down my broad shoulder jat eight or nine together under the star bear the dead man upon bed they be old man with gray beard and voice as deep as mine they light little fire ah how well know that fire!--and they drink tobacco and they nod their head together forward in ring or sideways toward the dead man upon the bank they say the english law will come with rope for this matter and that such man ’s family will be ashamed because such man must be hang in the great square of the jail then say the friend of the dead let he hang and the talk be all to do over again once twice twenty time in the long night then say one at last the fight be fair fight let we take blood money little more than be offer by the slayer and we will say no more about it then do they haggle over the blood money for the dead be strong man leave many son yet before amratvela sunrise they put the fire to he little as the custom be and the dead man come to and he say no more about it my child the mugger know the mugger know and my malwah jat be good people they be too close too narrow in the hand for my crop croak the adjutant they waste not the polish on the cow ’s horn as the saying be and again who can glean after malwai
ah glean they say the mugger now in calcutta of the south in the old day the adjutant go on everything be throw into the street
and we pick and choose those wear dainty season but to day they keep their street as clean as the outside of an egg and my people fly away to be clean be one thing to dust sweep and sprinkle seven time day weary the very god themselves there be down country jackal have it from brother who tell that in calcutta of the south all the jackal be as fat as otter in the rain say the jackal his mouth watering at the bare thought of it ah
but the white face be there the english
and they bring dog from somewhere down the river in boat big fat dog to keep those same jackal lean say the adjutant they be then as hard hearted as these people might have know neither earth sky nor water show charity to jackal see the tent of white face last season after the rain and also take new yellow bridle to eat the white face do not dress their leather in the proper way it make very sick that be well than my case say the adjutant when be in my third season young and bold bird go down to the river where the big boat come in the boat of the english be thrice as big as this village he have be as far as delhi and say all the people there walk on their head mutter the jackal the mugger open his left eye and look keenly at the adjutant it be true the big bird insist liar only lie when he hop to be believe no one who have not see those boat could believe this truth that be more reasonable say the mugger and then from the inside of this boat they be take out great piece of white stuff which in little while turn to water much split off and fall about on the shore and the rest they swiftly put into house with thick wall but boatman who laugh take piece no large than small dog and throw it to all my people swallow without reflection and that piece swallow as be our custom immediately be afflict with an excessive cold which begin in my crop run down to the extreme end of my toe and deprive even of speech while the boatman laugh at never have feel such cold dance in my grief and amazement till could recover my breath and then dance and cry out against the falseness of this world and the boatman deride till they fall down the chief wonder of the matter set aside that marvellous coldness be that there be nothing at all in my crop when have finish my lament the adjutant have do his very good to describe his feeling after swallow seven pound lump of wenham lake ice off an american ice ship in the day before calcutta make her ice by machinery
but as he do not know what ice be and as the mugger and the jackal know rather less the tale miss fire anything say the mugger shut his left eye again--“anythe be possible that come out of boat thrice the size of mugger ghaut my village be not small one there be whistle overhead on the bridge and the delhi mail slide across all the carriage gleam with light and the shadow faithfully follow along the river it clank away into the dark again but the mugger and the jackal be so well used to it that they never turn their head be that anything less wonderful than boat thrice the size of mugger ghaut say the bird look up see that build child stone by stone see the bridge pier rise and when the man fall off they be wondrous sure footed for the most part but when they fall be ready after the first pier be make they never think to look down the stream for the body to burn there again save much trouble there be nothing strange in the building of the bridge say the mugger but that which go across pull the roofed cart that be strange the adjutant repeat it be past any doubt new breed of bullock some day it will not be able to keep its foothold up yonder and will fall as the man do the old mugger will then be ready the jackal look at the adjutant and the adjutant look at the jackal if there be one thing they be more certain of than another it be that the engine be everything in the wide world except bullock the jackal have watch it time and again from the aloe hedge by the side of the line and the adjutant have see engine since the first locomotive run in india
but the mugger have only look up at the thing from below where the brass dome seem rather like bullock ’s hump yes new kind of bullock the mugger repeat ponderously to make himself quite sure in his own mind and certainly it be bullock say the jackal and again it might be---- begin the mugger pettishly certainly most certainly say the jackal without wait for the other to finish what say the mugger angrily for he could feel that the other know more than he do what might it be never finish my word you say it be bullock it be anything the protector of the poor please be his servant not the servant of the thing that cross the river whatever it be it be white face work say the adjutant and for my own part would not lie out upon place so near to it as this bar you do not know the english as do say the mugger there be white face here when the bridge be build and he would take boat in the evening and shuffle with his foot on the bottom board and whisper be he here be he there bring my gun could hear he before could see he each sound that he make creak and puff and rattle his gun up and down the river as surely as have pick up one of his workman and thus save great expense in wood for the burning so surely would he come down to the ghaut and shout in loud voice that he would hunt and rid the river of the mugger of mugger ghaut child have swim under the bottom of his boat for hour after hour and hear he fire his gun at log and when be well sure he be weary have rise by his side and snap my jaw in his face when the bridge be finish he go away all the english hunt in that fashion except when they be hunt who hunt the white face yap the jackal excitedly no one now but have hunt they in my time remember little of that hunting be young then say the adjutant clatter his beak significantly be well establish here my village be be build for the third time as remember when my cousin the gavial bring word of rich water above benare at first would not go for my cousin who be fish eater do not always know the good from the bad but hear my people talk in the evening and what they say make certain and what do they say the jackal ask they say enough to make the mugger of mugger ghaut leave water and take to my foot go by night use the little stream as they serve but it be the beginning of the hot weather and all stream be low cross dusty road go through tall grass climb hill in the moonlight even rock do climb child consider this well cross the tail of sirhind the waterless before could find the set of the little river that flow gungaward be month ’s journey from my own people and the river that know that be very marvellous what food on the way say the jackal who keep his soul in his little stomach and be not bit impressed by the mugger ’s land travel that which could find cousin say the mugger slowly drag each word now you do not call man cousin in india unless you think you can establish some kind of blood relationship and as it be only in old fairy tale that the mugger ever marry jackal the jackal know for what reason he have be suddenly lift into the mugger ’s family circle if they have be alone he would not have care but the adjutant ’s eye twinkle with mirth at the ugly jest assuredly father might have know say the jackal mugger do not care to be call father of jackal and the mugger of mugger ghaut say as much and great deal more which there be no use in repeat here the protector of the poor have claim kinship how can remember the precise degree moreover we eat the same food he have say it be the jackal ’s reply that make matter rather bad for what the jackal hint at be that the mugger must have eat his food on that land march fresh and fresh every day instead of keep it by he till it be in fit and proper condition as every self respect mugger and most wild beast do when they can indeed one of the bad term of contempt along the river bed be eater of fresh meat it be nearly as bad as call man cannibal that food be eat thirty season ago say the adjutant quietly if we talk for thirty season more it will never come back tell we now what happen when the good water be reach after thy most wonderful land journey if we listen to the howling of every jackal the business of the town would stop as the saying be the mugger must have be grateful for the interruption because he go on with rush by the right and leave of gunga when come there never do see such water be they well then
than the big flood of last season say the jackal that flood be no more than come every five year handful of drown stranger some chicken and dead bullock in muddy water with cross current but the season think of the river be low smooth and even and as the gavial have warn the dead english come down touch each other get my girth in that season my girth and my depth from agra by etawah and the broad water by allahabad---- oh the eddy that set under the wall of the fort at allahabad say the adjutant they come in there like widgeon to the reed and round and round they swing thus he go off into his horrible dance again while the jackal look on enviously he naturally could not remember the terrible year of the mutiny they be talk about the mugger continue yes by allahabad one lie still in the slack water and let twenty go by to pick one and above all the english be not cumber with jewellery and nose ring and anklet as my woman be nowadays to delight in ornament be to end with rope for necklace as the saying be all the mugger of all the river grow fat then but it be my fate to be fat than they all the news be that the english be be hunt into the river and by the right and leave of gunga we believe it be true so far as go south believe it to be true and go down stream beyond monghyr and the tomb that look over the river know that place say the adjutant since those day monghyr be lose city very few live there now thereafter work up stream very slowly and lazily and little above monghyr there come down boatful of white face alive they be as remember woman lie under cloth spread over stick and cry aloud there be never gun fire at we the watcher of the ford in those day all the gun be busy elsewhere we could hear they day and night inland come and go as the wind shift rise up full before the boat because have never see white face alive though know they well otherwise naked white child kneel by the side of the boat and stoop over must need try to trail his hand in the river it be pretty thing to see how child love run water have feed that day but there be yet little unfilled space within still it be for sport and not for food that rise at the child ’s hand they be so clear mark that do not even look when close but they be so small that though my jaw ring true be sure of that the child draw they up swiftly unhurt they must have pas between tooth and tooth those small white hand should have catch he cross wise at the elbow but as say it be only for sport and desire to see new thing that rise at all they cry out one after another in the boat and presently rise again to watch they the boat be too heavy to push over they be only woman but he who trust woman will walk on duckweed in pool as the saying be and by the right and leave of gunga that be truth once woman give some dry skin from fish say the jackal have hop to get her baby but horse food be well than the kick of horse as the saying be what do thy woman do she fire at with short gun of kind have never see before or since five time one after another the mugger must have meet with an old fashioned revolver and stay open mouthed and gap my head in the smoke never do see such thing five time as swiftly as wave my tail thus the jackal who have be grow more and more interested in the story have just time to leap back as the huge tail swing by like scythe not before the fifth shot say the mugger as though he have never dream of stunning one of his listeners--“not before the fifth shot do sink and rise in time to hear boatman tell all those white woman that be most certainly dead one bullet have go under neck plate of mine know not if it be there still for the reason can not turn my head look and see child it will show that my tale be true say the jackal shall an eater of old shoe bone cracker presume to doubt the word of the envy of the river may my tail be bite off by blind puppy if the shadow of such thought have cross my humble mind the protector of the poor have condescend to inform his slave that once in his life he have be wind by woman that be sufficient and will tell the tale to all my child ask for no proof over much civility be sometimes no well than over much discourtesy for as the saying be one can choke guest with curd do not desire that any child of thine should know that the mugger of mugger ghaut take his only wound from woman they will have much else to think of if they get their meat as miserably as do their father it be forget long ago it be never say there never be white woman there be no boat nothing whatever happen at all the jackal wave his brush to show how completely everything be wipe out of his memory and sit down with an air indeed very many thing happen say the mugger beat in his second attempt that night to get the well of his friend neither bore malice however eat and be eat be fair law along the river and the jackal come in for his share of plunder when the mugger have finish meal leave that boat and go up stream and when have reach arrah and the back water behind it there be no more dead english the river be empty for while then come one or two dead in red coat not english but of one kind all hindus and purbeeah then five and six abreast and at last from arrah to the north beyond agra it be as though whole village have walk into the water they come out of little creek one after another as the log come down in the rain when the river rise they rise also in company from the shoal they have rest upon and the fall flood drag they with it across the field and through the jungle by the long hair all night too go north hear the gun and by day the shod foot of man cross fords and that noise which heavy cart wheel make on sand under water and every ripple bring more dead at last even be afraid for say if this thing happen to man how shall the mugger of mugger ghaut escape there be boat too that come up behind without sail burn continually as the cotton boat sometimes burn but never sink ah say the adjutant boat like those come to calcutta of the south they be tall and black they beat up the water behind they with tail and they---- be thrice as big as my village my boat be low and white they beat up the water on either side of they and be no large than the boat of one who speak truth should be they make very afraid and leave water and go back to this my river hide by day and walk by night when could not find little stream to help come to my village again but do not hope to see any of my people there yet they be plough and sowing and reap and go to and fro in their field as quietly as their own cattle be there still good food in the river say the jackal more than have any desire for even and do not eat mud even be tired and as remember little frightened of this constant come down of the silent one hear my people say in my village that all the english be dead but those that come face down with the current be not english as my people see then my people say that it be good to say nothing at all but to pay the tax and plough the land after long time the river clear and those that come down it have be clearly drown by the flood as could well see and though it be not so easy then to get food be heartily glad of it little killing here and there be no bad thing but even the mugger be sometimes satisfied as the saying be most truly marvellous say the jackal be become fat through merely hear about so much good eating and afterward what if it be permit to ask do the protector of the poor do say to myself and by the right and leave of gunga lock my jaw on that vow say would never go rove any more so live by the ghaut very close to my own people and watch over they year after year and they love so much that they throw marigold wreath at my head whenever they see it lift
yes and my fate have be very kind to and the river be good enough to respect my poor and infirm presence
only---- no one be all happy from his beak to his tail say the adjutant sympathetically what do the mugger of mugger ghaut need more that little white child which do not get say the mugger with deep sigh he be very small but have not forget be old now but before die it be my desire to try one new thing it be true they be heavy footed noisy and foolish people and the sport would be small but remember the old day above benare and if the child life he will remember still it may be he go up and down the bank of some river tell how he once pass his hand between the tooth of the mugger of mugger ghaut and live to make tale of it my fate have be very kind but that plague sometimes in my dream the thought of the little white child in the bow of that boat he yawn and close his jaw and now will rest and think keep silent my child and respect the age he turn stiffly and shuffle to the top of the sand bar while the jackal draw back with the adjutant to the shelter of tree strand on the end nearest the railway bridge that be pleasant and profitable life he grin look up inquiringly at the bird who tower above he and not once mark you do he think fit to tell where morsel might have be leave along the bank yet have tell he hundred time of good thing wallow down stream how true be the say all the world forget the jackal and the barber when the news have be tell now he be go to sleep arrh how can jackal hunt with mugger say the adjutant coolly big thief and little thief it be easy to say who get the picking the jackal turn whine impatiently and be go to curl himself up under the tree trunk when suddenly he cower and look up through the draggled branch at the bridge almost above his head what now say the adjutant open his wing uneasily wait till we see the wind blow from we to they but they be not look for we those two man man be it my office protect all india know be holy the adjutant be first class scavenger be allow to go where he please
and so this one never flinch be not worth blow from anything well than an old shoe say the jackal and listen again hark to that footfall he go on that be no country leather but the shod foot of white face listen again iron hit iron up there it be gun friend those heavy footed foolish english be come to speak with the mugger warn he then he be call protector of the poor by some one not unlike starve jackal but little time ago let my cousin protect his own hide he have tell again and again there be nothing to fear from the white face they must be white face not villager of mugger ghaut would dare to come after he see say it be gun now with good luck we shall feed before daylight he can not hear well out of water and this time it be not woman shiny barrel glitter for minute in the moonlight on the girder the mugger be lie on the sand bar as still as his own shadow his fore foot spread out little his head drop between they snore like mugger voice on the bridge whisper it an odd shot straight down almost but as safe as house well try behind the neck what brute the villager will be wild if he ’s shoot though he the deota godling of these part do not care rap another voice answer he take about fifteen of my good cooly while the bridge be build
and it time he be put stop to ve be after he in boat for week stand by with the martini as soon as ve give he both barrel of this mind the kick then double four bore ’s no joke that for he to decide here go there be roar like the sound of small cannon the big sort of elephant rifle be not very different from some artillery and double streak of flame follow by the stinging crack of martini whose long bullet make nothing of crocodile ’s plate
but the explosive bullet do the work one of they strike just behind the mugger ’s neck hand ’s breadth to the left of the backbone while the other burst little low down at the beginning of the tail in ninety nine case out of hundred mortally wind crocodile can scramble to deep water and get away but the mugger of mugger ghaut be literally break into three piece he hardly move his head before the life go out of he and he lie as flat as the jackal thunder and lightning lightning and thunder say that miserable little beast have the thing that pull the covered cart over the bridge tumble at last it be no more than gun say the adjutant though his very tail feather quiver nothing more than gun he be certainly dead here come the white face the two englishman have hurry down from the bridge and across to the sand bar where they stand admire the length of the mugger then native with an axe cut off the big head and four man drag it across the spit the last time that have my hand in mugger ’s mouth say one of the englishman stoop down he be the man who have build the bridge it be when be about five year old come down the river by boat to monghyr be mutiny baby as they call it poor mother be in the boat too and she often tell how she fire dad ’s old pistol at the beast ’s head
well you ve certainly have your revenge on the chief of the clan even if the gun have make your nose bleed
hi you boatman haul that head up the bank and we ’ll boil it for the skull the skin too knock about to keep come along to bed now this be worth sit up all night for be not it curiously enough the jackal and the adjutant make the very same remark not three minute after the man have leave ripple song once ripple come to land in the golden sunset burning-- lap against maiden ’s hand by the ford return dainty foot and gentle breast-- here across be glad and rest maiden wait the ripple saith wait awhile for be death where my lover call go--
shame it be to treat he coldly-- twas fish that circle so turn over boldly dainty foot and tender heart wait the load ferry cart wait ah wait the ripple saith maiden wait for be death when my lover call haste-- dame disdain be never we ripple ripple round her waist clear the current eddy foolish heart and faithful hand little foot that touch no land far away the ripple speed ripple ripple run red the king ’s ankus these be the four that be never content that have never be fill since the dew began-- jacala ’s mouth and the glut of the kite and the hand of the ape and the eye of man jungle say kaa the big rock python have change his skin for perhaps the two hundredth time since his birth and mowgli who never forget that he owe his life to kaa for night ’s work at cold lair which you may perhaps remember go to congratulate he skin change always make snake moody and depressed till the new skin begin to shine and look beautiful kaa never make fun of mowgli any more but accept he as the other jungle people do for the master of the jungle and bring he all the news that python of his size would naturally hear what kaa do not know about the middle jungle as they call it,--the life that run close to the earth or under it the boulder burrow and the tree bole life,--might have be write upon the small of his scale
that afternoon mowgli be sit in the circle of kaa ’s great coil finger
the flaked and break old skin that lie all loop and twist among the rock just as kaa have leave it kaa have very courteously pack himself under mowgli ’s broad bare shoulder so that the boy be really rest in live arm chair even to the scale of the eye it be perfect say mowgli under his breath play with the old skin strange to see the covering of one ’s own head at one ’s own foot ay
but lack foot say kaa and since this be the custom of all my people do not find it strange do thy skin never feel old and harsh then go and wash flathead
but it be true in the great heat have wish could slough my skin without pain and run skinless wash and also take off my skin how look the new coat mowgli run his hand down the diagonal checkering of the immense back the turtle be hard back but
not so gay he say judgmatically the frog my name bearer be more gay but not so hard it be very beautiful to see like the mottling in the mouth of lily it need water new skin never come to full colour before the first bath let we go bathe will carry thee say mowgli and he stoop down laugh to lift the middle section of kaa ’s great body just where the barrel be thick man might just as well have try to heave up two foot water main and kaa lie still puff with quiet amusement then the regular evening game begin the boy in the flush of his great strength and the python in his sumptuous new skin stand up one against the other for wrestling match trial of eye and strength of course kaa could have crush dozen mowgli if he have let himself go
but he play carefully and never loose one tenth of his power ever since mowgli be strong enough to endure little rough handling kaa have teach he this game and it supple his limb as nothing else could sometimes mowgli would stand lap almost to his throat in kaa ’s shift coil strive to get one arm free and catch he by the throat then kaa would give way limply and mowgli with both quick move foot would try to cramp the purchase of that huge tail as it fling backward feeling for rock or stump they would rock to and fro head to head each wait for his chance till the beautiful statue like group melt in whirl of black and yellow coil and struggle leg and arm to rise up again and again now now now say kaa make feint with his head that even mowgli ’s quick hand could not turn aside touch thee here little brother here and here be thy hand numb here again the game always end in one way with straight drive blow of the head that knock the boy over and over mowgli could never learn the guard for that lightning lunge and as kaa say there be not the least use in try good hunting kaa grunt at last and mowgli as usual be shoot away half dozen yard gasp and laugh he rise with his finger full of grass and follow kaa to the wise snake ’s pet bathing place deep pitchy black pool surround with rock and make interesting by sink tree stump the boy slip in jungle fashion without sound and dive across rise too without sound and turn on his back his arm behind his head watch the moon rise above the rock and break up her reflection in the water with his toe kaa ’s diamond shape head cut the pool like razor and come out to rest on mowgli ’s shoulder they lie still soak luxuriously in the cool water it be very good
say mowgli at last sleepily now in the man pack at this hour as remember they lie they down upon hard piece of wood in the inside of mud trap and having carefully shut out all the clean wind draw foul cloth over their heavy head and make evil song through their nose it be well in the jungle hurry cobra slip down over rock and drink give they good hunting and go away sssh say kaa as though he have suddenly remember something so the jungle give thee all that thou hast ever desire little brother not all say mowgli laugh else there would be new and strong shere khan to kill once moon now could kill with my own hand ask no help of buffalo and also have wish the sun to shine in the middle of the rain and the rain to cover the sun in the deep of summer and also have never go empty but wish that have kill goat and also have never kill goat but wish it have be buck nor buck but wish it have be nilghai but thus do we feel all of we thou hast no other desire the big snake demand what more can wish have the jungle and the favour of the jungle be there more anywhere between sunrise and sunset now the cobra said---- kaa begin what cobra he that go away just now say nothing he be hunt it be another hast thou many deal with the poison people give they their own path they carry death in the fore tooth and that be not good for they be so small but what hood be this thou hast speak with kaa roll slowly in the water like steamer in beam sea three or four moon since say he hunt in cold lair which place thou hast not forget and the thing hunt flee shriek past the tank and to that house whose side once break for thy sake and run into the ground but the people of cold lair do not live in burrow mowgli know that kaa be tell of the monkey people this thing be not live but seek to live kaa reply with quiver of his tongue he run into burrow that lead very far follow and having kill sleep when wake go forward under the earth even so come at last upon white hood white cobra who speak of thing beyond my knowledge and show many thing have never before see new game be it
good hunting mowgli turn quickly on his side it be no game and would have break all my tooth but the white hood say that man he speak as one that know the breed that man would give the breath under his rib for only the sight of those thing we will look say mowgli now remember that be once man slowly slowly it be haste kill the yellow snake that eat the sun we two speak together under the earth and speak of thee name thee as man say the white hood
and he be indeed as old as the jungle it be long since have see man let he come and he shall see all these thing for the least of which very many man would die that must be new game and yet the poison people do not tell we when game be afoot they be an unfriendly folk it be not game it be it be can not say what it be we will go there have never see white hood and wish to see the other thing do he kill they they be all dead thing he say he be the keeper of they all ah as wolf stand above meat he have take to his own lair let we go mowgli swim to bank roll on the grass to dry himself and the two set off for cold lair the deserted city of which you may have hear mowgli be not the least afraid of the monkey people in those day but the monkey people have the lively horror of mowgli their tribe however be raid in the jungle and so cold lair stand empty and silent in the moonlight kaa lead up to the ruin of the queen pavilion that stand on the terrace slip over the rubbish and dive down the half choke staircase that go underground from the centre of the pavilion mowgli give the snake call,--“we be of one blood ye and i,”--and follow on his hand and knee they crawl long distance down slop passage that turn and twist several time and at last come to where the root of some great tree grow thirty foot overhead have force out solid stone in the wall they creep through the gap and find themselves in large vault whose domed roof have be also break away by tree root so that few streak of light drop down into the darkness safe lair say mowgli rise to his firm foot but over far to visit daily and now what do we see be nothing say voice in the middle of the vault and mowgli see something white move till little by little there stand up the huge cobra he have ever set eye on creature nearly eight foot long and bleach by be in darkness to an old ivory white even the spectacle mark of his spread hood have fade to faint yellow his eye be as red as ruby and altogether he be most wonderful good hunting say mowgli who carry his manner with his knife and that never leave he what of the city say the white cobra without answer the greeting what of the great the walled city the city of hundred elephant and twenty thousand horse and cattle past count the city of the king of twenty king grow deaf here and it be long since hear their war gong the jungle be above our head say mowgli know only hathi and his son among elephant bagheera have slay all the horse in one village and what be king tell thee say kaa softly to the cobra,--“i tell thee four moon ago that thy city be not the city the great city of the forest whose gate be guard by the king ’s tower can never pass they build it before my father ’s father come from the egg
and it shall endure when my son ’s son be as white as salomdhi son of chandrabija son of viyeja son of yegasuri make it in the day of bappa rawal whose cattle be ye it be lose trail
say mowgli turn to kaa know not his talk nor i. he be very old father of cobra there be only the jungle here as it have be since the beginning then who be he say the white cobra sit down before unafraid know not the name of the king talk our talk through man ’s lip who be he with the knife and the snake ’s tongue mowgli they call be the answer be of the jungle the wolf be my people and kaa here be my brother father of cobra who art thou be the warden of the king ’s treasure kurrun raja build the stone above in the day when my skin be dark that might teach death to those who come to steal then they let down the treasure through the stone and hear the song of the brahmin my master umm say mowgli to himself have deal with one brahmin already in the man pack and know what know evil come here in little five time since come here have the stone be lift but always to let down more and never to take away there be no riches like these rich the treasure of hundred king
but it be long and long since the stone be last move and think that my city have forget there be no city look up yonder be root of the great tree tear the stone apart tree and man do not grow together kaa insist twice and thrice have man find their way here the white cobra answer savagely
but they never speak till come upon they grope in the dark
and then they cry only little time
but ye come with lie man and snake both and would have believe the city be not and that my wardship end little do man change in the year but change never till the stone be lift and the brahmin come down sing the song that know and feed with warm milk and take to the light again i--_i and no other be the warden of the king ’s treasure the city be dead ye say and here be the root of the tree stoop down then and take what ye will earth have no treasure like to these man with the snake ’s tongue if thou canst go alive by the way that thou hast enter it the less king will be thy servant again the trail be lose say mowgli coolly can any jackal have burrow so deep and bite this great white hood he be surely mad father of cobra see nothing here to take away by the god of the sun and moon it be the madness of death upon the boy hiss the cobra before thine eye close will allow thee this favour look thou and see what man have never see before they do not well in the jungle who speak to mowgli of favour say the boy between his tooth but the dark change all as know will look if that please thee he star with pucker up eye round the vault and then lift up from the floor handful of something that glitter
oho say he this be like the stuff they play with in the man pack only this be yellow and the other be brown he let the gold piece fall and move forward the floor of the vault be bury some five or six foot deep in coin gold and silver that have burst from the sack it have be originally store in and in the long year the metal have pack and settle as sand pack at low tide on it and in it and rise through it as wreck lift through the sand be jewelled elephant howdahs of embossed silver stud with plate of hammer gold and adorn with carbuncle and turquoise there be palanquin and litter for carry queen frame and brace with silver and enamel with jade handle pole and amber curtain ring there be golden candlestick hang with pierced emerald that quiver on the branch there be studded image five foot high of forget god silver with jewelled eye there be coat of mail gold inlay on steel and fringe with rot and blacken seed pearl there be helmet crested and bead with pigeon ’s blood ruby there be shield of lacquer of tortoise shell and rhinoceros hide strap and boss with red gold and set with emerald at the edge there be sheaf of diamond hilte sword dagger and hunt knife there be golden sacrificial bowl and ladle and portable altar of shape that never see the light of day there be jade cup and bracelet there be incense burner comb and pot for perfume henna and eye powder all in embossed gold there be nose ring armlet head band finger ring and girdle past any counting there be belt seven finger broad of square cut diamond and ruby and wooden box trebly clamp with iron from which the wood have fall away in powder show the pile of uncut star sapphire opal cat ’s eye sapphire ruby diamond emerald and garnet within the white cobra be right no mere money would begin to pay the value of this treasure the sift picking of century of war plunder trade and taxation the coin alone be priceless leave out of count all the precious stone and the dead weight of the gold and silver alone might be two or three hundred ton every native ruler in india to day however poor have hoard to which he be always add and though once in long while some enlightened prince may send off forty or fifty bullock cart load of silver to be exchange for government security the bulk of they keep their treasure and the knowledge of it very closely to themselves but mowgli naturally do not understand what these thing mean the knife interest he little
but they do not balance so well as his own
and so he drop they at last he find something really fascinating lie on the front of howdah half bury in the coin it be three foot ankus or elephant goad something like small boat hook the top be one round shin ruby and eight inch of the handle below it be stud with rough turquoise close together give most satisfactory grip below they be rim of jade with flower pattern run round it only the leaf be emerald and the blossom be ruby sink in the cool green stone the rest of the handle be shaft of pure ivory while the point the spike and hook be gold inlay steel with picture of elephant catch and the picture attract mowgli who see that they have something to do with his friend hathi the silent the white cobra have be follow he closely be this not worth die to behold he say have not do thee great favour do not understand say mowgli the thing be hard and cold and by no mean good to eat but this”--he lift the ankus--“i desire to take away that may see it in the sun thou sayest they be all thine wilt thou give it to and will bring thee frog to eat the white cobra fairly shake with evil delight assuredly will give it he say all that be here will give thee till thou goest away but go now this place be dark and cold and wish to take the thorn pointed thing to the jungle look by thy foot what be that there mowgli pick up something white and smooth it be the bone of man ’s head he say quietly and here be two more they come to take the treasure away many year ago speak to they in the dark and they lie still but what do need of this that be call treasure if thou wilt give the ankus to take away it be good hunting if not it be good hunting none the less do not fight with the poison people and be also teach the master word of thy tribe there be
but one master word here it be mine kaa fling himself forward with blaze eye who bid bring the man he hiss surely the old cobra lisp it be long since have see man and this man speak our tongue
but there be no talk of kill how can go to the jungle and say that have lead he to his death say kaa talk not of kill till the time and as to thy going or not go there be the hole in the wall peace now thou fat monkey killer have but to touch thy neck and the jungle will know thee no long never man come here that go away with the breath under his rib be the warden of the treasure of the king ’s city but thou white worm of the dark tell thee there be neither king nor city the jungle be all about we cry kaa there be still the treasure
but this can be do wait awhile kaa of the rock and see the boy run there be room for great sport here life be good run to and fro awhile and make sport boy mowgli put his hand on kaa ’s head quietly the white thing have deal with man of the man pack until now he do not know he whisper he have ask for this hunting let he have it mowgli have be stand with the ankus hold point down he fling it from he quickly and it drop crossway just behind the great snake ’s hood pin he to the floor in flash kaa ’s weight be upon the writhe body paralyse it from hood to tail the red eye burn and the six spare inch of the head strike furiously right and leave kill say kaa as mowgli ’s hand go to his knife
he say as he draw the blade will never kill again save for food but look you kaa he catch the snake behind the hood force the mouth open with the blade of the knife and show the terrible poison fang of the upper jaw lie black and wither in the gum the white cobra have outlive his poison as snake will thuu it be dry up”--literally rot out tree stump say mowgli and motion kaa away he pick up the ankus set the white cobra
