free the king ’s treasure need new warden he say gravely thuu thou hast not do well run to and fro and make sport thuu be ashamed kill hiss the white cobra there have be too much talk of kill we will go now take the thorn point thing thuu because have fight and bad thee see then that the thing do not kill thee at last it be death remember it be death
there be enough in that thing to kill the man of all my city not long wilt thou hold it jungle man nor he who take it from thee they will kill and kill and kill for its sake my strength be dry up but the ankus will do my work it be death it be death it be death mowgli crawl out through the hole into the passage again and the last that he see be the white cobra strike furiously with his harmless fang at the stolid golden face of the god that lie on the floor and hiss it be death they be glad to get to the light of day once more and when they be back in their own jungle and mowgli make the ankus glitter in the morning light he be almost as pleased as though he have find bunch of new flower to stick in his hair this be bright than bagheera ’s eye he say delightedly as he twirl the ruby will show it to he but what do the thuu mean when he talk of death can not say be sorrowful to my tail ’s tail that he feel not thy knife there be always evil at cold lair above ground or below but now be hungry dost thou hunt with this dawn say kaa no bagheera must see this thing good hunting mowgli dance off flourish the great ankus and stop from time to time to admire it till he come to that part of the jungle bagheera chiefly use and find he drink after heavy kill mowgli tell he all his adventure from begin to end and bagheera sniff at the ankus between while when mowgli come to the white cobra ’s last word the panther purr approvingly then the white hood speak the thing which be mowgli ask quickly be bear in the king ’s cage at oodeypore
and it be in my stomach that know some little of man very many man would kill thrice in night for the sake of that one big red stone alone
but the stone make it heavy to the hand my little bright knife be well and see the red stone be not good to eat then why would they kill mowgli go thou and sleep thou hast live among man and---- remember man kill because they be not hunting;--for idleness and pleasure wake again bagheera for what use be this thorn point thing make bagheera half open his eye he be very sleepy with malicious twinkle it be make by man to thrust into the head of the son of hathi so that the blood should pour out have see the like in the street of oodeypore before our cage that thing have taste the blood of many such as hathi
but why do they thrust into the head of elephant to teach they man ’s law have neither claw nor tooth man make these thing and bad always
more blood when come near even to the thing the man pack have make say mowgli disgustedly he be get little tired of the weight of the ankus if have know this would not have take it
first it be messua ’s blood on the thong and now it be hathi ’s will use it no more the ankus fly sparkling and bury itself point down thirty yard away between the tree
so my hand be clean of death say mowgli rub his palm on the fresh moist earth the thuu say death would follow he be old and white and mad white or black or death or life be go to sleep little brother can not hunt all night and howl all day as do some folk bagheera go off to hunt lair that he know about two mile off mowgli make an easy way for himself up convenient tree knot three or four creeper together and in less time than it take to tell be swing in hammock fifty foot above ground though he have no positive objection to strong daylight mowgli follow the custom of his friend and use it as the jungle be wet enough to hold the light mark they kill as soon as they could but it be nearly three hour before they finish their meat and drink and buckle down to the trail the jungle people know that nothing make up for be hurry over your meal think you the pointed thing will turn in the man ’s hand and kill he mowgli ask the thuu say it be death we shall see when we find say bagheera trot mowgli cry here come another trail to meet he it be small foot this second trail and the toe turn inward then mowgli run up and look it be the foot of gond hunter he say here he drag his bow on the grass that be why the first trail turn aside so quickly big foot hide from little foot that be true say bagheera now lest by cross each other ’s track we foul the sign let each take one trail be big foot little brother and thou art little foot the gond bagheera leap back to the original trail leave mowgli stoop above the curious narrow track of the wild little man of the wood now say bagheera move step by step along the chain of footprint big foot turn aside here now hide behind rock and stand still not dare to shift my foot cry thy trail little brother now little foot come to the rock say mowgli run up his trail now sit down under the rock lean upon my right hand and rest my bow between my toe wait long for the mark of my foot be deep here also say bagheera hide behind the rock wait rest the end of the thorn point thing upon stone it slip for here be scratch upon the stone cry thy trail little brother one two twig and big branch be break here say mowgli in an undertone now how shall cry that it be plain now little foot go away make noise and trample so that big foot may hear he move away from the rock pace by pace among the tree his voice rise in the distance as he approach little cascade go far away to where the noise of fall water cover my noise and here wait cry thy trail bagheera big foot the panther have be cast in every direction to see how big foot ’s trail lead away from behind the rock then he give tongue come from behind the rock upon my knee drag the thorn point thing see no one run big foot run swiftly the trail be clear let each follow his own bagheera sweep on along the clearly mark trail and mowgli follow the step of the gond for some time there be silence in the jungle where art thou little foot cry bagheera mowgli ’s
voice answer he not fifty yard to the right um say the panther with deep cough the two run side by side draw near they race on another half mile always keep about the same distance till mowgli whose head be not so close to the ground as bagheera ’s cry they have meet good hunting look here stand little foot with his knee on rock and yonder be big foot indeed not ten yard in front of they stretch across pile of break rock lie the body of villager of the district long small feather gond arrow through his back and breast be the thuu
so old and so mad little brother say bagheera gently here be one death at least follow on but where be the drinker of elephant ’s blood the red eyed thorn little foot have it perhaps it be single foot again now the single trail of light man who have be run quickly and bear burden on his left shoulder hold on round long low spur of dry grass where each footfall seem to the sharp eye of the tracker mark in hot iron neither speak till the trail run up to the ash of camp fire hide in ravine again say bagheera check as though he have be turn into stone the body of little wizened gond lie with its foot in the ash and bagheera look inquiringly at mowgli that be do with bamboo say the boy after one glance have use such thing among the buffalo when serve in the man pack the father of cobra be sorrowful that make jest of he know the breed well as might have know say not that man kill for idleness indeed they kill for the sake of the red and blue stone bagheera answer remember be in the king ’s cage at oodeypore one two three four track
say mowgli stoop over the ash four track of man with shod foot they do not go so quickly as gond now what evil have the little woodman do to they see they talk together all five stand up before they kill he bagheera let we go back my stomach be heavy in and yet it heave up and down like an oriole ’s nest at the end of branch it be not good hunting to leave game afoot follow say the panther those eight shod foot have not go far no more be say for fully an hour as they work up the broad trail of the four man with shod foot it be clear hot daylight now and bagheera say smell smoke man be always more ready to eat than to run mowgli answer trot in and out between the low scrub bush of the new jungle they be explore bagheera little to his left make an indescribable noise in his throat here be one that have do with feed say he tumble bundle of gay coloured clothe lie under bush and round it be some spilt flour that be do by the bamboo again say mowgli that white dust be what man eat they have take the kill from this one,--he carry their food,--and give he for kill to chil the kite it be the third say bagheera will go with new big frog to the father of cobra and feed he fat
say mowgli to himself the drinker of elephant ’s blood be death himself but still do not understand follow
say bagheera they have not go half mile far when they hear ko the crow sing the death song in the top of tamarisk under whose shade three man be lie half dead fire smoke in the centre of the circle under an iron plate which hold blacken and burn cake of unleavened bread close to the fire and blaze in the sunshine lie the ruby and turquoise ankus the thing work quickly all end here say bagheera how do these die mowgli there be no mark on any jungle dweller get to learn by experience as much
as many doctor know of poisonous plant and berry mowgli sniff the smoke that come up from the fire break off morsel of the blacken bread taste it and spit it out again apple of death he cough the first must have make it ready in the food for these who kill he having first kill the gond good hunting indeed the kill follow close say bagheera apple of death be what the jungle call thorn apple or dhatura the ready poison in all india what now say the panther must thou and kill each other for yonder red eyed slayer can it speak say mowgli in whisper do do it wrong when throw it away between we two it can do no wrong for we do not desire what man desire if it be leave here it will assuredly continue to kill man one after another as fast as nut fall in high wind have no love to man but even would not have they die six in night what matter they be only man they kill one another and be well pleased say bagheera that first little woodman hunt well they be cub none the less and cub will drown himself to bite the moon ’s light on the water the fault be mine say mowgli who speak as though he know all about everything will never again bring into the jungle strange thing not though they be as beautiful as flower this”--he handle the ankus gingerly--“goe back to the father of cobra
but first we must sleep and we can not sleep near these sleeper also we must bury he lest he run away and kill another six dig hole under that tree but little brother say bagheera move off to the spot tell thee it be no fault of the blood drinker the trouble be with the man all one say mowgli dig the hole deep when we wake will take he up and carry he back two night later as the white cobra sit mourning in the darkness of the vault ashamed and rob and alone the turquoise ankus whirl through the hole in the wall and clash on the floor of golden coin father of cobra say mowgli he be careful to keep the other side of the wall get thee young and ripe one of thine own people to help thee guard the king ’s treasure so that no man may come away alive any more ah ha it return then say the thing be death how come it that thou art still alive the old cobra mumble twin lovingly round the ankus haft by the bull that buy do not know that thing have kill six time in night let he go out no more the song of the little hunter ere mor the peacock flutter ere the monkey people cry ere chil the kite swoop down furlong sheer through the jungle very softly flit shadow and sigh-- he be fear little hunter he be fear very softly down the glade run wait watch shade and the whisper spread and widen far and near and the sweat be on thy brow for he pass even now--
he be fear little hunter he be fear
ere the moon have climb the mountain
ere the rock be rib with light when the downward dip trail be dank and drear come breathe hard behind thee snuffle snuffle through the night-- it be fear little hunter it be fear on thy knee and draw the bow bid the shrilling arrow go in the empty mock thicket plunge the spear but thy hand be loose and weak and the blood have leave thy
it be fear little hunter it be fear when the heat cloud suck the tempest when the sliver pine tree fall when the blind blare rain squall lash and veer through the war gong of the thunder ring voice more loud than all-- it be fear little hunter it be fear now the spate be bank and deep now the footless boulder leap-- now the lightning show each little leaf rib clear--
but thy throat be shut and dry and thy heart against thy side hammer fear little hunter this be fear quiquern the people of the eastern ice they be melt like the snow-- they beg for coffee and sugar they go where the white man go the people of the western ice they learn to steal and fight they sell their fur to the trading post they sell their soul to the white the people of the southern ice they trade with the whaler ’s crew their woman have many ribbon but their tent be tear and few but the people of the eld ice beyond the white man ’s ken--
their spear be make of the narwhal horn and they be the last of the man translation he have open his eye look put he in the skin again he will be strong dog on the fourth month we will name he for whom say amoraq kadlu ’s eye roll round the skin line snow house till it fall on fourteen year old kotuko sit on the sleep bench make button out of walrus ivory name he for say kotuko with grin shall need he one day kadlu grin back till his eye be almost bury in the fat of his flat cheek and nod to amoraq while the puppy ’s fierce mother whine to see her baby wriggle far out of reach in the little sealskin pouch hang above the warmth of the blubber lamp kotuko go on with his carving and kadlu throw roll bundle of leather dog harness into tiny little room that open from one side of the house slip off his heavy deerskin hunting suit put it into whalebone net that hang above another lamp and drop down on the sleep bench to whittle at piece of frozen seal meat till amoraq his wife should bring the regular dinner of boil meat and blood soup he have be out since early dawn at the seal hole eight mile away and have come home with three big seal half way down the long low snow passage or tunnel that lead to the inner door of the house you could hear snap and yelp as the dog of his sleigh team release from the day ’s work scuffle for warm place when the yelping grow too loud kotuko lazily roll off the sleep bench and pick up whip with an eighteen inch handle of springy whalebone and twenty five foot of heavy plait thong he dive into the passage where it sound as though all the dog be eat he alive but that be no more than their regular grace before meal when he crawl out at the far end half dozen furry head follow he with their eye as he go to sort of gallow of whale jawbone from which the dog ’s meat be hang split off the frozen stuff in big lump with broad head spear and stand his whip in one hand and the meat in the other each beast be call by name the weak first and woe betide any dog that move out of his turn for the taper lash would shoot out like thonged lightning and flick away an inch or so of hair and hide each beast growl snap choke once over his portion and hurry back to the protection of the passage while the boy stand upon the snow under the blaze northern light and deal out justice the last to be serve be the big black leader of the team who keep order when the dog be harness and to he kotuko give double allowance of meat as well as an extra crack of the whip ah say kotuko coil up the lash have little one over the lamp that will make great many howling get in he crawl back over the huddle dog dust the dry snow from his fur with the whalebone beater that amoraq keep by the door tap the skin line roof of the house to shake off any icicle that might have fall from the dome of snow above and curl up on the bench the dog in the passage snore and whine in their sleep the boy baby in amoraq ’s deep fur hood kick and choke and gurgle and the mother of the newly name puppy lie at kotuko ’s side her eye fix on the bundle of sealskin warm and safe above the broad yellow flame of the lamp and all this happen far away to the north beyond labrador beyond hudson ’s strait where the great tide heave the ice about north of melville peninsula north even of the narrow fury and hecla strait on the north shore of baffin land where bylot ’s island stand above the ice of lancaster sound like pudding bowl wrong side up north of lancaster sound there be little we know anything about except north devon and ellesmere land but even there live few scatter people next door as it be to the very pole kadlu be an inuit,--what you call
an esquimau,--and his tribe some thirty person all tell belong to the tununirmiut--“the country lie at the back of something in the map that desolate coast be write navy board inlet but the inuit name be good because the country lie at the very back of everything in the world for nine month of the year there be only ice and snow and gale after gale with cold that no one can realise who have never see the thermometer even at zero for six month of those nine it be dark and that be what make it so horrible in the three month of the summer it only freeze every other day and every night and then the snow begin to weep off on the southerly slope and few ground willow put out their woolly bud tiny stonecrop or so make believe to blossom beach of fine gravel and rounded stone run down to the open sea and polished boulder and streak rock lift up above the granulated snow but all that be go in few week and the wild winter lock down again on the land while at sea the ice tear up and down the offing jamming and ramming and split and hitting and pound and ground till it all freeze together ten foot thick from the land outward to deep water in the winter kadlu would follow the seal to the edge of this land ice and spear they as they come up to breathe at their blow hole the seal must have open water to live and catch fish in and in the deep of winter the ice would sometimes run eighty mile without break from the near shore in the spring he and his people retreat from the floe to the rocky mainland where they put up tent of skin and snare the sea bird or spear the young seal bask on the beach later they would go south into baffin land after the reindeer and to get their year ’s store of salmon from the hundred of stream and lake of the interior come back north in september or october for the musk ox hunting and the regular winter sealery this travel be do with dog sleigh twenty and thirty mile day or sometimes down the coast in big skin woman boat when the dog and the baby lie among the foot of the rower and the woman sing song as they glide from cape to cape over the glassy cold water all the luxury that the tununirmiut know come from the south driftwood for sleigh runner rod iron for harpoon tip steel knife tin kettle that cook food much well than the old soap stone affair flint and steel and even match as well as coloured ribbon for the woman ’s hair little cheap mirror and red cloth for the edging of deerskin dress jacket kadlu trade the rich creamy twisted narwhal horn and musk ox tooth these be just as valuable as pearl to the southern inuit and they in turn trade with the whaler and the missionary post of exeter and cumberland sound
and so the chain go on till kettle pick up by ship ’s cook in the bhendy bazaar might end its day over blubber lamp somewhere on the cool side of the arctic circle kadlu be good hunter be rich in iron harpoon snow knife bird dart and all the other thing that make life easy up there in the great cold and he be the head of his tribe or as they say the man who know all about it by practice this do not give he any authority except now
and then he could advise his friend to change their hunting ground but kotuko use it to domineer little in the lazy fat inuit fashion over the other boy when they come out at night to play ball in the moonlight or to sing the child ’s song to the aurora borealis but at fourteen an inuit feel himself man and kotuko be tired of make snare for wild fowl and kit fox and most tired of all of help the woman to chew seal and deer skin that supple they as nothing else
can the long day through while the man be out hunting he want to go into the quaggi the singing house when the hunter gather there for their mystery and the angekok the sorcerer frighten they into the most delightful fit after the lamp be put out and you could hear the spirit of the reindeer stamp on the roof and when spear be thrust out into the open black night it come back cover with hot blood he want to throw his big boot into the net with the tired air of the head of family and to gamble with the hunter when they drop in of an evening and play sort of home make roulette with tin pot and nail there be hundred of thing that he want to do but the grown man laugh at he and say wait till you have be in the buckle kotuko hunting be not all catch now that his father have name puppy for he thing look bright an inuit do not waste good dog on his son till the boy know something of dog driving and kotuko be more than sure that he know more than everything if the puppy have not have an iron constitution he would have die from over stuffing and over handle kotuko make he tiny harness with trace to it and haul he all over the house floor shout aua ja aua go to the right ja choiachoi go to the left the puppy do not like it at all but be fish for in this way be pure happiness beside be put to the sleigh for the first time he just sit down on the snow and play with the seal hide trace that run from his harness to the pitu the big thong in the bow of the sleigh then the team start and the puppy find the heavy ten foot sleigh run up his back and drag he along the snow while kotuko laugh till the tear run down his face there follow day and day of the cruel whip that hiss like the wind over ice and his companion all bite he because he do not know his work and the harness chafe he
and he be dot allow to sleep with kotuko any more but have to take the cold place in the passage it be sad time for the puppy the boy learn too as fast as the dog though dog sleigh be heart break thing to manage each beast be harnes the weak near to the driver by his own separate trace which run under his leave fore leg to the main thong where it be fasten by sort of button and loop which can be slip by turn of the wrist thus free one dog at time this be very necessary because young dog often get the trace between their hind leg where it cut to the bone
and they one and all will go visit their friend as they run jump in and out among the trace then they fight and the result be more mix than wet fishing line next morning great deal of trouble can be avoid by scientific use of the whip every inuit boy pride himself as be master of the long lash
but it be easy to flick at mark on the ground and difficult to lean forward and catch shirk dog just behind the shoulder when the sleigh be go at full speed if you call one dog ’s name for visit and accidentally lash another the two will fight it out at once and stop all the other again if you travel with companion and begin to talk or by yourself and sing the dog will halt turn round and sit down to hear what you have to say kotuko be run away from once or twice through forget to block the sleigh when he stop
and he break many lashing and ruin few thong before he could be trust with full team of eight and the light sleigh then he feel himself person of consequence and on smooth black ice with bold heart and quick elbow he smoke along over the level as fast as pack in full cry he would go ten mile to the seal hole and when he be on the hunting ground he would twitch trace loose from the pitu and free the big black leader who be the clever dog in the team as soon as the dog have scent breathing hole kotuko would reverse the sleigh drive couple of saw off antler that stick up like perambulator handle from the back rest deep into the snow so that the team could not get away then he would crawl forward inch by inch and wait till the seal come up to breathe then he would stab down swiftly with his spear and run line and presently would haul his seal up to the lip of the ice while the black leader come up and help to pull the carcass across the ice to the sleigh that be the time when the harness dog yell and foam with excitement and kotuko lie the long lash like red hot bar across all their face till the carcass freeze stiff go home be the heavy work the loaded sleigh have to be humour among the rough ice and the dog sit down and look hungrily at the seal instead of pull at last they would strike the well wear sleigh road to the village and toodle kiyi along the ring ice head down and tail up while kotuko strike up the an gutivaun tai na tau na ne taina the song of the return hunter and voice hail he from house to house under all that dim star littern sky when kotuko the dog come to his full growth he enjoy himself too he fight his way up the team steadily fight after fight till one fine evening over their food he tackle the big black leader kotuko the boy see fair play and make second dog of he as they say so he be promote to the long thong of the lead dog run five foot in advance of all the other it be his bounden duty to stop all fight in harness or out of it and he wear collar of copper wire very thick and heavy on special occasion he be feed with cooked food inside the house and sometimes be allow to sleep on the bench with kotuko he be good seal dog and would keep musk ox at bay by run round he and snap at his heel he would even and this for sleigh dog be the last proof of bravery he would even stand up to the gaunt arctic wolf whom all dog of the north as rule fear beyond anything that walk the snow he and his master they do not count the team of ordinary dog as company hunt together day after day and night after night fur wrap boy and savage long haired narrow eyed white fanged yellow brute all an inuit have to do be to get food and skin for himself and his family the woman folk make the skin into clothing and occasionally help in trap small game but the bulk of the food and they eat enormously must be find by the man if the supply fail there be no one up there to buy or beg or borrow from the people must die an inuit do not think of these chance till he be force to kadlu kotuko amoraq and the boy baby who kick about in amoraq ’s fur hood and chew piece of blubber all day be as happy together as any family in the world they come of very gentle race an inuit seldom lose his temper and almost never strike child who do not know exactly what tell real lie mean still less how to steal they be content to spear their living out of the heart of the bitter hopeless cold to smile oily smile and tell queer ghost and fairy tale of evening and eat till they could eat no more and sing the endless woman ’s song amna aya aya amna ah ah through the long lamp light day as they mend their clothe and their hunting gear but one terrible winter everything betray they the tununirmiut return from the yearly salmon fishing and make their house on the early ice to the north of bylot ’s island ready to go after the seal as soon as the sea freeze but it be an early and savage autumn all through september there be continuous gale that break up the smooth seal ice when it be only four or five foot thick and force it inland and pile great barrier some twenty mile broad of lump and rag and needly ice over which it be impossible to draw the dog sleigh the edge of the floe off which the seal be use to fish in winter lie perhaps twenty mile beyond this barrier and out of reach of the tununirmiut even so they might have manage to scrape through the winter on their stock of frozen salmon and store blubber and what the trap give they but in december one of their hunter come across tupik skin tent of three woman and girl nearly dead whose man have come down from the far north and be crush in their little skin hunt boat while they be out after the long horn narwhal kadlu of course could only distribute the woman among the hut of the winter village for no inuit dare refuse meal to strange he never know when his own turn may come to beg amoraq take the girl who be about fourteen into her own house as sort of servant from the cut of her sharp pointed hood and the long diamond pattern of her white deer skin legging they suppose she come from ellesmere land she have never see tin cooking pot or wooden shod sleigh before but kotuko the boy and kotuko the dog be rather fond of she then all the fox go south and even the wolverine that growl blunt head little thief of the snow do not take the trouble to follow the line of empty trap that kotuko set the tribe lose couple of their good hunter who be badly crippled in fight with musk ox and this throw more work on the other kotuko go out day after day with light hunting sleigh and six or seven of the strong dog look till his eye ache for some patch of clear ice where seal might perhaps have scratch breathing hole kotuko the dog range far and wide and in the dead stillness of the ice field kotuko the boy could hear his half choke whine of excitement above seal hole three mile away as plainly as though he be at his elbow when the dog find hole the boy would build himself little low snow wall to keep off the bad of the bitter wind and there he would wait ten twelve twenty hour for the seal to come up to breathe his eye glue to the tiny mark he have make above the hole to guide the downward thrust of his harpoon little seal skin mat under his foot and his leg tie together in the tutareang the buckle that the old hunter have talk about this help to keep man ’s leg from twitch as he wait and wait and wait for the quick eared seal to rise though there be no excitement in it you can easily believe that the sitting still in the buckle with the thermometer perhaps forty degree below zero be the hard work an inuit know when seal be catch kotuko the dog would bind forward his trace trail behind he and help to pull the body to the sleigh where the tired and hungry dog lie sullenly under the lee of the break ice seal do not go very far for each mouth in the little village have right to be fill and neither bone hide nor sinew be waste the dog meat be take for human use and amoraq feed the team with piece of old summer skin tent rake out from under the sleep bench and they howl and howl again and wake to howl hungrily one could tell by the soap stone lamp in the hut that famine be near in good season when blubber be plentiful the light in the boat shape lamp would be two foot high cheerful oily and yellow now it be bare six inch amoraq carefully prick down the moss wick when an unwatched flame brighten for moment and the eye of all the family follow her hand the horror of famine up there in the great cold be not so much die as die in the dark all the inuit dread the dark that press on they without break for six month in each year and when the lamp be low in the house the mind of people begin to be shake and confuse but bad be to come the underfed dog snap and growl in the passage glare at the cold star and snuff into the bitter wind night after night when they stop howl the silence fall down again as solid and heavy as snowdrift against door and man could hear the beating of their blood in the thin passage of the ear and the thumping of their own heart that sound as loud as the noise of sorcerer drum beat across the snow one night kotuko the dog who have be unusually sullen in harness leap up and push his head against kotuko ’s knee kotuko pat he but the dog still push blindly forward fawn then kadlu wake and grip the heavy wolf like head and star into the glassy eye the dog whimper and shiver between kadlu ’s knee the hair rise about his neck and he growl as though stranger be at the door then he bark joyously and roll on the ground and bit at kotuko ’s boot like puppy what be it say kotuko for he be begin to be afraid the sickness kadlu answer it be the dog sickness kotuko the dog lift his nose and howl and howl again have not see this before what will he do say kotuko kadlu shrug one shoulder little and cross the hut for his short stabbing harpoon the big dog look at he howl again and slink away down the passage while the other dog draw aside right and leave to give he ample room when he be out on the snow he bark furiously as though on the trail of musk ox and bark and leap and frisking pass out of sight his trouble be not hydrophobia but simple plain madness the cold and the hunger and above all the dark have turn his head and when the terrible dog sickness once show itself in team it spread like wild fire next hunting day another dog sicken and be kill then and there by kotuko as he bite and struggle among the trace then the black second dog who have be the leader in the old day suddenly give tongue on an imaginary reindeer track and when they slip he from the pitu he fly at the throat of an ice cliff and run away as his leader have do his harness on his back after that no one would take the dog out again they need they for something else and the dog know it and though they be tie down and feed by hand their eye be full of despair and fear to make thing bad the old woman begin to tell ghost tale and to say that they have meet the spirit of the dead hunter lose that autumn who prophesy all sort of horrible thing kotuko grieve more for the loss of his dog than anything else for though an inuit eat enormously he also know how to starve but the hunger the darkness the cold and the exposure tell on his strength and he begin to hear voice inside his head and to see people who be not there out of the tail of his eye one night he have unbuckle himself after ten hour wait above blind seal hole and be stagger back to the village faint and dizzy he halt to lean his back against boulder which happen to be support like rock stone on single jut point of ice his weight disturb the balance of the thing it roll over ponderously and as kotuko spring aside to avoid it slide after he squeak and hiss on the ice slope that be enough for kotuko he have be bre up to believe that every rock and boulder have its owner its inua who be generally one eyed kind of woman thing call tornaq and that when tornaq mean to help man she roll after he inside her stone house and ask he whether he would take she for guardian spirit in summer thaw the ice prop rock and boulder roll and slip all over the face of the land so you can easily see how the idea of live stone arise kotuko hear the blood beat in his ear as he have hear it all day and he think that be the tornaq of the stone speak to he before he reach home he be quite certain that he have hold long conversation with she and as all his people believe that this be quite possible no one contradict he she say to jump down jump down from my place on the snow cry kotuko with hollow eye lean forward in the half light hut she say will be guide she say will guide you to the good seal hole to morrow go out and the tornaq will guide then the angekok the village sorcerer come in and kotuko tell he the tale second time it lose nothing in the telling follow the tornait the spirit of the stone and they will bring we food again say the angekok now the girl from the north have be lie near the lamp eat very little and say less for day past but when amoraq and kadlu next morning pack and lash little hand sleigh for kotuko and load it with his hunting gear and as much blubber and frozen seal meat as they could spare she take the pull rope and step out boldly at the boy ’s side your house be my house she say as the little bone shod sleigh squeak and bump behind they in the awful arctic night my house be your house say kotuko but think that we shall both go to sedna together now sedna be the mistress of the underworld and the inuit believe that every one who die must spend year in her horrible country before go to quadliparmiut the happy place where it never freeze and the fat reindeer trot up when you call through the village people be shout the tornait have speak to kotuko they will show he open ice he will bring we the seal again their voice be soon swallow up by the cold empty dark and kotuko and the girl shoulder close together as they strain on the pull rope or humour the sleigh through the ice in the direction of the polar sea kotuko insist that the tornaq of the stone have tell he to go north and north they go under tuktuqdjung the reindeer those star that we call the great bear no european could have make five mile day over the ice rubbish and the sharp edge drift but those two know exactly the turn of the wrist that coax sleigh round hummock the jerk that nearly lift it out of an ice crack and the exact strength that go to the few quiet stroke of the spear head that make path possible when everything look hopeless the girl say nothing but bow her head and the long wolverine fur fringe of her ermine hood blow across her broad dark face the sky above they be an intense velvety black change to band of indian red on the horizon where the great star burn like street lamp from time to time greenish wave of the northern light would roll across the hollow of the high heaven flick like flag and disappear or meteor would crackle from darkness to darkness trail shower of spark behind then they could see the ridged and furrowed surface of the floe tip and lace with strange colour red copper and bluish but in the ordinary starlight everything turn to one frost bite gray the floe as you will remember have be batter and torment by the autumn gale till it be one frozen earthquake there be gully and ravine and hole like gravel pit cut in ice lump and scatter piece freeze down to the original floor of the floe blotch of old black ice that have be thrust under the floe in some gale and heave up again roundish boulder of ice see like edge of ice carve by the snow that fly before the wind and sink pit where thirty or forty acre lie below the level of the rest of the field from little distance you might have take the lump for seal or walrus overturn sleigh or man on hunt expedition or even the great ten legged white spirit bear himself but in spite of these fantastic shape all on the very edge of start into life there be neither sound nor the least faint echo of sound and through this silence and through this waste where the sudden light flap
and go out again the sleigh and the two that pull it crawl like thing in nightmare nightmare of the end of the world at the end of the world when they be tired kotuko would make what the hunter call half house very small snow hut into which they would huddle with the travel lamp and try to thaw out the frozen seal meat when they have sleep the march begin again thirty mile day to get ten mile northward the girl be always very silent but kotuko mutter to himself and break out into song he have learn in the singing house summer song and reindeer and salmon song all horribly out of place at that season he would declare that he hear the tornaq growl to he and would run wildly up hummock toss his arm and speak in loud threaten tone to tell the truth kotuko be very nearly crazy for the time be but the girl be sure that he be be guide by his guardian spirit and that everything would come right she be not surprised therefore when at the end of the fourth march kotuko whose eye be burn like fire ball in his head tell she that his tornaq be follow they across the snow in the shape of two head dog the girl look where kotuko point and something seem to slip into ravine it be certainly not human but everybody know that the tornait prefer to appear in the shape of bear and seal and such like it might have be the ten legged white spirit bear himself or it might have be anything for kotuko and the girl be so starve that their eye be untrustworthy they have trap nothing and see no trace of game since they have leave the village their food would not hold out for another week and there be gale come polar storm can blow for ten day without break and all that while it be certain death to be abroad kotuko lie up snow house large enough to take in the hand sleigh never be separate from your meat and while he be shape the last irregular block of ice that make the key stone of the roof he see thing look at he from little cliff of ice half mile away the air be hazy and the thing seem to be forty foot long and ten foot high with twenty foot of tail and shape that quiver all along the outline the girl see it too but instead of cry aloud with terror say quietly that be quiquern what come after he will speak to say kotuko but the snow knife tremble in his hand as he speak because however much man may believe that he be friend of strange and ugly spirit he seldom like to be take quite at his word quiquern too be the phantom of gigantic toothless dog without any hair who be suppose to live in the far north and to wander about the country just before thing be go to happen they may be pleasant or unpleasant thing but not even the sorcerer care to speak about quiquern he make the dog go mad like the spirit bear he have several extra pair of legs,--six or eight,--and this thing jump up and down in the haze have more leg than any real dog need kotuko and the girl huddle into their hut quickly of course if quiquern have want they he could have tear it to piece above their head but the sense of foot thick snow wall between themselves and the wicked dark be great comfort the gale break with shriek of wind like the shriek of train and for three day and three night it hold never vary one point and never lull even for minute they feed the stone lamp between their knee and nibble at the half warm seal meat and watch the black soot gather on the roof for seventy two long hour the girl count up the food in the sleigh there be not more than two day supply and kotuko look over the iron head and the deer sinew fastening of his harpoon and his seal lance and his bird dart there be nothing else to do we shall go to sedna soon very soon the girl whisper in three day we shall lie down and go
will your tornaq do nothing sing she an angekok ’s song to make she come here he begin to sing in the high pitch howl of the magic song and the gale go down slowly in the middle of his song the girl start lay her mittened hand
and then her head to the ice floor of the hut kotuko follow her example and the two kneel star into each other ’s eye and listen with every nerve he rip thin sliver of whalebone from the rim of bird snare that lie on the sleigh and after straighten set it upright in little hole in the ice firm it down with his mitten it be almost as delicately adjust as compass needle and now instead of listen they watch the thin rod quiver little the least little jar in the world then it vibrate steadily for few second come to rest and vibrate again this time nod to another point of the compass too soon say kotuko some big floe have break far away outside the girl point at the rod and shake her head it be the big breaking she say listen to the ground ice it knock when they kneel this time they hear the most curious muffled grunt and knock apparently under their foot sometimes it sound as though blind puppy be squeak above the lamp then as if stone be be grind on hard ice and again like muffled blow on drum but all drag out and make small as though they travel through little horn weary distance away we shall not go to sedna lie down say kotuko it be the breaking the tornaq have cheat we we shall die all this may sound absurd enough but the two be face to face with very real danger the three day gale have drive the deep water of baffin ’s bay southerly and pile it on to the edge of the far reach land ice that stretch from bylot ’s island to the west also the strong current which set east out of lancaster sound carry with it mile upon mile of what they call pack ice rough ice that have not freeze into field and this pack be bombard the floe at the same time that the swell and heave of the storm work sea be weaken and undermine it what kotuko and the girl have be listen to be the faint echo of that fight thirty or forty mile away and the little tell tale rod quiver to the shock of it now as the inuit say when the ice once wake after its long winter sleep there be no know what may happen for solid floe ice change shape almost as quickly as cloud the gale be evidently spring gale send out of time and anything be possible yet the two be happy in their mind than before if the floe break up there would be no more wait and suffer spirit goblin and witch people be move about on the racking ice and they might find themselves step into sedna ’s country side by side with all sort of wild thing the flush of excitement still on they when they leave the hut after the gale the noise on the horizon be steadily grow and the tough ice moan and buzz all round they it be still wait say kotuko on the top of hummock sit or crouch the eight legged thing that they have see three day before and it howl horribly let we follow say the girl it may know some way that do not lead to sedna
but she reel from weakness as she take the pull rope the thing move off slowly and clumsily across the ridge head always toward the westward and the land
and they follow while the growl thunder at the edge of the floe roll near and near the floe ’s lip be split and crack in every direction for three or four mile inland and great pan of ten foot thick ice from few yard to twenty acre square be jolt and duck and surge into one another and into the yet unbroken floe as the heavy swell take and shake and spout between they this batter ram ice be so to speak the first army that the sea be fle against the floe the incessant crash and jar of these cake almost drown the rip sound of sheet of pack ice drive bodily under the floe as card be hastily push under tablecloth where the water be shallow these sheet would be pile one atop of the other till the bottommost touch mud fifty foot down and the discoloured sea bank behind the muddy ice till the increase pressure drive all forward again in addition to the floe and the pack ice the gale and the current be bring down true berg sailing mountain of ice snap off from the greenland side of the water or the north shore of melville bay they pound in solemnly the wave break white round they and advance on the floe like an old time fleet under full sail berg that seem ready to carry the world before it would ground helplessly in deep water reel over and wallow in lather of foam and mud and fly frozen spray while much small and low one would rip and ride into the flat floe fling ton of ice on either side and cut track half mile long before it be stop some fall like sword shear raw edge canal and other splinter into shower of block weigh score of ton apiece that whirl and skirt among the hummock other again rise up bodily out of the water when they shoal twist as though in pain and fall solidly on their side while the sea thresh over their shoulder this trampling and crowd and bend and buckle and arching of the ice into every possible shape be go on as far as the eye could reach all along the north line of the floe from where kotuko and the girl be the confusion look no more than an uneasy ripple crawl movement under the horizon
but it come toward they each moment and they could hear far away to landward heavy booming as it might have be the boom of artillery through fog that show that the floe be be jam home against the iron cliff of bylot ’s island the land to the southward behind they this have never be before say kotuko star stupidly this be not the time how can the floe break now follow that the girl cry point to the thing half limp half run distractedly before they they follow tug at the hand sleigh while near and near come the roar march of the ice at last the field round they crack and star in every direction and the crack open and snap like the tooth of wolf but where the thing rest on mound of old and scatter ice block some fifty foot high there be no motion kotuko leap forward wildly drag the girl after he and crawl to the bottom of the mound the talking of the ice grow louder and louder round they
but the mound stay fast and as the girl look at he he throw his right elbow upward and outward make the inuit sign for land in the shape of an island and land it be that the eight legged limp thing have lead they to some granite tip sand beach islet off the coast shod and sheathe and mask with ice so that no man could have tell it from the floe but at the bottom solid earth and not shift ice the smashing and rebound of the floe as they ground and splinter mark the border of it and friendly shoal run out to the northward and turn aside the rush of the heavy ice exactly as ploughshare turn over loam there be danger of course that some heavily squeeze ice field might shoot up the beach and plane off the top of the islet bodily
but that do not trouble kotuko and the girl when they make their snow house and begin to eat and hear the ice hammer and skid along the beach the thing have disappear and kotuko be talk excitedly about his power over spirit as he crouch round the lamp in the middle of his wild say the girl begin to laugh and rock herself backward and forward behind her shoulder crawl into the hut crawl by crawl there be two head one yellow and one black that belong to two of the most sorrowful and ashamed dog that ever you see kotuko the dog be one and the black leader be the other both be now fat
well look and quite restore to their proper mind but couple to each other in an extraordinary fashion when the black leader run off you remember his harness be still on he he must have meet kotuko the dog and play or fight with he for his shoulder loop have catch in the plaited copper wire of kotuko ’s collar and have draw tight so that neither could get at the trace to gnaw it apart but each be fasten sidelong to his neighbour ’s neck that with the freedom of hunt on their own account must have help to cure their madness they be very sober the girl push the two shamefaced creature towards kotuko and sob with laughter cry that be quiquern who lead we to safe ground look at his eight leg and double head kotuko cut they free and they fall into his arm yellow and black together try to explain how they have get their sen back again kotuko run hand down their rib which be round and well clothe they have find food he say with grin do not think we shall go to sedna so soon my tornaq send these the sickness have leave they as soon as they have greet kotuko these two who have be force to sleep and eat and hunt together for the past few week fly at each other ’s throat and there be beautiful battle in the snow house empty dog do not fight kotuko say they have find the seal let we sleep we shall find food when they wake there be open water on the north beach of the island and all the loosen ice have be drive landward the first sound of the surf be one of the most delightful that the inuit can hear for it mean that spring be on the road kotuko and the girl take hold of hand and smile for the clear full roar of the surge among the ice remind they of salmon and reindeer time and the smell of blossom ground willow even as they look the sea begin to skim over between the float cake of ice so intense be the cold but on the horizon there be vast red glare and that be the light of the sunken sun it be more like hear he yawn in his sleep than see he rise and the glare last for only few minute but it mark the turn of the year nothing they feel could alter that kotuko find the dog fight over fresh kill seal who be follow the fish that gale always disturb he be the first of some twenty or thirty seal that land on the island in the course of the day and till the sea freeze hard there be hundred of keen black head rejoice in the shallow free water and float about with the float ice it be good to eat seal liver again to fill the lamp recklessly with blubber and watch the flame blaze three foot in the air but as soon as the new sea ice bore kotuko and the girl load the hand sleigh and make the two dog pull as they have never pull in their life for they fear what might have happen in their village the weather be as pitiless as usual but it be easy to draw sleigh load with good food than to hunt starve they leave five and twenty seal carcass bury in the ice of the beach all ready for use and hurry back to their people the dog show they the way as soon as kotuko tell they what be expect and though there be no sign of landmark in two day they be give tongue outside kadlu ’s house only three dog answer they the other have be eat and the house be all dark but when kotuko shout ojo boil meat weak voice reply and when he call the muster of the village name by name very distinctly there be no gap in it an hour later the lamp blaze in kadlu ’s house snow water be heat the pot be begin to simmer and the snow be dripping from the roof as amoraq make ready meal for all the village and the boy baby in the hood chew at strip of rich nutty blubber and the hunter slowly and methodically fill themselves to the very brim with seal meat kotuko and the girl tell their tale the two dog sit between they and whenever their name come in they cock an ear apiece and look most thoroughly ashamed of themselves dog who have once go mad and recover the inuit say be safe against all further attack so the tornaq do not forget we say kotuko the storm blow the ice break and the seal swim in behind the fish that be frighten by the storm now the new seal hole be not two day distant let the good hunter go to morrow and bring back the seal have spear twenty five seal bury in the ice when we have eat those we will all follow the seal on the floe what do you do say the sorcerer in the same sort of voice as he use to kadlu rich of the tununirmiut kadlu look at the girl from the north and say quietly we build house he point to the north west side of kadlu ’s house for that be the side on which the marry son or daughter always live the girl turn her hand palm upward with little despairing shake of her head she be foreigner pick up starve and could bring nothing to the housekeeping amoraq jump from the bench where she sit and begin to sweep thing into the girl ’s lap stone lamp iron skin scraper tin kettle deer skin embroider with musk ox tooth and real canvas needle such as sailor use the fine dowry that have ever be give on the far edge of the arctic circle and the girl from the north bow her head down to the very floor also these say kotuko laugh and sign to the dog who thrust their cold muzzle into the girl ’s face ah say the angekok with an important cough as though he have be think it all over as soon as kotuko leave the village go to the singing house and sing magic sing all the long night and call upon the spirit of the reindeer my singing make the gale blow that break the ice and draw the two dog toward kotuko when the ice would have crush his bone my song draw the seal in behind the break ice my body lie still in the quaggi but my spirit run about on the ice and guide kotuko and the dog in all the thing they do do it everybody be full and sleepy so no one contradict and the angekok by virtue of his office help himself to yet another lump of boil meat and lie down to sleep with the other in the warm well light oil smell home now kotuko who draw very well in the inuit fashion scratch picture of all these adventure on long flat piece of ivory with hole at one end when he and the girl go north to ellesmere land in the year of the wonderful open winter he leave the picture story with kadlu who lose it in the shingle when his dog sleigh break down one summer on the beach of lake netille at nikosiring and there lake inuit find it next spring and sell it to man at imigen who be interpreter on cumberland sound whaler
and he sell it to hans olsen who be afterward quartermaster on board big steamer that take tourist to the north cape in norway when the tourist season be over the steamer run between london and australia stop at ceylon and there olsen sell the ivory to cingalese jeweller for two imitation sapphire find it under some rubbish in house at colombo and have translate it from one end to the other angutivaun taina this be very free translation of the song of the return hunter as the man use to sing it after seal spear the inuit always repeat thing over and over again our glove be stiff with the frozen blood our fur with the drift snow as we come in with the seal the seal in from the edge of the floe au jana aua oha haq and the yelp dog team go and the long whip crack and the man come back back from the edge of the floe we track our seal to his secret place we hear he scratch below we make our mark
and we watch beside out on the edge of the floe we raise our lance when he rise to breathe we drive it downward so
and we play he thus and we kill he thus out on the edge of the floe our glove be glue with the frozen blood our eye with the drift snow
but we come back to our wife again back from the edge of the floe au jana aua oha haq and the loaded dog team go and the wife can hear their man come back back from the edge of the floe red dog for our white and our excellent night for the night of swift run fair range far see good hunting sure cunning for the smell of the dawn untainted ere dew have depart for the rush through the mist and the quarry blind start for the cry of our mate when the sambhur have wheel and be stand at bay for the risk and the riot of night for the sleep at the lair mouth by day it be meet and we go to the fight it be after the letting in of the jungle that the pleasant part of mowgli ’s life begin he have the good conscience that come from pay debt all the jungle be his friend and just little afraid of he the thing that he do and see and hear when he be wander from one people to another with or without his four companion would make many many story each as long as this one so you will never be tell how he meet the mad elephant of mandla who kill two and twenty bullock draw eleven cart of coin silver to the government treasury and scatter the shiny rupee in the dust how he fight jacala the crocodile all one long night in the marsh of the north and break his skin knife on the brute ’s back plate how he find new and long knife round the neck of man who have be kill by wild boar and how he track that boar and kill he as fair price for the knife how he be catch up once in the great famine by the moving of the deer and nearly crush to death in the sway hot herd how he save hathi the silent from be once more trap in pit with stake at the bottom and how next day he himself fall into very cunning leopard trap and how hathi break the thick wooden bar to piece above he how he milk the wild buffalo in the swamp and how----
but we must tell one tale at time father and mother wolf die and mowgli roll big boulder against the mouth of their cave and cry the death song over they baloo grow very old and stiff and even bagheera whose nerve be steel and whose muscle be iron be shade slow on the kill than he have be akela turn from gray to milky white with pure age his rib stick out and he walk as though he have be make of wood and mowgli kill for he
but the young wolf the child of the disband seeonee pack throve and increase and when there be about forty of they masterless full voice clean footed five year old akela tell they that they ought to gather themselves together and follow the law and run under one head as befit the free people this be not question in which mowgli concern himself for as he say he have eat sour fruit and he know the tree it hang from but when phao son of phaona his father be the gray tracker in the day of akela ’s headship fight his way to the leadership of the pack accord to the jungle law and the old call and song begin to ring under the star once more mowgli come to the council rock for memory ’s sake when he choose to speak the pack wait till he have finish and he sit at akela ’s side on the rock above phao those be day of good hunting and good sleep no strange care to break into the jungle that belong to mowgli ’s people as they call the pack and the young wolf grow fat and strong and there be many cub to bring to the look over mowgli always attend look over remember the night when black panther buy naked brown baby into the pack and the long call
look look well wolf make his heart flutter otherwise he would be far away in the jungle with his four brother taste touch seeing and feel new thing one twilight when he be trot leisurely across the range to give akela the half of buck that he have kill while the four jog behind he spar little and tumble one another over for joy of be alive he hear cry that have never be hear since the bad day of shere khan it be what they call in the jungle the pheeal hideous kind of shriek that the jackal give when he be hunt behind tiger or when there be big kill afoot if you can imagine mixture of hate triumph fear and despair with kind of leer run through it you will get some notion of the pheeal that rise and sink and waver and quaver far away across the waingunga the four stop at once bristle and growl mowgli ’s hand go to his knife
and he check the blood in his face his eyebrow knot there be no striped one dare kill here he say that be not the cry of the forerunner answer gray brother it be some great killing it break out again half sob and half chuckle just as though the jackal have soft human lip then mowgli draw deep breath and run to the council rock overtake on his way hurry wolf of the pack phao and akela be on the rock together and below they every nerve strain sit the other the mother and the cub be canter off to their lair for when the pheeal cry it be no time for weak thing to be abroad they could hear nothing except the waingunga rush and gurgle in the dark and the light evening wind among the tree top till suddenly across the river wolf call it be no wolf of the pack for they be all at the rock the note change to long despair bay and dhole it say dhole dhole dhole they hear tired foot on the rock and gaunt wolf streak with red on his flank his right fore paw useless and his jaw white with foam fling himself into the circle and lie gasp at mowgli ’s foot good hunting under whose headship say phao gravely good hunting win tolla be be the answer he mean that he be solitary wolf fend for himself his mate and his cub in some lonely lair as do many wolf in the south win tolla mean an outlier one who lie out from any pack then he pant
and they could see his heart beat shake he backward and forward what move say phao for that be the question all the jungle ask after the pheeal cry the dhole the dhole of the dekkan red dog the killer they come north from the south say the dekkan be empty and kill out by the way when this moon be new there be four to my mate and three cub she would teach they to kill on the grass plain hide to drive the buck as we do who be of the open at midnight hear they together full tongue on the trail at the dawn wind find they stiff in the grass four free people four when this moon be new then seek my blood right and find the dhole how many say mowgli quickly the pack growl deep in their throat do not know three of they will kill no more but at the last they drive like the buck on my three leg they drive look free people he thrust out his mangle fore foot all dark with dry blood there be cruel bite low down on his side and his throat be tear and worry eat say akela rise up from the meat mowgli have bre he and the outlier fling himself on it this shall be no loss he say humbly when he have take off the first edge of his hunger give little strength free people and also will kill my lair be empty that be full when this moon be new and the blood debt be not all pay phao hear his tooth crack on haunch bone and grunt approvingly we shall need those jaw say he be there cub with the dhole
nay nay red hunter all grow dog of their pack heavy and strong for all that they eat lizard in the dekkan what win tolla have say mean that the dhole the red hunting dog of the dekkan be move to kill and the pack know well that even the tiger will surrender new kill to the dhole they drive straight through the jungle and what they meet they pull down and tear to piece though they be not as big nor half as cunning as the wolf they be very strong and very numerous the dhole for instance do not begin to call themselves pack till they be hundred strong whereas forty wolf make very fair pack indeed mowgli ’s wander have take he to the edge of the high grassy down of the dekkan and he have see the fearless dhole sleep and play and scratch themselves in the little hollow and tussock that they use for lair he despise and hat they because they do not smell like the free people because they do not live in cave and above all because they have hair between their toe while he and his friend be clean footed
but he know for hathi have tell he what terrible thing dhole hunt pack be even hathi move aside from their line and until they be kill or till game be scarce they will go forward akela know something of the dhole too for he say to mowgli quietly it be well to die in full pack than leaderless and alone this be good hunting and my last
but as man live thou hast very many more night and day little brother go north and lie down and if any live after the dhole have go by he shall bring thee word of the fight ah say mowgli quite gravely must go to the marsh and catch little fish and sleep in tree or must ask help of the bandar log and crack nut while the pack fight below it be to the death
say akela thou hast never meet the dhole
the red killer even the striped one---- aowa aowa say mowgli pettingly have kill one striped ape and sure be in my stomach that shere khan would have leave his own mate for meat to the dhole if he have wind pack across three range listen now there be wolf my father and there be wolf my mother and there be an old gray wolf not too wise he be white now be my father and my mother therefore i-- he raise his voice say that when the dhole come and if the dhole come mowgli and the free people be of one skin for that hunting and say by the bull that buy by the bull bagheera pay for in the old day which ye of the pack do not remember--_i say that the tree and the river may hear and hold fast if forget say that this my knife shall be as tooth to the pack and do not think it be so blunt this be my word which have go from thou dost not know the dhole man with wolf ’s tongue say win tolla look only to clear the blood debt against they ere they have in many piece they move slowly kill out as they go but in two day little strength will come back to and turn again for the blood debt but for ye free people my word be that ye go north and eat but little for while till the dhole be go there be no meat in this hunting hear the outlier say mowgli with laugh free people we must go north and dig lizard and rat from the bank lest by any chance we meet the dhole he must kill out our hunting ground while we lie hide in the north till it please he to give we our own again he be dog and the pup of dog red yellow belly lairless and haired between every toe he count his cub six and eight at the litter as though he be chikai the little leap rat surely we must run away free people and beg leave of the people of the north for the offal of dead cattle
ye know the say north be the vermin south be the lice we be the jungle choose ye choose it be good hunting for the pack for the full pack for the lair and the litter for the in kill and the out kill for the mate that drive the doe and the little little cub within the cave it be met!--it be met!--it be meet the pack answer with one deep crash bark that sound in the night like big tree fall it be meet they cry stay with these say mowgli to the four we shall need every tooth phao and akela must make ready the battle go to count the dog it be death win tolla cry half rise what can such hairless one do against the red dog even the strip one remember---- thou art indeed an outlier mowgli call back
but we will speak when the dhole be dead good hunting all he hurry off into the darkness wild with excitement hardly look where he set foot and the natural consequence be that he trip full length over kaa ’s great coil where the python lie watch deer path near the river kssha say kaa angrily be this jungle work to stamp and tramp and undo night ’s hunting when the game be move so well too the fault be mine
say mowgli pick himself up indeed be seek thee flathead but each time we meet thou art long and broad by the length of my arm there be none like thee in the jungle wise old strong and most beautiful kaa now whither do this trail lead kaa ’s voice be gentle not moon since there be manle with knife throw stone at my head and call bad little tree cat name because lie asleep in the open ay and turn every drive deer to all the wind and mowgli be hunt and this same flathead be too deaf to hear his whistle and leave the deer road
free mowgli answer composedly sit down among the paint coil now this same manling come with soft tickle word to this same flathead tell he that he be wise and strong and beautiful and this same old flathead believe and make place thus for this same stone throw manling and art thou at ease now could bagheera give thee so good rest place kaa have as usual make sort of soft half hammock of himself under mowgli ’s weight the boy reach out in the darkness and gather in the supple cable like neck till kaa ’s head rest on his shoulder and then he tell he all that have happen in the jungle that night wise may be say kaa at the end but deaf surely be else should have hear the pheeal small wonder the eater of grass be uneasy how many be the dhole have not yet see come hot foot to thee thou art old than hathi
but oh kaa,”--here mowgli wriggle with sheerjoy,--“it will be good hunting few of we will see another moon dost thou strike in this remember thou art man and remember what pack cast thee out let the wolf look to the dog thou art man last year ’s nut be this year ’s black earth say mowgli it be true that be man but it be in my stomach that this night have say that be wolf call the river and the tree to remember be of the free people kaa till the dhole have go by free people kaa grunt free thief and thou hast tie thyself into the death knot for the sake of the memory of the dead wolf this be no good hunting it be my word which have speak the tree know the river know till the dhole have go by my word come not back to this change all trail have think to take thee away with to the northern marsh but the word even the word of little naked hairless manling be the word now kaa say---- think well flathead lest thou tie thyself into the death knot also need no word from thee for well know---- be it
so then say kaa will give no word but what be in thy stomach to do when the dhole come they must swim the waingunga think to meet they with my knife in the shallow the pack behind and so stab and thrust we little might turn they down stream or cool their throat the dhole do not turn and their throat be hot say kaa there will be neither manle nor wolf cub when that hunting be do but only dry bone if we die we die it will be most good hunting
but my stomach be young and have not see many rain be not wise nor strong hast thou well plan kaa have see hundred and hundred rain ere hathi cast his milk tush my trail be big in the dust by the first egg be old than many tree and have see all that the jungle have do
but this be new hunting say mowgli never before have the dhole cross our trail what be have be what will be be no more than forget year strike backward be still while count those my year for long hour
mowgli lie back among the coil while kaa his head motionless on the ground think of all that he have see and know since the day he come from the egg the light seem to go out of his eye and leave they like stale opal and now and again he make little stiff pass with his head right and leave as though he be hunt in his sleep mowgli doze quietly for he know that there be nothing like sleep before hunt and he be train to take it at any hour of the day or night then he feel kaa ’s back grow big and broad below he as the huge python puff himself out hiss with the noise of sword draw from steel scabbard have see all the dead season kaa say at last and the great tree and the old elephant and the rock that be bare and sharp pointed ere the moss grow art thou still alive manle it be only little after moonset say mowgli do not understand---- hssh be again kaa know it be but little time now we will go to the river and will show thee what be to be do against the dhole he turn straight as an arrow for the main stream of the waingunga plunge in little above the pool that hide the peace rock mowgli at his side nay do not swim go swiftly my back little brother mowgli tuck his left arm round kaa ’s neck drop his right close to his body and straighten his foot then kaa breast the current as he alone could and the ripple of the check water stand up in frill round mowgli ’s neck and his foot be wave to and fro in the eddy under the python ’s lash side mile or two above the peace rock the waingunga narrow between gorge of marble rock from eighty to hundred foot high and the current run like mill race between and over all manner of ugly stone but mowgli do not trouble his head about the water little water in the world could have give he moment ’s fear he be look at the gorge on either side and sniff uneasily for there be sweetish sourish smell in the air very like the smell of big ant hill on hot day instinctively he lower himself in the water only raise his head to breathe from time to time and kaa come to anchor with double twist of his tail round sink rock hold mowgli in the hollow of coil while the water race on this be the place of death say the boy why do we come here they sleep say kaa hathi will not turn aside for the strip one yet hathi and the strip one together turn aside for the dhole and the dhole they say turn aside for nothing and yet for whom do the little people of the rock turn aside tell master of the jungle who be the master of the jungle these mowgli whisper it be the place of death let we go nay look well for they be asleep it be as it be when be not the length of thy arm the split and weatherworn rock of the gorge of the waingunga have be use since the beginning of the jungle by the little people of the rock the busy furious black wild bee of india and as mowgli know well all trail turn off half mile before they reach the gorge for century the little people have hive and swarm from cleft to cleft and swarm again stain the white marble with stale honey and make their comb tall and deep in the dark of the inner cave where neither man nor beast nor fire nor water have ever touch they the length of the gorge on both siaes be hung as it be with black shimmery velvet curtain and mowgli sink as he look for those be the clot million of the sleep bee there be other lump and festoon and thing like decayed tree trunk stud on the face of the rock the old comb of past year or new city build in the shadow of the windless gorge and huge mass of spongy rotten trash have roll down and stick among the tree and creeper that cling to the rock face as he listen he hear more than once the rustle and slide of honey load comb turn over or fail away somewhere in the dark gallery then boom of angry wing and the sullen drip drip drip of the waste honey gutter along till it lip over some ledge in the open air and sluggishly trickle down on the twig there be tiny little beach not five foot broad on one side of the river and that be pile high with the rubbish of uncounted year there be dead bee drone sweeping and stale comb and wing of maraud moth that have stray in after honey all tumble in smooth pile of the fine black dust the mere sharp smell of it be enough to frighten anything that have no wing and know what the little people be kaa move up stream again till he come to sandy bar at the head of the gorge here be this season ’s kill say he on the bank lie the skeleton of couple of young deer and buffalo mowgli could see that neither wolf nor jackal have touch the hone which be lie out naturally they come beyond the line they do not know the law murmur mowgli and the little people kill they let we go ere they wake they do not wake till the dawn say kaa now will tell thee hunt buck from the south many many rain ago come hither from the south not know the jungle pack on his trail be make blind by fear he leap from above the pack run by sight for they be hot and blind on the trail the sun be high and the little people be many and very angry many too be those of the pack who leap into the waingunga but they be dead ere they take water those who do not leap die also in the rock above but the buck live how because he come first run for his life leap ere the little people be aware and be in the river when they gather to kill the pack follow be altogether lose under the weight of the little people the buck live mowgli repeat slowly at least he do not die then though none wait his come down with strong body to hold he safe against the water as certain old fat deaf yellow flathead would wait for manle yea though there be all the dhole of the dekkan on his trail what be in thy stomach kaa ’s head be close to mowgli ’s ear and it be little time before the boy answer it be to pull the very whisker of death but kaa thou art indeed the wise of all the jungle so many have say look now if the dhole follow thee---- as surely they will follow
