ho have many little thorn under my tongue to prick into their hide if they follow thee hot and blind look only at thy shoulder those who do not die up above will take water either here or low down for the little people will rise up and cover they now the waingunga be hungry water and they will have no kaa to hold they but will go down such as live to the shallow by the seeonee lair and there thy pack may meet they by the throat well could not be till the rain fall in the dry season there be now only the little matter of the run and the leap will make know to the dhole so that they shall follow very closely hast thou see the rock above thee from the landward side indeed no that have forget go look it be all rotten ground cut and full of hole one of thy clumsy foot set down without see would end the hunt see leave thee here and for thy sake only will carry word to the pack that they may know where to look for the dhole for myself be not of one skin with any wolf when kaa dislike an acquaintance he could be more unpleasant than any of the jungle people except perhaps bagheera he swim down stream and opposite the rock he come on phao and akela listen to the night noise hssh dog he say cheerfully the dhole will come down stream if ye be not afraid ye can kill they in the shallow when come they say phao and where be my man cub say akela they come when they come say kaa wait and see as for thy man cub from whom thou hast take word and so lay he open to death thy man cub be with and if he be not already dead the fault be none of thine bleach dog wait here for the dhole and be glad that the man cub and strike on thy side kaa flash up stream again and moor himself in the middle of the gorge look upward at the line of the cliff presently he see mowgli ’s head move against the star and then there be whizz in the air the keen clean schloop of body fall foot
first and next minute the boy be at rest again in the loop of kaa ’s body it be no leap by night say mowgli quietly have jump twice as far for sport but that be an evil place above low bush and gully that go down very deep all full of the little people have put big stone one above the other by the side of three gully these shall throw down with my foot in run and the little people will rise up behind very angry that be man ’s talk and man ’s cunning say kaa thou art wise but the little people be always angry nay at twilight all wing near and far rest for while will play with the dhole at twilight for the dhole hunt well by day he follow now win tolla ’s blood trail chil do not leave dead ox nor the dhole the blood trail say kaa then will make he new blood trail of his own blood if can and give he dirt to eat thou wilt stay here kaa till come again with my dhole ay
but what if they kill thee in the jungle or the little people kill thee before thou canst leap down to the river when to morrow come we will kill for to morrow say mowgli quote jungle say and again when be dead it be time to sing the death song good hunt kaa he loose his arm from the python ’s neck and go down the gorge like log in freshet paddle toward the far bank where he find slack water and laugh aloud from sheer happiness there be nothing mowgli like well than as he himself say to pull the whisker of death and make the jungle know that he be their overlord he have often with baloo ’s help rob bee nest in single tree and he know that the little people hat the smell of wild garlic
so he gather small bundle of it tie it up with bark string and then follow win tolla ’s blood trail as it run southerly from the lair for some five mile look at the tree with his head on one side and chuckle as he look
mowgli the frog have be say he to himself mowgli the wolf have say that be now mowgli the ape must be before be mowgli the buck at the end shall be mowgli the man and he slide his thumb along the eighteen inch blade of his knife win tolla ’s trail all rank with dark blood spot run under forest of thick tree that grow close together and stretch away north eastward gradually grow thin and thin to within two mile of the bee rock from the last tree to the low scrub of the bee rock be open country where there be hardly cover enough to hide wolf mowgli trot along under the tree judge distance between branch and branch occasionally climb up trunk and take trial leap from one tree to another till he come to the open ground which he study very carefully for an hour then he turn pick up win tolla ’s trail where he have leave it settle himself in tree with an outrun branch some eight foot from the ground and sit still sharpen his knife on the sole of his foot and sing to himself little before mid day when the sun be very warm he hear the patter of foot and smell the abominable smell of the dhole pack as they trot pitilessly along win tolla ’s trail see from above the red dhole do not look half the size of wolf but mowgli know how strong his foot and jaw be he watch the sharp bay head of the leader snuff along the trail and give he good hunting the brute look up and his companion halt behind he score and score of red dog with low hung tail heavy shoulder weak quarter and bloody mouth the dhole be very silent people as rule and they have no manner even in their own jungle fully two hundred must have gather below he but he could see that the leader sniff hungrily on win tolla ’s trail and try to drag the pack forward that would never do or they would be at the lair in broad daylight and mowgli mean to hold they under his tree till dusk by whose leave do ye come here say mowgli all jungle be our jungle be the reply and the dhole that give it bar his white tooth mowgli look down with smile and imitate perfectly the sharp chitter chatter of chikai the leap rat of the dekkan mean the dhole to understand that he consider they no well than chikai the pack close up round the tree trunk and the leader bay savagely call mowgli tree ape for an answer mowgli stretch down one naked leg and wriggle his bare toe just above the leader ’s head that be enough and more than enough to wake the pack to stupid rage those who have hair between their toe do not care to be remind of it mowgli catch his foot away as the leader leap up and say sweetly dog red dog go back to the dekkan and eat lizard go to chikai thy brother dog dog red red dog there be hair between every toe he twiddle his toe second time come down ere we starve thee out hairless ape yell the pack and this be exactly what mowgli want he lie himself down along the branch his cheek to the bark his right arm free
and there he tell the pack what he think and know about they their manner their custom their mate and their puppy there be no speech in the world so rancorous and so stinging as the language the jungle people use to show scorn and contempt when you come to think of it you will see how this must be so as mowgli tell kaa he have many little thorn under his tongue and slowly and deliberately he drive the dhole from silence to growl from growl to yell and from yell to hoarse slavery rave they try to answer his taunt but cub might as well have try to answer kaa in rage and all the while mowgli right hand lie crooked at his side ready for action his foot lock round the branch the big bay leader have leap many time in the air but mowgli dare not risk false blow at last make furious beyond his natural strength he bind up seven or eight foot clear of the ground then mowgli ’s hand shoot out like the head of tree snake and grip he by the scruff of his neck and the branch shake with the jar as his weight fall back almost wrench mowgli to the ground
but he never loose his grip and inch by inch he haul the beast hang like drown jackal up on the branch with his left hand he reach for his knife and cut off the red bushy tail fling the dhole back to earth again that be all he need the pack would not go forward on win tolla ’s trail now till they have kill mowgli or mowgli have kill they he see they settle down in circle with quiver of the haunch that mean they be go to stay
and so he climb to high crotch settle his back comfortably and go to sleep after three or four hour he wake and count the pack they be all there silent husky and dry with eye of steel the sun be begin to sink in half an hour the little people of the rock would be end their labour and as you know the dhole do not fight well in the twilight do not need such faithful watcher he say politely stand up on branch but will remember this ye be true dhole but to my thinking over much of one kind for that reason do not give the big lizard eater his tail again art thou not pleased red dog myself will tear out thy stomach yell the leader scratch at the foot of the tree nay
but consider wise rat of the dekkan there will now be many litter of little tailless red dog yea with raw red stump that sting when the sand be hot go home red dog and cry that an ape have do this ye will not go come then with and will make you very wise he move bandar log fashion into the next tree and so on into the next and the next the pack follow with lift hungry head now
and then he would pretend to fall and the pack would tumble one over the other in their haste to be at the death it be curious sight the boy with the knife that shine in the low sunlight as it sift through the upper branch and the silent pack with their red coat all aflame huddle and follow below when he come to the last tree he take the garlic and rub himself all over carefully and the dhole yell with scorn ape with wolf ’s tongue dost thou think to cover thy scent they say we follow to the death take thy tail
say mowgli fling it back along the course he have take the pack instinctively rush after it and follow now to the death he have slip down the tree trunk and head like the wind in bare foot for the bee rock before the dhole see what he would do they give one deep howl and settle down to the long lob canter that can at the last run down anything that run mowgli know their pack pace to be much slow than that of the wolf or he would never have risk two mile run in full sight they be sure that the boy be theirs at last
and he be sure that he hold they to play with as he please all his trouble be to keep they sufficiently hot behind he to prevent their turning off too soon he run cleanly evenly and springily the tailless leader not five yard behind he and the pack tail out over perhaps quarter of mile of ground crazy and blind with the rage of slaughter
so he keep his distance by ear reserve his last effort for the rush across the bee rock the little people have go to sleep in the early twilight for it be not the season of late blossom flower but as mowgli ’s first foot fall ring hollow on the hollow ground he hear sound as though all the earth be hum then he run as he have never run in his life before spurn aside one two three of the pile of stone into the dark sweet smell gully hear roar like the roar of the sea in cave see with the tail of his eye the air grow dark behind he see the current of the waingunga far below and flat diamond shape head in the water leap outward with all his strength the tailless dhole snap at his shoulder in mid air and drop foot first to the safety of the river breathless and triumphant there be not sting upon he for the smell of the garlic have check the little people for just the few second that he be among they when he rise kaa ’s coil be steady he and thing be bind over the edge of the cliff great lump it seem of clustered bee fall like plummet but before any lump touch water the bee fly upward and the body of dhole whirl down stream overhead they could hear furious short yell that be drown in roar like breaker the roar of the wing of the little people of the rock some of the dhole too have fall into the gully that communicate with the underground cave and there choke and fight and snap among the tumble honeycomb and at last bear up even when they be dead on the heaving wave of bee beneath they shoot out of some hole in the river face to roll over on the black rubbish heap there be dhole who have leap short into the tree on the cliff and the bee blot out their shape but the great number of they madden by the sting have fling themselves into the river and as kaa say the waingunga be hungry water kaa hold mowgli fast till the boy have recover his breath we may not stay here he say the little people be rouse indeed swim low and diving as often as he could mowgli go down the river knife in hand slowly slowly say kaa one tooth do not kill hundred unless it be cobra ’s and many of the dhole take water swiftly when they see the little people rise the more work for my knife then how the little people follow mowgli sink again the face of the water be blanket with wild bee buzz sullenly and ste all they find nothing be ever yet lose by silence say kaa no sting could penetrate his scales--“and thou hast all the long night for the hunting hear they howl nearly half the pack have see the trap their fellow rush into and turn sharp aside have fling themselves into the water where the gorge break down in steep bank their cry of rage and their threat against the tree ape who have bre they to their shame mix with the yell and growl of those who have be punish by the little people to remain ashore be death and every dhole know it their pack be sweep along the current down to the deep eddy of the peace pool but even there the angry little people follow and force they to the water again mowgli could hear the voice of the tailless leader bid his people hold on and kill out every wolf in seeonee
but he do not waste his time in listen one kill in the dark behind we snap dhole here be taint water mowgli have dive forward like an otter twitch struggle dhole under water before he could open his mouth and dark ring rise as the body plop up turn on its side the dhole try to turn but the current prevent they and the little people dart at the head and ear and they could hear the challenge of the seeonee pack grow louder and deep in the gathering darkness again
mowgli dive and again dhole go under and rise dead and again the clamour break out at the rear of the pack some howl that it be good to go ashore other call on their leader to lead they back to the dekkan and other bid mowgli show himself and be kill they come to the fight with two stomach and several voice say kaa the rest be with thy brother below yonder the little people go back to sleep they have chase we far now too turn back for be not of one skin with any wolf good hunt little brother and remember the dhole bite low wolf come run along the bank on three leg leap up and down lie his head sideways close to the ground hunch his back and break high into the air as though he be play with his cub it be win tolla the outlier
and he say never word
but continue his horrible sport beside the dhole they have be long in the water now and be swim wearily their coat drench and heavy their bushy tail drag like sponge so tired and shake that they too be silent watch the pair of blaze eye that move abreast this be no good hunting say one pant good hunting say mowgli as he rise boldly at the brute ’s side and send the long knife home behind the shoulder push hard to avoid his die snap art thou there man cub say win tolla across the water ask of the dead outlier mowgli reply have none come down stream have fill these dog mouth with dirt have trick they in the broad daylight and their leader lack his tail but here be some few for thee still whither shall drive they will wait
say win tolla the night be before near and near come the bay of the seeonee wolf for the pack for the full pack it be meet and bend in the river drive the dhole forward among the sand and shoal opposite the lair then they see their mistake they should have land half mile high up and rush the wolf on dry ground now it be too late the bank be line with burn eye and except for the horrible pheeal that have never stop since sundown there be no sound in the jungle it seem as though win tolla be fawn on they to come ashore and turn and take hold say the leader of the dhole the entire pack fling themselves at the shore thresh and squattere through the shoal water till the face of the waingunga be all white and tear and the great ripple go from side to side like bow wave from boat mowgli follow the rush stabbing and slicing as the dhole huddle together rush up the river beach in one wave then the long fight begin heave and strain and splitting and scatter and narrow and broaden along the red wet sand and over and between the tangle tree root and through and among the bush and in and out of the grass clump for even now the dhole be two to one
but they meet wolf fight for all that make the pack and not only the short high deep chested white tusked hunter of the pack but the anxious eyed lahini the she wolf of the lair as the saying be fight for their litter with here and there yearle wolf his first coat still half woolly tugging and grappling by their side wolf you must know fly at the throat or snap at the flank while dhole by preference bite at the belly so when the dhole be struggle out of the water and have to raise their head the odd be with the wolf on dry land the wolf suffer but in the water or ashore mowgli ’s knife come and go without cease the four have worry their way to his side gray brother crouch between the boy ’s knee be protect his stomach while the other guard his back and either side or stand over he when the shock of leap yell dhole who have throw himself full on the steady blade bear he down for the rest it be one tangle confusion lock and sway mob that move from right to left and from left to right along the bank and also grind round and round slowly on its own centre here would be heave mound like water blister in whirlpool which would break like water blister and throw up four or five mangle dog each strive to get back to the centre here would be single wolf bear down by two or three dhole laboriously drag they forward and sink the while here yearling cub would be hold up by the pressure round he though he have be kill early while his mother craze with dumb rage roll over and over snap and pass on and in the middle of the thick press perhaps one wolf and one dhole forget everything else would be manoeuvre for first hold till they be whirl away by rush of furious fighter once mowgli pass akela dhole on either flank and his all but toothless jaw close over the loin of third and once he see phao his tooth set in the throat of dhole tug the unwilling beast forward till the yearling could finish he but the bulk of the fight be blind flurry and smother in the dark hit trip and tumble yelp groan and worry worry worry round he and behind he and above he as the night wear on the quick giddy go round motion increase the dhole be cow and afraid to attack the strong wolf but do not yet dare to run away mowgli feel that the end be come soon and content himself with strike merely to cripple the yearling be grow bold there be time now and again to breathe and pass word to friend and the mere flicker of the knife would sometimes turn dog aside the meat be very near the bone gray brother yell he be blee from score of flesh wound but the bone be yet to be crack say mowgli thus do we do in the jungle the red blade run like flame along the side of dhole whose hind quarter be hide by the weight of cling wolf my kill snort the wolf through his wrinkled nostril leave he to be thy stomach still empty outlier say mowgli win tolla be fearfully punish but his grip have paralyse the dhole who could not turn round and reach he by the bull that buy say mowgli with bitter laugh it be the tailless one and indeed it be the big bay colour leader it be not wise to kill cub and lahini mowgli go on philosophically wipe the blood out of his eye unless one have also kill the outlier and it be in my stomach that this win tolla kill thee dhole leap to his leader ’s aid but before his tooth have find win tolla ’s flank mowgli ’s knife be in his throat and gray brother take what be leave and thus do we do in the jungle say mowgli win tolla say not word only his jaw be close and close on the backbone as his life ebb the dhole shudder his head drop and he lie still and win tolla drop above he huh the blood debt be pay say mowgli sing the song win tolla he hunt no more say gray brother and akela too be silent this long time the bone be crack thunder phao son of phaona they go kill kill out hunter of the free people dhole after dhole be slink away from those dark and bloody sand to the river to the thick jungle up stream or down stream as he see the road clear the debt the debt shout mowgli pay the debt they have slay the lone wolf let not dog go he be fly to the river knife in hand to check any dhole who dare to take water when from under mound of nine dead rise akela ’s head and fore quarter and mowgli drop on his knee beside the lone wolf say not it would be my last fight akela gasp it be good hunting and thou little brother live having kill many even so die and would would die by thee little brother mowgli take the terrible scarred head on his knee and put his arm round the torn neck it be long since the old day of shere khan and man cub that roll naked in the dust nay nay be wolf be of one skin with the free people mowgli cry it be no will of mine that be man thou art man little brother wolfling of my watching thou art man or else the pack have flee before the dhole my life owe to thee and to day
thou hast save the pack even as once save thee hast thou forget all debt be pay now go to thine own people tell thee again eye of my eye this hunting be end go to thine own people will never go will hunt alone in the jungle have say it after the summer come the rain and after the rain come the spring go back before thou art drive who will drive mowgli will drive mowgli go back to thy people go to man when mowgli drive mowgli will go mowgli answer there be no more to say say akela little brother canst thou raise to my foot also be leader of the free people very carefully and gently mowgli lift the body aside and raise akela to his foot both arm round he and the lone wolf draw long breath and begin the death song that leader of the pack should sing when he die it gather strength as he go on lift and lifting and ring far across the river till it come to the last good hunting and akela shake himself clear of mowgli for an instant and leap into the air fall backward dead upon his last and most terrible kill mowgli sit with his head on his knee careless of anything else while the remnant of the fly dhole be be overtake and run down by the merciless lahinis little by little the cry die away and the wolf return limp as their wound stiffen to take stock of the loss fifteen of the pack as well as half dozen lahini lie dead by the river and of the other not one be unmarked and mowgli sit through it all till the cold daybreak when phao ’s wet red muzzle be drop in his hand and mowgli draw back to show the gaunt body of akela good hunting say phao as though akela be still alive and then over his bite shoulder to the other howl dog wolf have die to night but of all the pack of two hundred fighting dhole whose boast be that all jungle be their jungle and that no live thing could stand before they not one return to the dekkan to carry that word chil ’s song this be the song that chil sing as the kite drop down one after another to the river bed when the great fight be finish chil be good friend with everybody
but he be cold blooded kind of creature at heart because he know that almost everybody in the jungle come to he in the long run these be my companion go forth by night-- for chil look you for chil now come to whistle they the ending of the fight chil vanguard of chil word they give overhead of quarry newly slay word give they underfoot of buck upon the plain here an end of every trail they shall not speak again they that call the hunting cry they that follow fast-- for chil look you for chil they that bid the sambhur wheel or pin he as he passed-- chil vanguard of chil they that lag behind the scent they that run before they that shun the level horn they that overbore here an end of every trail they shall not follow more these be my companion pity twas they die for chil look you for chil now come to comfort they that know they in their pride chil vanguard of chil tattered flank and sink eye open mouth and red lock and lank and lone they lie the dead upon their dead here an end of every trail and here my host be feed the spring run man go to man cry the challenge through the jungle he that be our brother go away hear now and judge ye people of the jungle,-- answer who shall turn he who shall stay man go to man he be weep in the jungle he that be our brother sorrow sore man go to man
oh we love he in the jungle to the man trail where we may not follow more the second year after the great fight with red dog and the death of akela mowgli must have be nearly seventeen year old he look old for hard exercise the good of good eating and bath whenever he feel in the least hot or dusty have give he strength and growth far beyond his age he could swing by one hand from top branch for half an hour at time when he have occasion to look along the tree road he could stop young buck in mid gallop and throw he sideways by the head he could even jerk over the big blue wild boar that live in the marsh of the north the jungle people who use to fear he for his wit fear he now for his strength and when he move quietly on his own affair the mere whisper of his come clear the wood path and yet the look in his eye be always gentle even when he fight his eye never blaze as bagheera do they only grow more and more interested and excited and that be one of the thing that bagheera himself do not understand he ask mowgli about it and the boy laugh and say when miss the kill be angry when must go empty for two day be very angry do not my eye talk then the mouth be hungry say bagheera but the eye say nothing hunting eat or swimming it be all one like stone in wet or dry weather mowgli look at he lazily from under his long eyelash and as usual the panther ’s head drop bagheera know his master they be lie out far up the side of hill overlook the waingunga and the morning mist hang below they in band of white and green as the sun rise it change into bubble sea of red gold churn off and let the low ray stripe the dry grass on which mowgli and bagheera be rest it be the end of the cold weather the leaf and the tree look worn and fade and there be dry tick rustle everywhere when the wind blow little leaf tap tap tap furiously against twig as single leaf catch in current will it rouse bagheera for he snuff the morning air with deep hollow cough throw himself on his back and strike with his fore paw at the nod leaf above the year turn he say the jungle go forward the time of new talk be near that leaf know it be very good the grass be dry mowgli answer pull up tuft even eye of the spring that be little trumpet shape waxy red flower that run in and out among the grasses]--even eye of the spring be shut and bagheera be it well for the black panther so to lie on his back and beat with his paw in the air as though he be the tree cat aowh say bagheera he seem to be think of other thing say be it well for the black panther so to mouth and cough and howl and roll remember we be the master of the jungle thou and i. indeed yes hear man cub bagheera roll over hurriedly and sit up the dust on his ragged black flank he be just cast his winter coat we be surely the master of the jungle who be so strong as mowgli who so wise there be curious drawl in the voice that make mowgli turn to see whether by any chance the black panther be make fun of he for the jungle be full of word that sound like one thing but mean another say we be beyond question the master of the jungle bagheera repeat have do wrong do not know that the man cub no long lie upon the ground do he fly then mowgli sit with his elbow on his knee look out across the valley at the daylight somewhere down in the wood below bird be try over in husky reedy voice the first few note of his spring song it be no more than shadow of the liquid tumbling call he would be pour later but bagheera hear it say the time of new talk be near growl the panther switch his tail hear mowgli answer bagheera why dost thou shake all over the sun be warm that be ferao the scarlet woodpecker say bagheera he have not forget now too must remember my song and he begin purr and croon to himself hark back dissatisfied again and again there be no game afoot say mowgli little brother be both thine ear stop that be no kill word but my song that make ready against the need have forget shall know when the time of new talk be here because then thou and the other all run away and leave
alone mowgli speak rather savagely but indeed little brother bagheera begin we do not always---- say ye do say mowgli shoot out his forefinger angrily ye do run away and who be the master of the jungle must need walk alone how be it last season when would gather sugar cane from the field of man pack send runner send thee!--to hathi bid he to come upon such night and pluck the sweet grass for with his trunk he come only two night later say bagheera cower little and of that long sweet grass that please thee
so he gather more than any man cub could eat in all the night of the rain that be no fault of mine he do not come upon the night when send he the word no he be trumpet and run and roar through the valley in the moonlight his trail be like the trail of three elephant for he would not hide among the tree he dance in the moonlight before the house of the man pack see he
and yet he would not come to and be the master of the jungle it be the time of new talk say the panther always very humble perhaps little brother thou didst not that time call he by master word listen to ferao and be glad mowgli ’s bad temper seem to have boil itself away he lie back with his head on his arm his eye shut do not know nor do care he say sleepily let we sleep bagheera my stomach be heavy in make rest for my head the panther lie down again with sigh because he could hear ferao practise and repractise his song against the springtime of new talk as they say in an indian jungle the season slide one into the other almost without division there seem to be only two the wet and the dry
but if you look closely below the torrent of rain and the cloud of char and dust you will find all four go round in their regular ring spring be the most wonderful because she have not to cover clean bare field with new leaf and flower but to drive before she and to put away the hanging on over survive raffle of half green thing which the gentle winter have suffer to live and to make the partly dress stale earth feel new and young once more and this she do so well that there be no spring in the world like the jungle spring there be one day when all thing be tired and the very smell as they drift on the heavy air be old and use one can not explain this but it feel so then there be another day to the eye nothing whatever have change when all the smell be new and delightful and the whisker of the jungle people quiver to their root and the winter hair come away from their side in long draggle lock then perhaps little rain fall and all the tree and the bush and the bamboo and the moss and the juicy leaved plant wake with noise of grow that you can almost hear and under this noise run day and night deep hum that be the noise of the spring vibrate boom which be neither bee nor fall water nor the wind in tree top but the purring of the warm happy world up to this year mowgli have always delight in the turn of the season it be he who generally see the first eye of the spring deep down among the grass and the first bank of spring cloud which be like nothing else in the jungle his voice could be hear in all sort of wet star light blossom place help the big frog through their chorus or mock the little upside down owl that hoot through the white night like all his people spring be the season he choose for his flitting move for the mere joy of rush through the warm air thirty forty or fifty mile between twilight and the morning star and come back pant and laugh and wreathe with strange flower the four do not follow he on these wild ringing of the jungle but go off to sing song with other wolf the jungle people be very busy in the spring and mowgli could hear they grunt and scream and whistle accord to their kind their voice then be different from their voice at other time of the year and that be one of the reason why spring in the jungle be call the time of new talk but that spring as he tell bagheera his stomach be change in he ever since the bamboo shoot turn spotty brown he have be look forward to the morning when the smell should change but when the morning come and mor the peacock blaze in bronze and blue and gold cry it aloud all along the misty wood and mowgli open his mouth to send on the cry the word choke between his tooth and feeling come over he that begin at his toe and end in his hair feeling of pure unhappiness so that he look himself over to be sure that he have not tread on thorn mor cry the new smell the other bird take it over and from the rock by the waingunga he hear bagheera ’s hoarse scream something between the scream of an eagle and the neighing of horse there be yell and scattering of bandar log in the new bud branch above and there stand mowgli his chest fill to answer mor sink in little gasp as the breath be drive out of it by this unhappiness he star all round he
but he could see no more than the mock bandar log scud through the tree and mor his tail spread in full splendour dance on the slope below the smell have change scream mor good hunt little brother where be thy answer little brother good hunting whistle chil the kite and his mate swoop down together the two baffe under mowgli ’s nose so close that pinch of downy white feather brush away light spring rain elephant rain they call it drive across the jungle in belt half mile wide leave the new leaf wet and nod behind and die out in double rainbow and light roll of thunder the spring hum break out for minute and be silent but all the jungle folk seem to be give tongue at once all except mowgli have eat good food he say to himself have drunk good water nor do my throat burn and grow small as it do when bite the blue spot root that oo the turtle say be clean food
but my stomach be heavy and have give very bad talk to bagheera and other people of the jungle and my people now too be hot and now be cold and now be neither hot nor cold but angry with that which can not see it be time to make run to night will cross the range
yes will make spring run to the marsh of the north and back again have hunt too easily too long the four shall come with for they grow as fat as white grub he call
but never one of the four answer they be far beyond earshot sing over the spring song the moon and sambhur song with the wolf of the pack for in the spring time the jungle people make very little difference between the day and the night he give the sharp bark note
but his only answer be the mock maiou of the little spot tree cat wind in and out among the branch for early bird nest at this he shake all over with rage and half draw his knife
then he become very haughty though there be no one to see he and stalk severely down the hillside chin up and eyebrow down but never single one of his people ask he question for they be all too busy with their own affair
yes say mowgli to himself though in his heart he know that he have no reason let the red dhole come from the dekkan or the red flower dance among the bamboo and all the jungle run whine to mowgli call he great elephant name
but now because eye of the spring be red and mor forsooth must show his naked leg in some spring dance the jungle go mad as tabaqui by the bull that buy be the master of the jungle or be not be silent what do ye here couple of young wolf of the pack be canter down path look for open ground in which to fight you will remember that the law of the jungle forbid fight where the pack can see their neck bristle be as stiff as wire
and they bay furiously crouch for the first grapple mowgli leap forward catch one outstretched throat in either hand expect to fling the creature backward as he have often do in game or pack hunt but he have never before interfere with spring fight the two leap forward and dash he aside and without word to waste roll over and over close lock mowgli be on his foot almost before he fall his knife and his white tooth be bar and at that minute he would have kill both for no reason but that they be fight when he wish they to be quiet although every wolf have full right under the law to fight he dance round they with low shoulder and quivering hand ready to send in double blow when the first flurry of the scuffle should be over but while he wait the strength seem to ebb from his body the knife point lower and he sheathe the knife and watch have surely eat poison he sigh at last since break up the council with the red flower since kill shere khan none of the pack could fling aside and these be only tail wolf in the pack little hunter my strength be go from and presently shall die
oh mowgli why dost thou not kill they both the fight go on till one wolf run away and mowgli be leave alone on the torn and bloody ground look now at his knife and now at his leg and arm while the feeling of unhappiness he have never know before cover he as water cover log he kill early that evening and eat but little so as to be in good fettle for his spring run and he eat alone because all the jungle people be away sing or fight it be perfect white night as they call it all green thing seem to have make month ’s growth since the morning the branch that be yellow leaved the day before drip sap when mowgli break it the moss curl deep and warm over his foot the young grass have no cut edge and all the voice of the jungle boom like one deep harp string touch by the moon the moon of new talk who splash her light full on rock and pool slip it between trunk and creeper and sift it through million leaf forget his unhappiness mowgli sing aloud with pure delight as he settle into his stride it be more like fly than anything else for he have choose the long downward slope that lead to the northern marsh through the heart of the main jungle where the springy ground deaden the fall of his foot man teach man would have pick his way with many stumble through the cheating moonlight
but mowgli ’s muscle train by year of experience bear he up as though he be feather when rotten log or hide stone turn under his foot he save himself never check his pace without effort and without thought when he tire of ground go he throw up his hand monkey fashion to the near creeper and seem to float rather than to climb up into the thin branch whence he would follow tree road till his mood change and he shoot downward in long leafy curve to the level again there be still hot hollow surround by wet rock where he could hardly breathe for the heavy scent of the night flower and the bloom along the creeper bud dark avenue where the moonlight lie in belt as regular as checkered marble in church aisle thicket where the wet young growth stand breast high about he and throw its arm round his waist and hilltop crown with broken rock where he leap from stone to stone above the lair of the frightened little fox he would hear very faint and far off the chug drug of boar sharpen his tusk on bole and would come across the great gray brute all alone scribe and rend the bark of tall tree his mouth drip with foam and his eye blaze like fire or he would turn aside to the sound of clash horn and hiss grunt and dash past couple of furious sambhur stagger to and fro with low head strip with blood that show black in the moonlight or at some rush ford he would hear jacala the crocodile bellow like bull or disturb twin knot of the poison people but before they could strike he would be away and across the glisten shingle and deep in the jungle again so he run sometimes shout sometimes sing to himself the happy thing in all the jungle that night till the smell of the flower warn he that he be near the marsh and those lie far beyond his farth hunt ground here again man train man would have sink overhead in three stride but mowgli ’s foot have eye in they and they pass he from tussock to tussock and clump to quake clump without ask help from the eye in his head he run out to the middle of the swamp disturb the duck as he run and sit down on moss coat tree trunk lap in the black water the marsh be awake all round he for in the spring the bird people sleep very lightly and company of they be come or go the night through
but no one take any notice of mowgli sit among the tall reed hum song without word and look at the sol of his hard brown foot in case of neglect thorn all his unhappiness seem to have be leave behind in his own jungle and he be just begin full throat song when it come back again ten time bad than before this time
mowgli be frighten it be here also he say half aloud it have follow and he look over his shoulder to see whether the it be not stand behind he there be no one here the night noise of the marsh go on but never bird or beast speak to he and the new feeling of misery grow have surely eat poison he say in an awe stricken voice it must be that carelessly have eat poison and my strength be go from be afraid and yet it be not that be afraid mowgli be afraid when the two wolf fight akela or even phao would have silence they yet mowgli be afraid that be true sign have eat poison but what do they care in the jungle they sing and howl and fight and run in company under the moon and hai mai!--i be die in the marsh of that poison which have eat he be so sorry for himself that he nearly weep and after he go on they will find lie in the black water nay will go back to my own jungle and will die upon the council rock and bagheera whom love if he be not scream in the valley bagheera perhaps may watch by what be leave for little lest chil use as he use akela large warm tear splash down on his knee and miserable as he be mowgli feel happy that he be so miserable if you can understand that upside down sort of happiness as chil the kite use akela he repeat on the night save the pack from red dog he be quiet for little thinking of the last word of the lone wolf which you of course remember now akela say to many foolish thing before he die for when we die our stomach change he say none the less am of the jungle in his excitement as he remember the fight on waingunga bank he shout the last word aloud and wild buffalo cow among the reed spring to her knee snort man uhh say mysa the wild buffalo mowgli could hear he turn in his wallow that be no man it be only the hairless wolf of the seeonee pack on such night run he to and fro uhh say the cow drop her head again to graze think it be man say no
oh mowgli be it danger low mysa
oh mowgli be it danger the boy call back mockingly that be all mysa think for be it danger but for mowgli who go to and fro in the jungle by night watch what do ye care how loud he cry say the cow thus do they cry mysa answer contemptuously who having tear up the grass know not how to eat it for less than this mowgli groan to himself for less than this
even last rain have prick mysa out of his wallow and ride he through the swamp on rush halter he stretch hand to break one of the feathery reed but draw it back with sigh mysa go on steadily chew the cud and the long grass rip where the cow graze will not die here he say angrily mysa who be of one blood with jacala and the pig would see let we go beyond the swamp and see what come never have run such spring run hot and cold together up mowgli he could not resist the temptation of steal across the reed to mysa and prick he with the point of his knife the great dripping bull break out of his wallow like shell explode while mowgli laugh till he sit down say now that the hairless wolf of the seeonee pack once herd thee mysa he call wolf thou the bull snort stamp in the mud all the jungle know thou wast herder of tame cattle such man ’s brat as shout in the dust by the crop yonder thou of the jungle what hunter would have crawl like snake among the leech and for muddy jest jackal ’s jest have sham before my cow come to firm ground and will will mysa froth at the mouth for mysa have nearly the bad temper of any one in the jungle mowgli watch he puff and blow with eye that never change when he could make himself hear through the pattering mud he say what man pack lair here by the marsh mysa this be new jungle to go north then roar the angry bull for mowgli have prick he rather sharply it be naked cow herd ’s jest go and tell they at the village at the foot of the marsh the man pack do not love jungle tale nor do think mysa that scratch more or less on thy hide be any matter for council but will go and look at this village yes will go softly now it be not every night that the master of the jungle come to herd thee he step out to the shiver ground on the edge of the marsh
well know that mysa would never charge over it and laugh as he run to think of the bull ’s anger my strength be not altogether go he say it may be that the poison be not to the bone there be star sit low yonder he look at it between his half shut hand by the bull that buy it be the red flower the red flower that lie beside before before come even to the first seeonee pack now that have see will finish the run the marsh end in broad plain where light twinkle it be long time since mowgli have concern himself with the doing of man but this night the glimmer of the red flower draw he forward will look say he as do in the old day and will see how far the man pack have change forget that he be no long in his own jungle where he could do what he please he tread carelessly through the dew load grass till he come to the hut where the light stand three or four yelp dog give tongue for he be on the outskirt of village ho
say mowgli sit down noiselessly after send back deep wolf growl that silence the cur what come will come mowgli what hast thou to do any more with the lair of the man pack he rub his mouth remember where stone have strike it year ago when the other man pack have cast he out the door of the hut open and woman stand peer out into the darkness child cry and the woman say over her shoulder sleep it be but jackal that wake the dog in little time morning come mowgli in the grass begin to shake as though he have fever he know that voice well but to make sure he cry softly surprised to find how man ’s talk come back messua who call say the woman quiver in her voice hast thou forget say mowgli his throat be dry as he speak if it be thou what name do give thee she have half shut the door and her hand be clutch at her breast nathoo ohe nathoo say mowgli for as you remember that be the name messua give he when he first come to the man pack come my son she call and mowgli step into the light and look full at messua the woman who have be good to he and whose life he have save from the man pack so long before she be old and her hair be gray but her eye and her voice have not change woman like she expect to find mowgli where she have leave he and her eye travel upward in puzzled way from his chest to his head that touch the top of the door my son she stammer and then sink to his foot
but it be no long my son it be godle of the wood as he stand in the red light of the oil lamp strong tall and beautiful his long black hair sweep over his shoulder the knife swing at his neck and his head crown with wreath of white jasmine he might easily have be mistake for some wild god of jungle legend the child half asleep on cot spring up and shriek aloud with terror messua turn to soothe he while mowgli stand still look in at the water jar and the cooking pot the grain bin and all the other human belonging that he find himself remember so well what wilt thou eat or drink messua murmur this be all thine we owe our life to thee but art thou he call nathoo or godling indeed be nathoo
say mowgli be very far from my own place see this light and come hither do not know thou wast here after we come to khanhiwara messua say timidly the english would have help we against those villager that seek to burn we rememberest thou indeed have not forget but when the english law be make ready we go to the village of those evil people and it be no more to be find that also remember say mowgli with quiver of his nostril my man therefore take service in the field and at last for indeed he be strong man we hold little land here it be not so rich as the old village
but we do not need much we two where be he the man that dig in the dirt when he be afraid on that night he be dead year and he mowgli point to the child my son that be bear two rain ago if thou art godling give he the favour of the jungle that he may be safe among thy thy people as we be safe on that night she lift up the child who forget his fright reach out to play with the knife that hang on mowgli ’s chest and mowgli put the little finger aside very carefully and if thou art nathoo whom the tiger carry away messua go on choke he be then thy young brother give he an eld brother ’s blessing hai mai what do know of the thing call blessing be neither godle nor his brother and mother mother my heart be heavy in he shiver as he set down the child like enough say messua bustle among the cooking pot this come of run about the marsh by night beyond question the fever have soak thee to the marrow mowgli smile little at the idea of anything in the jungle hurt he will make fire and thou shalt drink warm milk put away the jasmine wreath the smell be heavy in so small place mowgli sit down mutter with his face in his hand all manner of strange feeling that he have never feel before be run over he exactly as though he have be poison and he feel dizzy and little sick he drink the warm milk in long gulp messua pat he on the shoulder from time to time not quite sure whether he be her son nathoo of the long ago day or some wonderful jungle be but glad to feel that he be at least flesh and blood son she say at last,--her eye be full of pride,--“have any tell thee that thou art beautiful beyond all man
hah say mowgli for naturally he have never hear anything of the kind messua laugh softly and happily the look in his face be enough for she be the first then it be right though it come seldom that mother should tell her son these good thing thou art very beautiful never have look upon such man mowgli twist his head and try to see over his own hard shoulder and messua laugh again so long that mowgli not know why be force to laugh with she and the child run from one to the other laugh too nay thou must not mock thy brother say messua catch he to her breast when thou art one half as fair we will marry thee to the young daughter of king and thou shalt ride
great elephant mowgli could not understand one word in three of the talk here the warm milk be take effect on he after his long run so he curl up and in minute be deep asleep and messua put the hair back from his eye throw cloth over he and be happy jungle fashion he sleep out the rest of that night and all the next day for his instinct which never wholly sleep warn he there be nothing to fear he wake at last with bind that shake the hut for the cloth over his face make he dream of trap and there he stand his hand on his knife the sleep all heavy in his roll eye ready for any fight messua laugh and set the evening meal before he there be only few coarse cake bake over the smoky fire some rice and lump of sour preserve tamarind just enough to go on with till he could get to his evening kill the smell of the dew in the marsh make he hungry and restless he want to finish his spring run but the child insist on sit in his arm and messua would have it that his long blue black hair must be comb out
so she sing as she comb foolish little baby song now call mowgli her son and now beg he to give some of his jungle power to the child the hut door be close but mowgli hear sound
he know well and see messua ’s jaw drop with horror as great gray paw come under the bottom of the door and gray brother outside whine muffled and penitent whine of anxiety and fear out and wait ye would not come when call say mowgli in jungle talk without turn his head and the great gray paw disappear do not do not bring thy thy servant with thee say messua we have always live at peace with the jungle it be peace
say mowgli rise think of that night on the road to khanhiwara there be score of such folk before thee and behind thee but see that even in springtime the jungle people do not always forget mother go messua draw aside humbly he be indeed wood god
she think but as his hand be on the door the mother in she make she throw her arm round mowgli ’s neck again and again come back she whisper son or no son come back for love thee
look he too grieve the child be cry because the man with the shiny knife be go away come back again messua repeat by night or by day this door be never shut to thee mowgli ’s throat work as though the cord in it be be pull and his voice seem to be drag from it as he answer will surely come back
and now he say as he put by the head of the fawning wolf on the threshold have little cry against thee gray brother why come ye not all four when call so long ago so long ago it be but last night we be sing in the jungle the new song for this be the time of new talk rememberest thou truly truly and as soon as the song be sing gray brother go on earnestly follow thy trail run from all the other and follow hot foot but little brother what hast thou do eat and sleep with the man pack if ye have come when call this have never be say mowgli run much fast and now what be to be say gray brother mowgli be go to answer when girl in white cloth come down some path that lead from the outskirt of the village gray brother drop out of sight at once and mowgli back noiselessly into field of high spring crop he could almost have touch she with his hand when the warm green stalk close before his face and he disappear like ghost the girl scream for she think she have see spirit
and then she give deep sigh mowgli part the stalk with his hand and watch she till she be out of sight and now do not know he say sigh in his turn why do ye not come when call we follow thee we follow thee gray brother mumble lick at mowgli ’s heel we follow thee always except in the time of the new talk and would ye follow to the man pack mowgli whisper do not follow thee on the night our old pack cast thee out who wake thee lie among the crop
ay but again have not follow thee to night
ay but again and again and it may be again gray brother gray brother be silent when he speak he growl to himself the black one speak truth and he say man go to man at the last raksha our mother said---- so also say akela on the night of red dog mowgli mutter so also say kaa who be wise than we all what dost thou say gray brother they cast thee out once with bad talk they cut thy mouth with stone they send buldeo to slay thee they would have throw thee into the red flower thou and not hast say that they be evil and senseless thou and not follow my own people didst let in the jungle upon they thou and not didst make song against they more bitter even than our song against red dog ask thee what thou say they be talk as they run gray brother canter on while without reply and then he said,--between bind and bind as it were,--“man cub master of the jungle son of raksha lair brother to though forget for little while in the spring thy trail be my trail thy lair be my lair thy kill be my kill and thy death fight be my death fight speak for the three but what wilt thou say to the jungle that be well think between the sight and the kill it be not good to wait go before and cry they all to the council rock and will tell they what be in my stomach
but they may not come in the time of new talk they may forget hast thou then forget nothing snap gray brother over his shoulder as he lie himself down to gallop and mowgli follow think at any other season the news would have call all the jungle together with bristle neck
but now they be busy hunting and fighting and killing and sing from one to another gray brother run cry the master of the jungle go back to man come to the council rock and the happy eager people only answer he will return in the summer heat the rain will drive he to lair run and sing with we gray brother but the master of the jungle go back to man gray brother would repeat eee yoawa be the time of new talk any less sweet for that they would reply so when mowgli heavy hearted come up through the well remember rock to the place where he have be bre into the council he find only the four baloo who be nearly blind with age and the heavy cold blooded kaa coil around akela ’s empty seat thy trail end here then manle say kaa as mowgli throw himself down his face in his hand cry thy cry we be of one blood thou and man and snake together why do not die under red dog the boy moan my strength be go from and it be not any poison by night and by day hear double step upon my trail when turn my head it be as though one have hide himself from that instant go to look behind the tree and he be not there call
and none cry again but it be as though one listen and keep back the answer lie down but do not rest run the spring run but be not make still bathe but be not make cool the kill sicken but have no heart to fight except kill the red flower be in my body my bone be water and know not what know what need of talk
say baloo slowly turn his head to where mowgli lie akela by the river say it that mowgli should drive mowgli back to the man pack say it but who listen now to baloo bagheera where be bagheera this night?--he know also it be the law when we meet at cold lair manle know it say kaa turn little in his mighty coil man go to man at the last though the jungle do not cast he out the four look at one another and at mowgli puzzle but obedient the jungle do not cast out then mowgli stammer gray brother and the three growl furiously begin so long as we live none shall dare----
but baloo check they teach thee the law it be for to speak he say and though can not now see the rock before see far little frog take thine own trail make thy lair with thine own blood and pack and people but when there be need of foot or tooth or eye or word carry swiftly by night remember master of the jungle the jungle be thine at call the middle jungle be thine also say kaa speak for no small people hai mai my brother cry mowgli throw up his arm with sob know not what know would not go but be draw by both foot how shall leave these night nay look up little brother baloo repeat there be no shame in this hunting when the honey be eat we leave the empty hive having cast the skin say kaa we may not creep into it afresh it be the law listen dear of all to say baloo there be neither word nor will here to hold thee back look up who may question the master of the jungle see thee play among the white pebble yonder when thou wast little frog and bagheera that buy thee for the price of young bull newly kill see thee also of that look over we two only remain for raksha thy lair mother be dead with thy lair father the old wolf pack be long since dead thou knowest whither shere khan go and akela die among the dhole where but for thy wisdom and strength the second seeonee pack would also have die there remain nothing but old bone it be no long the man cub that ask leave of his pack but the master of the jungle that change his trail who shall question man in his way but bagheera and the bull that buy say mowgli would not---- his word be cut short by roar and crash in the thicket below and bagheera light strong and terrible as always stand before he therefore he say stretch out drip right paw do not come it be long hunt
but he lie dead in the bush now bull in his second year the bull that free thee little brother all debt be pay now for the rest my word be baloo ’s word he lick mowgli ’s foot remember bagheera love thee he cry and bind away at the foot of the hill he cry again long and loud good hunting on new trail master of the jungle remember bagheera love thee thou hast hear say baloo there be no more go now but first come to wise little frog come to it be hard to cast the skin say kaa as mowgli sob and sob with his head on the blind bear ’s side and his arm round his neck while baloo try feebly to lick his foot the star be thin say gray brother snuff at the dawn wind where shall we lair to day for from now we follow new trail and this be the last of the mowgli story the outsong this be the song that mowgli hear behind he in the jungle till he come to messua ’s door again baloo for the sake of he who show one wise frog the jungle road keep the law the man pack make-- for thy blind old baloo ’s sake clean or taint hot or stale hold it as it be the trail through the day and through the night quest neither leave nor right for the sake of he who love thee beyond all else that move when thy pack would make thee pain say tabaqui sing again when thy pack would work thee ill say shere khan be yet to kill when the knife be draw to slay keep the law and go thy way root and honey palm and spathe guard cub from harm and scathe wood and water wind and tree jungle favour go with thee kaa anger be the egg of fear-- only lidless eye be clear cobra poison none may leech even so with cobra speech open talk shall call to thee strength whose mate be courtesy send no lunge beyond thy length lend no rotten bough thy strength gauge thy gape with buck or goat lest thine eye should choke thy throat after gorge wouldst thou sleep look thy den be hide and deep lest wrong by thee forget draw thy killer to the spot east and west and north and south wash thy hide and close thy mouth pit and rift and he sit in defiance of municipal order astride the gun zam zammah on her brick platform opposite the old ajaib gher the wonder house as the native call the lahore museum who hold zam zammah that fire breathing dragon hold the punjab for the great green bronze piece be always first of the conqueror ’s loot there be some justification for kim he have kick lala dinanath ’s boy off the trunnion since the english hold the punjab and kim be english though he be burn black as any native though he speak the vernacular by preference and his mother tongue in clip uncertain sing song though he consort on term of perfect equality with the small boy of the bazar kim be white poor white of the very poor the half caste woman who look after he she smoke opium and pretend to keep second hand furniture shop by the square where the cheap cab wait tell the missionary that she be kim ’s mother ’s sister but his mother have be nursemaid in colonel ’s family and have marry kimball o’hara young colour sergeant of the maverick an irish regiment he afterwards take post on the sind punjab and delhi railway and his regiment go home without he the wife die of cholera in ferozepore and o’hara fall to drink and loaf up and down the line with the keen eyed three year old baby society and chaplain anxious for the child try to catch he but o’hara drift away till he come across the woman who take opium and learn the taste from she and die as poor white die in india his estate at death consist of three paper one he call his ne varietur because those word be write below his signature thereon and another his clearance certificate the third be kim ’s birth certificate those thing he be use to say in his glorious opium hour would yet make little kimball man on no account be kim to part with they for they belong to great piece of magic such magic as man practise over yonder behind the museum in the big blue and white jadoo gher the magic house as we name the masonic lodge
it would he say all come right some day and kim ’s horn would be exalt between pillar monstrous pillar of beauty and strength the colonel himself rid on horse at the head of the fine regiment in the world would attend to kim little kim that should have be well off than his father nine hundred first class devil whose god be red bull on green field would attend to kim if they have not forget o’hara poor o’hara that be gang foreman on the ferozepore line then he would weep bitterly in the broken rush chair on the veranda so it come about after his death that the woman sew parchment paper and birth certificate into leather amulet case which she string round kim ’s neck and some day she say confusedly remember o’hara ’s prophecy there will come for you great red bull on green field and the colonel rid on his tall horse yes and drop into english—“nine hundred devil ah say kim shall remember red bull and colonel on horse will come but first my father say will come the two man make ready the ground for these matter that be how my father say they always do and it be always so when man work magic if the woman have send kim up to the local jadoo gher with those paper he would of course have be take over by the provincial lodge and send to the masonic orphanage in the hill but what she have hear of magic she distrust kim too hold view of his own as he reach the year of indiscretion he learn to avoid missionary and white man of serious aspect who ask who he be and what he do for kim do nothing with an immense success true he know the wonderful walled city of lahore from the delhi gate to the outer fort ditch be hand in glove with man who lead life strange than anything haroun al raschid dream of and he live in life wild as that of the arabian night but missionary and secretary of charitable society could not see the beauty of it his nickname through the ward be little friend of all the world and very often be lithe and inconspicuous he execute commission by night on the crowded housetop for sleek and shiny young man of fashion it be intrigue,—of course
he know that much as he have know all evil since he could speak,—but what he love be the game for its own sake the stealthy prowl through the dark gully and lane the crawl up waterpipe the sight and sound of the woman ’s world on the flat roof and the headlong flight from housetop to housetop under cover of the hot dark then there be holy man ash smear faquir by their brick shrine under the tree at the riverside with whom he be quite familiar greet they as they return from beg tour and when no one be by eat from the same dish the woman who look after he insist with tear that he should wear european clothe trouser shirt and battered hat kim find it easy to slip into hindu or mohammedan garb when engage on certain business one of the young man of fashion he who be find dead at the bottom of well on the night of the earthquake have once give he complete suit of hindu kit the costume of lowcaste street boy and kim store it in secret place under some baulk in nila ram ’s timber yard beyond the punjab high court where the fragrant deodar log lie seasoning after they have drive down the ravi when there be business or frolic afoot kim would use his property return at dawn to the veranda all tired out from shout at the heel of marriage procession or yell at hindu festival sometimes there be food in the house more often there be not
and then kim go out again to eat with his native friend as he drum his heel against zam zammah he turn now and again from his king of the castle game with little chota lal and abdullah the sweetmeat seller ’s son to make rude remark to the native policeman on guard over row of shoe at the museum door the big punjabi grin tolerantly he know kim of old so do the water carrier sluice water on the dry road from his goat skin bag so do jawahir singh the museum carpenter bent over new packing case so do everybody in sight except the peasant from the country hurry up to the wonder house to view the thing that man make in their own province and elsewhere the museum be give up to indian art and manufacture and anybody who seek wisdom could ask the curator to explain off let up cry abdullah climb up zam zammah ’s wheel thy father be pastry cook thy mother steal the ghi sing kim all mussalman fall off zam zammah
long ago let up shrill little chota lal in his gilt embroider cap his father be worth perhaps half million sterling
but india be the only democratic land in the world the hindus fall off zam zammah too the mussalman push they off thy father be pastry cook he stop for there shuffle round the corner from the roar motee bazar such man as kim who think he know all caste have never see he be nearly six foot high dress in fold upon fold of dingy stuff like horse blanketing and not one fold of it could kim refer to any know trade or profession at his belt hang long open work iron pencase and wooden rosary such as holy man wear on his head be gigantic sort of tam o’-shanter his face be yellow and wrinkle like that of fook shing the chinese bootmaker in the bazar his eye turn up at the corner and look like little slit of onyx who be that say kim to his companion perhaps it be man say abdullah finger in mouth star without doubt return kim
but he be no man of india have ever see priest perhaps say chota lal spy the rosary he go into the wonder house nay nay say the policeman shake his head do not understand your talk the constable speak punjabi friend of all the world what do he say send he hither say kim drop from zam zammah flourish his bare heel he be foreigner and thou art buffalo the man turn helplessly and drift towards the boy he be old and his woollen gaberdine still reek of the stinking artemisia of the mountain pass child what be that big house he say in very fair urdu the ajaib gher the wonder house kim give he no title such as lala or mian he could not divine the man ’s creed the wonder house can any enter it be write above the door
all can enter without payment go in and out be no banker laugh kim be an old man do not know then finger his rosary he half turn to the museum what be your caste where be your house have you come far kim ask come by kulu from beyond the kaila but what know you from the hill where”—he sighed—“the air and water be fresh and cool
aha khitai chinaman say abdullah proudly fook shing have once chase he out of his shop for spit at the joss above the boot pahari hillman say little chota lal aye child hillman from hills thou’lt never see didst hear of bhotiyal tibet be no khitai but bhotiya tibetan since you must know lama or say guru in your tongue guru from tibet say kim have not see such man they be hindus in tibet then we be follower of the middle way live in peace in our lamasserie and go to see the four holy place before die now do you who be child know as much as do who be old he smile benignantly on the boy hast thou eaten he fumble in his bosom and draw forth wear wooden begging bowl the boy nod all priest of their acquaintance beg do not wish to eat yet he turn his head like an old tortoise in the sunlight be it true that there be many image in the wonder house of lahore he repeat the last word as one make sure of an address that be true say abdullah it be full of heathen būts thou also art an idolater never mind he say that be the government ’s house and there be no idolatry in it but only sahib with white beard come with and will show strange priest eat boy whisper chota lal and he be strange and būt parast idolater say abdullah

there be any translation of their record he draw in his breath as he turn helplessly over the page of beal and stanislas julien tis all here treasure lock then he compose himself reverently to listen and where it touch earth there break out stream which presently become river whose nature by our lord ’s beneficence and that merit he acquire ere he free himself be that whoso bath in it wash away all taint and speckle of sin

but there be no place to sleep the old man be use to the order of his monastery and though he sleep on the ground as the rule decree prefer decency in these thing we shall get good lodging at the kashmir serai say kim laugh at his perplexity have friend there the hot and crowded bazar blaze with light as they make their way through the press of all the race in upper india and the lama moon through it like man in dream it be his first experience of large manufacture city and the crowded tram car with its continually squeal brake frighten he half push half tow he arrive at the high gate of the kashmir serai that huge open square over against the railway station surround with arched cloister where the camel and horse caravan put up on their return from central asia here be all manner of northern folk tend tethered pony and kneel camel load and unload bale and bundle
draw water for the evening meal at the creak well windlass pile grass before the shriek wild eyed stallion cuff the surly caravan dog pay off camel driver take on new groom swear shout argue and chaffering in the pack square the cloister reach by three or four masonry step make haven of refuge around this turbulent sea most of they be rent to trader as we rent the arch of viaduct the space between pillar and pillar be bricke or board off into room which be guard by heavy wooden door and cumbrous native padlock lock door show that the owner be away and few rude sometimes very rude chalk or paint scratch tell where he have go thus lutuf ullah be go to kurdistan below in coarse verse allah who sufferest lice to live on the coat of kabuli why hast thou allow this louse lutuf to live so long kim fend the lama between excited man and excited beast sidle along the cloister to the far end near therailway station where mahbub ali the horse trader live when he come in from that mysterious land beyond the pass of the north kim have have many deal with mahbub in his little life especially between his tenth and his thirteenth year and the big burly afghan his beard dye scarlet with lime for he be elderly and do not wish his grey hair to show know the boy ’s value as gossip sometimes he would tell kim to watch man who have nothing whatever to do with horse to follow he for one whole day and report every soul with whom he talk kim would deliver himself of his tale at evening and mahbub would listen without word or gesture it be intrigue of some kind
kim know but its worth lie in say nothing whatever to anyone except mahbub who give he beautiful meal all hot from the cookshop at the head of the serai and once as much as eight anna in money he be here say kim hit bad temper camel on the nose ohé mahbub ali he halt at dark arch and slip behind the bewilder lama the horse trader his deep embroider bokhariot belt unloose be lie on pair of silk carpet saddle bag pull lazily at an immense silver hookah he turn his head very slightly at the cry and see only the tall silent figure chuckle in his deep chest red lama it be far from lahore to the pass what dost thou

it be against the rule to sit on bench say the lama moreover it cramp say begin the money lender purse his lip that there be not one rule of right living which these te rain do not cause we to break we sit for example side by side with all caste and people yea and with most outrageously shameless one say the wife scowl at the amritzar girl make eye at the young sepoy say we might have go by cart along the road say the husband and thus have save some money yes and spend twice over what we save on food by the way that be talk out ten thousand time ay by ten thousand tongue grunt he the god help we poor woman if we may not speak he be of that sort which may not look at or reply to woman for the lama constrain by his rule take not the faint notice of she and his disciple be like he nay mother say kim most promptly not when the woman be well look and above all charitable to the hungry beggar ’s answer say the sikh laugh thou hast bring it on thyself sister kim ’s hand be crooked in supplication and whither goest
thou say the woman hand he the half of cake from greasy package even to benare juggler belike the young soldier suggest have ye any trick to pass the time why do not that yellow man answer because say kim stoutly he be holy and think upon matter hide from thee that may be well we of the ludhiana sikhs”—he roll it out sonorously—“do not trouble our head with doctrine we fight my sister ’s brother ’s son be naik corporal in that regiment say the sikh craftsman quietly there be also some dogra company there the soldier glare for dogra be of other caste than sikh and the banker titter they be all one to say the amritzar girl that we believe snort the cultivator ’s wife malignantly nay
but all who serve the sirkar with weapon in their hand be as it be one brotherhood there be one brotherhood of the caste but beyond that again”—she look round timidly—“the bond of the pulton_—the regiment eh my brother be in jat regiment say the cultivator dogra be good man thy sikhs at least be of that opinion say the soldier with scowl at the placid old man in the corner thy sikhs think so when our two company come to help they at the pirzai kotal in the face of eight afridi standard on the ridge not three month go he tell the story of border action in which the dogra company of the ludhiana sikhs have acquit themselves well the amritzar girl smile for she know the talk be to win her approval alas say the cultivator ’s wife at the end so their village be burn and their little child make homeless they have mark our dead they pay great payment after we of the sikhs have school they so it be be this amritzar ay and here they cut our ticket say the banker fumble at his belt the lamp be pal in the dawn when the half caste guard come round ticket collecting be slow business in the east where people secrete their ticket in all sort of curious place kim produce his and be tell to get out but go to umballa he protest go with this holy man thou canst go to jehannum for aught care this ticket be only kim burst into flood of tear protest that the lama be his father and his mother that he be the prop of the lama ’s decline year and that the lama would die without his care all the carriage bid the guard be merciful the banker be specially eloquent here but the guard haul kim on to the platform the lama blink
