and he be mad the smooth shaven priest reply he turn to the lama three kos six mile to the westward run the great road to calcutta but would go to benare to benare and to benare also it cross all stream on this side of hind now my word to thee holy one be rest here till tomorrow then take the road it be the grand trunk road he mean and test each stream that it overpass for as understand the virtue of thy river lie neither in one pool nor place but throughout its length then if thy god will be assure that thou wilt come upon thy freedom that be well say the lama be much impressed by the plan we will begin tomorrow and blessing on thee for show old foot such near road deep sing song chinese half chant close the sentence even the priest be impressed and the headman fear an evil spell but none could look at the lama ’s simple eager face and doubt he long seest thou my chela he say dive into his snuff gourd with an important sniff it be his duty to repay courtesy with courtesy see and hear the headman roll his eye where kim be chat to girl in blue as she lie crackle thorn on fire he also have search of his own no river but bull yea red bull on green field will some day raise he to honour he be think not altogether of this world he be send of sudden to aid in this search and his name be friend of all the world the priest smile ho
there friend of all the world he cry across the sharp smell smoke what art thou this holy one ’s disciple say kim he say thou be būt spirit can būts eat say kim with twinkle for be hungry it be no jest cry the lama certain astrologer of that city whose name have forget that be no more than the city of umballa where we sleep last night kim whisper to the priest ay umballa be it he cast horoscope and declare that my chela should find his desire within two day but what say he of the meaning of the star friend of all the world kim clear his throat and look around at the village greybeard the meaning of my star be war he reply pompously somebody laugh at the little tattered figure strut on the brickwork plinth under the great tree where native would have lie down kim ’s white blood set he upon his foot ay war he answer that be sure prophecy rumble deep voice for there be always war along the border as know it be an old withered man who have serve the government in the day of the mutiny as native officer in newly raise cavalry regiment the government have give he good holding in the village and though the demand of his son now grey bearded officer on their own account have impoverish he he be still person of consequence english official deputy commissioner even turn aside from the main road to visit he and on those occasion he dress himself in the uniform of ancient day and stand up like ramrod but this shall be great war war of eight thousand kim ’s voice shrill across the quick gather crowd astonish himself redcoat or our own regiment the old man snap as though he be ask an equal his tone make man respect kim redcoats say kim at venture redcoat and gun
but the astrologer say no word of this cry the lama snuff prodigiously in his excitement but know the word have come to who be this holy

but now he be play for large thing the sheer excitement and the sense of power he draw new breath and go on old man give sign do underling order the going of eight thousand redcoat with gun still the old man answer as though kim be an equal dost thou know who he be then that give the order have see he to know again have know he since he be lieutenant in the top khana the artillery tall man tall man with black hair walk thus
kim take few pace in stiff wooden style but that anyone may have see the crowd be breathless still through all this talk that be true say kim but will say more look now first the great man walk thus then he think thus kim draw forefinger over his forehead and downwards till it come to rest by the angle of the jaw anon he twitch his finger thus anon he thrust his hat under his left armpit kim illustrate the motion and stand like stork the old man groan inarticulate with amazement and the crowd shiver

but what do he when he be about to give an order he rub the skin at the back of his neck thus then fall one finger on the table and he make small sniff noise through his nose then he speak say loose such and such regiment call out such gun the old man rise stiffly and salute for’”—kim translate into the vernacular the clinching sentence he have hear in the dressing room at umballa—“‘for say he we should have do this long ago it be not war it be chastisement snff enough have see he thus in the smoke of battle see and hear it be he see no smoke”—kim ’s voice shift to the rapt sing song of the wayside fortune teller see this in darkness first come man to make thing clear then come horseman then come he stand in ring of light the rest follow as have say old man have speak truth it be he past all doubt it be he the crowd draw long quaver breath star alternately at the old man still at attention and rag kim against the purple twilight say not say not he be from the other world cry the lama proudly he be the friend of all the world he be the friend of the star at least it do not concern we man cry thou young soothsayer if the gift abide with thee at all season have red spot cow she may be sister to thy bull for aught know

but the woman begin but thou and thy husband hop to get the cow cure for handful of thank the shot tell they be notoriously the close fiste couple in the village it be not well to cheat the temple give young calf to thine own priest and unless thy god be angry past recall she will give milk within month master beggar art thou purr the priest approvingly not the cunning of forty year could have do well surely thou hast make the old man rich little flour little butter and mouthful of cardamom kim retort flush with the praise but still cautious—“doe one grow rich on that and as thou canst see he be mad but it serve while learn the road at least he know what the faquir of the taksali gate be like when they talk among themselves and copy the very inflection of their lewd disciple be his search then truth or cloak to other end it may be treasure he be mad many time mad there be nothing else here the old soldier bobble up and ask if kim would accept his hospitality for the night the priest recommend he to do so but insist that the honour of entertain the lama belong to the temple at which the lama smile guilelessly kim glance from one face to the other and draw his own conclusion where be the money he whisper beckon the old man off into the darkness in my bosom where else give it quietly and swiftly give it but why here be no ticket to buy be thy chela or be not do not safeguard thy old foot about the way give the money and at dawn will return it he slip his hand above the lama ’s girdle and bring away the purse be it
so be it so the old man nod his head this be great and terrible world never know there be so many man alive in it next morning the priest be in very bad temper
but the lama be quite happy and kim have enjoy most interesting evening with the old man who bring out his cavalry sabre and balance it on his dry knee tell tale of the mutiny and young captain thirty year in their graf till kim drop off to sleep certainly the air of this country be good say the lama sleep lightly as do all old man but last night sleep unwaking till broad day even now be heavy drink draught of hot milk say kim who have carry not few such remedy to opium smoker of his acquaintance it be time to take the road again the long road that overpasse all the river of hind say the lama gaily let we go but how thinkest thou chela to recompense these people and especially the priest for their great kindness truly they be būt parast
but in other life maybe they will receive enlightenment rupee to the temple the thing within be no more than stone and red paint but the heart of man we must acknowledge when and where it be good holy one hast thou ever take the road alone kim look up sharply like the indian crow so busy about the field surely child from kulu to pathânkot from kulu where my first chela die when man be kind to we we make offering and all man be well dispose throughout all the hill it be otherwise in hind say kim drily their god be many armed and malignant let they alone would set thee on thy road for little friend of all the world thou and thy yellow man the old soldier amble up the village street all shadowy in the dawn on punt scissor hock pony last night break up the fountain of remembrance in my so dry heart and it be as blessing to truly there be war abroad in the air smell it have bring my sword he sit long legged on the little beast with the big sword at his side hand drop on the pommel star fiercely over the flat land towards the north tell again how he show in thy vision come up and sit behind the beast will carry two be this holy one ’s disciple say kim as they clear the village gate the villager seem almost sorry to be rid of they but the priest ’s farewell be cold and distant he have waste some opium on man who carry no money that be well speak be not much use to holy man but respect be always good there be no respect in these day not even when commissioner sahib come to see
but why should one whose star lead he to war follow holy man but he be holy man say kim earnestly in truth and in talk and in act holy he be not like the other have never see such an one we be not fortune teller or juggler or beggar thou art not that can see but do not know that other he march well though the first freshness of the day carry the lama forward with long easy camel like stride he be deep in meditation mechanically click his rosary they follow the rut and wear country road that wound across the flat between the great dark green mango grove the line of the snowcapped himalayas faint to the eastward all india be at work in the field to the creaking of well wheel the shouting of plowman behind their cattle and the clamour of the crow even the pony feel the good influence and almost break into trot as kim lie hand on the stirrup leather it repent that do not give rupee to the shrine say the lama on the last bead of his eighty one the old soldier growl in his beard so that the lama for the first time be aware of he seekest thou the river also say he turn the day be new be the reply what need of river save to water at before sundown come to show thee short lane to the big road that be courtesy to be remember man of good will but why the sword the old soldier look as abashed as child interrupt in his game of make believe the sword he say fumble it
oh that be fancy of mine an old man ’s fancy truly the police order be that no man must bear weapon throughout hind but”—he cheer up and slap the hilt—“all the constabeel hereabout
know it be not good fancy say the lama what profit to kill man very little as know but if evil man be not now and then slay it would not be good world for weaponless dreamer do not speak without knowledge who have see the land from delhi south awash with blood what madness be that then the god who send it for plague alone know madness eat into all the army and they turn against their officer that be the first evil but not past remedy if they have then hold their hand
but they choose to kill the sahib wife and child then come the sahib from over the sea and call they to most strict account some such rumour believe reach once long ago they call it the black year as remember what manner of life hast thou lead not to know the year rumour indeed all earth know and tremble our earth never shake but once upon the day that the excellent one receive enlightenment see delhi shake at least and delhi be the navel of the world so they turn against woman and child that be bad deed for which the punishment can not be avoid many strove to do so but with very small profit be then in regiment of cavalry it break of six hundred and eighty sabre stand fast to their salt how many think you of whom be one the great merit merit we do not consider it merit in those day my people my friend my brother fall from they say the time of the english be accomplish let each strike out little holding for himself but have talk with the man of sobraon of chilianwallah of moodkee and ferozeshah say abide little and the wind turn there be no blessing in this work in those day ride seventy mile with an english memsahib and her babe on my saddle bow that be horse fit for man place they in safety and back come to my officer the one that be not kill of our five give work say for be an outcast among my own kind and my cousin ’s blood be wet on my sabre be content say he there be great work forward when this madness be over there be recompense ay there be recompense when the madness be over surely the lama mutter half to himself they do not hang medal in those day on all who by accident have hear gun fire in nineteen pitch battle be in six and forty skirmish of horse and in small affair without number nine wound bear medal and four clasp and the medal of an order for my captain who be now general remember when the kaisar hind have accomplish fifty year of her reign and all the land rejoice they say give he the order of berittish india carry it upon my neck now have also my jaghir hold from the hand of the state free gift to and mine the man of the old day they be now commissioner come rid to through the crop high upon horse so that all the village see and oh afterwards they go away but not before my village have see and at the last what wilt thou do at the last shall die and after let the god order it have never pester they with prayer do not think they will pester look you have notice in my long life that those who eternally break in upon those above with complaint and report and bellowing and weeping be presently send for in haste as our colonel use to send for slack jawed down country man who talk too much no have never weary the god they will remember this and give quiet place where can drive my lance in the shade and wait to welcome my son have no less than three rissaldar major all in the regiment and they likewise bind upon the wheel go forth from life to life from despair to despair say the lama below his breath hot uneasy snatch ay the old soldier chuckle three rissaldar major in three regiment gambler little

wash from all taint of sin ascend the far bank one be assure of freedom do not know thy life but thy face be the face of the honourable and courteous thou hast cling to thy way render fidelity when it be hard to give in that black year of which now remember other tale enter now upon the middle way which be the path to freedom hear the most excellent law and do not follow dream speak then old man the soldier smile half salute we be all babbler at our age the lama squat under the shade of mango whose shadow play checkerwise over his face the soldier sit stiffly on the pony and kim make sure that there be no snake lie down in the crotch of the twisted root there be drowsy buzz of small life in hot sunshine coo of dove and sleepy drone of well wheel across the field slowly and impressively the lama begin at the end of ten minute the old soldier slide from his pony to hear well as he say and sit with the rein round his wrist the lama ’s voice falter the period lengthen kim be busy watch grey squirrel when the little scold bunch of fur close press to the branch disappear preacher and audience be fast asleep the old officer ’s strong cut head pillow on his arm the lama ’s throw back against the tree bole where it show like yellow ivory naked child toddle up star and move by some quick impulse of reverence make solemn little obeisance before the lama only the child be so short and fat that it topple over sideways and kim laugh at the sprawling chubby leg the child scar and indignant yell aloud hai hai say the soldier leap to his foot what be it what order it be child dream it be an alarm little one little one do not cry have sleep that be discourteous indeed be afraid roar the child what be it to fear two old man and boy how wilt thou ever make soldier princele the lama have wake too but take no direct notice of the child click his rosary what be that say the child stop yell midway have never see such thing give they

kim for the lama be markedly silent it be long since have ride this way but thy boy ’s talk stir see holy one the great road which be the backbone of all hind for the most part it be shaded as here with four line of tree the middle road all hard take the quick traffic in the day before rail carriage the sahib travel up and down here in hundred now there be only country cart and such like leave and right be the rough road for the heavy cart grain and cotton and timber fodder lime and hide man go in safety here for at every few kos be police station the police be thief and extortioner myself would patrol it with cavalry young recruit under strong captain but at least they do not suffer any rival all caste and kind of man move here brahmin and chumar banker and tinker barber and bunnia pilgrim and potter all the world go and come it be to as river from which be withdraw like log after flood and truly the grand trunk road be wonderful spectacle it run straight bear without crowd india ’s traffic for fifteen hundred mile such river of life as nowhere else exist in the world they look at the green arched shade fleck length of it the white breadth speckle with slow pacing folk and the two room police station opposite who bear arm against the law constable call out laughingly as he catch sight of the soldier ’s sword be not the police enough to destroy evil doer it be because of the police buy it be the answer do all go well in hind rissaldar sahib all go well be like an old tortoise look you who put his head out from the bank and draw it in again ay this be the road of hindustan all man come by this way son of swine be the soft part of the road mean for thee to scratch thy back upon father of all the daughter of shame and husband of ten thousand virtueless one thy mother be devoted to devil be lead thereto by her mother thy aunt have never have nose for seven generation thy sister what owl ’s folly tell thee to draw thy cart across the road break wheel then take broken head and put the two together at leisure the voice and venomous whip cracking come out of pillar of dust fifty yard away where cart have break down thin high kathiawar mare with eye and nostril aflame rocket out of the jam snort and wince as her rider bend she across the road in chase of shout man he be tall and grey bearded sit the almost mad beast as piece of she and village crow the policeman draw back abash and kim hoot at he all down the road be there ever such disciple as he cry merrily to the lama all earth would have pick thy bone within ten mile of lahore city if have not guard thee consider in my own mind whether thou art spirit sometimes or sometimes an evil imp say the lama smile slowly be thy chela kim drop into step at his side that indescribable gait of the long distance tramp all the world over now let we walk mutter the lama and to the click of his rosary they walk in silence mile upon mile the lama as usual be deep in meditation but kim ’s bright eye be open wide this broad smile river of life he consider be vast improvement on the cramped and crowd lahore street there be new people and new sight at every stride caste he know and caste that be altogether out of his experience they meet troop of long haired strong scented sansis with basket of lizard and other unclean food on their back their lean dog sniff at their heel these people keep their own side of the road move at quick furtive jog trot and all other caste give they ample room for the sansi be deep pollution behind they walk wide and stiffly across the strong shadow the memory of his leg iron still on he stride one newly release from the jail his full stomach and shiny skin to prove that the government feed its prisoner well than most honest man could feed themselves kim know that walk well and make broad jest of it as they pass then an akali wild eyed wild haired sikh devotee in the blue check clothe of his faith with polished steel quoit glisten on the cone of his tall blue turban stalk past return from visit to one of the independent sikh state where he have be sing the ancient glory of the khalsa to college train princeling in top boot and white cord breech kim be careful not to irritate that man for the akali ’s temper be short and his arm quick here and there they meet or be overtake by the gaily dress crowd of whole village turn out to some local fair the woman with their babe on their hip walk behind the man the old boy prance on stick of sugar cane drag rude brass model of locomotive such as they sell for halfpenny or flash the sun into the eye of their well from cheap toy mirror one could see at glance what each have buy and if there be any doubt it need only to watch the wife compare brown arm against brown arm the newly purchase dull glass bracelet that come from the north west these merry maker step slowly call one to the other and stop to haggle with sweetmeat seller or to make prayer before one of the wayside shrine sometimes hindu sometimes mussalman which the low caste of both creed share with beautiful impartiality solid line of blue rise and fall like the back of caterpillar in haste would swing up through the quivering dust and trot past to chorus of quick cackle that be gang of changars_—the woman who have take all the embankment of all the northern railway under their charge flat footed big bosomed strong limbed blue petticoate clan of earth carrier hurry north on news of job and waste no time by the road they belong to the caste whose man do not count and they walk with square elbow swing hip and head on high as suit woman who carry heavy weight little later marriage procession would strike into the grand trunk with music and shouting and smell of marigold and jasmine stronger even than the reek of the dust one could see the bride ’s litter blur of red and tinsel stagger through the haze while the bridegroom ’s bewreathed pony turn aside to snatch mouthful from pass fodder cart then kim would join the kentish fire of good wish and bad joke wish the couple hundred son and no daughter as the saying be still more interesting and more to be shout over it be when stroll juggler with some half train monkey or panting feeble bear or woman who tie goat horn to her foot and with these dance on slack rope set the horse to shy and the woman to shrill long draw quaver of amazement the lama never raise his eye he do not note the money lender on his goose rumpe pony hasten along to collect the cruel interest or the long shout deep voice little mob still in military formation of native soldier on leave rejoice to be rid of their breech and puttee and say the most outrageous thing to the most respectable woman in sight even the seller of ganges water he do not see and kim expect that he would at least buy bottle of that precious stuff he look steadily at the ground and stride as steadily hour after hour his soul busy elsewhere
but kim be in the seventh heaven of joy the grand trunk at this point be build on an embankment to guard against winter flood from the foothill so that one walk as it be little above the country along stately corridor see all india spread out to left and right it be beautiful to behold the many yoke grain and cotton wagon crawl over the country road one could hear their axle complain mile away come near till with shout and yell and bad word they climb up the steep incline and plunge on to the hard main road carter revile carter it be equally beautiful to watch the people little clump of red and blue and pink and white and saffron turn aside to go to their own village disperse and grow small by two and three across the level
plain kim feel these thing though he could not give tongue to his feeling and so content himself with buy peel sugar cane and spit the pith generously about his path from time to time the lama take snuff and at last kim could endure the silence no long this be good land the land of the south say he the air be good the water be good eh and they be all bind upon the wheel say the lama bind from life after life to none of these have the way be show he shake himself back to this world and now we have walk weary way say kim surely we shall soon come to parao rest place shall we stay there look the sun be slop who will receive we this evening that be all one this country be full of good folk besides he sink his voice beneath whisper—“we have money the crowd thicken as they near the rest place which mark the end of their day ’s journey line of stall sell very simple food and tobacco stack of firewood police station
well horse trough few tree and under they some trample ground dot with the black ash of old fire be all that mark parao on the grand trunk if you except the beggar and the crow both hungry by this time the sun be drive broad golden speak through the low branch of the mango tree the parakeet and dove be come home in their hundred the chatter grey back seven sister talk over the day ’s adventure walk back and forth in two and three almost under the foot of the traveller and shuffle and scuffle in the branch show that the bat be ready to go out on the night picket swiftly the light gather itself together paint for an instant the face and the cartwheel and the bullock horn as red as blood then the night fall change the touch of the air draw low even haze like gossamer veil of blue across the face of the country and bring out keen and distinct the smell of wood smoke and cattle and the good scent of wheaten cake cook on ash the evening patrol hurry out of the police station with important coughing and reiterate order and live charcoal ball in the cup of wayside carter ’s hookah glow red while kim ’s eye mechanically watch the last flicker of the sun on the brass tweezer the life of the parao be very like that of the kashmir serai on small scale kim dive into the happy asiatic disorder which if you only allow time will bring you everything that simple man need his want be few because since the lama have no caste scruple cook food from the near stall would serve but for luxury ’s sake kim buy handful of dung cake to build fire all about come and go round the little flame man cry for oil or grain or sweetmeat or tobacco jostle one another while they wait their turn at the well and under the man ’s voice you hear from halt shutter cart the high squeal and giggle of woman whose face should not be see in public nowadays well educate native be of opinion that when their womenfolk travel and they visit good deal it be well to take they quickly by rail in properly screen compartment and that custom be spread but there be always those of the old rock who hold by the use of their forefather and above all there be always the old woman more conservative than the man who toward the end of their day go on pilgrimage they be wither and undesirable do not under certain circumstance object to unveil after their long seclusion during which they have always be in business touch with thousand outside interest they love the bustle and stir of the open road the gathering at the shrine and the infinite possibility of gossip with like minded dowager very often it suit longsuffere family that strong tongue iron will old lady should disport herself about india in this fashion for certainly pilgrimage be grateful to the god so all about india in the most remote place as in the most public you find some knot of grizzled servitor in nominal charge of an old lady who be more or less curtained and hide away in bullock cart such man be staid and discreet and when european or high caste native be near will net their charge with most elaborate precaution but in the ordinary haphazard chance of pilgrimage the precaution be not take the old lady be after all intensely human and live to look upon life kim mark down gaily ornament ruth or family bullock cart with broidered canopy of two dome like double hump camel which have just be draw into the parao eight man make its retinue and two of the eight be arm with rusty sabre sure sign that they follow person of distinction for the common folk do not bear arm an increase cackle of complaint order and jest and what to european would have be bad language come from behind the curtain here be evidently woman use to command kim look over the retinue critically half of they be thin legged grey bearded ooryas from down country the other half be duffle clothe feel hatted hillman of the north and that mixture tell its own tale even if he have not overhear the incessant sparring between the two division the old lady be go south on visit probably to rich relative most probably to son in law who have send up an escort as mark of respect the hillman would be of her own people kulu or kangra folk it be quite clear that she be not take her daughter down to be we or the curtain would have be lace home and the guard would have allow no one near the car merry and high spirited dame think kim balance the dung cake in one hand the cooked food in the other and pilot the lama with nudge shoulder something might be make out of the meeting the lama would give he no help but as conscientious chela kim be delighted to beg for two he build his fire as close to the cart as he dare wait for one of the escort to order he away the lama drop wearily to the ground much as heavy fruit eat bat cower and return to his rosary stand far off beggar the order be shout in break hindustani by one of the hillman it be only pahari hillman say kim over his shoulder since when have the hill ass own all hindustan the retort be swift and brilliant sketch of kim ’s pedigree for three generation kim ’s voice be sweet than ever as he break the dung cake into fit piece in my country we call that the beginning of love talk harsh thin cackle behind the curtain put the hillman on his mettle for second shot not so bad not so bad say kim with calm but have care my brother lest we—_we say be minded to give curse or so in return and our curse have the knack of bite home the ooryas laugh the hillman spring forward threateningly the lama suddenly raise his head bring his huge tam o’-shanter hat into the full light of kim ’s new start fire what be it say he the man halt as though strike to stone be save from great sin he stammer the foreigner have find he priest at last whisper one of the ooryas why be that beggar brat not well beat the old woman cry the hillman draw back to the cart and whisper something to the curtain there be dead silence then mutter this go well think kim pretend neither to see nor hear when when he have eaten”—the hillman fawn on kim—“it it be request that the holy one will do the honour to talk to one who would speak to he after he have eat he will sleep kim return loftily he could not quite see what new turn the game have take but stand resolute to profit by it now will get he his food the last sentence speak loudly end with sigh as of faintness myself and the other of my people will look to that if it be permit it be permit say kim more loftily than ever holy one these people will bring we food the land be good all the country of the south be good great and terrible world mumble the lama drowsily let he sleep say kim but look to it that we be well feed when he wake he be very holy man again one of the oorya say something contemptuously he be not faquir he be not down country beggar kim go on severely address the star he be the most holy of holy man he be above all caste be his chela come here say the flat thin voice behind the curtain and kim come conscious that eye he could not see be star at he one skinny brown finger heavy with ring lie on the edge of the cart and the talk go this way who be that one an exceedingly holy one he come from far off he come from tibet where in tibet from behind the snow from very far place he know the star he make horoscope he read nativity but he do not do this for money he do it for kindness and great charity be his disciple be call also the friend of the star thou art no hillman ask he he will tell thee be send to he from the star to show he an end to his pilgrimage humph consider brat that be an old woman and not altogether fool lama know and to these give reverence but thou art no more lawful chela than this my finger be the pole of this wagon thou art casteless hindu bold and unblushing beggar attach belike to the holy one for the sake of gain do we not all work for gain kim change his tone promptly to match that alter voice have heard”—thi be bow draw at venture—“i have hear what hast thou hear she snap rap with the finger nothing that well remember but some talk in the bazar which be doubtless lie that even rajah small hill rajah but none the less of good rajput blood assuredly of good blood that these even sell the more comely of their womenfolk for gain down south they sell they to zemindar and such all of oudh if there be one thing in the world that the small hill rajah deny it be just this charge but it happen to be one thing that the bazar believe when they discuss the mysterious slave traffic of india the old lady explain to kim in tense indignant whisper precisely what manner and fashion of malignant liar he be have kim hint this when she be girl he would have be pommel to death that same evening by an elephant this be perfectly true be only beggar ’s brat as the eye of beauty have say he wail in extravagant terror eye of beauty forsooth who be that thou shouldst fling beggar endearment at and yet she laugh at the long forget word forty year ago that might have be say and not without truth thirty year ago

she would speak to thee be this also thy work the lama dip deep into his snuff gourd who else watch over thee since our wonderful journey begin kim ’s eye dance in his head as he blow the rank smoke through his nostril and stretch he on the dusty ground have fail to oversee thy comfort holy one blessing on thee the lama incline his solemn head have know many man in my so long life and disciple not few but to none among man if so be thou art woman bear have my heart go out as it have to thee thoughtful wise and courteous but something of small imp and have never see such priest as thou kim consider the benevolent yellow face wrinkle by wrinkle it be less than three day since we take the road together and it be as though it be hundred year perhaps in former life it be permit that should have render thee some service maybe”—he smiled—“i free thee from trap or having catch thee on hook in the day when be not enlighten cast thee back into the river maybe say kim quietly he have hear this sort of speculation again and again from the mouth of many whom the english would not consider imaginative now as regard that woman in the bullock cart think she need second son for her daughter that be no part of the way sigh the lama
but at least she be from the hill ah the hill and the snow of the hill he rise and stalk to the cart kim would have give his ear to come too but the lama do not invite he and the few word he catch be in an unknown tongue for they speak some common speech of the mountain the woman seem to ask question which the lama turn over in his mind before answer now and again he hear the singsong cadence of chinese quotation it be strange picture that kim watch between droop eyelid the lama very straight and erect the deep fold of his yellow clothing slash with black in the light of the parao fire embroidery behind the cart be wall of uncertain darkness speckle with little flame and alive with half catch form and face and shadow the voice of early evening have settle down to one soothe hum whose deep note be the steady chumping of the bullock above their chop straw and whose high be the tinkle of bengali dancing girl ’s sitar most man have eat and pull deep at their gurgle grunt hookah which in full blast sound like bull frog at last the lama return hillman walk behind he with wad cotton quilt and spread it carefully by the fire she deserve ten thousand grandchild think kim none the less but for those gift would not have come virtuous woman and wise one the lama slacken off joint by joint like slow camel the world be full of charity to those who follow the way he fling fair half of the quilt over kim and what say she kim roll up in his share of it she ask many question and propound many problem the most of which be idle tale which she have hear from devil serve priest who pretend to follow the way some answer and some say be foolish many wear the robe but few keep the way that be true kim use the thoughtful conciliatory tone of those who wish to draw confidence but by her light she be most right minded she desire greatly that we should go with she to buddh gaya her road be ours as understand for many day journey to the southward and patience little to this say that my search come before all thing she have hear many foolish legend but this great truth of my river she have never hear such be the priest of the low hill she know the abbot of lung cho
but she do not know of my river nor the tale of the arrow and speak therefore of the search and of the way and of matter that be profitable she desire only that should accompany she and make prayer for second son
aha we woman do not think of anything save child say kim sleepily now since our road run together for while do not see that we in any way depart from our search if so be we accompany she at least as far as have forget the name of the city ohé say kim turn and speak in sharp whisper to one of the ooryas few yard away where be your master ’s house little behind saharunpore among the fruit garden he name the village that be the place say the lama so far at least we can go with she fly go to carrion say the oorya in an abstracted voice for the sick cow crow for the sick man brahmin kim breathe the proverb impersonally to the shadow top of the tree overhead the oorya grunt and hold his peace
so then we go with she holy one be there any reason against can still step aside and try all the river that the road overpass she desire that should come she very greatly desire it kim stifle laugh in the quilt when once that imperious old lady have recover from her natural awe of lama he think it probable that she would be worth listen to he be nearly asleep when the lama suddenly quote proverb the husband of the talkative have great reward hereafter then kim hear he snuff thrice and doze off still laugh the diamond bright dawn wake man and crow and bullock
together kim sit up and yawn shake himself and thrill with delight this be see the world in real truth this be life as he would have it bustle and shout the buckling of belt and beating of bullock and creaking of wheel lighting of fire and cooking of food and new sight at every turn of the approve eye the morning mist sweep off in whorl of silver the parrot shoot away to some distant river in shriek green host all the well wheel within ear shoot go to work india be awake and kim be in the middle of it more awake and more excited than anyone chew on twig that he would presently use as toothbrush for he borrow right- and leave handedly from all the custom of the country he know and love there be no need to worry about food no need to spend cowrie at the crowd stall he be the disciple of holy man annex by strong willed old lady all thing would be prepare for they and when they be respectfully invite so to do they would sit and eat for the rest kim giggle here as he clean his tooth his hostess would rather heighten the enjoyment of the road he inspect her bullock critically as they come up grunt and blow under the yoke if they go too fast it be not likely there would oh shabash murmur kim unable to contain himself as the man slink away well do indeed it be shame and scandal that poor woman may not go to make prayer to her god except she be jostle and insult by all the refuse of hindustan that she must eat gâli abuse as man eat ghi but have yet wag leave to my tongue word or two well speak that serve the occasion and still be without my tobacco who be the one eyed and luckless son of shame that have not yet prepare my pipe it be hastily thrust in by hillman and trickle of thick smoke from each corner of the curtain show that peace be restore if kim have walk proudly the day before disciple of holy man today he pace with tenfold pride in the train of semi royal procession with recognize place under the patronage of an old lady of charming manner and infinite resource the escort their head tie up native fashion fall in on either side the cart shuffle enormous cloud of dust the lama
kim walk little to one side kim chew his stick of sugarcane and make way for no one under the status of priest they could hear the old lady ’s tongue clack as steadily as rice husker she bid the address they and thus the propriety be more or less observed dark sallowish district superintendent of police faultlessly uniformed an englishman trot by on tired horse and see from her retinue what manner of person she be chaff she mother he cry do they do this in the zenana suppose an englishman come by and see that thou hast no nose what she shrill back thine own mother have no nose why say so then on the open road it be fair counter the englishman throw up his hand with the gesture of man hit at sword play she laugh and nod be this face to tempt virtue aside she withdraw all her veil and star at he it be by no mean lovely but as the man gather up his rein he call it moon of paradise disturber of integrity and few other fantastic epithet which double she up with mirth that be nut cut rogue she say all police constable be nut cut but the police wallah be the bad hai my son thou hast never learn all that since thou camest from belait europe who suckle thee pahareen_—a hillwoman of dalhousie my mother keep thy beauty under shade dispenser of delight and he be go these be the sort”—she take fine judicial tone and stuff her mouth with pan_—“these be the sort to oversee justice they know the land and the custom of the land the other all new from europe suckle by white woman and learn our tongue from book be bad than the pestilence they do harm to king then she tell long long tale to the world at large of an ignorant young policeman who have disturb some small hill rajah ninth cousin of her own in the matter of trivial land case wind up with quotation from work by no means devotional then her mood change
and she bid one of the escort ask whether the lama would walk alongside and discuss matter of religion so kim drop back into the dust and return to his sugar cane for an hour or more the lama ’s tam o’shanter show like moon through the haze and from all he hear kim gather that the old woman weep one of the ooryas half apologize for his rudeness overnight say that he have never know his mistress of so bland temper and he ascribe it to the presence of the strange priest personally he believe in brahmin though like all native he be acutely aware of their cunning and their greed still when brahmin but irritated with beg demand the mother of his master ’s wife and when she send they away so angry that they curse the whole retinue which be the real reason of the second off side bullock go lame and of the pole break the night before he be prepared to accept any priest of any other denomination in or out of india to this kim assent with wise nod and bid the oorya observe that the lama take no money and that the cost of his and kim ’s food would be repay hundred time in the good luck that would attend the caravan henceforward he also tell story of lahore city and sing song or two which make the escort laugh as town mouse well acquaint with the late song by the most fashionable composer they be woman for the most part kim have distinct advantage over man from little fruit village behind saharunpore but he let that advantage be infer at noon they turn aside to eat and the meal be good plentiful and well serve on plate of clean leaf in decency out of drift of the dust they give the scrap to certain beggar that all requirement might be fulfil and sit down to long luxurious smoke the old lady have retreat behind her curtain but mix most freely in the talk her servant argue with and contradict she as servant do throughout the east she compare the cool and the pine of the kangra and kulu hill with the dust and the mango of the south she tell tale of some old local god at the edge of her husband ’s territory she roundly abuse the tobacco which she be then smoke revile all brahmin and speculate without reserve on the come of many grandson chapter here come to my own again feed forgive and know again claim by bone of my bone again and sib to flesh of my flesh the fatted calf
be dress for but the husk have great zest for think my pig will be good for so off to the stye afresh the prodigal son once more the lazy string tie shuffle procession get under way and she sleep till they reach the next halting stage it be very short march and time lack an hour to sundown
so kim cast about for mean of amusement but why not sit and rest say one of the escort only the devil and the english walk to and fro without reason never make friend with the devil monkey or boy no man know what they will do next say his fellow kim turn scornful back he do not want to hear the old story how the devil play with the boy and repent of it and walk idly across country the lama stride after he all that day whenever they pass stream he have turn aside to look at it
but in no case have he receive any warning that he have find his river insensibly too the comfort of speak to someone in reasonable tongue and of be properly consider and respect as her spiritual adviser by well bear woman have wean his thought little from the search and far he be prepared to spend serene year in his quest have nothing of the white man ’s impatience but great faith where goest thou he call after kim nowhither

purple in the afternoon light with heavy clump of mango in the centre it strike kim as curious that no shrine stand in so eligible spot the boy be observe as any priest for these thing far across the plain walk side by side four man make small by the distance he look intently under his curved palm and catch the sheen of brass white soldier say he let we see it be always soldier when thou and go out alone together but have never see the white soldier they do no harm except when they be drunk keep behind this tree they step behind the thick trunk in the cool dark of the mango tope two little figure halt the other two come forward uncertainly they be the advance party of regiment on the march send out as usual to mark the camp they bear five foot stick with fluttering flag and call to each other as they spread over the flat earth at last they enter the mango grove walk heavily it here or hereabout officer tent under the tree take it an the rest of we can stay outside have they mark out for the baggage wagon behind they cry again to their comrade in the distance and the rough answer come back faint and mellow shove the flag in here then say one what do they prepare say the lama and down the shaded cave of greenery and return it kim star with all his eye his breath come short and sharp between his tooth the soldier stamp off into the sunshine holy one he gasp my horoscope the and kim ’s limitation be as curious and sudden as his expansion the man the nine hundred thorough devil of his father ’s prophecy might pray to the beast after dark as hindus pray to the holy cow that at least would be entirely right and logical and the padre with the gold cross would be therefore the man to consult in the matter on the other hand remember sober faced padre whom he have avoid in lahore city the priest might be an inquisitive nuisance who would bid he learn but have it not be prove at umballa that his sign in the high heaven portend war and armed man be he not the friend of the star as well as of all the world cram to the tooth with dreadful secret lastly and firstly as the undercurrent of all his quick thought this adventure though he do not know the english word be stupendous lark delightful continuation of his old flight across the housetop as well as the fulfilment of sublime prophecy he lie belly flat and wriggle towards the mess tent door hand on the amulet round his neck it be as he suspect the sahib pray to their god for in the centre of the mess table its sole ornament when they be on the line of march stand golden bull fashion from old time loot of the summer palace at pekin red gold bull with low head ramp upon field of irish green to he the sahib hold out their glass and cry aloud confusedly now the reverend arthur bennett always leave mess after that toast and be rather tired by his march his movement be more abrupt than usual kim with slightly raise head be still star at his totem on the table when the chaplain step on his right shoulder blade kim flinch under the leather and roll sideways bring down the chaplain who ever man of action catch he by the throat and nearly choke the life out of he kim then kick he desperately in the stomach mr bennett gasp and double up but without relax his grip roll over again and silently haul kim to his own tent the maverick be incurable practical joker and it occur to the englishman that silence be good till he have make complete inquiry why it boy he say as he draw his prize under the light of the tent pole lantern then shake he severely cry what be you do you ’re thief his hindustanee be very limited and the ruffled and disgust kim intend to keep to the character lie down for he as he recover his breath he be invent beautifully plausible tale of his relation to some hundred white man the story of his appearance in camp the discovery of his parentage and his prophecy have lose nothing in the telling big shapeless white woman on pile of bedding ask he mysteriously whether he think her husband would come back from the war kim reflect gravely and say that he would and the woman give he food in many respect this big procession that play music at interval this crowd that talk and laugh so easily resemble festival in lahore city so far there be no sign of hard work and he resolve to lend the spectacle his patronage at evening there come out to meet they band of music and play the maverick into camp near umballa railway station that be an interesting night man of other regiment come to visit the maverick the maverick go visit on their own account their picket hurry forth to bring they back meet picket of strange regiment on the same duty and after while the bugle blow madly for more picket with officer to control the tumult the maverick have reputation for liveliness to live up to but they fall in on the platform next morning in perfect shape and condition and kim leave behind with the sick woman and boy find himself shout farewell excitedly as the train draw away life as sahib be amusing so far but he touch it with cautious hand then they march he back in charge of drummer boy to empty lime wash barrack whose floor be cover with rubbish and string and paper and whose ceiling give back his lonely footfall native fashion he curl himself up on strip cot and go to sleep an angry man stump down the veranda wake he up and say he be schoolmaster this be enough for kim and he retire into his shell he could just puzzle out the various english police notice in lahore city because they affect his comfort and among the many news”—father victor ’s voice interrupt the reverie ye can run along now and play with the other boy they ’ll teach ye something but do not think ye’ll like it the day drag to its weary end when he wish to sleep he be instruct how to fold up his clothe and set out his boot the other boy deride bugle wake he in the dawn the schoolmaster catch he after breakfast thrust page of meaningless character under his nose give they senseless name and whack he without reason kim meditate poison he with opium borrow from barrack sweeper but reflect that as they all eat at one table in public this be peculiarly revolting to kim who prefer to turn his back on the world at meal the stroke might be dangerous then he attempt run off to the village where the priest have try to drug the lama the village where the old soldier live but far see sentry at every exit head back the little scarlet figure trouser and jacket crippled body and mind alike

thy come all unnoised heaven hath she high as earth her baser war heir to these tumult this affright that fraye by adam ’s father own sin bind alway peer up draw out thy horoscope and say which planet mend thy threadbare fate or mars sir john christie in the afternoon the red face schoolmaster tell kim that he have be strike off the strength which convey no meaning to he till he be order to go away and play then he run to the bazar and find the young letter writer to whom he owe stamp now pay say kim royally and now need another letter to be write mahbub ali be in umballa say the writer jauntily he be by virtue of his office bureau of general misinformation this be not to mahbub but to priest take thy pen and write quickly to teshoo lama the holy one from bhotiyal seek for river who be now in the temple of the tirthankar at benare take more ink in three day be to go down to nucklao to the school at nucklao the name of the school be xavier do not know where that school be but it be at nucklao but know nucklao the writer interrupt know the school tell he where it be and give half an anna the reed pen scratch busily he can not mistake the man lift his head who watch we across the street kim look up hurriedly and see colonel creighton in tennis flannel

no be kim this be the great world and be only kim who be kim he consider his own identity thing he have never do before till his head swim he be one insignificant person in all this roar whirl of india go southward to he know not what fate presently the colonel send for he and talk for long time so far as kim could gather he be to be diligent and enter the survey of india as chain man if he be very good and pass the proper examination he would be earn thirty rupee month at seventeen year old and colonel creighton would see that he find suitable employment kim pretend at first to understand perhaps one word in three of this talk then the colonel see his mistake turn to fluent and picturesque urdu and kim
be content no man could be fool who know the language so intimately who move so gently and silently and whose eye be so different from the dull fat eye of other sahib

except bombay the queen of all more beautiful in her garish style than lucknow whether you see she from the bridge over the river or from the top of the imambara look down on the gilt umbrella of the chutter munzil and the tree in which the town be bed king have adorn she with fantastic building endow she with charity cram she with pensioner and drench she with blood she be the centre of all thing where an english guide would have talk of the mutiny now we will go to the school say kim at last the great old school of st xavier in partibus block on block of low white building stand in vast
