but why didst thou not stay with the kulu woman holy one in what way didst thou get to benare my heart have be heavy since we part the woman weary by constant flux of talk and require charm for child separate myself from that company permit she to acquire merit by gift she be at least woman of open hand and make promise to return to her house if need arise then perceive myself alone in this great and terrible world bethink of the te rain to benare where know one abode in the tirthankar temple who be seeker even as i. ah thy river say kim have forget the river so soon my chela have never forget it but when have leave thee it seem well that should go to the temple and take counsel for look you india be very large and it may be that wise man before we some two or three have leave record of the place of our river there be debate in the temple of the tirthankar on this matter some say one thing and some another they be courteous folk so be it
but what dost thou do now acquire merit in that help thee my chela to wisdom the priest of that body of man who serve the red bull write that all should be as desire for thee send the money to suffice for one year and then come as thou se to watch for thee go up into the gate of learn day and half have wait not because be lead by any affection towards thee that be no part of the way but as they say at the tirthankar temple because money having be pay for learn it be right that should oversee the end of the matter they resolve my doubt most clearly have fear that perhaps come because wish to see thee misguided by the red mist of affection it be not so moreover be trouble by dream but surely holy one thou hast not forget the road and all that befall on it
surely it be little to see that thou didst come the horse be cold and it be past their feeding time whine the driver go to jehannum and abide there with thy reputationless aunt kim snarl over his shoulder be all alone in this land know not where go nor what shall befall my heart be in that letter send thee except for mahbub ali and he be pathan have no friend save thee
holy one do not altogether go away have consider that also the lama reply in shake voice it be manifest that from time to time shall acquire merit if before that have not find my river by assure myself that thy foot be set on wisdom what they will teach thee do not know but the priest write that no son of sahib in all india will be well teach than thou so from time to time therefore will come again maybe thou wilt be such sahib as he who give these spectacles”—the lama wipe they elaborately—“in the wonder house at lahore that be my hope for he be fountain of wisdom wise than many abbot again maybe thou wilt forget and our meeting if eat thy bread cry kim passionately how shall ever forget thee
no no he put the boy aside must go back to benare from time to time now that know the custom of letter writer in this land will send thee letter and from time to time will come and see thee but whither shall send my letter wail kim clutch at the robe all forgetful that he be sahib to the temple of the tirthankar at benare that be the place have choose till find my river do not weep for look you all desire be illusion and new binding upon the wheel go up to the gate of learn let see thee go dost thou love then go or my heart crack will come again surely will come again the lama watch the ticca gharri rumble into the compound and stride off snuff between each long stride the gate of learn shut with clang the country bear and breed boy have his own manner and custom which do not resemble those of any other land and his teacher approach he by road which an english master would not understand therefore you would scarcely be interested in kim ’s experience as st xavier ’s boy among two or three hundred precocious youth most of whom have never see the sea he suffer the usual penalty for break out of bind when there be cholera in the city this be before he have learn to write fair english and so be oblige to find bazar letter writer he be of course indict for smoking and for the use of abuse more full flavour than even st xavier have ever hear he learn to wash himself with the levitical scrupulosity of the native bear who in his heart consider the englishman rather dirty he play the usual trick on the patient cooly pull the punkah in the sleeping room where the boy thresh through the hot night tell tale till the dawn and quietly he measure himself against his self reliant mate they be son of subordinate official in the railway telegraph and canal service of warrant officer sometimes retire and sometimes act as commander in chief to feudatory rajah ’s army of captain of the indian marine government pensioner planter presidency shopkeeper and missionary few be cadet of the old eurasian house that have take strong root in dhurrumtollah pereiras de souzas and d’silva their parent could well have educate they in england but they love the school that have serve their own youth and generation follow sallow hue generation at st xavier ’s their home range from howrah of the railway people to abandon cantonment like monghyr and chunar lose tea garden shillong way village where their father be large landholder in oudh or the deccan mission station week from the near railway line seaport thousand mile south face the brazen indian surf and cinchona plantation south of all the mere story of their adventure which to they be no adventure on their road to and from school would have crisp western boy ’s hair they be use to jog off alone through hundred mile of jungle where there be always the delightful chance of be delay by tiger
but they would no more have bath in the english channel in an english august than their brother across the world would have lie still while leopard snuff at their palanquin there be boy of fifteen who have spend day and half on an islet in the middle of flood river take charge as by right of camp of frantic pilgrim return from shrine there be senior who have requisition chance meet rajah ’s elephant in the name of st francis xavier when the rain once blot out the cart track that lead to their father ’s estate and have all but lose the huge beast in quicksand
there be boy who he say and none doubt have help his father to beat off with rifle from the veranda rush of akas in the day when those head hunter be bold against lonely plantation and every tale be tell in the even passionless voice of the native bear mix with quaint reflection borrow unconsciously from native foster mother and turn of speech that show they have be that instant translate from the vernacular kim watch listen and approve this be not insipid single word talk of drummer boy it deal with life he know and in part understand the atmosphere suit he and he throve by inch they give he white drill suit as the weather warm
and he rejoice in the new find bodily comfort as he rejoice to use his sharpen mind over the task they set he his quickness would have delight an english master but at st xavier ’s they know the first rush of mind develop by sun and surround as they know the half collapse that set in at twenty two or twenty three none the less he remember to hold himself lowly when tale be tell of hot night kim do not sweep the board with his reminiscence for st xavier look down on boy who go native all together one must never forget that one be sahib and that some day when examination be pas one will command native kim make note of this for he begin to understand where examination lead then come the holiday from august to october the long holiday impose by the heat and the rain kim be inform that he would go north to some station in the hill behind umballa where father victor would arrange for he barrack school say kim who have ask many question and think more yes suppose so say the master it will not do you any harm to keep you out of mischief you can go up with young de castro as far as delhi kim consider it in every possible light he have be diligent even as the colonel advise boy ’s holiday be his own property of so much the talk of his companion have advise him,—and barrack school would be torment after st xavier ’s moreover this be magic worth anything else he could write in three month he have discover how man can speak to each other without third party at the cost of half an anna and little knowledge no word have come from the lama but there remain the road kim yearn for the caress of soft mud squish up between the toe as his mouth water for mutton stew with butter and cabbage for rice speckle with strong scented cardamom for the saffron tint rice garlic and onion and the forbidden greasy sweetmeat of the bazar they would feed he raw beef on platter at the barrack school and he must smoke by stealth but again he be sahib and be at st xavier ’s and that pig mahbub ali no he would not test mahbub ’s hospitality and yet he think it out alone in the dormitory and come to the conclusion he have be unjust to mahbub the school be empty nearly all the master have go away colonel creighton ’s railway pass lie in his hand and kim puff himself that he have not spend colonel creighton ’s or mahbub ’s money in riotous living he be still lord of two rupee seven anna his new bullock trunk mark k. o’h
and bed roll lie in the empty sleeping room sahib be always tie to their baggage say kim nod at they you will stay here he go out into the warm rain smile sinfully and seek certain house whose outside he have note down some time before arre dost thou know what manner of woman we be in this quarter
oh shame be bear yesterday
kim squat native fashion on the cushion of that upper room little dyestuff and three yard of cloth to help out jest be it much to ask who be she thou art full young as sahib go for this devilry
oh she she be the daughter of certain schoolmaster of regiment in the cantonment he have beat twice because go over their wall in these clothe now would go as gardener ’s boy old man be very jealous that be true hold thy face still while dab on the juice not too black naikan would not appear to she as hubshi nigger
oh love make nought of these thing and how old be she twelve year think say the shameless kim spread it also on the breast it may be her father will tear my clothe off and if be piebald he laugh the girl work busily dab twist of cloth into little saucer of brown dye that hold long than any walnut juice now send out and get cloth for the turban woe be my head be all unshaved and he will surely knock off my turban be not barber but will make shift thou wast bear to be breaker of heart all this disguise for one evening remember the stuff do not wash away she shake with laughter till her bracelet and anklet jingle but who be to pay for this huneefa herself could not have give thee well stuff trust in the god my sister say kim gravely screw his face round as the stain dry besides hast thou ever help to paint sahib thus before never indeed but jest be not money it be worth much more child thou art beyond all dispute the most shameless son of shaitan that have ever know to take up poor girl ’s time with this play and then to say be not the jest enough thou wilt go very far in this world she give the dance girl salutation in mockery all one make haste and rough cut my head kim shift from foot to foot his eye ablaze with mirth as he think of the fat day before he he give the girl four anna and run down the stair in the likeness of low caste hindu boy perfect in every detail cookshop be his next point of call where he feast in extravagance and greasy luxury on lucknow station platform he watch young de castro all cover with prickly heat get into second class compartment kim patronize third and be the life and soul of it he explain to the company that he be assistant to juggler who have leave he behind sick with fever and that he would pick up his master at umballa as the occupant of the carriage change he vary this tale or adorn it with all the shoot of bud fancy the more rampant for be hold off native speech so long in all india that night be no human be so joyful as kim at umballa he get out and head eastward plash over the sodden field to the village where the old soldier live about this time colonel creighton at simla be advise from lucknow by wire that young o’hara have disappear mahbub ali be in town sell horse and to he the colonel confide the affair one morning canter round annandale racecourse
oh that be nothing say the horse dealer man be like horse at certain time they need salt and if that salt be not in the manger they will lick it up from the earth he have go back to the road again for while the madrissah weary he know it would another time will take he upon the road myself do not be trouble creighton sahib it be as though polo pony break loose run out to learn the game alone then he be not dead think you fever might kill he do not fear for the boy otherwise monkey do not fall among tree next morning on the same course mahbub ’s stallion range alongside the colonel it be as have think say the horse dealer he have come through umballa at least and there he have write letter to having learn in the bazar that be here read say the colonel with sigh of relief it be absurd that man of his position should take an interest in little country breed vagabond but the colonel remember the conversation in the train and often in the past few month have catch himself think of the queer silent self possess boy his evasion of course be the height of insolence
but it argue some resource and nerve mahbub ’s eye twinkle as he rein out into the centre of the cramped little plain where none could come near unseen the friend of the star who be the friend of all the world what be this name we give he in lahore city the friend of all the world take leave to go to his own place he will come back upon the appoint day let the box and the bedding roll be send for and if there have be fault let the hand of friendship turn aside the whip of calamity there be yet little more but no matter read certain thing be not know to those who eat with fork it be well to eat with both hand for while speak soft word to those who do not understand this that the return may be propitious now the manner in which that be cast be of course the work of the letter writer but see how wisely the boy have devise the matter of it so that no hint be give except to those who know be this the hand of friendship to avert the whip of calamity laugh the colonel see how wise be the boy he would go back to the road again as say not know yet thy trade be not at all sure of that the colonel mutter he turn to to make peace between you be he not wise he say he will return he be but perfect his knowledge think sahib he have be three month at the school
and he be not mouthed to that bit for my part rejoice the pony learn the game
ay but another time he must not go alone he go alone before he come under the colonel sahib ’s protection when he come to the great game he must go alone alone and at peril of his head then if he spit or sneeze or sit down other than as the people do whom he watch he may be slay why hinder he now remember how the persians say the jackal that live in the wild of mazanderan can only be catch by the hound of mazanderan it be true mahbub ali and if he come to no harm do not desire anything well
but it be great insolence on his part he do not tell even whither he go say mahbub he be no fool when his time be accomplish he will come to it be time the healer of pearl take he in hand he ripen too quickly as sahib reckon this prophecy be fulfil to the letter month later mahbub have go down to umballa to bring up fresh consignment of horse and kim meet he on the kalka road at dusk rid alone beg an alm of he be swear at and reply in english there be nobody within earshot to hear mahbub ’s gasp of amazement and where hast thou be up and down down and up come under tree out of the wet and tell stay for while with an old man near umballa anon with household of my acquaintance in umballa with one of these go as far as delhi to the southward that be wondrous city then drive bullock for teli an oilman come north but hear of great feast forward in patiala and thither go in the company of firework maker it be great feast kim rub his stomach see rajah and elephant with gold and silver trapping and they light all the firework at once whereby eleven man be kill my fire work maker among they and be blow across tent but take no harm then come back to the rêl with sikh horseman to whom be groom for my bread and so here shabash say mahbub ali but what do the colonel sahib say do not wish to be beat the hand of friendship have avert the whip of calamity but another time when thou takest the road it will be with this be too early late enough for have learn to read and to write english little at the madrissah shall soon be altogether sahib hear he laugh mahbub look at the little drenched figure dance in the wet salaam sahib
and he salute
ironically well art tired of the road or wilt thou come on to umballa with and work back with the horse come with thee mahbub ali chapter viii something owe to the soil that grow more to the life that feed but most to allah who give two separate side to my head would go without shirt or shoe friend tobacco or bread soon than for an instant lose either side of my head then in god ’s name take blue for red say mahbub allude to the hindu colour of kim ’s disreputable turban kim counter with the old proverb will change my faith and my bedding but thou must pay for it the dealer laugh till he nearly fall from his horse at shop on the outskirt of the city the change be make and kim stand up externally at least mohammedan mahbub hire room over against the railway station send for cooked meal of the fine with the almond curd sweet meat balushai we call it and fine chop lucknow tobacco this be well than some other meat that eat with the sikh say kim grin as he squat and assuredly they give no such victual at my madrissah have desire to hear of that same madrissah mahbub stuff himself with great bolus of spiced mutton fry in fat with cabbage and golden brown onion but tell first altogether and truthfully the manner of thy escape for friend of all the world,”—he loose his crack belt—“i do not think it be often that sahib and the son of sahib run away from there how should they they do not know the land it be nothing say kim and begin his tale when he come to the disguisement and the interview with the girl in the bazar mahbub ali ’s gravity go from he he laugh aloud and beat his hand on his thigh shabash
oh well do little one what will the healer of turquoise say to this now slowly let we hear what befall afterwards step by step omit nothing step by step then kim tell his adventure between cough as the full flavour tobacco catch his lung say growl mahbub ali to himself say it be the pony break out to play polo the fruit be ripe already except that he must learn his distance and his pacing and his rod and his compass listen now have turn aside the colonel ’s whip from thy skin and that be no small service kim pull serenely that be true but it be not to be think that this run out and in be any way good it be my holiday hajji be slave for many week why should not run away when the school be shut look too how live upon my friend or work for my bread as do with the sikh have save the colonel sahib great expense mahbub ’s lip twitch under his well prune mohammedan moustache what be few rupees”—the pathan throw out his open hand
carelessly—“to the colonel sahib he spend they for purpose not in any way for love of thee that say kim slowly know very long time ago who tell the colonel sahib himself not in those many word but plainly enough for one who be not altogether mud head yea he tell in the te rain when we go down to lucknow be it so then will tell thee more friend of all the world though in the telling lend thee my head it be forfeit to say kim with deep relish in umballa when thou didst pick up on the horse after the drummer boy beat speak little plain all the world may tell lie save thou and i. for equally be thy life forfeit to if choose to raise my finger here and this be know to also say kim readjust the live charcoal ball on the weed it be very sure tie between we indeed thy hold be sure even than mine for who would miss boy beat to death or it may be throw into well by the roadside most people here and in simla and across the pass behind the hill would on the other hand say what have come to mahbub ali if he be find dead among his horse surely too the colonel sahib would make inquiry but again,”—kim face pucker with cunning,—“he would not make overlong inquiry lest people should ask what have this colonel sahib to do with that horse dealer but if live as thou wouldst surely die it may be but say if live and alone would know that one have come by night as common thief perhaps to mahbub ali ’s bulkhead in the serai and there have slay he either before or after that thief have make full search into his saddlebag and between the sol of his slipper be that news to tell to the colonel or would he say to me—(i have not forget when he send back for cigar case that he have not leave behind him)—‘what be mahbub ali to up go gout of heavy smoke there be long pause then mahbub ali speak in admiration and with these thing on thy mind dost thou lie down and rise again among all the sahib little son at the madrissah and meekly take instruction from thy teacher it be an order say kim blandly who be to dispute an order most finish son of eblis say mahbub ali but what be this tale of the thief and the search that which see say kim the night that my lama and lie next thy place in the kashmir seral the door be leave unlocked which think be not thy custom mahbub he come in as one assure that thou wouldst not soon return my eye be against knot hole in the plank he search as it be for something not rug not stirrup nor bridle nor brass pot something little and most carefully hide else why do he prick with an iron between the sol of thy slipper ha mahbub ali smile gently and see these thing what tale didst thou fashion to thyself well of the truth put my hand upon my amulet which lie always next to my skin and remember the pedigree of white stallion that have bite out of piece of mussalmani bread go away to umballa perceive that heavy trust be lie upon at that hour have choose thy head be forfeit it need only to say to that man have here paper concern horse which can not read and then kim peer at mahbub under his eyebrow then thou wouldst have drunk water twice perhaps thrice afterwards do not think more than thrice say mahbub simply it be true think of that little
but most think that love thee mahbub therefore go to umballa as thou know
but and this thou dost not know lie hide in the garden grass to see what colonel creighton sahib might do upon read the white stallion ’s pedigree and what do he for kim have bite off the conversation
dost thou give news for love or dost thou sell it
kim ask sell and buy mahbub take four anna piece out of his belt and hold it up eight say kim mechanically follow the huckster instinct of the east mahbub laugh and put away the coin it be too easy to deal in that market friend of all the world tell for love our life lie in each other ’s hand very good see the jang lat sahib the commander in chief come to big dinner see he in creighton sahib ’s office see the two read the white stallion ’s pedigree hear the very order give for the opening of great war mahbub nod with deep eye afire the game be well play that war be do now and the evil we hope nip before the flower thank to and thee what didst thou later make the news as it be hook to catch victual and honour among the villager in village whose priest drug my lama but bear away the old man ’s purse and the brahmin find nothing so next morning he be angry
ho ho and also use the news when fall into the hand of that white regiment with their bull that be foolishness mahbub scowl news be not mean to be throw about like dung cake but use sparingly like bhang so think now and moreover it do no sort of good but that be very long ago he make as to brush it all away with thin brown hand—“and since then and especially in the night under the punkah at the madrissah have think very greatly be it permit to ask whither the heaven bear ’s think might have lead say mahbub with an elaborate sarcasm smooth his scarlet beard it be permit say kim and throw back the very tone they say at nucklao that no sahib must tell black man that he have make fault mahbub ’s hand shoot into his bosom for to call pathan black man kala admi be blood insult then he remember and laugh speak sahib thy black man hear but say kim be not sahib and say make fault to curse thee mahbub ali on that day at umballa when think be betray by pathan be senseless for be but newly catch and wish to kill that low caste drummer boy say now hajji that it be well do and see my road all clear before to good service will stay in the madrissah till be ripe well say especially be distance and number and the manner of use compass to be learn in that game
one wait in the hill above to show thee will learn their teaching upon condition that my time be give to without question when the madrissah be shut ask that for of the colonel but why not ask the colonel in the sahib tongue the colonel be the servant of the government he be send hither and yon at word and must consider his own advancement see how much have already learn at nucklao moreover the colonel know since three month only have know one mahbub ali for six year to the madrissah will go at the madrissah will learn in the madrissah will be sahib but when the madrissah be shut then must be free and go among my people otherwise die and who be thy people friend of all the world this great and beautiful land say kim wave his paw round the little clay walled room where the oil lamp in its niche burn heavily through the tobacco smoke and far would see my lama again and far need money that be the need of everyone say mahbub ruefully will give thee eight anna for much money be not pick out of horse hoof and it must suffice for many day as to all the rest be well pleased and no further talk be nee make haste to learn and in three year or it may be less thou wilt be an aid even to have be such hindrance till now say kim with boy ’s giggle do not give answer mahbub grunt thou art my new horse boy go and bed among my man they be near the north end of the station with the horse they will beat to the south end of the station if come without authority mahbub feel in his belt wet his thumb on cake of chinese ink and dab the impression on piece of soft native paper from balkh to bombay man know that rough ridged print with the old scar run diagonally across it that be enough to show my headman come in the morning by which road say kim by the road from the city there be
but one
and then we return to creighton sahib have save thee beat what be beat when the very head be loose on the shoulder kim slide out quietly into the night walk half round the house keep close to the wall and head away from the station for mile or so then fetch wide compass he work back at leisure for he need time to invent story if any of mahbub ’s retainer ask question they be camp on piece of waste ground beside the railway and be native have not of course unload the two truck in which mahbub ’s animal stand among consignment of country breed buy by the bombay tram company the headman break down consumptive look mohammedan promptly challenge kim but be pacify at sight of mahbub ’s sign manual the hajji have of his favour give service say kim testily if this be doubt wait till he come in the morning meantime place by the fire follow the usual aimless babble that every low caste native must raise on every occasion it die down and kim lie out behind the little knot of mahbub ’s follower almost under the wheel of horse truck borrow blanket for cover now bed among brickbat and ballast refuse on damp night between overcrowded horse and unwashed baltis would not appeal to many white boy but kim be utterly happy change of scene service and surrounding be the breath of his little nostril and thinking of the neat white cot of st xavier all arow under the punkah give he joy as keen as the repetition of the multiplication table in english be very old he think sleepily every month become year more old be very young and fool to boot when take mahbub ’s message to umballa even when be with that white regiment be very young and small and have no wisdom but now learn every day and in three year the colonel will take out of the madrissah and let go upon the road with mahbub hunt for horse pedigree or maybe shall go by myself or maybe shall find the lama and go with he yes that be good to walk again as chela with my lama when he come back to benare the thought come more slowly and disconnectedly he be plunge into beautiful dreamland when his ear catch whisper thin and sharp above the monotonous babble round the fire it come from behind the iron skin horse truck he be not here then where should he be but roystere in the city who look for rat in frog pond come away he be not our man he must not go back beyond the pass second time it be the order hire some woman to drug he it be few rupee only and there be no evidence except the woman it must be more certain and remember the price upon his head
but the police have long arm and we be far from the border if it be in peshawur now yes in peshawur the second voice sneer peshawur full of his blood kin full of bolt hole and woman behind whose clothe he will hide
yes peshawur or jehannum would suit we equally well then what be the plan fool have not tell it hundred time wait till he come to lie down
and then one sure shoot the truck be between we and pursuit we have but to run back over the line and go our way they will not see whence the shot come wait here at least till the dawn what manner of faquir art thou to shiver at little watch
oho think kim behind close shut eye once again it be mahbub indeed white stallion ’s pedigree be not good thing to peddle to sahib or maybe mahbub have be sell other news now what be to do kim know not where mahbub house and if he come here before the dawn they will shoot he that would be no profit for thee kim and this be not matter for the police that would be no profit for mahbub and”—he giggle almost aloud—“i do not remember any lesson at nucklao which will help here be kim and yonder be they first then kim must wake and go away so that they shall not suspect bad dream wake man thus he throw the blanket off his face and raise himself suddenly with the terrible bubble meaningless yell of the asiatic rouse by nightmare urr urr urr urr ya la la la la narain the churel the churel churel be the peculiarly malignant ghost of woman who have die in child bed she haunt lonely road her foot be turn backwards on the ankle
and she lead man to torment loud rise kim ’s quavering howl till at last he leap to his foot and stagger off sleepily while the camp curse he for wake they some twenty yard far up the line he lie down again take care that the whisperer should hear his grunt and groan as he recompose himself after few minute he roll towards the road and steal away into the thick darkness he paddle along swiftly till he come to culvert and drop behind it his chin on level with the cop stone here he could command all the night traffic himself unseen two or three cart pass jingle out to the suburb cough policeman and hurry foot passenger or two who sing to keep off evil spirit then rap the shod foot of horse this be more like mahbub think kim as the beast shy at the little head above the culvert ohé mahbub ali he whisper have care the horse be rein back almost on its haunch and force towards the culvert never again say mahbub will take shod horse for night work they pick up all the bone and nail in the city he stoop to lift its forefoot and that bring his head within foot of kim ’s down keep down he mutter the night be full of eye two man wait thy come behind the horse truck they will shoot thee at thy lie down because there be price on thy head hear sleep near the horse didst thou see they hold still sire of devil this furiously to the horse no be one dress belike as faquir one say to the other what manner of faquir art thou to shiver at little watch good go back to the camp and lie down do not die tonight mahbub wheel his horse and vanish kim tear back down the ditch till he reach point opposite his second rest place slip across the road like weasel and re coil himself in the blanket at least mahbub know he think contentedly and certainly he speak as one expect it do not think those two man will profit by tonight ’s watch an hour pass and with the good will in the world to keep awake all night he sleep deeply now and again night train roar along the metal within twenty foot of he but he have all the oriental ’s indifference to mere noise and it do not even weave dream through his slumber mahbub be anything but asleep it annoy he vehemently that people outside his tribe and unaffected by his casual amours should pursue he for the life his first and natural impulse be to cross the line low down work up again and catch his well wisher from behind summarily slay they here he reflect with sorrow another branch of the government totally unconnected with colonel creighton might demand explanation which would be hard to supply and he know that south of the border perfectly ridiculous fuss be make about corpse or so he have not be trouble in this way since he send kim to umballa with the message and hop that suspicion have be finally divert then
most brilliant notion strike he the english do eternally tell the truth he say therefore we of this country be eternally make foolish by allah will tell the truth to an englishman of what use be the government police if poor kabuli be rob of his horse in their very truck this be as bad as peshawur should lay complaint at the station well still some young sahib on the railway they be zealous and if they catch thief it be remember to their honour he tie up his horse outside the station and stride on to the platform hullo mahbub ali say young assistant district traffic superintendent who be wait to go down the line tall tow haired horsey youth in dingy white linen what be you do here sell weed eh no be not troubled for my horse come to look for lutuf ullah have truck load up the line could anyone take they out without the railway ’s knowledge should not think so mahbub you can claim against we if they do have see two man crouch under the wheel of one of the truck nearly all night faquir do not steal horse so give they no more think would find lutuf ullah my partner the deuce you do and you do not bother your head about it pon my word it just almost as well that meet you what be they like eh they be only faquir they will no more than take little grain perhaps from one of the truck there be many up the line the state will never miss the dole come here seek for my partner lutuf ullah never mind your partner where be your horse truck little to this side of the farth place where they make lamp for the train the signal box yes and upon the rail near to the road upon the right hand side look up the line thus but as regard lutuf ullah tall man with break nose and persian greyhound aie the boy have hurry off to wake up young and enthusiastic policeman for as he say the railway have suffer much from depredation in the good yard mahbub ali chuckle in his dye beard they will walk in their boot make noise
and then they will wonder why there be no faquir they be very clever boy barton sahib and young sahib he wait idly for few minute expect to see they hurry up the line girt for action light engine slide through the station and he catch glimpse of young barton in the cab do that child an injustice he be not altogether fool say mahbub ali to take fire carriage for thief be new game when mahbub ali come to his camp in the dawn no one think it worth while to tell he any news of the night no one at least but one small horseboy newly advanced to the great man ’s service whom mahbub call to his tiny tent to assist in some packing it be all know to whisper kim bend above saddlebag two sahib come up on te train be run to and fro in the dark on this side of the truck as the te train move up and down slowly they fall upon two man sit under this truck hajji what shall do with this lump of tobacco wrap it in paper and put it under the salt bag
yes and strike they down but one man strike at sahib with faquir ’s buck ’s horn kim mean the conjoined black buck horn which be faquir ’s sole temporal weapon)—“the blood come
so the other sahib first smite his own man senseless smite the stabber with short gun which have roll from the first man ’s hand they all rag as though mad together mahbub smile with heavenly resignation
no that be not so much dewanee madness or case for the civil court the word can be pun upon both way as nizamut criminal case gun sayest thou ten good year in jail then they both lie still but think they be nearly dead when they be put on the te train their head move thus and there be much blood on the line come and see have see blood before jail be the sure place and assuredly they will give false name and assuredly no man will find they for long time they be unfriend of mine thy fate and mine seem on one string what tale for the healer of pearl now swiftly with the saddle bag and the cooking platter we will take out the horse and away to simla swiftly as oriental understand speed with long explanation with abuse and windy talk carelessly amid hundred check for little thing forget the untidy camp break up and lead the half dozen stiff and fretful horse along the kalka road in the fresh of the rain sweep dawn kim regard as mahbub ali ’s favourite by all who wish to stand well with the pathan be not call upon to work they stroll on by the easy of stage halt every few hour at wayside shelter very many sahib travel along the kalka road and as mahbub ali say every young sahib must need esteem himself judge of horse and though he be over head in debt to the money lender must make as if to buy that be the reason that sahib after sahib roll along in stage carriage would stop and open talk some would even descend from their vehicle and feel the horse leg ask inane question or through sheer ignorance of the vernacular grossly insult the imperturbable trader when first deal with sahib and that be when colonel soady sahib be governor of fort abazai and flood the commissioner ’s camping ground for spite mahbub confide to kim as the boy fill his pipe under tree do not know how greatly they be fool and this make wroth as thus and he tell kim tale of an expression misuse in all innocence that double kim up with mirth now see however,”—he exhale smoke slowly—“that it be with they as with all man in certain matter they be wise and in other most foolish very foolish it be to use the wrong word to stranger for though the heart may be clean of offence how be the strange to know that he be more like to search truth with dagger true talk say kim solemnly fool speak of cat when woman be bre to bed for instance have hear they therefore in one situate as thou art it particularly behove thee to remember this with both kind of face among sahib never forget thou art sahib among the folk of hind always remember thou art he pause with puzzled smile what be mussalman hindu jain or buddhist that be hard knot thou art beyond question an unbeliever and therefore thou wilt be damn so say my law or think it do but thou art also my little friend of all the world and love thee so say my heart this matter of creed be like horseflesh the wise man know horse be good that there be profit to be make from all and for myself but that be good sunni and hate the man of tirah could believe the same of all the faith now manifestly kathiawar mare take from the sand of her birthplace and remove to the west of bengal founder nor be even balkh stallion and there be no well horse than those of balkh be they not so heavy in the shoulder of any account in the great northern desert beside the snow camel have see therefore say in my heart the faith be like the horse each have merit in its own country but my lama say altogether different thing
oh he be an old dreamer of dream from bhotiyal my heart be little angry friend of all the world that thou shouldst see such worth in man so little know it be true hajji but that worth do see and to he my heart be draw and his to thine hear heart be like horse they come and they go against bit or spur shout gul sher khan yonder to drive in that bay stallion ’s picket more firmly we do not want horse fight at every rest stage and the dun and the black will be lock in little now hear be it necessary to the comfort of thy heart to see that lama it be one part of my bond say kim if do not see he and eye be blue and their nail be blacken with low caste blood many of they son of mehteeranees_—brother in law to the bhungi we need not follow the rest of the pedigree but kim make his little point clearly and without heat chew piece of sugar cane the while friend of all the world say mahbub push over the pipe for the boy to clean have meet many man woman and boy and not few sahib have never in didst thou not tell before
oh he will go to jail and be safe for some year there be no need to tell more than be necessary at any one time besides do not then need money for sweetmeat allah kerim say mahbub ah wilt thou some day sell my head for few sweetmeat if the fit take thee kim will remember till he die that long lazy journey from umballa through kalka and the pinjore garden near by up to simla sudden spate in the gugger river sweep down one horse the most valuable be sure and nearly drown kim among the dance boulder far up the road the horse be stampede by government elephant and be in high condition of grass food it cost day and half to get they together again then they meet sikandar khan come down with few unsaleable screw remnant of his string and mahbub who have more of horse cop in his little fingernail than sikandar khan in all his tent must need buy two of the bad and that mean eight hour laborious diplomacy and untold tobacco
but it be all pure delight the wander road climb dip and sweep about the grow spur the flush of the morning lie along the distant snow the branch cacti tier upon tier on the stony hillside the voice of thousand water channel the chatter of the monkey the solemn deodar climb one after another with down droop branch the vista of the plain roll out far beneath they the incessant twanging of the tonga horn and the wild rush of the lead horse when tonga swing round curve the halt for prayer mahbub be very religious in dry washing and bellow when time do not press the evening conference by the halting place when camel and bullock chew solemnly together and the stolid driver tell the news of the road all these thing lift kim ’s heart to song within he but when the singing and dancing be do say mahbub ali come the colonel sahib ’s
and that be not so sweet fair land most beautiful land be this of hind and the land of the five river be fair than all kim half chant into it will go again if mahbub ali or the colonel lift hand or foot against once go who shall find look hajji be yonder the city of simla allah what city my father ’s brother
and he be an old man when mackerson sahib ’s well be new at peshawur could recall when there be but two house in it he lead the horse below the main road into the low simla bazar

over country with compass and level and straight eye carry away picture of that country which might be sell for large sum in coin silver but as it be occasionally inexpedient to carry about measure chain boy would do well to know the precise length of his own foot pace so that when he be deprive of what hurree chunder call adventitious aid he might still tread his distance to keep count of thousand of pace hurree chunder ’s experience have show he nothing more valuable than rosary of eighty one or hundred and eight bead for it be divisible and sub divisible into many multiple and sub multiple through the volleying drift of english kim catch the general trend of the talk and it interest he very much
here be new craft that man could tuck away in his head and by the look of the large wide world unfold itself before he it seem that the more man know the well for he say the babu when he have talk for an hour and half hope some day to enjoy your offeecial acquaintance ad interim if may be pardon that expression shall give you this betel box which be highly valuable article and cost two rupee only four year ago it be cheap heart shape brass thing with three compartment for carry the eternal betel nut lime and pan_-leaf but it be fill with little tabloid bottle that be reward of merit for your performance in character of that holy man you see you be so young you think you will last for ever and not take care of your body it be great nuisance to go sick in the middle of business be fond of drug myself and they be handy to cure poor people too these be good departmental drug quinine and so on give it you for souvenir now good bye have urgent private business here by the roadside he slip out noiselessly as cat on the umballa road hail pass cart and jingle away while kim tongue tie twiddle the brass betel box in his hand the record of boy ’s education interest few save his parent and as you know kim be an orphan it be write in the book of st xavier in partibus that report of kim ’s progress be forward at the end of each term to colonel creighton and to father victor from whose hand duly come the money for his schooling it be far record in the same book that he show great aptitude for mathematical study as well as map make and carry away prize the life of lord lawrence tree calf two vol nine rupee eight annas for proficiency therein and the same term play in st xavier ’s eleven against the alighur mohammedan college his age be fourteen year and ten month he be also re vaccinate from which we may assume that there have be another epidemic of smallpox at lucknow about the same time pencil note on the edge of an old muster roll record that he be punish several time for converse with improper person and it seem that he be once sentence to heavy pain for absent himself for day in the company of street beggar that be when he get over the gate and plead with the lama through whole day down the bank of the gumti to accompany he on the road next holiday for one month for little week and the lama set his face as flint against it aver that the time have not yet come kim ’s business say the old man as they eat cake together be to get all the wisdom of the sahib and then he would see the hand of friendship must in some way have avert the whip of calamity for six week later kim seem to have pass an examination in elementary surveying with great credit his age be fifteen year and eight month from this date the record be silent his name do not appear in the year ’s batch of those who enter for the subordinate survey of india but against it stand the word remove on appointment several time in those three year cast up at the temple of the tirthankar in benare the lama little thin and shade yellow if that be possible but gentle and untainted as ever sometimes it be from the south that he come from south of tuticorin whence the wonderful fire boat go to ceylon where be priest who know pali sometimes it be from the wet green west and the thousand cotton factory chimney that ring bombay and once from the north where he have double back eight hundred mile to talk for day with the keeper of the image in the wonder house he would stride to his cell in the cool cut marble the priest of the temple be good to the old man,—wash off the dust of travel make prayer and depart for lucknow well accustom now to the way of the rail in third class carriage return it be noticeable as his friend the seeker point out to the head priest that he cease for while to mourn the loss of his river or to draw wondrous picture of the wheel of life but prefer to talk of the beauty and wisdom of certain mysterious chela whom no man of the temple have ever see yes he have follow the trace of the bless foot throughout all india the curator have still in his possession most marvellous account of his wandering and meditation there remain nothing more in life but to find the river of the arrow yet it be show to he in dream that it be matter not to be undertake with any hope of success unless that seeker have with he the one chela appoint to bring the event to happy issue and verse in great wisdom such wisdom as white haired keeper of image possess for example here come out the snuff gourd and the kindly jain priest make haste to be silent long and long ago when devadatta be king of benare let all listen to the jâtaka!_—an elephant be capture for time by the king ’s hunter and ere he break free beringe with grievous legiron this he strove to remove with hate and frenzy in his heart and hurry up and down the forest beseech his brother elephant to wrench it asunder one by one with their strong trunk they try and fail at the last they give it as their opinion that the ring be not to be break by any bestial power and in thicket new bear wet with moisture of birth lie day old calf of the herd whose mother have die the fettered elephant forget his own agony say if do not help this suckle it will perish under our foot so he stand above the young thing make his leg buttress against the uneasily move herd and he beg milk of virtuous cow and the calf throve and the ringed elephant be the calf ’s guide and defence now the day of an elephant let all listen to the jâtaka!_—are thirty five year to his full strength and through thirty five rain the ring elephant befriend the young and all the while the fetter eat into the flesh then one day the young elephant see the half bury iron and turn to the elder say what be this it be even my sorrow say he who have befriend he then that other put out his trunk and in the twinkling of an eyelash abolish the ring say the appoint time have come so the virtuous elephant who have wait temperately and do kind act be relieve at the appoint time by the very calf whom he have turn aside to cherish let all listen to the jâtaka for the elephant be ananda and the calf that break the ring be none other than the lord himself then he would shake his head benignly and over the ever click rosary point out how free that elephant calf be from the sin of pride he be as humble as chela who see his master sit in the dust outside the gate of learn over leap the gate though they be lock and take his master to his heart in the presence of the proud stomached city rich would be the reward of such master and such chela when the time come for they to seek freedom together
so do the lama speak come and go across india as softly as bat sharp tongue old woman in house among the fruit tree behind saharunpore honour he as the woman honour the prophet but his chamber be by no mean upon the wall in an apartment of the forecourt overlook by coo dove he would sit while she lie aside her useless veil and chatter of spirit and fiend of kulu of grandchild unborn and of the free tongue brat who have talk to she in the rest place once too he stray alone from the grand trunk road below umballa to the very village whose priest have try to drug he but the kind heaven that guard lama send he at twilight through the crop absorb and unsuspicious to the rissaldar ’s door here be like to have be grave misunderstanding for the old soldier ask he why the friend of the star have go that way only six day before that may not be say the lama he have go back to his own people he sit in that corner tell hundred merry tale five night ago his host insist true he vanish somewhat suddenly in the dawn after foolish talk with my granddaughter he grow apace
but he be the same friend of the star as bring true word of the war have ye part
yes and no the lama reply
we we have not altogether part
but the time be not ripe that we should take the road together he acquire wisdom in another place we must wait all one but if it be not the boy how do he come to speak so continually of thee and what say he ask the lama eagerly sweet word an hundred thousand that thou art his father and mother and such all pity that he do not take the qpeen ’s service he be fearless this news amaze the lama who do not then know how religiously kim keep to the contract make with mahbub ali and perforce ratify by colonel creighton there be no hold the young pony from the game say the horse dealer when the colonel point out that vagabond over india in holiday time be absurd if permission be refuse to go and come as he choose he will make light of the refusal then who be to catch he colonel sahib only once in thousand year be horse bear so well fit for the game as this our colt and we need man chapter your tiercel too long at hack sire he no eyass but passage hawk that foot ere we catch he dangerously free the air faith be he mine as mine the glove he bind to for his tiring ’d fly he with make hawk he in yarak plume to the very point so man so weathered give he the firmament god make he for and what shall take the air of him?—_old play lurgan sahib do not use as direct speech but his advice tally with mahbub ’s and the upshot be good for kim he know

there kim as mahbub admit earn his keep and perhaps little over by spend four curious day as scullion in the house of fat commissariat sergeant from whose office box in an auspicious moment he remove little vellum ledger which he copy out it seem to deal entirely with cattle and camel sale by moonlight lie behind an outhouse all through one hot night then he return the ledger to its place and at mahbub ’s word leave that service unpaid rejoin he six mile down the road the clean copy in his bosom that soldier be small fish mahbub ali explain but in time we shall catch the large one he only sell oxen at two price one for himself and one for the government which do not think be sin why could not take away the little book and be do with it then he would have be frighten and he would have tell his master then we should miss perhaps great number of new rifle which seek their way up from quetta to the north the game be so large that one see but little at time

he sit for the most part in front of roar wood fire jakko road be four foot deep in snow that year and the small hindu have go away to be marry help lurgan to thread pearl he make kim learn whole when you administer they and in the evening he write charm on parchment elaborate pentagram crown with the name of devil murra and awan the companion of king all fantastically write in the corner more to the point he advise kim as to the care of his own body the cure of fever fit and simple remedy of the road week before it be time to go down colonel creighton sahib this be unfair send kim write colonel order he to make map of that wild walled city and since mohammedan horse boy and pipe tender be not expect to drag survey chain round the capital of an independent native state kim be force to colour cake and three brush he achieve something not remotely unlike the city of jeysulmir mahbub laugh great deal and advise he to make up write report as well and in the back of the big account book mean that you may be member of the sit bhai_—the seven brother which be hindi and it be popularly suppose to be extinct society but have write note to show it be still extant you see it be all my invention verree good sit bhai have many member and perhaps before they jolly well cut your throat they may give you just chance of life that be useful anyhow
and moreover these foolish native if they be not too excited they always stop to think before they kill man who say he belong to any speecific organization you see you say then when you be in tight place be son of the charm and you get perhaps ah your second wind that be only in extreme instance or to open negotiation with stranger can you quite see verree good but suppose now or any one of the department come to you dress quite different you would not know at all unless choose bet you some day will prove it come as ladakhi trader
oh anything and say to you you want to buy precious stone you say do look like man who buy precious stone then say even verree poor man can buy turquoise or tarkeean that be kichree_—vegetable curry say kim of course it be you say let see the tarkeean then say it be cook by woman and perhaps it be bad for your caste then you say there be no caste when man go to look for tarkeean you stop little between those word to look that be thee whole secret the little stop before the word kim repeat the test sentence that be all right then will show you my turquoise if there be time

people mock to scorn so tis not with juggler bear pinch of dust or withered flower chance fling fruit or borrow staff serve his need and shore his power bind the spell or loose the laugh but man who etc op follow sudden natural reaction now be alone all alone he think in all india be no one so alone as if die today who shall bring the news and to whom if live and god be good there will be price upon my head for be son of the charm kim very few white people but many asiatic can throw themselves into mazement as it be by repeat their own name over and over again to themselves let the mind go free upon speculation as to what be call personal identity when one grow old the power usually depart but while it last it may descend upon man at any moment who be kim kim kim he squat in corner of the clang waiting room rapt from all other think hand fold in lap and pupil contract to pin point in minute in another half second he feel he would arrive at the solution of the tremendous puzzle but whither goest thou toward kashi benare there be no god there have prove they go to prayag allahabad for the fifth time seek the road to enlightenment of what faith art thou too be seeker say kim use one of the lama ’s pet word though”—he forget his northern dress for the moment—“though allah alone knoweth what seek the old fellow slip the bairagi crutch under his armpit and sit down on patch of ruddy leopard ’s skin as kim rise at the call for the benare train go in hope little brother he say it be long road to the foot of the one but thither
do we all travel kim do not feel so lonely after this and ere he have sit out twenty mile in the crowded compartment be cheer his neighbour with string of most wonderful yarn about his own and his master ’s magical gift benare strike he as peculiarly filthy city though it be pleasant to find how his cloth be respect at least one third of the population pray eternally to some group or other of the many million deity and so revere every sort of holy man kim be guide to the temple of the tirthankar about mile outside the city near sarnath by chance meet punjabi farmer kamboh from jullundur way who have appeal in vain to every god of his homestead to cure his small son and be try benare as last resort thou art from the north he ask shoulder through the press of the narrow stinking street much like his own pet bull at home
ay know the punjab my mother be pahareen
but my father come from amritzar by jandiala say kim oil his ready tongue for the need of the road jandiala jullundur oho then we be neighbour in some sort as it be he nod tenderly to the wail child in his arm whom dost thou serve most holy man at the temple of the tirthanker they be all most holy and most greedy say the jat with bitterness have walk the pillar and tread the temple till my foot be flay and the child be no whit well and the mother be sick too hush then little one we change his name when the fever come we put he into girl ’s clothe there be nothing we do not do except say to his mother when she bundle off to benare she should have come with say sakhi sarwar sultan would serve we good we know his generosity but these down country god be strange the child turn on the he good mind at least say the father proudly to think he should have listen so cleverly yonder be thy temple now be poor man many priest have deal with but my son be my son and if gift to thy master can cure he be at my very wit end kim consider for while tingle with pride three year ago he would have make prompt profit on the situation and go his way without thought but now the very respect the jat pay he prove that he be man moreover he have taste fever once or twice already and know enough to recognize starvation when he see it call he forth and will give he bond on my good yoke so that wonderful convention crowd with hundred of little figure whose every line carry meaning few can translate the picture parable there be not twenty in all the world who can draw it surely without copy please he to curl himself up into the sudden sleep of old age kim watch the last dusty sunshine fade out of the court and play with his ghost dagger and rosary the clamour of benare old of all earth ’s city awake before the god day and night beat round the wall as the sea ’s roar round breakwater now and again jain priest cross the court with some small offering to the image and sweep the path about he lest by chance he should take the life of live thing lamp twinkle and there follow the sound of prayer kim watch the star as they rise one after another in the still sticky dark till he fall asleep at the foot of the altar that night he dream in hindustani with never an english word holy one there be the child to whom we give the medicine he say about three o’clock in the morning when the lama also wake from dream would have fare forth on pilgrimage the jat will be here at the light be well answer in my haste would have do wrong he sit down on the cushion and return to his rosary surely old folk be as child he say pathetically they desire matter behold it must be do at once
or they fret and weep many time when be upon the road have be ready to stamp with my foot at the hindrance of an ox cart in the way or mere cloud of dust it be not so when be man long time ago none the less it be wrongful but thou art indeed old holy one the thing be do cause be put out into the world and old or young sick or sound know or unknowing who can rein in the effect of that cause do the wheel hang still if child spin it or drunkard chela this be great and terrible world think it good kim yawn what be there to eat have not eat since yesterday even have forget thy need yonder be good bhotiyal tea and cold rice we can not walk far on such stuff kim feel all the european ’s lust for flesh meat which be not accessible in jain temple yet instead of go out at once with the begging bowl he stay his stomach on slab of cold rice till the full dawn it bring the farmer voluble stuttering with gratitude in the night the fever break and the sweat come he cry feel here his skin be fresh and new he esteem the salt lozenge and take milk with greed he draw the cloth from the child ’s face
and it smile sleepily at kim little knot of jain priest silent but all observant gather by the temple door they know
and kim know that they know how the old lama have meet his disciple be courteous folk they have not obtrude themselves overnight by presence word or gesture wherefore kim repay they as the sun rise thank the god of the jains brother he say not know how those god be name the fever be indeed break the lama beam in the background upon his host of three year be there ever such chela he follow our lord the healer now the jains officially recognize all the god of the hindu creed as well as the lingam and the snake they wear the brahminical thread they adhere to every claim of hindu caste law
but because they know and love the lama because he be an old man because he seek the way because he be their guest and because he collogue long of night with the head priest as free think metaphysician as ever split one hair into seventy they murmur assent remember,”—kim bend over the child—. this trouble may come again not if thou hast the proper spell say the father but in little while we go away true say the lama to all the jains we go now together upon the search whereof have often speak wait till my chela be ripe behold he we go north never again shall look upon this place of my rest people of good will but be not beggar the cultivator rise to his foot clutch the child be still do not trouble the holy one priest cry go kim whisper meet we again under the big railway bridge and for the sake of all the god of our punjab bring food curry pulse cake fry in fat and sweetmeat specially sweetmeat be swift the pallor of hunger suit kim very well as he stand tall and slim in his sand colour sweeping robe one hand on his rosary and the other in the attitude of benediction faithfully copy from the lama an english observer might have say that he look rather like the young saint of stain glass window whereas he be but grow lad faint with emptiness long and formal be the farewell thrice end and thrice renew the seeker he who have invite the lama to that haven from far away tibet silver face hairless ascetic take no part in it but meditate as always alone among the image the other be very human press small comfort upon the old man betel box fine new iron pencase food bag and such like warn he against the danger of the world without and prophesy happy end to the search meantime kim lonely than ever squat on the step and swear to himself in the language of st xavier ’s
but it be my own fault he conclude with mahbub eat mahbub ’s bread or lurgan sahib at st xavier ’s three meal day here must jolly well look out for myself besides be not in good training how could eat plate of beef now be it finish holy one the lama both hand raise intone final blessing in ornate chinese must lean on thy shoulder say he as the temple gate close we grow stiff think the weight of six foot man be not light to steady through mile of crowded street and kim load down with bundle and package for the way be glad to reach the shadow of the railway bridge here we eat he say resolutely as the kamboh blue rob and smile hove in sight basket in one hand and the child in the other fall to holy one he cry from fifty yard they be by the shoal under the first bridge span out of sight of hungry priest rice and good curry cake all warm and well scented with hing asafœtida curd and sugar king of my fields,”—this to the small son—“let we show these holy man that we jat of jullundur can pay service have hear the jains would eat nothing that they have not cook but truly”—he look away politely over the broad river—“where there be no eye there be no caste
and we say kim turn his back and heap leafplatter for the lama be beyond all caste they gorge themselves on the good food in silence nor till he have lick the last of the sticky sweetstuff from his little finger do kim note that the kamboh too be girt for travel if our road lie together he say roughly go with thee one do not often find worker of miracle and the child be still weak but be not altogether reed he pick up his lathi_—a five foot male bamboo ring with band of polished iron and flourish it in the air the jat be call quarrel some but that be not true except when we be cross we be like our own buffalo

to be where he be to look out upon the flat north western landscape and to talk to the change mob of fellow passenger even today ticket and ticket clipping be dark oppression to indian rustic they do not understand why when they have pay for magic piece of paper stranger should punch great piece out of the charm so long and furious be the debate between traveller and eurasian ticket collector kim into the compartment as the train be move off mean lean little person mahratta so far as kim could judge by the cock of the tight turban his face be cut his muslin upper garment be badly tear and one leg be bandage he tell they that country cart have upset and nearly slay he he be go to delhi where his son live kim watch he closely if as he assert he have be roll over and over on the earth there should have be sign of gravel rash on the skin
but all his injury seem clean cut and mere fall from cart could not cast man into such extremity of terror as with shake finger he knot up the tear cloth about his neck he lie bare an amulet of the kind call keeper up of the heart now amulet be common enough but they be not generally string on square plait copper wire and still few amulet bear black enamel on silver there be none except the kamboh and the lama in the compartment which luckily be of an old type with solid end kim make as to scratch in his bosom and thereby lift his own amulet the mahratta ’s face change altogether at the sight and he dispose the amulet fairly on his breast yes he go on to the kamboh be in haste and the cart drive by bastard bind its wheel in water cut and besides the harm do to there be lose full dish of tarkeean be not son of the charm lucky man that day that be great loss say the kamboh withdraw interest his experience of benare have make he suspicious who cook it say kim woman the mahratta raise his eye but all woman can cook tarkeean say the kamboh it be good curry as know
oh yes it be good curry say the mahratta and cheap say kim but what about caste
oh there be no caste where man go to look for tarkeean the mahratta reply in the prescribed cadence of whose service art thou of the service of this holy one kim point to the happy drowsy lama who wake with jerk at the well love word ah he be send from heaven to aid he be call the friend of all the world he be also call the friend of the star he walk as physician his time be ripe great
be his wisdom and son of the charm say kim under his breath as the kamboh make haste to prepare pipe lest the mahratta should beg and who be that the mahratta ask glance sideways nervously one whose child we have cure who lie under great debt to we sit by the window man from jullundur here be sick one have no desire to mix with chance meet wastrel my ear be not long be not woman wish to overhear secret the jat slide himself heavily into far corner art thou anything of healer be ten league deep in calamity cry the mahratta pick up the cue this man be cut and bruise all over go about to cure he kim retort none interfere between thy babe and be rebuke say the kamboh meekly be thy debtor for the life of my son thou art miracle worker know it show the cut kim bend over the mahratta ’s neck his heart nearly choke he for this be the great game with vengeance now tell thy tale swiftly brother while say charm come from the south where my work lie one of we they slew by the roadside hast thou hear kim shake his head he of course know nothing of ’s predecessor slay down south in the habit of an arab trader having find certain letter which be send to seek come away escape from the city and run to mhow so sure be that none know do not change my face at mhow woman bring charge against of theft of jewellery in that city which have leave then see the cry be out against run from mhow by night bribe the police who have be bribe to hand over without question to my enemy in the south then lie in old chitor city week penitent in temple but could not get rid of the letter which be my charge bury it under the queen ’s stone at chitor in the place know to we all kim do not know but not for world would he have break the thread at chitor
look you be all in king country for kotah to the east be beyond the queen ’s law and east again lie jaipur and gwalior neither love spy and there be no justice be hunt like wet jackal but break through at bandakui where hear there be charge against of murder in the city have leave of the murder of boy they have both the corpse and the witness wait but can not the government protect we of the game be beyond protection if we die we die our name be blot from the book that be all at bandakui where live one of we think to slip the scent by change my face and so make mahratta then come to agra and would have turn back to chitor to recover the letter so sure be have slip they therefore do not send tar telegram to any one say where the letter lay wish the credit of it all kim nod he understand that feel well but at agra walk in the street man cry debt against and approach with many witness would hale to the court then and
there oh they be clever in the south he recognize as his agent for cotton may he burn in hell for it and wast thou fool be the man they seek for the matter of the letter run into the flesher ward and come out by the house of the jew who fear riot and push forth come afoot to somna road have only money for my tikkut to delhi and there while lie in ditch with fever one spring out of the bush and beat and cut and search from head to foot within earshot of the te rain it be why do he not slay thee out of hand they be not so foolish if be take in delhi at the instance of lawyer upon prove charge of murder my body be hand over to the state that desire it go back guard and then die slowly for an example to the rest of we the south be not my country run in circle like goat with one eye have not eat for two day be marked”—he touch the filthy bandage on his leg—“so that they will know at delhi thou art safe in the te rain at least live year at the great game and tell that again the wire will be out against at delhi describe every tear and rag upon twenty hundred if need be will have see slay that boy and thou art useless kim know enough of native method of attack not to doubt that the case would be deadly complete even to the corpse the mahratta twitch his finger with pain from time to time the kamboh in his corner glare sullenly the lama be busy over his bead and kim fumble doctor
