we jat be all buffalo say the kamboh soften anew kim rub finger tip of bitterness on the child ’s trust little lip have ask for nothing he say sternly to the father except food dost thou grudge that go to heal another man have thy leave prince up fly the man ’s huge paw in supplication
nay nay do not mock thus it please to cure this sick one thou shalt acquire merit by aid what colour ash be there in thy pipe bowl that be auspicious be there raw turmeric among thy foodstuff open thy bundle it be the usual collection of small oddment bit of cloth quack medicine cheap fair clothful of atta_—greyish rough ground native flour twist of down country tobacco tawdry pipe stem and packet of curry stuff all wrap in quilt kim turn it over with the air of wise warlock mutter mohammedan invocation this be wisdom learn from the sahib he whisper to the lama and here when one think of his training at lurgan he speak no more than the truth there be great evil in this man ’s fortune as show by the star which which trouble he shall take it away friend of the star thou hast do well in all thing let it be at thy pleasure be it another heal quick be quick gasp the mahratta the train may stop healing against the shadow of death say kim mix the kamboh ’s flour with the mingle charcoal and tobacco ash in the red earth bowl of the pipe without word slip off his turban and shake down his long black hair that be my food priest the jat growl buffalo in the temple hast thou dare to look even thus far say kim must do mystery before fool but have care for thine eye be there film before they already save the babe and for return thou
oh shameless the man flinch at the direct gaze for kim be wholly in earnest shall curse thee or shall he pick up the outer cloth of the bundle and throw it over the bow head dare so much as to think wish to see and and even can not save thee be dumb be blind dumb forbear to curse co come child we will play game of hiding do not for my sake look from under the cloth see hope say e23 what be thy scheme this come next say kim pluck the thin body shirt e23 hesitate with all north west man ’s dislike of bar his body
what be caste to cut throat say kim rend it to the waist we must make thee yellow saddhu all over strip strip swiftly and shake thy hair over thine eye while scatter the ash now caste mark on thy forehead he draw from his bosom the little survey paint box and cake of crimson lake art thou only beginner say e23 labour literally for the dear life as he slide out of his body wrap and stand clear in the loin cloth while kim splash in noble caste mark on the ash smear brow
but two day enter to the game brother kim reply smear more ash on the bosom hast thou meet physician of sick pearl he switch out his long tight roll turban cloth and with swift hand roll it over and under about his loin into the intricate device of saddhu ’s cincture hah dost thou know his touch then he be my teacher for while we must bar thy leg ash cure wound smear it again be his pride once but thou art almost well the god be kind to we give that it be tin box of opium pill among the rubbish of the jat ’s bundle e23 gulp down half handful they be good against hunger fear and chill and they make the eye red too he explain now shall have heart to play the game we lack only saddhu ’s tong what of the old clothe kim roll they small and stuff they into the slack fold of his tunic with yellow ochre paint cake he smear the leg and the breast great streak against the background of flour ash and turmeric the blood on they be enough to hang thee brother maybe but no need to throw they out of the window it be finish his voice thrill with boy ’s pure delight in the game turn and look jat the god protect we say the hooded kamboh emerge like buffalo from the reed but whither go the mahratta what hast thou do kim have be train by lurgan sahib e23 by virtue of his business be no bad actor in place of the tremulous shrink trader there loll against the corner an all but naked ash smear ochre bar dusty haired saddhu his swollen eye opium take quick effect on an empty stomach luminous with insolence and bestial lust his leg cross under he kim ’s brown rosary round his neck and scant yard of worn flower chintz on his shoulder the child bury his face in his amazed father ’s arm look up princele we travel with warlock but they will not hurt thee
oh do not cry what be the sense of cure child one day and kill he with fright the next the child will be fortunate all his life he have see great healing when be child make clay man and horse have make they too sír banás he come in the night and make they all alive at the back of our kitchen midden pip the child and so thou art not frightened at anything eh prince be frightened because my father be frightened feel his arm shake
oh chicken man say kim and even the abashed jat laugh have do healing on this poor trader he must forsake his gain and his account book and sit by the wayside three night to overcome the malignity of his enemy the star be against he the few money lender the well say but saddhu or no saddhu he should pay for my stuff on his shoulder
but that be thy child on thy shoulder give over to the burn ghat not two day ago there remain one thing more do this charm in thy presence because need be great change his shape and his soul none the less if by any chance man from jullundur thou rememberest what thou hast see either among the elder sit under the village tree or in thine own house or in company of thy priest when he bless thy cattle murrain will come among the buffalo and fire in thy thatch and rat in the corn bin and the curse of our god upon thy field that they may be barren before thy foot and after thy ploughshare this be part of an old curse pick up from faquir by the taksali gate in the day of kim ’s innocence it lose nothing by repetition cease holy one in mercy cease cry the jat do not curse the household see nothing hear nothing be thy cow and he make to grab at kim ’s bare foot beat rhythmically on the carriage floor but since thou hast be permit to aid in the matter of pinch of flour and little opium and such trifle as have honour by use in my art so will the god return blessing and he give it at length to the man ’s immense relief it be one that he have learn from lurgan sahib the lama star through his spectacle as he have not star at the business of disguisement friend of the star he say at last thou hast acquire great wisdom beware that it do not give birth to pride no man have the law before his eye speak hastily of any matter which he have see or encounter no no

across and among the broad bloomful fruit garden by way of aminabad sahaigunge akrola of the ford and little phulesa the line of the siwaliks always to the north and behind they again the snow after long sweet sleep under the dry star come the lordly leisurely passage through wake village beg bowl hold forth in silence but eye rove in defiance of the law from sky ’s edge to sky ’s edge then would kim return soft footed through the soft dust to his master under the shadow of mango tree or the thin shade of white doon siris to eat and drink at ease at mid day after talk and little wayfaring they sleep meet the world refresh when the air be cool night find they adventure into new territory some choose village spy three hour before across the fat land and much discuss upon the road there they tell their tale new one each evening so far as kim be concern and there be they make welcome either by priest or headman after the custom of the kindly east when the shadow shorten and the lama lean more heavily upon kim there be always the wheel of life to draw forth to hold flat under wipe stone and with long straw to expound cycle by cycle here sit the god on high
and they be dream of dream here be our heaven and the world of the demi gods horseman fight among the hill here be the agony do upon the beast soul ascend or descend the ladder and therefore not to be interfere with here be the hell hot and cold and the abode of torment ghost let the chela study the trouble that come from over eat bloated stomach and burn bowel obediently then with bow head and brown finger alert to follow the pointer do the chela study
but when they come to the human world busy and profitless that be just above the hell his mind be distract for by the roadside trundle the very wheel itself eat drink trading marrying and quarrel all warmly alive often the lama make the live picture the matter of his text bid kim too ready note how the flesh take thousand shape desirable or detestable as man reckon but in truth of no account either way and how the stupid spirit bond slave to the hog the dove and the serpent lust after betel nut new yoke of oxen woman or the favour of king be bind to follow the body through all the heaven and all the hell and strictly round again sometimes woman or poor man watch the ritual it be nothing less when the great yellow chart be unfold would throw few flower or handful of cowry upon its edge it suffice these humble one that they have meet holy one who might be move to remember they in his prayer cure they if they be sick say the lama when kim ’s sporting instinct wake cure they if they have fever but by no means work charm remember what befall the mahratta then all do be evil kim reply lie out under big tree at the fork of the doon road watch the little ant run over his hand to abstain from action be well except to acquire merit at the gate of learn we be teach that to abstain from action be unbefitte sahib and be sahib friend of all the world,”—the lama look directly at kim—“i be an old man pleased with show as be child to those who follow the way there be neither black nor white hind nor bhotiyal we be all soul seek escape no matter what thy wisdom learn among sahib when we come to my river thou wilt be free from all illusion at my side my bone ache for that river as they ache in the te rain but my spirit sit above my bone wait the search be sure be answer be it permit to ask question the lama incline his stately head eat thy bread for three year as thou know holy one whence come there be much wealth as man count it in bhotiyal the lama return with composure in my own place have the illusion of honour ask for that need be not concern with the account that be for my monastery the black high seat in the monastery and novice all in order and he tell story trace with finger in the dust of the immense and sumptuous ritual of avalanche guard cathedral of procession and devil dance of the changing of monk and nun into swine of holy city fifteen thousand foot in the air of intrigue between monastery and monastery of voice among the hill and of that mysterious mirage that dance on dry snow he speak even of lhassa and of the dalai lama whom he have see and adore each long perfect day rise behind kim for barrier to cut he off from his race and his mother tongue he slip back to thinking and dream in the vernacular and mechanically follow the lama ’s ceremonial observance at eat drinking and the like the old man ’s mind turn more and more to his monastery as his eye turn to the steadfast snow his river trouble he nothing now and again indeed he would gaze long and long at tuft or twig expect he say the earth to cleave and deliver its blessing but he be content to be with his disciple at ease in the temperate wind that come down from the doon this be not ceylon nor buddh gaya nor bombay nor some grass tangle ruin that he seem to have stumble upon two year ago he speak of those place as scholar remove from vanity as seeker walk in humility as an old man wise and temperate illumine knowledge with brilliant insight bite by bit disconnectedly each tale call up by some wayside thing he speak of all his wander up and down hind till kim who have love he without reason now love he for fifty good reason so they enjoy themselves in high felicity abstain as the rule demand from evil word covetous desire not over eat not lie on high bed nor wear rich clothe their stomach tell they the time and the people bring they their food as the saying be they be lord of the village of aminabad sahaigunge akrola of the ford and little phulesa where kim give the soulless woman blessing but news travel fast in india and too soon shuffle across the crop land bear basket of fruit with box of kabul grape and gilt orange white whiskered servitor lean dry oorya beg they to bring the honour of their presence to his mistress distressed in her mind that the lama have neglect she so long now do remember”—the lama speak as though it be wholly new proposition she be virtuous but an inordinate talker kim be sit on the edge of cow ’s manger tell story to village smith ’s child she will only ask for another son for her daughter have not forget she he say let she acquire merit send word that we will come they cover eleven mile through the field in two day and be overwhelmed with attention at the end for the old lady hold fine tradition of hospitality to which she force her son in law who be under the thumb of his woman folk and buy peace by borrow of the money lender age have not weaken her tongue or her memory and from discreetly bar upper window in the hearing of not less than dozen servant she pay kim compliment that would have fling european audience into unclean dismay but thou art still the shameless beggar brat of the parao she shrill have not forget thee wash ye and eat the father of my daughter ’s son be go away awhile so we poor woman be dumb and useless for proof she harangue the entire household unsparingly till food and drink be bring and in the evening the smoke scent evening copper dun and turquoise across the field
it please she to order her palanquin to be set down in the untidy forecourt by smoky torchlight and there behind not too closely draw curtain she gossip have the holy one come alone should have receive he otherwise but with this rogue who can be too careful maharanee say kim choose as always the ample title be it my fault that none other than sahib polis_-sahib call the maharanee whose face he chutt that be on the pilgrimage when we travel thou know the proverb call the maharanee breaker of heart and dispenser of delight to remember that it be true so he do that be in the time of the bloom of my beauty she chuckle like contented parrot above the sugar lump now tell of thy go and come as much as may be without shame how many maid and whose wife hang upon thine eyelash ye hail from benare would have go there again this year

oh holy one say kim bubble with mirth at the lama ’s rueful face it be true give she one against wind tooth tooth tooth snap the old woman cure they if they be sick kim quote relishingly but by no mean work charm remember what befall the mahratta that be two rain ago she weary with her continual importunity the lama groan as the unjust judge have groan before he thus it come take note my chela_—that even those who would follow the way be thrust aside by idle woman three day through when the child be sick she talk to arre and to whom else should talk the boy ’s mother know nothing and the father in the night of the cold weather it was—‘pray to the god say he forsooth and turn over snore give she the charm what be an old man to do to abstain from action be well except to acquire merit ah chela if thou desertest be all alone he find his milk tooth easily at any rate say the old lady but all priest be alike kim cough severely be young he do not approve of her flippancy to importune the wise out of season be to invite calamity there be talk mynah_”—the thrust come back with the well remember snap of the jewelled fore
finger—“over the stable which have pick up the very tone of the family priest maybe forget honour to my guest but if ye have see he double his fist into his belly which be like half grow gourd and cry here be the pain ye would forgive be half minded to take the hakim ’s medicine he sell it cheap
and certainly it make he fat as shiv ’s own bull he do not deny remedy but doubt for the child because of the in auspicious colour of the bottle the lama under cover of the monologue have fade out into the darkness towards the room prepare thou hast anger he belike say kim not he he be weary and forget be grandmother none but grandmother should ever oversee child mother be only fit for bear tomorrow when he see how my daughter ’s son be grow he will write the charm then too he can judge of the new hakim ’s drug who be the hakim maharanee wanderer as thou art but most sober bengali from dacca master of medicine he relieve of an oppression after meat by mean of small pill that work like devil unchain he travel about now vend preparation of great value he have even paper print in angrezi tell what thing he have do for weak back man and slack woman he have be here four day but hear ye be come hakim and priest be snake and tiger the world over he have as take it go to cover while she draw breath after this volley the ancient servant sit unrebuked on the edge of the torchlight mutter this house be cattle pound as it be for all charlatan and priest let the boy stop eat mango but who can argue with grandmother he raise his voice respectfully sahiba the hakim sleep after his meat he be in the quarter behind the dovecote kim bristle like an expectant terrier to outface and down
talk calcutta teach bengali voluble dacca drug vendor would be good game it be not seemly that the lama and incidentally himself should be throw aside for such an one he know those curious bastard english advertisement at the back of native newspaper st xavier ’s boy sometimes bring they in by stealth to snigger over among their mate for the language of the grateful patient recount his symptom be most simple and reveal the oorya not unanxious to play off one parasite against the other slunk away towards the dovecote
yes say kim with measure scorn their stock in trade be little coloured water and very great shamelessness their prey be break down king and overfed bengalis their profit be in child who be not bear the old lady chuckle do not be envious charm be well eh never gainsay it see that thy holy one write good amulet by the morning none but the ignorant deny”—a thick heavy voice boom through the darkness as figure come to rest

and they play like child when they be grandfather strong back breed pip the voice inside the palanquin have too our drug which loosen humour of the head in hot and angry man siná well compound when the moon stand in the proper house yellow earth have—_arplan from china that make man renew his youth and astonish his household saffron from kashmir and the good salep of kabul many people have die before that surely believe say kim they know the value of my drug do not give my sick the mere ink in which charm be write but hot and rend drug which descend and wrestle with the evil very mightily they do so sigh the old lady the voice launch into an immense tale of misfortune and bankruptcy stud with plentiful petition to the government but for my fate which overrule all have be now in government employ bear degree from the great school at calcutta whither maybe the son of this house shall go he shall indeed if our neighbour ’s brat can in few year be make an first art she use the english word of which she have hear so often how much more shall child clever as some that know bear away prize at rich calcutta never say the voice have see such child bear in an auspicious hour

but the back of the house be unguarded and even in this soft air man think themselves to be man and woman we know the child ’s father be away too and must be chowkedar watchman in my old age take up the palanquin let the hakim and the young priest settle between they whether charm or medicine most avail ho worthless people fetch tobacco for the guest and round the homestead go the palanquin reel off follow by straggle torch and horde of dog twenty village know the sahiba she fail her tongue and her large charity twenty village cheat she after immemorial custom but no man would have steal or rob within her jurisdiction for any gift under heaven none the less she make great parade of her formal inspection the riot of which could be hear half way to mussoorie kim relax as one augur must when he meet another the hakim still squat slide over his hookah with friendly foot and kim pull at the good weed the hanger on expect grave professional debate and perhaps little free doctoring to discuss medicine before the ignorant be of one piece with teach the peacock to sing say the hakim true courtesy kim echo be very often inattention these be it understand be company manner design to impress have an ulcer on my leg cry scullion look at it get hence remove say the hakim be it the habit of the place to pester honour guest ye crowd in like buffalo if the sahiba know kim begin ai ai come away they be meat for our mistress when her young shaitan ’s colic be cure perhaps we poor people may be suffer to the mistress feed thy wife when thou wast in jail for break the money lender ’s head who speak against she the old servitor curl his white moustache savagely in the young moonlight be responsible for the honour of this house and he drive the underling before he say the hakim hardly more than shape the word with his lip how do you do mister o’hara be jolly glad to see you again kim ’s hand clench about the pipe stem anywhere on the open road perhaps he would not have be astonish
but here in this quiet backwater of life he be not prepare for hurree babu it annoy he too that he have be hoodwink ah ha tell you at lucknow—_resurgam_—i shall rise again
and you shall not know how much do you bet eh he chew leisurely upon few cardamom seed
but he breathe uneasily but why come here babuji ah thatt be the question as shakespeare hath it come to congratulate you on your extraordinary effeecient performance at delhi tell you we be all proud of you it be verree neat and handy our mutual friend he be old friend of mine he have be in some dam’-tight place now he will be in some more he tell tell mr lurgan
and he be pleased you graduate so nicely all the department be please for the first time in his life kim thrill to the clean pride it can be deadly pitfall none the less of departmental praise ensnare praise from an equal of work appreciate by fellow worker earth have nothing on the same plane to compare with it but cry the oriental in he babus do not travel far to retail compliment tell thy tale babu he say authoritatively oah it be nothing onlee be at simla when the wire come in about what our mutual friend say he have hide and old creighton he look to see how kim would take this piece of audacity the colonel sahib the boy from st xavier ’s correct of course he find at loose string and have to go down to chitor to find that beastly letter do not like the south too much railway travel but draw good travel allowance meet our mutual at delhi on the way back he lie quiett just now and say saddhu disguise suit he to the ground

so come on red lama be not inconspicuous person buy myself my drug box and be very good doctor really go to akrola of the ford and hear all about you and talk here and talk there all the common people know what you do know when the hospitable old lady send the dooli they have great recollection of the old lama ’s visit here know old lady can not keep their hand from medicine so be doctor
and you hear my talk think it be verree good my word mister o’hara they know about you and the lama for fifty mile the common people so come do you mind babuji say kim look up at the broad grin face be sahib my dear mister o’hara and hope to play the great game you be subordinate to departmentally at present then why talk like an ape in tree man do not come after one from simla and change their dress for the sake of few sweet word be not child talk hindi and let we get to the yolk of the egg thou art here speak not one word of truth in ten why art thou here give straight answer that be so verree disconcerting of the europeans mister o’hara you should know heap well at your time of life but want to know say kim laugh if it be the game may help how can do anything if you bukh babble all round the shop hurree babu reach for the pipe and suck it till it gurgle again now will speak vernacular you sit tight mister o’hara it concern the pedigree of white stallion that be finish long ago when everyone be dead the great game be finish not before listen to till the end there be five king who prepare sudden war three year ago when thou wast give the stallion ’s pedigree by mahbub ali upon they because of that news
and ere they be ready fall our army ay eight thousand man with gun remember that night
but the war be not push that be the government custom the troop be recall because the government believe the five king be cow and it be not cheap to feed man among the high pass hilás and bunár rajah with gun undertake for price to guard the pass against all come from the north they protest both fear and friendship he break off with giggle into english of course tell you this unoffeecially to elucidate political situation mister o’hara offeecially be debar from criticize any action of superior now go on.—this please the government anxious to avoid expense and bond be make for so many rupee month that hilás and bunár should guard the pass as soon as the state ’s troop be withdraw at that time it be after we two meet who have be sell tea in leh become clerk of account in the army when the troop be withdraw be leave behind to pay the cooly who make new road in the hill this road making be part of the bond between bunár hilás and the government and then tell you it be jolly beastly cold up there too after summer say hurree babu confidentially be afraid these bunár man would cut my throat every night for thee pay chest my native sepoy guard they laugh at by jove be such fearful man nevar mind thatt go on colloquially send word many time that these two king be sell to the north and mahbub ali who be yet far north amply confirm it nothing be do only my foot be freeze and toe drop off send word that the road for which be pay money to the digger be be make for the foot of strange and enemy for for the russians the thing be an open jest among the cooly then be call down to tell what know by speech of tongue mahbub come south too see the end over the pass this year after snow melting”—he shiver
afresh—“come two strange under cover of shoot wild goat they bear gun
but they bear also chain and level and compass the thing get clear they be well receive by hilás and bunár they make great promise they speak as the mouthpiece of kaisar with gift up the valley down the valley go they say here be place to build breastwork here can ye pitch fort here can ye hold the road against an army’—the very road for which pay out the rupee monthly the government know
but do nothing the three other king who be not pay for guard the pass tell they by runner of the bad faith of bunár and hilás when all the evil be do look you when these two stranger with the level and the compass make the five king to believe that great army will sweep the pass tomorrow or the next day hill people be all fool come the order to hurree babu go north and see what those stranger do say to creighton sahib this be not lawsuit that we go about to collect evidence hurree return to his english with jerk by jove say why the dooce do you not issue demi offeecial order to some brave man to poison they for an example it be if you permit the observation most reprehensible laxity on your part and colonel creighton he laugh at it be all your beastly english pride
you think no one dare conspire that be all tommy rott kim smoke slowly revolve the business so far as he understand it in his quick mind then thou goest forth to follow the strange no to meet they they be come in to simla to send down their horn and head to be dress at calcutta they be exclusively sport gentleman and they be allow special faceelitie by the government of course we always do that it be our british pride then what be to fear from they by jove they be not black people can do all sort of thing with black people of course they be russians and highly unscrupulous people do not want to consort with they without witness will they kill thee oah thatt be nothing be good enough herbert spencerian trust to meet little thing like death which be all in my fate you know
but but they may beat why hurree babu snap his finger with irritation of course shall affeeliate myself to their camp in supernumerary capacity as perhaps interpreter or person mentally impotent and hungree or some such thing and then must pick up what can suppose that be as easy for as play mister doctor to the old lady onlee onlee you see mister o’hara be unfortunately asiatic which be serious detriment in some respect and all_so be bengali fearful man god make the hare and the bengali what shame say kim quote the proverb it be process of evolution think from primal necessity but the fact remain in all the cui bono be
oh awfully fearful!—i remember once they want to cut off my head on the road to lhassa no have never reach to lhassa sit down and cry mister o’hara anticipate chinese torture do not suppose these two gentleman will torture but like to provide for possible contingency with european assistance in emergency he cough and spit out the cardamom it be purely unoffeecial indent to which you can say no babu if you have no press engagement with your old man perhaps you might divert he perhaps can seduce his fancy should like you to keep in departmental touch with till find those sport cove have great opeenion of you since meet my friend at delhi and also will embody your name in my offeecial report when matter be finally adjudicate it will be great feather in your cap that be why come really the end of the tale think be true but what of the fore part about the five king there be ever so much truth in it lot more than you would suppose say hurree earnestly you come eh go from here straight into the doon it be verree verdant and paint mead shall go to mussoorie to good old munsoorie pahar as the gentleman and lady say then by rampur into chini that be the only way they can come do not like wait in the cold
but we must wait for they want to walk with they to simla you see one russian be frenchman and know my french pretty well have friend in chandernagore he would certainly rejoice to see the hill again say kim meditatively all his speech these ten day past have be of little else if we go together oah we can be quite strange on the road if your lama prefer shall just be four or five mile ahead there be no hurry for hurree that be an europe pun ha ha!—and you come after there be plenty of time they will plot and survey and map of course shall go tomorrow and you the next day if you choose you go think on it till morning by jove it be near morning now he yawn ponderously and with never civil word lumber off to his sleep place but kim sleep little and his thought run in hindustani well be the game call great be four day scullion at quetta wait on the wife of the man whose book steal and that be part of the great game from the south god know how far come up the mahratta play the great game in fear of his life now shall go far and far into the north play the great game truly it run like shuttle throughout all hind and my share and my joy”—he smile to the darkness—“i owe to the lama here also to mahbub ali also to creighton sahib but chiefly to the holy one he be right great and wonderful world and be kim kim kim alone one person in the middle of it all but will see these stranger with their level and chain what be the upshot of last night ’s babble say the lama after his orison there come stroll seller of drug hanger on of the sahiba ’s he abolish by argument and prayer prove that our charm be worthy than his coloured water alas my charm be the virtuous woman still bend upon new one very strictly then it must be write or she will deafen with her clamour he fumble at his pencase in the plain say kim be always too many people in the hill as understand there be few
oh the hill and the snow upon the hill the lami tear off tiny square of paper fit to go in an amulet but what dost thou know of the hill they be very close kim thrust open the door and look at the long peaceful line of the himalayas flush in morning gold except in the dress of sahib have never set foot among they the lama snuff the wind wistfully if we go north,”—kim put the question to the waking sunrise—“would not much mid day heat be avoid by walk among the low hill at least be the charm make holy one have write the name of seven silly devil not one of whom be worth grain of dust in the eye thus do foolish woman drag we from the way hurree babu come out from behind the dovecote wash his tooth with ostentatious ritual
full flesh heavy haunched bull neck and deep voice he do not look like fearful man kim sign almost imperceptibly that matter be in good train and when the morning toilet be over hurree babu in flowery speech come to do honour to the lama they eat of course apart and afterwards the old lady more or less veiled behind window return to the vital business of green mango colic in the young the lama ’s knowledge of medicine be of course sympathetic only he believe that the dung of black horse mix with sulphur and carry in snake skin be sound remedy for cholera but the symbolism interest he far more than the science hurree babu defer to these view with enchanting politeness so that the lama call he courteous physician hurree babu reply that he be no more than an inexpert dabbler in the mystery but at least he thank the god therefore he know when he sit in the presence of master he himself have be teach by the sahib who do not consider expense in the lordly hall of calcutta but as he be ever first to acknowledge there lie wisdom behind earthly wisdom the high and lonely lore of meditation kim look on with envy the hurree babu of his knowledge oily effusive and nervous be go go too be the brazen drug vendor of overnight there remain polished
polite attentive sober learn son of experience and adversity gather wisdom from the lama ’s lip the old lady confide to kim that these rare level be beyond she she like charm with plenty of ink that one could wash off in water swallow and be do with else what be the use of the god she like man and woman
and she speak of they of kinglet she have know in the past of her own youth and beauty of the depredation of leopard and the eccentricity of love asiatic of the incidence of taxation rack rent funeral ceremony her son in law this by allusion easy to be follow the care of the young and the age ’s lack of decency and kim as interested in the life of this world as she soon to leave it squat with his foot under the hem of his robe drink all in while the lama demolish one after another every theory of body cure put forward by hurree babu at noon the babu strap up his brass bind drug box take his patent leather shoe of ceremony in one hand gay blue and white umbrella in the other and set off northwards to the doon where he say he be in demand among the less king of those part we will go in the cool of the evening chela say the lama that doctor learn in physic and courtesy affirm that the people among these low hill be devout generous and much in need of teacher in very short time so say the hakim_—we come to cool air and the smell of pine ye go to the hill and by kulu road

before he in the dust chapter xiii who hath desire the sea the immense and contemptuous surge the shudder the stumble the swerve ere the star stab bowsprit merge the orderly cloud of the trade and the ridged roaring sapphire thereunder unheralded cliff lurk flaw and the head sail low volley thunder his sea in no wonder the same his sea and the same in each wonder his sea that his be fulfil so
and no otherwise so and no otherwise hill man desire their hill the sea and the hill who go to the hill go to his mother they have cros the siwaliks and the half tropical doon leave mussoorie behind they and head north along the narrow hill road day after day they strike deeply into the huddled mountain and day after day kim watch the lama return to man ’s strength among the terrace of the doon he have lean on the boy ’s shoulder ready to profit by wayside halt under the great ramp to mussoorie he draw himself together as an old hunter face well remember bank and where he should have sink exhaust swing his long drapery about he draw deep double lungful of the diamond air and walk as only hillman can kim plain breed and plain fed sweat and pant astonish this be my country say the lama beside such zen this be flat than rice field and with steady drive stroke from the loin he stride upwards but it be on the steep downhill march three thousand foot in three hour that he go utterly away from kim whose back ache with hold back and whose big toe be nigh cut off by his grass sandal string through the speckled shadow of the great deodar forest through oak feather and plume with fern birch ilex rhododendron and pine out on to the bare hillside slippery sunburnt grass and back into the woodland coolth again till oak give way to bamboo and palm of the valley the lama swing untiring glance back in the twilight at the huge ridge behind he and the faint thin line of the road whereby they have come he would lay out with hillman ’s generous breadth of vision fresh march for the morrow or halt in the neck of some uplifted pass that give on spiti and kulu would stretch out his hand yearningly towards the high snow of the horizon in the dawn they flare windy red above stark blue as kedarnath and badrinath king of that wilderness take the first sunlight all day long they lie like molten silver under the sun and at evening put on their jewel again at first they breathe temperately upon the traveller wind good to meet when one crawl over some gigantic hog back but in few day at height of nine or ten thousand foot those breeze bit and kim kindly allow village of hillmen to acquire merit by give he rough blanket coat the lama be mildly surprised that anyone should object to the knife edge breeze which have cut the year off his shoulder these be but the low hill chela there be no cold till we come to the true hill air and water be good and the people be devout enough but the food be very bad kim growl and we walk as though we be mad or english it freeze at night too little maybe but only enough to make old bone rejoice in the sun we must not always delight in soft bed and rich food we might at least keep to the road kim have all plainsman ’s affection for the well tread track not six foot wide that snake among the mountain but the lama be tibetan could not refrain from short cut over spur and the rim of gravel strew slope as he explain to his limp disciple man breed among mountain can prophesy the course of mountain road and though low lie cloud might be hindrance to short cut stranger they make no earthly difference to thoughtful man thus after long hour of what would be reckon very fair mountaineering in civilized country they would pant over saddle back sidle past few landslip and drop through forest at an angle of forty five onto the road again along their track lie the village of the hillfolk mud and earth hut timber now and then rudely carve with an axe cling like swallow nest against the steep huddle on tiny flat half way down three thousand foot glissade jam into corner between cliff that funnel and focus every wander blast or for the sake of summer pasture cower down on neck that in winter would be ten foot deep in snow and the people the sallow greasy duffle clothe people with short bare leg and face almost esquimaux would flock out and adore the plain kindly and gentle have treat the lama as holy man among holy man but the hill worship he as one in the confidence of all their devil their be an almost obliterate buddhism overlay with nature worship fantastic as their own landscape elaborate as the terracing of their tiny field but they recognize the big hat the click rosary and the rare chinese text for great authority and they respect the man beneath the hat we see thee come down over the black breast of eua say betah who give they cheese sour milk and stone hard bread one evening we do not use that often except when calving cow stray in summer there be sudden wind among those stone that cast man down on the still day but what should such folk care for the devil of eua then do kim ache in every fibre dizzy with look down footsore with cramp desperate toe into inadequate cranny take joy in the day ’s march such joy as boy of st xavier ’s who have win the quarter mile on the flat might take in the praise of his friend the hill sweat the ghi and sugar suet off his bone the dry air take sobbingly at the head of cruel pass firm and build out his upper rib and the tilt level put new hard muscle into calf and thigh they meditate often on the wheel of life the more so since as the lama say they be free from its visible temptation except the grey eagle and an occasional far see bear grub and root on the hillside vision of furious paint leopard meet at dawn in still valley devour goat and now and again bright colour bird they be alone with the wind and the grass sing under the wind the woman of the smoky hut over whose roof the two walk as they descend the mountain be unlovely and unclean wife of many husband and afflict with goitre the man be woodcutter when they be not farmer meek and of an incredible simplicity but that suitable discourse might not fail fate send they overtake and overtake upon the road the courteous dacca physician who pay for his food in ointment good for goitre and counsel that restore peace between man and woman he seem to know these hill as well as he know the hill dialect and give the lama the lie of the land towards ladakh and tibet he say they could return to the plain at any moment meantime for such as love mountain yonder road might amuse this be not all reveal in breath but at evening encounter on the stone thresh floor when patient dispose of the doctor would smoke and the lama snuff while kim watch the wee cow graze on the housetop or throw his soul after his eye across the deep blue gulfs between range and range and there be talk apart in the dark wood when the doctor would seek herb and kim as bud physician must accompany he you see mister o’hara do not know what the deuce an all shall do when find our sport friend but if you will kindly keep within sight of my umbrella which be fine fix point for cadastral survey shall feel much well kim look out across the jungle of peak this be not my country hakim easy think to find one louse in bear skin oah thatt be my strong point there be no hurry for hurree they be at leh not so long ago they say they have come down from the karakorum with their head and horn and all be onlee afraid they will have send back all their letter and compromise thing from leh into russian territoree of course they will walk away as far to the east as possible just to show that they be never among the western state you do not know the hill he scratch with twig on you see to bushahr and chini valley that be ascertain by process of elimination and also by ask question from people that cure so well our friend have be long time play about and produce impression so they be well know from far off you will see catch they somewhere in chini valley please keep your eye on the umbrella it nod like wind blow harebell down the valley and round the mountain side and in due time the lama and kim who steer by compass would overhaul it vend ointment and powder at eventide we come by such and such way the lama would throw careless finger backward at the ridge and the umbrella would expend itself in compliment they cross snowy pass in cold moonlight when the lama mildly chaff kim go through up to his knee like bactrian camel the snow breed shag haired sort that come into the kashmir serai they dip across bed of light snow and snow powder shale where they take refuge from gale in camp of tibetans hurry down tiny sheep each laden with bag of borax they come out upon grassy shoulder still snow speckle and through forest to grass anew for all their marching kedarnath and badrinath be not impressed and it be only after day of travel that kim uplift upon some insignificant ten thousand foot hummock could see that shoulder knot or horn of the two great lord have ever so slightly change outline at last they enter world within world valley of league where the high hill be fashion of mere rubble and refuse from off the knee of the mountain here one day ’s march carry they
no far it seem than dreamer ’s clog pace bear he in nightmare they skirt shoulder painfully for hour and behold it be but an outlying boss in an outlying buttress of the main pile rounded meadow reveal itself when they have reach it for vast tableland run far into the valley three day later it be dim fold in the earth to southward surely the god live here say kim beat down by the silence and the appalling sweep and dispersal of the cloud shadow after rain this be no place for man long and long ago say the lama as to himself it be ask of the lord whether the world be everlaste on this the excellent one return no answer when be in ceylon wise seeker confirm that from the gospel which be write in pali certainly since we know the way to freedom the question be unprofitable but look and know illusion chela these be the true hill they be like my hill by suchzen never be such hill above they still enormously above they earth tower away towards the snow line where from east to west across hundred of mile rule as with ruler the last of the bold birch stop above that in scarp and block upheave the rock strove to fight their head above the white smother above these again changeless since the world ’s begin but change to every mood of sun and cloud lie out the eternal snow they could see blot and blur on its face where storm and wander wullie
wa get up to dance below they as they stand the forest slide away in sheet of blue green for mile upon mile below the forest be village in its sprinkle of terraced field and steep graze ground below the village they know though thunderstorm worry and growl there for the moment pitch of twelve or fifteen hundred foot give to the moist valley where the stream gather that be the mother of young sutluj as usual the lama have lead kim by cow track and by road far from the main route along which hurree babu that fearful man have bucket three day before through storm to which nine englishman out of ten would have give full right of way hurree be no game shoot the snick of trigger make he change colour but as he himself would have say he be fairly effeecient stalker and he have rake the huge valley with pair of cheap binocular to some purpose moreover the white of wear canvas tent against green carry far hurree babu have see all he want to see when he sit on the thresh floor of ziglaur twenty mile away as the eagle fly and forty by road that be to say two small dot which one day be just below the snow line and the next have move downward perhaps six inch on the hillside once clean out and set to the work his fat bare leg could cover surprising amount of ground and this be the reason why while kim and the lama lie in leaky hut at ziglaur till the storm should be over past an oily wet but always smile bengali talk the good of english with the vile of phrase be ingratiate himself with two sodden and rather rheumatic foreigner he have arrive revolve many wild scheme on the heel of thunderstorm which have split pine over against their camp and so convince dozen or two forcibly impressed baggage cooly the day be inauspicious for farther travel that with one accord they have throw down their load and jib they be subject of hill rajah who farm out their service as be the custom for his private gain and to add to their personal distress the strange sahib have already threaten they with rifle the most of they know rifle and sahib of old they be tracker and shikarris of the northern valley keen after bear and wild goat but they have never be thus treat in their life so the forest take they to her bosom and for all oath and clamour refuse to restore there be no need to feign madness or the babu have think of another mean of secure welcome he wring out his wet clothe slip on his patent leather shoe open the blue and white umbrella and with mince gait and heart beat against his tonsil appear as agent for his royal highness the rajah of rampur gentleman what can do for you please the gentleman be delight one be visibly french the other russian
but they speak english not much inferior to the babu ’s they beg his kind office their native servant have go sick at leh they have hurry on because they be anxious to bring the spoil of the chase to simla ere the skin grow moth eat they bear general letter of introduction the babu salaam to it orientally to all government official no they have not meet any other shoot party en route they do for themselves they have plenty of supply they only wish to push on as soon as might be at this he waylay cower hillman among the tree and after three minute talk and little silver one can not be economical upon state service though hurree ’s heart blee at the waste the eleven cooly and the three hanger on reappear at least the babu would be witness to their oppression my royal master he will be much annoyed but these people be onlee common people and grossly ignorant if your honour will kindly overlook unfortunate affair shall be much pleased in little while rain will stop and we can then proceed you have be shoot eh that be fine performance he skip nimbly from one kilta to the next make pretence to adjust each conical basket the englishman be not as rule familiar with the asiatic but he would not strike across the wrist kindly babu who have accidentally upset kilta with red oilskin top on the other hand he would not press drink upon babu be he never so friendly nor would he invite he to meat the stranger do all these thing and ask many question about woman mostly to which hurree return gay and unstudied answer they give he glass of whitish fluid like to gin and then more and in little time his gravity depart from he he become thickly treasonous and speak in term of sweeping indecency of government which have force upon he white man ’s education and neglect to supply he with white man ’s salary he babble tale of oppression and wrong till the tear run down his cheek for the misery of his land then he stagger off sing love song of low bengal and collapse upon wet tree trunk never be so unfortunate product of english rule in india more unhappily thrust upon alien they be all just of that pattern say one sportsman to the other in french when we get into india proper thou wilt see should like to visit his rajah one might speak the good word there it be possible that he have hear of we and wish to signify his good will we have not time we must get into simla as soon as may be his companion reply for my own part wish our report have be send back from hilás or even leh the english post be
well and safe remember we be give all facility and name of god!—they give they to we too be it unbelievable stupidity it be pride pride that deserve and will receive punishment to fight fellow continental in our game be something there be risk attach
but these people bah it be too easy pride all pride my friend now what the deuce be good of chandernagore be so close to calcutta and all say hurree snore open mouthed on the sodden moss if can not understand their french they talk so par_tic_ularly fast it would have be much well to cut their beastly throat when he present himself again he be rack with headache penitent and volubly afraid that in his drunkenness he might have be indiscreet he love the british government it be the source of all prosperity and honour and his master at rampur hold the very same opinion upon this the man begin to deride he and to quote past word till step by step with deprecate smirk oily grin and leer of infinite cunning the poor babu be beat out of his defence and force to speak truth when lurgan be tell the tale later he mourn aloud that he could not have be in the place of the stubborn inattentive cooly who with grass mat over their head and the raindrop puddle in their footprint wait on the weather all the sahib of their acquaintance rough clothe man joyously return year after year to their choose gully have servant and cook and orderly very often hillmen these sahib travel without any retinue therefore they be poor sahib and ignorant for no sahib in his sen would follow bengali ’s advice but the bengali appear from somewhere have give they money and could make shift with their dialect use to comprehensive ill treatment from their own colour they suspect trap somewhere and stand by to run if occasion offer then through the new wash air steam with delicious earth smell the babu lead the way down the slope walk ahead of the cooly in pride walk behind the foreigner in humility his thought be many and various the least of they would have interest his companion beyond word but he be an agreeable guide ever keen to point out the beauty of his royal master ’s domain he people the hill with anything thev have mind to slay thar ibex or markhor and bear by elisha ’s allowance he discourse of botany and ethnology with unimpeachable inaccuracy and his store of local legend he have be trust agent of the state for fifteen year remember be inexhaustible decidedly this fellow be an original say the tall of the two foreigner he be like the nightmare of viennese courier he represent in petto india in transition the monstrous hybridism of east and west the russian reply it be we who can deal with oriental he have lose his own country and have not acquire any other but he have most complete hatred of his conqueror he confide to last night say the other under the strip umbrella hurree babu be strain ear and brain to follow the quick pour french and keep both eye on kilta full of map and document an extra large one with double red oil skin cover he do not wish to steal anything he only desire to know what to steal and incidentally how to get away when he have steal it he thank all the god of hindustan and herbert spencer that there remain some valuable to steal on the second day the road rise steeply to grass spur above the forest and it be here about sunset that they come across an aged lama
but they call he bonze sit cross legged above mysterious chart hold down by stone which he be explain to young man evidently neophyte of singular though unwashen beauty the striped umbrella have be sight half march away and kim have suggest halt till it come up to they ha say hurree babu resourceful as puss in boot that be eminent local holy man probably subject of my royal master what be he do it be very curious he be expound holy picture—_all hand work the two man stand bareheade in the wash of the afternoon sunlight low across the gold colour grass the sullen cooly glad of the check halt and slide down their load look say the frenchman it be like picture for the birth of religion the first teacher and the first disciple be he buddhist of some debased kind the other answer there be no true buddhist among the hill but look at the fold of the drapery look at his eye how insolent why do this make one feel that we be so young people the speaker strike passionately at tall weed we have nowhere leave our mark yet that do you understand be what disquiet he scowl at the placid face and the monumental calm of the pose have patience we shall make your mark together
we and you young people meantime draw his picture the babu advance loftily his back out of all keep with his deferential speech or his wink towards kim holy one these be sahib my medicine cure one of flux and go into simla to oversee his recovery they wish to see thy picture to heal the sick be always good this be the wheel of life say the lama the same show thee in the hut at ziglaur when the rain fall and to hear thee expound it the lama ’s eye light at the prospect of new listener to expound the most excellent way be good have they any knowledge of hindi such as have the keeper of image little maybe hereat simply as child engross with new game the lama throw back his head and begin the full throated invocation of the doctor of divinity
ere he open the full doctrine the strange lean on their alpenstock and listen kim squat humbly watch the red sunlight on their face and the blend and parting of their long shadow they wear un english legging and curious girt in belt that remind he hazily of the picture in book in st xavier ’s library the adventure of young naturalist in mexico be its name
yes they look very like the wonderful m. sumichrast of that tale and very unlike the highly unscrupulous folk of hurree babu ’s imagine the cooly earth colour and mute crouch reverently some twenty or thirty yard away and the babu the slack of his thin gear snap like mark flag in the chill breeze stand by with an air of happy proprietorship these be the man hurree whisper as the ritual go on and the two white follow the grass blade sweep from hell to heaven and back again all their book be in the large kilta with the reddish top book and report and map and have see king ’s letter that either hilás or bunár have write they guard it most carefully they have send nothing back from hilás or leh that be sure who be with they only the beegar_-coolie they have no servant they be so close they cook their own food but what be to do wait and see only if any chance come to thou wilt know where to seek for the paper this be well in mahbub ali ’s hand than bengali ’s say kim scornfully there be the babu reply the lama of course would no more have part with his chart to casual wayfarer than an archbishop would pawn the holy vessel of his cathedral all tibet be full of cheap reproduction of the wheel but the lama be an artist as well as wealthy abbot in his own place perhaps in three day or four or ten if perceive that the sahib be seeker and of good understanding may myself draw he sahib pack themselves every morning thus it be prove say the shamlegh man adroitly that they be sahib of no account who ever hear of fostum sahib or yankling sahib or even the little peel sahib that yes if he have chela to prepare tea for he and to fold blanket for his head and to chase out calving cow smoky lamp burn in niche but the full moonlight beat it down and by the mixed light stoop above the food bag and cup kim move like tall ghost but now have let the blood cool my head still beat and drum and there be cord round the back of my neck no wonder it be strong blow
may he who deal it but for my own passion there would have be no evil what evil thou hast save the sahib from the death they deserve hundred time the lesson be not well learn chela the lama come to rest on fold blanket as kim go forward with his evening routine the blow be but shadow upon shadow evil in itself my leg weary apace these latter days!—it meet evil in anger rage and lust to return evil these work in my blood wake tumult in my stomach and dazzle my ear here he drink scald black tea ceremonially take the hot cup from kim ’s hand have be passionless the evil blow would have do only bodily evil scar or bruise which be illusion
but my mind be not abstract for rush in straightway lust to let the spiti man kill in fight that lust my soul be tear and wrench beyond thousand blow not till have repeat the blessing he mean the buddhist beatitude do achieve calm

to eat kim affect blank ignorance dost thou not know the meaning of the walnut priest she say coyly and hand he the half shell well think of he slip the piece of paper between they quickly hast thou bedfellow hai my”—it be on the tip of his tongue to say mother
but he turn it to sister—“thou art wise and witty woman by this time all the village know what have befall the sahib eh true news be at ziglaur by midnight and by tomorrow should be at kotgarh the village be both afraid and angry no need tell the village to feed the sahib and pass they on in peace we must get they quietly away from our valley to steal be one thing to kill another the babu will understand and there will be no after complaint be swift must tend my master when he wake so be it after service thou hast said?—come the reward be the woman of shamlegh and hold from the rajah be no common bearer of babe shamlegh be thine hoof and horn and hide milk and butter take or leave she turn resolutely uphill her silver necklace click on her broad breast to meet the morning sun fifteen hundred foot above they this time kim think in the vernacular as he wax down the oilskin edge of the packet how can man follow the way or the great game when he be so always pester by woman there be that girl at akrola of the ford and there be the scullion ’s wife behind the dovecot not count the other and now come this one when be child it be well enough but now be man and they will not regard as man walnut
indeed ho ho it be almond in the plain he go out to levy on the village not with beg bowl which might do for down country but in the manner of prince shamlegh ’s summer population be only three family four woman and eight or nine man they be all full of tin meat and mixed drink from ammoniated quinine to white vodka for they have take their full share in the overnight loot the neat continental tent have be cut up and share long ago and there be patent aluminium saucepan abroad
but they consider the lama ’s presence perfect safeguard against all consequence and impenitently bring kim of their good even to drink of chang_—the barley beer that come from ladakh way then they thaw out in the sun and sit with their leg hang over infinite abyss chatter laugh and smoke they judge india and its government solely from their experience of wander sahib who have employ they or their friend as shikarris kim hear tale of shot miss upon ibex serow or markhor by sahib twenty year in their graf every detail light from behind like twig on tree top see against lightning they tell he of their little disease and more important the disease of their tiny sure footed cattle of trip as far as kotgarh where the strange missionary live and beyond even to marvellous simla where the street be pave with silver and anyone look you can get service with the sahib who ride about in two wheel cart and spend money with spade presently grave and aloof walk very heavily the lama join himself to the chatter under the eave and they give he great room the thin air refresh he and he sit on the edge of precipice with the good of they and when talk languish fling pebble into the void thirty mile away as the eagle fly lie the next range seam and channel and pit with little patch of brush forest each day ’s dark march behind the village shamlegh hill itself cut off all view to southward it be like sit in swallow ’s nest under the eave of the roof of the world from time to time the lama stretch out his hand and with little low voice prompting would point out the road to spiti and north across the parungla beyond where the hill lie thick lie de ch’en
he mean han lé the great monastery s’tag stan ras ch’en build it and of he there run this tale whereupon he tell it fantastic pile narrative of bewitchment and miracle that set shamlegh gasp turn west little he steer for the green hill of kulu and seek kailung under the glacier for thither come in the old old day from leh come over the baralachi
yes yes we know it say the far fare people of shamlegh and sleep two night with the priest of kailung these be the hill of my delight shadow bless above all other shadow there my eye open on this world there my eye be open to this world there find enlightenment and there girt my loin for my search out of the hill come the high hill and the strong wind
oh just be the wheel he bless they in detail the great glacier the naked rock the pile moraine and tumble shale dry upland hide salt lake age old timber and fruitful water shot valley one after the other as die man bless his folk and kim marvel at his passion
yes yes there be no place like our hill say the people of shamlegh and they fall to wonder how man could live in the hot terrible plain where the cattle run as big as elephant unfit to plough on hillside where village touch village they have hear for hundred mile where folk go about steal in gang and what the robber spar the police carry utterly away so the still forenoon wear through and at the end of it kim ’s messenger drop from the steep pasture as unbreathed as when she have set out send word to the hakim kim explain while she make reverence he join himself to the idolater nay remember he do healing upon one of they he have acquire merit though the heal employ his strength for evil just be the wheel what of the hakim fear that thou hadst be bruise and and know he be wise kim take the wax walnut shell and read in english on the back of his note your favour receive can not get away from present company at present but shall take they into simla after which hope to rejoin you inexpedient to follow angry gentleman return by same road you come and will overtake highly gratified about correspondence due to my forethought he say holy one that he will escape from the idolater and will return to we shall we wait awhile at shamlegh then the lama look long and lovingly upon the hill and shake his head that may not be chela from my bone outward do desire it
but it be forbid have see the cause of thing when the hill give thee back thy strength day by day
remember we be weak and faint down below there in the doon become strong to do evil and to forget brawler and swashbuckler upon the hillside be i. kim bite back smile just and perfect be the wheel swerve not hair when be man long time ago do pilgrimage to guru ch’wan among the poplar he point bhotanward where they keep the sacred horse quiet be quiet say shamlegh all arow he speak of
jam lin nin k’or the horse that can go round the world in day speak to my chela only say the lama in gentle reproof and they scatter like frost on south eave of morning do not seek truth in those day but the talk of doctrine all illusion drink the beer and eat the bread of guru ch’wan next day one say we go out to fight sangor gutok down the valley to discover mark again how lust be tie to anger which abbot shall bear rule in the valley and take the profit of the prayer they print at sangor gutok go and we fight day
but how holy one with our long pencase as could have show say we fight under the poplar both abbot and all the monk and one lie open my forehead to the bone he tilt back his cap and show puckered silvery scar just and perfect be the wheel yesterday the scar itch and after fifty year recall how it be deal and the face of he who deal it dwell little in illusion follow that which thou didst see strife and stupidity just be the wheel the idolater ’s blow fall upon the scar then be shake in my soul my soul be darken and the boat of my soul rock upon the water of illusion not till come to shamlegh could meditate upon the cause of thing or trace the run grass root of evil strove all the long night but holy one thou art innocent of all evil may be thy sacrifice kim be genuinely distressed at the old man ’s sorrow and mahbub ali ’s phrase slip out unawares in the dawn the lama go on more gravely ready rosary click between the slow sentence come enlightenment it be here be an old man hill breed hill fed never to sit down among my hill three year travel through hind but can earth be strong than mother earth my stupid body yearn to the hill and the snow of the hill from below there say
and it be true my search be sure so at the kulu woman ’s house turn hillward over persuade by myself there be no blame to the hakim he follow desire foretell that the hill would make strong they strengthen to do evil to forget my search delight in life and the lust of life desire strong slope to climb cast about to find they measure the strength of my body which be evil against the high hill make mock of thee when thy breath come short under jamnotri jest when thou wouldst not face the snow of the pass but what harm be afraid it be just be not hillman and love thee for thy new strength more than once remember”—he rest his cheek dolefully on his hand—“i seek thy praise and the hakim for the mere strength of my leg thus evil follow evil till the cup be full just be the wheel all hind for three year do all honour from the fountain of wisdom in the wonder house to”—he
smiled—“a little child play by big gun the world prepare my road and why because we love thee it be only the fever of the blow myself be still sick and shake it be because be upon the way tune as be si nen cymbal to the purpose of the law depart from that ordinance the tune be break follow the punishment in my own hill on the edge of my own country in the very place of my evil desire come the buffet here he touch his brow as novice be beat when he misplace the cup so be beat who be abbot of such zen no word
look you but blow chela but the sahib do not know thee holy one we be well match ignorance and lust meet ignorance and lust upon the road and they beget anger the blow be sign to who be no well than stray yak that my place be not here who can read the cause of an act be halfway to freedom back to the path say the blow the hill be not for thee thou canst not choose freedom and go in bondage to the delight of life would we have never meet that curse russian our lord himself can not make the wheel swing backward and for my merit that have acquire gain yet another sign he put his hand in his bosom and draw forth the wheel of life consider this after have meditate there remain untorn by the idolater no more than the breadth of my fingernail so much then be the span of my life in this body have serve the wheel all my day now the wheel serve but for the merit have acquire in guide thee upon the way there would have be add to yet another life ere have find my river be it

oh the weary road kim feel that he too need rest that be lawful let we eat and go the arrow fall in the plain but yield to desire make ready chela kim turn to the woman with the turquoise headgear who have be idly pitch pebble over the cliff she smile very kindly find he like stray buffalo in cornfield the babu snort and sneeze with cold he be so hungry that he forget his dignity and give sweet word the sahib have nothing she fling out an empty palm one be very sick about the stomach thy work kim nod with bright eye speak to the bengali first and to the people of near by village after the sahib will be give food as they need it nor will the people ask money the plunder be already distribute the babu make lie speech to the sahib why do he not leave they out of the greatness of his heart be never bengali yet have one big than dry walnut

two hundred cartridge be elsewhere shoot musk deer for the market and yankling sahib will learn next season how very ill he have be up the valley of bushahr the far behold eagle of the himalayas swerve at his new blue and white gore umbrella hurry bengali once fat and well look now lean and weather wear he have receive the thank of two foreigner of distinction pilot not unskilfully to mashobra tunnel which lead to the great and gay capital of india it be not his fault that blanket by wet mist he convey they past the telegraph station and european colony of kotgarh it be not his fault but that of the god of whom he discourse so engagingly that he lead they into the border of nahan where the rajah of that state mistake they for desert british soldiery hurree babu explain the greatness and glory in their own country of his companion till the drowsy kinglet smile he explain it to everyone who ask many time aloud variously he beg food arrange accommodation prove skilful leech for an injury of the groin such blow as one may receive roll down rock cover hillside in the dark and in all thing indispensable the reason of his friendliness do he credit with million of fellow serf he have learn to look upon russia as the great deliverer from the north he be fearful man he have be afraid that he could not save his illustrious employer from the anger of an excited peasantry he himself would just as lief hit holy man as not but he be deeply grateful and sincerely rejoice that he have do his little possible towards bring their venture to bar the lose baggage successful issue he have forget the blow deny that any blow have be deal that unseemly first night under the pine he ask neither pension nor retain fee
but if they deem he worthy would they write he testimonial it might be useful to he later if other their friend come over the pass he beg they to remember he in their future greatness for he opine subtly that he even he mohendro lal dutt ma of calcutta have do the state some service they give he certificate praise his courtesy helpfulness and unerring skill as guide he put it in his waist belt and sob with emotion they have endure so many danger together he lead they at high noon along crowd simla mall to the alliance bank of simla where they wish to establish their identity thence he vanish like dawn cloud on jakko behold he too fine draw to sweat too press to vaunt the drug in his little brass bind box ascend shamlegh slope just man make perfect watch he all babudom lie aside smoke at noon on cot while woman with turquoise stud headgear point south easterly across the bare grass litter she say do not travel as fast as single man but his bird should now be in the plain the holy man would not stay though lispeth press he the babu groan heavily gird up his huge loin and be off again he do not care to travel after dusk but his day march there be none to enter they in book would astonish folk who mock at his race kindly villager remember the dacca drug vendor of two month ago give he shelter against evil spirit of the wood he dream of bengali gods university text book of education and the royal society london england next dawn the bob blue and white umbrella go forward on the edge of the doon mussoorie

irresponsible pulp half hypnotize by the perpetual flick and readjustment of the uneasy chudder that veil their eye kim slide ten thousand mile into slumber thirty six hour of it sleep that soak like rain after drought then she feed he and the house spin to her clamour she cause fowl to be slay she send for vegetable and the sober slow think gardener nigh as old as she sweat for it she take spice cousin,”—thi to the poor relation never weary of extoll her patroness ’s charity—“he be get bloom on the skin of new curry horse our work be like polish jewel to be throw to dance girl eh kim sit up and smile the terrible weakness have drop from he like an old shoe his tongue itch for free speech again and but week back the light word clog it like ash the pain in his neck he must have catch it from the lama have go with the heavy dengue ache and the evil taste in the mouth the two old woman little but not much more careful about their veil now cluck as merrily as the hen that have enter peck through the open door where be my holy one he demand hear he thy holy one be well she snap viciously though that be none of his merit know charm to make he wise ’d sell my jewel and buy it to refuse good food that cook myself and go rove into the field for two night on an empty belly and to tumble into brook at the end of it call you that holiness then when he have nearly break what thou hast leave of my heart with anxiety he tell that he have acquire merit

and he be fearful man must get into the world again at first his leg bent like bad pipe stem and the flood and rush of the sunlit air dazzle he he squat by the white wall the mind rummage among the incident of the long dooli journey the lama ’s weakness and now that the stimulus of talk be remove his own self pity of which like the sick he have great store the unnerved brain edge away from all the outside as raw horse once rowel sidle from the spur it be enough amply enough that the spoil of the kilta be away off his hand out of his possession he try to think of the lama to wonder why he have tumble into brook but the bigness of the world see between the forecourt gate sweep link think aside then he look upon the tree and the broad field with the thatched hut hide among crop look with strange eye unable to take up the size and proportion and use of thing star for still half hour all that while he feel though he could not put it into word that his soul be out of gear with its surround cog wheel unconnected with any machinery just like the idle cog wheel of cheap beheea sugar crusher lie by in corner the breeze fan over he the parrot shriek at he the noise of the populated house behind squabble order and reproofs hit on dead ear be kim be kim and what be kim his soul repeat it again and again he do not want to cry have never feel less like cry in his life but of sudden easy stupid tear trickle down his nose and with an almost audible click he feel the wheel of his be lock up anew on the world without thing that ride meaningless on the eyeball an instant before slide into proper proportion road be mean to be walk upon house to be live in cattle to be drive field to be till and man and woman to be talk to they be all real and true solidly plant upon the foot perfectly comprehensible clay of his clay neither more nor less he shake himself like dog with flea in his ear and ramble out of the gate say the sahiba to whom watchful eye report this move let he go have do my share mother earth must do the rest when the holy one come back from meditation tell he there stand an empty bullock cart on little knoll half mile away with young banyan tree behind look out as it be above some new plough level and his eyelid bath in soft air grow heavy as he near it the ground be good clean dust no new herbage that living be half way to death already but the hopeful dust that hold the seed of all life he feel it between his toe pat it with his palm and joint by joint sigh luxuriously lie he down full length along in the shadow of the wooden pin cart and mother earth be as faithful as the sahiba she breathe through he to restore the poise he have lose lie so long on cot cut off from her good current his head lie powerless upon her breast and his open hand surrender to her strength the many root tree above he and even the dead manhandle wood beside know what he seek as he himself do not know hour upon hour he lie deeply than sleep towards evening when
