the soul have pas beyond the illusion of time and space and of thing by this know that be free see thee lie in thy cot and see thee fall downhill under the idolater at one time in one place in my soul which as say have touch the great soul also see the stupid body of teshoo lama lie down and the hakim from dacca kneel beside shout in its ear then my soul be all alone and see nothing for be all thing having reach the great soul and meditate thousand thousand year passionless well aware of the cause of all thing then voice cry what shall come to the boy if thou art dead and be shake back and forth in myself with pity for thee and say will return to my chela lest he miss the way upon this my soul which be the soul of teshoo lama withdraw itself from the great soul with strive and yearning and retching and agony not to be tell as the egg from the fish as the fish from the water as the water from the cloud as the cloud from the thick air so put forth so leap out so draw away so fume up the soul of teshoo lama from the great soul then voice cry the river take heed to the river and look down upon all the world which be as have see it before one in time one in place and see plainly the river of the arrow at my foot at that hour my soul be hamper by some evil or other whereof be not wholly cleanse and it lie upon my arm and coil round my waist but put it aside and cast forth as an eagle in my flight for the very place of the river push aside world upon world for thy sake see the river below the river of the arrow and descend the water of it close over and behold be again in the body of teshoo lama but free from sin and the hakim from decca bear up my head in the water of the river it be here it be behind the mango tope here even here

complete but to day greatly fear that my king be dead and if want crown must go and hunt it for myself the beginning of everything be in railway train upon the road to mhow from ajmir there have be deficit in the budget which necessitate travel not second class which be only half as dear as first class but by intermediate which be very awful indeed there be no cushion in the intermediate class and the population be either intermediate which be eurasian or native which for long night journey be nasty or loafer which be amusing though intoxicate intermediate do not patronize refreshment room they carry their food in bundle and pot and buy sweet from the native sweetmeat seller and drink the roadside water that be why in the hot weather intermediate be take out of the carriage dead and in all weather be most properly look down upon my particular intermediate happen to be empty till reach nasirabad when huge gentleman in shirt sleeve enter and follow the custom of intermediate pass the time of day he be wanderer and vagabond like myself but with an educate taste for whiskey he tell tale of thing he have see and do of out of the way corner of the empire into which he have penetrate and of adventure in which he risk his life for few day food if india be fill with man like you and not know more than the crow where they ’d get their next day ’s ration it be not seventy million of revenue the land would be pay it seven hundred million say he and as look at his mouth and chin be dispose to agree with he we talk politic the politic of loaferdom that see thing from the underside where the lath and plaster be not smooth off and we talk postal arrangement because my friend want to send telegram back from the next station to ajmir which be the turn off place from the bombay to where though should resume touch with the treasury there be no telegraph office be therefore unable to help he in any way we might threaten station master and make he send wire on tick say my friend my message he get out at little roadside station and reflect have hear more than once of man personate correspondent of newspaper and bleed small native state with threat of exposure but have never meet any of the caste before they lead hard life and generally die with great suddenness the native state have wholesome horror of english newspaper which may throw light on their peculiar method of government and do their good to choke correspondent with champagne or drive they out of their mind with four in hand barouche they do not understand that nobody care straw for the internal administration of native state so long as oppression and crime be keep within decent limit and the ruler be not drug drunk or diseased from one end of the year to the other native state be create by providence in order to supply picturesque scenery tiger and tall writing they be the dark place of the earth full of unimaginable cruelty touch the railway and the telegraph on one side and on the other the day of harun al raschid when leave the train do business with diver king and in eight day pass through many change of life sometimes wear dress clothe and consort with prince and political drink from crystal and eat from silver sometimes lie out upon the ground and devour what could get from plate make of flapjack and drink the run water and sleep under the same rug as my servant it be all in day ’s work then head for the great indian desert upon the proper date as have promise and the night mail set down at marwar junction where funny little happy go lucky native manage railway run to jodhpore the bombay mail from delhi make short halt at marwar she arrive as get in and have just time to hurry to her platform and go down the carriage there be only one second class on the train great and shin face ticket again say he
no say i. be to tell you that he be go south for the week he be go south for the week the train have begin to move out the red man rub his eye he have go south for the week he repeat now that just like his impudence do he say that be to give you anything?—’cause will not he do not say and drop away and watch the red light die out in the dark it be horribly cold because the wind be blow off the sand climb into my own train not an intermediate carriage this time and go to sleep if the man with the beard have give rupee should have keep it as memento of rather curious affair
but the consciousness of having do my duty be my only reward later on reflect that two gentleman like my friend could not do any good if they foregather and personate correspondent of newspaper and might if they stick up one of the little rat trap state of central india or southern rajputana get themselves into serious difficulty therefore take some trouble to describe they as accurately as could remember to people who would be interested in deport they and succeed so be later inform in have they head back from the degumber border then become respectable and return to an office where there be no king and no incident except the daily manufacture of newspaper newspaper office seem to attract every conceivable sort of person to the prejudice of discipline zenana mission lady arrive and beg that the editor will instantly abandon all his duty to describe christian prize give in back slum of perfectly inaccessible village colonel who have be overpasse for command sit down and sketch the outline of series of ten twelve or twenty four lead article on seniority versus selection missionary wish to know why they have not be permit to escape from their regular vehicle of abuse and swear at brother missionary under special patronage of the editorial we strand theatrical company troop up to explain that they can not pay for their advertisement but on their return from new zealand or tahiti will do so with interest inventor of patent punkah pull machine carriage coupling and unbreakable sword and axle tree call with specification in their pocket and hour at their disposal tea company enter and elaborate their prospectus with the office pen secretary of ball committee clamor to have the glory of their last dance more fully expound strange lady rustle in and say:—“i want hundred lady ’s card print at once please which be manifestly part of an editor ’s duty and every dissolute ruffian that ever tramp the grand trunk road make it his business to ask for employment as proof reader and all the time the telephone bell be re madly and king be be kill on the continent and empire be say you ’re another and mister gladstone be call down brimstone upon the british dominion and the little black copy boy be whine kaa pi chayha yeh copy want like tired bee and most of the paper be as blank as modre ’s shield but that be the amusing part of the year there be other six month wherein none ever come to call and the thermometer walk inch by inch up to the top of the glass and the office be darken to just above read light and the press machine be red hot of touch and nobody write anything but account of amusement in the hill station or obituary notice then the telephone become tinkle terror because it tell you of the sudden death of man and woman that you know intimately and the prickly heat cover you as with garment and you sit down and write:—“a slight increase of sickness be report from the khuda janta khan district the outbreak be purely sporadic in its nature and thank to the energetic effort of the district authority be now almost at an end it be however with deep regret we record the death etc then the sickness really break out and the less recording and report the well for the peace of the subscriber but the empire and the king continue to divert themselves as selfishly as before and the foreman think that daily paper really ought to come out once in twenty four hour and all the people at the hill station in the middle of their amusement say:—“good gracious why can not the paper be sparkle sure there plenty go on up here that be the dark half of the moon and as the advertisement say must be experience to be appreciate it be in that season and remarkably evil season that the paper begin run the last issue of the week on saturday night which be to say sunday morning after the custom of london paper this be great convenience for immediately after the paper be put to bed the dawn would lower the thermometer from to almost for almost half an hour and in that chill you have no idea how cold be on the grass until you begin to pray for it very tired man could set off to sleep ere the heat rouse he one saturday night it be my pleasant duty to put the paper to bed alone king or courtier or courtesan or community be go to die or get new constitution or do something that be important on the other side of the world and the paper be to be hold open till the late possible minute in order to catch the telegram it be pitchy black night as stifle as june night can be and the loo the red hot wind from the westward be boom among the tinder dry tree and pretend that the rain be on its heel now and again spot of almost boil water would fall on the dust with the flop of frog but all our weary world know that be only pretence it be shade cool in the press room than the office so sit there while the type tick and click and the night jar hoot at the window and the all but naked compositor wipe the sweat from their forehead and call for water the thing that be keep we back whatever it be would not come off though the loo drop and the last type be set and the whole round earth stand still in the choke heat with its finger on its lip to wait the event drowse and wonder whether the telegraph be bless and whether this die man or struggle people be aware of the inconvenience the delay be cause there be no special reason beyond the heat and worry to make tension but as the clock hand creep up to three o’clock and the machine spin their fly wheel two and three time to see that all be in order before say the word that would set they off could have shriek aloud then the roar and rattle of the wheel shiver the quiet into little bit rise to go away but two man in white clothe stand in front of the first one said:—“it he the second said—“so it be and they both laugh almost as loudly as the machinery roar and mop their forehead we see there be light burn across the road and we be sleep in that ditch brother peachey carnehan that he and brother daniel dravot that be and the less say about our profession the well for we have be most thing in our time soldier sailor compositor photographer proof reader street preacher and correspondent of the backwoodsman when we think the paper want one carnehan be sober and so be i. look at we first and see that sure it will save you cut into my talk we ’ll take one of your cigar apiece and you shall see we light watch the test the man be absolutely sober
so give they each tepid peg well and good
say carnehan of the eyebrow wipe the froth from his mustache let talk now dan we have be all over india mostly on foot we have be boiler fitter engine driver petty contractor and all that
and we have decide that india be not big enough for such as we they certainly be too big for the office dravot ’s beard seem to fill half the room and carnehan ’s shoulder the other half as they sit on the big table carnehan continued:—“the country be not half work out because they that govern it will not let you touch it they spend all their bless time in govern it and you can not lift spade nor chip rock nor look for oil nor anything like that without all the government saying—‘leave it alone and let we govern therefore such as it be we will let it alone and go away to some other place where man be not crowd and can come to his own we be not little man and there be nothing that we be afraid of except drink and we have sign contrack on that therefore we be go away to be king king in our own right mutter dravot yes of course say you ve be tramp in the sun and it very warm night and have not you well sleep over the notion come to morrow neither drunk nor sunstruck say dravot we have sleep over the notion half year and require to see book and atlas and we have decide that there be only one place now in the world that two strong man can sar whack they call it kafiristan by my reckon its the top right hand corner of afghanistan not more than three hundred mile from peshawar they have two and thirty heathen idol there and we ’ll be the thirty third it mountainous country and the woman of those part be very beautiful but that be provide against in the contrack say carnehan neither woman nor liquor daniel and that all we know except that no one have go there and they fight and in any place where they fight man who know how to drill man can always be king we shall go to those part and say to any king we find—‘d you want to vanquish your foe and we will show he how to drill man for that we know well than anything else then we will subvert that king and seize his throne and establish dy nasty you ’ll be cut to piece before you ’re fifty mile across the border say you have to travel through afghanistan to get to that country it one mass of mountain and peak and glacier and no englishman have be through it the people be utter brute and even if you reach they you could not do anything that more like say carnehan if you could think we little more mad we would be more pleased we have come to you to know about this country to read book about it and to be show map we want you to tell we that we be fool and to show we your book he turn to the book case be you at all in earnest little say dravot sweetly as big map as you have get even if it all blank where kafiristan be and any book you ve get we can read though we be not very educate uncase the big thirty two mile to the inch map of india and two small frontier map haul down volume inf kan of the right at jagdallak through laghmann territory then we get among the hill fourteen thousand foot fifteen thousand it will be cold work there but it do not look very far on the map hand he wood on the source of the oxus carnehan be deep in the encyclopædia they ’re mix lot say dravot reflectively and it will not help we to know the name of their tribe the more tribe the more they ’ll fight and the well for we from jagdallak to ashang h’mm but all the information about the country be as sketchy and inaccurate as can be protest no one know anything about it really here the file of the united and we will not steal any of the paper do not you sit up we ’re two harmless lunatic and if you come to morrow evening down to the serai we ’ll say good by to you you be two fool answer you ’ll be turn back at the frontier or cut up the minute you set foot in afghanistan do you want any money or recommendation down country can help you to the chance of work next week next week we shall be hard at work ourselves
thank you say dravot it be not so easy be king as it look when we ve get our kingdom in go order we ’ll let you know and you can come up and help we to govern it would two lunatic make contrack like that say carnehan with subdue pride show greasy half sheet of note paper on which be write the follow copy it then and there as curiosity this contract between and you persue witnesseth in the name of god amen and so forth one that and you will settle this matter together i.e. to be king of kafiristan two that you and will not while this matter be be settle look at any liquor nor any woman black white or brown so as to get mix up with one or the other harmful three that we conduct ourselves with dignity and discretion and if one of we get into trouble the other will stay by he sign by you and this day peachey taliaferro carnehan daniel dravot both gentleman at large there be no need for the last article say carnehan blush modestly but it look regular now you know the sort of man that loafer be we be loafer dan until we get out of india and do you think that we could sign contrack like that unless we be in earnest we have keep away from the two thing that make life worth have you will not enjoy your life much long if you be go to try this idiotic adventure do not set the office on fire say and go away before nine o’clock leave they still pore over the map and make note on the back of the contrack be sure to come down to the serai to morrow be their parting word the kumharsen serai be the great four square sink of humanity where the string of camel and horse from the north load and unload all the nationality of central asia may be find there and most of the folk of india proper balkh and bokhara there meet bengal and bombay and try to draw eye tooth you can buy pony turquoise persian pussy cat saddle bag fat tailed sheep and musk in the kumharsen serai and get many strange thing for nothing in the afternoon go down there to see whether my friend intend to keep their word or be lie about drunk priest attire in fragment of ribbon and rag stalk up to gravely twist child ’s paper whirligig behind he be his servant bend under the load of crate of mud toy the two be load up two camel and the inhabitant of the serai watch they with shriek of laughter the priest be mad say horse dealer to he be go up to kabul to sell toy to the amir he will either be raise to be complete to the native mind there be just the chance therefore that carnehan and dravot would be able to wander through afghanistan without detection but beyond they would find death certain and awful death ten day later native friend of mine give the news of the day from peshawar wind up his letter with:—“there have be much laughter here on account of certain mad priest who be go in his estimation to sell petty gauds and insignificant trinket which he ascribe as great charm to h. h. the amir of bokhara he pass through peshawar and associate himself to the second summer caravan that go to kabul the merchant be pleased because through superstition they imagine that such mad fellow bring good fortune the two then be beyond the border would have pray for they but that night real king die in europe and demand an obituary notice the wheel of the world swing through the same phase again and again summer pass and winter thereafter and come and pass again the daily paper continue and with it and upon the third summer there fall hot night night issue and strained wait for something to be telegraph from the other side of the world exactly as have happen before few great man have die in the past two year the machine work with more clatter and some of the tree in the office garden be few foot tall but that be all the difference pass over to the press room and go through just such scene as have already describe the nervous tension be strong than it have be two year before and feel the heat more acutely at three o’clock cry print off and turn to go when there creep to my chair what be leave of man he be bent into circle his head be sink between his shoulder and he move his foot one over the other like bear could hardly see whether he walk or crawl this rag wrap whine cripple who address by name cry that he be come back can you give drink he whimper for the lord ’s sake give drink go back to the office the man follow with groan of pain and turn up the lamp do not you know he gasp drop into chair and he turn his draw face surmount by shock of gray hair to the light look at he intently once before have see eyebrow that meet over the nose in an inch broad black band but for the life of could not tell where do not know you say hand he the whiskey what can do for you he take gulp of the spirit raw and shiver in spite of the suffocate heat ve come back he lord be more than little astonished and express my feeling accordingly it true say carnehan with dry cackle nurse his foot which be wrap in rag true as gospel king we be with crown upon our head and dravot poor dan
oh poor poor dan that would never take advice not though beg of he take the whiskey say and take your own time tell all you can recollect of everything from begin to end you get across the border on your camel dravot dress as mad priest and you his servant do you remember that ai not mad yet but will be that way soon of course remember keep look at or maybe my word will go all to piece keep look at in my eye and do not say anything lean forward and look into his face as steadily as could he drop one hand upon the table and grasp it by the wrist it be twist like bird ’s claw and upon the back be ragged red diamond shape scar no do not look there look at say carnehan that come afterwards but for the lord ’s sake do not distrack we leave with that caravan and dravot play all sort of antic to amuse the people we be with dravot use to make we laugh in the evening when all the people be cook their dinner cook their dinner and what do they do then they light little fire with spark that go into dravot ’s beard and we all laugh fit to die little red fire they be go into dravot ’s big red beard so funny his eye leave mine
and he smile foolishly you go as far as jagdallak with that caravan say at venture after you have light those fire to jagdallak where you turn off to try to get into kafiristan
no we do not neither what be you talk about we turn off before jagdallak because we hear the road be good but they be not good enough for our two camel mine and dravot ’s when we leave the caravan dravot take off all his clothe and mine too and say we would be heathen because the kafir do not allow mohammedan to talk to they so we dress betwixt and between and such sight as daniel dravot never see yet nor expect to see again he burn half his beard and sling sheep skin over his shoulder and shave his head into pattern he shave mine too and make wear outrageous thing to look like heathen that be in most mountaineous country and our camel could not go along any more because of the mountain they be tall and black and come home see they fight like wild goat there be lot of goat in kafiristan and these mountain they never keep still no more than the goat always fight they be and do not let you sleep at night take some more whiskey say very slowly what do you and daniel dravot do when the camel could go no far because of the rough road that lead into kafiristan what do which do there be party call peachey taliaferro carnehan that be with dravot shall tell you about he he die out there in the cold slap from the bridge fall old peachey turn and twisting in the air like penny whirligig that you can sell to the amir
no they be two for three ha’pence those whirligig or be much mistaken and woful sore and then these camel be no use and peachey say to dravot—‘for the lord ’s sake let ’s get out of this before our head be chop off and with that they kill the camel all among the mountain not have anything in particular to eat
but first they take off the box with the gun and the ammunition till two man come along drive four mule dravot up and dance in front of they singing,—‘sell four mule say the first man,—‘if you be rich enough to buy you be rich enough to rob but before ever he could put his hand to his knife dravot break his neck over his knee and the other party run away
so carnehan load the mule with the rifle that be take off the camel and together we start forward into those bitter cold mountainous part and never road broad than the back of your hand he pause for moment while ask he if he could remember the nature of the country through which he have journey be tell you as straight as can but my head be not as good as it might be they drive nail through it to make hear well how dravot die the country be mountainous and the mule be most contrary and the inhabitant be disperse and solitary they go up and up and down and down and that other party carnehan be implore of dravot not to sing and whistle so loud for fear of bring down the tremenjus avalanche but dravot say that if king could not sing it be not worth be king and whack the mule over the rump and never take no heed for ten cold day we come to big level valley all among the mountain and the mule be near dead
so we kill they not have anything in special for they or we to eat we sit upon the box and play odd and even with the cartridge that be jolt out then ten man with bow and arrow run down that valley chase twenty man with bow and arrow and the row be tremenju they be fair man fair than you or with yellow hair and remarkable well build say dravot unpack the guns—‘this be the beginning of the business we ’ll fight for the ten man and with that he fire two rifle at the twenty man and drop one of they at two hundred yard from the rock where we be sit the other man begin to run but carnehan and dravot sit on the box pick they off at all range up and down the valley then we go up to the ten man that have run across the snow too and they fire footy little arrow at we dravot he shoot above their head and they all fall down flat then he walk over they and kick they
and then he lift they up and shake hand all around to make they friendly like he call they and give they the box to carry and wave his hand for all the world as though he be king already they take the box and he across the valley and up the hill into pine wood on the top where there be half dozen big stone idol dravot he go to the big fellow they call imbra and lay rifle and cartridge at his foot rub his nose respectful with his own nose pat he on the head and salute in front of it he turn round to the man and nod his head and says,—‘that all right in the know too and these old jim jam be my friend then he open his mouth and point down it and when the first man bring he food he says—‘no and when the second man bring he food he says—‘no
but when one of the old priest and the boss of the village bring he food he says—‘yes very haughty and eat it slow that be how we come to our first village without any trouble just as though we have tumble from the sky
but we tumble from one of those damn rope bridge you see and you could not expect man to laugh much after that take some more whiskey and go on say that be the first village you come into how do you get to be king be not king say carnehan dravot he be the king and handsome man he look with the gold crown on his head and all he and the other party stay in that village and every morning dravot sit by the side of old imbra and the people come and worship that be dravot ’s order then lot of man come into the valley and carnehan and dravot pick they off with the rifle before they know where they be and run down into the valley and up again the other side and find another village same as the first one and the people all fall down flat on their face and dravot says,—‘now
what be the trouble between you two village and the people point to woman as fair as you or that be carry off and dravot take she back to the first village and count up the dead eight there be for each dead man dravot pour little milk on the ground and wave his arm like whirligig and that all right say he then he and carnehan take the big boss of each village by the arm and walk they down into the valley and show they how to scratch line with spear right down the valley and give each sod of turf from both side the line then all the people come down and shout like the devil and all and dravot says,—‘go and dig the land and be fruitful and multiply which they do though they do not understand then we ask the name of thing in their lingo bread and water and fire and idol and such and dravot lead the priest of each village up to the idol and say he must sit there and judge the people and if anything go wrong he be to be shoot next week they be all turn up the land in the valley as quiet as bee and much pretty and the priest hear all the complaint and tell dravot in dumb show what it be about that just the beginning say dravot they think we ’re god he and carnehan pick out twenty good man and show they how to click off rifle and form four and advance in line and they be very pleased to do so and clever to see the hang of it then he take out his pipe and his baccy pouch and leave one at one village and one at the other and off we two go to see what be to be do in the next valley that be all rock and there be little village there and carnehan says,—‘send ’em to the old valley to plant and take ’em there and give ’em some land that be not take before they be poor lot and we blood ’em with kid before let ’em into the new kingdom that be to impress the people and then they settle down quiet and carnehan go back to dravot who have get into another valley all snow and ice and most mountainous there be no people there and the army get afraid so dravot shoot one of they and go on till he find some people in village and the army explain that unless the people want to be kill they have well not shoot their little matchlock for they have matchlock we make friend with the priest and stay there alone with two of the army teach the man how to drill and thunder big chief come across the snow with kettledrum and horn twang because he hear there be new god kick about carnehan sight for the brown of the man half mile across the snow and wing one of they then he send message to the chief that unless he wish to be kill he must come and shake hand with and leave his arm behind the chief come alone first and carnehan shake hand with he and whirl his arm about same as dravot use and very much surprised that chief be and stroke my eyebrow then carnehan go alone to the chief and ask he in dumb show if he have an enemy he hat have say the chief so carnehan weed out the pick of his man and set the two of the army to show they drill and at the end of two week the man can manœuvre about as well as volunteer so he march with the chief to great big plain on the top of mountain and the chief man rush into village and take it we three martini fire into the brown of the enemy
so we take that village too and give the chief rag from my coat and say occupy till come which be scriptural by way of reminder when and the army be eighteen hundred yard away drop bullet near he stand on the snow and all the people fall flat on their face then send letter to dravot wherever he be by land or by sea at the risk of throw the creature out of train interrupted,—“how could you write letter up yonder the letter?—oh the letter keep look at between the eye please it be string talk letter that we ’d learn the way of it from blind beggar in the punjab remember that there have once come to the office blind man with knotted twig and piece of string which he wind round the twig accord to some cypher of his own he could after the lapse of day or hour repeat the sentence which he have reel up he have reduce the alphabet to eleven primitive sound and try to teach his method but fail send that letter to dravot say carnehan and tell he to come back because this kingdom be grow too big for to handle and then strike for the first valley to see how the priest be work they call the village we take along with the chief bashkai and the first village we take er heb the priest at er heb be do all right but they have lot of pende case about land to show and some man from another village have be fire arrow at night go out and look for that village and fire four round at it from thousand yard that use all the cartridge care to spend and wait for dravot who have be away two or three month and keep my people quiet one morning hear the devil ’s own noise of drum and horn and dan dravot march down the hill with his army and tail of hundred of man and which be the most amazing great gold crown on his head my gord
carnehan say daniel this be tremenjus business and we ve get the whole country as far as it worth have be the son of alexander by queen semiramis and you ’re my young brother and god too it the big thing we ve ever see ve be march and fight for six week with the army and every footy little village for fifty mile have come in rejoiceful and more than that ve get the key of the whole show as you ’ll see and ve get crown for you tell ’em to make two of ’em at place call shu where the gold lie in the rock like suet in mutton gold ve see and turquoise ve kick out of the cliff and there garnet in the sand of the river and here chunk of amber that man bring call up all the priest and here take your crown one of the man open black hair bag and slip the crown on it be too small and too heavy but wear it for the glory hammer gold it be five pound weight like hoop of barrel peachey say dravot we do not want to fight no more the craft the trick so help and he bring forward that same chief that leave at bashkai billy fish we call he afterwards because he be so like billy fish that drive the big tank engine at mach on the bolan in the old day shake hand with he say dravot and shake hand and nearly drop for billy fish give the grip say nothing but try he with the fellow craft grip he answer all right and try the master ’s grip but that be slip fellow craft he be say to dan do he know the word he do say dan and all the priest know it miracle the chief and the priest can work fellow craft lodge in way that very like our
and they ve cut the mark on the rock but they do not know the third degree
and they ve come to find out it gord ’s truth ve know these long year that the afghans know up to the fellow craft degree but this be miracle god and grand master of the craft be and lodge in the third degree will open and we ’ll raise the head priest and the chief of the village it against all the law say hold lodge without warrant from any one and we never hold office in any lodge it master stroke of policy say dravot it mean run the country as easy as four wheel bogy on down grade we can not stop to inquire now or they ’ll turn against we ve forty chief at my heel and pass and raise accord to their merit they shall be billet these man on the village and see that we run up lodge of some kind the temple of imbra will do for the lodge room the woman must make apron as you show they ’ll hold levee of chief tonight and lodge to morrow be fair rim off my leg but be not such fool as not to see what pull this craft business give we show the priest family how to make apron of the degree but for dravot ’s apron the blue border and mark be make of turquoise lump on white hide not cloth we take great square stone in the temple for the master ’s chair and little stone for the officer chair and paint the black pavement with white square and do what we could to make thing regular at the levee which be hold that night on the hillside with big bonfire dravot give out that he and be god and son of alexander and past grand master in the craft and be come to make kafiristan country where every man should eat in peace and drink in quiet and specially obey we then the chief come round to shake hand and they be so hairy and white and fair it be just shake hand with old friend we give they name accord as they be like man we have know in india billy fish holly dilworth pikky kergan that be bazar master when be at mhow and so on and so on the most amazing miracle be at lodge next night one of the old priest be watch we continuous and feel uneasy for know we ’d have to fudge the ritual and do not know what the man know the old priest be strange come in from beyond the village of bashkai the minute dravot put on the master ’s apron that the girl have make for he the priest fetch whoop and howl and try to overturn the stone that dravot be sit on it all up now say that come of meddle with the craft without warrant dravot never wink an eye not when ten priest take and tilt over the grand master ’s chair which be to say the stone of imbra the priest begin rub the bottom end of it to clear away the black dirt and presently he show all the other priest the master ’s mark same as be on dravot ’s apron cut into the stone not even the priest of the temple of imbra know it be there the old chap fall flat on his face at dravot ’s foot and kiss ’em luck again say dravot across the lodge to they say it the miss mark that no one could understand the why of we ’re more than safe now then he bang the butt of his gun for gavel and says:—‘by virtue of the authority vest in by my own right hand and the help of peachey declare myself grand master of all freemasonry in kafiristan in this the mother lodge the country and king of kafiristan equally with peachey at that he put on his crown and put on mine be do senior warden
and we open the lodge in most ample form it be amazing miracle the priest move in lodge through the first two degree almost without tell as if the memory be come back to they after that peachey and dravot raise such as be worthy high priest and chief of far off village billy fish be the first and can tell you we scar the soul out of he it be not in any way accord to ritual but it serve our turn we do not raise more than ten of the big man because we do not want to make the degree common and they be clamor to be raise in another six month say dravot we ’ll hold another communication and see how you be work then he ask they about their village and learn that they be fight one against the other and be fair sick and tired of it and when they be not do that they be fight with the mohammedans you can fight those when they come into our country say dravot tell off every tenth man of your tribe for frontier guard and send two hundred at time to this valley to be drill nobody be go to be shoot or spear any more so long as he do well and know that you will not cheat because you ’re white people son of alexander and not like common black mohammedans you be my people and by god say he run off into english at the end—‘i’ll make damned fine nation of you or ’ll die in the making can not tell all we do for the next six month because dravot do lot could not see the hang of and he learn their lingo in way never could my work be to help the people plough and now and again to go out with some of the army and see what the other village be do and make ’em throw rope bridge across the ravine which cut up the country horrid dravot be very kind to but when he walk up and down in the pine wood pull that bloody red beard of his with both fist know he be think plan could not advise he about and just wait for order but dravot never show disrespect before the people they be afraid of and the army but they love dan he be the good of friend with the priest and the chief but any one could come across the hill with complaint and dravot would hear he out fair and call four priest together and say what be to be do he use to call in billy fish from bashkai and pikky kergan from shu and an old chief we call kafuzelum it be like enough to his real name and hold council with ’em when there be any fighting to be do in small village that be his council of war and the four priest of bashkai shu khawak and madora be his privy council between the lot of ’em they send with forty man and twenty rifle and sixty man carry turquoise into the ghorband country to buy those hand make martini rifle that come out of the amir ’s workshop at kabul from one of the amir ’s herati regiment that would have sell the very tooth out of their mouth for turquoise stay in ghorband month and give the governor the pick of my basket for hush money and bribe the colonel of the regiment some more and between the two and the tribe people we get more than hundred hand make martini hundred good kohat jezail that ’ll throw to six hundred yard and forty manload of very bad ammunition for the rifle come back with what have and distribute ’em among the man that the chief send in to to drill dravot be too busy to attend to those thing but the old army that we first make help and we turn out five hundred man that could drill and two hundred that know how to hold arm pretty straight even those cork screw hand make gun be miracle to they dravot talk big about powder shop and factory walk up and down in the pine wood when the winter be come on will not make nation say he ’ll make an empire these man be not nigger they ’re english look at their eye look at their mouth look at the way they stand up they sit on chair in their own house they ’re the lost tribe or something like it and they ve grow to be english ’ll take census in the spring if the priest do not get frightened there must be fair two million of ’em in these hill the village be full little child two million people two hundred and fifty thousand fight man and all english they only want the rifle and little drilling two hundred and fifty thousand man ready to cut in on russia ’s right flank when she try for india peachey man he say chew his beard in great hunk we shall be emperor emperor of the earth rajah brooke will be suckle to we ’ll treat with the viceroy on equal term ’ll ask he to send twelve pick english twelve that know of to help we govern bit there mackray sergeant pensioner at segowli many the good dinner he ’s give and his wife pair of trouser there donkin the warder of tounghoo jail there hundred that could lay my hand on if be in india the viceroy shall do it for ’ll send man through in the spring for those man and ’ll write for dispensation from the grand lodge for what ve do as grand master that and all the snider that ’ll be throw out when the native troop in india take up the martini they ’ll be wear smooth
but they ’ll do for fight in these hill twelve english hundred thousand snider run through the amir ’s country in driblet ’d be content with twenty thousand in one year
and we ’d be an empire when everything be ship shape ’d hand over the crown this crown wear now to queen victoria on my knee
and she ’d say:—“rise up sir daniel dravot
oh its big it big tell you
but there so much to be do in every place bashkai khawak shu and everywhere else what be it there be no more man come in to be drill this autumn look at those fat black cloud they ’re bring the snow it be not that say daniel put his hand very hard on my shoulder and do not wish to say anything that against you for no other live man would have follow and make what be as you have do you ’re first class commander in chief and the people know you but it big country
and somehow you can not help peachey in the way want to be help go to your blast priest then say and be sorry when make that remark
but it do hurt sore to find daniel talk so superior when ’d drill all the man and do all he tell do not let ’s quarrel peachey say daniel without curse you ’re king too and the half of this kingdom be yours
but can not you see peachey we want clever man than we now three or four of em that we can scatter about for our deputy it hugeous great state and can not always tell the right thing to do and have not time for all want to do and here the winter come on and all he put half his beard into his mouth and it be as red as the gold of his crown sorry daniel say i. ve do all could ve drill the man and show the people how to stack their oat well and ve bre in those tinware rifle from ghorband
but know what you ’re drive at take it king always feel oppress that way there another thing too say dravot walk up and down the winter ’s come
and these people will not be give much trouble and if they do we can not move about want wife for gord ’s sake leave the woman alone we ve both get all the work we can though be fool remember the contrack and keep clear woman the contrack only last till such time as we be king and king we have be these month past say dravot weigh his crown in his hand you go get wife too peachey nice strappin plump girl that ’ll keep you warm in the winter they ’re pretty than english girl and we can take the pick of ’em boil ’em once or twice in hot water and they ’ll come as fair as chicken and ham do not tempt will not have any dealing with woman not till we be dam side more settle than we be now ve be do the work two man
and you ve be do the work three let ’s lie off bit and see if we can get some well tobacco from afghan country and run in some good liquor but no woman who ’s talk woman say dravot say wife queen to breed king ’s son for the king queen out of the strong tribe that ’ll make they your blood brother and that ’ll lie by your side and tell you all the people think about you and their own affair that what want do you remember that bengali woman keep at mogul serai when be plate layer say i. fat lot good
she be to she teach the lingo and one or two other thing but what happen she run away with the station master ’s servant and half my month ’s pay then she turn up at dadur junction in tow of half caste and have the impidence to say be her husband all among the driver of the running shed we ve do with that say dravot these woman be white than you or and queen will have for the winter month for the last time ask dan do not say it ’ll only bring we harm the bible say that king ai not to waste their strength on woman specially when they ve get new raw kingdom to work over for the last time of answering will say dravot and he go away through the pine tree look like big red devil the low sun hit his crown and beard on one side and the two blaze like hot coal but get wife be not as easy as dan think he put it before the council and there be no answer till billy fish say that he ’d well ask the girl dravot damn they all round what wrong with he shout stand by the idol imbra be dog or be not enough of man for your wench have not put the shadow of my hand over this country who stop the last afghan raid it be really but dravot be too angry to remember who buy your gun who repair the bridge who the grand master of the sign cut in the stone
and he thump his hand on the block that he use to sit on in lodge and at council which open like lodge always billy fish say nothing and no more do the other keep your hair on dan say and ask the girl that how it do at home and these people be quite english the marriage of king be matter of state say dan in white hot rage for he could feel hope that he be go against his well mind he walk out of the council room and the other sit still look at the ground billy fish say to the chief of bashkai what the difficulty here straight answer to true friend you know say billy fish how should man tell you who know everything how can daughter of man marry god or devil it not proper remember something like that in the bible
but if after see we as long as they have they still believe we be god it be not for to undeceive they god can do anything say i. if the king be fond of girl he ’ll not let she die she ’ll have to say billy fish there be all sort of god and devil in these mountain and now and again girl marry one of they and be not see any more besides you two know the mark cut in the stone only the god know that we think you be man till you show the sign of the master wish then that we have explain about the loss of the genuine secret of master mason at the first go off but say nothing all that night there be blow of horn in little dark temple half way down the hill and hear girl cry fit to die one of the priest tell we that she be be prepare to marry the king ’ll have no nonsense of that kind say dan do not want to interfere with your custom but ’ll take my own wife the girl little bit afraid say the priest she think she ’s go to die and they be heartening of she up down in the temple hearten she very tender then say dravot or ’ll hearten you with the butt of gun so that you ’ll never want to be hearten again he lick his lip do dan and stay up walk about more than half the night think of the wife that he be go to get in the morning be not any mean comfortable for know
that deal with woman in foreign part though you be crown king twenty time over could not but be risky get up very early in the morning while dravot be asleep and see the priest talk together in whisper and the chief talk together too and they look at out of the corner of their eye what be up fish say to the bashkai man who be wrap up in his fur and look splendid to behold can not rightly say say he
but if you can induce the king to drop all this nonsense about marriage you ’ll be do he and and yourself great service that do believe say i.
but sure you know billy as well as having fight against and for we that the king and be nothing more than two of the fine man that god almighty ever make nothing more do assure you that may be say billy fish and yet should be sorry if it be he sink his head upon his great fur cloak for minute and think king say he be you man or god or devil ’ll stick by you to day have twenty of my man with and they will follow we ’ll go to bashkai until the storm blow over little snow have fall in the night and everything be white except the greasy fat cloud that blow down and down from the north dravot come out with his crown on his head swing his arm and stamp his foot and look more pleased than punch for the last time drop it dan say in whisper billy fish here say that there will be row row among my people say dravot not much peachy you ’re fool not to get wife too where the girl say he with voice as loud as the braying of jackass call up all the chief and priest and let the emperor see if his wife suit he there be no need to call any one they be all there lean on their gun and spear round the clearing in the centre of the pine wood deputation of priest go down to the little temple to bring up the girl and the horn blow up fit to wake the dead billy fish saunter round and get as close to daniel as he could and behind he stand his twenty man with matchlock not man of they under six foot be next to dravot and behind be twenty man of the regular army up come the girl and strap wench she be cover with silver and turquoise but white as death and look back every minute at the priest she ’ll do say dan look she over what to be afraid of lass come and kiss he put his arm round
she she shut her eye give bit of squeak and down go her face in the side of dan ’s flame red beard the slut ’s bite say he clap his hand to his neck and sure enough his hand be red with blood billy fish and two of his matchlock man catch hold of dan by the shoulder and drag he into the bashkai lot while the priest howl in their lingo,—‘neither god nor devil but man be all take aback for priest cut at in front and the army behind begin fire into the bashkai man god mighty say dan what be the meaning this come back come away
say billy fish ruin and mutiny be the matter we ’ll break for bashkai if we can try to give some sort of order to my man the man the regular army
but it be no use so fire into the brown of ’em with an english martini and drill three beggar in line the valley be full of shout howl creature and every soul be shriek not god nor devil but only man the bashkai troop stick to billy fish all they be worth but their matchlock be not half as good as the kabul breech loader and four of they drop dan be bellow like bull for he be very wrathy and billy fish have hard job to prevent he run out at the crowd we can not stand say billy fish make run for it down the valley the whole place be against we the matchlock man run
and we go down the valley in spite of dravot ’s protestation he be swear horribly and cry out that he be king the priest roll great stone on we and the regular army fire hard and there be not more than six man not count dan billy fish and that come down to the bottom of the valley alive then they stop fire and the horn in the temple blow again come away for gord ’s sake come away say billy fish they ’ll send runner out to all the village before ever we get to bashkai can protect you there but can not do anything now my own notion be that dan begin to go mad in his head from that hour he star up and down like stuck pig then he be all for walk back alone and kill the priest with his bare hand which he could have do an emperor be say daniel and next year shall be knight of the queen all right dan say but come along now while there time it your fault say he for not look after your army well there be mutiny in the midst and you do not know you damn engine drive plate lie missionary ’s pass hunting hound he sit upon rock and call every foul name he could lay tongue to be too heart sick to care though it be all his foolishness that bring the smash sorry dan say
but there no accounting for native this business be our fifty seven maybe we ’ll make something out of it yet when we ve get to bashkai let ’s get to bashkai then say dan and by god when come back here again ’ll sweep the valley so there be not bug in blanket leave we walk all that day and all that night dan be stump up and down on the snow chew his beard and mutter to himself there no hope get clear say billy fish the priest will have send runner to the village to say that you be only man why do not you stick on as god till thing be more settle dead man say billy fish and he throw himself down on the snow and begin to pray to his god next morning we be in cruel bad country all up and down no level ground at all and no food either the six bashkai man look at billy fish hungry wise as if they want to ask something
but they say never word at noon we come to the top of flat mountain all cover with snow and when we climb up into it behold there be an army in position wait in the middle the runner have be very quick
say billy fish with little bit of laugh they be wait for we three or four man begin to fire from the enemy ’s side and chance shoot take daniel in the calf of the leg that bring he to his sen he look across the snow at the army and see the rifle that we have bre into the country we ’re do for say he they be englishman these
people,—and it my blasted nonsense that have bre you to this get back billy fish and take your man away you ve do what you could and now cut for it carnehan say he shake hand with and go along with billy maybe they will not kill you ’ll go and meet ’em alone it that do it the king go say i. go to hell dan with you here billy fish you clear out and we two will meet those folk chief say billy fish quite quiet stay with you my man can go the bashkai fellow do not wait for second word but run off and dan and and billy fish walk across to where the drum be drum and the horn be horn it be cold awful cold ve get that cold in the back of my head now there lump of it there the punkah cooly have go to sleep two kerosene lamp be blaze in the office and the perspiration pour down my face and splash on the blotter as lean forward carnehan be shiver and fear that his mind might go wipe my face take fresh grip of the piteously mangle hand and said:—“what happen after that the momentary shift of my eye have break the clear current what be you pleased to say whine carnehan they take they without any sound not little whisper all along the snow not though the king knock down the first man that set hand on he not though old peachey fire his last cartridge into the brown of ’em not single solitary sound do those swine make they just close up tight and tell you their fur stink there be man call billy fish good friend of we all and they cut his throat sir then and there like pig and the king kick up the bloody snow and says:—‘we’ve have dash fine run for our money what ’s come next but peachey peachey taliaferro tell you sir in confidence as betwixt two friend he lose his head sir
no he do not neither the king lose his head so he do all along one of those cunning rope bridge kindly let have the paper cutter sir it tilt this way they march he mile across that snow to rope bridge over ravine with river at the bottom you may have see such they prod he behind like an ox damn your eye say the king d’you suppose can not die like gentleman he turn to peachey peachey that be cry like child ve bring you to this peachey say he bring you out of your happy life to be kill in kafiristan where you be late commander in chief of the emperor ’s force say you forgive peachey do say peachey fully and freely do forgive you dan shake hand peachey say he go now out he go look neither right nor leave and when he be plumb in the middle of those dizzy dancing rope cut you beggar he shout and they cut and old dan fall turn round and round and round twenty thousand mile for he take half an hour to fall till he strike the water and could see his body catch on rock with the gold crown close beside
but do you know what they do to peachey between two pine tree they crucify he sir as peachey ’s hand will show they use wooden peg for his hand and his foot
and he do not die he hang there and scream and they take he down next day and say it be miracle that he be not dead they take he down poor old peachey that have not do they any harm that have not do they any he rock to and fro and weep bitterly wipe his eye with the back of his scarred hand and moan like child for some ten minute they be cruel enough to feed he up in the temple because they say he be more of god than old daniel that be man then they turn he out on the snow and tell he to go home and peachey come home in about year beg along the road quite safe for daniel dravot he walk before and said:—‘come along peachey it big thing we ’re do the mountain they dance at night and the mountain they try to fall on peachey ’s head but dan he hold up his hand and peachey come along bent double he never let go of dan ’s hand
and he never let go of dan ’s head they give it to he as present in the temple to remind he not to come again and though the crown be pure gold and peachey be starve never would peachey sell the same you know dravot sir you know right worshipful brother dravot look at he now he fumble in the mass of rag round his bent waist bring out black horsehair bag embroider with silver thread and shake therefrom on to my table the dry wither head of daniel dravot the morning sun that have long be pal the lamp strike the red beard and blind sink eye strike too heavy circlet of gold stud with raw turquoise that carnehan place tenderly on the batter temple you behold now say carnehan the emperor in his habit as he live the king of kafiristan with his crown upon his head poor old daniel that be monarch once shudder for in spite of defacement manifold recognize the head of the man of marwar junction carnehan rise to go attempt to stop he he be not fit to walk abroad let take away the whiskey and give little money he gasp be king once ’ll go to the deputy commissioner and ask to set in the poor house till get my health no thank
you can not wait till you get carriage for ve urgent private affair in the south at marwar he shamble out of the office and depart in the direction of the deputy commissioner ’s house that day at noon have occasion to go down the blind hot mall and see crooked man crawl along the white dust of the roadside his hat in his hand quaver dolorously after the fashion of street singer at home there be not soul in sight and he be out of all possible earshot of the house and he sing through his nose turn his head from right to left the son of man go forth to war golden crown to gain his blood red banner stream afar who follow in his train wait to hear no more but put the poor wretch into my carriage and drive he off to the near missionary for eventual transfer to the asylum he repeat the hymn twice while he be with whom he do not in the least recognize and leave he sing to the missionary two day later inquire after his welfare of the superintendent of the asylum he be admit suffer from sun stroke he die early yesterday morning say the superintendent be it true that he be half an hour bareheade in the sun at midday

but it might have be the kurile island for aught we know very early in the morning there pass we not cable 's length away
but as unseen as the spirit of the dead steamer of the same line as our she howl melodiously in answer to our bellowing and pass on suppose she have hit we say man from saigon then we should have go down answer the chief officer sweetly beastly thing to go down in fog say young gentleman who be travel for pleasure choke man both way know we be comfortably gather in the smoking room the weather be too cold to venture on the deck conversation naturally turn upon accident of fog the horn toot significantly in the pause between the tale hear of the wreck of the eric the cutting down of the strathnairn within half mile of harbour and the carrying away of the bow plate of the sigismund outside sandy hook it be astonishing say the man from saigon how many true story be put down as sea yarn it make man almost shrink from tell an anecdote
oh please do not shrink on our account say the smoking room with one voice it be not my own story say the man from saigon fellow on massagerie boat tell it he have be third officer of sort on geordie tramp one of those lumber dish bottom coal barge where the machinery be tie up with string and the plate be rivette with putty the way he tell his tale be this the tramp have be creep along some sea or other with chart ten year old and the hazy sort of chronometer when she get into fog just such fog as we have now here the smoking room turn round as one man and look through the window in the man 's own word just when the fog be thick the engine break down they have be do this for some week and we be too weary to care go forward of the bridge and lean over the side wonder where should ever get something that could call ship and whether the old hulk would fall to piece as she lie the fog be as thick as any london one but as white as steam while they be tinker at the engine below hear voice in the fog about twenty yard from the ship 's side call out can you climb on board if we throw you rope that startle because fancy we be go to be run down the next minute by ship engage in rescue man overboard shout for the engine room whistle and it whistle about five minute but never the sound of ship could we hear the ship 's boy come forward with some biscuit for as he put it into my hand hear the voice in the fog cry out about throw we rope this time it be the boy that yell ship on we and off go the whistle again while the man in the engine room it generally take the ship 's crew to repair the hespa 's engine tumble upon deck to know what we be do tell they about the hail and we listen in the smother of the fog for the sound of screw we listen for ten minute then we blow the whistle for another ten then the crew begin to call the ship 's boy fool mean that the third mate be no well when they be go down below hear the hail the third time so do the ship 's boy there you be say it be not twenty yard from we the engineer sing out hear it too be you all asleep then the crew begin to swear at the engineer and what with discussion argument and little swearing,--for there be not much discipline on board tramp,--we raise such row that our skipper come aft to enquire the engineer and the ship 's boy stick to our tale voice or no voice say the captain you would well patch the old engine up and see if you have get enough steam to whistle with have notion that we have get into rather too crowded way the engineer stay on deck while the man go down below the skipper have not get back to the chart room before see thirty foot of bowsprit hang over the break of the fo'c'sle thirty foot of bowsprit sir do not belong to anything that sail the sea except sailing ship or man of war speculate quite long time with my hand on the bulwark as to whether our friend be soft wood or steel plat it would not have make much difference to we anyway but feel there be more honour in be ram
you know then know all about it it be ram we open out be not exaggerate we open out sir like cardboard box the other ship cut we two third through little behind the break of the fo'c'sle our deck split up lengthways the mizzen mast bind out of its place and we heel over then the other ship blow fog horn remember think as take water from the port bulwark that this be rather ostentatious after she have do all the mischief after that be mile and half under sea try to go to sleep as hard as could some one catch hold of my hair and wake up be hang to what be leave of one of our boat under the lee of large english ironclad there be two man with the three of we begin to yell man on the ship sing out can you climb on board if we throw you rope they be not go to let down fine new man of war 's boat to pick up three half drown rat we accept the invitation we climb the engineer and the ship 's boy about half an hour later the fog clear entirely except for the half of the boat away in the offing there be neither stick nor string on the sea to show that the hespa have be cut down and what do you think of that now say the man from saigon private learoyd 's story and he tell tale.--_chronicle of gautama buddha far from the haunt of company officer who insist upon kit inspection far from keen nosed sergeant who sniff the pipe stuff into the bedding roll two mile from the tumult of the barrack lie the trap it be an old dry well shadow by twisted pipal tree and fence with high grass here in the year go by do private ortheris establish his depot and menagerie for such possession dead and live as could not safely be introduce to the barrack room here be gather houdin pullet and fox terrier of undoubted pedigree and more than doubtful ownership for ortheris be an inveterate poacher and pre eminent among regiment of neat handed dog stealer never again will the long lazy evening return wherein ortheris whistle softly move surgeon wise among the captive of his craft at the bottom of the well when learoyd sit in the niche give sage counsel on the management of tyke and mulvaney from the crook of the overhang pipal wave his enormous boot in benediction above our head delight we with tale of love and war and strange experience of city and man ortheris land at last in the little stuff bird shop for which your soul long learoyd back again in the smoky stone rib north amid the clang of the bradford loom mulvaney grizzled tender and very wise ulysses swelter on the earthwork of central india line judge if have forget old day in the trap orth'ri as allus think he knaw more than other foak say she be not real laady but nobbut hewrasian do not gainsay as her culler be bit doosky like
but she be laady why she ride iv carriage an good osse too an her air be that oil as you could see your faice in it an she wear dimond ring an goold chain an silk an satin dress as mun cost deal for it be not cheap shop as keep enough one pattern to fit figure like hers her name be mrs desussa an't waay coom to be acquaint wi she be along of our colonel 's laady 's dog rip have see vast dog but rip be pretty picter of cliver fox tarrier at iver set eye on he could do owt you like but speeak an colonel 's laady set more store by he than if he he be christian she he bairn of her awn
but they be england and rip seem to get all coodlin and pettin as belong to bairn by good right but rip be bit on rover an he habit breakin out barrick like and trottin round plaice as if he be cantonment magistrate coom round inspectin the colonel leather he once or twice but rip do not care an keep on gooin his round wi his taail waggin as if he be flag signallin to world at large at he be gettin on nicely thank yo and how be yo'sen an then colonel as be noa sort of hand wi dog tee he oop real clipper of dog an it be
