but he fall by himself-- fall by himself with himself to blame one may attain and to he be the pelf loot of the city in gold of fame plunder of earth shall be all his own who travel the fast and travel alone wherefore the more ye be holpen and stayed-- stay by friend in the hour of toil sing the heretical song have made--
his be the labour and your be the spoil win by his aid and the aid of disown-- he travel the fast who travel alone dray wara yow dee for jealousy be the rage of man therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance.--_prov vi almond and raisin sahib grape from kabul or pony of the rare if the sahib will only come with he be thirteen three sahib play polo go in cart carry lady and holy kurshe and the bless imam it be the sahib himself my heart be make fat and my eye glad may you never be tired as be cold water in the tirah so be the sight of friend in far place and what do you in this accursed land south of delhi sahib you know the saying--'rat be the man and trull the woman it be an order an order be an order till one be strong enough to disobey my brother my friend we have meet in an auspicious hour be all well in the heart and the body and the house in lucky day have we two come together again be to go with you your favour be great will there be picket room in the compound have three horse and the bundle and the horse boy moreover remember that the police here hold horse thief what do these lowland bastard know of horse thief do you remember that time in peshawur when kamal hammer on the gate of jumrud mountebank that he be and lift the colonel 's horse all in one night kamal be dead now
but his nephew have take up the matter and there will be more horse amisse if the khaiber levy do not look to it the peace of god and the favour of his prophet be upon this house and all that be in it shafizullah rope the mottled mare under the tree and draw water the horse can stand in the sun but double the felt over the loin nay my friend do not trouble to look they over they be to sell to the officer fool who know so many tiling of the horse the mare be heavy in foal the gray be devil unlicke and the dun
but you know the trick of the peg when they be sell go back to pubbi or it may be the valley of peshawur friend of my heart it be good to see you again have be bow and lie all day to the officer sahib in respect to those horse and my mouth be dry for straight talk before meal tobacco be good do not join for we be not in our own country sit in the veranda and will spread my cloth here but first will drink in the name of god return thank thrice this be sweet water indeed sweet as the water of sheoran when it come from the snow they be all well and pleased in the north khoda baksh and the other yar khan have come down with the horse from kurdistan six and thirty head only and full half pack pony and have say openly in the kashmir serai that you english should send gun and blow the amir into hell there be fifteen roll now on the kabul road and at dakka when he think he be clear yar khan be strip of all his balkh stallion by the governor this be great injustice and yar khan be hot with rage and of the other mahbub ali be still at pubbi write god know what tugluq khan be in jail for the business of the kohat police post faiz beg come down from ismail ki dhera with bokhariot belt for thee my brother at the closing of the year
but none know whither thou hadst go there be no news leave behind the cousin have take new run near pakpattan to breed mule for the government cart and there be story in bazar of priest such salt tale listen-- sahib why do you ask that my clothe be foul because of the dust on the road my eye be sad because of the glare of the sun my foot be swollen because have wash they in bitter water and my cheek be hollow because the food here be bad fire burn your money what do want with it be rich and think you be my friend but you be like the other sahib be man sad give he money say the sahib be he dishonour give he money say the sahib hath he wrong upon his head give he money say the sahib such be the sahib and such art thou even thou nay do not look at the foot of the dun pity it be that ever teach you to know the leg of horse be it so what of that the road be hard and the mare footsore she bear double burden sahib and now pray you give permission to depart great favour and honour have the sahib do and graciously have he show his belief that the horse be steal will it please he to send to the thana to call sweeper and have lead away by one of these lizard man be the sahib 's friend have drunk water in the shadow of his house and he have blacken my face remain there anything more to do will the sahib give eight anna to make smooth the injury and complete the insult-- forgive my brother know not know not now what say yes lie to you will put dust on my head and be an afridi the horse have be march footsore from the valley to this place and my eye be dim and my body ache for the want of sleep and my heart be dry up with sorrow and shame
but as it be my shame so by god the dispenser of justice by allah al mumit it shall be my own revenge we have speak together with naked heart before this and our hand have dip into the same dish and thou hast be to as brother therefore pay thee back with lie and ingratitude as pathan listen now when the grief of the soul be too heavy for endurance it may be little ease by speech and moreover the mind of true man be as well and the pebble of confession drop therein sink and be no more see from the valley have come on foot league by league with fire in my chest like the fire of the pit and why hast thou then so quickly forget our custom among this folk who sell their wife and their daughter for silver come back with to the north and be among man once more come back when this matter be accomplish and call for thee the bloom of the peach orchard be upon all the valley and here be only dust and great stink there be pleasant wind among the mulberry tree and the stream be bright with snow water and the caravan go up and the caravan go down and hundred fire sparkle in the gut of the pass and tent peg answer hammer nose and pack horse squeal to pack horse across the drift smoke of the evening it be good in the north now come back with let we return to our own people whence be my sorrow do man tear out his heart and make fritter thereof over slow fire for aught other than woman do not laugh friend of mine for your time will also be woman of the abazai be she and take she to wife to staunch the feud between our village and the man of ghor be no long young the lime have touch my beard have no need of the wedding nay
but love she
what saith rahman into whose heart love enter there be folly and naught else by glance of the eye she hath blind thee and by the eyelid and the fringe of the eyelid take thee into the captivity without ransom and naught else dost thou remember that song at the sheep roast in the pindi camp among the uzbeg of the amir the abazai be dog and their woman the servant of sin there be lover of her own people but of that her father tell naught my friend curse for in your prayer as curse at each pray from the fakr to the isha the name of daoud shah abazai whose head be still upon his neck whose hand be still upon his wrist who have do dishonour who have make my name laugh stock among the woman of little malikand go into hindustan at the end of two month to cherat be go twelve day only but have say that would be fifteen day absent this do to try she for it be write trust not the incapable come up the gorge alone in the fall of the light hear the voice of man sing at the door of my house and it be the voice of daoud shah and the song that he sing be dray wara
yow dee_'--'all three be one it be
as though heelrope have be slip round my heart and all the devil be draw it tight past endurance creep silently up the hill road but the fuse of my matchlock be wet with the rain and could not slay daoud shah from afar moreover it be in my mind to kill the woman also thus he sing sit outside my house and anon the woman open the door and come near crawl on my belly among the rock have only my knife to my hand but stone slip under my foot and the two look down the hillside
and he leave his matchlock flee from my anger because he be afraid for the life that be in he
but the woman move not till stand in front of she cry woman what be this that thou hast do
and she void of fear though she know my thought
laugh say it be little thing love he and thou art dog and cattle thief come by night and be still blind by her beauty for my friend the woman of the abazai be very fair say hast thou no fear
and she answer none but only the fear that do not die then say have no fear
and she bow her head and smite it off at the neck bone so that it leap between my foot thereafter the rage of our people come upon and hack off the breast that the man of little malikand might know the crime and cast the body into the water course that flow to the kabul river dray wara yow dee dray wara
yow dee the body without the head the soul without light and my own darkle heart all three be one all three be one that night make no halt go to ghor and demand news of daoud shah man say he be go to pubbi for horse what wouldst thou of he there be peace between the village make answer aye the peace of treachery and the love that the devil atala bear to gurel so fire thrice into the gate and laugh and go my way in those hour brother and friend of my heart 's heart the moon and the star be as blood above and in my mouth be the taste of dry earth also break no bread and my drink be the rain of the valley of ghor upon my face at pubbi find mahbub ali the writer sit upon his charpoy and give up my arm accord to your law but be not grieve for it be in my heart that should kill daoud shah with my bare hand thus as man strip bunch of raisin mahbub ali say daoud shah have even now go hot foot to peshawur and he will pick up his horse upon the road to delhi for it be say that the bombay tramway company be buy horse there by the truck load eight horse to the truck and that be true say then see that the hunting would be no little thing for the man be go into your border to save himself against my wrath and shall he save himself so be not alive though he run northward to the dora and the snow or southerly to the black water will follow he as lover follow the footstep of his mistress and come upon he will take he tenderly
aho so tenderly!--in my arm say well hast thou do and well shalt thou be repay and out of that embrace daoud shah shall not go forth with the breath in his nostril where be the pitcher be as thirsty as mother mare in the first month your law what be your law to when the horse fight on the run do they regard the boundary pillar or do the kite of ali musjid forbear because the carrion lie under the shadow of ghor kuttri the matter begin across the border it shall finish where god please here in my own country or in hell
all three be one listen now sharer of the sorrow of my heart and will tell of the hunting follow to peshawur from pubbi and go to and fro about the street of peshawur like houseless dog seek for my enemy once think see he wash his mouth in the conduit in the big square but when come up he be go it may be that it be he and see my face he have flee girl of the bazar say that he would go to nowshera say heart 's heart do daoud shah visit thee
and she say even so say would fain see he for we be friend part for two year hide pray here in the shadow of the window shutter and will wait for his come and the girl say pathan look into my eye and turn lean upon her breast and look into her eye swear that speak the very truth of god
but she answer never friend wait friend with such eye lie to god and the prophet but to woman ye can not lie get hence there shall be no harm befall daoud shah by cause of would have strangle that girl but for the fear of your police and thus the hunting would have come to naught therefore only laugh and depart
and she lean over the window bar in the night and mock down the street her name be jamun when have make my account with the man will return to peshawur and her lover shall desire she no more for her beauty 's sake she shall not be jamun but ak the cripple among

ho shall she be at peshawur buy the horse and grape and the almond and dry fruit that the reason of my wandering might be open to the government and that there might be no hindrance upon the road but when come to nowshera he be go and know not where to go stay one day at nowshera and in the night voice speak in my ear as sleep among the horse all night it fly round my head and would not cease from whisper be upon my belly sleep as the devil sleep and it may have be that the voice be the voice of devil it say go south and thou shalt come upon daoud shah listen my brother and chief among friend listen be the tale long one think how it be long to have tread every league of the road from pubbi to this place and from nowshera my guide be only the voice and the lust of vengeance to the uttock go
but that be no hindrance to man may turn the word twice even in his trouble the uttock be no uttock obstacle to and hear the voice above the noise of the water beat on the big rock say go to the right so go to pindigheb and in those day my sleep be take from utterly and the head of the woman of the abazai be before night and day even as it have fall between my foot dray wara yow dee dray wara yow there be sahib in camp at pindigheb who buy from white mare at good price and tell that one daoud shah have pas to shahpur with horse then see that the warning of the voice be true and make swift to come to the salt hill the jhelum be in flood but could not wait and in the cross bay stallion be wash down and drown herein be god hard to not in respect of the beast of that have no care but in this snatching while be upon the right bank urge the horse into the water daoud shah be upon the left for--_alghias alghias!_--the hoofs of my mare scatter the hot ash of his fire when we come up the hither bank in the light of morning but he have flee his foot be make swift by the terror of death and go south from shahpur as the kite fly dare not turn aside lest should miss my vengeance which be my right from shahpur skirt by the jhelum for think that he would avoid the desert of the rechna but presently at sahiwal turn away upon the road to jhang samundri and gugera till upon night the mottled mare breast the fence of the rail that turn to montgomery and that place be okara and the head of the woman of the abazai lie upon the sand between my foot thence go to fazilka and they say that be mad to bring starve horse there the voice be with and be not mad but only weary because could not find daoud shah it be write that should not find he at rania nor bahadurgarh and come into delhi from the west and there also find he not my friend have see many strange thing in my wandering have see devil riot across the rechna as the stallion riot in spring have hear the djinn call to each other from hole in the sand and have see they pass before my face there be no devil say the sahib they be very wise but they do not know all thing about devil or horse ho ho say to you who be laugh at my misery that have see the devil at high noon whoop and leap on the shoal of the chenab and be afraid my brother when the desire of man be set upon one thing alone he fear neither god nor man nor devil if my vengeance fail would splinter the gate of paradise with the butt of my gun or would cut my way into hell with my knife and would call upon those who govern there for the body of daoud shah what love so deep as hate do not speak know the thought in your heart be the white of this eye cloud how do the blood beat at the wrist there be no madness in my flesh but only the vehemence of the desire that have eat up listen south of delhi know not the country at all therefore can not say where go but pass through many city know only that it be lie upon to go south when the horse could march no more throw myself upon the earth and wait till the day there be no sleep with in that journey and that be heavy burden dost thou know brother of mine the evil of wakefulness that can not break when the bone be sore for lack of sleep and the skin of the temple twitch with weariness and yet there be no sleep there be no sleep dray wara yow dee dray wara yow dee the eye of the sun the eye of the moon and my own unrestful eye all three be one all three be one there be city the name whereof have forget and there the voice call all night that be ten day ago it have cheat afresh have come hither from place call hamirpur and behold it be my fate that should meet with thee to my comfort and the increase of friendship this be good omen by the joy of look upon thy face
the weariness have go from my foot and the sorrow of my so long travel be forget also my heart be peaceful for know that the end be near it may be that shall find daoud shah in this city go northward since hillman will ever head back to his hill when the spring warn and shall he see those hill of our country surely shall overtake he surely my vengeance be safe surely god hath he in the hollow of his hand against my claiming there shall no harm befall daoud shah till come for would fain kill he quick and whole with the life stick firm in his body pomegranate be sweetest when the clove break away unwilling from the rind let it be in the daytime that may see his face and my delight may be crown and when have accomplish the matter and my honour be make clean shall return thank unto god the holder of the scale of the law and shall sleep from the night through the day and into the night again shall sleep and no dream shall trouble and now my brother the tale be all tell the judgment of dungara see the pale martyr with his shirt on fire.--_printer 's error they tell the tale even now among the grove of the berbulda hill and for corroboration point to the roofless and windowless mission house the great god dungara the god of thing as they be most terrible one eyed bear the red elephant tusk do it all and he who refuse to believe in dungara will assuredly be smite by the madness of yat the madness that fall upon the son and the daughter of the buria kol when they turn aside from dungara and put on clothe so say athon daze who be high priest of the shrine and warden of the red elephant tusk but if you ask the assistant collector and agent in charge of the buria kol he will laugh not because he bear any malice against mission but because he himself see the vengeance of dungara execute upon the spiritual child of the reverend justus krenk pastor of the tubingen mission and upon lotta his virtuous wife yet if ever man merit good treatment of the god it be the reverend justus one time of heidelberg who on the faith of call go into the wilderness and take the blonde blue eyed lotta with he
we will these heathen now by idolatrous practice so darken well make
say justus in the early day of his career yes he add with conviction they shall be good and shall with their hand to work learn for all good christians must work and upon stipend more modest even than that of an english lay reader justus krenk keep house beyond kamala and the gorge of malair beyond the berbulda river close to the foot of the blue hill of panth on whose summit stand the temple of dungara in the heart of the country of the buria kol the naked good temper timid shameless lazy buria kol do you know what life at mission outpost mean try to imagine loneliness exceed that of the small station to which government have ever send you isolation that weigh upon the wake eyelid and drive you by force headlong into the labour of the day there be no post there be no one of your own colour to speak to there be no road there be indeed food to keep you alive but it be not pleasant to eat and whatever of good or beauty or interest there be in your life must come from yourself and the grace that may be plant in you in the morning with patter of soft foot the convert the doubtful and the open scoffer troop up to the veranda you must be infinitely kind and patient and above all clear sighted for you deal with the simplicity of childhood the experience of man and the subtlety of the savage your congregation have hundred material want to be consider and it be for you as you believe in your personal responsibility to your maker to pick out of the clamour crowd any grain of spirituality that may lie therein if to the cure of soul you add that of body your task will be all the more difficult for the sick and the maim will profess any and every creed for the sake of healing and will laugh at you because you be simple enough to believe they as the day wear and the impetus of the morning die away there will come upon you an overwhelming sense of the uselessness of your toil this must be strive against and the only spur in your side will be the belief that you be play against the devil for the live soul it be great joyous belief but he who can hold it unwavere for four and twenty consecutive hour must be bless with an abundantly strong physique and equable nerve ask the gray head of the bannockburn medical crusade what manner of life their preacher lead speak to the racine gospel agency those lean americans whose boast be that they go where no englishman dare follow get pastor of the tubingen mission to talk of his experience if you can you will be refer to the print report but these contain no mention of the man who have lose youth and health all that man may lose except faith in the wild of english maiden who have go forth and die in the fever stricken jungle of the panth hill know from the first that death be almost certainty few pastor will tell you of these thing any more than they will speak of that young david of st bee who set apart for the lord 's work break down in utter desolation and return half distraught to the head mission cry there be no god but have walk with the devil the report be silent here because heroism failure doubt despair and self abnegation on the part of mere cultured white man be thing of no weight as compare to the saving of one half human soul from fantastic faith in wood spirit goblin of the rock and river fiend and gallio the assistant collector of the country side care for none of these thing he have be long in the district and the buria kol love he and bring he offering of spear fish orchid from the dim moist heart of the forest and as much game as he could eat in return he give they quinine and with athon daze the high priest control their simple policy when you have be some year in the country say gallio at the krenk table you grow to find one creed as good as another will give you all the assistance in my power of course but do not hurt my buria kol they be good people and they trust will they the word of the lord teach say justus his round face beam with enthusiasm and will assuredly to their prejudice no wrong hastily without think make
but my friend this in the mind impartiality of creed judgment be look be very bad heigh ho say gallio have their body and the district to see to
but you can try what you can do for their soul only do not behave as your predecessor do or be afraid that can not guarantee your life and that say lotta sturdily hand he cup of tea he go up to the temple of dungara to be sure he be new to the country and begin hammer old dungara over the head with an umbrella so the buria kol turn out and hammer he rather savagely be in the district and he send runner to with note say persecute for the lord 's sake send wing of regiment the near troop be about two hundred mile off but guess what he have be do ride to panth and talk to old athon daze like father tell he that man of his wisdom ought to have know that the sahib have sunstroke and be mad you never see people more sorry in your life athon daze apologise send wood and milk and fowl and all sort of thing and give five rupee to the shrine and tell macnamara that he have be injudicious he say that have bow down in the house of rimmon
but if he have only just go over the brow of the hill and insult palin deo the idol of the suria kol he would have be impale on charred bamboo long before could have do anything and then should have have to have hang some of the poor brute be gentle with they padri
but do not think you will do much not say justus but my master we will with the little child begin many of they will be sick that be so after the child the mother and then the man but would greatly that you be in internal sympathy with we prefer gallio depart to risk his life in mend the rotten bamboo bridge of his people in kill too persistent tiger here or there in sleep out in the reek jungle or in track the suria kol raider who have take few head from their brother of the buria clan he be knock kneed shamble young man naturally devoid of creed or reverence with longing for absolute power which his undesirable district gratify no one want my post he use to say grimly and my collector only poke his nose in when he be quite certain that there be no fever be monarch of all survey and athon daze be my viceroy because gallio pride himself on his supreme disregard of human life though he never extend the theory beyond his own he naturally ride forty mile to the mission with tiny brown girl baby on his saddle bow here be something for you padri say he the kol leave their surplus child to die do not see why they should not
but you may rear this one pick it up beyond the berbulda fork have notion that the mother have be follow through the wood ever since it be the first of the fold say justus and lotta catch up the scream morsel to her bosom and hush it craftily while as wolf hang in the field matui who have bear it and in accordance with the law of her tribe have expose it to die pant weary and footsore in the bamboo brake watch the house with hungry mother eye what would the omnipotent assistant collector do would the little man in the black coat eat her daughter alive as athon daze say be the custom of all man in black coat matui wait among the bamboo through the long night and in the morning there come forth fair white woman the like of whom matui have never see and in her arm be matui 's daughter clothe in spotless raiment lotta know little of the tongue of the buria kol but when mother call to mother speech be easy to follow by the hand stretch timidly to the hem of her gown by the passionate guttural and the longing eye lotta understand with whom she have to deal so matui take her child again would be servant even slave to this wonderful white woman for her own tribe would recognise she no more and lotta weep with she exhaustively after the german fashion which include much blow of the nose first the child then the mother and last the man and to the glory of god all say justus the hopeful and the man come with bow and arrow very angry indeed for there be no one to cook for he but the tale of the mission be long one and have no space to show how justus forgetful of his injudicious predecessor grievously smite moto the husband of matui for his brutality how moto be startled but be release from the fear of instant death take heart and become the faithful ally and first convert of justus how the little gathering grow to the huge disgust of athon daze how the priest of the god of thing as they be argue subtilely with the priest of the god of thing as they should be and be bad how the due of the temple of dungara fall away in fowl and fish and honeycomb how lotta lighten the curse of eve among the woman and how justus do his good to introduce the curse of adam how the buria kol rebel at this say that their god be an idle god and how justus partially overcome their scruple against work and teach they that the black earth be rich in other produce than pig nut only all these thing belong to the history of many month and throughout those month the white haired athon daze meditate revenge for the tribal neglect of dungara with savage cunning he feign friendship towards justus even hint at his own conversion but to the congregation of dungara he say darkly they of the padri 's flock have put on clothe and worship busy god therefore dungara will afflict they grieviously till they throw themselves howl into the water of the berbulda at night the red elephant tusk boom and groan among the hill and the faithful wake and say the god of thing as they be mature revenge against the backslider be merciful dungara to we thy child and give we all their crop late in the cold weather the collector and his wife come into the buria kol country go and look at krenk 's mission say gallio he be do good work in his own way and think he would be pleased if you open the bamboo chapel that he have manage to run up at any rate you will see civilised buria kol great be the stir in the mission now he and the gracious lady will that we have do good work with their own eye see
and yes we will he our convert in all their new clothe by their own hand construct exhibit it will great day be for the lord always say justus and lotta say amen justus have in his quiet way feel jealous of the basel weaving mission his own convert be unhandy but athon daze have latterly induce some of they to hackle the glossy silky fibre of plant that grow plenteously on the panth hill it yield cloth white and smooth almost as the tappa of the south sea and that day the convert be to wear for the first time clothe make therefrom justus be proud of his work they shall in white clothe clothe to meet the collector and his well bear lady come down sing now thank we all our god
then he will the chapel open
and yes even gallio to believe will begin stand
so my child two by two and lotta why do they thus themselves bescratch it be not seemly to wriggle nala my child the collector will be here and be pain the collector his wife and gallio climb the hill to the mission station the convert be draw up in two line shin band nearly forty strong
hah say the collector whose acquisitive bent of mind lead he to believe that he have foster the institution from the first advance see by leap and bind never be true word speak the mission be advance exactly as he have say at first by little hop and shuffle of shamefaced uneasiness but soon by the leap of fly sting horse and the bound of madden kangaroo from the hill of panth the red elephant tusk deliver dry and anguished blare the rank of the convert waver break and scatter with yell and shriek of pain while justus and lotta stand horror stricken it be the judgment of dungara shout voice to the river or we die the mob wheel and head for the rock that over hang the berbulda writhe stamp twisting and shed its garment as it run pursue by the thunder of the trumpet of dungara justus and lotta flee to the collector almost in tear can not understand yesterday pant justus they have the ten commandments.--what be this praise the lord all good spirit by land and by sea oh shame with bind and scream there alight on the rock above their head nala once the pride of the mission maiden of fourteen summer good docile and virtuous now naked as the dawn and spit like wild cat be it for this she rave hurl her petticoat at justus be it for this leave my people and dungara for the fire of your bad place blind ape little earthworm dry fish that you be you say that should never burn dungara burn now burn now have mercy god of thing as they be she turn and fling herself into the berbulda and the trumpet of dungara bellow jubilantly the last of the convert of the tubingen mission have put quarter of mile of rapid river between herself and her teacher yesterday
gulp justus she teach in the school d.--oh it be the work of satan but gallio be curiously regard the maiden 's petticoat where it have fall at his foot he feel its texture draw back his shirt sleeve beyond the deep tan of his wrist and press fold of the cloth against the flesh blotch of angry red rise on the white skin
ah say gallio calmly think so what be it say justus should call it the shirt of nessus but where do you get the fibre of this cloth from athon daze say justus he show the boy how it should manufacture be the old fox do you know that he have give you the nilgiri nettle scorpion--_girardenia heterophylla_--to work up no wonder they squirm why it sting even when they make bridge rope of it unless it be soak for six week the cunning brute it would take about half an hour to burn through their thick hide and then-- gallio burst into laughter but lotta be weep in the arm of the collector 's wife and justus have cover his face with his hand girardenia heterophylla repeat gallio krenk why do not you tell could have save you this weave fire anybody but naked kol would have know it and if be judge of their way you will never get they back he look across the river to where the convert be still wallow and wail in the shallow and the laughter die out of his eye for he see that the tubingen mission to the buria kol be dead never again though they hang mournfully round the deserted school for three month could lotta or justus coax back even the most promising of their flock the end of conversion be the fire of the bad place fire that run through the limb and gnaw into the bone who dare second time tempt the anger of dungara let the little man and his wife go elsewhere the buria kol would have none of they an unofficial message to athon daze that if hair of their head be touch athon daze and the priest of dungara would be hang by gallio at the temple shrine protect justus and lotta from the stumpy poison arrow of the buria kol but neither fish nor fowl honeycomb salt nor young pig be bring to their door any more and alas man can not live by grace alone if meat be want let we go mine wife say justus there be no good here and the lord have will that some other man shall the work take in good time in his own good time we will go away and will yes some botany bestudy if any one be anxious to convert the buria kol afresh there lie at least the core of mission house under the hill of panth but the chapel and school have long since fall back into jungle at howli thana his own shoe his own head.--_native proverb as messenger if the heart of the presence be move to so great favour and on six rupee yes sahib for have three little child whose stomach be always empty and corn be now but forty pound to the rupee will make so clever messenger that you shall all day long be pleased with and at the end of the year bestow turban know all the road of the station and many other thing aha sahib be clever give service be aforetime in the police bad character now without doubt an enemy have tell this tale never be scamp be man of clean heart and all my word be true they know this when be in the police they say afzal khan be true speaker in whose word man may trust be delhi pathan sahib all delhi pathans be good man you have see delhi
yes it be true that there be many scamp among the delhi pathans how wise be the sahib nothing be hide from his eye and he will make his messenger and will take all his note secretly and without ostentation nay sahib god be my witness that mean no evil have long desire to serve under true sahib virtuous sahib many young sahib be as devil unchain with these sahib would take no service not though all the stomach of my little child be cry for bread why be not still in the police will speak true talk an evil come to the thana to ram baksh the havildar and maula baksh and juggut ram and bhim singh and suruj bul ram baksh be in the jail for space and so also be maula baksh it be at the thana of howli on the road that lead to gokral seetarun wherein be many dacoit we be all brave man rustum wherefore we be send to that thana which be eight mile from the next thana all day and all night we watch for dacoit why do the sahib laugh nay will make confession the dacoit be too clever and see this we make no further trouble it be in the hot weather what can man do in the hot day be the sahib who be so strong be he even vigorous in that hour we make an arrangement with the dacoit for the sake of peace that be the work of the havildar who be fat ho ho sahib he be now get thin in the jail among the carpet the havildar say give we no trouble and we will give you no trouble at the end of the reaping send we man to lead before the judge man of infirm mind against whom the trump up case will break down thus we shall save our honour to this talk the dacoit agree and we have no trouble at the thana and could eat melon in peace sit upon our charpoy all day long sweet as sugar cane be the melon of howli now there be an assistant commissioner stunt sahib in that district call yunkum sahib he be hard hard even as be the sahib who without doubt will give the shadow of his protection many eye have yunkum sahib and move quickly through his district man call he the tiger of gokral seetarun because he would arrive unannounced and make his kill and before sunset would be give trouble to the tehsildar thirty mile away no one know the come or the going of yunkum sahib he have no camp and when his horse be weary he ride upon devil carriage do not know its name but the sahib sit in the midst of three silver wheel that make no creaking and drave they with his leg prance like bean feed horse thus shadow of hawk upon the field be not more without noise than the devil carriage of yunkum sahib it be here it be there it be go and the rapport be make and there be trouble ask the tehsildar of rohestri how the hen steal come to be know sahib it fall upon night that we of the thana sleep accord to custom upon our charpoy having eat the evening meal and drunk tobacco when we awake in the morning behold of our six rifle not one remain also the big police book that be in the havildar 's charge be go
see these thing we be very much afraid think on our part that the dacoit regardless of honour have come by night and put we to shame then say ram baksh the havildar be silent the business be an evil business
but it may yet go well let we make the case complete bring kid and my tulwar see you not now fool kick for horse but word be enough for
man we of the thana perceive quickly what be in the mind of the havildar and greatly fear that the service would be lose make haste to take the kid into the inner room and attend to the word of the havildar twenty dacoit come say the havildar
and we take his word repeat after he accord to custom there be great fight say the havildar and of we no man escape unhurt the bar of the window be break suruj bul see thou to that and man put speed into your work for runner must go with the news to the tiger of gokral seetarun thereon suruj bul lean with his shoulder brake in the bar of the window and beat she with whip make the havildar 's mare skip among the melon bed till they be much tread with hoof print these thing be make return to the thana and the goat be slay and certain portion of the wall be blacken with fire and each man dip his clothe little into the blood of the goat know sahib that wound make by man upon his own body can by those skilled be easily discern from wound work by another man therefore the havildar take his tulwar smite one of we lightly on the forearm in the fat and another on the leg and third on the back of the hand thus deal he with all of we till the blood come and suruj bul more eager than the other take out much hair sahib never be so perfect an arrangement yea even would have swear that the thana have be treat as we say there be smoke and breaking and blood and trample earth ride now maula baksh say the havildar to the house of the stunt sahib and carry the news of the dacoity do you also afzal khan run there and take heed that you be mire with sweat and dust on your incoming the blood will be dry on the clothe will stay and send straight report to the dipty sahib and we will catch certain that ye know of villager so that all may be ready against the dipty sahib 's arrival thus maula baksh ride and run hang on the stirrup and together we come in an evil plight before the tiger of gokral seetarun in the rohestri tehsil our tale be long and correct sahib for we give even the name of the dacoit and the issue of the fight and beseech he to come
but the tiger make no sign and only smile after the manner of sahib when they have wickedness in their heart swear ye to the rapport say he
and we say thy servant swear the blood of the fight be but newly dry upon we judge thou if it be the blood of the servant of the presence or not
and he say see ye have do well
but he do not call for his horse or his devil carriage and scour the land as be his custom he say rest now and eat bread for ye be weary man will wait the coming of the dipty sahib now it be the order that the havildar of the thana should send straight report of all dacoity to the dipty sahib at noon come he fat man and an old and overbearing withal
but we of the thana have no fear of his anger dread more the silence of the tiger of gokral seetarun with he come ram baksh the havildar and the other guard ten man of the village of howli all man evil affect towards the police of the sirkar as prisoner they come the iron upon their hand cry for mercy imam baksh the farmer who have deny his wife to the havildar and other ill condition rascal against whom we of the thane bore spite it be well do and the havildar be proud but the dipty sahib be angry with the stunt for lack of zeal and say dam dam after the custom of the english people and extoll the havildar yunkum sahib lie still in his long chair have the man swear say yunkum sahib aye and capture ten evildoer say the dipty sahib there be more abroad in your charge take horse ride and go in the name of the sirkar truly there be more evil doer abroad say yunkum sahib but there be no need of horse come all man with see the mark of string on the temple of imam baksh do the presence know the torture of the cold draw see also the face of the tiger of gokral seeta run the evil smile be upon it and stand back ready for what might befall
well it be sahib that do this thing yunkum sahib unlock the door of his bath room and smile anew within lie the six rifle and the big police book of the thana of howli he have come by night in the devil carriage that be noiseless as ghoul and move among we asleep have take away both the gun and the book twice have he come to the thana take each time three rifle the liver of the havildar be turn to water and he fall scrabble in the dirt about the boot of yunkum sahib crying--'have mercy sahib be delhi pathan and young man with little child the havildar 's mare be in the compound run to she and ride the black wrath of the sirkar be behind and know not whither to go till she drop and die ride the red mare and by the blessing of god who be without doubt on the side of all just man escape but the havildar and the rest be now in jail be scamp it be as the presence please god will make the presence lord and give he rich mem sahib as fair as peri to wife and many strong son if he make his orderly the mercy of heaven be upon the sahib yes will only go to the bazar and bring my child to these so palace like quarter and then the presence be my father and my mother and afzal khan be his slave ohe sirdar ji also be of the household of the sahib gemini great be the justice of the white man great the power of lie --_native proverb this be your english justice protector of the poor look at my back and loin which be beat with stick heavy stick be poor man and there be no justice in court there be two of we and we be bear of one birth but swear to you that be bear the first and ram dass be the young by three full breath the astrologer say so and it be write in my horoscope the horoscope of durga dass
but we be alike and my brother who be beast without honour so alike that none know together or apart which be durga dass be mahajun of pali in marwar and an honest man this be true talk when we be man we leave our father 's house in pali and go to the punjab where all the people be mud head and son of ass we take shop together in isser jang and my brother near the big well where the governor 's camp draw water but ram dass who be without truth make quarrel with and we be divide he take his book and his pot and his mark and become bunnia_--a money lender in the long street of isser jang near the gateway of the road that go to montgomery it be not my fault that we pull each other 's turban be mahajun of pali and always speak true talk ram dass be the thief and the liar now no man not even the little child could at one glance see which be ram dass and which be durga dass but all the people of isser jang may they die without sons!--said that we be thief they use much bad talk but take money on their bedstead and their cooking pot and the stand crop and the calf unborn from the well in the big square to the gate of the montgomery road they be fool these people unfit to cut the toe nail of marwari from pali lend money to they all little very little only here pice and there pice god be my witness that be poor man the money be all with ram dass may his son turn christian and his daughter be burn fire and shame in the house from generation to generation may she die unwed and be the mother of multitude of bastard let the light go out in the house of ram dass my brother this pray daily twice with offering and charm thus the trouble begin we divide the town of isser jang between we and my brother there be landholder beyond the gate living but one short mile out on the road that lead to montgomery and his name be muhammad shah son of nawab he be great devil and drink wine so long as there be woman in his house and wine and money for the marriage feast he be merry and wipe his mouth ram dass lend he the money lakh or half lakh how do know?--and so long as the money be lend the landholder care not what he sign the people of isser jang be my portion and the landholder and the out town be the portion of ram dass for
so we have arrange be the poor man for the people of isser jang be without wealth do what could but ram dass have only to wait without the door of the landholder 's garden court and to lend he the money take the bond from the hand of the steward in the autumn of the year after the lending ram dass say to the landholder pay my money but the landholder give he abuse but ram dass go into the court with the paper and the bond all correct and take out decree against the landholder and the name of the government be across the stamp of the decree ram dass take field by field and mango tree by mango tree and well by well put in his own man debtor of the out town of isser jang to cultivate the crop so he creep up across the land for he have the paper and the name of the government be across the stamp till his man hold the crop for he on all side of the big white house of the landholder it be well do but when the landholder see these thing he be very angry and curse ram dass after the manner of the muhammadan and thus the landholder be angry but ram dass laugh and claim more field as be write upon the bond this be in the month of phagun take my horse and go out to speak to the man who make lac bangle upon the road that lead to montgomery because he owe debt there be in front of upon his horse my brother ram dass and when he see he turn aside into the high crop because there be hatred between we and go forward till come to the orange bush by the landholder 's house the bat be fly and the evening smoke be low down upon the land here meet four man swashbuckler and muhammadan with their face bind up lay hold of my horse 's bridle and cry out this be ram dass beat they beat with their stave heavy stave bind about with wire at the end such weapon as those swine of punjabi use till have cry for mercy fall down senseless
but these shameless one still beat say ram dass this be your interest well weigh and count into your hand ram dass cry aloud that be not ram dass but durga dass his brother yet they only beat the more and when could make no more outcry they leave but see their face there be elahi baksh who run by the side of the landholder 's white horse and nur ali the keeper of the door and wajib ali the very strong cook and abdul latif the messenger all of the household of the landholder these thing can swear on the cow 's tail if need be but--_ahi ahi!_--it have be already swear and be poor man whose honour be lose when these four have go away laugh my brother ram dass come out of the crop and mourn over as one dead but open my eye and pray he to get water when have drunk he carry on his back and by byway bring into the town of isser jang my heart be turn to ram dass my brother in that hour because of his kindness and lose my enmity but snake be snake till it be dead and liar be liar till the judgment of the god take hold of his heel be wrong in that trust my brother the son of my mother when we have come to his house and be little restore tell he my tale
and he say without doubt it be whom they would have beat
but the law court be open and there be the justice of the sirkar above all and to the law court do thou go when this sickness be overpast now when we two have leave pali in the old year there fall famine that run from jeysulmir to gurgaon and touch gogunda in the south at that time the sister of my father come away and live with we in isser jang for man must above all see that his folk do not die of want when the quarrel between us twain come about the sister of my father lean she dog without tooth say that ram dass have the right and go with he into her hand because she know medicine and many cure ram dass my brother put faint with the beating and much bruise even to the pouring of blood from the mouth when have two day sickness the fever come upon and set aside the fever to the account write in my mind against the landholder the punjabi of isser jang be all the son of belial and she ass
but they be very good witness bear testimony unshakingly whatever the pleader may say would purchase witness by the score and each man should give evidence not only against nur ali wajib ali abdul latif and elahi baksh but against the landholder say that he upon his white horse have call his man to beat and far that they have rob of two hundred rupee for the latter testimony would remit little of the debt of the man who sell the lac bangle
and he should say that he have put the money into my hand and have see the robbery from afar but be afraid have run away this plan tell to my brother ram dass and he say that the arrangement be good and bid take comfort and make swift work to be abroad again my heart be open to my brother in my sickness and tell he the name of those whom would call as witness all man in my debt but of that the magistrate sahib could have no knowledge nor the landholder the fever stay with and after the fever be take with colic and grip very terrible in that day think that my end be at hand but know now that she who give the medicine the sister of my father widow with widow 's heart have bre about my second sickness ram dass
my brother say that my house be shut and lock and bring the big door key and my book together with all the money that be in my house even the money that be bury under the floor for be in great fear lest thief should break in and dig speak true talk there be but very little money in the house perhaps ten rupee perhaps twenty how can tell god be my witness that be poor man one night when have tell ram dass all that be in my heart of the lawsuit that would bring against the landholder and ram dass have say that he have make the arrangement with the witness give their name write be take with new great sickness and they put on the bed when be little recover can not tell how many day afterwards make enquiry for ram dass and the sister of my father say that he have go to montgomery upon lawsuit take medicine and sleep very heavily without wake when my eye be open there be great stillness in the house of ram dass and none answer when call not even the sister of my father this fill with fear for know not what have happen take stick in my hand go out slowly till come to the great square by the well and my heart be hot in against the landholder because of the pain of every step take call for jowar singh the carpenter whose name be first upon the list of those who should bear evidence against the landholder say be all thing ready and do you know what should be say jowar singh answer
what be this and whence do you come durga dass say from my bed where have so long lie sick because of the landholder where be ram dass my brother who be to have make the arrangement for the witness surely you and yours know these thing then jowar singh say what have this to do with we liar have bear witness and have be pay and the landholder have by the order of the court pay both the five hundred rupee that he rob from ram dass and yet other five hundred because of the great injury he do to your brother the well and the jujube tree above it and the square of isser jang become dark in my eye but lean on my stick and say nay this be child 's talk and senseless it be who suffer at the hand of the landholder and be come to make ready the case where be my brother ram dass but jowar singh shake his head and woman cry what lie be here what quarrel have the landholder with you bunnia it be only shameless one and one without faith who profit by his brother 's smart have these bunnia no bowel cry again say by the cow by the oath of the cow by the temple of the blue throate mahadeo and only be beat beat to the death let your talk be straight people of isser jang and will pay for the witness and totter where stand for the sickness and the pain of the beating be heavy upon then ram narain who have his carpet spread under the jujube tree by the well and write all letter for the man of the town come up and say to day be the one and fortieth day since the beating and since these six day the case have be judge in the court and the assistant commissioner sahib have give it for your brother ram dass allow the robbery to which too bear witness and all thing else as the witness say there be many witness and twice ram dass become senseless in the court because of his wound and the stunt sahib the baba stunt sahib give he chair before all the pleader why do you howl durga dass these thing fall as have say be it not so and jowar singh say that be truth be there and there be red cushion in the chair and ram narain say great shame have come upon the landholder because of this judgment and fear his anger ram dass and all his house have go back to pali ram dass tell we that you also have go first the enmity be heal between you to open shop in pali indeed it be well for you that you go even now for the landholder have swear that if he catch any one of your house he will hang he by the heel from the well beam and swing he to and fro will beat he with stave till the blood run from his ear what have say in respect to the case be true as these man here can testify even to the five hundred rupee say be it five hundred and kirpa ram the jat say five hundred for bear witness also and groan for it have be in my heart to have say two hundred only then new fear come upon and my bowel turn to water and run swiftly to the house of ram dass seek for my book and my money in the great wooden chest under my bedstead there remain nothing not even cowrie 's value all have be take by the devil who say he be my brother go to my own house also and open the board of the shutter
but there also be nothing save the rat among the grain basket in that hour
my sens leave and tear my clothe run to the well place cry out for the justice of the english on my brother ram dass and in my madness tell all that the book be lose when man see that would have jump down
the well they believe the truth of my talk more especially because upon my back and bosom be still the mark of the stave of the landholder jowar singh the carpenter withstand and turn in his hand for he be very strong man show the scar upon my body and bow down with laughter upon the well curb he cry aloud so that all hear he from the well square to the caravanserai of the pilgrim oho the jackal have quarrel and the gray one have be catch in the trap in truth this man have be grievously beat and his brother have take the money which the court decree
oh bunnia
this shall be tell for year against you the jackal have quarrel
and moreover the book be burn people indebted to durga dass and know that ye be many the book be burn then all isser jang take up the cry that the book be burned--_ahi ahi that in my folly have let that escape my mouth and they laugh throughout the city they give the abuse of the punjabi which be terrible abuse and very hot pelt also with stick and cow dung till fall down and cry for mercy ram narain the letter writer bid the people cease for fear that the news should get into montgomery and the policeman might come down to enquire he say use many bad word this much mercy will do to you durga dass though there be no mercy in your dealing with my sister 's son over the matter of the dun heifer have any man pony on which he set no store that this fellow may escape if the landholder hear that one of the twain and god know whether he beat one or both but this man be certainly beat be in the city there will be murder do and then will come the police make inquisition into each man 's house and eat the sweet seller 's stuff all day long kirpa ram the jat say have pony very sick but with beat he can be make to walk for two mile if he die the hide seller will have the body then chumbo the hide seller say will pay three annas for the body and will walk by this man 's side till such time as the pony die if it be more than two mile will pay two anna only kirpa ram say be it so man bring out the pony and ask leave to draw little water from the well because be dry up with fear then ram narain say here be four anna god have bring you very low durga dass and would not send you away empty even though the matter of my sister 's son 's dun heifer be an open sore between we it be long way to your own country go and if it be so willed live but above all do not take the pony 's bridle for that be mine and go out of isser jang amid the laughing of the huge thighe jat and the hide seller walk by my side wait for the pony to fall dead in one mile it die and be full of fear of the landholder run till could run no more and come to this place but swear by the cow swear by all thing whereon hindus and musalman and even the sahib swear that and not my brother be beat by the landholder
but the case be shut and the door of the law court be shut and god know where the baba stunt sahib the mother 's milk be not yet dry upon his hairless lip be go ahi ahi have no witness and the scar will heal and be poor man but on my father 's soul on the oath of mahajun from pali and not my brother be beat by the landholder what can do the justice of the english be as great river having go forward it do not return
howbeit do you sahib take pen and write clearly what have say that the dipty sahib may see and reprove the stunt sahib who be colt yet unlicke by the mare so young be he and not my brother be beat and he be go to the west do not know where but above all thing write so that sahib may read and his disgrace be accomplish that ram dass my brother son of purun dass mahajun of pali be swine and night thief taker of life an eater of flesh jackal spawn without beauty or faith or cleanliness or honour at twenty two narrow as the womb deep as the pit and dark as the heart of man --_sonthal miner 's proverb weaver go out to reap but stay to unravel the corn stalk be there any sense in weaver
janki meah glare at kundoo but as janki meah be blind kundoo be not impressed he have come to argue with janki meah
and if chance favour to make love to the old man 's pretty young wife this be kundoo 's grievance and he speak in the name of all the five man who with janki meah compose the gang in number seven gallery of twenty two
janki meah have be blind for the thirty year during which he have serve the jimahari colliery with pick and crowbar all through those thirty year he have regularly every morning before go down draw from the overseer his allowance of lamp oil just as if he have be an eyed miner what kundoo 's gang resent as hundred of gang have resent before be janki meah 's selfishness he would not add the oil to the common stock of his gang but would save and sell it know these work before you be bear
janki meah use to reply do not want the light to get my coal out by and be not go to help you the oil be mine and intend to keep it strange man in many way be janki
meah the white haired hot temper sightless weaver who have turn pitman all day long except on sunday and monday when he be usually drunk he work in the twenty two shaft of the jimahari colliery as cleverly as man with all the sen at evening he go up in the great steam haul cage to the pit bank and there call for his pony rusty coal dusty beast nearly as old as janki
meah the pony would come to his side and janki meah would clamber on to its back and be take at once to the plot of land which he like the other miner receive from the jimahari company the pony know that place and when after six year the company change all the allotment to prevent the miner from acquire proprietary right
janki meah represent with tear in his eye that be his hold shift he would never be able to find his way to the new one my horse only know that place plead
janki meah
and so he be allow to keep his land on the strength of this concession and his accumulate oil saving
janki meah take second wife girl of the jolaha main stock of the meah and singularly beautiful
janki meah could not see her beauty wherefore he take she on trust and forbid she to go down the pit he have not work for thirty year in the dark without know that the pit be no place for pretty woman he load she with ornament not brass or pewter but real silver one
and she reward he by flirt outrageously with kundoo of number seven gallery gang kundoo be really the gang head
but janki meah insist upon all the work be enter in his own name and choose the man that he work with custom strong even than the jimahari company dictate that janki by right of his year should manage these thing and should also work despite his blindness in indian mine where they cut into the solid coal with the pick and clear it out from floor to ceiling he could come to no great harm at home where they undercut the coal and bring it down in crash avalanche from the roof he would never have be allow to set foot in pit he be not popular man because of his oil saving but all the gang admit that janki know all the khad or work that have ever be sink or work since the jimahari company first start operation on the tarachunda field pretty little unda only know that her old husband be fool who could be manage she take no interest in the colliery except in so far as they swallow up kundoo five day out of the seven and cover he with coal dust kundoo be great workman and do his good not to get drunk because when he have save forty rupee unda be to steal everything that she could find in janki 's house and run with kundoo to land where there be no mine and every one keep three fat bullock and milch buffalo while this scheme ripen it be his custom to drop in upon janki and worry he about the oil saving unda sit in corner and nod approval on the night when kundoo have quote that objectionable proverb about weaver janki grow angry
listen you pig say he blind be and old be but before ever you be bear be gray among the coal even in the day when the twenty two khad be unsunk and there be not two thousand man here be know to have all knowledge of the pit what khad be there that do not know from the bottom of the shaft to the end of the last drive be it the baromba khad the old or the twenty two where tibu 's gallery run up to number five hear the old fool talk say kundoo nod to unda no gallery of twenty two will cut into five before the end of the rain we have month 's solid coal before we the babuji say so babuji what do these fat slug from calcutta know he draw and draw and draw and talk and talk and talk and his map be all wrong
janki know that this be so when man have be shut up in the dark for thirty year god give he knowledge the old gallery that tibu 's gang make be not six foot from number five without doubt god give the blind knowledge say kundoo with look at unda let it be as you say for my part do not know where lie the gallery of tibu 's gang but be not wither monkey who need oil to grease his joint with kundoo swing out of the hut laugh and unda giggle janki turn his sightless eye towards his wife and swear have land and have sell great deal of lamp oil muse
janki but be fool to marry this child week later the rain set in with vengeance and the gang paddle about in coal slush at the pit bank then the big mine pump be make ready and the manager of the colliery plough through the wet towards the tarachunda river swell between its soppy bank lord send that this beastly beck do not misbehave say the manager piously and he go to take counsel with his assistant about the pump
but the tarachunda misbehave very much indeed after fall of three inch of rain in an hour it be oblige to do something it top its bank and join the flood water that be hem between two low hill just where the embankment of the colliery main line cross when large part of rain feed river and few acre of flood water make dead set for nine foot culvert the culvert may spout its fine but the water can not all get out the manager prance upon one leg with excitement and his language be improper he have reason to swear because he know that one inch of water on land mean pressure of one hundred ton to the acre and here be about five foot of water form behind the railway embankment over the shallow working of twenty two you must understand that in coal mine the coal near the surface be work first from the central shaft that be to say the miner may clear out the stuff to within ten twenty or thirty foot of the surface and when all be work out leave only skin of earth uphold by some few pillar of coal in deep mine where they know that they have any amount of material at hand man prefer to get all their mineral out at one shaft rather than make number of little hole to tap the comparatively unimportant surface coal and the manager watch the flood the culvert spout nine foot gush but the water still form and word be send to clear the man out of twenty two the cage come up crammed and cram again with the man near the pit eye as they call the place where you can see daylight from the bottom of the main shaft all away and away up the long black gallery the flare lamp be wink and dance like so many firefly and the man and the woman wait for the clank rattle thunder cage to come down and fly up again but the out work be very far off and word could not be pas quickly though the head of the gang and the assistant shout and swear and tramp and stumble the manager keep one eye on the great troubled pool behind the embankment and pray that the culvert would give way and let the water through in time with the other eye he watch the cage come up and see the headman count the roll of the gang with all his heart and soul he swear at the winder who control the iron drum that wind up the wire rope on which hang the cage in little time there be down draw in the water behind the embankment suck whirlpool all yellow and yeasty the water have smash through the skin of the earth and be pour into the old shallow working of twenty two deep down below rush of black water catch the last gang wait for the cage and as they clamber in the whirl be about their waist the cage reach the pit bank and the manager call the roll the gang be all safe except gang janki gang mogul and gang rahim eighteen man with perhaps ten basket woman who load the coal into the little iron carriage that run on the tramway of the main gallery these gang be in the out work three quarter of mile away on the extreme fringe of the mine once more the cage go down but with only two englishman in it and drop into swirl roar current that have almost touch the roof of some of the low side gallery one of the wooden balk with which they have prop the old working shoot past on the current just miss the cage if we do not want our rib knock out we would well go say the manager we can not even save the company 's prop the cage draw out of the water with splash and few minute later it be officially report that there be at least ten foot of water in the pit 's eye now ten foot of water there mean that all other place in the mine be flood except such gallery as be more than ten foot above the level of the bottom of the shaft the deep working would be full the main gallery would be full but in the high work reach by incline from the main road there would be certain amount of air cut off so to speak by the water and squeeze up by it the little science primer explain how water behave when you pour it down test tube the flooding of twenty two be an illustration on large scale by the holy grove what have happen to the air it be sonthal gangman of gang mogul in number nine gallery and he be drive six foot way through the coal then there be rush from the other gallery and gang janki and gang rahim stumble up with their basket woman water have come in the mine they say and there be no way of get out go down say janki--'down the slope of my gallery and feel the water there have be no water in the cutting in our time clamour the woman why can not we go away be silent say janki long ago when my father be here water come to ten no eleven cutting and there be great trouble let we get away to where the air be well the three gang and the basket woman leave number nine gallery and go far up number sixteen at one turn of the road they could see the pitchy black water lap on the coal it have touch the roof of gallery that they know well gallery where they use to smoke their huqas and manage their flirtation see this they call aloud upon their god and the meah who be thrice bastered muhammadan strove to recollect the name of the prophet they come to great open square whence nearly all the coal have be extract it be the end of the out working and the end of the mine far away down the gallery small pumping engine use for keep dry deep working and feed with steam from above be throb faithfully they hear it cease they have cut off the steam say kundoo hopefully they have give the order to use all the steam for the pit bank pump they will clear out the water if the water have reach the smoking gallery say janki all the company 's pump can do nothing for three day it be very hot moan jasoda the meah basket woman there be very bad air here because of the lamp put they out say janki why do you want lamp the lamp be put out and the company sit still in the utter dark somebody rise quietly and begin walk over the coal it be janki who be touch the wall with his hand where be the ledge he murmur to himself sit sit say kundoo if we die we die the air be very bad but janki still stumble and creep and tap with his pick upon the wall the woman rise to their foot stay all where you be without the lamp you can not see and be always see say janki then he pause and call out
oh you who have be in the cutting more than ten year what be the name of this open place be an old man and have forget bullia 's room answer the sonthal who have complain of the vileness of the air again say janki bullia 's room then have find it say janki the name only have slip my memory tibu 's gang 's gallery be here
lie say kundoo there have be no gallery in this place since my day three pace be the depth of the ledge mutter janki without heeding--'and
oh my poor bones!--i have find it it be here up this ledge come all you one by one to the place of my voice and will count you there be rush in the dark and janki feel the first man 's face hit his knee as the sonthal scramble up the ledge who cry janki sunua manji sit you down say janki who next one by one the woman and the man crawl up the ledge which run along one side of bullia 's room degrade muhammadan pig eat musahr and wild sonthal janki run his hand over they all now follow after say he catch hold of my heel and the woman catch the man 's clothe he do not ask whether the man have bring their pick with they miner black or white do not drop his pick one by one janki leading they creep into the old gallery six foot way with scant four foot from hill to roof the air be well here say jasoda they could hear her heart beat in thick sick bump slowly slowly say janki be an old man and forget many thing this be tibu 's gallery but where be the four brick where they use to put their huqa fire on when the sahib never see slowly slowly you people behind they hear his hand disturb the small coal on the floor of the gallery and then dull sound this be one unbaked brick and this be another and another kundoo be young man let he come forward put knee upon this brick and strike here when tibu 's gang be at dinner on the last day before the good coal end they hear the man of five on the other side and five work their gallery two sunday later or it may have be one strike there kundoo but give room to go back kundoo doubt drive the pick but the first soft crush of the coal be call to he he be fight for his life and for unda pretty little unda with ring on all her toe for unda and the forty rupee the woman sing the song of the pick the terrible slow swing melody with the muttered chorus that repeat the sliding of the loosen coal and to each cadence kundoo smite in the black dark when he could do no more sunua manji take the pick and strike for his life and his wife and his village beyond the blue hill over the tarachunda river an hour the man work
and then the woman clear away the coal it be far than think say janki the air be very bad but strike kundoo strike hard for the fifth time kundoo take up the pick as the sonthal crawl back the song have scarcely recommence when it be break by yell from kundoo that echo down the gallery
