hua we be through we be through the imprison air in the mine shoot through the opening and the woman at the far end of the gallery hear the water rush through the pillar of bullia 's room and roar against the ledge having fulfil the law under which it work it rise no far the woman scream and press forward the water have come we shall be kill let we go kundoo crawl through the gap and find himself in prop gallery by the simple process of hit his head against beam do know the pit or do not chuckle janki this be the number five go you out slowly give your name ho rahim count your gang now let we go forward each catch hold of the other as before they form line in the darkness and janki lead they for pit man in strange pit be only one degree less liable to err than an ordinary mortal underground for the first time at last they see flare lamp and gang janki mogul and rahim of twenty two stumble dazed into the glare of the draught furnace at the bottom of five janki feel his way and the rest behind water have come into twenty two god know where be the other have bring these man from tibu 's gallery in our cutting make connection through the north side of the gallery take we to the cage say janki meah at the pit bank of twenty two some thousand people clamour and weep and shout one hundred man one thousand man have be drown in the cutting they would all go to their home to morrow where be their man little unda her cloth drench with the rain stand at the pit mouth call down the shaft for kundoo they have swe the cage clear of the mouth and her only answer be the murmur of the flood in the pit 's eye two hundred and sixty foot below look after that woman she will chuck herself down the shaft in minute shout the manager but he need not have trouble unda be afraid of death she want kundoo the assistant be watch the flood and see how far he could wade into it there be lull in the water and the whirlpool have slacken the mine be full and the people at the pit bank howl my faith we shall be lucky if we have five hundred hand on the place to morrow say the manager there be some chance yet of run temporary dam across that water shove in anything tub and bullock cart if you have not enough brick make they work now if they never work before you ganger make they work little by little the crowd be break into detachment and push towards the water with promise of overtime the dam make begin and when it be fairly under way the manager think that the hour have come for the pump there be no fresh inrush into the mine the tall red iron clamp pump beam rise and fall and the pump snore and gutter and shriek as the first water pour out of the pipe we must run she all to night say the manager wearily but there be no hope for the poor devil down below look here gur sahai if you be proud of your engine show what they can do now gur sahai grin and nod with his right hand upon the lever and an oil can in his left he could do no more than he be do but he could keep that up till the dawn be the company 's pump to be beat by the vagary of that troublesome tarachunda river never never and the pump sob and pant never never the manager sit in the shelter of the pit bank roofing try to dry himself by the pump boiler fire and in the dreary dusk he see the crowd on the dam scatter and fly that be the end he groan twill take we six week to persuade they that we have not try to drown their mate on purpose
oh for decent rational geordie but the flight have no panic in it man have run over from five with astounding news and the foreman could not hold their gang together presently surround by clamorous crew gang rahim mogul and janki and ten basket woman walk up to report themselves and pretty little unda steal away to janki 's hut to prepare his evening meal alone find the way explain janki meah and now will the company give pension the simple pit folk shout and leap and go back to the dam reassure in their old belief that whatever happen so great be the power of the company whose salt they eat none of they could be kill but gur sahai only bar his white tooth and keep his hand upon the lever and prove his pump to the uttermost say say the assistant to the manager week later do you recollect germinal yes queer thing think of it in the cage when that balk go by why oh this business seem to be germinal upside down janki be in my veranda all this morning tell that kundoo have elope with his wife unda or anda think her name be hillo and those be the cattle that you risk your life to clear out of twenty two no be think of the company 's prop not the company 's man sound well to say so now but do not believe you old fellow in flood time tweed say tae till what gars ye rin sae still till say tae tweed though ye rin wi speed an rin slaw-- yet where ye droon ae man droon twa there be no get over the river to night sahib they say that bullock cart have be wash down already and the ekka that go over half hour before you come have not yet reach the far side be the sahib in haste will drive the ford elephant in to show he ohe mahout there in the shed bring out ram pershad and if he will face the current good an elephant never lie sahib and ram pershad be separate from his friend kala nag he too wish to cross to the far side well do well do my king go half way across mahoutji and see what the river say well do ram pershad pearl among elephant go into the river hit he on the head fool be the goad make only to scratch thy own fat back with bastard what be the boulder to thee ram pershad my rustum my mountain of strength go in go in no sahib it be useless you can hear he trumpet he be tell kala nag that he can not come over he have swing round and be shake his head he be no fool he know what the barhwi mean when it be angry indeed thou art no fool my child salaam ram pershad bahadur take he under the tree mahout and see that he get his spice well do thou chiefest among tusker salaam to the sirkar and go to sleep what be to be do the sahib must wait till the river go down it will shrink to morrow morning if god please or the day after at the late now why do the sahib get so angry be his servant before god do not create this stream what can do my hut and all that be therein be at the service of the sahib and it be begin to rain come away my lord how will the river go down for your throwing abuse at it in the old day the english people be not thus the fire carriage have make they soft in the old day when they drave behind horse by day or by night they say naught if river bar the way or carriage sit down in the mud it be the will of god not like fire carriage which go and go and go and would go though all the devil in the land hang on to its tail the fire carriage hath spoil the english people after all what be day lose or for that matter what be two day be the sahib go to his own wedding that he be so mad with haste be an old man and see few sahib forgive if have forget the respect that be due to they the sahib be not angry his own wedding ho ho the mind of an old man be like the numah_-tree fruit bud blossom and the dead leaf of all the year of the past flourish together old and new and that which be go out of remembrance all three be there sit on the bedstead sahib and drink milk or would the sahib in truth care to drink my tobacco it be good it be the tobacco of nuklao my son who be in service there send it to drink then sahib if you know how to handle the tube the sahib take it like musalman where do he learn that his own wedding ho ho the sahib say that there be no wedding in the matter at all now be it likely that the sahib would speak true talk to who be only black man small wonder then that he be in haste thirty year have beat the gong at this ford but never have see sahib in such haste thirty year sahib that be very long time thirty year ago this ford be on the track of the bunjara and have see two thousand pack bullock cross in one night now the rail have come and the fire carriage say buz buz buz and hundred lakh of maund slide across that big bridge it be very wonderful but the ford be lonely now that there be no bunjara to camp under the tree nay do not trouble to look at the sky without it will rain till the dawn the boulder be talk tonight in the bed of the river hear they they would be husk your bone sahib have you try to cross see will shut the door and no rain can enter ahi ugh thirty year on the bank of the ford an old man be and where be the oil for the lamp your pardon but because of my year sleep no sounder than dog and you move to the door look then sahib look and listen full half kos from bank to bank be the stream now you can see it under the star and there be ten foot of water therein it will not shrink because of the anger in your eye and it will not be quiet on account of your curse which be loud sahib your voice or the voice of the river call to it perhaps it will be ashamed lie down and sleep afresh sahib know the anger of the barhwi when there have fall rain in the foot hill swim the flood once on night ten fold bad than this and by the favour of god be release from death when have come to the very gate thereof may tell the tale very good talk will fill the pipe anew thirty year ago it be when be young man and have but newly come to the ford be strong then and the bunjara have no doubt when say this ford be clear have toil all night up to my shoulder blade in run water amid hundred bullock mad with fear and have bre they across lose not hoof when all be do fetch the shiver man and they give for reward the pick of their cattle the bell bullock of the drive so great be the honour in which be hold but to day when the rain fall and the river rise creep into my hut and whimper like dog my strength be go from be an old man and the fire carriage have make the ford desolate they be will not to call the strong one of the barhwi behold my face sahib it be the face of monkey and my arm it be the arm of an old woman swear to you sahib that woman have love this face and have rest in the hollow of this arm twenty year ago sahib believe this be true talk twenty year ago come to the door and look across can you see thin fire very far away down the stream that be the temple fire in the shrine of hanuman of the village of pateera north under the big star be the village itself but it be hide by bend of the river be that far to swim sahib would you take off your clothe and adventure yet swim to pateera not once but many time and there be mugger in the river too love know no caste else why should musalman and the son of musalman have seek hindu woman widow of the hindus the sister of the headman of pateera
but it be even so they of the headman 's household come on pilgrimage to muttra when she be but newly bride silver tire be upon the wheel of the bullock cart and silken curtain hide the woman sahib make no haste in their conveyance for the wind part the curtain and see she when they return from pilgrimage the boy that be her husband have die and see she again in the bullock cart by god these hindus be fool what be it to whether she be hindu or jain scavenger leper or whole would have marry she and make she home by the ford the seventh of the nine bar say that man may not marry one of the idolater be that truth both shiah and sunni say that musalman may not marry one of the idolater be the sahib priest then that he know so much will tell he something that he do not know there be neither shiah nor sunni forbidden nor idolater in love and the nine bar be but nine little fagot that the flame of love utterly burn away in truth would have take she
but what could do the headman would have send his man to break my head with stave be not be not afraid of any five man but against half village who can prevail therefore it be my custom these thing having be arrange between us twain to go by night to the village of pateera and there we meet among the crop no man know aught of the matter behold now be will not to cross here skirt the jungle to the river bend where the railway bridge be and thence across the elbow of land to pateera the light of the shrine be my guide when the night be dark that jungle near the river be very full of snake little karait that sleep on the sand and moreover her brother would have slay have they find in the crop but none know none know save she and and the blow sand of the river bed cover the track of my foot in the hot month it be an easy thing to pass from the ford to pateera and in the first rain when the river rise slowly it be an easy thing also set the strength of my body against the strength of the stream and nightly eat in my hut here and drink at pateera yonder she have say that one hirnam singh thief have seek she and he be of village up the river but on the same bank all sikhs be dog and they have refuse in their folly that good gift of god tobacco be ready to destroy hirnam singh that ever he have come nigh she and the more because he have swear to she that she have lover and that he would lie in wait and give the name to the headman unless she go away with he what cur be these sikhs after that news swim always with little sharp knife in my belt and evil would it have be for man have he stay know not the face of hirnam singh but would have kill any who come between and she upon night in the beginning of the rain be minded to go across to pateera albeit the river be angry now the nature of the barhwi be this sahib in twenty breath it come down from the hill wall three foot high and have see it between the lighting of fire and the cooking of chupatty grow from runnel to sister of the jumna when leave this bank there be shoal half mile down and make shift to fetch it and draw breath there ere go forward for feel the hand of the river heavy upon my heel yet what will young man not do for love 's sake there be but little light from the star and midway to the shoal branch of the stinking deodar tree brush my mouth as swim that be sign of heavy rain in the foot hill and beyond for the deodar be strong tree not easily shake from the hillside make haste the river aid but ere touch the shoal the pulse of the stream beat as it be within and around and behold the shoal be go and ride high on the crest of wave that run from bank to bank have the sahib ever be cast into much water that fight and will not let man use his limb to my head upon the water it seem as though there be naught but water to the world 's end and the river drave with its driftwood man be very little thing in the belly of flood and this flood though know it not be the great flood about which man talk still my liver be dissolve and lie like log upon my back in the fear of death there be live thing in the water cry and howl grievously beast of the forest and cattle and once the voice of man ask for help but the rain come and lash the water white and hear no more save the roar of the boulder below and the roar of the rain above thus be whirl down stream wrestle for the breath in it be very hard to die when one be young can the sahib stand here see the railway bridge look there be the light of the mail train go to peshawur the bridge be now twenty foot above the river but upon that night the water be roar against the lattice work and against the lattice come foot first but much driftwood be pile there and upon the pier and take no great hurt only the river press as strong man press weak scarcely could take hold of the lattice work and crawl to the upper boom sahib the water be foam across the rail foot deep judge therefore what manner of flood it must have be could not hear could not see could but lie on the boom and pant for breath after while the rain cease and there come out in the sky certain new wash star and by their light see that there be no end to the black water as far as the eye could travel and the water have rise upon the rail there be dead beast in the driftwood on the pier and other catch by the neck in the lattice work and other not yet drown who strove to find foothold on the lattice work buffalo and kine and wild pig and deer one or two and snake and jackal past all count their body be black upon the left side of the bridge but the small of they be force through the lattice work and whirl down stream thereafter the star die and the rain come down afresh and the river rise yet more and feel the bridge begin to stir under as man stir in his sleep
ere he wake but be not afraid sahib swear to you that be not afraid though have no power in my limb know that should not die till have see she once more but be very cold and feel that the bridge must go there be tremble in the water such trembling as go before the coming of great wave and the bridge lift its flank to the rush of that come so that the right lattice dip under water and the leave rise clear on my beard sahib be speak god 's truth as mirzapore stone boat careen to the wind so the barhwi bridge turn thus and in no other manner slide from the boom into deep water and behind come the wave of the wrath of the river hear its voice and the scream of the middle part of the bridge as it move from the pier and sink and know no more till rise in the middle of the great flood put forth my hand to swim and lo it fall upon the knotted hair of the head of man he be dead for no one but the strong one of barhwi could have live in that race he have be dead full two day for he ride high wallowing and be an aid to laugh then know for surety that should yet see she and take no harm and twist my finger in the hair of the man for be far spend and together we go down the stream he the dead and the living lack that help should have sink the cold be in my marrow and my flesh be rib and sodden on my bone
but he have no fear who have know the uttermost of the power of the river and let he go where he choose at last we come into the power of side current that set to the right bank and strove with my foot to draw with it
but the dead man swing heavily in the whirl and fear that some branch have strike he and that he would sink the top of the tamarisk brush my knee
so know we be come into flood water above the crop and after let down my leg and feel bottom the ridge of field and after the dead man stay upon knoll under fig tree and draw my body from the water rejoice do the sahib know whither the backwash of the flood have bear to the knoll which be the eastern boundary mark of the village of pateera no other place draw the dead man up on the grass for the service that he have do and also because know not whether should need he again then go cry thrice like jackal to the appoint place which be near the byre of the headman 's house
but my love be already there weep she fear that the flood have sweep my hut at the barhwi ford when come softly through the ankle deep water she think it be ghost and would have flee but put my arm round she and be no ghost in those day though be an old man now ho ho dry corn in truth maize without juice ho ho footnote grieve to say that the warden of barhwi ford be responsible here for two very bad pun in the vernacular.--r
k. tell she the story of the breaking of the barhwi bridge
and she say that be great than mortal man for none may cross the barhwi in full flood and have see what never man have see before hand in hand we go to the knoll where the dead lay and show she by what help have make the ford she look also upon the body under the star for the latter end of the night be clear and hide her face in her hand cry it be the body of hirnam singh say the swine be of more use dead than live my beloved
and she say surely for he have save the dear life in the world to my love none the less he can not stay here for that would bring shame upon the body be not gunshot from her door then say roll the body with my hand god hath judge between we hirnam singh that thy blood might not be upon my head now whether have do thee wrong in keep thee from the burn ghat do thou and the crow settle together so cast he adrift into the flood water and he be draw out to the open ever wag his thick black beard like priest under the pulpit board and see no more of himam singh before the breaking of the day we two part and move towards such of the jungle as be not flood with the full light see what have do in the darkness and the bone of my body be loosen in my flesh for there run two kos of rag water between the village of pateera and the tree of the far bank and in the middle the pier of the barhwi bridge show like broken tooth in the jaw of an old man nor be there any life upon the water neither bird nor boat but only an army of drown thing bullock and horse and man and the river be red than blood from the clay of the foot hill never have see such flood never since that year have see the like and sahib no man live have do what have do there be no return for that day not for all the land of the headman would venture second time without the shield of darkness that cloak danger go kos up the river to the house of blacksmith say that the flood have sweep from my hut and they give food seven day stay with the blacksmith till boat come and return to my house there be no trace of wall or roof or floor naught but patch of slimy mud judge therefore sahib how far the river must have rise it be write that should not die either in my house or in the heart of the barhwi or under the wreck of the barhwi bridge for god send down hirnam singh two day dead though know not how the man die to be my buoy and support hirnam singh have be in hell these twenty year and the thought of that night must be the flower of his torment listen sahib the river have change its voice it be go to sleep before the dawn to which there be yet one hour with the light it will come down afresh how do know have be here thirty year without know the voice of the river as father know the voice of his son every moment it be talk less angrily swear that there will be no danger for one hour or perhaps two can not answer no not even to give sweetmeat to the baby folk my house look you be empty and be an old man dutt ram pershad good luck go with you sahib the sending of dana da when the devil rid on your chest remember the chamar --_native proverb once upon time some people in india make new heaven and new earth out of break tea cup miss brooch or two and hair brush these be hide under bush or stuff into hole in the hillside and an entire civil service of subordinate god use to find or mend they again and every one say there be more thing in heaven and earth than be dream of in our philosophy several other thing happen also but the religion never seem to get much beyond its first manifestation though it add an air line postal service and orchestral effect in order to keep abreast of the tune and choke off competition this religion be too elastic for ordinary use it stretch itself and embrace piece of everything that the medicine man of all age have manufacture it approve of and steal from freemasonry loot the latter day rosicrucians of half their pet word take any fragment of egyptian philosophy that it find in the encyclopaedia britannica annex as many of the veda as have be translate into french or english and talk of all the rest build in the german version of what be leave of the zend avesta encourage white gray and black magic include spiritualism palmistry fortune telling by card hot chestnut double kernelled nut and tallow dropping would have adopt voodoo and oboe
have it know anything about they and show itself in every way one of the most accommodating arrangement that have ever be invent since the birth of the sea when it be in thorough working order with all the machinery down to the subscription complete dana da come from nowhere with nothing in his hand and write chapter in its history which have hitherto be unpublished he say that his first name be dana and his second be da now set aside dana of the new york sun dana be bhil name and da fit no native of india unless you except the bengali de as the original spelling da be lap or finnish and dana da be neither finn chin bhil bengali lap nair gond romaney magh bokhariot kurd armenian levantine jew persian punjabi madrasi parsee nor anything else know to ethnologist he be simply dana da and decline to give further information for the sake of brevity and as roughly indicate his origin he be call the native he might have be the original old man of the mountain who be say to be the only authorised head of the tea cup creed some people say that he be
but dana da use to smile and deny any connection with the cult explain that he be an independent experimenter as have say he come from nowhere with his hand behind his back and study the creed for three week sit at the foot of those good competent to explain its mystery then he laugh aloud and go away but the laugh might have be either of devotion or derision when he return he be without money but his pride be unabate he declare that he know more about the thing in heaven and earth than those who teach he and for this contumacy be abandon altogether his next appearance in public life be at big cantonment in upper india and he be then tell fortune with the help of three leaden dice very dirty old cloth and little tin box of opium pill he tell well fortune when he be allow half bottle of whiskey but the thing which he invent on the opium be quite worth the money he be in reduce circumstance among other people 's he tell the fortune of an englishman who have once be interested in the simla creed but who later on have marry and forget all his old knowledge in the study of baby and thing the englishman allow dana da to tell fortune for charity 's sake and give he five rupee dinner and some old clothe when he have eat dana da profess gratitude and ask if there be anything he could do for his host in the esoteric line be there any one that you love say dana da the englishman love his wife but have no desire to drag her name into the conversation he therefore shake his head be there any one that you hate say dana da the englishman say that there be several man whom he hat deeply very good
say dana da upon whom the whiskey and the opium be begin to tell only give their name and will despatch send to they and kill they now send be horrible arrangement first invent they say in iceland it be thing send by wizard and may take any form but most generally wander about the land in the shape of little purple cloud till it find the sendee and he it kill by change into the form of horse or cat or man without face it be not strictly native patent though chamar of the skin and hide caste can if irritated despatch send which sit on the breast of their enemy by night and nearly kill he very few native care to irritate chamar for this reason let despatch send say dana da be nearly dead now with want and drink and opium but should like to kill man before die can send send anywhere you choose and in any form except in the shape of man the englishman have no friend that he wish to kill but partly to soothe dana da whose eye be roll and partly to see what would be do he ask whether modify send could not be arrange for such sending as should make man 's life burden to he and yet do he no harm if this be possible he notify his willingness to give dana da ten rupee for the job be not what be once say dana da and must take the money because be poor to what englishman shall send it send send to lone sahib say the englishman name man who have be most bitter in rebuke he for his apostasy from the tea cup creed dana da laugh and nod could have choose
no well man myself say he will see that he find the send about his path and about his bed he lie down on the hearth rug turn up the white of his eye shiver all over and begin to snort this be magic or opium or the send or all three when he open his eye he vow that the send have start upon the war path and be at that moment fly up to the town where lone sahib live give my ten rupee say dana da wearily and write letter to lone sahib tell he and all who believe with he that you and friend be use power great than theirs they will see that you be speak the truth he depart unsteadily with the promise of some more rupee if anything come of the send the englishman send letter to lone sahib couch in what he remember of the terminology of the creed he write also in the day of what you hold to be my backsliding have obtain enlightenment and with enlightenment have come power then he grow so deeply mysterious that the recipient of the letter could make neither head nor tail of it and be proportionately impressed for he fancy that his friend have become fifth round when man be fifth rounder he can do more than slade and houdin combine lone sahib read the letter in five different fashion and be begin sixth interpretation when his bearer dash in with the news that there be cat on the bed now if there be one thing that lone sahib hat more than another it be cat he scold the bearer for not turn it out of the house the bearer say that he be afraid all the door of the bedroom have be shut throughout the morning and no real cat could possibly have enter the room he would prefer not to meddle with the creature lone sahib enter the room gingerly and there on the pillow of his bed sprawl and whimper wee white kitten not jumpsome frisky little beast but slug like crawler with its eye barely open and its paw lack strength or direction kitten that ought to have be in basket with its mamma lone sahib catch it by the scruff of its neck hand it over to the sweeper to be drown and fin the bearer four anna that evening as he be read in his room he fancy that he see something move about on the hearth rug outside the circle of light from his reading lamp when the thing begin to myowl he realise that it be kitten wee white kitten nearly blind and very miserable he be seriously angry and speak bitterly to his bearer who say that there be no kitten in the room when he bring in the lamp and real kitten of tender age generally have mother cat in attendance if the presence will go out into the veranda and listen say the bearer he will hear no cat how therefore can the kitten on the bed and the kitten on the hearth rug be real kitten lone sahib go out to listen and the bearer follow he but there be no sound of any one mew for her child he return to his room having hurl the kitten down the hillside and write out the incident of the day for the benefit of his co religionist those people be so absolutely free from superstition that they ascribe anything little out of the common to agency as it be their business to know all about the agency they be on term of almost indecent familiarity with manifestation of every kind their letter drop from the ceiling unstamped and spirit use to squatter up and down their staircase all night but they have never come into contact with kitten lone sahib write out the fact note the hour and the minute as every psychical observer be bind to do and append the englishman 's letter because it be the most mysterious document and might have have bear upon anything in this world or the next an outsider would have translate all the tangle thus look out you laugh at once and now be go to make you sit up lone sahib 's co religionist find that meaning in it but their translation be refine and full of four syllable word they hold sederunt and be fill with tremulous joy for in spite of their familiarity with all the other world and cycle they have very human awe of thing send from ghost land they meet in lone sahib 's room in shrouded and sepulchral gloom and their conclave be break up by clink among the photo frame on the mantelpiece wee white kitten nearly blind be loop and writhe itself between the clock and the candlestick that stop all investigation or double here be the manifestation in the flesh it be so far as could be see devoid of purpose but it be manifestation of undoubted authenticity they draft round robin to the englishman the backslider of old day adjure he in the interest of the creed to explain whether there be any connection between the embodiment of some egyptian god or other have forget the name and his communication they call the kitten ra or toth or tum or something and when lone sahib confes that the first one have at his most misguided instance be drown by the sweeper they say consolingly that in his next life he would be bounder and not even round of the low grade these word may not be quite correct but they accurately express the sense of the house when the englishman receive the round robin it come by post he be startled and bewilder he send into the bazar for dana da who read the letter and laugh that be my send say he tell you would work well now yourself say dana da what do that letter mean in little time they will all be at my foot and your and glory!--will be drug or drunk all daylong dana da know his people when man who hat cat wake up in the morning and find little squirm kitten on his breast or put his hand into his ulster pocket and find little half dead kitten where his glove should be or open his trunk and find vile kitten among his dress shirt or go for long ride with his mackintosh strap on his saddle bow and shake little squawling kitten from its fold when he open it or go out to dinner and find little blind kitten under his chair or stay at home and find writhe kitten under the quilt or wriggle among his boot or hang head downwards in his tobacco jar or be mangle by his terrier in the veranda,--when such man find one kitten neither more nor less once day in place where no kitten rightly could or should be he be naturally upset when he dare not murder his daily trove because he believe it to be manifestation an emissary an embodiment and half dozen other thing all out of the regular course of nature he be more than upset he be actually distressed some of lone sahib 's co religionist think that he be highly favour individual but many say that if he have treat the first kitten with proper respect as suit oth ra tum sennacherib embodiment all this trouble would have be avert they compare he to the ancient mariner but none the less they be proud of he and proud of the englishman who have send the manifestation they do not call it send because icelandic magic be not in their programme after sixteen kitten that be to say after one fortnight for there be three kitten on the first day to impress the fact of the send the whole camp be uplift by letter it come fly through window from the old man of the mountain the head of all the creed explain the manifestation in the most beautiful language and soak up all the credit of it for himself the englishman say the letter be not there at all he be backslider without power or asceticism who could not even raise table by force of volition much less project an army of kitten through space the entire arrangement say the letter be strictly orthodox work and sanction by the high authority within the pale of the creed there be great joy at this for some of the weak brother see that an outsider who have be work on independent line could create kitten whereas their own ruler have never go beyond crockery and break at good be show desire to break line on their own trail in fact there be the promise of schism second round robin be draft to the englishman begin scoffer and end with selection of curse from the rite of mizraim and memphis and the commination of jugana who be fifth round upon whose name an upstart third round once trade papal first he be furiously angry and then he laugh for five minute have think he say that they would have come to in another week would have show that send the send and they would have discrowne the old sending shall cease in two day time in that day there shall be twelve kitten and thenceforward none at all the people shall judge between we this be sign by dana da who add pentacle and pentagram and
