and they remember then that dana da have laugh at they some year ago it be officially announce that the old man of the mountain would treat the matter with contempt dana da be an independent investigator without single round at the back of he
but this do not soothe his people they want to see fight they be very human for all their spirituality lone sahib who be really be wear out with kitten submit meekly to his fate he feel that he be be kitten to prove the power of dana da as the poet say when the state day dawn the shower of kitten begin some be white and some be tabby and all be about the same loathsome age three be on his hearth rug three in his bath room and the other six turn up at interval among the visitor who come to see the prophecy break down never be more satisfactory sending on the next day there be no kitten and the next day and all the other day be kittenless and quiet the people murmur and look to the old man of the mountain for an explanation letter write on palm leaf drop from the ceiling but every one except lone sahib feel that letter be not what the occasion demand there should have be cat there should have be cats,--full grow one the letter prove conclusively that there have be hitch in the psychic current which collide with dual identity have interfere with the percipient activity all along the main line the kitten be still go on but owe to some failure in the develop fluid they be not materialise the air be thick with letter for few day afterwards unseen hand play gluck and beethoven on finger bowl and clock shade but all man feel that psychic life be mockery without materialise kitten even lone sahib shout with the majority on this head dana da 's letter be very insulting and if he have then offer to lead new departure there be no know what might not have happen
but dana da be die of whiskey and opium in the englishman 's godown and have small heart for honour they have be put to shame say he never be such send it have kill nonsense say the englishman you be go to die dana da and that sort of stuff must be leave behind will admit that you have make some queer thing come about tell honestly now how be it do give ten more rupee say dana da
faintly and if die before spend they bury they with the silver be count out while dana da be fight with death his hand close upon the money and he smile grim smile bend low he whisper the englishman bent bunnia_--mission school expelled--_box wallah peddler)--ceylon pearl merchant all mine english education out cast and make up name dana da england with american thought read man and and you give ten rupee several time give the sahib 's bearer two eight month for cat little little cat write and he put they about very clever man very few kitten now in the bazar
ask lone sahib 's sweeper 's wife so say dana da gasp and pass away into land where if all be true there be no materialisation and the making of new creed be discourage but consider the gorgeous simplicity of it all on the city then she let they down by cord through the window for her house be upon the town wall and she dwell upon the wall.--_joshua ii lalun be member of the most ancient profession in the world lilith be she very great grandmamma and that be before the day of eve as every one know in the west people say rude thing about lalun 's profession and write lecture about it and distribute the lecture to young person in order that morality may be preserve in the east where the profession be hereditary descend from mother to daughter nobody write lecture or take any notice and that be distinct proof of the inability of the east to manage its own affair lalun 's real husband for even lady of lalun 's profession in the east must have husband be big jujube tree her mamma who have marry fig tree spend ten thousand rupee on lalun 's wedding which be bles by forty seven clergyman of mamma 's church and distribute five thousand rupee in charity to the poor and that be the custom of the land the advantage of have jujube tree for husband be obvious you can not hurt his feeling and he look impose lalun 's husband stand on the plain outside the city wall and lalun 's house be upon the east wall face the river if you fall from the broad window seat you drop thirty foot sheer into the city ditch
but if you stay where you should and look forth you see all the cattle of the city be drive down to water the student of the government college playing cricket the high grass and tree that fringe the river bank the great sand bar that rib the river the red tomb of dead emperor beyond the river and very far away through the blue heat haze glint of the snow of the himalayas wali dad use to lie in the window seat for hour at time watch this view he be young muhammadan who be suffer acutely from education of the english variety and know it his father have send he to mission school to get wisdom and wali dad have absorb more than ever his father or the missionary intend he should when his father die wali dad be independent and spend two year experiment with the creed of the earth and read book that be of no use to anybody after he have make an unsuccessful attempt to enter the roman catholic church and the presbyterian fold at the same time the missionary find he out and call he name
but they do not understand his trouble he discover lalun on the city wall and become the most constant of her few admirer he possess head that english artist at home would rave over and paint amid impossible surround face that female novelist would use with delight through nine hundred page in reality he be only clean breed young muhammadan with pencil eye brow small cut nostril little foot and hand and very tired look in his eye by virtue of his twenty two year he have grow neat black beard which he stroke with pride and keep delicately scent his life seem to be divide between borrow book from and make love to lalun in the window seat he compose song about she and some of the song be sing to this day in the city from the street of the mutton butcher to the copper smiths ward one song the pretty of all say that the beauty of lalun be so great that it trouble the heart of the british government and cause they to lose their peace of mind that be the way the song be sing in the street
but if you examine it carefully and know the key to the explanation you will find that there be three pun in it on beauty heart and peace of mind,'--so that it run by the subtlety of lalun the administration of the government be trouble and it lose such and such man when wali dad sing that song his eye glow like hot coal and lalun lean back among the cushion and throw bunch of jasmine bud at wali dad
but first it be necessary to explain something about the supreme government which be above all and below all and behind all gentleman come from england spend few week in india walk round this great sphinx of the plain and write book upon its way and its work denounce or praise it as their own ignorance prompt consequently all the world know how the supreme government conduct itself
but no one not even the supreme government know everything about the administration of the empire year by year england send out fresh draft for the first fighting line which be officially call the indian civil service these die or kill themselves by overwork or be worried to death or break in health and hope in order that the land may be protect from death and sickness famine and war and may eventually become capable of stand alone it will never stand alone but the idea be pretty one and man be willing to die for it and yearly the work of push and coax and scold and pet the country into good living go forward if an advance be make all credit be give to the native while the englishman stand back and wipe their forehead if failure occur the englishman step forward and take the blame overmuch tenderness of this kind have bree strong belief among many native that the native be capable of administer the country and many devout englishman believe this also because the theory be state in beautiful english with all the late political colour there be other man who though uneducated see vision and dream dream
and they too hope to administer the country in their own way that be to say with garnish of red sauce such man must exist among two hundred million people and if they be not attend to may cause trouble and even break the great idol call pax britannic which as the newspaper say live between peshawur and cape comorin be the day of doom to dawn to morrow you would find the supreme government take measure to allay popular excitement and put guard upon the graveyard that the dead might troop forth orderly the young civilian would arrest gabriel on his own responsibility if the archangel could not produce deputy commissioner 's permission to make music or other noise as the license say whence it be easy to see that mere man of the flesh who would create tumult must fare badly at the hand of the supreme government
and they do there be no outward sign of excitement there be no confusion there be no knowledge when due and sufficient reason have be give weigh and approve the machinery move forward and the dreamer of dream and the seer of vision be go from his friend and follow he enjoy the hospitality of government there be no restriction upon his movement within certain limit but he must not confer any more with his brother dreamer once in every six month the supreme government assure itself that he be well and take formal acknowledgment of his existence no one protest against this detention because the few people who know about it be in deadly fear of seem to know he and never single newspaper take up his case or organise demonstration on his behalf because the newspaper of india have get behind that lie proverb which say the pen be mighty than the sword and can walk delicately so now you know as much as you ought about wali dad the educational mixture and the supreme government lalun have not yet be describe she would need so wali dad say thousand pen of gold and ink scent with musk she have be variously compare to the moon the dil sagar lake spot quail gazelle the sun on the desert of kutch the dawn the star and the young bamboo these comparison imply that she be beautiful exceedingly accord to the native standard which be practically the same as those of the west her eye be black and her hair be black and her eyebrow be black as leech her mouth be tiny and say witty thing her hand be tiny and have save much money her foot be tiny and have tread on the naked heart of many man but as wali dad sing lalun be lalun and when you have say that you have only come to the beginning of knowledge the little house on the city wall be just big enough to hold lalun and her maid and pussy cat with silver collar big pink and blue cut glass chandelier hang from the ceiling of the reception room petty nawab have give lalun the horror
and she keep it for politeness sake the floor of the room be of polished chunam white as curd lattice window of carve wood be set in one wall there be profusion of squabby pluffy cushion and fat carpet everywhere and lalun 's silver huqa stud with turquoise have special little carpet all to its shin self wali dad be nearly as permanent fixture as the chandelier as have say he lie in the window seat and meditate on life and death and lalun specially lalun the foot of the young man of the city tend to her doorway and then retire for lalun be particular maiden slow of speech reserve of mind and not in the least inclined to orgy which be nearly certain to end in strife if be of no value be unworthy of this honour say lalun if be of value they be unworthy of and that be crooked sentence in the long hot night of latter april and may all the city seem to assemble in lalun 's little white room to smoke and to talk shiah of the grim and most uncompromising persuasion sufis who have lose all belief in the prophet and retain but little in god wander hindu priest pass southward on their way to the central india fair and other affair pundit in black gown with spectacle on their nose and undigested wisdom in their inside bearded headman of the ward sikhs with all the detail of the late ecclesiastical scandal in the golden temple red eyed priest from beyond the border look like trap wolf and talk like raven m. a. 's of the university very superior and very voluble all these people and more also you might find in the white room wali dad lie in the window seat and listen to the talk it be lalun 's salon
say wali dad to and it be electic be not that the word outside of freemason 's lodge have never see such gathering din once with jew yahoudi he spit into the city ditch with apology for allow national feeling to overcome he though have lose every belief in the world say he and try to be proud of my losing can not help hat jew lalun admit no jews here but what in the world do all these man do the curse of our country say wali dad they talk it be like the athenian always hear and tell some new thing ask the pearl and she will show you how much she know of the news of the city and the province lalun know everything lalun say at random she be talk to gentleman of the kurd persuasion who have come in from god
where--'when do the 175th regiment go to agra it do not go at all say lalun without turn her head they have order the 118th to go in its stead that regiment go to lucknow in three month unless they give fresh order that be so say wali dad without shade of doubt can you with your telegram and your newspaper do well always hear and tell some new thing he go on my friend have your god ever smite european nation for gossip in the bazar india have gossip for century always stand in the bazar until the soldier go by therefore you be here today instead of starve in your own country and be not muhammadan be product demnition product that also owe to you and your that can not make an end to my sentence without quote from your author he pull at the huqa and mourn half feelingly half in earnest for the shatter hope of his youth wali dad be always mourn over something or other the country of which he despair or the creed in which he have lose faith or the life of the english which he could by no mean understand lalun never mourn she play little song on the sitar and to hear she sing peacock cry again be always fresh pleasure she know all the song that have ever be sing from the war song of the south that make the old man angry with the young man and the young man angry with the state to the love song of the north where the sword whinny whicker like angry kite in the pause between the kiss and the pass fill with armed man and the lover be tear from his beloved and cry ai ai ai she know how to make up tobacco for the huqa so that it smell like the gate of paradise and waft you gently through they she could embroider strange thing in gold and silver and dance softly with the moonlight when it come in at the window also she know the heart of man and the heart of the city and whose wife be faithful and whose untrue and more of the secret of the government office than be good to be set down in this place nasiban her maid say that her jewellery be worth ten thousand pound and that some night thief would enter and murder she for its possession but lalun say that all the city would tear that thief limb from limb and that he whoever he be know it
so she take she sitar and sit in the window seat and sing song of old day that have be sing by girl of her profession in an armed camp on the eve of great battle the day before the fords of the jumna run red and sivaji flee fifty mile to delhi with toorkh stallion at his horse 's tail and another lalun on his saddle bow it be what man call mahratta laonee
and it said:--- their warrior force chimajee before the peishwa lead the child of the sun and fire behind he turn and flee and the chorus said:--
with they there fight who rid so free with sword and turban red the warrior youth who earn his fee at peril of his head at peril of his head
say wali dad in english to thank to your government all our head be protect and with the educational facility at my command'--his eye twinkle wickedly--'i might be distinguish member of the local administration perhaps in time might even be member of legislative council do not speak english say lalun bend over her sitar afresh the chorus go out from the city wall to the blacken wall of fort amara which dominate the city no man know the precise extent of fort amara three king build it hundred of year ago and they say that there be mile of underground room beneath its wall it be people with many ghost detachment of garrison artillery and company of infantry in its prime it hold ten thousand man and fill its ditch with corps at peril of his head sing lalun again and again
head move on one of the rampart the gray head of an old man and voice rough as shark skin on sword hilt send back the last line of the chorus and break into song that could not understand though lalun and wali dad listen intently what be it who be it consistent man say wali dad he fight you in when he be warrior youth refought you in and he try to fight you in but you have learn the trick of blow man from gun too well now he be old but he would still fight if he could be he wahabi then why should he answer to mahratta laonee if he be wahabi or sikh say i. do not know say wali dad he have lose perhaps his religion perhaps he wish to be king perhaps he be king do not know his name that be lie wali dad if you know his career you must know his name that be quite true belong to nation of liar would rather not tell you his name think for yourself lalun finish her song point to the fort and say simply khem singh hm say wali dad if the pearl choose to tell you the pearl be fool translate to lalun who laugh choose to tell what choose to tell they keep khem singh in burma say she they keep he there for many year until his mind be change in he so great be the kindness of the government find this they send he back to his own country that he might look upon it before he die he be an old man but when he look upon this country his memory will come moreover there be many who remember he he be an interesting survival say wali dad pull at the huqa he return to country now full of educational and political reform but as the pearl say there be many who remember he he be once great man there will never be any more great man in india they will all when they be boy go whore after strange god
and they will become citizens--“fellow citizens”--“illustrious fellow citizen what be it that the native paper call they wali dad seem to be in very bad temper lalun look out of the window and smile into the dust haze go away think about khem singh who have once make history with thousand follower and would have be princele but
for the power of the supreme government aforesaid the senior captain commanding fort amara be away on leave but the subaltern his deputy have drift down to the club where find he and enquire of he whether it be really true that political prisoner have be add to the attraction of the fort the sub altern explain at great length for this be the first time that he have hold command of the fort and his glory lie heavy upon he
yes say he man be send in to about week ago from down the line thorough gentleman whoever he be of course do all could for he he have his two servant and some silver cooking pot and he look for all the world like native officer call he subadar sahib just as well to be on the safe side y'know look here subadar sahib say you be hand over to my authority and be suppose to guard you now do not want to make your life hard
but you must make thing easy for all the fort be at your disposal from the flagstaff to the dry ditch and shall be happy to entertain you in any way can
but you must not take advantage of it give your word that you will not try to escape subadar sahib and will give you my word that you shall have no heavy guard put over you think the good way of get at he be by go at he
straight y'know
and it be by jove the old man give his word and move about the fort as contented as sick crow he be rummy chap always ask to be tell where he be and what the building about he be have to sign slip of blue paper when he turn up acknowledge receipt of his body and all that and be responsible y'know that he do not get away queer thing though look after johnnie old enough to be your grandfather be not it come to the fort one of these day and see he for reason which will appear never go to the fort while khem singh be then within its wall know he only as gray head see from lalun 's window gray head and harsh voice but native tell that day by day as he look upon the fair land round amara his memory come back to he and with it the old hatred against the government that have be nearly efface in far off burma so he rag up and down the west face of the fort from morning till noon and from evening till the night devise vain thing in his heart and croak war song when lalun sing on the city wall as he grow more acquainted with the subaltern he unburden his old heart of some of the passion that have wither it sahib he use to say tap his stick against the parapet when be young man be one of twenty thousand horseman who come out of the city and ride round the plain here sahib be the leader of hundred then of thousand then of five thousand and now!'--he point to his two servant but from the beginning to to day would cut the throat of all the sahib in the land if could hold fast sahib lest get away and return to those who would follow forget they when be in burma
but now that be in my own country again remember everything do you remember that you have give your honour not to make your tendance hard matter say the subaltern yes to you only to you sahib say khem singh to you because you be of pleasant countenance if my turn come again sahib will not hang you nor cut your throat
thank you say the subaltern gravely as he look along the line of gun that could pound the city to powder in half an hour let we go into our own quarter khem singh come and talk with after dinner khem singh would sit on his own cushion at the subaltern 's foot drink heavy scented anise seed brandy in great gulp and tell strange story of fort amara which have be palace in the old day of begum and ranee torture to death aye in the very vaulted chamber that now serve as mess room would tell story of sobraon that make the subaltern 's cheek flush and tingle with pride of race and of the kuka rise from which so much be expect and the foreknowledge of which be share by hundred thousand soul
but he never tell tale of because as he say he be the subaltern 's guest and be year that no man black or white care to speak of once only when the anise seed brandy have slightly affect his head he say sahib speak now of matter which lie between sobraon and the affair of the kukas it be ever wonder to we that you stay your hand at all and that having stay it you do not make the land one prison now hear from without that you do great honour to all man of our country and by your own hand be destroy the terror of your name which be your strong rock and defence this be foolish thing will oil and water mix now in be not bear then subadar sahib say the subaltern and khem singh reel to his quarter the subaltern would tell of these conversation at the club and my desire to see khem singh increase
but wali dad sit in the window seat of the house on the city wall say that it would be cruel thing to do and lalun pretend that prefer the society of grizzled old sikh to hers here be tobacco here be talk here be many friend and all the news of the city and above all here be myself will tell you story and sing you song and wali dad will talk his english nonsense in your ear be that bad than watch the cage animal yonder go to morrow then if you must but to day such and such an one will be here and he will speak of wonderful thing it happen that to morrow never come and the warm heat of the latter rain give place to the chill of early october almost before be aware of the flight of the year the captain command the fort return from leave and take over charge of khem singh accord to the law of seniority the captain be not nice man he call all native nigger which besides be extreme bad form show gross ignorance what be the use of tell off two tommie to watch that old nigger say he fancy it soothe his vanity say the subaltern the man be order to keep well out of his way but he take they as tribute to his importance poor old wretch will not have line man take off regular guard in this way put on couple of native infantry sikhs say the subaltern lift his eyebrow sikhs pathans dogra
they be all alike these black vermin and the captain talk to khem singh in manner which hurt that old gentleman 's feeling fifteen year before when he have be catch for the second time every one look upon he as sort of tiger he like be regard in this light but he forget that the world go forward in fifteen year and many subaltern be promote to captaincy the captain pig be in charge of the fort say khem singh to his native guard every morning and the native guard say yes subadar sahib in deference to his age and his air of distinction
but they do not know who he be in those day the gathering in lalun 's little white room be always large and talk more than before the greeks say wali dad who have be borrow my book the inhabitant of the city of athens where they be always hear and tell some new thing rigorously seclude their woman who be fool hence the glorious institution of the heterodox woman be it not?--who be amusing and not fool all the greek philosopher delight in their company tell my friend how it go now in greece and the other place upon the continent of europe be your woman folk also fool wali dad say you never speak to we about your woman folk and we never speak about ours to you that be the bar between we yes say wali dad it be curious to think that our common meeting place should be here in the house of common how do you call she he point with the pipe mouth to lalun lalun be nothing but lalun say and that be perfectly true but if you take your place in the world wali dad and give up dream dreams-- might wear an english coat and trouser might be lead muhammadan pleader might be receive even at the commissioner 's tennis party where the english stand on one side and the native on the other in order to promote social intercourse throughout the empire heart 's heart say he to lalun quickly the sahib say that ought to quit you the sahib be always talk stupid talk return lalun with laugh in this house be queen and thou art king the sahib'--she put her arm above her head and think for moment--'the sahib shall be our vizier thine and mine wali dad because he have say that thou shouldst leave wali dad laugh immoderately and laugh too be it so say he my friend be you willing to take this lucrative government appointment lalun what shall his pay be but lalun begin to sing and for the rest of the time there be no hope of get sensible answer from she or wali dad when the one stop the other begin to quote persian poetry with triple pun in every other line some of it be not strictly proper but it be all very funny and it only come to an end when fat person in black with gold pince nez send up his name to lalun and wali dad drag into the twinkle night to walk in big rose garden and talk heresy about religion and government and man 's career in life the mohurrum the great mourning festival of the muhammadan be close at hand and the thing that wali dad say about religious fanaticism would have secure his expulsion from the loose think muslim sect there be the rose bush round we the star above we and from every quarter of the city come the boom of the big mohurrum drum you must know that the city be divide in fairly equal proportion between the hindus and the musalman and where both creed belong to the fighting race big religious festival give ample chance for trouble when they can that be to say when the authority be weak enough to allow it the hindus do their good to arrange some minor feast day of their own in time to clash with the period of general mourning for the martyr hasan and hussain the hero of the mohurrum gilt and paint paper presentation of their tomb be bear with shout and wail music torch and yell through the principal thoroughfare of the city which fakement be call tazia their passage be rigorously lie down beforehand by the police and detachment of police accompany each tazia lest the hindus should throw brick at it and the peace of the queen and the head of her loyal subject should thereby be break mohurrum time in fight town mean anxiety to all the official because if riot break out the official and not the rioter be hold responsible the former must foresee everything and while not make their precaution ridiculously elaborate must see that they be at least adequate listen to the drum say wali dad that be the heart of the people empty and make much noise how think you will the mohurrum go this year think that there will be trouble he turn down side street and leave alone with the star and sleepy police patrol then go to bed and dream that wali dad have sack the city and be make vizier with lalun 's silver huqa for mark of office all day the mohurrum drum beat in the city and all day deputation of tearful hindu gentleman besiege the deputy commissioner with assurance that they would be murder ere next dawning by the muhammadan which say the deputy commissioner in confidence to the head of police be pretty fair indication that the hindus be go to make emselves unpleasant think we can arrange little surprise for they have give the head of both creed fair warning if they choose to disregard it so much the bad for they there be large gathering in lalun 's house that night but of man that have never see before if except the fat gentleman in black with the gold pince nez wali dad lie in the window seat more bitterly scornful of his faith and its manifestation than have ever know he lalun 's maid be very busy cut up and mix tobacco for the guest we could hear the thunder of the drum as the procession accompany each tazia march to the central gathering place in the plain outside the city preparatory to their triumphant re entry and circuit within the wall all the street seem ablaze with torch and only fort amara be black and silent when the noise of the drum cease no one in the white room speak for time the first tazia have move off say wali dad look to the plain that be very early say the man with the pince nez it be only half past eight the company rise and depart some of they be man from ladakh say lalun when the last have go they bring brick tea such as the russians sell and tea urn from peshawur show now how the english memsahib make tea the brick tea be abominable when it be finish wali dad suggest go into the street be nearly sure that there will be trouble to night he say all the city think so and vox populi be vox dei as the babu say now tell you that at the corner of the padshahi gate you will find my horse all this night if you want to go about and to see thing it be most disgraceful exhibition where be the pleasure of say ya hasan ya hussain twenty thousand time in night all the procession there be two and twenty of they be now well within the city wall the drum be beat afresh the crowd be howl ya hasan ya hussain and beat their breast the brass band be play their loud and at every corner where space allow muhammadan preacher be tell the lamentable story of the death of the martyr it be impossible to move except with the crowd for the street be not more than twenty foot wide in the hindu quarter the shutter of all the shop be up and cross bar as the first tazia gorgeous erection ten foot high be bear aloft on the shoulder of score of stout man into the semi darkness of the gully of the horseman brickbat crash through its talc and tinsel side into thy hand lord murmur wali dad
profanely as yell go up from behind and native officer of police jam his horse through the crowd another brickbat follow and the tazia stagger and sway where it have stop go on in the name of the sirkar go forward shout the policeman but there be an ugly cracking and splintering of shutter and the crowd halt with oath and growl before the house whence the brickbat have be throw then without any warning break the storm not only in the gully of the horseman but in half dozen other place the tazias rock like ship at sea the long pole torch dip and rise round they while the man shout the hindus be dishonour the tazia into their temple for the faith the six or eight policeman with each tazia draw their baton and strike as long as they could in the hope of force the mob forward but they be overpower and as contingent of hindus pour into the street the fight become general half mile away where the tazia be yet untouched the drum and the shriek of ya hasanl ya hussain continue but not for long the priest at the corner of the street knock the leg from the bedstead that support their pulpit and smite for the faith while stone fall from the silent house upon friend and foe and the pack street bellow din din din tazia catch fire and be drop for flame barrier between hindu and musalman at the corner of the gully
then the crowd surge forward and wali dad draw close to the stone pillar of well it be intend from the beginning he shout in my ear with more heat than blank unbelief should be guilty of the brick be carry up to the house beforehand these swine of hindus we shall be gutte kine in their temple to night tazia after tazia some burn other tear to piece hurry past we and the mob with they howl shriek and strike at the house door in their flight at last we see the reason of the rush hugonin the assistant district superintendent of police boy of twenty have get together thirty constable and be force the crowd through the street his old gray police horse show no sign of uneasiness as it be spur breast on into the crowd and the long dog whip with which he have arm himself be never still they know we have not enough police to hold they he cry as he pass mop cut on his face they know we have not be not any of the man from the club come down to help get on you son of burn father the dog whip crack across the writhe back and the constable smite afresh with baton and gun butt with these pass the light and the shouting and wali dad begin to swear under his breath from fort amara shoot up single rocket then two side by side it be the signal for troop petitt the deputy commissioner cover with dust and sweat but calm and gently smile canter up the clean sweep street in rear of the main body of the rioter no one kill yet he shout will keep they on the run till dawn do not let they halt hugonin trot they about till the troop come the science of the defence lie solely in keep the mob on the move if they have breathing space they would halt and fire house and then the work of restore order would be more difficult to say the least of it flame have the same effect on crowd as blood have on wild beast word have reach the club and man in evening dress be begin to show themselves and lend hand in head off and break up the shout mass with stirrup leather whip or chance find stave they be not very often attack for the rioter have sense enough to know that the death of european would not mean one hang but many and possibly the appearance of the thrice dread artillery the clamour in the city redouble the hindus have descend into the street in real earnest and ere long the mob return it be strange sight there be no tazias_--only their riven platform and there be no police here and there city dignitary hindu or muhammadan be vainly implore his co religionist to keep quiet and behave themselves advice for which his white beard be pull then native officer of police unhorse but still use his spur with effect would be bear along warn all the crowd of the danger of insult the government everywhere man strike aimlessly with stick grasp each other by the throat howl and foam with rage or beat with their bare hand on the door of the house it be lucky thing that they be fight with natural weapon say to wali dad else we should have half the city kill turn as speak and look at his face his nostril be distend his eye be fix and he be smite himself softly on the breast the crowd pour by with renew riot gang of musalmans hard press by some hundred hindu fanatic wali dad leave my side with an oath and shout ya hasan ya hussain plunge into the thick of the fight where lose sight of he flee by side alley to the padshahi gate where find wali dad 's house and thence ride to the fort once outside the city wall the tumult sink to dull roar very impressive under the star and reflect great credit on the fifty thousand angry able bodied man who be make it the troop who at the deputy commissioner 's instance have be order to rendezvous quietly near the fort show no sign of be impress two company of native infantry squadron of native cavalry and company of british infantry be kick their heel in the shadow of the east face wait for order to march in be sorry to say that they be all pleased unholily please at the chance of what they call little fun the senior officer to be sure grumble at having be keep out of bed and the english troop pretend to be sulky but there be joy in the heart of all the subaltern and whisper run up and down the line no ball cartridge what beastly shame d'you think the beggar will really stand up to we hope shall meet my money lender there owe he more than can afford
oh they will not let we even unsheathe sword up go the fourth rocket fall in there the garrison artillery who to the last cherish wild hope that they might be allow to bombard the city at hundred yard range line the parapet above the east gateway and cheer themselves hoarse as the british infantry double along the road to the main gate of the city the cavalry canter on to the padshahi gate and the native infantry march slowly to the gate of the butcher the surprise be intend to be of distinctly unpleasant nature and to come on top of the defeat of the police who have be just able to keep the muhammadan from fire the house of few lead hindus the bulk of the riot lie in the north and north west ward the east and south east be by this time dark and silent and ride hastily to lalun 's house for wish to tell she to send some one in search of wali dad the house be unlighte but the door be open and climb upstairs in the darkness one small lamp in the white room show lalun and her maid lean half out of the window breathe heavily and evidently pull at something that refuse to come thou art late very late gasp lalun without turn her head
help we now fool if thou hast not spend thy strength howl among the tazia nasiban and can do no more sahib be it you the hindus have be hunt an old muhammadan round the ditch with club if they find he again they will kill he help we to pull he up put my hand to the long red silk waist cloth that be hang out of the window and we three pull and pull with all the strength at our command there be something very heavy at the end and it swear in an unknown tongue as it kick against the city wall pull
oh pull say lalun at the last pair of brown hand grasp the window sill and venerable muhammadan tumble upon the floor very much out of breath his jaw be tie up his turban have fall over one eye and he be dusty and angry lalun hide her face in her hand for an instant and say something about wali dad that could not catch then to my extreme gratification she throw her arm round my neck and murmur pretty thing be in no haste to stop she and nasiban be handmaiden of tact turn to the big jewel chest that stand in the corner of the white room and rummage among the content the muhammadan sit on the floor and glare one service more sahib since thou hast come so opportunely say lalun wilt thou'--it be very nice to be thou ed by lalun--'take this old man across the city the troop be everywhere and they might hurt he for he be old to the kumharsen gate there think he may find carriage to take he to his house he be friend of mine and thou art more than friend therefore ask this nasiban bend over the old man tuck something into his belt and raise he up and lead he into the street in cross from the east to the west of the city there be no chance of avoid the troop and the crowd long before reach the gully of the horseman hear the shout of the british infantry cry cheeringly hutt ye beggar hutt ye devil get along go forward there then follow the ringing of rifle butt and shriek of pain the troop be bang the bare toe of the mob with their gun butt for not bayonet have be fix my companion mumble and jabber as we walk on until we be carry back by the crowd and have to force our way to the troop catch he by the wrist and feel bangle there the iron bangle of the sikhs but have no suspicion for lalun have only ten minute before put her arm round thrice we be carry back by the crowd and when we make our way past the british infantry it be to meet the sikh cavalry drive another mob before they with the butt of their lance what be these dog say the old man sikhs of the cavalry father say and we edge our way up the line of horse two abreast and find the deputy commissioner his helmet smash on his head surround by knot of man who have come down from the club as amateur constable and have help the police mightily we will keep they on the run till dawn say petitt who be your villainous friend have only time to say the protection of the sirkar when fresh crowd fly before the native infantry carry we
hundred yard near to the kumharsen gate and petitt be sweep away like shadow do not know can not see this be all new to moan my companion how many troop be there in the city perhaps five hundred say lakh of man beat by five hundred and sikhs among they surely surely be an old man but the kumharsen gate be new who pull down the stone lion where be the conduit sahib be very old man and alas can not stand he drop in the shadow of the kumharsen gate where there be no disturbance fat gentleman wear gold pince nez come out of the darkness you be most kind to my old friend he say suavely he be landholder of akala he should not be in big city when there be religious excitement but have carriage here you be quite truly kind will you help to put he into the carriage it be very late we bundle the old man into hire victoria that stand close to the gate and turn back to the house on the city wall the troop be drive the people to and fro while the police shout to your house get to your house and the dog whip of the assistant district superintendent crack remorselessly terror stricken bunnia cling to the stirrup of the cavalry cry that their house have be rob which be lie and the burly sikh horseman pat they on the shoulder and bid they return to those house lest bad thing should happen party of five or six british soldier join arm sweep down the side gully their rifle on their back stamp with shouting and song upon the toe of hindu and musalman never be religious enthusiasm more systematically squash and never be poor breaker of the peace more utterly weary and footsore they be rout out of hole and corner from behind well pillar and byre and bid to go to their house if they have no house to go to so much the bad for their toe on return to lalun 's door stumble over man at the threshold he be sob hysterically and his arm flap like the wing of goose it be wali dad agnostic and unbeliever shoeless turbanless and froth at the mouth the flesh on his chest bruise and bleed from the vehemence with which he have smite himself break torch handle lie by his side and
his quivering lip murmur ya hasan ya hussain as stoop over he push he few step up the staircase throw pebble at lalun 's city window and hurry home most of the street be very still and the cold wind that come before the dawn whistle down they in the centre of the square of the mosque man be bend over corpse the skull have be smash in by gun butt or bamboo stave it be expedient that one man should die for the people say petitt grimly raise the shapeless head these brute be begin to show their tooth too much and from afar we could hear the soldier sing two lovely black eye as they drive the remnant of the rioter within door of course you can guess what happen be not so clever when the news go abroad that khem singh have escape from the fort do not since be then live this story not write it connect myself or lalun or the fat gentleman with the gold pince nez with his disappearance nor do it strike that wali dad be the man who should have convoy he across the city or that lalun 's arm round my neck be put there to hide the money that nasiban give to khem singh and that lalun have use and my white face as even well safeguard than wali dad who prove himself so untrustworthy all that know at the time be that when fort amara be take up with the riot khem singh profit by the confusion to get away and that his two sikh guard also escape but later on receive full enlightenment and so do khem singh he flee to those who know he in the old day but many of they be dead and more be change and all know something of the wrath of the government he go to the young man but the glamour of his name have pas away and they be enter native regiment of government office and khem singh could give they neither pension decoration nor influence nothing but glorious death with their back to the mouth of gun he write letter and make promise and the letter fall into bad hand and wholly insignificant subordinate officer of police track they down and gain promotion thereby moreover khem singh be old and anise seed brandy be scarce and he have leave his silver cooking pot in fort amara with his nice warm bedding and the gentleman with the gold pince nez be tell by those who have employ he that khem singh as popular leader be not worth the money pay great be the mercy of these fool of english say khem singh when the situation be put before he will go back to fort amara of my own free will and gain honour give good clothe to return in so at his own time khem singh knock at the wicket gate of the fort and walk to the captain and the subaltern who be nearly gray head on account of correspondence that daily arrive from simla mark private have come back captain sahib say khem singh put no more guard over it be no good out yonder week later see he for the first time to my knowledge and he make as though there be an understanding between we it be well do sahib say he and greatly admire your astuteness in thus boldly face the troop when whom they would have doubtless tear to piece be with you now there be man in fort ooltagarh whom bold man could with ease help to escape this be the position of the fort as draw it on the sand-- but be think how have become lalun 's vizier after all
